eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Le Farasii yaab nin Sadusi yaab dan Yisa ban ki nan mag u lig, le ki baan u ke, un wɔɔn bi limagil lan nyan paab ni.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni ye titalangbann nan mann kujɔɔg ke, ni ti bei le ke, Won nan a taaŋ wo li ween.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Amaa ni ye ke njakpanyiɛn titalangbann mann ki nan bɔnn ke, ni ti bei le ke Dinan ma, ntafaam li da. Ni bugir titalangbann a sam, amaa le kaa sin fir bugir n-yonn nan ma.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Npooton nin iwanchɔgin toon a ninfob nan ban bin kan ligaril a toon; amaa maa yin tun ligaril a toonl liba ki sei bi; see Jona a garil a toonlin a binba. Le Yisa fɔr ki jiɛn bi.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Tɔ, Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah tan lɔŋ limɔgil a dool n-yonn man ni, le u bɔɔnɔɔbin ni tiɛr ke baa ŋmag boroboro.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Le u bei ke, Ni li nyi ki li guu ni ba ki jiin Farasii yaab nin Sadusi yaab a boroboro a nyɔgimin bu.

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Le bi nin piin ki bei tab ke, Taah kaa ŋmag boroboro ki dan ni bu.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Le u biɛ baah pag man nmɔn ki baan bi ke, Nim yada parim dam nan, babu wee ni bei tab ke naah kaa jɔɔ boroboro-n buu?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Naa le laa biɛ ni taai yaa? Naa tiɛr maah nan ta boroboro kon iŋmu ki jin bijab turiŋmu-n naa? Tikachar ti ŋaa wee ni nan lagin tijiir tan nan gurin naa?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Bee naa ji tiɛr bijab turina ban wee n nan ta boroboro kon ilile ki jin bin naa? Ni nan lagin tijiir tan nan gurin ki gbeen tikachar ti ŋaa?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Nii wee naa biɛ ke naa ye boroboro le n pag gaa? Ni li nyi Farasii yaab nin Sadusi yaab a boroboro a nyɔgimin bu.

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin nin biɛ ke naa ye boroboro a nyɔgim bamɔn bu le u pag bi ke bi li guu bi ba; amaa Farasii yaab nin Sadusi yaab a wɔɔnimin bu.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Yisa kaah nan tan foo Sizariya Filipi a tingbɔŋin n-yonn man ni, le ki baan u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, Bininfob ke Uninfosaal a Big ye ŋmiɛ.

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Le bi jiin u ke, Biba ke a ye Jɔnn Wan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin le, le biba mu ke a ye Elaja le, le biba mu ke a ye Jerimaya le, bee a ye bilinlem ban nan be yaayoo a ni uba le.

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Le u nin baan bi ke, Amaa ke ni mu, ni ke n ye ŋmiɛ.

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita bei u ke, Fin le ye Masiya, Uwonbɔr wan fo nan a bija.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Fin Simɔnn Jona a bija nan, tikaar be a bu, nan bu-n naa ye uninfosaal le kpiir tibɔr nan ki wɔɔn niŋi, amaa n Baa wan be paab nan le kpiir ti ki wɔɔniŋ.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: N beiŋ le Piita , a ye litangbagil le; litangbagil nan bu le n yin ta biwonjiɛm a laguŋ ki tɔɔn; le nkum kaa li fir nyaŋ bi.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: N li seiŋ paab a naamin a naanganpiim; nan wee a li lol dulinya nan yaan, ni ma le yin lol paab, nan wee a ji lor jaa dulinya nan yaan ni, man mu le ni yin lor jaa paab.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Le Yisa nan kpaan u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke bi li nyi bin bei uba ke u ma ye Masiya.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Naah nan nyan iyaayoo ki jɔɔ chaa, le Yisa nan piin ki bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke ni kpaa le ke un jo Jerusalam, amaa kitiŋ a ninkpeeb, nin biminmanib a yudam, nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nan, li mugir u takpem ki ku u; amaa iweehn itaa daal Uwonbɔr li fiin u nkumin.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Le Piita yin u ki nyan ngbaan, le ki fuun u bu ke, Aai, Uyudaan! Tibɔbir nan kaa li dan a bu.

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Le Yisa gbiɛ lig Piita ki bei u ke, Sintani nan, fɔɔr n ban na! A ye litangbag gbiɛrikaal le n samin, nan bu-n ŋaa tiɛr nan ye Uwonbɔr yaan bu, le ki tiɛr nan ye uninfosaal yaan.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Amaa le ki bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, Wan ban un nɔɔm ke, see ke u yii u ba le ki yuur u kum a dapunpunl ki nɔɔm.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Nan bu-n wan kaa chee un weeh u sayiig n bu ke, u ma na yin lan u miɛfol, amaa wan weeh u sayiig n bu ke u ma na yin kan limiɛfol nin ibamɔn.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Ni ye uninfo jɔɔ dulinya nan a jɔɔm biɛn, wee ki nan lann u miɛfol ke, ba a nyɔɔr wee u yin kan naa? Ba wee uninfo li fir ta ki kpenn u miɛfolaa?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Nan bu-n uninfosaal a Big na li jirin nin u Baa a pag, nin u malakam iyaayoo nmɔn, u na li yɔ uninfo biɛn nin u toon kaah ye man.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, biba be doo kaa li kpo see ke bi nan kan Uninfosaal a Big kaah na li foon nin nnaam man.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Le Kinaaŋ che Yisa don kukunkɔɔg bu ke ulinlaril ntan bigin u.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Waah nan lol bimɔb iweehn pina nin tinyiɛr pina-n a bɔɔn bu, le nkom nan choo u.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Le ulinlaril dan u ban ki nan bei u ke, Ni ye ke a sin ye Uwonbɔr a Bija ke, a bei itangbag nan wee i kpann tijiir.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, le Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin nan nan be kukunkɔɔg bu, Judiya a tingbɔŋin, ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni sɔb ke: Uninfosaal kaa be tijiir a binba bu, amaa imɔleen yan biɛn nyann Uwonbɔr a mɔnn ni. .

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: ki ke Ni jiɛn tibira, nan bu-n paab a naamin a jim mala.

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Le ulinlaril ta u ki jon Jerusalam, kan a yinl ji ye kasi a tiŋin, le ki ta u don iwonjiɛn niin a mɔɔ pin yul bu.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Wan wee Ulinlenn Azaya nan pagin sɔ, waah nan ke: Uninfo uba be kukunkɔɔg bu ki kuun ke Ni mann Uyudaan a dam a samin ki bila, ki mann u sam nmɔn wee mi li tela. .

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Le ki bei u ke Ni ye a sin ye Uwonbɔr a Bija ke, a yug ki lu tuma. Nan bu-n ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: U li che u malakam nguuriŋ, wee ki keŋ nin bi nɔɔi, le a taal kaa li gbiɛr litangbagili. .

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Alakoomi a gbann le bi nan ta ŋaa Jɔnn a wan yeewann, le ki nan lol nchangban u chaŋin, le u jiir nan ye itɔɔn nin tiseer.

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, Ni ji sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Laa mag Uyudaan a Wonbɔr wi. .

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Le bininfob nan nyan Jerusalam, nin Judiya a tingbɔŋ biɛn, nin Jɔdann a tingbɔŋin biɛn ki jon u ban.

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Le ulinlaril ji ta u ki don lijɔɔl lan kper a paab, ki wɔɔn u kutingbɔŋ nan a yagi nin i ŋam biɛn.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: Le ki tan kpiir bi toonbir, le u fun bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful Jɔdann a kpenn.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: Le ki ke, A bi li gbaan ki jiɛm ke, n bi li ta ni biɛn ki seiŋ.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Amaa waah nan nan kan Farasii yaab nin Sadusi yaab takpem mu chaan u ban ke un fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful nan, le u bei bi ke, Nim iwaalab a buwaab nan! Ŋma kpaan ni ke ni guun ki nya Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ kan yin dan ni bu nan naa?.

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Fɔr n ban na, Sintani! Nan bu-n ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Li jiɛn Uyudaan wan ye a Wonbɔr win, ki li tun tenn u binba. .

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Ni tun nan li wɔɔn ke ni sin jiɛn ni toonbira.

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Le ulinlaril fɔr ki sin jiɛn u, le malakam dan ki nan ter u.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Ni laa tiɛr ke ni li fir bei niba ke, Ti jɔɔ Abiraham wan ye ti yaaja. N bei ni le ke Uwonbɔr li fir fiin itangbag nan wee yin kpann Abiraham a yaabili.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Yisa kaah nan nan gbir ke bi loon Jɔnn kuloondiigin ni, le u nan jir Galilii.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Lilajal pii tɔɔ isiig a nyagi bu le, le busub ban biɛn kaa lo iloon ŋan ke, bi li ga i jaa nŋmi-n.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: U nan nyan Nazarif le, ki tan be Kapeniɛm kan be Zebulun nin Nafitali a mɔgil a gbaamin.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Min fun ni nnyum man wɔɔn ke ni jiɛn ni toonbira, amaa wan yin ŋɔɔrin n bɔɔn nan ma jon ma, le maa pii kpaan nin n tu u naatagili, u ma yin fun ni Kasi a Naaŋ nin nŋmi .

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: Ni niɛ nan nan tun ki gbeen ulinlenn Azaya kaah nan pag nan ke:.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: U ŋmag u dilenn yikpeŋin le ki yin lenn idi nmɔn biɛn nbamɔm, ki nyan ndibimin ki ŋuun u binbolin, le ki nyɔɔ kufoŋin nŋmi man kaa jɔɔ njunimi.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: Zebulun a tiŋin nin Nafitali a tiŋin, man be limɔgil a sam bu-n, ki yeg Jɔdann ni, ban kaa ye Juu yaab a Galilii;.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Le Yisa nyan Galilii ki jon Jɔdann a kpenn ke Jɔnn ntan fu u Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: bininfob ban be libunbɔnlin nan ni kan nwonween takpen na; le ban ka nkum a jinjiimin a tingbɔŋin ni kan nween nin kaah won ki pii ba.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Amaa le Jɔnn kaa chee. Le ki bei u ke, Min li bi ye n dan a ban wee a fum, le a mu ji chaan n ban naa.

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa piin ki mɔɔn ke, Ni jiɛn ni toonbirin na ke paab a naamin malin na.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Che ni ŋa man daandaan nan, ni ŋan nin ti tun man wee nin gbeen nbuchin binben biɛn. Iyaayoo nmɔn le Jɔnn chee.

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Yisa kaah nan chonn Galilii a mɔgil a gbaamin, le u nan kan nnabum milee, bi ma nan ye Simɔnn wan wee bi nan yi u ke Piita, nin u waal Anduru. Bi nan ye bijanchoob le ki be jaar kujanlaaŋ limɔgilin.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Waah fun Yisa Uwonbɔr a nyunful diɛ-n, le Yisa nyan nnyumin weem. Libol nmɔn le kitilangbɔŋ loor, le u kan Uwonbɔr a Naaŋ kaah jiirin u bu ki nann ulanjel, ki nan wonn u bu.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni dan, ki na li nɔɔm ni, wee n che ni li chor bininfob ke naah chor ijan man ni.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Le likukɔl nyan paab ni ki len ke, N bija sɔ, u ma le N jiɛ, u ma le N num gbee.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Le bi nan jiɛn bi janlann weem ki nɔɔ u.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Waah nan nyan niin ki chaa-n, le u kan Zebedi a naachim bilee, Jemisi nin u waal Jɔnn. Bi ma nin bi baa li nan be kuŋaluŋin ki mann bi janlann. Le Yisa yin bi.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: libol nmɔn, le bi jiɛn bi ŋaluŋ nmɔn weem, le ki nɔɔ u.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Yisa nan chonn jin Galilii a tim biɛn ni le, ki wɔɔn tiwonjiɛndirin, ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a naam a bɔŋann ni, ki che ban bu iwiɛn a booli biɛn kan kutebug.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Le u yinl nan mɔɔn Siriya a tiŋin. Le bi nan ŋmagin bibaarib a booli biɛn, ki dan u ban: Tigbanann wiɛn, ban gbee nin nnaanbiim, ban nan lur likpikpiil, nin tigbanann kpiindam, le u teb bi biɛn.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋ nan nyan Galilii, nin Ntim Piigin nin Jerusalam nin Judiya, nin Jɔdann a kpenn dool yaab biɛn nan nɔɔ u.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Yisa Masiya a baa nin u yaajam a piinim a gbɔŋ sɔ. U ye Defid a yaabil le, le Defid mu ye Abiraham a yaabil:.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abiraham le nan ma Azig, le Azig ma Jekɔb, le Jekɔb mu ma Juda nin u watiib.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Juda nan ma Perezi nin Zera, wan a na nan ye Tamar nan; le Perezi nan ma Hezironn, le Hezironn ma Ram.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: Ram le nan ma Aminadab, le Aminadab ma Nasonn, le Nasonn mu ma Salimɔnn.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Lidaal nmɔn le Yisa nyan iniin nmɔn yaan ki jon ki tan kal limɔgil a gbaam.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: Salimɔnn le nan ma Bɔɔzi, wan a na nan ye Rahab nan, le Bɔɔzi ma Obed, wan a na nan ye Ruf nan, le Obed mu ma Jesi.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋ lagin u ban; mambu, le u jon ki tan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin, amaa le kuninfogbaluŋin ma sir limɔgil a dool.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: Jesi le nan ma ubɔr Defid. Le Defid mu ma Solomɔnn, wan a na nan ye Yuriya a poo-n.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Le u gaa bi ibɔŋal tiwann takpemin ke, Uja uba le nan jon bukpaabin ke un tan yaal u dii.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Solomɔnn le nan ma Rehoboam, le Rehoboam ma Abija, le Abija mu ma Asa.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: Waah nan yaalin le idi iba lu nsan bu, le nnɔɔm dan ki nan pii i jin.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: Asa le nan ma Jehosafa, le Jehosafa ma Jehoram, le Jehoram mu ma Uzaya.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Le iba mu lu kutintiɛg bu, nan yaan wee titann kaa yab nan, le i pan ki yabig weem, nan bu-n titann kaah kaa nan yab niin ni.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: Uzaya le nan ma Jotam, le Jotam ma Ahazi, le Ahazi mu ma Hezekaya.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Amaa le nween kaah nan toŋ n-yonn man ni, le i jɔɔg. Yaah kaa nan fir jiin inyaan nin bu, i biɛn nan kpou.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: Hezekaya le nan ma Manaasa, le Manaasa ma Amonn, le Amonn mu ma Josaya.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Le iba mu lu ikunkon nin, le ki nan lagin pan nin ikunkon nin, le ikunkon nan yabig donn ki piin i bu.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: Le Josaya ma Jekonaya nin u watiib, baah nan choo Iziral yaab ki ta bi jon Babilɔnn n-yonn man nin.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Le iba mu lu kitinŋaŋ a bol, ki naan; le iba naan kokobig le iba naan piloob piloob le iba mu naan pita pita.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Baah nan choo bi ki ta bi jon Babilɔnn nan a bɔɔn bu, le Jekonaya ma Salatiɛl, le Salatiɛl mu ma Zerubabel.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Wan jɔɔ itul ke ni che un gbir.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: Zerubabel le nan ma Abiud, le Abiud ma Elayakim, le Elayakim mu ma Azor.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin dan u ban ki nan baan u ke, Ba ŋa wee a gaar ibɔŋal ki tenn bininfob nan naa.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: Azor le nan ma Zadog, le Zadog ma Akim, le Akim mu ma Eliud.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Nim ma, Uwonbɔr ta paab a naamin a bɔr woon kaar ki sei na. Amaa bi ma, waa che bi biɛ.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: Eliud le nan ma Eliɛzar, le Eliɛzar ma Matann. Le Matann ma Jekɔb.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Wan jɔɔ ke bi li sei u kpiɛ, wee u li jɔɔ takpem. Wan kaa jɔɔ ke, hali nan wee u jɔɔ-n gbaa bi li gaa ni u ban.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: Jekɔb le nan ma Josef, wan a poo nan ye Meri-n, le Meri ma Yisa wan wee bi yi u ke Masiya nan.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Ni ma bu le n gaar bi ibɔŋal: Hali baah le ligin baa ga; Hali baah le peligin baa gbir bee baa biɛri.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Naah nan nyan Abiraham a yonn ki nan kpaa Defid a yonn bi nan ye yaajam piig nin bina le. Le Defid a yonn ki tan kpaa n-yonn man wee bi nan chor bininfob ki jɔɔ bi chaa Babilɔnn nan, bi nan ye yaajam piig nin bina. Le Naah nan nyan iyaayoo ki tan kpaa Masiya a maalim a yoo, bi mu nan ye yaajam piig nin bina le.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Bi ŋaar nmɔn sin nan gbeen ulinlenn Azaya kaah nan len ke: Ni le yin gbaa gbir le amaa le kaa biɛri; Ni le yin ga le amaa le kaa yin biɛr ni taai.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Baah nan ma Yisa Masiya man sɔ: U na Meri nan ye Josef a jakpeŋ le, amaa baa la nan kɔɔn tabi, le bi nan kan ki miin Meri jɔɔ lipool le. Kasi a Naaŋ a pɔɔm bu.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Nan bu-n bininfobin a lam deg le, bi tuli kaa gbiri. Le bi dabin bi numbili. Naa ye man ke, bi bi li kan nin bi num ki gbir nin bi tuli ki ji biɛ nin bi sufu ni. Ki kpenn bi binbem wee n teb bi.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: Josef wan nan ye u chalin nan ye uninfo buchinn le, mambu, waa nan ban un nyan ti npaan nin ni, wee Meri n ji ifa, amaa u nan lol u sufu ni ke waa ji yin kɔɔn wi.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Amaa tikaar ye ni yaar le, nan bu-n ni numbili ga le ni tuli gbir.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Amaa waah ni nan tiɛr ni niɛ nan bu-n, le Uwonbɔr a malaka degirin u bu tidindɔgin ni, le ki nan bei u ke, Fin Josef wan ye Defid a yaabil nan, laa san bijawaam ke a ta Meri kun ki tan ŋaa u a poo, nan bu-n waah jɔɔ lipool lan ni, Kasi a Naaŋ a pɔɔm le che u choo lipool nmɔn.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, bilinlem nin bibuchim takpem nan ban bin kan naah kan nan nan, amaa le kaa kan ni, ki ji nan ban bin gbir tibɔr tan wee ni gbir nan, amaa le kaa gbiri.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: U na li maa kibijag, le a yin ki yinl ke Yisa, nan bu-n u na li nyan u ninfob bi toonbirin.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Ni pela wee ki biɛ ukpaar a bɔŋal nan a taai.

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn nan tun ke nin gbeen nan wee Uwonbɔr nan che u linlenu nan pag ni bu-n ke:.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Ni ye uninfo gbir paab a naam nan a bɔr nan wee kaa biɛ titaai ke, kininbɔŋ le dan ki nan choo nyan nan bun u sufu ni, ni ma ye ndibim man nan lu nsan ni.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: Lig! Usapɔɔn wan kaa nyi uja na li choo lipool ki maa kibijag, le bi na li yin u yinl ke Imanuel. Ni taai le ye, Uwonbɔr be nin ti.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Ndibim man nan lu kutintiɛg a paab nan mu le ye wan gbir Uwonbɔr a bɔr wee ki gaa ti weem nin nmɔn.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Josef kaah nan nan fii njiɛmin n-yonn man ni, le u tun ke Uwonbɔr a malaka kaah bei u ke un tun man ni, le ki ta Meri kun tan ŋa u poo.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Amaa waah kaa baan inyaan nin, ni ma bu waa ŋmag ti yuuri. Iyoo yan wee tibɔmug kaar bee limugil pii u tibɔr nmɔn bu ke, le un ta gab weem.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Amaa waa la nan ta u ki ŋa u poo nin waah nan nan ma kibijagin. Le Josef yin u yinl ke Yisa.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Ndibim man lu ikunkon nin nan mu le ye wan gbir Uwonbɔr a bɔr, amaa le dulinya nan a mugil nin njɔɔm a bam lu ki nyagin i bu, le taa li fir naan ni.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Amaa ndibim man nan lu kitinŋaŋ n-yaan nin mu le ye wan gbir Uwonbɔr a bɔr, wee ki biɛ titaai, wee i nan yabig ki naan, le iba naan kokobig bee piloob piloob bee pita pita.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Le Yisa ji nan gaa bi libɔŋal nan ki kpiɛ ke, Paab a naamin nann uja wan nan jon ki tan bun idibool ŋan u kpaabin le.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Amaa le baah nan dɔɔn jiɛn kunyiɛg nan, le u dataam fii ki jon ki tan bun tinaniin ki ŋmarin idibool ŋan nin yaan, le ki fɔr.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Idi-n kaah nan nan pob ki maa n-yonn man ni le bi biɛ ke tinaniin be niin.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Le u niin a toontunnu dan ki nan baan ukpadaan nin ke, N dindaau, naa ye idibool ŋan a binba le a nan ta bun a kpaab n-yaan naa? Ke ni ŋa nii wee tinaniin nan be niin naa.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Le u jiin ke, Kidataag kiba le ŋa man. Le u toontumin baan u ke, A ban ti tan ŋabir tinaniin nmɔn le bee.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Le u jiin ke, Aai, ni yin ŋab ke, ni na li ŋmar ŋab idi ŋan ni.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Mambu, ni jiɛn na wee i li be ki tan kpaa lidichiɛl a yonn, wee n che bidichiɛb n chinn ki gaar tinaniin ni ki poon wee n nyɔɔi nŋmi; wee ki ta idi ŋan ni ki kun tan ŋa n naapɔɔgin. .

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Le Yisa ji gaa bi libɔŋal nan ki kpiɛ ke: Paab a naamin nann nnyiɛnbim man wee uba nan ta bun u kpaabin nin le.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Hali gbaa maah kaa yab foo iwanbool biɛn ni, amaa le ki nan pan ki yabig ke, ki yab jon ikpasiig biɛn na, le nnɔɔm chaan ki nan tɔɔg ki bon bu.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Le Yisa ji gaa bi libɔŋal ki kpiɛ ke: Paab a naamin nann boroboro a nyɔgim man wee u pii ti ŋa ke un ta ŋmarin boroboro a yom man yabin wee min nan miir ki fugir ki do-n le.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn, Yisa nan ta ibɔŋal le ki pag sei kuninfogbaluŋ; waa nan pag niba ki sei bi wee naa ye ibɔŋali.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: Le ni nan gbeen ulinlenn kaah nan pag ki ke: N li loor n mɔb ki gaa ibɔŋal, n li pag tiwann tan nan woo hali dulinya a naan daal.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Le Yisa nan jiɛn kuninfogbaluŋin niin, ki jon tan kɔɔ iniin. Le u bɔɔnɔɔb dan u ban ki nan bei ke, Wɔɔn ti tinaniin tan be bukpaabin ni a bɔŋal a taai ni.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Le u jiin ke, Uninfosaal a Big le ye wan nan bun idibool ŋan nin.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: Dulinya nan mu le ye bukpaabin, idibool ŋan nin le ye Uwonbɔr a naamin a ninfob; le tinaniin nin mu le ye kininbɔŋ a ninfob;.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: kidataag kan bun tinaniin nin mu le ye ulinlaril; lidichiɛl a yonn mu le ye dulinya a diɛm; le bidichiɛb n mu le ye Uwonbɔr a malakam.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Baah yin lagin tinaniin nin ki nyɔɔ nŋmi man ni, dulinya a diɛ daal na yin be man le.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Uninfosaal a Big na li tunn malakam bi nan gann tininfobir ban che bi tab tun titoonbir nan biɛn, nin tibir dam nan biɛn.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Bi na li ta bi ki jaa nŋmi man kaa jun nin yaan, nin yaan le bi na yin be ki mɔr ki fabin ki ŋma bi nyin.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, le bibuchim na yin be bi Baa a naamin, ki nyagin ke nween kaah nyagin man ni. Wan jɔɔ itul ke ni che un gbir.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: Paab a naamin nann lifaal lan woo nkpapaan ni le, le uja uba nan kan ki mann woon li, le ki kun nin npopeen ki tan kɔɔr waah jɔɔ nan biɛn, le ki daa nkpapaan nmɔn.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Paab a naam ji nann unyanjir wan gu jin ki ban salima konl, un daa-n le.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: U nan tan kan lan jɔɔ linungbeel, le u jir kun ki tan kɔɔr waah jɔɔ nan biɛn, le ki daa salima nmɔn.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: Paab a naamin nann kujanlaaŋ kan wee bi ta jaa limɔgilin ki choo ijan a booli biɛn ni le.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Kujanlaaŋ nmɔn kaah nan nan gbee n-yonn man ni, le bi dar don likpengonl. Le ki kal ki gann ijan yan ŋan ni ŋa tikacharin, amaa le ki ta ijan yan kaa ŋan ni ki gab.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Ni na yin ye man le dulinya a diɛ daal. Malakam na li dan ki nan gann bibuchim, ki jiɛn tininfobir.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: le bin nan ta tininfobirin ki tan jaa nŋmi takpen ni yaan. Ni yaan nmɔn le bininfob na yin mɔr ki fabin ki ŋma bi nyin.

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Le Yisa baan ke, Ni biɛ ibɔŋal nmɔn biɛn a taai waa? Le bi jiin ke, Nn.

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Mambu, ban wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a siim biɛn wee bi wɔɔn bi ki jiin paab a naamin paam nbamɔm ke, bi nann uniindaan wan nyan u wan kpegir nin u wan pɔnn biɛn u naapɔɔgin le.

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Yisa kaah nan gaa ibɔŋal nan ki diɛ n-yonn man ni, le u nyan nin yaan.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: Ki kun u baantɔɔl a tiŋ, ki tan wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr, kuwonjiɛndiigin. U wɔɔnim nmɔn nan ŋa bininfobin ligaril le, le bi baan tab ke, La ban wee uja nan kan nlam nin npɔɔn ki tun ligaril a toon nan naa?.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Naa ye kanpinta a bija-n le yen naa? Naa ye u na le bi yin Meri nnaa? U watiib le kaah ye Jemisi nin Josef nin Simɔnn nin Judasi nnaa?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: Naa ye u ninsiɛb nin tim le bee? Ke u kan ni niɛ nan biɛn laa.

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Mambu le bi ŋuui nan fii u bu. Le u bei bi ke, Ulinlenn kaa jɔɔ jilima u baantɔɔl a tiŋ nin u niin ni.

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Le waa nan tun ligaril a toon takpem niin ni, nan bu-n baah kaa nan ŋa u yada n bu.

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Le Yisa nan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ, ki don limɔgilin a dool liba paam, ki kun u tiŋin.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Lig, bininfob biba nan ŋmag; tigbanann kpiindaan uba ki ta u paan kikpaarig bu, ki ta u dan Yisa ban. Yisa kaah kan ke bi ŋa u yada man ni, le u bei ubaarin ke, N bija, che a sufu li pɔɔu ke n jiɛn a bir ki periŋa.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Lig, le ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin a ni biba nin tiɛr bi sufu-n ke! U sugir Uwonbɔr le nmɔn.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Yisa ma nan biɛ baah tiɛr man na, le ki baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni tiɛr tipotiɛbir nan ni sufu-n naa?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Maah bei ubaarin ke, n jiɛn u bir ki per waan, bee maah bi li bei u ke, fiim ki li choon n naa, ti bɔr tilee nan ti la ba pɔɔ?.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Amaa wee ni biɛ ke Uninfosaal a Big jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm kutingbɔŋ nan yaan ki li fir jiɛn bininfob a bir ki per bi… Le ki bei uja wan wee u gbanann kpo nan ke, Fiim wee ki yuur a wan dɔɔn waŋ ki li kun na.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Le uja nmɔn fii ki kun.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Kuninfogbaluŋin kaah kan man ni, le bijawaam choo bi, le bi pag Uwonbɔr nin waah sei uninfosaal tininkper a pɔɔm nan a booli.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Le Yisa nyan nin yaan ki chaa, ki tan kan uja uba, le bi yi u ke Matiu, ki miin u ka u toon yin le bilanpogaab a nanbonn; le Yisa bei u ke, Fii ki li nɔɔm ni. Le u fii ki nɔɔ u.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Yisa kaah nan be Matiu a niin ki ji kujɔɔg a jiir n-yonn man ni, le bilanpogaab biba nin tibirdam takpem kɔɔn ki nan kal kpiɛ Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔbin bu ki ji bi ban.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Farasii yaab kaah nan kan man ni, le bi baan u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, Ba ŋa wee wan wɔɔnn ni nmɔn lagin ji nin bilanpogaab nin tibirdam ban naa.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Le Yisa gbir baah pag man nmɔn le ki len ke, Ban pɔɔ kaa ban utebiri, see ban bu-n.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Amaa ni li chaau ki tan tinn ki biɛ ni taai: N ban linyunyunbasanl le, naa ye kumanugi. Nan bu-n maa dan ke n nan yin bibuchim le, amaa tibirdam.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Le Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin nan a bɔɔnɔɔb dan Yisa ban ki nan baan u ke, Tim nin Farasii yaab lolig bimɔb le n-yonn biɛn; ke ba ŋa wee a bɔɔnɔɔbin ma kaa lolig gaa.

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Le Yisa jiin ki baan bi ke, Lipiichanil be ke, bicham le jɔɔ npoobiin, n-yonn man wee lipiichanil daan la be bi ban nan naa? Aai. N-yonn chaan, le bi na li nyan lipiichanil daan ni ban, iyaayoo le bi yin lol imɔɔŋ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Uba kaa li ta litanl pɔnl ki legin daala kpegi, ni ye u ŋa man ke, litanl pɔnlin li chiɛ daala kpegin, le nchiɛm nmɔn mann ki yann.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Uba mu kaa yin chee ki ta ndapɔm ki ŋuun libokpegilin ni, ni ye wan ŋa man wee ndapɔmin nan mɔɔ ke, min yin pɔɔ liboolin le, le ndaamin wur, le liboolin mu n bii. Aai bi jɔɔ ndapɔm ki ŋu liboopɔnlin le, wee ni biɛn n laa bii.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Yisa kaah la nan be pag ki tenn bi n-yonn man ni, lig, ki miin uyudaan uba dan ki nan gbaan u nintogil, ki bei u ke, N bisa le kpo; mambu, dan ki nan ta a nɔɔ ki paan u bu-u, wee un kan limiɛfol.

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Le Yisa nan fii nin u bɔɔnɔɔb le bi nɔɔ u jon.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Ki miin upii uba mu nan kan tipiir ke taa diɛri, ki tan kpaa ibin piig nin ilee; le u nan dan Yisa ban ki linn Yisa a bɔɔn le ki maa u daala takpeŋ a gbaam.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Upii nmɔn nan tiɛr u sufu ni ke, Ni ye n le pii u daala a gbaam ki maa ke, n li kan kutebug.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Le Yisa gbiɛ lig ki kan u, le ki len ke, N bisa, che a sufu li pɔɔu. Ŋaah ŋaam yada-n bu le a kan kutebug. Libol nmɔn, le u sin kan kutebug.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Yisa kaah nan tan kɔɔ uyudaan a niin n-yonn man ni, le u kan biwopeeb pee iwol, le kuninfogbaluŋ mu mɔr tikpemɔr ki fabin.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: le u ke, Ni li chaau. Kibisiɛgin kaa kpo, u jiɛn le. Amaa le bi laa u.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Baah nan ŋɔɔr bininfobin ki nyan a bɔɔn; le Yisa kɔɔ ki choo kibisiɛgin a nɔɔ, le u fii sir paab.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Le bi nan mɔɔn tibɔr nmɔn kutingbɔŋ nmɔn a gbanann biɛn.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyan niin ki nan jo chaa nintogil nan, le bijajɔɔm bilee nan paa u bɔɔn, le ki yin u ki len ke, Fin Defid a yaabil! San ti kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Yisa kaah nan chaa ki tan kɔɔ iniin iba ni n-yonn man ni, le bijɔɔmin foon ki nan kɔɔ niin. Le u baan bi ke, Ni ŋa yada ke n li fir sei ni kutebugaa? Le bi jiin ki ke, Nn, Uyudaan.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Le u ta u nɔɔ ki maa binumbili, ki ke, Naah ŋa yada man ni, nin tun man ki sei ni;.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: le binumbili ligir, le u kpaan bi nbamɔm ke, Ni li nyi ni bei uba nan tun nan bu.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Amaa le bi nyan ki mɔɔn u bɔr nmɔn kutingbɔŋ nmɔn yaan biɛn.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Baah nan fii ki nyan n-yonn man ni, le ki miin bi ŋmag uja uba ki dan Yisa ban, wan wee kinaanbiig nan be u ni wee waa fin pagi.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Le Yisa nyan kinaanbiigin uja nmɔn ni, le u ni nan piin ki pag. Ni nan gar kuninfogbaluŋin le, le bi nin pag ke, Taa la nyi kan nan ni, tim Iziral a tiŋ nan yaan ni.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Amaa le Farasii yaab len ke, Nnaanbiim a bɔru le sei u npɔɔn ke un nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Yisa nan choon ki gɔnn ntintakpem nin ntinbum biɛn nin, ki kɔɔ tiwonjiɛndirin, ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a naam a bɔŋann ni ki wɔɔn bininfob, ki che ban bu iwiɛn a booli kan kutebug.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: Waah nan kan kuninfogbaluŋin, le kunyunyunbaag choo u, nan bu-n ni nan mugir bi le bi be kaa jɔɔ wan li ter bi, ki nann ipee yan kaa jɔɔ upeekpaari.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Le u bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, Idi-n beer ki yaba, amaa le bidichiɛbin waar.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Tɔ le Yisa kaah nan kan kuninfogbaluŋin, le u nan don lijɔɔl a paab le ki tan kal. U bɔɔnɔɔb nan dan u ban.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Mambu, ni li miɛ bukpaab nmɔn daan na wee un kpiɛn bidichiɛb u kpaab nmɔn yaan ni.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: le u nan piin ki wɔɔn bi ke:.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Ni laa ji tab tibɔri, naa ye man ke Uwonbɔr mu li ji ni tibɔr.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Tikaar be ban ye bilanjiim kinaaŋin ni, nan bu-n paab a nnaamin ye bi yaal le.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Nan bu-n naah ji tab a bɔr man ni, man mu le bi na yin ji ni bɔr, kiyiikpoŋ kan wee ni jɔɔ magir ki tenn ni tabin, ki ma le bi na yin ta mag ki jiin ni.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Tikaar be ban mɔr tikpemɔr bu, nan bu-n bi li balin bi.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: A ŋa nii kan lidabul lan be a kpiɛg a numbim ni naa, wee kaa kan lidagenl lan be a numbilin naa?.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Tikaar be ban jiin bi ba tum nan bu, nan bu-n bi li ji dulinya nan a faal.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Ke a yin ŋa nii ki bei a kpiɛg ke, Che n nyan lidabul lan be a numbilin nan, wee isaŋ biɛn lidagenl be a tɔɔl a numbilin naa?.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Galilii a bɔrbig Herɔd nan gbir Yisa a bɔr bu.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Tikaar be nbuchin binben a kom nin nnyunyum kaah jɔɔ ban bu, nan bu-n bi na li gbee.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Fin ulinlaril nan, chinn nyan lidagenl lan be a tɔɔl numbilin ni na, wee ki ni fir kan nbamɔm ki nyan lidabul lan be a kpiɛg a numbilin ni.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: le ki bei u toontum ban nan be u ban ni ke, Uja nan ye Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin le, u fii nkumin le! Mambu le che u tun ligaril a toon yan jɔɔ npɔɔn ni.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Ni choom niba ki laa ŋaan tibuchinn bininfob bu, ni ŋa man wee bin kan ki pag ni. Ni tun man ke naa yin kan niba a yɔr ni Baa wan be paab ban ni.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Tikaar be binyunyunbasam bu, nan bu-n bi ma le bi na li san kunyunyunbaag.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Ni laa ta niwann nan be kasi ki sei ibɔ, ni laa ta nan ye tinyɔɔr a wann ke salima-n ki gab idooli. Ni ye a ŋa man ke bi li ta bi taafar ki ta i bu ki jin labir ki tɔr tɔriŋ.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Tɔ, Herɔd pii nan choo Jɔnn ki lol u ki ta u ki ŋa kuloondiigin, Herɔdiya wan ye u waal Filib a poo-n bu.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Farasiim nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nan nyan Jerusalam dan ki nan baan Yisa ke.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Ni ye a yin pu bilanjiim ipiin ke, a laa mɔɔn ke bilinlarib kaah ŋaan man tiwonjiɛndirin, nin isanjal bu ke bininfob n kan bi toon ŋan wee ki pag bin ni. Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, bi pii ga bi yɔra.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Yisa kaah nan wɔɔn u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee-n ki diɛ n-yonn man ni, le u nyan niin ki jon Galilii a timin un tan wɔɔn bininfob ki sin mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Tikaar be ban a sufu be kasi nan bu, nan bu-n bi ma na li kan Uwonbɔr.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Li miɛu ke bi li ta seiŋ; li ban na, ke a li kan; li kpaar linaanganla, ke li li loor ki seiŋ.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Nan bu-n Jɔnn pii nan gbaa bei Herɔd le ke: Naa ŋan nin a fee a waal a poo ki kɔɔn ni.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: Ba ŋa wee a bɔɔnɔɔb kaa nɔɔ yaajam a kaalaa? Bi ji le kaa sin nir bi nɔɔi.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Amaa ni ye a pu ulanjiin ke, laa che a nɔɔgam li nyi a nɔɔjiim kaah tun nan ni.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Baah nan ma Yisa Betiliham a tiŋ kan be Judiya a tingbɔŋin n-yonn man wee Herɔd nan ye ubɔr nan. Ni bɔɔn bu le bibiɛndam biba nan nyan liwonpɔɔnl paam ki dan Jerusalam.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Jɔnn kaah nan be kuloondiigin, ki nan gbir Yisa Masiya a toon a bɔrin, le u nan tun u bɔɔnɔɔb Yisa ban.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Tikaar be ban ban nposɔɔn bu, nan bu-n bi ma le bi na li yin bi Uwonbɔr a buwaab.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Nan bu-n wan biɛn miɛ ke u gaan le; le wan biɛn mu ban ke u li kan; le wan kpaar linaanganl le li loor tenn.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Herɔd nan ban un ku Jɔnn le amaa le ki sin san Juu yaab, nan bu-n bi nan chee ke Jɔnn ye ulinlenn le.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ba ŋa wee nim bii Uwonbɔr a siim, ni yaaja kaal buu?.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Le a piin nmɔn li ye liwoolin. Wee a Baa wan ga nan ŋaan liwoolin nan, na li yɔŋ.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Yisa nan yin u tooi piig nin bilee le, ki sei bi tininkper a pɔɔm ke bi li nyann nnaanbiim bininfobin, ki che ban bu iwiɛn a booli a booli n kan kutebug.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: ki nan baan bininfob ke, Juu yaab a bɔru wan wee bi ma-n, u be laa? Ti kan u ŋmabil le liwonpɔɔnl paam le ki dan ke ti nan jiɛn u.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyan lijɔɔl a paab ki jiirin n-yonn man ni, le bininfob takpem nɔɔ u bɔɔn.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: ke bin tan baan u ke, Fin le ye wan yin dan ni bee, ti li guu u baa.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Tikaar be ban kan limugil nbuchin binben bu, nan bu-n paab a naamin ye bi yaal le.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Ni ŋma, wee ni ye ke u big miɛ u boroboro ke, un ta litangbagil ki sei waa?.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Le ubɔr Herɔd a maa daal nan foo, le Herɔdiya a bisa nyann ki nan waa bi nintogil, le ni mɔŋ ubɔr Herɔd takpem.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan chaa ki tan foo biyookpaab biba ni likpakɔɔl daal; le nkom jɔɔ u bɔɔnɔɔbin, le bi genn iyoo ki pur ki ŋma.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Nan bu-n, Uwonbɔr ke, Sei a na nin a baa jilima; Wan sug u na bee u baa ke, bin ku wa.

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Ni ye a miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, a laa miɛ ke bilinlarib kaah tun man nin ni, nan bu-n bi ban bi li si tiwonjiɛndirin le, nin kitiŋ poolin a sanjali bu, ke bininfobin kan bi. N bei ni ibamɔn le, bi ma gaa bi yɔr ki gbeeu.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: U tooi piig nin bilee nmɔn a yin sɔ: Chinchinn le ye Simɔnn, , nin u waal Anduru, ki kpiɛ Jemisi nin Jɔnn ban nan ye Zebedi a naachim nan.

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Ubɔr Herɔd kaah nan gbir man ni, ni nan bii u ma nin Jerusalam yaab biɛn a sufu le.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Le ki miin kikɔŋ kiba mu dan ki nan gbaan u nintogil ki ke, Uyudaan, ni ye a ban ke a li fir che n wiɛm nan n diɛ.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni li jir ki tan wɔɔn u naah gbir man, ki kan man.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Tikaar be nim ban wee bininfob sugir, ki mugir, ki por tibɔkar tan bir biɛn ki tenn ni-n bu.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Bee u miɛ ujan ke un ta liwaalabil ki sei waa?.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: le u poo bimɔb ke u li sei kibisiɛgin waah li len ke u ban nan biɛn.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Farasii yaab kaah kan man ni, le bi bei Yisa ke, Lig! A bɔɔnɔɔb tun nan wee nsiim kaa chee sei nin ti li tun likpakɔɔl daal le.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Amaa nim ma wɔɔn ke, u ninfo bei u na bee u baa ke, Ŋaah bi li kan literil lan n ban ni, ni kpann Uwonbɔr yaan na.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Amaa ni ye a miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, a kɔɔm a diigin ki loon linaanganl, wee ki miɛ a Baa Uwonbɔr wan gbir tibɔr woon kaarin, wee un yɔŋ.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: Filib nin Batolomiu; Tomasi nin Matiu wan nan ye ulanpogaar nan, nin Jemisi wan nan ye Alifiu a bija nan, nin Tadiɛsi.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: Le u yin kumanug a yudam nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim ki baan bi ke la ban wee bi yin maa Masiya nmɔn naa?.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Le Yisa tann u nɔɔ ki maa u ki bei u ke, N ban man, a wiɛm nmɔn n diɛm! Libol nmɔn le u wiɛm nmɔn sin diɛ.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: ke ni kan bijɔɔm ligir, le nwɔbim a taai tann le bi choon, le nkɔm a gbanann ŋaŋ, le bitukpaab gbir, le bitakpiib fii nkumin, le tibɔŋann nin nan mɔɔn ki sei bilanjiim.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Che ni pool li sɔɔ ke ni mɔ na, ke ni nyɔɔr na li yab paab, nan bu-n man le bi pii nan mugir bilinlem baah kaa la nan ma ni-n.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Ke ni ye nim ban ye tibirdam nan nyi ipiin ŋan ni ta sei ni buwaab ke, ni Baa Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan ma kaa nyi nan ŋan kag ki li ta sei ban miɛ waa!.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Le u na wɔɔn waah li baan nan bu, le kibisiɛg nmɔn bei ubɔr Herɔd ke, Tugir Jɔnn Wan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin a yul ki kpeen kusɔŋin ki seim ni.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Le u jiin ke, Naa karin Defid kaah nan tun man n-yonn man wee nkom nan choo u ma nin u ninɔɔtabin naa?.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: naa ji ye limugil nin un sei u baa niba. Naah tun man nan, ni ma wɔɔn ke naa ŋmag Uwonbɔr a siimin ni, ni yaaja kaalin bu.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Le ni ye a miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, a laa jabin ke ban kaa jɔɔ Nween ni kaah ŋaan man ni ni, bi tiɛr ke bi miɛm takpem nmɔn le yin che Uwonbɔr ngbir.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: nin Simɔnn wan wee bi yi u ke Zealo nin Judasi Isikariya wan nan kɔɔr Yisa nan.

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Le bi jiin u ke, Betiliham, kitiŋ kan be Judiya a tingbɔŋin ni; nan bu-n man le ulinlenn nan sɔb:.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Le Yisa kpaan u ke, Li nyi a bei uba tibɔr nan, amaa li chaa Uwonbɔr a minmanu ban ki tan ta a ba ki wɔɔn u, wee ki jin mann kumanug kan wee Moziz nan siin ni, wee ni li ye siɛra.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Tikaar be wan kan limugil wee kaa ta n bɔr gab nan bu.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Ni ye kutingbɔŋ nan a yaam le, amaa n-yaam nan lann nmimalig ke, mu ji yin ŋa nii ki mɔŋ ŋaa? Maa ji ŋan ki li fir ŋa niba, man ke bin kpaar mi wur, le bininfob ni taan mi bu.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Nan wee a ban a kpiɛg li tun ki tenn niŋin, a mu li tun man ki tenn a kpiɛtiiba, nan bu-n Moziz a siim nin Bilinlem a wɔɔnimin wɔɔn man le.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Ni nan bii ubɔr n-yaa sufu le; u mu kaah chinn ki poo bimɔb nin u chamin bu, waa ji li fir yii, le ki jaa bi ke bin tun man.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: U nan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan le, ki tan yuur kasi a boroboro-n le u ma nin u ninɔɔtabin ŋman, amaa nsiim mu kaa nan chee sei nin bi ni uba n ŋman boroboro nmɔn ni, see Uwonbɔr a minmanib a binba.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Nim bilinlarib nan! Azaya kaah nan pag ilinlen leen ki jiin ni paami sin ye ibamɔn le:.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Ni laa be ke baah be man nmɔn ni, nan bu-n ni Baa Uwonbɔr pii nyi nan wee ni ban, ke naa la miɛ wi.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Le Yisa nan tun u tooi piig nin bilee nmɔn, le ki kpaan bi ke, Ni li nyi ni jo ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ban, ki laa kɔɔ Samariya yaab a timin ni.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: Amaa fin Betiliham kan be Juda a tingbɔŋin ni, ŋaa ye kitinbig Juda a paamin ni, nan bu-n a ban le ubɔr na li nya wan na li ye upeekpaar N ninfob ban ye Iziral yaab. .

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Yisa kaah nan tan kɔɔ Kapeniɛm n-yonn man ni, le Rom a tɔb a sanliiru wan ŋmag bitɔtogib kobig nan dan u ban ki nan balin u ke un ter u.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Tɔ, Jɔnn a bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah nan nyan nin yaan ki jir kun n-yonn man nin, le Yisa nan piin ki pag tenn kuninfogbaluŋin Jɔnn bu: Naah nan jon Jɔnn ban lisagilin ni, ni nan tiɛr ke ni tan yin kan baa? Igbingbaan yan wee ntafaam daarin naa?.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Ni ye dulinya nan a wonweem le. Kitiŋ kan be ligungonl a paab kaa li fir woon ni.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Kɔɔm ntimansanmɔnn man kaa yalin nan yaan; nan bu-n ntimansanmɔnn man yalin chaa nŋmi-n le, le bininfob takpem kɔɔ niin, nan bu-n nsan man chaa niin ni yalin le.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: le ki tun biba kuloondiigin le bi tan tugir Jɔnn a yul.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Bee naa karin nsiim a gbɔŋin, biminmanib kaah ti tun man likpakɔɔl daal biɛn, ki bii nsiimin, amaa wee kaa sin jɔɔ tibirin naa?.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Bininfob nan tenn nim jilima bi mɔɔŋi a binba le, amaa bi sufu fɔg nima.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Naah li miɛ Uwonbɔr man sɔ: Ti Baa wan be paab, che a yinl li be kasi.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Amaa ni li chaa Iziral yaab ban nann ipee yan borin ban.

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Le ubɔr Herɔd woon yin bibiɛndam nmɔn ki tan baan bi, ki feen bi nbamɔm un biɛ n-yonn man wee liŋmabilin nan nyann.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: Le ki ke, Uyudaan, n toontunnu le dɔɔ iniin, le u gbanann kpo, le ni mugir u pam.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Naa ye man ke, ni nan nyan jon ke ni tan kan baa? Uninfo wan yee tiwann yɔŋann bee? Aai, ban yee nnaam a wan yɔɔrin be ibɔrniin le.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Bininfob mu kaa nyɔɔr fila wee ki ta kiyiig chigin ki bu. Amaa bi jɔɔ tɔɔg nan kper bu le, wee ki li tenn iniin a ninfob biɛn nwonween.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: Limiɛfol a timansanmɔnn n ma kaa yalin ni, le mi sanchonl pɔɔ nin nnɔɔm, mambu che bininfob wama nan le kan mi.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Le bi nan ŋmagin u yul nmɔn, ki nan ta sei kibisiɛg nmɔn; le u ga tan sei u na.

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: N bei ni le ke, wan yab jon iwonjiɛn niin be do.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Bi jiɛnim yɔli le, nan bu-n bi jɔɔ bininfosaalib a siinim le ki wɔɔn. .

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: che a naamin foon, che a bam nŋa dulinya nan yaan, ke naah ŋaan paab man ni.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Naah chaa nan, ni li mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn ke: Paab a naamin mala.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Le ki bei bi ke, Ni li chaa Betiliham wee ki tan ban kibig nmɔn nbamɔma; ni tan kan ki n-yonn man ke ni jirin ki nan beim wee n mu n jo ki tan jiɛn u.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, N li jo ki tan teb u.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Ke ni lee nan jon ke ni tan kan baa? Ulinlenn naa? N bei ni le, u jon ulinlenn na.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Ni mu li ŋaan man na, ni che ni wonweem li ween bininfob ban na, wee bi li ga ni ŋaar kaah ŋan wee ki don ni Baa wan be paab nan a yinl.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: Ni li nyi tibɔkar a linlem nan bu, ban chaan ni ban ki nann ipee, wee ki sin ye igbingbel bamɔn nan.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Le Jɔnn a bɔɔnɔɔb dan ki nan ta u gbanann ki pii, le ki jon ki tan bei Yisa.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Ni ye ni bi nyi ke Uwonbɔr ban linyunyunbasanl le ki jon kumanug ke, naa bi li pag ki bii ban kaa jɔɔ biri.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Le Yisa nan yin kuninfogbaluŋin ki bei bi ke, Ni pela man, wee ki biɛ nan wee n yin pag nan.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Sei ti dinan a jiir.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Ni li teb bibaariba, ki li fiin bitakpiiba, ki jin che nkɔm nkan kutebug, ki nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin na. Ni gaa yɔli le, ni mu li tenn yɔliu.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Bibiɛndamin kaah nan gbir ubɔr a bɔr ki diɛn a bɔɔn, le bi gaa nsan, le ki kan liŋmabil lan wee bi pii nan kan liwonpɔɔnl paam min, le bi nɔɔ li. Le liŋmabilin nmɔn liin bi sam ki tan sir nan yaan wee kibigin be-n a paab.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Le bitɔb a sanliir win jiin ke, Uyudaan, maa kpaan nin a dan n niin ni. Amaa a pag limɔbonl a binba ke n toontunnu nmɔn li kan kutebug.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: U yaa bɔr le Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ pii nan sɔb ki wɔɔn ke, N li tun n toom un liir a nintogil, ki tan mann a dam a sam.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: Ni laa tiɛr ke n dan n nan bii Nsiimin bee Bilinlemin le; Maa dan n nan bii i, amaa n na yin gbeen i le.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Bi loon yin le ni li biɛ baah ye ban. Bininfob ker igbiin bikunkom a sub buu, bee bi ker ikinkan kujagorug buu?.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Yisa kaah gbir nan tun ni, le u fii niin ki kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ u binba, ki jon lisagilin. Kuninfogbaluŋin kaa kan man ni, le bi choon tum bi taai bu ki kpagin limɔgil ki tan kenn u limɔgil a dool.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Nan bu-n Uninfosaal a Big le ye likpakɔɔl daal nmɔn a yudaau.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: Nan kɔɔ uninfosaal a mɔnn kaa ŋa u tijaginn ni; amaa nan nya u mɔnn ni, ni ma ŋaan u tijaginn. .

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Jiɛn ti bir ki per ta. Ke ti mu kaah jiɛn per ti pɔndam man ni.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Ni laa ŋmag salima bee tikurninann bee tikurbinn ni bɔɔlimin ni.

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: Baah nan kan liŋmabil nmɔn ni le ni mɔŋ bi takpem.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Nan bu-n n baantɔɔl ye wan cheel tininkper a pɔɔm le, ki ŋmag bitɔtogib n bɔɔn. N bei uba le ke, Li chaau, le un jo; ki bei utɔ mu ke, Dan ni, le un dan. N le bei n toontunnu ke, Ŋa nan na ke, u yin tun man le.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le: Baa laa nyi ma uba wee u daan jon Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin ni, amaa hali wan nan joor paab a naamin ni jon Jɔnn na.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, paab nin tum kaa na li be, hali nsɔbimin a wama kaa li nyi bor nsiimin yaan ni, see ke ni biɛn nan nan tun ki diɛ.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Kisiŋaŋ biɛn lo iloon ŋan le; amaa kisibiig biɛn mu lo tiloonbir le.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Yisa kaah nyan kuŋaluŋ n-yaan ki kan kuninfogbaluŋin ni, le kunyunyunbaag choo u, le u teb bi wiɛn.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Le Yisa nyan nin yaan ki jon tan kɔɔ bi wonjiɛndiigin.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔb nan dan u ban ki nan baan u ke, A nyi ke Farasiimin kaah gbir a bɔrin bi ŋuui fii a buu.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Ki laa liin ti ki kɔɔn libiginlin ni, amaa wee ki nyan ti titoonbirdaan a nɔɔn.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: Ni laa ŋmag ipɔg ki chonn nsam nmɔn ni, bee ni ŋmag daala ilelee, bee inaatagi, bee njaangbiɛmi; nan bu-n u toontunn ŋan nin un ji u toon yin le.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Le bi kɔɔ iniin nmɔn yaan, ki kan kibigin nin u na Meri; le bi gbaan ki jiɛn kibig nmɔn, le ki biir bi pɔgi ki nyan ipiin ki sei kibig nmɔn; ipiin nmɔn nan ye salima, nin nnyɔgkusim, nin tulaali kan wee bi yi ki ke miɛr nan.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yisa kaah nan gbir man nmɔn, le ni gar u; le u gbiɛ lig bininfob ban nɔɔ u bɔɔn nin ki bei bi ke, Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, maa laa kan uba wee u jɔɔ yada nan a booli Iziral yaabin ni.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Naah nyan Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin a yonn ki nan kpaa daandaan nan, paab a naamin yabirig le nin ikɔn, le ban kɔnn nnaam nmɔn choo mi ki ŋmag.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Uninfo wan bii nsiim nan a wan finfiŋ, wee ki sin wɔɔn u tab ke bi mu li ŋaan man ke, bi na li yin u kuwan finfiŋ paab a naamin; amaa wan tun nsiim kaah ban man, ki ji wɔɔn u tab nsiimin nmɔn nbamɔm ke, bi na li yin u lininfojal paab a naamin.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Kisiŋaŋ kaa li fir lo iloon biri; kisibiig mu kaa li fir lo iloon ŋan ni.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Nween kaah nan nan diŋ n-yonn man nin, le u bɔɔnɔɔb dan u ban, ki bei u ke, Taah be nan yaan nan ye kulɔŋ le, nween mu ban min lu le; mambu che bininfobin jo ntinbum man mal do nan ki tan kan tijiir daa.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: uja uba mu nan be niin, le u nɔɔnkpaŋ kpo. Le biba mu ban bin kan Yisa a bir le ki bii u; mambu, le bi baan u ke, Ti siim chee sei nin uninfo li teb ubaar likpakɔɔl daal waa.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Le u jiin ke, Niwann nan biɛn wee n Baa wan be paab nan kaa ŋuun ni, bi li ŋab ni ki gab.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Nan bu-n ni ye ni jiɛn ni tab a bir ki per bi ke, ni Baa Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan mu li jiɛn per ni.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Amaa ntintakpem bee ntinbum man wee ni li kɔɔ-n, ni baan ki kan upoopendaan a niin na, wee ki kɔɔ ki li be niin nin ni fii daal.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Tɔ, le Uwonbɔr kpaan bi tidindɔgin ni, ke bi laa ji jir ubɔr Herɔd ban ni, mambu le bi ni nan nɔɔ nsan nba ki jir kun bi tiŋ.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: N bei ni le, bininfob takpem na li nyann titogil nin bɔɔn, le bi ma nin Abiraham nin Azig nin Jekɔb li lagin kal ki ji tijiir, paab a naamin.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Nan bu-n Bilinlemin biɛn nin Nsiimin pii nan pag ilinlen leen na, le Jɔnn nin dan.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: N bei ni le ke, naa che ni buchim yab jon Farasii nin ban wɔɔnn nsiimin a buchinn ke, naa sin li fir kɔɔ paab a naamin ni.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Bisib ban biɛn kaa lo iloon ŋan ke bi li ga bi jaa le ki ta bi gab nŋmi-n.
