eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Le u jiin ke, Bi laa chaa. Ni tɔɔl n seim bi tijiir wee bin ji.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Le u mu ji labir ki baan bi ke, Ni ye ni ni uba bi jɔɔ upee uba, wee ni ye upee nmɔn tan lu kinyunbuŋin likpakɔɔl daal ke, waa li don u likpakɔɔl daal nmɔn naa?.

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Ni jiɛn baah, bi nann bijɔɔm le ki da bi jɔɔn tab; ke ni ye ujɔɔn da ujɔɔn kpiɛg ke, see ke bi biɛn tan lu kiboogin.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Amaa naa jiɛn per ni tab bi bir ke, ni Baa Uwonbɔr mu kaa yin jiɛn ni bir ki per ni.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Naah yin kɔɔ iniin yan yaan ni, ni poor iniin nmɔn yaab nbamɔma.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Baah nan kun a bɔɔn le Uyudaan a malaka nan dan Josef ban tidindɔgin nin, ki bei u ke. Fiim ki ta kibiliɛg nmɔn nin ki na ki li gu chaa Ijibir a tiŋin na, hali nin maah na li yin ni, nan bu-n ubɔr Herɔd yin ban kibigin a bol le un ku ki.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Amaa nim ban ye nnaam nmɔn a ninfob nan, bi na li ta ni ki gab ngbaan, libunbɔnlin; nin yaan nmɔn le ni tan yin fabin ki mɔr ki ŋma inyin.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Ni ye ni ban ni che tibɔr nmɔn ke, u ma ye Elaja wan nan yin dan ni.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: Ni pii gbir baah nan bei bininfob iyoo yan jon ni ke, Laa ku uninfo, le wan ku uninfo ke u na li kan litudaril tibɔr a ji daal.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: Mambu, bi looi yin le ni li biɛ bi.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Le bi jiin ke, Boroboro kon iŋmu nin njanbum milee le be ti ban doo.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Ni li nyi ke uninfosaal jɔɔ tinyɔɔr ki jon upeeu! Mambu, nsiim chee sei nin ti li tun itoon ŋan likpakɔɔl daala.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Le Piita len ke, Wɔɔn ti libɔŋal nan a taai ni.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Ni ye ni lol imɔɔŋ ke, ni laa bii ni num paam ke bilinlarib kaah ŋaan man nin ni, nan bu-n bi ti bii bi num paam le, ke bininfob biɛn n kan ki biɛ ke bi lol bi mɔɔŋi le. N bei ni ibamɔn le, bi pii gaa bi yɔra.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Ni ye iniin nmɔn yaab ye bibuchim ke, ni che ni posɔɔmin paar bi bu; amaa ni ye bibuchim kaa be iniin nmɔn yaan ke, ni che ni posɔɔm nmɔn jirin ni ban.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Le u nan fii kunyiɛg ki ta kibigin nin ki na ki guun jon Ijibir a tiŋin.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Le Yisa bei bitɔb a sanliir win ke, Li kun na ŋaah ŋa yada man ni, nin tun man ki seiŋa. Le u toontunnu nmɔn sin nan kan kutebug n-yonn nmɔn.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Wan jɔɔ itul ke, ni che un gbir.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Amaa N bei ni le ke uninfo wan fiin u ŋuul u nabig bu ke u li kan litudaril tibɔr a ji daal. Le wan mu ji sug u nabig ke, wan yɔli nan ke, ni ye bin ta u ki jo Juu yaab a yudam a laguŋ ban le. Bee wan mu ji sug u nabig ke, kujɔrug nan! Ke, u na li be nŋmi man kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin a daŋin.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Naa ye ban biɛn beim ke, Uyudaan, Uyudaan, li na li kɔɔ paab a naamin ni, amaa wan chee ki tun n Baa wan be paab a bamin.

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni ta i seim ni man.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Le u bei uja-n ke, Tann a nɔɔu. Le u tann, le mi jir pɔɔg ke mi miɛnin ni.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Le Yisa baan bi ke, Naa le laa biɛ?.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Amaa ni ye a lol bimɔb ke, a ŋunn a num paama ki ta nkpam ki ŋa a yul;.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Wan yii kaa gaa ni ke, wee ki ji yii ni bɔŋann a gbirim, wee ni yin nyan iniin nmɔn yaan bee kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ke, ni pir pir ni taafar a tann ki jaa nin yaan na.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: nan yaan wee u nan tan be hali nin ubɔr Herɔd kaah nan nan kpo. Ni niɛ nan biɛn nan tun ki gbeen nan wee Uwonbɔr che u linlem nan pag ke, Ijibir a tiŋin le n yin n bija nmɔn nyann.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: N-yonn man wee Yisa nan kɔɔ Piita a niin ni, le u kan Piita a chɔɔpii kaah dɔ bu, ni nan ye kugbanantoŋ le.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Nim daandaan nan a ninfob, n yin ta ni ki bigin baa? Ni nann nbuwaab kaah ti ŋa ke bin lagin tab ki tan ka kinyaŋin ki yi tab ke:.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Mambu ni ye a yin pu ipiin iwonjiɛn niin wee ki tiɛr a nabig kaah galiŋ bu ke.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Lidaal nmɔn nan foo ke, bininfob takpem na yin yim le ki ke, Uyudaan, Uyudaan, taa nan pag ilinlen leen a yinl buu? Naa ye a yinl bu le ti nan nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin, ki ji tun ligaril a toon yin naa.

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: le ki sin bei bininfobin ke bin kalim timɔɔr bu. Le u yuur boroboro kon iŋmu-n nin njanbum milee-n, ki waan lig paab, ki sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog tijiir n bu, le ki war war boroboro-n ki sei u bɔɔnɔɔb, le bi tol tol bininfobin.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Amaa le Farasii yaabin nyan nkaafiin, ki lol baah li ŋa man ki ku Yisa.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Naa nyi ke nan kɔɔ bimɔbin yin jiir lipoolin le ki ji tan nya tigbanann ni naa.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: wee ni laa che bininfobin biɛ ke a lol bimɔbi, amaa a Baa wan wee baa ga win a binba, nin a Baa wan ga liwoolin ni, le li yɔŋ.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, lidaal lan wee Uwonbɔr nan yin ji bininfob tibɔrin, kitiŋ nmɔn yaab a tudaril na li jo Sodom nin Gomoriya yaab yaal.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Herɔd kaah nan nan biɛ ke bibiɛndam nmɔn lar u le-n, liŋuul nan gbir u pam, le u tun bitɔtogib ke bi li chaa Betiliham nin kitinbum ni ki tan ku nbijabum ban kaa lagir ibin ilee biɛn na, ke waah nan baan bibiɛndamin wee bi bei u liŋmabilin kaah nyan n-yonn man ni.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Le Yisa maa u nɔɔ; le u gbanantoŋ nmɔn diɛ, le u fii ki ŋa bi kuchɔŋ.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: Ti pee iwol ki tenn ni, le naa waa. Le ti mɔr tikpemɔr ki tenn ni, le naa fabin ni.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: jiɛn a piini nmɔn iwonjiɛn niin na. Chinn jo a nabigin ban wee ni tan tugin ni nabitiig nmɔn wee a nin jirin ki nan ta a piini nmɔn ki sei.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le n mu na li bei bi npaan ni ke, Maa nyi ni, nim titoonbirdam nan, ni fɔr n ban na.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Le bi biɛn jin gbee ki gur. Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin lagin tijiir tan gurin, le ti gbee tikachar piig nin tilee.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Le Yisa biɛ bi lol nmɔn, ki nyan niin. Le bininfob takpem nɔɔ u bɔɔn. Le u che bibaarib biɛn kan kutebug.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Amaa tiwann tan nya bimɔbin ni nyann sufu ni le, ni ma ŋaan uninfo tijaginn.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Laa jɔɔ a jɔɔm ki woog dulinya nan yaan ni, nan yaan wee ikootug nin gamlali nin ndundum li mɔɔ ki bii mi; bee binaayigib na li kɔɔ ki su.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Ni lig man, n tun ni le, ni yin be le ke ipee kaah be igbingbel a sisiigin man ni. Ni ma bu ni che ni num li ween ke iwaa-n, amaa wee ki li be soon ke ilanjelin.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: Le ni nan gbeen nan wee ulinlenn Jerimaya nan pagin:.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Lidaal nmɔn a jɔɔg, bi nan ta bininfob takpem ban wee nnaanbiim nɔɔ bin ki dan Yisa ban; le u pag limɔbonl ki nyan nnaanbiim nmɔn, le ki ji teb bibaarib nmɔn biɛn.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Nan bu-n Jɔnn nan dan le ki lolig bimɔb, kaa nyu ndaami, le bi ke, U waar le.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: Fin nin uba jɔɔ tibɔr, wee u yin taaŋ jo bibɔjib ban ke, a che ni ŋa mɔb biba ki mann ni bɔr nmɔn, n-yonn man wee ni la be nsan bu-n; naa ye man ke, u li taaŋ ki jo ubɔjir ban, le u mu li taaŋ ki ŋa uloondiiguur a nɔɔn, le un kɔɔniŋ kuloondiigin.

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: Mambu, wan biɛn gbir n bɔr wee ki ŋmag ti nbamɔm ke, u nann ulandaan wan nan maa u niin ki tɔɔn kutanpiig bu-n le.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Bijab ban nan jin tijiir nmɔn nan ŋa bininfob turiŋmu paam ma, bipiib nin nbuwaab a kanl kaa nan kpiɛ niin ni.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: le ki kpaan bi ke bi laa ta u ki mɔɔn ni.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: Uninfo a sufu ni le tipotiɛbir, nin lininfokul, nin iwanchɔgin toon, nin lipiibanl nin lijabanl, nin nnaayigim, nin tibɔkar a siɛra, nin kutingbiig nya.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Amaa ta a jɔɔm ki woon paab, nan yaan wee ikootug nin gamlali kaa li fir bii, bee binaayigib kaa li fir kɔɔ su.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Ni li nyi bininfob bu, bi na li choo ni ki ta ni jo bibɔjib ban, ki boo ni tiwonjiɛndirin.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: Ti gbir ikuun Rama a tiŋin, ifabin nin tikpemɔr tan yab, Rekiɛl le mɔr u buwaab bu, ki yii ibalin, nan bu-n baa ji be.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: Waah nan tun man ni, nan gbeen ulinlenn Azaya kaah nan pag man n le ke, U ma baantɔɔl le gaa ti gbanantoŋ nin ti wiɛn biɛn ki tu.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Le Uninfosaal a Big nan foon ki ji ki nyu, le bi ji ke, Bin lig, u ye ujijir le nin udanyugbeer, ki ji ye bilanpogaab nin tibirdam a jɔ. Amaa nlangɔr kaam le wɔɔn mi bamɔn.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, ŋaa li nyi ki nya, see ke a nan yɔn lipɔnlin biɛn ke koobu kaa guri.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Le utaal nan nun, le ikpen gbee, le nfaam daa, ki piɛr iniin nmɔn a dikakpin; amaa yaa nan lu, nan bu-n i fiɛl nan tɔɔ kutanpiig bu le.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Libol nmɔn le Yisa nan che u bɔɔnɔɔb kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki lii u sam ki chaa limɔgil a dool, amaa le u ma paar ke un chabir kuninfogbaluŋin.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: U nan tun man le, ke nan wee ulinlenn Azaya nan sɔbin ngbeen.

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: Ni nan biɛn li ŋaan uninfosaal tijaginn amaa tinɔɔjaginn a jim kaa li fir ŋa uninfo tijaginn ni.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Nan bu-n, nan yaan wee a jɔɔm be-n a potiɛl mu be niin le.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Min bu bi na li ta ni ki jo kitiŋ a yudam nin bibɔrtiib ban wee ni li ye siɛradam ki sei bima nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ban.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Herɔd kaah nan nan kpo a bɔɔn, le Uwonbɔr a malaka degirin Josef bu tidindɔgin nin Ijibir a tiŋin.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Yisa kaah nan nan kan ki miin kuninfogbaluŋ gɔɔn u n-yonn man ni, le u bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke bin fiim wee bin lɔŋ limɔgil nan a dool.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Le Yisa piin ki pag ki bii ntim man nan kan u garil a toon gbingbemi yan wee u tun ni, nan bu-n baah nan kpenn bi binbemin.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Ni pii nan gbir baah ke, Laa tun iwanchɔgin toon ni.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Amaa wan biɛn gbir n bɔr nan, wee kaa ŋmag ti ke, u nann kujajɔrug kan nan maa u niin litanbiiril ni le.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: Waah chabir kuninfogbaluŋin ki diɛ n-yonn man ni, le u don lijɔɔl a paab ke un miɛ Uwonbɔr. Le kunyiɛg nan ŋmiɛ ke u la be niin u binba.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: N toontunnu sɔ, u ma le n gann, wan wee n jiɛ, le n pool sɔɔn u bu. N li ta n naaŋin ki ŋa u ni. Le u li mɔɔn tibɔbamɔnn ki sei ibol biɛn.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Tɔ, le Yisa nyan nin yaan ki jon Taya nin Sidonn a yagil.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Linunbil le ye tigbanann a fila. Ni ye a numbili ŋan ke, a gbanann biɛn yin ye nwonween le.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Amaa bi nan choo ni ke, ni laa che ni mug ni, nin naah li pag man ni, nan bu-n iyaayoo le Uwonbɔr yin sei ni imɔbon le ni ta jiin bi.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: ki bei u ke, Fiim ki yuur kibig nmɔn nin ki na, ki jir Iziral a tingbɔŋin, nan bu-n ban nan lol bin ku kibigin kpou.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Le ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim n-yaa ni uba dan ki nan bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn n li nɔɔŋ ki jo ŋaah chaa nan yaan biɛn.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: Woo Korazin yaab; woo Befisada yaab, ni ye ligaril a toon gbingbemi nan, nan bi tun Taya nin Sidonn a timin, ke naah nan tun man ni timin nan ke, naah bi nan bi jiɛn bi toonbir weem, ki pug bi ba nfatama.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Amaa N bei ni le ke wan lig upii nin kɔlisa ke u pii tun iwanchɔgin toon nin upii nmɔn u sufu ni na.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Le utaal nan nun, le ikpen gbee, le nfaam daa, ki piɛr iniin nmɔn a dikakpin, le iniin nmɔn lu ki weeh weeh.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: Iyaayoo ki miin u bɔɔnɔɔb ma be kuŋaluŋ a poolin nnyumin, ki bol nan fɔg nin limɔgil a doola, le kunyungbɔŋ jɔɔ bi ki yeŋ nan bu-n bi nan too nfaam le.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: Waa na yin jɔɔ limɔkpeli bee ki mɔri. Uba mu kaa li gbir u kukɔl isanjal bu.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Ki miin Kenann a tiŋ a pii uba mu nan be liyagil nmɔn paam, ki dan Yisa ban ki nan kuun paab ke, Uyudaan, Defid a yaabil, sam kunyunyunbaag ni. Kinaanbiig le be n bisa ni, ki mugir u takpem.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: Amaa ni ye a numbil kaa ŋan ke, a gbanann biɛn yin be libunbɔnlin le. Mambu ni ye nwonween man be a ni-n nan bɔŋ ke, ni yin ye libunbɔnl takpenl le!.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: nan bu-n naa ye nim li na yin pagi, amaa ni Baa a Naaŋin li na yin che ni pag.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Le Josef fii ki ta kibig nmɔn nin ki na ki jir Iziral a tingbɔŋin.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Tiŋɔgir jɔɔ ifal, le nnɔɔm man laan paab nan mu jɔɔ titar, amaa Uninfosaal a Big kaa jɔɔ waah li dɔɔn nan yaan ni.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Amaa n bei ni le, lidaal lan wee Uwonbɔr na yin ji bininfob tibɔr nan, ni tudaril na li jo Taya nin Sidonn yaab yaal.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Ni ye a nɔɔjiim a numbil yin che a tun tibir ke, a logir mi gaba, a lann a gbanann bɔgil liba ke, ni sɔ nin bin nan ta a gbanann biɛn ki gab nŋmi-n.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Yisa kaah nan pag imɔbon nan ki diɛ n-yonn man ni, le u wɔɔnim nmɔn nan gar kuninfogbaluŋin.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Le Yisa nan nyan kikojamɔɔŋ ki jiir limɔgilin, ki choon nnyum bu ki chaa bi ban.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: Waa na yin gen ligbingbaanl lan gen ki gbiɛ gbaari, ki mu kaa ji na yin junn fila kan kaa gaan nbamɔm nan a ŋmiim ki gbaari. Hali nin waah na li che tibɔbamɔnn nkan nyaŋim.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Le Yisa kaa jiin tiba; amaa le u bɔɔnɔɔb bei u ke, Ŋɔɔr upii nan na, nan bu-n u nɔɔ ti bɔɔn le kuun.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Uba kaa li fir ki tun ki tenn biyudam bilee. U yin nan uba le ki sin jiɛ uba; bee u baga yin be uba ni le kaa sin gar uba paami. Naa li fir jiɛn Uwonbɔr nin iligi.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Uninfo na li gbii u na big bu le bin ku u; le baam na li gbii bi buwaab bu; le nbuwaab na li kɔnn bi dam ki ku bi.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Amaa waah nan gbir ke Akeliɛsi jin nnaam ki ka u baa Herɔd a naangbann bu Judiya a tingbɔŋin ni, le bijawaam choo u nin un kun nin yaan, le Uwonbɔr ji kpaan u tidindɔgin ni ke un jir ki li chaa Galilii a tingbɔŋin na.

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔru uba mu nan bei u ke, N Yudaau, che n chinn jo tan pii n baa wee.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Ke nim Kapeniɛm yaab, Uwonbɔr na li don ni papaab baa? Aai. Ni yin jiir kitakpiin tiŋ le, ni ye ligaril a toon gbingbemi nan, nan bi tun Sodom a tiŋ ke naah tun man ni tiŋ nan ke, Sodom a tiŋ la bi li be.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Le ni ye a nɔɔjiim le che a tun tibir ke, a ga mi gaba. A lann a gbanann bɔgil liba ke, ni sɔ nin a gbanann biɛn nan kɔɔ nŋmi-n.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: nan bu-n u nan wɔɔn bi waah jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔmin le, kaa nann bi yaa ninfob ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin ni.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔb kaah nan kan u choon nnyum bu ki chaan bi ban ni, le bijawaam choo bi takpem, le bi ke, Ni ye utakpiir le. Le ki sin yigin bijawaam a yigin ni.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: U yinl bu le ibol biɛn nan na li jɔɔ tama.

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Bi nan tum Iziral yaab ban nann ipee ki borin a binba ban le.

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: Mambu n bei ni le, ni laa che ni mugir ni limiɛfol bu, nin naah li ji nan bee naah li nyu nan bu; bee ni gbanann ni, naah li yee nan bu. Limiɛfol kaa ŋan jon tijiiraa, le tigbanann kaa ŋan jon tiwann yeewann naa?.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Uninfo biɛn na li nan ni. Naah nɔɔ min bu; amaa wan nan fir sir nin nton nan a joorim ke, u li kan nŋmarim.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: Le u nan jon ki tan kal kitiŋ kan wee bi yi ki ke, Nazarif nan. Le ni nan gbeen nan wee bilinlem nan sɔb ki ke, Bi na li yin u Nazarif a ninfou.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Li nɔɔm ni wee ki che bitakpiib li pii bi taba.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Amaa n bei ni le, tibɔr a ji daal, ni tudaril na li jo Sodom yaab yaal.

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: Bi nan gbaa len ke, Wan yii u poo ke, un sei u lipiiyiil a gbɔŋ.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Amaa libol nmɔn le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni che ni sufu li pɔɔu! Ni ye min le. Ni laa san bijawaami.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Le upii nmɔn dan ki nan gbaan u nintogil ki bei u ke, Uyudaan terim ni.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Lig nnɔɔm man laan paab nan; baa bu; bi mu kaa ji chiɛ ki ŋaan tinaapɔrin ni, amaa le ni Baa wan be paab nan jin bi. Naa jɔɔ tinyɔɔr ki jon nnɔɔm maa?.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Bi nan mugir ni kitiŋ kiba ni ke, ni nya niin wee ki lɔŋ ki tɔu; N bei ni ibamɔn le, naa li fir gɔnn Juu yaab a tim nan biɛn ni, le Uninfosaal a Big ndan.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Le bi nan ŋmagin uja uba le nnaanbiim be u ni, ki ŋa u kubirug, ki ji ŋa u ujɔɔn, le Yisa che u kan kutebug, ki nin gaa ki sin pag.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Le Yisa kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin, le u bɔɔnɔɔbin biɛn nɔɔ kɔɔ.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa len ke, N Baa wan ye paab nin tum a Yudaau nan, n pooriŋ, nan bu-n a ta a bɔrin ki woon bilandam nin bibiɛndam, amaa le ki kpiir ti ki wɔɔn nbuwaab finfim;.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Amaa N bei ni le ke wan biɛn yii u poo wee naa ye u tun iwanchɔgin toon le ke, u ma che u tun iwanchɔgin toon, ni ye wan kɔɔn upii nmɔn ke u tɔɔl tun iwanchɔgin toon na.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ni ŋma a binbem mug u wee u li fir fɔbig u miɛfolaa?.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Le u jiin ke, Naa ŋan nin bin ta nbuwaab a jiir ki sei bi bɔyi.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Wan tinn kaa jon wan wɔɔnn ni wi, uyom mu kaa jon u yudaau wi.

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Le Piita bei u ke, Uyudaan, ni sin ye fin ke, beim ni wee mu tɔɔn nnyum nan bu ki dan a ban.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Le ni nan ŋa bininfobin ligaril, le bi baan tab ke, U ma ye Defid a yaabilin naa.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Le ki miin ntafapɔɔrim fii ki daar limɔgil n-yaan, le kunyungbɔŋ daar ki yugir kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ n-yaa poolin ki nan ban min gbee, amaa le Yisa ma dɔ ki jiɛn.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Nn, n Baa ŋaah ban nin tun man le yen.

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: Ni ji gbir ke bi nan bei bininfob iyoo yan nan jon ni ke, Ni laa poog tipoor wee kaa nɔɔ ti, amaa ni li ŋmag tipoor tan wee ni poo sei Uyudaan ni na.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: Babu wee ni mugir ni ba tiwann yeekaar buu? Lig tikpentunn tan be nkpenn nan a puum, taa tun ni, ki mu kaa loo tiwann yeekaar ki tenn tiba.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Le upii nmɔn jiin ke, Nn, Uyudaan, ni sin ye ibamɔn le; amaa ibɔ yan dɔ bi dam a jijibol kaa lan ijibul yan lur bi dam a nintogil a piimi.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: Ni ŋan nin wan tinn a binbem li be ke wan wɔɔnn u a binbemin, le uyom mu li be ke u yudaau kaah be man ni. Ni ye bi nan yin uniindaan Beelizebul ke bi nin yin yin u niin yaab ke baa?.

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Le u jiin Piita ke, Li chaan. Le Piita nyan kuŋaluŋ a poolin ki tɔɔn n nyumin bu, ki piin ki choon ki too Yisa.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Amaa Farasii yaabin ma kaah gbir man nmɔn, le bi len ke Nnaanbiim a bɔru le sei u npɔɔn nmɔn, ke u li nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin dan ki nan fiin u, ki bei u ke, Uyudaan, fee ti ki jaau ke nnyum yin ji ti le.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Tɔ, n Baa le wɔɔm tibɔr nan biɛn. Uba kaa nyi min Bija-n kaah ye wan ni see n Baa-n; le uba mu kaa nyi n Baa-n ni, see min, wan ye u bija nan, ke maah kpiir ki wɔɔn ban wee n ban ni.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Amaa N bei ni le, Ni laa poo kolibi: Laa poo paabi, nan bu-n ni ye Uwonbɔr a naam a bol le;.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Amaa n bei ni le ke, ubɔr Solomɔnn kaah le nan jɔɔ u wan yɔɔ ŋan tan biɛn ni, waa nan nyi ki yee u wan yeeg wee ki ŋan ke tikpentunn a puumi.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Upii, a jɔɔ yada takpem! Nan wee a ban nan tun na. Libol nmɔn le u bisa nmɔn kan kutebug.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Mambu ni laa san bi bijawaami. Niba li kaa be ki pii wee kaa na li piiri, bee ki woo wee kaa na li nya npaan nin ni.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: Amaa waah nan kan nfaam daarin, le bijawaam choo u, le u piin ki niɛg nnyumin, le ki yigin ki ke, Uyudaan, feem jaau.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Le Yisa biɛ baah tiɛr man, ki bei bi ke, Ni ye nnaam nba a ninfob kɔn nin tab ke, bi naam nmɔn yin gab yɔli le. Le kitiŋ kiba a ninfob bee iniin iba a ninfob kɔn nin tab ke iniin nmɔn kaa yin siri.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Nim yada parim dam nan, ba ŋa wee ni san bijawaamaa? Le ki fii ki tiɛn nfaam nin kunyungbɔŋ n bu ke min ŋmin na, le mi sin ŋmin soon.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Ni dan n ban ni, nim ban biɛn tu itug yan kpiɛg ki bag nan, wee n sei ni lifooril.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: bee ni poo kutingbɔŋ nan ni, nan bu-n ki ye Uwonbɔr a taai a sisibol le; ni mu laa ji len ke ni poo Jerusalami, nan bu-n ki ye Libɔrgbingbemil a tiŋ le;.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Ke ni ye Uwonbɔr li ŋaŋir tikpentunn tan be dinan, wee bin won nyɔɔ ti nŋmi nan, le kaa li fir yeen nim maa? Nim ban a yada par nan!.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Le Yisa nyan niin ki jon Galilii a mɔgilin yaan. Le ki don lijɔɔl a paab ki tan kal.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Nan wee n bei ni libunbɔnlin nan, amaa ni pag nween nin na, nan mu wee n lɔɔg ki bei ni nan, ni mɔɔn npaan nin na.

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Libol nmɔn le Yisa tann u nɔɔ ki choo u. Le ki ke, Fin yada pariŋ daan nan, ba ŋa wee a biɛn naa.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Ni ye Sintani ŋɔɔr Sintani ki nyan ke, ni wɔɔn ke u kɔn ki bɔgir u ba le nmɔn; ni yin ŋa nii wee u naam nmɔn n siraa?.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Le ni gar bi, le bi baan tab ke, Uninfo nan ye ninfo bolin baa? Wee nfaam nin kunyungbɔŋ chee u mɔb baa.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Ta n daagin ki paan a ba bu, wee ki tinn n ban, nan bu-n n ye jiininbadaan le ki jɔɔ jiininba n sufu ni, wee ni naam nkan lifooril.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: A mu laa ji len ke a poo a yuli, nan bu-n ŋaa li fir che kuyukpobig kuba npenn bee ku nbɔnn ni.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Mambu, ni laa che ni mugir ni wee ni tiɛr ke, N yin ji baa, bee N yin nyu baa, bee N yin yee baa ni.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋ dan u ban ki ŋmag nwɔbim nin bijɔɔm nin ndiɛm nin nbirim nin ban bu iwiɛn a booli biɛn, ki nan bil u nintogil; le u che bi biɛn kan kutebug.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Ni laa san ban li fir ku ni gbanann, wee kaa sin li fir ku ni naamin ni, amaa ni li san Uwonbɔr wan li fir ku ni gbanann nin ni naam biɛn nŋmi-n ni na.

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Baah nan jir ki don kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin n-yonn man ni, le nfaam jiɛn kaa ji daari.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Ke ni ye ke Beelizebul le tenn nim npɔɔn ke n nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin ke, ke nim mu, ŋma sei ni ninfobin nsan ke bi li nyan nnaanbiim maa? Wee bin ŋa ni bɔjib.

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Yisa kaah nan lɔŋ Galilii a mɔgilin ki foo Gadara a tiŋin nan, le bijab bilee ban nan gbee nin nnaanbiim nan nyan tikarin ki kenn u. Bi nan bir pam ni bu che ni nan pɔɔ nin uba n fir ki nɔɔ nsan man chaa tikarin nmɔn yaan.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Nan bu-n N nɔɔm kaa pɔɔ le n tugil kaa kpiɛgi.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Fin le li che a Nn ye Nn bee a Aai li ye Aai, nan biɛn kpiɛ ni nan bu ke, ni nyan ulinlaril ban le.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: Ban kaa ye Juu yaab n ti mugir bi potiɛl ni niɛ nan biɛn bu le; amaa ni Baa Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan pii nyi ke ni ban ni niɛ biɛn.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: Kuninfogbaluŋin kaah kan nbirim pag, ndiɛm choon nbamɔmin, nwɔbim mu choon, bijɔɔm a num ligir wee bi ga-n, le ni gar bi le bi pag ki don Iziral yaab a Wonbɔru a yinl.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Naa ye bi kɔɔr ijakangbal ilee koobu lee? Bi ni uba kaa li lu tum wee naa ye ni Baa a bami.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Le ban be kuŋaluŋin a poolin ni dan ki nan gbaan ki jiɛn Yisa ki ke, Ibamɔn a sin ye Uwonbɔr a Bija le.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Amaa ni ye n nyann nnaanbiim, Uwonbɔr a Naaŋ bu ke, Uwonbɔr a naamin foon ni ban na.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Le bi tiɛn ki ke, Fin Uwonbɔr a bija, tim nin fin babu, a dan a nan jin ti fala wee iyoo kaa la foo.

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: Ni gbir baah nan len ke, Ni ye uba fɔɔr a numbim ke, a mu fɔɔr u yaam ki jiin na; bee ni ye uba faaŋ wee a nyil fonn ke a mu n fonn u nyinl ki jiin na.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Amaa ni chinn ban U naamin nin U buchinn binbemin na, le u li ta ni niɛ biɛn ki sei ni.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Le Yisa yin u bɔɔnɔɔb ki bei bi ke, Kunyunyunbaag jɔɔm nin bininfob nan, nan bu-n iweehn itaa sɔ le bi be n ban do, kaa jɔɔ tijiir bin ji. Maa ban bin kun nin nkomi, ke bi ta li fig nsann.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Hali U nyi ni yukper tan be ni yuli bu-n a kanl.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Bee jigin, uba li ŋa niin ki fir kɔɔ lijawagidil a niin ki tan su u wan yɔɔr ki ta nya, see ke u yaa daan chinn ki lol u iŋmin, le ki nin fir kɔɔ ki tan su u wan yɔɔr.

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Baah nan don limɔgil a dool n-yonn man ni, le bi nan foo Genesareti.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Idool takpem nan si ngbaan ki mal nin yaan ki ji.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Amaa min bei ni le ke, ni laa yii titoonbirdaan ni. Ni ye uba peb a nɔɔjiim paam a tapag ke, a labir a gam paam ki sei u wee un peb.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Mambu, ni laa che ke won a bɔr mugir ni, lidaal biɛn jɔɔ libɔmug kaar lan kpaan li le.

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔb baan u ke, La ban wee ti yin kan tijiir kukunkɔɔg nan yaan ki sei kuninfogbaluŋ nan wee tin foo bi biɛn naa.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Mambu, ni laa san bijawaami, ni jɔɔ tinyɔɔr ki jon ijakangbal takpema.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Wan kaa si n bɔɔn ke, u kɔnim le; le wan mu kaa lagin bininfob ki tenn min ke, u ŋɔɔ bi jaar le.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: Kitiŋ nmɔn a ninfob kaah nan biɛ Yisa nan, le bi mɔɔn ki sei kitiŋ nmɔn a tingbaai biɛn, le bi lagin bibaarib ki ta bi dan u ban.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Le nnaanbiim nmɔn nan balin Yisa ki ke, Ni ye a nyan ti ke a jaa ti wee ti tan kɔɔ idool nan yaan.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Ni ye uba taaŋ ki jon bibɔjib ban ke un ga a daala ke, a cheeu wee un gbaar a tanl a kinkenl ki li jɔɔ chaau.

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Le u baan ke, Ni jɔɔ boroboro kon i ŋaa? Le bi jiin u ke, Ilile nin njanbum wama.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Mambu le n bei ni nan, Uwonbɔr li jiɛn per bininfob a bir nin ban sugir Uwonbɔr nan biɛn; amaa wan sugir Kinaaŋ nan ke, Uwonbɔr kaa li nyi jiɛn per u daan ni.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Wan nyan u ba npaan ni ke u ye n bɔɔnɔɔru ke, n mu na li nyan u npaan ni, n Baa wan be paab nan ban.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: le ki nan balin u ke un che bibaarib nan n maa u daala takpeŋ a mɔgbeli. Le ban biɛn nan maa u kan kutebug.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni li chaau! Le bi nyan bijab n-yaan, ki jon tan kɔɔ idool nmɔn yaan; le idool nmɔn biɛn nan guun ki jiir limɔgil takpenl n-yaan le nnyum jin bi.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Ni ye uba mugiŋ ki ke a tu u tugil lan kpiɛg ki choon maal u ba ke, fin tum li ki choon ke maal ilee paam ki sei wa ki laa ŋulin ni.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Le u bei bininfobin ke bin kalim tum.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Wan pag tibɔbir ki tenn Uninfosaal a Big ke, Uwonbɔr li jiɛn per u; amaa wan pag tibɔbir ki tenn Kasi a Naaŋin ke, Uwonbɔr kaa li nyi jiɛn per u daan dulinya nan yaan ni, bee dulinya kan na yin dan nin yaan ni.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Wan mu ke waa ye n bɔɔnɔɔru bininfobin ke, n mu na li ke maa nyi wi, n Baa wan be paab nan ban.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Le ban guu idool nmɔn fii ki guun jon kitiŋ n-yaan, ki tan mɔɔn nan biɛn tun, nin nnaanbiim kaah nyan bijab n-yaan man.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Ni ye uba miɛŋ niba ke, a sei mu, wan mu ji ban a pen u niba ke a pen mu ki laa mɔn wi.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Le ki yuur boroboro kon ilile nin njanbumin, ki sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog i bu, le ki war war sei u bɔɔnɔɔb, le bi ta tol bininfob nmɔn.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: Ni laa tiɛr ke n ta nposɔɔn le dan dulinya nan yaab bu, maa dan nin nposɔɔn ni, amaa n dan nin takoobi le.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Ŋa kisiig ki li ŋan na wee ki loon mu li ŋan, bee ŋa kisiig ki li bira wee ki loon mu li bir, nan bu-n kisiig a looi yin le bi biɛr ki.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Lig, le ki miin kitiŋ n-yaa ninfob biɛn fii ki guun too Yisa. Baah nan kan u n-yonn man ni, le bi balin u ke un nya bi tingbɔŋin na.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: Ni gbir baa nan len ke, Ni li jiɛ ni kunkɔɔn taba, ki li nan ni dataama.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Le bininfobin biɛn jin ki gbee ki gur; le u bɔɔnɔɔb lagin tijiir tan gur nmɔn, le ti gbee tikachar tilile.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: Nan bu-n n dan n nan tugin uja nin u baa le, kibisiɛg nin ki na, upii nin u yaabisa.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Nim iwaalab a buwaab nan, naah ye tininfobir man nan, ni yin ŋa nii wee tibɔŋann nfir nya ni mɔnn naa? Nan bu-n nan wee uninfo tiɛr u sufu ni, ni ma nya u mɔnn.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Amaa n bei ni le, ni li jiɛ ni dataama, ki li miɛ Nween ki tenn ban mugir ni nan na.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: Bijab ban nan jin tijiir nmɔn nan ye turina le, bipiib nin nbuwaab a kanl kaa nan kpiɛ niin ni.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Uninfo a dataag le na yin ye u niin yaab.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Uninfo ŋan le tun itoon ŋan, nan bu-n u sufu ni ŋan le, le kuninfobirug mu tun titoonbir, nan bu-n u sufu bir le.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: wee ni fir ŋa ni Baa wan be paab nan a buwaab, nan bu-n u ma che nween wonn tininfobir nin bininfoŋam bu, ki ji che utaal nu bibuchim nin ban kaa ye bi buchim a timin.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa jaa bininfob nmɔn, le ki kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin ki jon Magadann a tingbɔŋin.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Wan ban u baa bee u na ki jon min ke, waa kpaan nin un ŋa n bɔɔnɔɔru wi, wan mu ji ban u bija bee u bisa ki jon min ke, waa kpaan nin un ŋa n bɔɔnɔɔru wi;.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: Amaa n bei ni le, tibɔr a ji daal Uwonbɔr na li ji uninfo biɛn tibɔr nin baah pag tibɔyɔɔr tan biɛn nin bu.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Ni ye ni jiɛ ban jiɛ ni a binba ke, baa a nyɔɔr wee ni yin kan naa? Naa ye man le bilanpogaab mu ŋaan naa?.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: le wan kaa ta u kum a dapunpunl ki nɔɔm ke, waa kpaan nin un ŋa n bɔɔnɔɔru wi.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: Nan bu-n uninfo biɛn a pipagir le na yin nyan u bee ki ku u.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Le ni ye ni poor ni nabum a binba ke, itoon ŋan ilaba wee ni tun ki jon ni tab baa? Naa ye man le ban kaa jɔɔ Nween ni mu ŋaan naa?.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Wan jɔɔ u miɛfol ki debin ni, na li lann li, amaa wan mu kaa debin u miɛfol n bu ke u na li kan li.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Mambu, ni li be nbamɔm ke ni paab a Baa kaah be man ni.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Le ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nin Farasii yaab bei Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn, ti ban ti kan ligaril a toon le a ban.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Wan gaa ni kuchɔŋ ke, u yaa daan gaa min le, wan mu gaa min kuchɔŋ ke u gaa n Baa Uwonbɔr wan tum min le.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Le u jiin bi ke, Daandaan nan a ninfob ban ye titoonbirdam kaa si bogil liba nan ban bin kan ligaril a toon le; amaa n mu kaa yin ŋa ligaril a toonl liba ki wɔɔn bi; ulinlenn Jona a magilin a binba.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Wan gaa u linlenn kuchɔŋ waah ye linlenn nin bu ke, u na li gaa bilinlem a yɔr. Wan gaa ubuchin kuchɔŋ waah ye ubuchin nin bu ke, u na li gaa ubuchin a yɔr.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Nan bu-n Jona kaah nan be ujan takpen a poolin ki tan kpaa iweehn itaa nin tinyiɛr titaa nan, man le Uninfosaal a Big mu na li be tum iweehn itaa nin tinyiɛr ti taa.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Wan minn nnyunsɔɔm wama ki sei n bɔɔnɔɔb ban waar nan a ni uba, waah ye n bɔɔnɔɔru win bu ke, n bei ni ibamɔn le, waa li nyi ki lann u yɔri.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Tibɔr a ji daal, Ninefa a tiŋ a ninfob na li sir ki galin daandaan nan a ninfob, nan bu-n Jona kaah nan mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔri ki sei bi n ni bi nan jiɛn bi bira; liga, uba be ki jon Jona u.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Tibɔr a ji daal Itopiya a Piibɔru na li nyann nnɔɔgan paam ki fii sir paab ki galin daandaan nan a ninfob, nin waah nan nyan kitiŋ a diɛtarig ki dan ke un nan gbir ubɔr Solomɔnn a lamgɔrikaam a bɔr, liga, uba be ki jon Solomɔnn na.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Ni ye kinaanbiig nan nyan uninfo ni ke, ki choon yelin le ki jin kukunkɔɔg a paab ki ban lifooril a bol, le kaa ga.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: Mambu, u ti nan bei u ba le ke, N li jir iniin yan wee n nan nyan ni. U jir ki tan foo, ki miin iniin nin dɔ yɔli wee bi tigin ki mann ni ke.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: ki yin jir nya le ki jo ki tan yin u nnaanbiim tab milile, ban bir ki jon u, ki ta bi dan, le bin kɔɔ ki lagin be, uninfo nmɔn a binbem li bir takpem ki jo waah nan chinn be man ni. Ni ma na yin dan daandaan nan a ninfobir nan bu.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Yisa kaah la nan be pag ki tenn kuninfogbaluŋin n-yonn man ni, le ki miin u na nin u watiib dan ki nan si nkaafiin, le ki ban bin kan u.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Le uba bei u ke, Lig a na nin a watiib si nkaafiin ki ban bin kaŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Le Yisa baan uninfo wan bei win ke, Ŋma ye n naa? Ke ŋmam ye n watiib baa.

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Le u tann u nɔɔ ki wɔɔn u bɔɔnɔɔb ki len ke, Lig n na nin n watiib sɔ.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Nan bu-n wan tun n Baa wan be paab nan a bam ke, u ma ye n waal nin n ninsa nin n na.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Yisa kaah pag imɔbon nan ki diɛ n-yonn man ni, le u nyan Galilii ki jon Judiya a yagil lan be Jɔdann a kpenn a doolin paam.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋ nɔɔ u bɔɔn, le u teb bi niin.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Le Farasii yaab biba dan u ban ki ban bin mag u lig. Le ki baan ke, Nsiim chee ke uja nbin yii u poo niba bu waa.

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Le u jiin ke, Naa karin Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ kaah wɔɔn man ni naa? Ke npiinimin na, Uwonbɔr nan naan uja nin upii le.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: ki len ke, Ni bu uja li nya ki jiɛn u baa nin u na wee ki kɔɔn u poo, wee bininfob bilee nmɔn ŋa gbanann tibaa?.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Tɔ, ni ye man ke, baa ji ye ninfob bilee, amaa bi nin ye uninfo uba le. Ni bu ban wee Uwonbɔr ta bi ki lagin tabin, uninfosaal li nyi nin un choo bi ki bɔgir.

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Le bi baan ke, Ba ŋaa, Moziz nan jaa ke uja li fir sei u poo lipiiyiil a gbɔŋ wee ki nin yii waa.

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni tukangbir bu le Moziz nan jaa yaag ke ni li yiin ni piiba. Amaa naa nan ye man npiinimin ni.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, wan yii u poo wee naa ye u tun iwanchɔgin toon le, wee ki kɔɔn upii uba ke, uja nmɔn tun iwanchɔgin toon na.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin bei u ke, Ni ye man yin be uja nin u poo a sisiigin ke, uja kaa kɔɔn u pii ke ni sɔ.

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke Naa ye uninfo biɛn le li fir nɔɔ nwɔɔm nmɔn ni, see ban wee Uwonbɔr ta sei bin a binba le li fir ŋmag.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Nan bu-n nbinben nba le che bijab biba kaa kɔɔn bipiibi, bi ti li fir maa biba ki miin baa ye bijabi, biba mu, bininfob li ti faar bi, le biba mu paab a naamin bu le bin yii lipiikɔɔnl, amaa wan li fir ki ŋmag nwɔɔnim nmɔn ke u li ŋmaga.

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Tɔ, le bininfob biba ŋmag bi buwaab ki dan Yisa ban, ke un ta u nɔɔ ki paan bi bu ki miɛ Uwonbɔr ki sei bi, amaa le u bɔɔnɔɔb fuun bininfobin bu.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni che nbuwaab nmɔn n dan n ban ni ki laa ŋɔɔ bi, nan bu-n bi bosu le kpaan nin paab a naamin.

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Le n-yonn man wee u ta u nɔɔ ki ma ma bi-n le u fɔr niin.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Le unaachipɔɔn uba dan Yisa ban ki nan baan u ke, Wan wɔɔn, litoonl ŋanl li la baa wee n li tun ki nan kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm maa.

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ba ŋa wee a baanim itoon ŋan buu? Uninfo uba kpeŋileŋ le ye wan ŋan, amaa ni ye ke a ban limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm ke, a li ŋmag Uwonbɔr a siimin na.

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Le unaachipɔɔn nin ji baan Yisa ke Nsiim mi la baa? Le u jiin ke, Laa ku uninfo, laa tun iwanchɔgin toon ni, laa su, laa ji tibɔkar a siɛra.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: li tenn a baa nin a na jilimau, ki li jiɛ a kunkɔɔn tab ke ŋaah jiɛ a ba man ni.

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Iyaayoo le u bɔɔnɔɔb dan u ban ki nan baan ke, Ŋma ye wan jon bi biɛn paab a naamin naa.

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Le unaachipɔɔn nin bei u ke, N pii nɔɔ nsiim nan biɛn le. Ni la ba ji guraa?.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Le u yin kibig kiba ki nan siin bi sisiigin.

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Ni ye a ban a binbem li be chasi ke, a li chaa ki tan kɔɔr ŋaah jɔɔ nan biɛn, ki ta iligin ki sei faladama, wee ki li jɔɔ njɔɔm Uwonbɔr a niin, ki li nɔɔm ni.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: le ki bei bi ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, wan kaa kpenn u binbem wee ki be ke kibigin ke, waa li kɔɔ paab a naamin yaan ni.

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Unaachipɔɔn nmɔn kaah gbir man n-yonn man ni, le u fɔr nin npoobiin, nan bu-n u jɔɔmin nan yab le takpem.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Wan jiin u ba ki be ke kibig nan ke, u ma na yin ye wan jon ni biɛn paab a naamin.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Le Yisa bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, ni li pɔɔ nin ujɔɔndaan nfir kɔɔ paab a naamin.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Wan gaa kibig nan a binbem nan a bosu n yinl bu ke, u gaa min le.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: N ji bei ni le ke, ni pɔɔ pam nin ujɔɔndaan nnan kɔɔ paab a naamin ki jon alakoomi kaah li kuur siɛrig a bolin ki nyau.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Amaa nbuwaab ban ŋa min yada nan, wan che bi ni uba tun tibir ke, ni bi li ŋan bin lol linaal jɔn u niɛn ki ta u jaa limɔgilin wee nnyum nji u.

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔb kaah nan gbir man ni, ni nan gar bi le takpem, le bi piin ki baan tab ke, Ni ye man ke ŋma ji li fir kan nŋmarimaa.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Woo, nton li be dulinya nan a ninfob ban lar bitab wee bi tun titoonbir nan bu. Libiginl ma li dan, amaa ni li bir ki sei uninfo wan bu wee libiginl nmɔn nɔɔ ki lur bininfob bu-n.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Le Yisa lig bi chimm le ki bei bi ke, Uninfosaal ban ni pɔɔ, amaa Uwonbɔr ma ban niwann biɛn kaa pɔɔ.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Ni ye a nɔɔ bee a taal li yin che a tun tibir ke, a gaa mi ki tugira. Ni ye a jɔɔ nɔɔn nba bee taal liba wee ki kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm ke, ni jon nin a li jɔɔ inɔɔ ilee bee itaa ilee wee Uwonbɔr n nan taaŋ ki ŋa nŋmi man kaa jɔɔ njunimin yaan na.

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Piita bei u ke, Lig tim bu ke ti jiɛn niwann biɛn le ki nɔɔŋ. Ti nyɔɔr na yin ye baa.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: Ni ye a numbil li yin che a tun tibir ke, a logir mi gaba. Ni ye a jɔɔ numbil liba ki kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin ke, ni jon nin a li jɔɔ numbil ilee wee ki tan kɔɔ nŋmi-n na.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, n-yonn man wee Uninfosaal a Big nan kal u naangbann bu nin npɔɔn takpen, dulinya pɔŋ n-yaan ke, nim ban ye n bɔɔnɔɔb nan mu na yin kal tinaangbann piig nin tilee bu le, ki li ji Iziral yaab piig nin bilee a bɔr.

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ni li nyi man ki laa yin nbuwaab nan a ni uba yɔli; nan bu-n n bei ni le, bi malakam ban be paab nan be n Baa wan be paabin a nintogil le iyoo biɛn.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Le wan biɛn jiɛn u niin, bee u kpetiib, bee u watiib, bee u baa, bee u na, bee u buwaab, bee u kpaann, n yinl bu ke, u na li kan nibooli kobig, ki kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmi.

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Ni ye uninfo jɔɔ ipee kobig, wee upee uba nan bor ke, ni tiɛr ke u yin ŋa nii? Waa li jiɛn ipee piwiɛ nin iwiɛn ligungonl a paab le bi ŋma, le ki jo ki tan ban wan borin naa?.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Amaa bininfob takpem ban chinn nintogil daandaan nan, bi na li joor bɔɔn, bininfob takpem ban mu joor bɔɔn na li jo nintogil.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: Ni ye u kan u n-yonn man ke, n bei ni ibamɔn le, u li jɔɔ npopeen upee borikaa wan wee u kan ni bu, ki jon ipee piwiɛ nin iwiɛ yan kaa borin na.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Man mu le ni Baa Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan kaa ban u buwaab nan a ni uba n bori.

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Ni ye a na a big tun niba ki biiŋ ke, a jo u ban n-yonn man wee u la be u binba nan, ki tan pag ki wɔɔn u bir; wee ni ye ke u biɛ u bir ke, ni ji mann ki ŋa n nabum le nmɔn.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Amaa ni ye ke waa chee un gbir seiŋ ke, a yin uninfo uba bee bininfob bilee ki kpiɛ a ba bu, wee nin gbeen nsiim man wɔɔn ke ni ye bininfob bilee bee bita nan gbir tibɔr nmɔn ki kpiɛ a bu ke bi ma ye a siɛra dam.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: Ni ye ke waa chee un gbir ki sei bi ke, a wɔɔn biwonjiɛm a laguŋ; ni ye ke waa ji chee un gbir ki sei biwonjiɛm a laguŋin ke, ni nin jiɛn waah wee u li be ke naah bi li be nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab man ni, bee ulanpogaarin.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, nan wee ni lol dulinya nan yaan nin, man le lol paab a naamin; nan mu wee ni choo lor jaa dulinya nan yaan nin, ni ma mu li choo lor jaa paab a naamin.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: N ji bei ni le, ni ninfob bilee kpaag bimɔb dulinya nan yaan ki miɛ n Baa Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan niba bu ke, u li tun naah ban man ni ki sei ni.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Nan bu-n nan yaan wee bininfob bilee bee bita lagin tab n yinl bu-n, n mu li be bi sisiigin nin yaan.

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Le Piita dan Yisa ban ki nan baan u ke, Uyudaan, ni ye n nabig tun ki galim ke, itaa iŋaa wee n yin jiɛn per waa? Itaa ililee.

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Aai, naa ye itaa ilile a binba, amaa ni ye itaa pilile nin ilile a kerim milile le.

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: Mambu, paab a naamin nann ubɔr wan nan lagin u toontum ke un kan bi ligin a pɔnlin le.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: Waah piin nkam n-yonn man ni, le bi ta uja uba kɔɔn, ki miin u jɔɔ ubɔr a pɔnl salima a bɔɔlim turpiig.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Ki mu kaa li fir kan ki yɔ; le ubɔrin ke bin kɔɔr uja nmɔn nin u poo nin u buwaab nin waah jɔɔ nan biɛn, ki nan yɔ lipɔnl nmɔn.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Le ujan nan gbaan ubɔr n-yaa nintogil nin ibalin, ki ke N baa, n li yɔ li biɛn; fin mɔra.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Le kunyunyunbaag choo ubɔrin, le u jiɛn lipɔnlin ki per u ke u li chaau.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: Ujan ma kaah nyann n-yonn man ni, le ki kenn u toontun kpiɛg wan jɔɔ u pɔnl kobig a ligibim, le u bib u ki choo u niɛn, ki ke, Yɔ n pɔnlin ni daandaan nan.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Le u kpiɛgin mu gbaan u nintogil ki balin u, ki bei u ke Mɔra, ke n li yɔ.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Amaa le waa chee, le ki che ke bi ta u ŋa kuloondiigin, ke see u nan yɔn u pɔnlin le u ni nya u.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: U toontun kpiɛtiibin kaah kan man ni, le ni bii bi sufu takpem, le bi jir ki tan bei ubɔrin nan biɛn tun.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Le ubɔrin tun ke bin yin uja nmɔn ni, le ki ke, Fin npookparim daan nan, ŋaah bi balim wee n jiɛn ŋaah jɔɔ n pɔnl lan ni biɛn ki per ŋin.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Ŋaa bi li fir san a kpiɛg nmɔn kunyunyunbaag ke maah saŋ man ni naa.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Ubɔr nmɔn a ŋuul kaah fii ujan bu-n, le u ta u ki tan ŋa kuloondiigin ki ke u li be niin na ki tan kpaa lidaal man u na li yɔ lipɔnlin biɛn.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Naa jiɛn per ni tab nin ni sufu biɛn ke, man le n Baa wan be paab nan yin ŋa ni mu.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Nan bu-n Paab a naam nann ukpadaan wan nan chinn nyan njakpanyiɛn ki ban bininfob un ta wee bin tan ko u kpaabin le.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: Le u chee ke u li yɔ bi lidaal a yɔr, le ki che bi jon u kpaabin.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Naah nan nan foo tikur tiwiɛ le ukpadaan nmɔn fii ki jon kinyaŋin, ki tan kan biba ki miin bi si le kaa tun niba.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni li chaa n kpaabin ki ta li kou, wee n yɔ ni nan kpaan ni.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Le bi jon. Le ni nan nan foo tikur piig nin tilee a yoo, le u ji jir ki tan ŋa man, le tikur titaa a yonn nan foo, le u ji jir ki tan ŋa man.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Le ni nan nan foo tikur tiŋmu le u jir ki tan kan biba ki miin bi si le, le u baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni si do yɔli liwonmunl kaa tun ni baa.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Le bi jiin u ke, Nan bu-n uba kaah kaa ta ti itoon nin bu. Le u bei bi ke, Ni mu li chaa n kpaabin ki ta li kou.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Naah nan bɔŋ n-yonn man ni, le ukpadaan bei u toontum a ninkpelu ke, Li chaa ki tan yin bikpaabin ni, ki nan yɔ ba. A yin yɔ bi ke, a tan piin ki li yɔ ban yɔɔn foon ni, ki tan joor ban bi chinn foon ni.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Le u toontunn ninkpelu n piin ki yɔ ban bi yɔɔn foon ni lidaal a yɔr.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Tɔ, naah nan foo un yɔ ban bi chinn foon njakpanyiɛn nan bi yɔrin, le bi tiɛr ke bi yaa yɔr yin yab le, amaa le u yɔn bi mu lidaal a yɔr jigin.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Baah gaa bi ligi diɛ n-yonn man le bi piin ki ŋulin ki sugir ukpadaan ni.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: ki ke, Lig, ban yɔɔn ki dan tikur tiŋmu kon wama le, amaa le tim ban chinn ki dan ki jin fala njakpanyiɛn ni, wee nween fa ti takpem nan, le tim nin bi ma nan lagin gaa tiyɔr tiba jigin naa.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Le ukpadaan ni jiin bi ni uba ke, N jɔ pela, maa ŋaliŋi. Nan bu-n taa bi jin lidaal a yɔr le a chee?.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Mambu yuurim a yɔr ki li chaau. N yin sei ban nan joor ki dan nan ke maah seiŋ man ni le.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: Naa ŋan nin n ta n ligi ki tun maah ban man naa? Bee ni jɔɔ lipiiponl le maah kaa ŋuuŋin buu.

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Ni ma bu, ban joor bɔɔn na li liin nsan, le ban mu lii nsan na li joor bɔɔn.

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Yisa kaah chaa do Jerusalam n-yonn man ni, le ki yin u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee-n ki nyan ngbaan.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: Ni lig man, ti chaa do Jerusalam le nan, le bi tan li ta Uninfosaal a Big ki ŋa kumanug a yudam nin ban wɔɔnn nsiim a nɔɔn, le bi li ke u kpaan nin nkuma.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: bi na li ta u sei ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan le bin laa u, ki boo u inaalab, ki kpaa u lidapunpunl bu, amaa iweehn itaa daal u li fii nkumin.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Zebedi a poo ta u buwaab bilee Jemisi nin Jɔnn ki dan Yisa ban ki nan gbaan u nintogil, ki miɛ u ke un ter u.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Le Yisa baan ke, A ban baa? Le u jiin ke, Che nbuwaab nan uba n nan kal a nɔɔjiim paam wee uba mu n kal a nɔɔgam paam a naamin.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Yisa nan bei bi ke, Naa nyi naah miɛ nan ni. Ni li fir nyu kiyiinyɔɔg kan wee n yin nyu nan naa? Le bi jiin ke, Ti li fir.

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Tɔ ni sin li fir nyu n yiinyɔɔg nmɔn yaan, naa ye min le yin gann wan li kal n nɔɔjiim bee n nɔɔgami, nan bu-n kikaabol nmɔn pii ŋa sili ki guu n Baa kaah nan yin ta ban ki kaan niin n le.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb piig nan kaah gbir man n-yonn man ni, le bi ŋuui fii bi bilee nmɔn bu.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Le Yisa lagin bi u ban, ki bei bi ke, Ni tɔɔl nyi ke ban ŋmag ban kaa ye Juu yaab mugir bi le, le bi yudam jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm ki ŋmag bi.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: amaa nim ma bu ke ni laa ŋaan man ni sisiigin ni. Ni ye uba ban tininkper ni ni ke, ni ŋan nin un kpann utoontunn ki li tun ki tenn ni biɛn.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: Ni laa che man be ni ban ni, le wan ban u liin ni sam ke un kpann ni toontunnu.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Ke Uninfosaal a Big kaah be man nan, waa dan ke bininfob ntun sei wi, amaa u dan ke u nan tun sei bininfob le, ki jin ta u miɛl ki daa bininfob takpem.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Tɔ, Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb kaah nan nan chaa nyann Jeriko n-yonn man ni, le kuninfogbaluŋ nan nɔɔ Yisa a bɔɔn.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Le bijɔɔm bilee ka nsan gbaan ki gbir ke Yisa chaan un nan nɔɔ niin le. Le bi piin ki yigin papaab ke, Uyudaan, wan ye Defid a yaabil nan, san ti kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
