local: Le kuninfogbaluŋin tiɛn bi bu ke bin ŋmin na. Le bi mann yigin papaab ke, Uyudaan, Defid a yaabil san ti kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Le Yisa sir ki yin bi ki nan baan bi ke, Ni ban n tun ba ki sei naa.

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Le bi jiin ke, Uyudaan ti ban a ligir ti numbili le.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Iweehn iloob daal a bɔɔn, le Yisa ta Piita, nin Jemisi nin u na a big Jɔnn, ki don lijɔɔl lan kper a paab bi binba.

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Le kunyunyunbaag choo Yisa le u ma ma bi numbili, libol nmɔn le bi ligir, le ki nin nɔɔ u.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Iyaayoo le bi kan waah kpenn. Le u num paam kpenn ki nyagin ke nween ni, le u wann yee kaar penn ki nyagin.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Le bi kan Moziz nin Elaja degirin nba bu, ki si ki pag nin Yisa.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Le Piita bei u ke, Uyudaan, ni ŋan nin ti li be doo nan. Ni ye a bi li chee ke, n bi yin fa tinanbonn titaa le, kuba li ye a yaag, kuba mu li ye Moziz yaag, wee kuba mu li ye Elaja yaag.

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Piita a mɔb kaa la nan lu tumi, le kitalangbɔŋ peŋ nyan paab ki nan degin bi bu, le bi gbir likukɔl liba pag kitilangbɔŋin, ki ke, N bija sɔ, wan wee n num gbee, ni pelim u bɔr man.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb nan kaah gbir likukɔl nmɔn ni, le bijawaam choo bi, le bi lu gbaan tum.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Amaa le Yisa dan ki nan ma ma bi, le ki bei bi ke, Ni fiim man. Ni laa san bijawaami.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Le bi yaar ki lig u, kaa ji kan uba bi ban ni see Yisa a binba.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Baah nyan lijɔɔl a paab ki jiirin n-yonn man ni, le Yisa kpaan bi ke, Ni laa bei uba naah kan nan ni, ki tan kpaa n-yonn man wee Uninfosaal a Big na li fii nkumin.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn baan u ke, Ba ŋa wee ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin ke Elaja li yin chinn dan naa.

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni sin ye ibamɔn le ke Elaja nmɔn le yin chinn dan ki nan mann niwann biɛn.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Amaa n bei ni le, Elaja pii nan dan na, amaa baa nan biɛ wi, ki ŋa u yɔli yɔli baah ban man. Man le bi yin ŋa Uninfosaal a Big mu, ki che un ji fala.

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin nan biɛ ke u pag Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunful nan a bɔr le.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Baah nan foo kuninfogbaluŋin ban ni; le uja uba dan ki nan gbaan Yisa a nintogil. Le ki bei u ke.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: Uyudaan, san n bija nan kunyunyunbaag ni, u lur likpikpiil le, li mugir u pam ki che ke u lur nŋmi-n nin nnyumin iyoo biɛn.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: N ta u dan a bɔɔnɔɔb ban le bi walin bin teb u gur.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Le Yisa jiin baan bi ke, Ni daandaan nan a ninfob a kaa jɔɔ yada ki jɔɔ kuŋmaruŋ nan, n yin ŋa nii ki be ni ban ki yuura? Isaŋ iŋaa wee n li kan limɔril ki sei naa? Ta kibig nmɔn dan do ni.

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Le Yisa fuun kinaanbiigin bu, le ki nyan ki jiɛn kibigin. Libol nmɔn le u kan kutebug.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb dan u ban bi binba, ki nan baan u ke, Ba ŋa wee tim kaa bi fir nyan kinaanbiig nmɔn naa.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni yada kaah waarin bu. N bei ni le, ni ye ke ni ŋa yada hali wee yada-n foo nnyiɛnbim ke, ni li fir bei lijɔɔl ke, Fii ki tan lu nan buu, li sin bi yin fii le; niba kaa bi li pɔɔ ki sei ni.

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Tɔ, Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb kaah nan lagin tab Galilii a tingbɔŋin n-yonn man ni, le u bei bi ke, Bi na li ta Uninfosaal a Big ki ŋubin bininfob a nɔɔn.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: Bi na li ku u, le iweehn itaa daal u li fii nkumin. Le ni bii u bɔɔnɔɔbin a sufu.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb kaah foo Kapeniɛm n-yonn man ni, le iwonjiɛn niin n-yaa ligaab dan ki nan baan Simɔnn Piita ke, Wan wɔɔn ni nan, u yɔ iwonjiɛn niin a ligin naa.

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Le Piita jiin ke, Nn, u yɔ. Le ki jon kɔɔ ndiin. Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa chinn ki piin tibɔr nmɔn ki baan ke, Simɔnn, a tiɛr nii? Bi la ba ti yɔ lampo ki tenn dulinya nan a bɔrtiib baa? Bi tɔɔl a bol a ninfob bee bicham maa.

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Le u jiin ke, Bicham. Le Yisa bei u ke, Tɔ, ni ye man ke, ni wɔɔn ke bi yaa bol a ninfob a nɔɔ kaa be niin ni.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Amaa taah kaa ban ti bii bi sufu-n bu, li chaa limɔgilin ki tan ta njanpiin ki jaau. Ujan wan wee a chinn choo-n, a yaar u mɔb, a li kan ilig yan li fir yɔ min nin fin bu wee a ta yɔu.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Baah nan tan mal Jerusalam ki be Befeg a tiŋ kan tab lijɔɔl lan wee bi yi li ke Olif a Siigi a Jɔɔlin , le Yisa tun u bɔɔnɔɔbin bilee.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: ki bei bi ke, Ni li chaa kitinbig kan be ni nintogil nan yaan. Libol nmɔn ni li kan baah lol kiboŋ nin ki big, wee ni lor i ki ta i dan n ban ni.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Ni ye uba baan ni niba ke, ni bei u ke, Uyudaau le ban bi, u li che ni ta bi dan.

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Ni niɛ nan tun ke nin gbeen nan wee ulinlenn Azaya nan len ke:.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: Bei Zayɔnn a tiŋ yaab ke, Ni lig man, ni bɔru li chaan ni ban ni. U ye wan jiin u ba le, ki jig kiboŋ kan ye kibonbig.

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb sin jon ki tan tun ke waah bei bi man ni.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: ki ŋmagin kiboŋ nin ki bigin, ki ta bi wan yeekaar ki paan i bɔɔn le Yisa jig ki.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Le bininfob biba ta bi wan yɔɔr yar nsan bu, le biba mu gar isiig a bon nin tisitugir ki yar nsan bu.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋ kan lii u samin nin ban nan paa u bɔɔn nin nan yigin ki len ke: Jilima daan, Ubɔr Defid a yaabil! Tikaar be wan chaan Uyudaan a yinl bu-n! A ji ye jilima daan le paab.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Waah nan tan kɔɔ Jerusalam n-yonn man ni, le kitiŋ nmɔn a ninfob biɛn fii, ki baan tab ke, Ŋma sɔɔ.

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋin jiin ke Yisa wan ye ulinlenn ki nyan Nazarif kan be Galilii a tingbɔŋin ni.

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Yisa nan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin ki ŋɔɔr ban kɔɔr nin ban daag nin biɛn nyan, ki labir biligkpenib a teebulim nin bilangekɔɔrib a jali chigin.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: Le ki bei bi ke, Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, U ma Uwonbɔr len ke, N niin ye liwonmiɛl a niin le, nim le ta i kpann ban feen a kikaabol. .

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Le bijɔɔm nin nwɔbim dan u ban iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn yaan, le u che bi biɛn kan kutebug.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Amaa kumanug a yudam nin ban wɔɔnn nsiimin kaah kan ligaril a toon yan wee u tun ni ki ji gbir nbuwaab kaah yigin iwonjiɛn niin ke, Jilima daan, Defid a yaabil, le ni fiin bi ŋuui.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Le bi baan u ke, A gbir nbuwaabin kaah len man waa? Le Yisa jiin bi ke, Nn, amaa naa nyi karin Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin naa? Ni sɔb ke, Nbuwaab nin nbiliɛm a mɔnn le a yin kan kupag kan ŋan. .

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Le u nyan ki jiɛn bi Jerusalam ki jon Betani a tiŋin ki tan dɔ.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Waah nan chaa jir kitiŋin yaan ni le nkom choo u.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Le u kan bikinkam kaah si nsan gbaan, le u jon bi ban, amaa kaa kan niba see tifasɔnn a binba. Le u bei kisiigin ke, Naah nyan dinan ki chaa, ŋaa ji li lo iloon ni. Libol nmɔn le kisiigin koor.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah kan man n-yonn man ni, le ni gar bi, le bi baan tab ke, Ni ŋa nii wee bikinkam nan koor man weemaa.

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, ni ye ni ŋa Uwonbɔr yada nin ibamɔn wee kaa biɛn u ke, ni biɛn yin tun le ke maah tun kisiig nan man nan, amaa naa ye man a binba le ni yin tun ni, ni ye ni bei lijɔɔl takpenl nan gbaa ke lin fii ki tan lu limɔgil nan yaan ke, li li fii.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Nan biɛn wee ni li miɛ Uwonbɔr ni bu, ni ye ni ŋa yada ke, ni sin li kan.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Yisa kaah nan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin a loob n-yaan ki wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr n-yonn man ni, le kumanug a yudam nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninkpeeb dan u ban ki nan baan u ke, Ŋma a ninkper a pɔɔm wee a jɔɔ tun itoon nan biɛn naa? Ke ŋma seiŋ tininkper a pɔɔm nmɔn naa.

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N mu jɔɔ nbinbaan ki li baan ni, ni ye ni fir jiim ke n mu li wɔɔn ni maah kan tininkper a pɔɔm nmɔn nan yaan ki jɔɔ tun.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Jɔnn a nyun fulin nan nyan laa? Li nan nyan paab a naamin le bee bininfob ban naa? Le bi jir gbeer tab ke, Ni ye ti ke ni nan nyan paab le ke, u li baan ti ke ba ŋa wee taa ŋa u yadaa?.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Le ti mu ji san kuninfogbaluŋin bijawaam nin ti len ke, bininfob le sei u npɔɔn nmɔn, nan bu-n bininfob biɛn ŋa yada ke Jɔnn sin nan ye ulinlenn le.

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Le bi bei Yisa ke, Taa nyi. Le Yisa mu bei bi ke, Tɔ, n mu kaa yin wɔɔn ni wan a ninkper a pɔɔm wee n jɔɔ tun nan ni.

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Le Yisa baan bi ke, Ni tiɛr niin? Uja uba le nan be ki jɔɔ u buwaab bilee, le ki nan bei uninkpelin ke, N big, li chaa bukpaabin ki tan ko dinan.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Le u bija-n bei u ke, Maa li fir jo. Ni bɔɔn le u nan kpenn u potiɛl le ki jon.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Le u baa jon wan ye kibigin ban ki tan bei u ke waah bi bei uninkpelin man ni. Le u ma chee ke u li jo, le kaa sin jon ni.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Nbuwaab bilee nan, ŋma tun u baa a bamaa? Le bi jiin ke, Uninkpelin. Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, bilanpogaab nin ban tun iwanjaab toon na li chinn kɔɔ ni Uwonbɔr a niin a naam n-yaan.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Nan bu-n Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin nan dan ni ban ki wɔɔn ni nbuchin binben na, amaa le naa ŋa u yada, le bilanpogaab nin ban tun iwanjaab toon ni ŋa u yada. Nin naah kan nan biɛn tun nan a bɔɔn, naa kpenn ni binbem ki ŋa u yada.

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Le Yisa ji bei bi ke, Ni pelim libɔŋal ki kpiɛ. Ukpaar uba le nan be ki kon u dikpaab, le ki maa ligonl ki gɔɔn bukpaabin, le ki maa libinbinl fufɔbil ke bi li kaa li paab ki guu bukpaab nmɔn, le ki mann lidiboopal bukpaabin a poolin. Le ki ta bukpaabin ŋa bitoontum a nɔɔn ke bi li liga, le ki yaar jon kitiŋ kiba.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Le idi a chiɛm nan foo, le ukpadaan nin tun u toontum ke bi li chaa bikpaabin ban ki tan gaa u dii nni.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Le bikpaabin tan choo u toontumin, ki boo uba ki ku uba le ki sin gabir wan gurin mu itangbag.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Le u ji mann tun bitoontum biba, le bi yab ki jon ban bi chinn nin, le bikpaabin ji ŋa bi baah bi ŋa bi miɛnin man ni.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Ni joorim le u nin tun u tɔɔl a bija, le ki len ke, N nyi ke bi li sei n bija nan ma jilima.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Amaa bikpaabin kaah nan nan lig kan u bija-n chaan nin, le bi bei tab ke, Ufaajirin sɔ. Ni che ti ku u, wee u faalin kpann ti yaal.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Bi nan choo u le ki nyan bukpaabin a gbaam ki tan ku u.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Mambu ukpadaan nmɔn nan dan ke, u na yin ŋa bikpaab nan niin.

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Le bi jiin ke, U na li ku titoonbirdam nan nkumbirim ki diɛ, le ki ta u kpaab nmɔn ki sei bitoontum biba, ban li chee ki chiɛ u dii nmɔn ki tenn u.

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Le Yisa bei ke, Naa la nyi karin naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin man ni naa? Ni sɔb ke: Kudaag kan wee bitanmaab yii ki nan, ki ma na yin ye kijaantelig ŋaŋ, Uwonbɔr le ŋa man, le ni ye ligaril ti ban.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Ni ma bu, n bei ni le nan, Uwonbɔr na li gaa u naam ni ban ki sei bininfob ban li lo mi looi.

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Amaa wan lu kudaag nan bu ke, u yin burbur le, kudaag nmɔn mu lu wan bu ke, ki yin mar u le.

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Kumanug a ninkpeeb nin Farasii yaab kaah gbir libɔŋal nan diɛ, le bi biɛ ke Yisa ga libɔŋalin ki den bi ma le.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Le bi nan ban bin choo u, le ki sin san kuninfogbaluŋin bijawaam, bininfob biɛn ŋa yada ke u sin ye ulinlenn le.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Le Yisa pag ki bei kuninfogbaluŋin nin u bɔɔnɔɔb ke:.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: Ban wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a siim nin Farasii yaabin si Moziz a taalin le.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyan iwonjiɛn niin ki chaa-n, le u bɔɔnɔɔb dan u ban ki nan wɔɔn u iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn a dir.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Ni kpaa le nin ni li cheel ki nɔɔ baah wɔɔn man nin biɛn, amaa wee ki laa gaa bi binbemi, nan bu-n baa tun baah wɔɔn man nin ni.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ni kan ni niɛ nan biɛn waa? N bei ni ibamɔn le, bi na li tir ni biɛn ki jaa tum, le litangbagil liba kaa na yin gur ki paa li kpiɛg bu.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Bi boŋ itug yan kpiɛg le ki paag bininfob a bɔsipar bu, amaa bitɔɔl kaa li chee ki ta bi nɔɔbil ki ter uba wee un tu.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Yisa kaah nan jon tan ka lijɔɔl lan wee bi yi li Olif a Siigi a Jɔɔl a paab nan, le u bɔɔnɔɔb dan u ban, bi binba, ki nan baan u ke, Ibayoo wee ni niɛ nan biɛn na yin ŋaa? Ba nan yin ye a dam nmɔn a siɛra nin dulinya nan a diɛm paam maa? Bei ti ni.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Bi tun man biɛn ki ban bininfob le nkan: Lig baah ta Uwonbɔr a siim ki sɔb tabin bi yutun bu, ki ŋa bi daalam a mɔgbai fɔg ki ban bininfobin kan;.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni li nyi ki laa che uba n lar ni.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Bi jiɛ jilima a kikaabol le tijiir a chiigin, ki jiɛ bi li kaa bininkpeeb a kikaabol tiwonjiɛn dirin;.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Nan bu-n bininfob takpem na li ta n yinl ki dan ki ke bi ma ye min Masiya nan, ki lar bininfob takpem.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: ki jiɛ bininfob li poor bi nnyamin, ki li yi bi ban wɔɔnn.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ni na li gbir bitɔb a fur nin bitɔb a bɔr, amaa ni laa jeeli, ni kpaa le ke ni niɛ nan biɛn n tun, amaa naa wɔɔn ke dulinya a diɛm le yen ni.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Amaa nim kaa ye bin yin ni ban wɔɔnn ni. Nan bu-n wan wɔɔnn ni ye uba le kpeŋileŋ le ni mu ye nnabum nin tab.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Libol nin libol na li kɔn nin tab, le nnaam miba a ninfob na li kɔn nin nnaam mitɔ a ninfob. Le nkom nin kutingbɔŋ a damim na li be iyag iba ni.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Ni laa yi uba ke ni Baa, dulinya nan yaan ni, nan bu-n ni jɔɔ Baa uba le kpeŋileŋ, le u be paab.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn na yin nann nmarwiɛn a piinim le.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Bee bin yin ni ban wɔɔnn ni, nan bu-n ni jɔɔ wan wɔɔnn uba le kpeŋileŋ u ma ye Masiya.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: N-yonn nmɔn bi na li choo ni ki jin ni fala, ki ku ni, le kutingbɔŋ biɛn nan yin nan ni le naah nɔɔ min bu.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Wan ye wan jon ni biɛn ni sisiigin ni, un ŋaam ni biɛn a toontunnu.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, bininfob takpem na li ta n bɔr gab, ki gbii tab bu, ki ji nan tab.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Wan don uba ke, Uwonbɔr na li jiin u, amaa wan jiin uba ke, Uwonbɔr na li don u.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: le tibɔkar a linlem na li nyann ki nan lar bininfob takpem.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Woo nim ban wɔɔnn nsiimin nin Farasii yaab, bilinlarib nan! Ni loon paab a naam a timansanmɔnn ke bininfobin laa kɔɔ. Ni baantɔɔl kaa kɔɔ, kaa ji che ban ban bin kɔɔ-n kɔɔ.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Nan bu-n npooton kaah na li yabig dulinya nan yaan ni bu, bininfob takpem a jiɛm kaa na li yabi.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Woo nim ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nin Farasii yaab nan, nim le ye bilinlarib nmɔn, nan bu-n ni don imɔg ki lagir ntim ki ban wan li kpenn u binbem ki li nɔɔ ni bɔɔn le, le ni ye ni nan kan uba wee u nɔɔ ni ke, ni che u labir Sintani a ŋmiimin a big, le u binbem nmann ki kpiɛ nfim milee ki jo ni yaal.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Amaa wan nan sir kenken ki tan kpaa ndiɛm ke u li kan feegab.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Woo, nim bijɔɔm ban da ni jɔɔn tab nan! Ni ke, Ni ye uninfo poo iwonjiɛn niin ke, naa ye niba, amaa ni ye u poo salima kan be iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn ke, see ke u gbeen u mɔpool nmɔn.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Le bi na li mɔɔn paab a naam nan a bɔŋann ilog biɛn nin ki ŋa siɛra ki sei ibol biɛn wee dulinya a diɛmin nin foon.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Nim bijɔɔm ban jɔr nan! Ni la jon ni tɔɔ: Salima nan bee iwonjiɛn niin yan che salima ŋa kasi nnaa?.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Ni na li kan liwanbiril lan wee ulinlenn Daniɛl nan pag li bɔrin , nan wee Uwonbɔr kɔn kaah si iwonjiɛn niin ni.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Le ni ji len ke, Ni ye uninfo poo kumanug a binbinlin ke naa ye niba amaa ni ye u poo ipiin yan wee bi ta paan libinbinlin bu ke, see ke u gbeen u mɔsiinl nmɔn.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn nan foo ke, che ban be Judiya a tiŋ poolin ni, bin guun ki do lijɔɔl a paaba.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Nim bijɔɔm nan, ni la jon ni tɔɔ? Ipiin nan bee libinbinl lan che ipiin nan be kasi nnaa?.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Wan mu nan be u niin a mɔɔpinl a paab ke, u laa ji jiirin ki nan kɔɔ u diin ke un yuur ni ba.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Mambu, ni ye uba poo kumanug a binbinl nan ke, u poo na ma nin nan biɛn paa li bu-n le.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Wan mu nan be u kpaabin ke, u laa ji jir iniin ke un tan yuur u daala.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Ni ye ke uba mu poo iwonjiɛn niin ke, u poo iwonjiɛn niin nin wan be i yaan ni le.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Naa na li ŋan ki sei bipiipoodam nin ban ŋmag nbuwaabi!.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Le wan poo paab a naam ke, u poo Uwonbɔr a naangbɔŋ le, nin wan ka ki bu-n.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Ni miɛ Uwonbɔr wee u laa che ni guun kufaag a yonn ni bee likpakɔɔl daali.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Woo nim ban wɔɔnn nsiim nin Farasii yaab nan, nim bilinlarib nan! Ni nyan piig a poolin, ni kpinn tan ye: Ikanjo, buŋu nin inil le, amaa le ki le jiɛn nan kpaa talar nsiim a poolin, ni ma ye ibamɔn, linyunyunbasanl, nin yada a binbem. Ni bi ye ni li tun nan joor nan le, wee ki laa sin yii nan chinn nmɔn ni.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Nan bu-n n-yonn nmɔn bininfob na li kan limugil takpem, Uwonbɔr kaah naan dulinya nan ki nan kpaa dinan, baa laa kan limugil nmɔn a boli, limugil nmɔn a bosu mu kaa ji li nyi dan ni bɔɔn bu.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Nim bijɔɔm ban da ni jɔɔn tab nan! Ni lor kusubig kan poo nnyunyukaam bu-n le ki gab amaa le ki sin niɛ alakoomi nin nnyum.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Naa ye Uwonbɔr tɔɔl li nan war limugil lan chaan nmɔn a weehn ke, uba kaa yin ŋmari. U ninfogannkaab a nyunyunbasanl bu le u li war limugil nmɔn a weehn.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Woo, nim ban wɔɔnn nsiim nin Farasii yaab nan, nim bilinlarib nan! Ni nir tiyiinyɔr nin nsam a bɔɔn le, amaa le ni poolin gbeen nin nnaayigim nin kujaŋuuŋ.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, uba nan bei ni ke, Ni lig, Masiya sɔ! Bee, U be do! Ni laa ŋa ni yada.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Nim Farasii yaab ban ye bijɔɔm nan, ni chinn nir tiyiinyɔr nin nsam a poolin na, ke ti bɔɔn mu li nya.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Nan bu-n tibɔkar a masiyam nin tibɔkar a linlem na li degirin ki ŋa ligaril a siɛra ki tun ligaril a toon gbingbemi, ki lar Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaabin, ni ye ni bi li fir ke.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Woo, nim ban wɔɔnn nsiim nin Farasii yaab nan, nim bilinlarib nan! Ni nann tikar tan peen, wee ti paab ŋan nbamɔmin le, amaa wee ti poolin gbee nin ikpab nin tijaginn nin le.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: A kan na, n chinn bei ni le, ke ni yonn kaa laa foo.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Man le bininfob lig ni, ni num paam nann bibuchim le amaa le ni poolin gbee nin kularug nin npooton.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Mambu, ni ye bi nan ke, Lig, u be kukunkɔɔg bu ke, ni laa nya; bee, Lig, u be ndiin ke, ni laa ŋa ni yada.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Woo, nim ban wɔɔnn nsiim nin Farasii yaab nan, nim bilinlarib nan! Ni maa bilinlem a kar, ki ŋaŋir bibuchim a kar ki siig.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Nan bu-n Uninfosaal a Bigin a dam na yin be le ke utaal kaah ti nyagir liwonpɔɔnl wee bi ga ki liwonlul man ni.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: Amaa le ki sin len ke, Tim nan bi be ti yaajam a yonn ke, taa nan bi yin tun baah nan tun man ki ku bilinlemin ni.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Nan yaan wee kuwankpiiŋ be-n, nin yaan le tijuur lagin.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Ni ma bu ni chee ke ni ye ban nan ku bilinlemin a yaabili le.

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Libol lan wee bi na yin kan limugilin a bɔɔn, Nween li bɔŋ, le uŋmal kaa ji li ween ni, le iŋmabil na li nya kitilangbɔŋin ki lunn, le nan be paab biɛn na li dam dam.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Mambu, ni gbaar ni yaajam kaah nan piin ki tun titoonbir tan nin na!.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: N-yonn nmɔn, bi na li kan Uninfosaal a Big a siɛra kitilangbɔŋin, le kutingbɔŋ nan biɛn na li fabin, ki kan Uninfosaal a Big kaah be kitilangbɔŋin ki chaan nin npɔɔn nin kupag takpem.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Nim iwaalab nan! Nim iwaalab a buwaab nan! Ni yin ŋa nii wee ki ŋmar nŋmi-n a kɔɔm maa?.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Kunaatuŋ na li mɔɔ, le u li tun u malakam le bin jo ki tan lagin u ninfogannkaab ban be paab a logi biɛn.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Mambu n li tun bilinlem nin bilandam nin ban wɔɔnn ni ban, amaa ni na li ku bibam ki kpaa bi tɔ ki boo bibam mu tiwonjiɛndirin, le ki ji ŋɔɔ bi taai ki nya kitiŋ kiba ki jo ki tɔ ni.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Ni ta bikinkam ki tinn nlam: Ni ti nan kan busub nmɔn bunn tifar ke, ni biɛ ke likpanyigil mala.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Le baah nan ku bibuchim ban biɛn nan a tudaril be ni bu, naah nyan bininfob buchim Ebiɛl a yonn ki tan kpaa Berekiya a bija Zakariya, wan wee bi nan ku iwonjiɛn niin nin kumanug a binbinlin a sisiigin ni.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Ni ye man le, ni nan kan ni niɛ nan biɛn ke, ni li nyi ke ni mala, ki be ni timansanmɔnn.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, ni niɛ nan biɛn li dan daandaan nan a ninfo bu.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, daandaan nan a ninfob kaa yin kpo diɛ le ni niɛ nan biɛn ni tun.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Jerusalam yaab, Jerusalam yaab, nim ban ku bilinlem, ki ji gabir u tooi ban wee u tun bi ni ban ni itangbagin, nfim miŋaa wee n gbaar n lagin ni ke ukool kaah ti lagin u buwaab ki ŋa u jiɛgin man nin, amaa le naa chee.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Paab nin tum na li jo, amaa n bɔr kaa jɔɔ tin jo.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Iweehn ilee kaah nan jon a bɔɔn, le Yisa ji nan jir Kapeniɛm; le bi gbir ke u jir iniin na.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Ni lig man, Uwonbɔr fɔr ki jiɛn na, ni wonjiɛn niin dɔ le yɔli.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Uba kaa nyi ki jiin lidaal bee n-yonn nmɔn ni, hali malakam ban be paab bee Bija-n tɔɔl kaa nyi, amaa Baa-n a binba le nyi.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Le bininfob takpem lagin, hali ke npaan wama kaa ji nan be iniin nmɔn yaan ni, wee uninfo n fir ki nɔɔ foo ntimansanmɔnn ni; le u mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki tenn bi.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Nan bu-n n bei ni le, naah nyan dinan ki chaa, naa ji li nyi kam mi ki tan kpaa lidaal lan wee ni na li len ke, Tikaar be wan chaan Uwonbɔr a yinl bu nan. .

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Nowa a yonn kaah nan ye man ni, Uninfosaal a Big mu a dam a yonn na yin ye man le.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Bininfob biba le nan ta tigbanann kpiin daan uba ki dan Yisa ban; bi nan ye bininfob bina le.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Nan bu-n n-yonn man wee nnyum kaa la nan jin dulinya-n nan, bininfob nan ji le ki nyu, ki kɔɔn bipiib, ki jɔɔ bi buwaab ki tenn bijab, ki nan kpaa lidaal lan wee Nowa nan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ takpoŋ n-yaan;.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Baa nan fir ki foon u ban ni, bininfobin kaah nan yab nbu, le bi nan ga logir kudiigin a yul bu, le ki ta tigbanann kpiin daan nin pɔbin likinkenlin ki jiin tan bil Yisa a nintogil.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: bininfob nmɔn kaa le la nan biɛ niwann nan tun ni, ki tan kpaa lidaal lan wee kunyungbeeŋ nan dan ki nan jin bi biɛn nin. Ni ji na yin ye man le, lidaal lan wee Uninfosaal a Big na yin dan nin.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan baah ŋa u yada man nin, le u nan bei tigbanann kpiindaan n ke, N big, n jiɛn a bir ki periŋa.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Bijab bilee na li bɔɔ bukpaabin, le n na li ta uba ki jiɛn wan gurin.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn ban nan wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a siimin a ni bi ba nan ka niin le ki tug bi sufu ni ke.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Bipiib bilee na li bɔɔ naan linaal, le n na li ta uba ki jiɛn wan gurin.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: Ba ŋaa wee uja nan pag man naa? U sugir Uwonbɔr le. Ŋma li fir ki jiɛn uninfo a bir ki per waa, kaa ye Uwonbɔr a binbaa.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Mambu, ni li nyi man, nan bu-n naa nyi lidaal lan wee ni Yudaau yin dan ni.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Lipotiɛl nan kaah nan be bi sufu ni n-yonn man nin, libol nmɔn Yisa nan nyi u sufu ni baah tug man, le ki baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni tiɛr man naa?.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Amaa ni biɛm: Ni ye u niin daan bi nyi n-yonn man wee unaayig yin dan u niin ke, waa bi li chee dɔɔn ni, u bi yin ka le ki guu, waa yin che unaayig nmɔn n nan kɔɔ ki su wi.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Maah bei ubaarin ke, N jiɛn u bir ki pir nbu bee maah li bei u ke, Fiim ki yuur a wan dɔɔn wan ki li chaa nnaa, tibɔr a kerim milee nan ti la ba pɔɔ?.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Mambu, ni ŋan nin ni mu ŋa sili, nan bu-n Uninfosaal a Big mu na yin dan n-yonn man wee naa yin nyi u bu-n le.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: Amaa ni li nyi ke Uninfosaal a Big jɔɔ npɔɔn kutingbɔŋ nan yaan, ki li fir jiɛn bininfob a bir ki per bi. Le ki nan gbiɛ lig wan a gbanann kpen ni ki bei u ke.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: Utoontunn u la ba ye yada daan bee ulandaan wan wee u ninkpelu ta u ki ŋa utoontunn ninkpel ke u li lig u toontunn tab, ki li tenn bi tijiir nin naah kpaan bin ji iyoo yan ni naa?.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: N beiŋ le fiim ki yuur a wan dɔɔn waŋ ki li chaa kun a niin na.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Ni ye u ninkpelu nan kunn ki miin u tun man ke, npopeen yin ye utoontunn nmɔn yaal le.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Libol nmɔn le u fii ki yuur u wan dɔɔn waŋ ki nyan bi biɛn nan a numbimin. Le ni gar bi takpem; le bi nan pag ki don Uwonbɔr a yinl ki ke Taa la nyi kan ligaril nmɔn a kpiɛgi.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, u yin ta u jɔɔm nbiɛn le ki ŋa utoontunn nmɔn a nɔɔn, ke u li liga.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa ji nan jir Galilii a mɔgil n-yaa gbaam, le bininfob takpem nan lagin u ban, le u wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Amaa ni ye utoontunn nmɔn a pool to wee u tiɛr ke, N ninkpelu kaa yin kunn weem.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: Waah nan choon ki chaa-n, le ki tan kan uja uba le u yinl ye Liifai, wan nan ye Alifiu a bigin, ki miin u be u toon yin le, lampogaab a nanbonn. Le Yisa bei u ke, Fii ki li nɔɔm ni. Le u fii ki nɔɔ u.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: ki piin ki boo u toontunn tab, wee u ma nin bidanyub lagin ki nyu ki ji ke.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Yisa kaah nan be ji kujɔɔg a jiir Liifai a niin nin, le bilanpogaab nin tibirdam lagin ki ka nin Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb ki ji tijiir. Bi nan yab le takpem ki nɔɔ u.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: U ninkpelu nmɔn na yin kunn lidaal lan wee u jaa u tama le, nin n-yonn man wee waa yin nyi.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Le ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim, ki nan ye Farasii yaabin, bi nan kan Yisa kaah lagin ki ka ji tijiir nin tibirdam nin bilanpogaab; le bi baan u bɔɔnɔɔb n ke, Ba ŋa wee ni lagin ki ka ji nin bininfob nan a bosuu.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: U na li gaa u ki por por, ki ta u ŋa bilinlarib a binbebol, nan yaan wee bininfob na li mɔr ki fabin ki ŋma bi nyin.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Yisa kaah gbir man diɛ le ki bei bi ke, Ban pɔɔ kaa ban utebiri, see ban bu-n. Maa dan ke n nan yin bibuchimi, amaa n dan ke n nan yin tibirdam le.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunful nan a bɔɔnɔɔb nin Farasii yaab nan lol bimɔb; le bininfob biba dan ki nan baan Yisa ke, Jɔnn a bɔɔnɔɔb nin Farasii yaab a bɔɔnɔɔb lolig imɔɔŋ le, ba ŋa wee a yaa bɔɔnɔɔbin ma kaa lolig bi mɔɔŋi yaa.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Le Yisa baan bi ke, Lipiichanil be ke, bicham li fir ki lol bimɔb iyoo yan wee upiidaan nan la be bi ban nan naa? Baa li firi, lipiichanil daan nan kaah la be bi ban nan, baa li fir ki lol bimɔbi.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Amaa iyoo chaan le bi na li nyan lipiichanil daan nin bi ban, iyaayoo le bi ni li lol imɔɔŋ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Uba kaa li chee ki taa litan pɔnl ki legin litan kpegili, ni ye u ŋa man ke litan pɔnlin yin chiɛ liwan kpegilin le, le libonl nmɔn n mann ki kpiɛ.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Man mu le uba kaa li fir ta ndapɔm ki ŋuun libookpegilin ni, ni ye u ŋa man wee ndapɔmin nan mɔɔ ke, mi yin pɔɔ liboolin le, le ndaam nmɔn n wur yɔli, le liboolin mu n bii. Mambu, bi jɔɔ ndapɔm ki ŋu liboopɔnlin le.

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Juu yaab a kpakɔɔl liba daal, le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nɔɔ bukpaab biba ni. Baah nan nɔɔ ki chaa nmɔn le u bɔɔnɔɔb n genn iyoo ki pur ŋma iwankal.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Le Farasii yaab n baan u ke, Ba ŋa wee bi tun nan wee ti kpakɔɔl daal kaa chee ke ti li tun naa.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Naa la nyi ki karin Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin Defid kaah nan tun man n-yonn man wee nkom nan choo uma nin uninfob wee baa nan jɔɔ tijiirin naa?.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: Abiata wan nan ye kumanug a yudaau a yonn, wee Defid nan kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a niin ki tan yuur boroboro wan wee bi ta sei Uwonbɔr nan ki ŋman le ki ŋa sei u ninfob le bi mu ŋman. Nsiim mu kaa nan chee ke uba n ŋman boroboro nmɔn ni see Uwonbɔr a minmanib a binba.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Le Yisa nan jir kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin, ki miin uja uba be niin le u nɔɔnkpaŋ kpo.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Le Yisa nan mann ki bei bi ke, Uninfosaal a terim bu le Uwonbɔr siin likpakɔɔl daal nmɔn, amaa naa ye likpakɔɔl daal bu le u nan naan uninfosaali.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Le bininfob biba nan be niin ki lig Yisa, n jee u li che kinɔɔnkpankpiiŋdaan nan n pɔɔg likpakɔɔl daalaa, wee bin galin u.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Ni ma bu, Uninfosaal a Big le ye likpakɔɔl daal nmɔn a yudaau.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Le Yisa nan yin kinɔɔnkpankpiiŋdaan nin ke Dan do.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Le ki baan bininfobin ke, Ti siim jaa ti ke ti li ter bininfob bee ti ku baa? Le bi ŋmin.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Le u lig bi nin liŋuul u sufu kaah nan bii takpem bi nunkoo-n nbu; le u nan gbiɛ lig uja-n ki ke, Tann a nɔɔ nmɔn na. Le u tann mi le mi pɔɔg.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Libol nmɔn le Farasii yaab n nan labir ki nyan, le bi ma nin ubɔr Herɔd a ninfob tan lol choo baah li ŋa man ki ku Yisa.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan fii ki jiir Galilii a mɔgil n-yaa gbaam, le kuninfogbaluŋ nan nɔɔ u bɔɔn. Bi nan nyan Galilii nin Judiya.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: nin Jerusalam nin Idumiya nin Jɔdann a mɔgil a dool, nin Taya nin Sidonn a tingbaan yin, bi nan yab pam, le bi nan lagin u ban; baah nan gbir ke u tun ligaril a toon n bu, le bi nan dan u ban limɔgil n-yaa gbaam.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Le u nan bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke bin ban kuŋaluŋ ki sei u ni.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: waah nan che bibaarib takpem pɔɔg nbu le ban biɛn nan bu-n nan fɔɔr ki mal u ki ban bin maa u.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Nnaanbiim kaah be bininfob ban bu-n n nan kan u ke, bi lur le ki gbaan u nintogil ki yigin ke, Fin le ye Uwonbɔr a Bija.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Le Yisa bei bi nin linunmanl ke, Ni laa taam ki mɔɔn ni.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa nan don ligungonl a paab le ki yin ban wee u ban nin, le bi dan u ban.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Le u gann bininfob piig nin bilee, ki yin bi ke u tooi, ke bi li be u ban na wee u li tun bi wee bi li mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: ki sin li jɔɔ npɔɔn ki li nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: Waah nan gann bininfob piig nin bilee ban nin a yin sɔ: Simɔnn ;.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: nin Jemisi nin u waal Jɔnn, ban nan ye Zebedi a naachim nan, ;.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: nin Anduru, nin Filib, nin Batolomiu, nin Matiu, nin Tomasi nin Jemisi wan ye Alifiu a bija nan, nin Tadiɛsi, nin Simɔnn wan wee bi yi u ke Zealo wee u nan kɔn u bol a kaal bu-n.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: ki kpiɛ Judasi Isikariya .

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa kun, le kuninfogbaluŋ lagin u ma nin u bɔɔnɔɔbin ban, hali ke bi nan lann isaŋ yan wee bi li ta ji tijiir.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: U niin yaab kaah nan gbir man nin, le bi dan ke bin nan choo u, bi nan ke, U waar le.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Ban wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a siim ki nyan Jerusalam mu nan ke, Beelizebul le be u yulin, nnaanbiim a bɔru li che u nyan nnaanbiim nmɔn.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Le Yisa yin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nmɔn ke bin dan, le ki ga bi libɔŋal ke, Sintani li fir ŋɔɔr Sintani jigin naa?.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Ni ye nnaam a ninfob kɔn ki tog tab ke, bi naam nmɔn kaa li fir siri.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Ke ni ye iniin iba a ninfob kɔn nin tab ke, bi niin nmɔn mu kaa li fir siri.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Ni ye Sintani mu kɔn nin tab wee ki bɔgir ke, waa li fir siri, u diɛm le yen.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Uba kaa li fir ki kɔɔ upɔɔndaan a niin ki tan su u wan yɔɔri, see ke u chinn ki lol u le ki nin fir ki fee u wan yɔɔr nmɔn.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, Uwonbɔr li jiɛn bininfob a bir ki per bi nin ban sugir win biɛn.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: Amaa wan pag ki bii Kasi a Naaŋ n ke, Uwonbɔr kaa li nyi ki jiɛn per u daan ni. Le u galin kaa li nyi ki diɛ.

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Nan bu nan che Yisa pag man nmɔn ye nan le, baah nan ke, Nnaanbiim le be u yulin nin bu.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa a na nin u watiib dan nkaafiin, le ki tun uba ke u yin u ni.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Ki miin kuninfogbaluŋ ka le ki gɔɔn u, le u be bi sisiigin; le bi bei u ke, A na nin a watiib be nkaafiin ki baŋ.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ŋma ji ye n naa, ke ŋmam mu ji ye n watiibaa.

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Le ki lig ban ka u ban nin ki ke, Lig, n na nin n watiib be n ban do.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Wan biɛn tun Uwonbɔr a bamin, u ma ye n kpel nin n waal nin n ninsa nin n na.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Le Yisa ji piin gaar ibɔŋal tenn bi ke:.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: Paab a naamin nann ubɔr wan nan ŋa lipiichanil a jiir ki sei u bija le.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: U nan tun u toontum ke bin tan bei ban wee u nan yin ni ke bin dan, amaa le bi yii kaa dan ni.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Le u mann tun bi toontum biba ki len ke, Ni tan bei ban wee n nan yin ni ke n ŋa tijiir a siliu: N ku n naajai nin n wankor tan jɔɔ nkpamin na, le ni biɛn ŋa siliu. Bin dan lipiichanil a jiir nmɔn a jim.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Amaa baa nan gbir sei wi le ki yal yal, uba jon u kpaabin, le uba mu jon u kpoŋkpoŋin.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: Le ban gurin choo u toontumin ki ŋa bi yɔli yɔli le ki ku bi.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Le ubɔrin a ŋuul nan fii. Le u tun u tɔtogib, le bi tan ku bininfokubin, le ki nyɔɔr bi tim.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: Le u bei u toontumin ke, Lipiichanil a jiir nan ŋa siliu, amaa bininfob ban wee n nan yin kaa kpaan bin dan ni.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Ni li chaa kitiŋ poolin a sanjali bu ki tan yin ban biɛn wee ni kan, wee bin dan ki nan ji lipiichanil a jiir nmɔn.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Le u toontumin nyan ki jon kitiŋ poolin a sanjali bu ki tan yin ban biɛn wee bi kan, tininfobir nin bininfoŋam biɛn, le bicham dan ki nan gbee lipiichanil a niin nin kenken.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Amaa ubɔrin kaah nan kɔɔ un nan poor bichamin, le u kan uja uba ŋmar bi sisiigin kaa yee lipiichanil a wan yɔɔri.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Le ubɔrin baan ke, N jɔ, a ŋa nii ki kɔɔ do wee kaa yee lipiichanil a wan yɔɔraa? Le uja nmɔn kaa jiin tiba.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Tɔ, le ubɔrin bei ban guu-n ke, Ni lol u nɔɔi nin u taai, wee ki ta u nya tan gab nkaafiin libunbɔnlin na. Nan yaan wee timɔr nin kufabuŋ nin linyinŋmanl na yin be-n.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Nan bu-n bininfob takpem le bi yin, amaa wama le bi gann.

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Le Farasii yaab jon ki tan lol baah li ŋa man ki choo u mɔboi.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Bi nan tun bi bɔɔnɔɔb biba nin Herɔd a ninfob u ban, le bi tan baan u ke, Wan wɔɔn, ti nyi ke a ye ubamɔn pagir le, ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr nbamɔm nan bu-n a baga kaa be bininfosaalib kaah ye ban ni.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Bei ti ni, a tiɛr niin ki jiin lampo a yɔm paam maa? Ni ŋan nin ti li yɔ lampo ki tenn Siiza bee ti laa yɔɔ.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Amaa le Yisa nan nyi bi potiɛbir nmɔn bu, Le ki ke, Nim bilinlarib nan, ba ŋa wee ni ban ni pem maa?.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Ni wɔɔm biligib ban wee bi jɔɔ yɔ lampo nmɔn ni. Le bi ta biligib nmɔn sei u.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ŋma a nunaŋ nin u daagim be biligib nmɔn buu.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Le bi jiin ke Siiza. Le Yisa bei bi ke, Tɔ, ni li yɔ ki tenn Siiza nan ye u yaala, wee ki li yɔ tenn Uwonbɔr nan ye Uwonbɔr yaala.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Baah gbir man nan le ni gar bi. Le bi fɔr ki jiɛn u.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Lidaal nmɔn le Sadusi yaab, ban ke bitakpiib kaa fii nkumin ni, biba nan dan Yisa ban ki nan baan ke.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: Wan wɔɔn, Moziz nan wɔɔn ke ni ye uja kɔɔn upii wee ki kpo kaa la ma ke, u na a big li taar lipukɔɔl nmɔn na ki maa nbuwaab ki sei utakpiir nmɔn.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Tɔ bijab bilile le nan ye pii uba a buwaab ki be ti ban le uninkpelin nan kɔɔn upii kaa maa kibig, le ki kpo, le u na a big ta lipukɔɔl nmɔn.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Ni nan ye man le ki sei wan kpiɛ bilee-n nin wan paa bita, hali nin wan tan joor bilile-n nmɔn.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Ni joorim le upii nmɔn mu nan kpo.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Daandaan nan, ni ye bitakpiib a fii daal nan foo ke, bijab bilile nan ŋma a poo nin tan yin ye upii nmɔn naa? Nan bu-n bi biɛn kɔɔn wa.

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni tura, nan bu-n naa nyi Uwonbɔr a bɔri, bee waah jɔɔ npɔɔn man ni.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Bitakpiib nan fii nkumin ke, bi yin be le ke malakam kaah be Uwonbɔr a niin man ni. Lipiikɔɔnl nin lijakunl kaa na yin be.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Tɔ, amaa ki jiin bitakpiib a fiim paam, naa nyi ki karin nan wee Uwonbɔr pag ni nnaa?.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: Min le ye Abiraham a weem nin Azig a weem nin Jekɔb a weem. Waa ye bitakpiib a Wonbɔr wi, amaa ban fo a Wonbɔru.

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Kuninfogbaluŋin kaah nan gbir u wɔɔnim nmɔn nin, le ni gar bi.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Farasii yaab kaah nan gbir ke Yisa a bɔrin nyaŋ Sadusi yaabin, le bi lagin tab.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Le bi ni uba wan nan nyi Uwonbɔr a siim biɛn nan, nan mag Yisa ki baan u nbinbaan ke.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: Wan wɔɔn, nsiim milaba jon nsiim nmɔn biɛn naa.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke: Li jiɛ a Yudaau Uwonbɔr nin a sufu biɛn, nin a naaŋ biɛn, nin a potiɛl biɛn.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Nsiim nmɔn le lii nsan ki ji jon nsiim biɛn.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Nan kpiɛ milee nann chinchinn a siim nmɔn le: Li jiɛ a kunkɔɔn tab ke ŋaah jiɛ a ba man ni.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Nsiim nmɔn biɛn, nin Bilinlem a wɔɔnimin biɛn jɔn nsiim milee nan bu le.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Farasii yaab kaah nan lagin tab n-yonn man ni, le Yisa baan bi ke.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: Ni tiɛr niin ki jiin Masiya paamaa? Ŋma a big ye waa? Le bi jiin ke, Defid a bija.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ke ba ŋa wee kinaaŋ a pɔɔm bu wee Defid nan yin u ke u Uyudaan naa? Nan bu-n u nan len ke.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: Uwonbɔr nan bei n Yudaau ke: Kalim n nɔɔjiim paam hali nin maah na li che a dataam nkpaan a taai a tɔɔn bol.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Ni ye Defid yin Masiya Uyudaan ke, nii wee u ji ye Defid a bijaa.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Uba kaa nan fir jiin u binbaam nmɔn ni, mambu naah nyan lidaal nmɔn ki chaa uba kaa ji nan jɔɔ sufu ki li baan Yisa nbinbaan ni.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: N-yonn nmɔn paab a naam na yin nann bisapɔm piig ban kaa nyi uja wee ki nan ta bi filaam ke bin too lipiichanil daan nsan bu-n le.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Bisapɔm biŋmu le nan ye tijɔri le biŋmu mu ye bilandam.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Ban jɔrin nan ŋmag filaam le kaa sin ŋmag fila nyum ki guun bi ba.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: Le bilandamin ma ŋmag fila nyum kpiɛ man be bi filaamin ni.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Lipiichanil daan nin kaa nan foon weemi, le njiɛm nan choo bisapɔmin biɛn, le bi dɔɔn jiɛn.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Le kunyiɛgtasiig le bi gbir likukɔl ke, Lig lipiichanil daan ni le chaan nin! Ni nyann ki nan too u.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Le bisapɔm piigin biɛn fii ki nyɔɔ bi filaam.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Le ban jɔrin miɛ bilandamin ke, Ni ŋa ni fila nyum nan wama ki sei ti. Ke ti filaam ban i junn le.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Le bi jiin ke, Aai, naa yin foo tim nin nimi. Ni li chaa ban kɔɔr fila nyumin ban ki tan da ni yaam.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Amaa baah nan labir jon ke bin tan da fila nyumin, le lipiichanil daan nmɔn foon. Bilandam biŋmu ban nan ŋa sili-n ma nan nɔɔ u le ki jon ipiichan niin ki tan kɔɔ ndiin le bi loon ki kpal linaanganl.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le tijɔrin mu nan foon, ki tan si ntimansanmɔnn ki kpaar linaanganl ki yi ke, Uninkpel! Uninkpel! Loor linaanganla wee ti kɔɔ.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Le upiidaan nin, jiin bi ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, maa nyi ni.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Mambu ni li guu ni ba man, nan bu-n naa nyi lidaal lan bee n-yonn man ni.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: Paab a naamin ji nann uniindaan uba kaah nan ŋa sili ke un jo nsan, wee ki yin u toontum bita ki nan ta u faal ki sei bi-n le.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: U nan sei chinchinn a toontunnu salima a bɔɔlim miŋmu, ki sei wan paa-n mu salima a bɔɔlim milee, ki sei wan gurin mu salima a bɔɔlig kiba. U nan sei bi biɛn baah li fir ŋmag man le ki yaar jon u sam.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Wan nan gaa miŋmu-n nan nyan le weem ki tan ta piin kpoŋkpoŋ, ki kan tinyɔɔr salima a bɔɔlim miŋmu ki kpiɛ i bu.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Man mu le wan nan gaa ilee-n mu ta ŋa kpoŋkpoŋ ki kan salima a bɔɔlim milee tinyɔɔr ki kpiɛ i bu.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Amaa wan nan gaa kiba-n ma nan nyan le, ki tan gbii kiboog ki ta u yudaau a ligi yin piin nin yaan.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Ni nan yuur pam, le u niin daan nmɔn ni nan jirin, le ki yin u toontumin, ke un lig bi ligi nmɔn ki kan baah kan tinyɔɔr man.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Wan nan gaa miŋmu-n kaah kɔɔ n-yonn man ni, le u ta nbɔɔlim miŋmu ki kpiɛ ki ta sei u yudaau ki ke, N yudaau, a nan seim salima a bɔɔlim miŋmu le, lig, n mu tun ki kan tinyɔɔr miŋmu le ki kpiɛ.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Le u yudaau nmɔn jiin bei u ke, N faan, a ye utoontunn ŋan le! Ki ji tun nbamɔm, ŋaah ŋmag wama nan nbamɔm nan, mambu, n yin ta tiwann takpem a ninkper le ki ŋa a nɔɔn. Npopeen man wee n jɔɔ nan, ni ye min nin fin yaal le.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Le wan mu nan gaa milee-n kɔɔ u yudaau ban, ki bei u ke, N yudaau, lig, a nan seim salima a bɔɔlim milee le, amaa le n ta kpeŋ ki kan milee tinyɔɔr ki kpiɛ.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Le u yudaau jiin bei u ke, Nfaan, a ye utoontunn ŋan le! Ki ji tun nbamɔm, ŋaah ŋmag wama nan nbamɔm nan, mambu n yin ta tiwann takpem le ki ŋa a nɔɔn. Npopeen man wee n jɔɔ nan, ni ye min nin fin yaal le.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Le wan nan gaa kiba nan ma kɔɔ u yudaau ban ki bei u ke, N yudaau, n nyi a bɔr kaah pɔɔ, ŋaah kaa bu nan ni a chiɛ ni ma le. Ŋaah kaa yaal tijiir tan ni, ti ma le a pii.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Mambu le n saŋ bijawaam, ki gbii kiboog, ki ta a ligin nmɔn piin. Lig, a ligi nmɔn sɔ, gaa a wan.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Le u yudaau jiin bei u ke, Fin utoontunn wan a pool to nan a ye ugbanyagdaan le! A pii nyi ke n chiɛ nan wee maa bun le, ki ji nyi ke n pii nan wee maa yaal ni le.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: Man ke, a bi ta n ligi nmɔn ki bil baŋ ni le. Maah bi nan jirin nan n bi li kan n ligi nmɔn ke tinyɔɔr paa i bu.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: Ni gaa ilig nmɔn u ban na man, ki ta kpiɛ wan jɔɔ bɔɔlim piigin na.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Nan bu-n wan biɛn jɔɔn ke bi li kpiɛ u, le un mann kan takpem, amaa wan kaa jɔɔ nan, hali waah le jɔɔ nan ni bi gaan ni le.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Wee ki ta utoontunn wan kaa jɔɔ tinyɔɔr nan gab nkaafiin libunbɔnlin na, nan yaan wee timɔr nin kufabuŋ nin linyinŋmanl na yin be.

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: Uninfosaal a Bigin nan jirin nin u pag nin malakam biɛn n-yonn man ke, u na yin kal u naangbɔŋ bu le nin u pag.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Dulinya nan a ninfob a booli biɛn na li lagin u nintogil, le un bɔgir bi ke upeekpaar kaah ti bɔgir ipee nin iŋɔɔb man ni.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: U na yin ta ipee le ki siin u nɔɔjiim paam, le ki ta iŋɔɔb mu ki siin u nɔɔgam paam.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Le ubɔrin na li bei ban si u nɔɔjiim paamin ke, Ni dan, nim ban wee n Baa kaab tikaar ki jaa ni bu nan, ni gaam ni faal, nnaam man wee Uwonbɔr nan ŋa mi sili ki guu ni dulinya a piinimin ni.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Nan bu-n nkom nan jɔɔm le ni seim tijiir, le nnyunyum jɔɔm le ni seim nnyum. N nan ye uchaan, le ni gaam ki ŋaam kuchɔŋ ni niin ni.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: N nan choon gaan le ni seim tiwann yɔɔr yee kaar, n nan bu le, le ni terim, bi nan taam ŋa kuloondiigin le, le ni dan nan gɔnnim.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Le bibuchim nmɔn na li jiin ki baan u ke, Uyudaan, ibayoo wee ti nan kaŋ wee nkom jɔɔŋ wee ti seiŋ tijiira? Bee ibayoo wee ti nan kaŋ wee nnyunyum jɔɔŋ wee ti seiŋ nnyuma?.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Bee ibayoo wee ti nan kaŋ wee a ye uchaan wee ti gaaŋ ki ŋaaŋ kuchɔŋ ti niin yin naa? Bee a choon ki gaan wee ti seiŋ tiwann yɔɔraa?.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Bee ibayoo wee ti nan kaŋ ki miin a bu wee ti teriŋ bee ibayoo wee ti nan kan a be kuloondiigin wee ti gɔnniŋ ŋaa.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Le ubɔrin mu na li jiin ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, naah nan tun man ki tenn n nabum ban waarin, ni nan ye min le ni nan tun ki tenn.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: Le u na li bei ban si u nɔɔgam paamin, Nim ban wee limɔpool nɔɔ nan, fɔɔr n ban na, ki li chaa ki tan kɔɔ nŋmi man gaan kaa jɔɔ njunim wee Uwonbɔr mann ki guun ulinlaril nin u malakam mi.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Nan bu-n nkom nan jɔɔm, le naa seim tijiiri, le nnyunyum jɔɔm, le naa seim nnyumi.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: N nan ye uchaan le, le naa gaam ni niin yin ni, n nan choon le ki gaan, amaa le naa seim tiwann yeekaar, le n nan bu ki be kuloondiigin, le naa che ni lig mi.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le bi mu na li jiin ki baan u ke, Uyudaan, ibayoo wee ti nan kaŋ ki miin nkom jɔɔŋ bee nnyunyum jɔɔŋ bee a ye uchaan, bee a choon gaan, bee a bu, bee a be kuloondiigin wee taa teriŋ ŋaa.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le u li jiin ni ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, nan wee ni nan yii kaa tun sei n nabum ban waarin, ni nan ye min le.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Le bi na li jo ki tan kɔɔ litudaril lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm ni, amaa le bibuchim n mu li jo ki tan gaa limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Juu yaab a kpakɔɔl kaah nan jin ki jon, ki won kutaag a jakpanyiɛn, nnyantoon a chinchinn a weem daal, le Meri wan nyan Magidalin nin u Meri kpiɛg nan fii ki jon Yisa a kaag a gɔnnim.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Libol nmɔn, kitiŋ nan dam takpem le, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a malaka le nan nyan paab ki jiirin, ki jon kukaag n-yaan ki tan binn likalool, ki kal li bu.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Le u gbanann nyagin ke utaal a jugin, le u wan yee wan peen a li ke kitilangbɔŋ peŋin.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Le bijawaam nan choo ban nan guu kukaagin takpem, le bi nin jegil ki nan lu lu ki dɔ ke bitakpiibin.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Naah nan won kutaag njakpiin, le kumanug a yudam nin bininkpeeb nan bon baah li ku Yisa man.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Le malaka n bei bipiibin ke, Ni laa san bijawaami. N nyi naah ban Yisa wan wee bi nan kpa u lidapunpunl bu-n.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Le bi lol u tikulor ki ta u ki ŋubin ubɔr yudaau wan wee bi yi u ke Palati a nɔɔn.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: Waa be do; u fii nkumin na, ke waah nan len man ni. Ni dan ki nan lig waah nan dɔ nan yaan ni.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Judasi, wan nan kɔɔr Yisa n kaah gbir ke bi yin ku Yisa n ni, le u sufu bii pam, le u ta tikur a ligibim pitaa-n ki ta jiin ki tan sei kumanug a yudam nin bininkpeebin.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: Ni li chaa weem weem ki tan bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke u fii nkumin na, le ki liir ni sam ki jon Galilii u. Daandaan nan n bei ni le, nin yaan le ni li kan u.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: Le ki len ke, N ŋa tibira, nan bu-n n kɔɔr uninfo wan kaa jɔɔ tibira. Le bi jiin ke, Aho, ti mu a baga ji be niin lee? Ni ye a yaa bɔr le.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Le bipiibin nyan kukaag nmɔn ban ki guun jon ke bin tan bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin, le bijawaam jɔɔ bi, amaa le ki sin jɔɔ npopeen.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Le Judasi ta iligin ki gab iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan le ki nyan ki tan jɔn uba.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
