local: Baah nan chaa-n, libol nmɔn le Yisa kenn bi nsann ki ke, N poor ni, le bi dan, ki choo u taafar le ki jiɛn u.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Le kumanug a yudam pii nligibimin nmɔn le ki ke, I ye ncham a ligi le, ti siim kaa chee ke ti ta iligi nmɔn ki kpiɛ iwonjiɛn niin a ligin bu.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni laa san bijawaam. Ni li chaa man ki tan bei n nabum nmɔn ke bi li chaa Galilii, nin yaan le bi ta li kam.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Mambu, le bi kal ki tiɛr jiin ilig nmɔn paam, ki nan ta daa uyɔgmaar a tiŋ ke bi li pii bicham nin yaan.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Bipiib nmɔn kaah nan chaa-n, le ban nan guu kukaag nan a ni biba nan jir kun kitiŋ poolin, ki tan bei kumanug a yudam niwann nan biɛn tun.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Mambu le che bi yi kitiŋ nmɔn ke, Ncham a tiŋ hali nin dinan.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Le kumanug a yudam nmɔn nin bininkpeebin nan lagin kal ki lol, le ki sei bikaguubin ilig takpem.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: Le ni gbeen ulinlenn Jerimaya kaah nan len nan ni ke: Bi ta tikur a ligibim pitaa ki yɔn u bu, ke Iziral yaab biba kaah chee ke u daag le ye man ni.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: le ki bei bi ke Ni len ke, Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb le dan kunyiɛgtasiig, iyoo yan wee ni dɔ jiɛn ni, le ki nan sun kur u gbanann.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: ki daa uyɔgmaar a tiŋ, ke Uyudaan Uwonbɔr kaah wɔɔnim man ni.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Ni ye Uyudaan gbir man ke, tim le li degin ni bu u ban ki nyan ni.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Le bi ta Yisa ki tan siin kitiŋ nmɔn a bɔr yudaau a nintogil, le ubɔr yudaan mu baan u ke, Fin le sin ye Juu yaab a bɔr win naa? Le u jiin ki ke, Nn, ke ŋaah len man ni.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Le bikaguubin gaa iligin, le ki jir ki tan mɔɔn, bininkpeeb nmɔn kaah wɔɔn bi man ni. Le Juu yaab gaa tibɔrin ki jin hali nin dinan.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Kumanug a yudam nin bininkpeebin kaah bii u man ni, waa nan jiin niba.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin uba-n jon Galilii a jɔɔl liba a paab, nan yaan wee Yisa nan bei bi ke bin jon.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Le Palati baan u ke, Ŋaa gbir baah pag man ki biiŋ ŋaa.

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Baah nan kan u n-yonn man nin, le bi jiɛn u. Amaa bi ni bibam nan biɛnn u le.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Amaa le Yisa si ŋmin hali kaa jiin niba. Le ni gar ubɔr yudaan nin takpem.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Tibɔŋann tan pag ki jiin Uwonbɔr a Bija Yisa Masiya paamin a piinim.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Le Yisa fɔɔr mal bi, ki len ke, Uwonbɔr seim paab nin tum a ninkpera.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Yisa kaah nan pag ni nan biɛn ki diɛ n-yonn man ni, le u bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Tɔ, yuglagir a chiig a jim a yonn biɛn ke, kitiŋ nmɔn a yudaau ti nyan uninfo uba le kuloondiigin, wan wee bininfob ban win ki jaa.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Ni nan sɔb ulinlenn Azaya a gbɔŋin le ke: N li tun n toon wan li chinn liir a sam, ki tan mann a sam.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Ni bu, ni li chaa kutingbɔŋ nan yaan, binib a booli biɛn ban ki ŋa bi n-yaa bɔɔnɔɔb, ki fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful, Baa nin Bija nin Kasi a Naaŋ a yinl bu.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: Ni nyi ke iweehn ilee le gur nin yuglagir a chiig. Bi na li ta Uninfosaal a Bigin ki sei le bin kpa u lidapunpunl bu.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Uba nan be kuloondiig nmɔn yaan iyaayoo, le u yinl ye Barabasi, ke bininfobin nyi u toonbir bu.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: Uba a kukɔl tann kukunkɔɔg bu ke, Ni ŋa npaan ki sei Uyudaan na, ni mann nsan man tel ki sei wa.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: ki wɔɔn bi wee bin chee nan wee n pii wɔɔn nimin n biɛn. Ibamɔn bu n yin be ni ban le hali nin dulinya a diɛm.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le kumanug a yudam nin Juu yaab a ninkpeeb lagin tab kumanug a yudam a ninkpelu, wan a yinl ye Kayafasi a niin.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Baah nan nan lagin n-yonn man ni, le Palati baan bi ke, Ŋma wee ni ban n nyan u kuloondiigin ki jaa: Barabasi bee Yisa wan wee bi yi u ke Masiya nnaa.

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Tɔ le Jɔnn nan dan, ki nan be kukunkɔɔg a paab ki fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin, ki mɔɔn linyunful lan ye nkpenim nin tibir a jiɛn per.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Yisa ji nan jir limɔgil n-yaa gbaam, ki tan be wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr. Le kuninfogbaluŋ nan lagin u ban, waah nan kan ki miin bininfob n yabin, le u kɔɔ ki kal kuŋaluŋ a poolin, ki jiir nnyumin, le bininfobin nan paar si limɔgil n-yaa gbaam.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: le bi nan lolig baah li ŋa man wee ki woon ki choo Yisa ki ku u.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Nan bu-n Palati nan nyi naah ye linunchɔril bu le bi choo Yisa ki nan ŋa u nɔɔn.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Le u nan ga bi libɔŋal ki wɔɔn bi tibɔr takpem.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Le bininfob nan nyan Judiya a tingbɔŋin biɛn nin Jerusalam a timin, ki dan u ban ki nan kpiir bi toonbir, le u nan fun bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful Jɔdann a kpenn.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Le ki nan ke, Amaa ni laa che ti choo u kuchiig nan yaan ni, nan bu-n kuninfogbaluŋin li gaa tijar.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Palati kaah la nan ka ji Yisa tibɔr nan, le u poo tun ntoon ke: Nyan a nɔɔ uninfo wan kaa jɔɔ bir nan a bɔrin na, nan bu-n kunyiɛg n won dɔgin uja nmɔn a dindɔgin ti mugim pam.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: U nan bei bi ke, Uja uba le nan jon u kpaabin un tan yaal ndibim.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Jɔnn nan yee alakoomi a gbɔŋ le, ki ji lol kugbɔŋ a changbam u chaŋin, u jiir nan ye itɔɔn nin inaanyoon a seer le.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: N-yonn man wee Yisa nan be Betani a tiŋin, Simɔnn wan nan ye kikɔŋin a niin.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Le kumanug a yudam nin Juu yaab a ninkpeeb bei kuninfogbaluŋin ke bin bei Palati ke un nyan Barabasi jaau wee ki sin ku Yisa.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: waah nan be yaal nan, le u wanbooli iba nan lu nsanpoolin, le nnɔɔm pii i biɛn ki jin.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: Le u mɔɔn nin nan ye nan le: Uninfo wan li ŋɔɔrin n bɔɔn ki dan nin joma, le maa kpaan nin n gbaan u titogil ki gbin u naatagil a ŋmim gbaa.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: u nan be ji tijiir le, le upii uba ŋmag tulaali a lulog kan a daag pɔɔ, ki nan kpaar wur Yisa a yul bu.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Le Palati le ji mann ki baan bi, Bininfob bilee nan ni ban n nyan ŋma jaa? Le bi jiin ke Barabasi.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Le iba mu ji lu kutintiɛgin nan yaan wee titann kaa yabin. Titann kaah kaa nan yabin, le i nan pan ki yabig weem.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Min fun ni nnyum le, amaa u ma na yin ta Kasi a Naaŋ le ki fu nin Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah nan kan man ni, le bi ŋulin tab ban nin liŋuul ki ke, Ba ŋa wee upii nan bii tulaali man naa?.

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Le Palati le ji mann baan bi ke, Ke Yisa wan wee bi yi u ke Masiya n ma, ni ban n ŋa u nii? Le bi biɛn len ke, Kpaa mu lidapunpunl buu.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: amaa nween kaah nan fa i wama-n, le i nan piɛg. Yaah kaa nan fir jiin inyaan nin bu, i biɛn nan kpo le.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Iyaayoo le Yisa nan nyan Nazarif a tiŋ kan be Galilii a tingbɔŋin nin, ki dan Jɔnn ban, le Jɔnn nan fun u Uwonbɔr a nyunful Jɔdann a kpenn.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Ti bi li fir kɔɔr tulaali nan ki gaa ilig takpen ki ta ter bilanjiim.

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Le Palati baan bi ke, Ba buu? U bir lee jann ye ti laa? Le bi mann tiɛn paab ki ke, Kpaa mu lidapunpunl buu.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Le iba mu nan lu ikunkon ni, ki nan yegin pan ki yabig nin ikunkon nin. Le ikunkon nin ŋuuri ki ku le yaa fir naan ni.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyan nnyum nmɔn yaan ki don nin, le u kan paab kaah loor, le Uwonbɔr a naaŋ jiirin ki nan tɔɔn ubu ki nann ulanjel.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Le Yisa biɛ bi ŋulin nmɔn bu, le ki baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni mugir upii nan man naa? Ni wan nan ŋan le u tun ki seim nmɔn.

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Palati kaah nan biɛ ke u pag ki chaa ke naa jɔɔ tinyɔɔrin, amaa wee bininfob a mɔb nan pɔɔgirin, le u ta nnyum ki nir u nɔɔi, bininfob n-yaa numin, le ki sin len ke, N nɔɔ kaa be uja wan kaa jɔɔ tibir nan a cham a nyamin ni. Ni ye ni yaa bɔr le.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Le iba mu lu ntanŋam a bol, le iba naan nbim pita pita, le iba mu naan piloob piloob, le iba mu naan kokobig.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Le likukɔl nan nyan paab ki ke: Fin le ye n bijiɛkaag; fin le n num gbee.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Bilanjiim man li gbaa be ni ban, amaa min kaa yin gbaa be ni ban ni.

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Le bininfobin jiin ke, Nn, che u cham li be tim nin ti buwaab a yuli buu.

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Yisa kaah nan ga bi libɔŋal nan ki diɛ n-yonn man nin, le u bei bi ke, Wan jɔɔ litul ke un gbirim.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Libol nmɔn le Uwonbɔr a naaŋin nan che Yisa don kukunkɔɔg bu.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Tulaali wan wee u kpaar ŋaam nan, u ŋa n gbanann le sili nin ti piim.

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Le Palati nyan Barabasi ki jaa. Amaa le ki sin che bi boo Yisa inaalab, ki jaa bi ke bin tan kpaamu lidapunpunl buu.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu bininfob biba nan be Yisa ban, bi ma nin u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee-n nan dan Yisa ban, n-yonn man wee u nan be u binba-n, ki nan baan u libɔŋal n-yaa taai.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: u nan be kukunkɔɔg nmɔn bu ki tan kpaa iweehn pina, le Sintani nan bigin u. Yisa nin kulɔŋ a wankor le nan lagin be; le Uwonbɔr a malakam jiirin ki nan ŋaa u tiŋann.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, nan yaan biɛn wee bi li mɔɔn tibɔŋann nan, dulinya nan yaan, bi li mɔɔn upii nan kaah tun nan nan mu, ki li ye litiɛril ki jiin u paam.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Le kitiŋ nmɔn a yudaau Palati a tɔtogib ta Yisa ki kɔɔn Palati a naakoon , le bitɔtogib lagin ki nan gɔɔn u.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Nim bu ke Uwonbɔr pii chinn ki kpiir ki wɔɔn ni U naam a bɔwoon kaar nna, bininfob ban kaa be U naamin ni le u gaar bi ibɔŋal.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Le bi nan choo Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunful nan, ki tan ŋa kuloondiigin; ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa nan jon Galilii a tingbɔŋin, ki tan mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin.

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee a ni uba, wan wee bi yi u ke Judasi Isikariya nan, jon kumanug a yudam ban.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Bi nan pir u daala n le, le ki ta daalamaŋ ki yeen u.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: Wee bi le li gbaa lig amaa le kaa yin kan ni, ki ji gbaa gbir kaa sin yin biɛ ni taai yi; ni ye bi biɛ ni taai ke nba ke bi li kpenn bi binbem wee Un jiɛn bi toonbir ki per bi. .

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: Ki ke, Isaŋ foou, Uwonbɔr a niin a naamin mala, ni ma bu, ni kpenn ni binbema wee ki ŋa tibɔŋann nan yadau.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: ki tan baan bi ke, Ni yin seim baa wee n che Yisa nkɔɔ ni nɔɔn naa? Le bi kan tikur a ligibim pita ki sei u.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: ki loo ikunkon a yuchigirig ki chigin u. Ki ta kijaangbiɛg ki ŋubin u nɔɔjiim. Le ki gbaan u nintogil ki laa u ki ke, Juu yaab a bɔru, ti pooriŋ.

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Le Yisa nan baan bi ke, Naa biɛ libɔŋal nan a taai yaa? Ke ni yin ŋa niin ki biɛ ibɔŋal yan gurin a taai yaa?.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Yisa kaah nan choon ki kpag Galilii a mɔgil n-yaa gbaamin, le ki nan kan Simɔnn nin u waal Anduru, ki miin bi be jaar tijanlann le limɔgil n-yaan, bi nan ye bijanchoob le.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le u nan piin ki ban isan yan wee u li ta Yisa ki ŋubin bi nɔɔn.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Ki piin ki siin timɔsann jaar u bu, le ki ga kijaangbiɛg kan wee bi bi ta ŋubin win ki ta fa fa u yul.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Wan nan yaal iwanboolin le ye wan pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni li nɔɔm ni, wee n che ni li mɔɔn bininfob ki tenn nim ke naah chor ijan man nin.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Boroboro wan kaa jɔɔ nnyɔg nan a jim a piin daal, le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb dan u ban, ki nan baan u ke, A ban ti jo laa ki tan ŋa sili wee a ji yuglagir a chiig a jiir aa.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Baah nan laa u ki gbee n-yonn man ni, le bi pir u daalamaŋin, le ki ta u tɔɔl a daala ki yeen u, le ki ta u ki jon nan yaan wee bin yin kpaa win.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: bininfob bi ba nann iwanbool yan lu nsanpoolin nin le, ni ye bi gbir Uwonbɔr a bɔr ke, Sintani kɔɔ bi ni le ki nyan ti bi sufu ni weem.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Libol nmɔn le bi nan jiɛn bi janlann nmɔn, ki fii ki nɔɔ u.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni li chaa kitiŋ nan yaan, uja uba a niin, ki bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn len ke: N saŋi mala. N ban min nin n bɔɔnɔɔb nji yuglagir a chiig a jiir le a niin nan. .

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Baah nan chaa nyan Palati a naakoon nin, le bi tan kan Sarene a tiŋ a jaa uba le u yinl ye Simɔnn, le bi choo u, ki mug u le u tu Yisa a dapunpunl.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Bininfob biba mu le ye iwanbool yan nan lu kutintiɛgin nin, bi gbir le ki ga tibɔr nmɔn weem nin npopeen.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: Le bi nan choon ki jon nintogil wama ki ji kan Jemisi nin u waal Jɔnn, bi nan ye Zebedi a naachim le, bi nan be bi ŋaluŋin le ki lig bi janlann.

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin sin tun ke waah wɔɔn bi man ni, ki ŋa yuglagir a chiig a jiir nmɔn a sili.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Le bi tan foo nan yaan wee bi yi ke, Goligota .

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: amaa le imɔleen nin kaa yin fir ki jiin inyaan bi sufu ni nbamɔmi, ni ma bu baa ŋmag ti yuur iyoo yan wee bininfob ŋa bi fala, bee limugil nan dan tibɔr nmɔn bu, le bin ta gab weem.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Libol nmɔn le u nan yin bi, le bi nyan ki jiɛn bi baa Zebedi nin u toontumin kuŋaluŋin yaan ki nɔɔ u.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Kujɔɔg kaah nan nan foo-n, le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee-n nan kal ki be ji.

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: Le bi ta ndaam nin tiwantunton ki ŋmarin tab ki ta sei Yisa ke un nyu, le u len mi ki yii kaa nyun mi.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Le bininfob biba mu nann iwanbool yan nan lu ikunkon nin le, bi gbir tibɔrin ke.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan tan kɔɔ Kapeniɛm a tiŋin, le Juu yaab a kpakɔɔl daal nan foo, le u paa kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin ki piin ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Baah nan be ji n-yonn man nin, le u ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, ni ni uba le yin kɔɔrim.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Baah nan kpa u lidapunpunl bu ki diɛ n-yonn man ni, le bi tɔn inaa ki por u wan yɔɔr.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: le dulinya nan a bɔr nin njɔɔm a bam nin tiwann ban kaar a booli a booli n kɔɔ ki nyagin tibɔr nbu bi sufu ni, ki ku ti le taa yin fir ki naan ni.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: U wɔɔnim nmɔn nan gar bi pam, nan bu-n u nan wɔɔn bininfob ke u jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm le, waa nan wɔɔn bi ke ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin kaah wɔɔn man nni.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin a sufu bii takpem, le bi piin ki baan ubaba ke, Uyudaan, ibamɔn buu, ni ye min lee.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: le ki nin ka niin ki guu u.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Amaa bininfob biba mu be le ke iwanbool yan nan lu ntanŋam a bol nan le. Bi gbir tibɔrin ke bi gaan ti le, le ti naan, pita pita, piloob piloob, nin kokobig.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn uja wan nan jɔɔ kinaanbiig n mu nan be kuwonjiɛndiig nmɔn yaan le ki nan yigin papaab ki ke.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Min nin wan lagin ki ŋuug kisaŋ kiba ni nan, u ma le yin kɔɔrim.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Bi nan sɔb baah ke u tun tibir tan ni le ki tabin lidapunpunlin Yisa a yul bu: YISA WAN YE JUU YAAB A BƆRU WIN SƆ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Le u ji baan bi ke, Ŋma la nyi ki nyɔɔ fila wee ki ta kiyiikpoŋ ki chigin ki bu, bee ki ta ki woon gadu a lɔŋin naa? Aai, see ke u ta ki tɔɔn niwann nan kper a paab.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Fin Yisa, Nazarif a ninfou nan tim nin fin babu? A dan ke a nan bii ti lee? N nyi ŋaah ye wan, a ye Uwonbɔr a Kasi daan nle.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Uninfosaal a Big li jo ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin u bu man ni. Amaa woo ni li bir ki sei wan yin kɔɔr Uninfosaal a Bigin! Baa bi ma udaan gbaa ke, ni bi sɔ.

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Bi mu ji nan kpaa binaayigib bilee ki kpiɛ Yisa bu, le uba be u nɔɔjiim paam le uba mu be u nɔɔgam paam.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Tibɔr tan biɛn woo nan see ke ti nan nyan npaan ni, le tan mu buu mu na li kpiir.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Le Yisa tiɛn u bu ke, Ŋmin tifura wee ki nya u ban na.

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Le Judasi wan na yin kɔɔr Yisa nan baan ke, Wan wɔɔn, ibamɔn buu, ni ye min lee? Le Yisa jiin u ke, Nn, ni ye fin le.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Le ban choon ki jo niin nmɔn gugin bi yuli ki sugir u.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Wan jɔɔ litul ki yin gbir ke un gbirim.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Le kinaanbiigin fiɛn uja-n ki jaa, le ki sin yigin papaab, le ki ni nyan u ban.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Baah nan be bin ji tijiir n-yonn man ni le Yisa yuur boroboro ki sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, le ki war war ki sei u bɔɔnɔɔbin, ki ke, Ni gaa ki ji n gbanann le yen.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: ke Fin wan nan ke a li fir ber iwonjiɛn niin nan ki ji ta iweehn itaa ki maa fin sɔ, feem a ba jaau! Ni ye a sin ye Uwonbɔr a bija ke, a nya lidapunpunl nan a paab ki jirin.

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: Le ki ji bei bi ke, Ni mann ki pel naah gbir tibɔr tan nan nbamɔma. Naah magir man ki tenn nin, man mu le bi na yin mag ki jiin ni, ke nin jo man.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Ni nan gar bi biɛn pam, le bi baan tab ke, Ba ye naa? Nwɔɔnpɔm! U jɔɔ npɔɔn ki pag nnaanbiim le bi cheel u mɔb.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Le u ji yuur kiyiinyɔɔg nin isiloon a daam ki sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, le ki ta sei bi, ki ke, Ni gaa man wee ni biɛn n nyu.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Man le kumanug a yudam nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin nin Juu yaab a ninkpeeb n mu nan laa u.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Wan jɔɔ-n le bi yin mann kpiɛ u takpem, wan mu kaa jɔɔ ke, u li lann wama nan wee u jɔɔ-n.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Libol nmɔn le ti mɔɔn Galilii a tingbɔŋin biɛn.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: N cham man ye Uwonbɔr a poorin sɔ, ncham man kpaar ki wur bininfob takpem bu, wee bi kan tibir a jiɛnper nan.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: Ki len ke U feen bitab le ki jaar, amaa le kaa sin li fir ki fee u ba jaa! U ye Iziral a bɔru le! Che un nya lidapunpunl nan a paab ki jiirin wee ti ŋa u yada.

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Le Yisa ji nan len ke, Uwonbɔr a naamin nann uninfo kaah ti ŋa ke un tan bu tibur u kpaabin nin le.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Baah nan nyan kuwonjiɛndiig nmɔn yaan nin, le u ma nin Jemisi nin Jɔnn jon ki tan kɔɔ Simɔnn nin Anduru a niin.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: N bei ni le, maa ji li nyi ki nyu isiloon a daam nan ni, see lidaal lan wee min nin nim na li lagin ki nyu miwanpɔm, n Baa a niin a naamin.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: U gaa Uwonbɔr ki jin na Uwonbɔr li fee u ki jaa daandaan nan ni ye u ban u ke, nan bu-n u nan len ke, N ye Uwonbɔr a bija le. .

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: wee ki kɔɔ jiɛn kunyiɛg, ki nan fii njakpanyiɛn, ki miin iwanboolin biɛn pan ki yabig wee waa nyi yaah ŋa man ki pan ki yabig man ni.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Ki miin Simɔnn a chɔɔpii bu kugbanantoŋ le ki dɔ ndiin, le bi bei Yisa u wiɛn nmɔn bu.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Le bi nan gaah iwonjiɛn lan, ki nyan ki jon Olif a Siigi a Jɔɔl a paab.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Baah nan kpaa binaayigib bilee ban ki kpiɛ u bu-n mu nan laa u man le.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Kitiŋin li ti che iwanboolin pa ki yabig ki ŋa nbim. Linyagil li ti chinn ki nya, ni bɔɔn bu le li yul nni ŋɔɔrin, le ki ni nan ŋa nbim.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Le Yisa jon ki tan choo u nɔɔ ki fiin u kaan, le u gbanantoŋ nmɔn diɛ, le u fii ki ŋa bi kuchɔŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Kunyiɛg nan, ni biɛn li guun ki jiɛnim, nan bu-n ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: N li ku upeekpaarin, le ipee yan biɛn wee u ti kpaa-n worin.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Naah nan nan foo nwosisiigin le kutingbɔŋ bɔŋ ki tan kpaa liwonkagil a kur titaa.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Idi nmɔn nan beer n-yonn man ke, le ukpaar nni piin u dii nmɔn a chiɛm nin u dichiɛjug, nan bu-n lidichiɛl fo le nmɔn.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Lidaal nmɔn a jɔɔg nween kaah nan lu-n, ni bɔɔn bu le bininfob nan ŋmag bibaarib nin ban wee nnaanbiim be bi bu nan, ki ta bi ki dan Yisa ban.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Amaa n kum a fiim a bɔɔn ke, n li liin ni nintogil ki jo Galilii.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Tikur titaa nmɔn a yonn, le Yisa kunn papaab npɔɔn ki ke Eli, Eli, lema sabagtani. Ni taai wɔɔn ke, N Wonbɔru n Wonbɔru ba ŋa wee a yii maa.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Le Yisa ji nan baan bi ke, Ti lee ni yin ta Uwonbɔr a naam nan ki bigin baa? Ti yin gaa bininfob libɔŋal li la baa wee ni won bininfob a num paam maa?.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Le kitiŋ nmɔn a ninfob biɛn lagin ki nan be kudiig nmɔn a timansanmɔnn.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Le Piita jiin ke, Hali ni ye bi biɛn guun ki jiɛn niŋ ke, min kaa li ŋa man ni.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Ban nan si niin ni a ni biba kaah gbir u yim nmɔn ni, le bi ke, U yi Elaja le nmɔn.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: Mi nann nnyiɛnbim man wee uninfo ti ŋa ke un ta bu u kpaabin nin le, maa yab foo kutingbɔŋ nan yaan a wanbooli biɛn ni.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: U nan teb bininfob ban nan bu iwiɛn biɛn a booli, ki ji nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin takpem, kaa che nnaanbiim nmɔn len tibɔri, bi nan nyi waah ye wan.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N beiŋ ibamɔn le, dinan a nyiɛg, a li niɛ ke ŋaa nyimi nfimitaa, le ukooja-n nin mɔɔ.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Libol nmɔn le bi ni uba guun ki tan yuur chiisɔgsu ki ta bɔ lidamunmɔŋilin ki ta sagin kudaagin, le ki ta sei Yisa ke un nyu.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: le min nan pan ki yabig takpem, ki jɔɔ ibon takpen; le nnɔɔm nin tiɛ bitɛr ki bon bu.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Le Yisa nan chinn ki nyan iniin ke ukooja kaa laa mɔɔ, ki kɔɔ kulɔŋ nan yaan ŋmin chii, ki tan miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Amaa le Piita jiin ke, Hali ni ye min nin fin nlagin kpo ke, maa li nyi ki niɛ ke maa nyiŋi. Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin biɛn mu len man.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Le u tabin bei u ke Jiɛn mu, n jee Elaja li dan ki nan fee u jaa.

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Yisa nan gaa ibɔŋal pam nin Uwonbɔr a bɔr npaam. U nan gaa ibɔŋal ki kpaan bi baah li ŋa man ki biɛ ni taai.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Le Simɔnn nin u tabin nin nan choon ki ban waah be nan yaan.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔbin jon nan yaan wee bi yi ke Gesemani, le u bei bi ke, Ni li ka doou, wee n jo ni nintogil ki tan miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Le Yisa ji mann kunn papaab, le ki nin kpo.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: Waa nan nyi ki pag tibɔr wee taa ye libɔŋali. Amaa u nan ti pag ki wɔɔn u bɔɔnɔɔbin le ibɔŋal nmɔn biɛn a taai n-yonn man wee bi ti nan be bi binba ke.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: bi nan tan kan u le ki bei u ke, Bininfob biɛn ban bin kan ŋaah be nan yaan le.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: Le u nan ta Piita nin Zebedi a naachim bilee ki kpiɛ uba bu, le bi jon. U sufu nan piin le ki bii, le ni mugir u.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le likinkenl lan ti jɔn iwonjiɛn niin n-yaa sisiigin bɔgir paab ki tan kpaa tum, le tum nan dam dam, le titangbapir wogir.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Lidaal nmɔn a jɔɔg le Yisa bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, Ni che ti lɔŋ limɔgil nan a dool.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni che ti jo ntim man mal nan yaan, wee n mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn nin yaan mu, ni ma bu le n dan.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Lisufubiil lan dan n bu nan yab le ki ban lin kum. Ni li be do ki li guu kpiɛg n buu.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Le tikar loor, le bitakpiib ban nan kpo ki ye kasi a ninfobin takpem fii nkumin.

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: N-yonn nmɔn le bi nan jiɛn kuninfogbaluŋin, le bi nan ta Yisa nin kuŋaluŋ kan yaan wee u nan be-n ki chaa. Tiŋalin tiba mu na be niin.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: U nan chonn Galilii a tingbɔŋ biɛn, ki mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn, tiwonjiɛndirin, ki ji nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Le u fɔr ki jon bi nintogil wama, ki gbaan le u yutunl tan ma tum, le ki miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, N Baa, ni bi li fir ke, a laa che n nyu kiyiinyɔɔg nan yaan ni. Amaa che a bam le n tun, wee ni laa ye min a bami.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: Ki nyan tikarin, le Yisa a kum a fiim a bɔɔn le bi kɔɔ Jerusalam, le ki degir bininfob takpem bu.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Baah nan be kuŋaluŋin ki chaan le bi nan miin litafaapɔɔril le daar, le kunyungbɔŋ piin ki fii ki ban mi li kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin a poolin, ke ki nan ban ki-n mii.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Tɔ, le kikɔŋ kiba nan dan Yisa ban, ki nan gbaan u nintogil ki balin u ke, Ni ye a ban ke, a li fir che n pɔɔg.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: Le u nan jir u bɔɔnɔɔbin ban, ki miin bi jiɛn le. Le u baan Piita ke, Nim kaa le li fir guur ki kpiɛ n bu kukurug kubaa?.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Bitɔtogibin a ninkpelu nin bitɔtogib ban kpiɛ u bu ki guu Yisa kaah kan kutingbɔŋ kaah damin nin nan biɛn tun ni, le bijawaam choo bi takpem, le bi len ke, Ibamɔn buu, uja nan sin ye Uwonbɔr a Bija le.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn Yisa nan dɔ kuŋaluŋ nmɔn a bɔɔn ki kul pira ki jiɛn. Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin nan faa u ligir ki bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn, nnyum kaah yin jin ti-n, ŋaa yin gar ti buu.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Le kunyunyunbaag choo Yisa, le u tann u nɔɔ ki maa u, le ki bei u ke, N ban man. A wiɛm diɛu.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Ni ligira wee ki li miɛ Uwonbɔr a, wee ki laa nan kɔɔ libiginlin ni. Kinaaŋ ma ban amaa le tigbanann kaa jɔɔ npɔɔn ni.

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Le bipiib takpem ban nan nɔɔ Yisa ki nyan Galilii ki nan ter win, nan si nan fɔg le ki lig nan tun nmɔn.

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Le u fii sir le ki tiɛn ntafaam nbu, ki sin bei limɔgilin mu ke, Ŋmin soon na. Le ntafaamin nan jiɛn, le kunyungbɔŋin mu ŋmin soon.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Libol nmɔn le iwiɛn nmɔn nan nyan u gbanann nin le u nan pɔɔg.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Le u ji nan jir nfim milee ki tan miɛ ke, N Baa, ni ye kiyiinyɔɔg nan kaa li fir lagir n bu wee ni ye n nyu ki yaan le ke, a che a bam ntun.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Bi ni biba le nan ye Meri wan nyan Magidalin, nin Meri wan ye Jemisi nin Josef a na nan, nin Zebedi a poo.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Le u nan baan u bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn ke, Ba ŋa wee ni san bijawaamaa? Naa la ŋa Uwonbɔr yadaa.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Le Yisa nan kpaan u nbamɔm.

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Le u jir ki tan foo ki miin u bɔɔnɔɔbin ji jiɛn na, nan bu-n njiɛm le nan jɔɔ bi.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Kujɔɔg kaah nan foo n-yonn man ni, le Arimatiya a tiŋ a jɔɔndaau uba dan, le u yinl ye Josef u mu nan ye wan nɔɔ Yisa le.

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Le bijawaam mann choo bi takpem. Le bi nin baan tab ke, Ninfo boliba yen naa? Wee ntafaam nin kunyungbɔŋ biɛn nan chee u mɔbaa.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: ki bei u ke, Li nyi a bei uba. Li chaa ki tan ta a baa ki wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a minmanu wee ki mann kumanug ke Moziz a siimin kaah wɔɔn man ki jiin nkɔm paamin, wee bininfob biɛn n sin biɛ ke a kɔŋin diɛu.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Le u ji nan jir nfimitaa, ki tan miɛ Uwonbɔr ke waah pii nan chinn miɛ man nin.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: Le u jon uyudaan Palati ban, ki tan miɛ u ke un che un tan nyan Yisa a gbanann ki tan pii. Le Palati jaa u ke bin tan nyan sei wa.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Le uja nmɔn nan jon ki tan jɔɔ u pɔɔgim a bɔr nmɔn ki mɔɔn iyag biɛn; ni ma nan che Yisa kaa nan fir ki nyan npaan ni ki kɔɔ ntim miba ni, le ki le nin be kulɔŋin nan yaan ŋmin chii; le bininfob takpem nya iyag iyag ki chaan u ban.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Le Josef yuur Yisa a gbanann ki pɔbin kikinkenpeŋin.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Le ki nan jirin u bɔɔnɔɔbin ban ki baan bi ke, Ni le la jiɛn le, ki fooraa? Ni lig, isaŋ nmɔn mala. Le bi ta Uninfosaal a Big ki ŋubin tibirdam a nɔɔ na.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: Le ki ta u jon ki tan pii. U baantɔɔl a kaapɔŋ kan wee u nan lug kutangbapiig bu-n le ki bilin likalool ki biin ki mɔb, le ki jir kun.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Ni fiiu man, wee ti jo! Ni lig, wan kɔɔrimin le chaan nin.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Le Meri wan nyan Magidalin nin Meri umiɛnin nan ka mal kukaag nmɔn ban.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: U mɔb kaa la nan lu, le Judasi wan ye u tooi piig nin bilee a ni uba, degirin nin kuninfogbaluŋ, le biba ŋmag bitɔb a jum, le biba mu ŋmag ilangul. Kumanug a yudaau nin bininkpeeb li na tun bi.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Naah nan yin won kutaag, Juu yaab a kpakɔɔl daalin, le kumanug a yudam nin Farasii yaab jon Palati ban.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Judasi wan kɔɔr Yisa nan pii nan wɔɔn bininfobin siɛra le ke: N tan poor wan ke, ni li nyi ke u ma yen, wee ki choo mu.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: Le ki tan bei u ke, Ti baa, ti tiɛr ke tibɔkar daan nmɔn kaah nan be-n, u nan bei ti ke, Iweehn itaa daal n li fii nkumin.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Judasi kaah nan foon n-yonn man ni le u jon Yisa ban weem ki tan poor u ke, Wan wɔɔn, a nyiɛg.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Mambu, sei bitɔtob bimɔb wee bi li ka ki guu kukaag nmɔn ki tan kpaa iweehn itaa nmɔn daal, naa ye man ke, u bɔɔnɔɔb na li dan ki nan kur u, ki sin len ke u fii nkumin le. Le bi bɔkar nmɔn na li jo chinchinn yaarin.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N jɔ, tun nan wee a dan a nan tun nmɔn na. Iyaayoo nmɔn le kuninfogbaluŋin daa dan ki nan bib Yisa.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Le Palati bei bi ke, Ni ta ban guu wee bin tan guur kukaag nmɔn naah ban man.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Ki miin u bɔɔnɔɔbin a ni uba ŋa weem ki nar bitɔb a jug ki ga kumanug a yudaau a toontunnu a tul ki par.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Le bi ta ban guu le bi tan guu kukaag nmɔn, le ki ŋa titann pɔn kukaagin nbamɔm.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Jiin a tɔb a jug nmɔn ki ŋuun kutɔɔgin na, nan bu-n wan kɔn nin kijug ke, u kpeg kijug a kum le.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: Ŋaa nyi ke n bi li fir miɛ n Baa wee un terim maa, u bi li tun malakam a tɔtogib weem ni, ke bin jo turpiig nin bilee?.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Amaa ni yin ŋa niin ki gbeen nan sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, ni kpaan ke nin sin ŋa man naa.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le u baan kuninfogbaluŋin ke, N ye ban kɔnn nin tininkper a sanliiru le ni dan nin njum nin ilangul ke ni nan choom maa? N nan ti be iwonjiɛn niin le iweehn biɛn ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr, amaa le naa ti chor mi.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Amaa ni niɛ nan tun ke nin gbeen bilinlem kaah nan sɔb nan Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin le. Iyaayoo nmɔn ki miin u bɔɔnɔɔbin biɛn guun ki jiɛn u binba le.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Le ban choo Yisa ta u ki jon kumanug a yudaau Kayafasi a niin, nan yaan wee ban wɔɔnn nsiim nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninkpeeb nan ka guu-n.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Baah nan jɔɔ Yisa chaa-n le Piita tan be nan fɔg ki nɔɔ u bɔɔn, le ki tan paar si kumanug a yudaau nmɔn a niin a kaafiin, ki nan kɔɔ nloob, ki tan ka ban guu-n ban, ke un kan ni joorim.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Le kumanug a yudamin nin Juu yaab a bɔjib ban wee bi yi bi ke sanhidirin nan pɔɔn bi ba ke bin gbir tibɔkar dam a mɔnn, ki kan Yisa a bir wee ki ku u.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Le bininfob sin jin tibɔkar dam nmɔn a siɛra u bu, amaa le kaa kan u biri. Ni bɔɔn bu le bilee nyan sir.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: ki len ke, Uja nan le ke, N jɔɔ npɔɔn man li fir ber iwonjiɛn niin nan ki ji ta iweehn itaa ki ma i. .

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Le kumanug a yudaau nmɔn fii sir, ki baan Yisa ke, Ŋaa yin jiin naa? Ba a siɛra wee bijab nan pag ki galiŋaa.

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Amaa le Yisa kaa jiin tiba. Le kumanug a yudaau nmɔn mann baan u ke, Poo Uwonbɔr wan fo nan bu, fin le sin ye Masiya Uwonbɔr a bija-n ke, a bei ti ni.

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, Nn, ke ŋaah len man ni. Amaa n bei ni le: Naah nyan daandaan nan ki chaa, ni li kan Uninfosaal a Big kaah ka Liwonbɔr Gbingbemilin a nɔɔjiim paam, ki na li kan waah be paab titalangbann ni ki chaan.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Le kumanug a ninkpelu nmɔn dar u baantɔɔl a daala ki chiɛ ki ke, U sug Uwonbɔr a. Ke ti pii ji ban siɛradam ti ŋa baa? Lig, ni biɛn nan gbir waah sug Uwonbɔr man na.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Ke ni tiɛr niin? Le bi jiin ke, U kpaan nkum le.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, le bi siin timɔsann ki jaar u bu, ki boo u. Le biba mu pebir u ntapam.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: le ki ke, Bugir ki sei ti ni, Masiya, biɛ njee ŋma pebiŋ nan naa.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Piita kaah nan ka kumanug a ninkpelu n-yaa niin a loobin, le iniin nmɔn a toontun bisiɛg kiba dan u ban ki nan bei u ke, A mu ti be nin Yisa wan nyan Galilii nan le.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Amaa le Piita niɛ bininfob nmɔn biɛn ban ke, Maa nyi nan wee a pag nan a taai.

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Le ki sin fii nyan ki tan be ntimansanmɔnn. Le kibisiɛg kiba mu ji nyan ki tan kan u ki bei ban be niin nin ke, Uja nan mu nan ti be nin Yisa wan nyan Nazarif nan ban le.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Le Piita ji niɛ ki poo ke, Maa nyi uja nmɔn ni.

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Le ni kpiɛ wama le bijab ban si niin ni fɔr ki mal Piita ki ke, A sin ye bi ni uba le, nan bu-n a pipagir le wɔɔn man.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Le Piita poo bimɔb ki jaa u ba bu ki ke, Maa nyi uja nmɔn ni. Libol nmɔn le ukooja mɔɔ.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Le Piita nin tiɛr Yisa kaah bi bei u tibɔr tan nin ke: Ukooja kaa la li mɔ le a niɛ nfimitaa ke ŋaa nyimi. Le Piita nyan nkaafiin ki tan mɔɔ takpem.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, le kuninfogbaluŋ yaan ti nan lagin tab Yisa ban, baa nan jɔɔ tijiiri, le Yisa yin u bɔɔnɔɔb ki nan bei bi ke.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: Kunyunyunbaag choom pam bininfob nan bu, iweehn itaa sɔ le bi be n ban, ki mu kaa jɔɔ tijiir ki ji.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: Ni ye n jaa bi ke bi li kun nin nkom ke, bi li fig nsan, bi ni bi bam a tim fɔga.

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin baan u ke, Ti yin kan tijiir la ban kukunkɔɔg nan yaan wee tin foo bi biɛn naa.

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ni jɔɔ boroboro kon i ŋaa? Le bi bei u ke, I bilile.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Iyaayoo le u che kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn kal tum. Le u ta boroboro kon ilile-n, le ki poor Uwonbɔr le ki war war boroboro ki sei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke bin ta tol bininfobin na.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Le Farasii yaab nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin nan nyan Jerusalam ki dan Yisa ban.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Bi ji nan jɔɔ njanbum wama bi ban; le u ji poor Uwonbɔr i bu, le ki bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke bin ta tol ba.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Le ki kan ke u bɔɔnɔɔb biba ji tinɔɔjaginn le kaa nir bi nɔɔi.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Le bininfobin nan jin ki gbee. Ni bɔɔn bu, le u bɔɔnɔɔb lagin tijiir tan gurin, le ti ji gbee tikachar tilile.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: Farasii yaab nin Juu yaab ti nɔɔ bi kaal le, bi yin ji ke see bi nir bi nɔɔi nbamɔm le ki nin ji.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Bijab ban nan be niin nan foo turina paama. Le u nin nan jaa bininfobin ke bi li kun na.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Ni ye bi nyan kinyaŋin ki foon ke, baa ji, see ke bi fun chinchinn wee ki nin ji. Bi biɛ nan ti ŋmag ikaa takpem le ki kpiɛ ni niɛ nan bu. Bi biɛ nan ŋmag nkaal man wɔɔn ke bi li nir bi yir nin bi binbom, ki sin li ŋunn bi wan dɔɔn wan na.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: amaa le u ma nin u bɔɔnɔɔb ŋa weem ki kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki jon Dalimanuta a tiŋin.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Le Farasii yaab nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nmɔn baan Yisa ke, Ba ŋa wee a bɔɔnɔɔb nan kaa ŋmag ti yaaja a kaal nan wee ki pa ji tijiir nin tinɔɔjaginn naa.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Farasii yaab biba nan dan Yisa ban ki nan baan u, ki mag u ki ke un ŋa ligaril a wan nibau wee bin biɛ ke Uwonbɔr sin nyi u bu.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Uwonbɔr a linlenu Azaya kaah nan len man nin sin ye ibamɔn le. Ni ye tibɔkar dam le ke waah nan sɔb man nin. Uwonbɔr len ke: Bininfob nan, yim Uwonbɔr, ki tenn nim jilima bi mɔɔŋin le, amaa le bi sufu fɔg nim takpem.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Le Yisa nan foor ki jaa u poolin, le ki baan bi, Ba ŋa wee ni daandaan nan a ninfob ban n tun ligaril a toonl wee ni kan naa? Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, min kaa yin tun ligaril a toonl ki sei ni daandaan nan a ninfob nan ni.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Bi jiɛnim yɔli le, bi jɔɔ bininfob a kaal le ki wɔɔn.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Le u jiɛn bi, le ki jiir kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin, ki lɔŋ limɔgil.

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Le Yisa mann ki bei bi ke, Nim n ta Uwonbɔr a siimin bil niba paama wee ki si li nɔɔ bininfosaalib a kaal kaah wɔɔn man nin na.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb nan tam kaa ŋmag boroboro takpemi, boroboro kon liba kpeŋileŋ le nan be bi ŋaluŋin.

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Nim li le yii Uwonbɔr a siimin a nɔɔm le ki ŋmag ni baantɔɔl a kaal kaah ye man!.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Le Yisa nan kpaan bi ke, Ni li nyi man, wee ki li guu ni baa nin Farasii yaab nin ubɔr Herɔd a boroboro a nyɔgim n bu.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Moziz nan siin ke, Li tenn a baa nin a na jilimau; ni ye wan sug u baa bee u na ke, bin ku wa.

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Le bi nin pag tab ban ke, Taah kaa ŋmag boroboro n bu le u pag nan.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Amaa nim ke, ni ye uninfo bei u baa bee u na ke, Maah bi yin seiŋ nan sɔ amaa ni kpann Uwonbɔr yaan na;.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Amaa, le u biɛ baah pag nan, le ki baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni pag ke naah kaa ŋmag boroboro n naa? Naa le la biɛ nibaa? Ni potiɛl kaa le la ligiraa?.

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: ke le u nin jɔɔ npɔɔn kaa ji yin ter u baa bee u na.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Ni jɔɔ inunbil, ke ba ŋa wee naa gaa? Ni ji jɔɔ itul, ke ba ŋa wee naa gbiraa?.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Naah wɔɔn bininfob nmɔn nkaal man nan, ni yii Uwonbɔr a bɔrin le nmɔn. Ni biɛ ŋaan tiwann tiba takpem le ti ye ni niɛ nan a tab.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: Naa tiɛr n-yonn man wee n nan war boroboro kon iŋmu ki tol bininfob turiŋmu n naa? Tikachar ti ŋa wee ni nan lagin tijiir tan nan gurin naa? Le bi jiin ke, piig nin tilee.

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Le Yisa nan mann ki yin kuninfogbaluŋin u ban le ki bei bi ke, Nim ban biɛn be do nan, ni pel nbamɔma wee ki biɛ maah yin pag tibɔr tan nan a taai.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Ke n-yonn man wee n nan war boroboro kon ilile-n ki jin bininfob turina-n, tikachar ti ŋaa wee ni nan lagin naa? Le bi jiin ke tilile.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Niba kaa be ki li kɔɔ uninfosaal a mɔnn wee ki ŋa u tijaginn ni, amaa nan yin nya uninfo a mɔn nin, ni ma yin ŋa u tijaginn.

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Le u nin baan bi, Ke le naa lee la biɛ nibaa.

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: Waah bei bi man n-yonn man nin, le u jiɛn bi, ki jon ki tan kɔɔ iniin iba ni. Le u bɔɔnɔɔb baan u bɔŋal nmɔn a taai.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Le bi nan foo Befisada, le bininfob biba nan ŋmag ujajɔɔn uba ki dan Yisa ban ki nan balin u ke un ta u nɔɔ ki maa wa.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ni mu ji ye tilankpiindam lee? Naa nyi ke ni wan nan biɛn wee uninfosaal ji-n, kaa ŋaan u tijaginn naa?.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Le Yisa nan choo u nɔɔ ki ta u nyan kitiŋ nmɔn a gbaam, le ki sii timɔsann ki ŋa ujajɔɔn nin a numin, le ki ta u nɔɔi ki paan u bu, le ki baan u ke, A gaa.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Naa kɔɔ u sufu ni, u poolin le ni kɔɔ, le ki ji nyan u gbanann nin.

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Le uja-n nan goor ki lig, ki ke Nn, n kan bininfob, amaa bi nann isiig le ki choon.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Le u ji len ke, Ni wan nan nya uninfosaal a sufu-n nin ni ma ŋaan u tijaginn.

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Le Yisa ji ta u nɔɔi ki paan uja nmɔn a numbili bu, iyaayoo le uja nmɔn ni na lig soon ki miin u numbili n ligira, le u nin ga tiwann yɔɔr biɛn nbamɔm.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: u sufu ni le u tiɛr niwann nan bir, nin iwanchɔgin toon upiibanlin nin ujabanlin, nin nnaayigim, nin lininfo kul, nin iwanchɔgin toon.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Le Yisa kpaan u ke u ji li nyi un jir kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, le ki che u kun u niin.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: nin kujaŋuuŋ, nin kininaŋ, nin kularug, nin ijagɔɔr toon, nin lipiiponl, nin kutingbiig, nin kalanbaani ki kpiɛ ijɔr toon.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan fɔɔr nin yaan ki jon Sizariya Filipi a tinbumin. Baah nan be nsan bu ki la chaa nan le u baan bi ke, Ni beim ni, bininfob ke n ye ŋmiɛ.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn nya uninfosaal a sufu ni le ki ŋaan u tijaginn.

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Le bi bei u ke, Biba ke a ye Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin le, le biba mu ke a ye Elaja le, le biba mu ji ke a ye Uwonbɔr a linlemin a ni uba le.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa nyan niin ki jon Taya nin Sidonn a tingbɔŋin, ki tan kɔɔ iniin iba ni, kaa ban uba li nyi u bu niin ni, amaa waa nan fir woon u baa.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Le u baan bi, Ke nim mu, ni ke n ye ŋmiɛ? Le Piita bei u ke, Fin le ye Masiya nmɔn.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Le upii wan wee kinaanbiig nan be u bisiɛbig bu nan, nan gbir u bu, le ki dan u ban ki nan gbaan u nintogil.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Le Yisa nan sei bi ikpaan ke bi li nyi bin bei uba tibɔr nmɔn.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: Upii nmɔn kaa nan ye Juu a ninfou wi, bi nan ma u Fonisiya a tiŋ le, Siriya a tingbɔŋin. Le u balin Yisa ke u nyan kinaanbiig u bisa nmɔn a gbanann nin na.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Le Yisa ni nan wɔɔn u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, see Uninfosaal a Big jin fala, le kitiŋ a ninkpeeb, nin kumanug a yudam, nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin na li yii u, ki ji ku u, amaa ni ween itaa daal le u li fii nkumin.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Che n jin nbuwaab bi jiir chinchinn na wee, naa ŋan nin uninfo n ta nbuwaab a jiir ki sei ibɔ.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: U nan pag ni niɛ nan ki wonn bi biɛn nan a num paam le nbamɔm. Le Piita yin u ki nyan ngbaan, ki tan fuun u bu ki ke u laa ji nyi ki len man ni.

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Le upii-n jiin ki bei u ke, N yudaau, ni sin ye ibamɔn le, amaa ibɔ yan dɔ teebul a lɔŋin ni, mu pii nbuwaab a jibul yan lur tumin le.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Le Yisa gbiɛ lig u bɔɔnɔɔbin, ki mu fuun Piita bu ke, Sintani nan, fɔɔr n ban do. Ŋaa tiɛr Uwonbɔr kaah tiɛr man ni, a tiɛr bininfosaalib kaah tiɛr man le.

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, A jiim nan bu, li kun na; a ta li foo iniin ki miin kinaanbiig n nyan a bisa-n a gbanann nin na.

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Le Yisa bei u bɔɔnɔɔb nin bininfob ban biɛn nan nɔɔ win ke, bin fɔɔr ki mal u ni, le ki bei bi ke, Wan ban un nɔɔ min ke, see ke u yii u ba, wee ki yuur u kum a dapunpunl ki li nɔɔm.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Le upii nmɔn nan fii ki kun ki tan foo ki miin u bigin dɔ u wan dɔɔn waŋ bu le, ki miin kinaanbiigin sin nyan na.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: wan kaa ban un weeh u sayiig n bu ke, u ma na yin lann ki, amaa wan weeh u sayiig min nin tibɔŋann n bu ke, u ma na yin kan limiɛfol nin ibamɔn.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Le Yisa nin jiɛn Taya a yagil nmɔn, le ki nɔɔ Sidonn a yagil, nin kitiŋ kan wee bi yi ki ke ntim piig nan yaan ki tan foo Galilii a mɔgilin.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Ke ni ye uninfo kan dulinya nan a jɔɔm biɛn wee ki sin lann u miɛfol ke, ba a nyɔɔr wee u kan naa?.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Le bininfob biba dan u ban ki ŋmag uja uba le u ye kubirug, le bi balin Yisa ke un ta u nɔɔ ki paan u bu u.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Bee uninfo li fir ta baa ki daa limiɛfolaa?.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Le Yisa ta u le bi nyan ngbaan, bi binba, le u ta u nɔɔbili ki bɔ uja nmɔn a tuli yin, le ki sii timɔsann ki ŋa uja nmɔn a lanbil.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Wan mu san ifa nin un ta n bɔr nan ki wɔɔn titoonbirdam nin tukangbirdam dulinya nan yaan ke, wee Uninfosaal a Big nan jiirin nin u Baa Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm nin u malakam ke, u mu na li san u ifa nin un ta u ki kpiɛ u ninfobin.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Le ki waan lig papaab le ki foor jiin u poolin, le ki bei uja-n ke, Efata, ni taai le ye Loora.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Libol nmɔn le uja nmɔn nin gbir, ki ji fir pag nbamɔm.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Le Yisa nan kpaan bininfobin ke bi li nyi bin bei uba waah tun man nan bu; le bi dii ta tibɔr nmɔn ki mɔɔn.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Le ban biɛn gbir tibɔr nmɔn, ni ŋa bi ligaril takpem, le bi len ke, Waah ŋa nan nan, ni ŋan, u che bitukpaab gbir, le nbirim pag.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan lɔŋ Galilii a mɔgil n-yaa dool, Gereza yaab a tiŋin.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyan kuŋaluŋin n-yonn man nin, libol nmɔn le uja uba nan nyan tikarin ki kenn u. Kinaanbiig le nan be uja nmɔn ni.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: le u dɔɔ tikarin. Uba kaa nan be ki li fir ki baan u libaali, kaa ji li fir lol u tikulori.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Ni ye bi baan u nɔɔi nin u taai ke, see ke u gen kubaandaagin, ki sin chii tikulor tan be u nɔɔi yin ni. U nan jɔɔ npɔɔn pam, ke uba kaa li fir gɔɔ wi.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Kunyiɛg nin nween biɛn ke u be tikar a gungonlin a paab le ki choon jin ki ŋmag itangbag ki jiil u ba.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: U la nan be nan fɔg le ki kan Yisa, le ki guun ki nan gbaan Yisa a nintogil.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: le ki yigin nkukɔpɔɔn ki ke, Yisa Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan a Bija, min nin fin babu? Poo Uwonbɔr bu ke, ŋaa li mugirimi.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: Yisa pii chinn ki fuun kinaanbiig nbu le ke, Fin kinaanbiig nan, nyau ki sin jiɛn uja-n na.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Le Yisa baan u ke, A yinl laa? Le u jiin Yisa ke, N yinl ye Bitɔtob, nan bu-n ti yaba.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Le uja-n nan balin Yisa ke u laa nyan bi kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ni.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Kidoogbaluŋ mu nan si ligungonl n-yaa paab ki ji.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Le nnaanbiim nan balin Yisa ke, Jaa ti wee ti tan kɔɔ idool nan yaan.

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Le Yisa nan jaa bi nsan, le bi nan nyan uja nmɔn ni, ki tan kɔɔ idoolin. Le idoolin biɛn nan guun burburbur ki jiir limɔgilin, le nnyum jin bi. Idoolin a kanl nan foo turilee paama.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Le ban nan guu idool nmɔn nan fii ki guun jon ntimin nin tikpaann nin ki tan mɔɔn tibɔr nmɔn. Le bininfob nan dan ke bin nan kan niwann nan biɛn tun nmɔn.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Baah nan nan foon Yisa ban ki kan uja wan wee nnaanbiim nan be u ni nan pɔɔg ki ka niin yaan, u potiɛl biɛn nan ŋan, le u nin yee tiwann yɔɔr, le bijawaam choo bi.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Le ban nan be niin ki kan nin, ni nan pag ki wɔɔn bi tab Yisa kaah ŋa man, nin waah ŋa idoolin mu man.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Le bininfobin ni nan balin Yisa ke, un nya bi tiŋin na.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Yisa kaah nan ŋa sili ke un kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin n-yonn man nin, le uja wan wee Yisa nan nyan nnaanbiim u niin ke, N baliŋ le, che min nin fin n nɔɔ ki jo.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Amaa Yisa kaa nan chee, le ki bei u ke, Li jir kun a ninfob ban ki tan bei bi Uwonbɔr kaah saŋ kunyunyunbaag wee ki tun litoonl gbingbemil ki seiŋ man.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Le uja nmɔn ni nan labir ki nɔɔ kitiŋ kan wee bi yi ki ke ntim piig nan yaan ki mɔɔn nan biɛn wee Yisa tun ki sei u, le ni nan gar bininfob n biɛn.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa ji nan jir don limɔgilin a dool lan gurin. Waah nan tan foo limɔgilin a dool, le kuninfogbaluŋ nan lagin ki gɔɔn u.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Le uja uba dan nin yaan, le u yinl ye Jairu; u nan ye kuwonjiɛndiig a ninkpelu le. Uja-n kaah nan kan Yisa n-yonn man nin, le ki gbaan u nintogil.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: Le ki balin u nin u sufu biɛn ke, N bisa le bu ki yin kpo. Ŋaa li dan ki nan ta a nɔɔ ki paan u bu wee un kan kutebug ki foo.

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Le u ma nin Yisa ni nan nɔɔ ki jon u niin. Le kuninfogbaluŋin mu ji nan nɔɔ ki mug ki tir Yisa.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Le upii wan nan kan tipiir ibin piig nin ilee wee taa diɛr nan, mu nan be nin yaan.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Ni bu le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan nyan nin yaan ki jir chaa kun u tɔɔl a tiŋin.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: U nan chonn binyɔgdam ban takpem ke un teb uba, ki nan diɛ u faal, ke iwiɛn le mann kpiɛg.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Le likpakɔɔl daal nan foo, le u kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin ke un wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr. Bininfobin kaah nan gbir u wɔɔnimin, le ni gar bi biɛn, le bi baan tab ke, Uja nan kan nlam nan la ban naa? U ŋa niin ki fir tun ligaril a toon nan naa? U kan ni niɛ nan biɛn laa?.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Le u nan gbir Yisa bu; le ki dan kuninfogbaluŋin yaan, ki nɔɔ Yisa a bɔɔn, le ki sin tiɛr u sufu ni ke.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Kanpinta-n le kaa yen naa? Naa ye Meri a bija-n le yen naa? U ma kaa ye Jemisi nin Josef nin Judasi nin Simɔnn a kpel n naa? U ninsiɛb le kaa be nan do nan naa? Ni ma bu le bi yii u bɔr.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: N le bi maa u daala nan a binba ke gbaa, n bi li kan kutebug.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ulinlenn a tiŋin, nin u tɔɔl a ninfobin, nin u tɔɔl a niin yaabin a binba, le baa tenn u jilima.
