eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: le ki sin tan maa u daala. Libol nmɔn, le u pɔɔg, ki biɛ u gbanann nin ke iwiɛn nmɔn diɛu.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Waa nan fir tun ligaril a toon niin ni, bibaarib bi ŋa nan bu, le u nan ta u nɔɔ ki paan bi bu, le bi kan kutebug.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Iyaayoo le Yisa biɛ u sufu ni ke npɔɔn nyan u ban na. Le u baan bininfob n ke, Ŋma maa n daalaa.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: amaa bininfobin kaah kaa nan ŋa u yada-n bu le ni nan gar u. Man le Yisa nan chonn ntinbumin ki wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔb n mu baan ke, Fin kan bininfob nan mug nan, le a baan ke ŋma maa ŋaa.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: le ki nan nan lagin u tooi piig nin bilee ki sei bi npɔɔn ke bin nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin, u nan tun bi bilee lee bilee lee le.

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Le u ji mann lig ke un kan uninfo wan maa u.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Le ki bei bi ke, n-yonn man wee ni chaa nan, ni laa ŋmag niba ki chaa, see ni jaangbiɛm a binba. Ni laa ŋmag tijiiri, ki laa ŋmag ipɔgi, ni mu laa ŋuun iligi.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Upii-n kaah nan biɛ ke niba tun u gbanann nin bu, le bijawaam nan choo u, le u gbanann jegil le u dan ki nan gbaan Yisa a nintogil, ki bei u ibamɔn biɛn.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: Ni li tag inaataga, amaa ni laa ŋmag daala kiba ki kpiɛ.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, N ninsa, ŋaah ŋa min yada-n bu le a pɔɔg. Li chaa nin nposɔɔn na. A wiɛn biɛn diɛu.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Ni ye ni foo iniin yan yaan wee bi gaa ni kuchɔŋ ke, ni li be niin ki tan kpaa ni nya daal.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yisa kaah nan be pag man nmɔn le bininfob biba nyan Jairu wan ye kuwonjiɛndiig a ninkpelu n-yaa niin ki dan u ban ki nan bei u ke A bisa-n pii kpou. Ke ba ŋa wee a ji mugir wan wɔɔnn man naa.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Ni ye ni foo kitiŋ kiba ni, wee bininfobin kaa gaa ni ke, ki mu kaa ji pel ni wɔɔnim n ke, ni nya kitiŋ nmɔn yaan na, wee ki pir ni taai a tan ki jaa kitiŋ nmɔn yaan na, wee ni li ye siɛra biig ki sei bi.

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Le u mu ŋa ke waa gbiri, le ki bei Jairu wan ye kuwonjiɛndiig a ninkpelu nmɔn ke, Laa san bijawaami. Ŋaa yadau.

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Le bi nan fii ki jon ki tan mɔɔn ke, bininfob n jiɛn bi toonbira.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Mambu le Yisa kaa nan ban uba n nɔɔ u ki jo iniin nmɔn yaan ni, see Piita nin Jemisi nin u waal Jɔnn.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: le bi nan nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin takpem le ki war nkpam ŋa bibaarib takpem le bi kan kutebug.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Baah nan tan foo kuwonjiɛndiig a ninkpelu a niin n-yonn man nin, le Yisa gbir kufuug nin tikpemɔr.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Ubɔr Herɔd nan gbir Yisa a bɔra, u yinl kaah nan don takpemin bu. Le bininfob biba ke, Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin le fii nkumin. Ni ma che u jɔɔ npɔɔn ki tun ligaril a toon man.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Le u kɔɔ ki baan bi ke Ba a fur ye naa? Ba ŋa ni fabin naa? Kibig nan kaa kpo, u jiɛn le.

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Le biba mu ke, U ye Uwonbɔr a linlenu Elaja le. Le biba mu ke, U ye Uwonbɔr a linlem ban nan be yaayoo n-yaa ni u ba le.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Baah gbir u bɔr nmɔn le bi laa u. Le u ŋɔɔr bi ki nyan, le ki ta kibigin a na nin ki baa ki kpiɛ u bɔɔnɔɔb bita-n bu, le bi kɔɔ kudiig kan yaan wee kibigin dɔ-n.

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Ubɔr Herɔd ma kaah gbir man n-yonn man nin, le u ke, U ye Jɔnn, wan wee n nan che bi jii u yul ki tugirin le, le u fii nkumin ki be u miɛlin.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Baah kɔɔ n-yonn man nin, le u choo kibigin a nɔɔ le ki bei ke Talita kum.

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Herɔd baantɔɔl le nan che bi choo Jɔnn ki baan u kubaandaag ki ta u ŋa kuloondiigin, u ma Herɔd a waal Filib a poo bu. Bi nan yi upii nmɔn a yinl ke Herɔdiya, Herɔd le nan fee u ki kɔɔn.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Libol nmɔn le kibisiɛg n nan fii ki sir ki choon gɔnn. Le ni gar bi takpem. U nan ye ibin piig ni ilee a big le.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Jɔnn nan bei Herɔd le ke Uwonbɔr a siim kaa chee nin un fee u waal a poo ki kɔɔn ni.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Le Yisa kpaan bi ke bi li nyi bin bei uba, le ki ji bei bi ke bin sei kibigin tijiir wee un ji.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Ni ma bu le nan che Herɔdiya nan nan Jɔnn, ki ban un ku u, amaa le kaa sin nyi waah li ŋa man ni.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: Nan bu-n, Herɔd nan san Jɔnn le, u nan nyi Jɔnn kaah ye upoopendaan wan san Uwonbɔr. Mambu le u nan fee Jɔnn ki jaa. Jɔnn kaah nan mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr nmɔn Herɔd nan pelig le, ni nan bii u sufu pam, amaa le u le pɔɔn uba ki pelig u bɔr nmɔn.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Herɔdiya a bonni nan nan choo Jɔnn. Herɔd a maalim a chiig daal kaah nan nan foo-n, le u yin u bɔrbum nin bitɔtob a ninkpeeb nin Galilii a tingbɔŋ a ninkpeeb biɛn.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: Le Herɔdiya a bisa nyan ki nan waa kiwaag, le ubɔr Herɔd nin u cham a pool pen, le u baan kibisiɛgin ke, Ba wee a ban n ŋa seiŋaa? N li seiŋ nan biɛn wee a ban.

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Le ki poo tipoor sei kibisiɛgin ke, N li seiŋ nan wee a ban, hali ni sir nin n bɔgir n naam nan a bɔgil ki seiŋ ke, n li seiŋ.

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Le kibisiɛg nan nan nyan ki tan baan u na ke, N le pii ni yin bei ubɔr ke n ban baa? Le u na bei u ke, Tan bei ke, a ban Jɔnn wan fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin a yul le.

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le kibisiɛg nmɔn nan nyan ki jir ubɔr Herɔd ban ki tan bei u ke, N ban Jɔnn wan fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin a yul le, a ta kpeen kitasaŋin ki seim daandaan nan.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Ni nan bii ubɔr a sufu pam, amaa, u mu kaa ji na li fir yii kibigin kaah ban nan nin ni, waah bi chinn ki poo tipoor bichamin ban ni bu.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Le ki nan tun bitɔb a jau libol nmɔn, ke un tan tugir Jɔnn a yul ki nan sei u. Le bitɔb a jau nan sin nan jon kuloondiigin ki tan tugir Jɔnn a yul.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: ki ta kpeen kitasaŋin, le ki nan ta sei kibisiɛg nmɔn. Le u mu ta ki tan sei u na.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Jɔnn a bɔɔnɔɔb kaah nan gbir man n-yonn man nin, le bi dan ki nan yuur u gbanann ki tan pii.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa a tooi nan jirin u ban ki nan bei u baah tan tun nan biɛn, ki sin ji wɔɔn bininfob man.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: Bininfob takpem le nan dan, biba nan chaan le biba mu jir hali ke Yisa nin u tooi kaa nan jɔɔ isaŋ nin bin ji. Ni ma bu le u nan bei bi ke, Ni che ti jo kulɔŋ, ti binba, ki foor wama.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Le bi kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin bi binba, ki lɔŋ ki jon kulɔŋin.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Baah nan chaa n-yonn man nin, le bininfob takpem nan kan ki biɛ bi, le ki nan nyan bi timin, ki guun ki kpagin limɔgil a gbaam ki chinn kenn bi.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyan kuŋaluŋin yaan nin, ki miin bininfob pii lagin le ki mug, le kunyunyunbaag choo u takpem, bi binbem nan nann ipee yan kaa jɔɔ upeekpaar le. Le u nan piin iyaayoo nmɔn ki wɔɔn bi tibɔr takpem.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Iyoo nmɔn kaah nan nan jon nin, le u bɔɔnɔɔb nan dan u ban ki nan bei u ke, Isaŋ jon na, le taah be nan yaan nan mu ye kulɔŋ le.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: Jaa bininfobin wee bin jo kitiŋin, nin ntinbum man mal, wee ki tan daa tijiir ki ji.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Le u mu bei bi ke, Ni tɔɔlin sei bi tijiir wee bin ji. Le bi jiin ki baan u ke, A ban ti taa iŋmal iniin a yɔɔr le ki tan daa tijiir ki nan sei bi wee bin jii.

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ni jɔɔ boroboro kon iŋaa? Ni tan liga man. Baah jon ki tan lig ki biɛ n-yonn man nin, le bi jirin ki nan bei u ke, I ye boroboro kon iŋmu le nin njanbum milee.

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Le Yisa bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke bin che bininfobin bɔgir ki kal ikur ikur timɔsɔɔn bu.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Le bi bɔgir ki kal ikur kobig bininfob piŋmu piŋmu.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Le u yuur boroboro-n nin ijan ilee-n, le ki waan lig paab, le ki poor Uwonbɔr, le ki war war boroboro-n ki ta sei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke bin ta tol bininfob nmɔn na, le ki por por ijan ilee nmu, le bi tol bininfobin biɛn.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Le bininfob n biɛn jin ki nan gbee ki sin gur.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔb lagin tijiir tan gurin, ki gbeen tikachar piig nin tilee.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Bijab ban nan jin tijiir nmɔn nan ye bininfob turiŋmu le.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Libol nmɔn le Yisa nan che u bɔɔnɔɔb kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki lii u sam ki tan lɔŋ limɔgil a dool, ki chaa Befisada; amaa le u ma paar ke un jaa bininfobin.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: Waah jaa bininfob nmɔn ki diɛ n-yonn man nin, le u don lijɔɔl a papaab ki tan be ki miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Le kunyiɛg nan ŋmiɛ, ke kuŋaluŋin la be nnyum a sisiigin, le u ma la be lijɔɔl n-yaa paab u binba.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: le u nan kan ki miin u bɔɔnɔɔbin be gbaar le ki kur ke kuŋaluŋ n jo, bi nan too nfaam le. Le u fii ki choon nnyum bu ki chaa bi ban kutaayɔg. Waah tan mal n-yonn man nin, le ki ŋa ke u yin jo jo bi ban le-n.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Le bi kan u ki miin u choon nnyum bu le, le bi nyi ke u ye utakpiir le, le ki nin yigin papaab.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: bi biɛn nan kan wa, le bijawaam choo bi takpem. Libol nmɔn le u bei bi ke, Ni che ni sufu li pɔɔu ke ni ye min le; ni laa san bijawaami.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Le ki kɔɔ kpiɛ bi bu kuŋaluŋ nmɔn yaan; le ntafaamin jir ki ŋmin soon. Le ni ŋa bi ligaril takpem.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: nan bu-n, baa laa nan biɛ boroboro-n a taai. Bi tuli le nan pɔɔ.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Baah tan nyan limɔgilin yaan wee ki don ligungonl a paab, Genesareti a tiŋin ni, le bi lol bi ŋaluŋ ki siin.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Niin a ninfob kaah nan kan Yisa nan, bi nan biɛ u le weem.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: le ki nan gu ki kɔɔ kitiŋ nmɔn a logi biɛn ki tug bibaarib ki jɔɔ chaan Yisa ban. Bi gbir ke u be nan yaan biɛn ke, bi tug bibaarib bi wan dɔɔn waŋ bu le ki chaan u ban.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Ni ye u nyan kitiŋ kan yaan bee ntinbum biɛn nin ke, bininfob jɔɔ bibaarib le ki too u nsan bu hali nin nnyamin na, ki balin u ke un le che bibaarib n maa u daala a gbaam, le ban biɛn nan pii u ki maa nmɔn biɛn nan kan kutebuga.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa ji len ke, Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, biba be do kaa li kpo see ke bi nan kan Uwonbɔr a naamin a dam a pɔɔm.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Ni ween iloob daal a bɔɔn, le Yisa nan ta Piita nin Jemisi nin Jɔnn ki don lijɔɔl fufɔgil a paab, bi binba. Iyaayoo le bi kan u gbanann kaah kpenn.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: le u wan yee waŋ nyagin ki peen parr. Uba kaa be kutingbɔŋ nan yaan ki li fir ŋunn tiwann yɔɔr wee tin peen man ni.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Le bi kan Elaja nin Moziz kaah degir u bu ki pag nin u.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Le Piita bei Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn, ni ŋan nin taah be do nan. Ti li ŋa tinanbonn titaa, kuba ye a yaag, le kuba mu ye Moziz yaag, le kan gurin mu ye Elaja yaag.

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Bijawaam kaah nan choo Piita nin u kpiɛtiibin bu le che waa ji nan nyi waah li pag man ni.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le kitilangbɔŋ nan piin bi bu, le bi gbir likukɔl kitilangbɔŋin a paab ke, N bijiɛkaag sɔ, ni chee u mɔba man.

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Le bi gbiɛ lig, le kaa ji kan uba bi ban ni. See Yisa a binba.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Baah nan nyan lijɔɔl nmɔn a paab ki nan chaa jiirin, le Yisa nan kpaan ke bi laa bei uba nan biɛn wee bi kan nin ki tan kpaa lidaal man wee u ma Uninfosaal a Big na li fii nkumin nan.

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Le bi chee u kpaani nmɔn, ki ŋmag tibɔr nmɔn bi sisiigin. Le bi baan tab ke, Nkum a fiim a taai ye nii.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Le bi nan baan u ke, Ba ŋa wee ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim bei ke see ke Uwonbɔr a linlenu Elaja le yin chinn ki dan naa.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ibamɔn see ke Elaja chinn, ke un nan mann tiwann yɔɔr biɛn nbamɔm. Ba ŋa wee ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Uninfosaal a Big na li ji fala, wee bin yii waa?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: N bei ni le nan, Elaja pii foon na, le bininfob ŋaa u yɔli yɔli baah ban man, ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin man nin.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Yisa nin Piita nin Jemisi nin Jɔnn kaah nan tan foo u bɔɔnɔɔbin ban n-yonn man nin, ki miin bininfob lagin; le ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin far bi limɔkpel.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn kaah nan kan Yisa n-yonn man nin, le ni ŋa bi ligaril, le bi guun dan ki nan poor u.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Le u baan u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, Ni niɛ bi babuu.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Le uja uba nyan kuninfogbaluŋin yaan ki jiin ke, Wan wɔɔn, min le ta n bija dan a ban, kinaanbiig le be u ban ki che waa fin pagi.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Nan yaan biɛn wee kinaanbiigin nan fii ke, ki nyiɛr u tum le, le kumɔsangbugbalug nya u mɔnn le u ŋma u nyii. Le u gbanann n pɔɔg kenken; le n balin a bɔɔnɔɔb nan ke, bin nyan kinaanbiig nmɔn le bi walin gur.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Iyaayoo le Yisa ni nan fu bi bu ki ke, Nim daandaan nan a ninfob ni yada waar pam, ni ban n yuur ni ban ki tan kpaan nii, wee ki li jɔɔ limɔril ni buu? Ta kibig nmɔn ki dan do.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Le bi ta ki dan Yisa ban. Kinaanbiigin kaah kan Yisa n-yonn man nin, le ki fii kibigin bu, ki nyiɛ u tum, le u dɔ ki bilin, le kumɔsangbugbalug nya u mɔnn.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Le Yisa baan u baa ke, Ibayoo wee ni piin ki ŋaa u man naa? Le u jiin ke Ni piin u buwaan nin le.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: N-yonn takpem le kinaanbiig nmɔn nyiɛr u nŋmi-n, nin nnyumin, ki yin ku u. Ni ye a li fir terim ke, a sam kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, Nn, ni ye a mu li fir ŋam yada ke, niba kaa pɔɔ ki sei wan ŋam yada ban ni.

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Libol nmɔn le uja-n paa yigin papaab ki ke, N ŋaŋ yadau, terim ni wee n ŋaŋ yada ki kpiɛ.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan ke bininfobin nan mann tir ki chaan bi ban ni, le u tiɛn kinaanbiig nbu ke, Fin kinaanbiig tukpaabig nan, min le seiŋ bimɔb ke a nya u ni ki laa ji nyi ki jirin u ni jigin ni.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Le kinaanbiig nmɔn nan yigin ki yuur kibigin ki nyiɛ tum gbab, le ki nyan u ni, le kibig nmɔn ni nan nann ke ki kpo le-n, le bininfobin ni nan fabin ki ke, Tɔ, u kpou.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Le Yisa ta u nɔɔ ki choo kibig nmɔn ki ter u le u fii sir.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Yisa kaah nan kɔɔ iniin nmɔn n-yonn man nin, le u bɔɔnɔɔb nin baan u bi binba ke, Ba ŋa wee tim kaa bi fir nyan kinaanbiig nmɔn naa.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Naa li fir nyan kinaanbiig nan a bosu; see ke ni miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn nyan niin ki nɔɔ Galilii a tingbɔŋin. Amaa waa nan ban uba n biɛ waah be nan yaan ni.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: ki wɔɔn u bɔɔnɔɔbin ni, le ki bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, Bi na li ta Uninfosaal a Big ki ŋubin bininfob a nɔɔn le bin ku u, amaa ni ween itaa daal, u li fii nkumin.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin kaa biɛ u bɔr nmɔn a taai, le ki mu sin san bijawaam nin bin baan.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah nan foo Kapeniɛm ki tan kɔɔ iniin nin n-yonn man nin, le u baan bi ke, Ni bi pag baa a bɔr nsan nin naa.

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Le bi ŋmin, nan bu-n nsan bi bi far tab limɔkpel le, tininkper bu.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Le u nan jir ki kal, ki che u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee ka ki mal u, le u bei bi ke, Wan biɛn ban u li ye uyudaan ke, see ke u ta uba ki kpann ulanjiin ni biɛn ban, le ki ye ni biɛn nan a yomu.

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Le u ta kibig kiba siin bi sisiigin, ki ta u nɔɔ yuur kibig nmɔn. Le ki bei bi ke.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: Wan biɛn gaa kibig nan a bosu n yinl bu ke, min le u gaa nmɔn; wan mu gaa min ke, waa gaa nbinba, amaa u gaa wan tunimin mu jigin na.

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Tɔ, le Jɔnn bei Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn, ti kan uja uba mu kaah jɔɔ a yinl ki nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin, le ti fuun u bu ke un ta jiɛn na, waah kaa ye ti ni uba-n bu.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni laa ŋɔɔ wi, wan yin n yinl ki tun ligaril a toon ke, ni bɔɔn bu waa ji li fir ki pag niba ki bii mi.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Wan biɛn kaa kɔn nin ti ke, u si ti bɔɔn le.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, wan biɛn sei ni nnyum n yinlin, naah ye Masiya a ninfob n bu ke, u daan kaa na li nyi ki lann u yɔɔri.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Le Yisa ji nan mann len ke, Wan che nbuwaab ban ŋa min yada nan a ni uba ta n bɔr gab ke, bi bi ta linaal takpenl ki lol jɔn u niɛn wee ki tir u jaa limɔgilin ke ni bi sɔ.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Mambu, ni ye a nɔɔ li che a yiim ke, a gaa mi ki gen na. Ni ŋan nin uninfo li jɔɔ nnɔɔ nba ki tan kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm ki jon uninfo li jɔɔ inɔɔ ilee wee ki tan kɔɔ nŋmi-n man kaa jɔɔ njunim nan yaan na.

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Ni ye a taal li yin che a yiim ke, a ga mi ki gen ki ta gaba, ni ŋan nin uninfo li jɔɔ litaal liba wee ki kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm ki jon nin u li jɔɔ itaa ilee wee ki tan kɔɔ nŋmi man kaa jɔɔ njunim nan yaan na.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: A numbil mu li ti ji yin che a yiim ke, a logir mi ki nyan na, ni ŋan nin uninfo li jɔɔ nnumbim nba ki tan kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a niin a naamin ki jon nin u li jɔɔ inunbil ilee wee ki tan kɔɔ nŋmi man kaa jɔɔ njunim n-yaan na.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Nin yaan le inaasanbil yan ŋma bi nan kaa yin kpo, le nŋmi nmɔn mu kaa yin junn ni.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Nŋmi le Uwonbɔr na yin ta ki mann uninfo biɛn kasi ke ucha kaah jɔɔ nŋmi ki mann tikur man nin.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: N-yaam mɔ; amaa n-yaam a mɔm nan sagir ke, a yin ŋa nii wee min ji mɔŋaa? Ni che ni binbem le be ke n-yaamin, wee nmɔn li be nim nin ni tab a sisiigin.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb kaah nan nan mal Jerusalam, ki foo ntim man wee bi yi mi ke, Befeg nin Betani nan, mi nan tab lijɔɔl lan wee bi yi ke Olif a Siigi a Jɔɔl nle, le u tun u bɔɔnɔɔb bilee, ki bei bi ke.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: Ni li chaa ntim man be ni nintogil nan, ni tan foo ke, ni li kan kiboŋ kan wee uba kaa la nyi jig ki, wee ki lol nkaafiin nin. Ni lor ki li jɔɔn n ban do.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Ni ye uba baan ni ke ba ŋa wee ni lor kiboŋ nmɔn ke, ni bei u ke, Uyudaan li ban ki, u li jiin ki weem ni.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Le bi sin jon ki tan kan kiboŋ lol iniin iba a kaafiin. Baah sin lor ki n-yonn man nin.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: le bininfob biba nan si niin ki baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni lor kiboŋ nmɔn naa.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Le bi bei bi Yisa kaah bi len man nin, le bininfob nmɔn jir ŋmin, le bi lor ki jon.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Baah nan ta kiboŋ nmɔn ki foo Yisa ban n-yonn man nin, le bi ta biwan yeewaŋ ki paan kiboŋ nmɔn bu, le Yisa jig.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Le bininfob takpem nan jɔɔ bi kinken ki yar nsan bu, le biba mu par tifasɔnn ki ter nsan.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Ban nan liir Yisa a sam nan nin ban nan paa u bɔɔn nan, bi nan yigin le ki ke, Jilima daan! Uwonbɔr a kaar be wan dan u yinl nmɔn nan bu.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Uwonbɔr a kaar biɛ li be ti yaaja Defid a naam man chaan nbu. A ji ye jilima daan le paab.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Le Yisa foo Jerusalam ki kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan, le ki lig tiwann biɛn; naah nan na yin bɔŋ n bu, le u ma nin u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee nyan ki jir Betani a tiŋin.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Ni nan won kutaag le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nyan Betani ki jir Jerusalam; le nkom nan jɔɔ u.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Le u nan kan bikinkam kaah jɔɔ tifasɔnn ki si nan fɔg. Le u jon busub nmɔn a taatum ke u ta li kan iloon, amaa u nan tan foo kaa kan niba, see tifar a binba, nan bu-n ikinkan a saŋi kaa la nan foo.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Le u bei busub nmɔn ke, Uba kaa ji li nyi ki ji a loon yi, la nyan dinan ki chaa. U bɔɔnɔɔbin nan gbir u mɔboi nmɔn na.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Le bi nan nan foo Jerusalam; le Yisa jon ki tan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan, ki ŋɔɔr ban kɔɔr nin ban daag biɛn ki nyan, ki tir ban kpenn ilig a teebul ki gabir, ki liɛr ban nan kɔɔr ilanjel nan a jali ki gabir.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: waa nan chee ke uba n ji tu niba ki jo iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn yaan ni.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: le ki nan bei bininfobin ke, Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, u ma Uwonbɔr len ke, Bi li yi N niin ke bininfob biɛn a wonmiɛl a niin ; le nim ta ŋa ban feen a kikaabol. .

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Le kumanug a yudam nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nan gbir waah ŋa man; le ki ni nan ban nsan man wee bi li ku u, le ki sin san u bijawaam, u wɔɔnim nan ŋa kuninfogbaluŋin ligaril le takpem.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Kijɔɔg kaah nan nan foo n-yonn man nin, le u ma nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan nyan Jerusalam a tiŋin.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Njakpanyiɛn jigin, le bi nɔɔ nsan ki chaa, le ki kan bikinkamin koor le nin ki siboi ki si.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Piita kaah tiɛr Yisa kaah won len man nin, le u bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn, lig, ŋaah won poo bimɔb ki ŋa bikinkam ban ni koora.

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni ŋa Uwonbɔr yadau man.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, wan biɛn bei lijɔɔl takpenl nan ke lin fii ki tan lu limɔgil takpenl nan yaan, wee kaa biɛn u sufu ni ke, wee ki ŋa yada ke waah len man nan li tun ke, ni sin li tun ki sei u.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Ni ma bu le n bei ni ke, ni li jɔɔ yada ni sufu ni ke naah miɛ ki ban niwann nan ni nmɔn ni pii gaa niwann nmɔn na, le ki sin li kan.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Ni ye ni yin miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, wee ki tiɛr ke uba tun ki bii ni ke, jiɛn per wa, wee ni baa wan be paab nan mu li jiɛn ni toonbir per ni.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn yaan ti nan jirin Jerusalam, le u kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin n-yaa poolin ki choon gɔnn. Le kumanug a ninkpeeb nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninkpeeb dan u ban.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: ki nan baan u ke, Ŋma seiŋ npɔɔn man wee a jɔɔ tun nan biɛn nan naa? Ŋma seiŋ npɔɔn nmɔn naa.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni che n baan ni nbinbaan nba chinchinn. Ni ye ni li fir ki jiin n binbaan nmɔn ke, n mu li wɔɔn ni maah kan npɔɔn nmɔn nan yaan wee ki jɔɔ tun ni niɛ nan biɛn.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Jɔnn kaah nan fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin, Uwonbɔr le nan sei u npɔɔn bee uninfosaalaa? Ni beim ni.

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Le bi jir ki tan gbeer tab ki ke, Ti len ke, Uwonbɔr le sei u npɔɔn nmɔn ke, u li baan ti ke, ke ba ŋa wee taa ŋa u yadaa;.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: ke ti ji li fir bei ke bininfob le nan sei u npɔɔn naa.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Mambu, le bi jiin Yisa ke, Taa nyi. Le u mu bei bi ke, Tɔ, n mu kaa yin bei ni wan seim npɔɔn man wee n jɔɔ ki tun nan ni.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa gaa libɔŋal ke, Uja uba le nan kon u dikpaab, le ki ŋuun ikunkon ki gɔɔn bi, le ki mann lidiboopal, le ki maa libinbinl lan kper ke wan guu-n li ka li paab ki guu bukpaab nmɔn. Le ki yin bikpaab biba ki ta bukpaabin ki ŋa bi nɔɔn ke bi li guu. I nan foo nchiɛm ke bin chiɛ sei u ni wee ki mu chiɛ bi yaan. Ni bɔɔn bu le u yaar ki jon kitiŋ kiba.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Idi a chiɛm kaah nan foo-n, le u tun u yomu ke un tan gaa idi u toontum n ban ni, le u jon.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Le bitoontumin tan choo u ki boo u nbamɔm, ki ŋɔɔr u gab le u kun yɔli.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Le ukpadaan nmɔn ji tun uyom uba, le bitoontumin ji tan fa u yul ki chib, ki jin u ifa.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Le ukpadaan nin ji mann tun uyom uba bi ban. Le bi tan ku u ma. Ni bɔɔn bu le u ji saar tun biyom takpem bi ban, le bi ji tan saar tun bi mu man, ki boo bibam, ki sin ku bibam.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Ukpadaan nmɔn a bijiɛkaag le ni nan gur u ban, le u tun u bitoontum nmɔn ban, ki ke, Bi li jiin bi ba ki sei n tɔɔl a bija nmɔn.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Le bitoontum nmɔn bei tab ke, U faajiru sɔ, che ti ku u, wee lifaal nmɔn n kpann ti yaal.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Le bi choo u ki ku ki ta u nyan nkpagbaan ki tan gab.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Yisa kaah pag man ki diɛ n-yonn man le u baan bi ke, Ni tiɛr ukpadaan nan ni yin ŋa niin? U yin dan le ki nan ku bitoontumin, le ki ta bukpaabin ki ŋa bitoontum biba a nɔɔn.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Naa la nyi ki karin naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin man n naa? Ni sɔb ke, Kudaag kan wee bitanmaab yii ki n ni, ki ma nan ye kijaantelig ŋaŋ.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: Uwonbɔr le ŋa man Le ni ŋa ti ligaril. .

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Yisa kaah pag man ki diɛ n-yonn man nin, le Juu yaab a yudam biɛ ke u ga libɔŋalin ki jiin bi paam le, le bi ban bin choo u, le ki sin san bininfobin bijawaam; ni bu le bi ta u ki jiɛn ki sin yaar.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Le bi nan tun Farasii yaab biba nin Ubɔr Herɔd a nib biba Yisa ban ke bin nan pen u lam.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Le bi nan foon ki baan u ke, Wan wɔɔn, ti nyi ŋaah pag ibamɔn, kaa san bijawaam nin bininfob kaah li tiɛr man ni. A biɛ wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a sam nin ibamɔn, le a baga kaa be nin uninfo kaah ye wan ni. Mambu, bei ti ni, ni ŋan nin ti yɔ lampo ki sei Rom a tiŋ a bɔru Siiza, bee ti laa yɔɔ.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Le Yisa biɛ ke bi ban bin pen u lam le, le ki jiin bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni ban ni kɔɔnim libiginlin naa? Li jɔɔn biligib nmɔn wee n lig.

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Le bi ta sei u. Le u baan bi ke, Ŋma a nunaŋ nin u yinl be biligib nmɔn buu? Le bi bei u ke, Siiza.

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Tɔ, ni ta nan ye Siiza yaan ki sei Siiza u, ki ta nan ye Uwonbɔr yaan ki sei Uwonbɔr a. Le ni gar bi takpem u jiim nmɔn bu.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Le Sadusi yaab biba nan dan u ban. Ban len ke binib kaa yin fii nkumin ni, le ki bei ke.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: Wan wɔɔn, Moziz nan sɔb nsiim ki ke, Uja kpo wee ki jɔɔ upii wee kaa ma kibig ke, ni ŋan u na a big n ta lipukɔɔlin ki maa nbuwaab sei utakpiirin.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Tɔ, bijab bilile le nan be ki ye tinabir, le uninkpelin kɔɔn u pii ki kpo kaa ma nbuwaabi.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Le wan pa u bu ta lipukɔɔl nmɔn, le ki mu kpo kaa ma bigi; le wan kpiɛ bita n mu ta upii nmɔn, le ki mu nan kpo, kaa ma kibigi.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Man le ban biɛn pa nmɔn kɔɔn upii nmɔn, le ki nan kpo, bi ni u ba kaa ma kibig nin upii nni. Ni bɔɔn bu, le upii nmɔn mu nan kpo.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Tɔ, bitakpiib a fii daal, bi ŋmiɛ na yin ye upii nmɔn naa? Nan bu-n bi bilile nmɔn biɛn kɔɔn wa.

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Naa nyi niba. Nan che naa nyi nmɔn ye nan le. Naa nyi Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋi, kaa ji nyi waah jɔɔ npɔɔn man ni.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Bitakpiib nan fii nkumin ke, bi na yin be le ke malakam kaah be Uwonbɔr a niin man nin; lipiikɔɔnl nin lijakunl kaa na yin be.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: Ki jiin nkum a fiim paam, naa nyi ki karin Uwonbɔr a linlenu Moziz a sɔbim n naa, naa nan pag ki jiin kujagorug a bɔr n naa? Ni sɔb ke Uwonbɔr nan bei Moziz ke u ma Uwonbɔr le ye Abiraham a weem, nin Azig a weem, nin Jekɔb a weem.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyan iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan n-yonn man nin, le u bɔɔnɔɔru uba bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn, lig tiditakpenn nan, nin itangbag takpen yan wee bi ta maa tidir nmɔn.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Waa ye ban kpo a weemi, u ye ban fo a weem le. Ni jɔɔ kuŋmaruŋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Le u jiin u ke, Ni kan tiditakpenn nmɔn waa? Bi na yin tir ti biɛn le ki jaa tum le tangbagil liba kaa ji na li gur ki pa li kpiɛg bu.

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Tɔ, ban wɔɔnn nsiim nmɔn a ni uba nan be niin ki gbir Yisa nin Sadusi yaab nan a bɔr pagilin, ki kan Yisa kaah jiin ibinbaan ni nbamɔm man. Ni bu le u dan Yisa ban, ki mu nan baan u ke, Ke nsiim mi la ba le ye nsiim man pɔɔ.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Le Yisa nan jon ki tan ka Olif a siigi a jɔɔl n-yaa paab, li nan be iwonjiɛn niin n-yaa nintogil le; le Piita nin Jemisi nin Jɔnn nin Anduru nan dan u ban, ki baan u ke.

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Le u jiin u ke, Nsiim man pɔɔ ye nan le: Iziral yaab, ni pelim man, ti Yudaau Uwonbɔr ye Uwonbɔr uba le.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: Bei ti ni, iba yoo wee ni na yin ŋa man naa? Ke ba a siɛra nan yin wɔɔn ke isaŋ foo nin ni yin gbeen naa.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Li jiɛ u nin a sufu biɛn, nin a naaŋ biɛn, nin a potiɛl biɛn, nin a pɔɔm biɛn na.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni li guu ni ba man, ki laa che uba n lar ni.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Nsiim man pɔɔ ki kpiɛ milee ye nan le: Li jiɛ a tau ke ŋaah jiɛ a ba man ni. Nsiim nba kaa ji be ki jon nsiim milee nan ni.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Bininfob takpem le na yin dan ki ta n yinl ki yin bi ba ke bi ma ye min Yisa, ki lar bininfob takpem.

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Le wan wɔɔnn nsiim nmɔn bei Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn, n pooriŋ. A pag ibamɔn le, ke ti Yudaau Uwonbɔr ye Uwonbɔr uba le, le Nween nba kaa ji be ki kpiɛ u bu.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ni na li gbir ke bininfob tog tab bitɔb nan mal nin dindanl; amaa ni laa san bijawaami. Ni kpaan le ke ni niɛ nan biɛn ye ni tun amaa naa ye dulinya a fiim le yen ni.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: ke uninfosaal li jiɛ Uwonbɔr nin u sufu biɛn, nin u potiɛl ke, ki ji li jiɛ u tau ke waah jiɛ u ba man nin ke. Ni ŋan ki jon nin uninfo n ta tiwankor ki mann kumanug ki nyɔɔ nŋmi sei Uwonbɔr a.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Libol nin libol na li kɔn nin tab; le nnaam miba a ninfob nin mitɔ na li to tab bitɔb. Kutingbɔŋ biɛn na li dam. Le nkom na li be. Ni na yin be le ke nmarwiɛn a piinim.

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan ke u jiimin jɔɔ nlamin, le u bei u ke, Ŋaa fɔg nin Uwonbɔr a niin a naami. Ni niɛ nan a bɔɔn uba kaa ji nan jɔɔ sufu nin un baan Yisa nbinbaan ni.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Ni li guu ni ba man. Bi na li choo ni ki ta ni jo bibɔjib ban ki boo ni tiwonjiɛndirin. Min bu ni na li sir ntim a ninkpeeb nin bibɔrtiib a nintogil, ki bei bi n bɔŋann ni.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Yisa kaah nan be wɔɔn bininfob iwonjiɛn niin n ni, le u nan baan bi nbinbaan ke, Ba ŋa wee ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin ke Masiya n ye Defid a yaabil lee?.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: Bi na li mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn ki sei libol biɛn. Le n-yonn nmɔn ni foo.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Kasi a Naaŋ le nan gbeen Defid, le u ke, Uwonbɔr nan bei n Yudaau ke, kalim n nɔɔjiim paam hali nin maah na li che a dataam nkpann a taai a tɔɔn bol.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Bi nan ta ni ki jon bibɔjib ban ke, ni laa che ni mug ni naah li pag man ni, n-yonn nmɔn nan foo ke, Kasi a Naaŋ li wɔɔn ni naah li pag man, tibɔr tan wee ni na yin pag nmɔn kaa na yin ye ni baantɔɔl a bɔri, ni na yin nya Kasi a Naaŋin ban le.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Defid baantɔɔl kaah nan yi Masiya ke u yudaau nan, ke ni ŋa niin wee Masiya ye Defid a yaabil laa? Bininfob takpem le nan pelig Yisa a wɔɔmin nin npopeen.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, uninfo na li gbii u baantɔɔl a na big bu ki che bin ku u, bibaam mu na yin ŋa man le ki sei bi tɔɔl a buwaab, le nbuwaab mu na yin kɔn nin bibaam le, ki che bin ku bi.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Waah nan wɔɔn bi nan, le ki bei bi ke, Ni li nyi ban wɔɔnn ni Uwonbɔr a siim nan a binbem bu man; bi ma ban bi li yee daala fufɔgir ki choon, ki ban ke bininfob li poor bi kinyaŋin.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Le bininfob biɛn na yin nan ni le naah nɔɔm n bu. Wan mu nan fir ki jin fala nmɔn ki tan diɛ ke, u yaa daan mu li kan nŋmarim.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: le bi kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin ke, bi ban bin kal bininkpeeb a kikaabol le; bi ji jon kuchiigin mu ke, bi ban bin kal biyudam a kikaabol le;.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Ni na li kan liwanbiril kaah si nan yaan kaa bi ŋan nin li n sir nii ni. . Iyaayoo nmɔn, ban na yin be Judiya a tingbɔŋin ni, bin guun ki do ijɔɔ a paab.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: bi ma ji ipukɔɔ dulim, le ki ji jirin ki nan miɛ Uwonbɔr ki yuur, ki ban bininfob n kan bi ki ke bi ye bininfoŋam le. Bi tudaril na li bir pam.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Wan mu nan be u diig a paab ke, u laa jiir ki ke u yin kɔɔ ndiin le u tan yuur niba.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Yisa nan ka iwonjiɛn niin le ki mal Uwonbɔr a piini a yiigin, ki lig bininfob kaah kpeen ilig man. Le bijɔɔndam takpem nan kpeen ilig takpem.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Wan mu nan be bukpaabin ke, u laa ji jir un tan yuur u daala.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: le kipukɔɔlanjiimig mu nan kpeen koobu ilee.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn bipiipoodam nin ban jɔɔ nbuwaab ban la mɔɔ na li jɔɔ kunyunyunbaag takpem.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Le Yisa yin u bɔɔnɔɔbin ki bei bi ke, Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, kipukɔɔlanjiimig nan kaah ta u piini yan kpeen kiyiig nan yaan nan, jon ipiin yan biɛn be niin nin na.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Miɛ Uwonbɔr ke un laa che ni ŋa kufaag a yoo.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: Nan bu-n, bi biɛn nan nyan bi piini nmɔn bi jɔɔmin le, amaa kipukɔɔlanjiimig nan ma ta u faal biɛn le sei.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah nan naan dulinya ki nan kpaa daandaan nan, uba kaa la nyi ki jin fala wan na yin be iyaayoo nmɔn ni, le fala uba mu kaa li nyi ki dan ki nann man ni.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Ni ye Uyudaan kaa war fala a dam nmɔn wee i yin yab man ke, uba kaa na li ŋmari, amaa u ninfogann kaabin a nyunyunbasanl bu, u li war fala a daai nmɔn a yabim.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le uninfo uba nan bei ni ke, Lig, Masiya nmɔn sɔ, ki ji bei ni ke, U be nin yaan ke, ni laa chee waah len man nmɔn ni.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Bilinlarib na li be. Biba mu na li bei ke bi ye Masiya le. Biba mu na li len ke bi ye Uwonbɔr a linlem le. Bi na li tun ligaril a toonl nin ligaril a siɛra ki pɔɔn bi ba ki lar Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: N pii bei ni niwann biɛn na, ke ni yoo kaa la foo. Ni li guu ni ba man.

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Amaa iyaayoo, limugil nan a bɔɔn, nween na li bɔŋ, le uŋmal kaa ji yin ween ni.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: le iŋmabil na yin nya papaab le ki lur, le nan be paab biɛn na li dam dam.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn bi li kan Uninfosaal a Big kaah degirin kitilangbɔŋin nin npɔɔn nin nween takpen.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, u na yin tun malakam le kutingbɔŋ nan yaan biɛn, liwonpɔɔnl nin liwonlul, nin nnɔɔgan, nin nnɔɔjiin, wee bin lagin Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaabin bogiliba.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Ni ta bikinkam ki tinn nlama man, naah ti nan kan busub nmɔn be bun tifar ke, ni biɛr ke kusiɛg foon.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Man le ni nan kan ni niɛ nan biɛn tun ke, ni biɛ ke ni malin na, naa ji li yuuri.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, daandaan nan a ninfob kaa yin kpo biɛn ni le nan be nan n tun.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Papaab nin tum na li jo, amaa n bɔr nan kaa li nyi ki jo.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Uba kaa nyi lidaal nmɔn ni bee n-yonn man wee n li jirin ni. Malakam ban be Uwonbɔr a niin nan kaa nyi, min Uwonbɔr a Bija mu kaa nyi; see Uwonbɔr a binba le nyi.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Ni li guu man, ki li ka lig naah kaa nyi lidaal man wee n na yin jirin ni bu.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: Ni na yin be le ke uja uba kaah yin nya u niin ki jo nsan man fɔg a dann le, ki bei u buwaab ke bi li ŋmag iniin ni nbamɔm le ki por bi biɛn nan nin bi toon, ki sin bei u niin guurin ke u li guu wee ki sin yaar jon u samin le.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Ni mu li guu man, nan bu-n naa nyi lidaal man wee ni yudaau yin jirin ni, niba ke kujɔɔg, bee kunyiɛgtasiig, bee kutaayɔg, bee njakpiin.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Ni ye u nan jirin weem ke, naa ŋan ni u nan kan ni n miin ni jiɛn ni.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: Mambu, maah bei ni man nan, man le n bei uninfo biɛn, ni li guu man.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Le Yisa nan nyan ki chaa Judiya a tingbɔŋin ki tan don Jɔdann a mɔgilin. Le bininfob takpem nan lagin u ban, le u yaan ti nan wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr a bɔr ke waah pii ti wɔɔn man nin.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Le Farasii yaab dan u ban ki ban bin mag u lig, le ki baan u ke, Nsiim chee sei ke uja n yii u poo.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Le u mu baan bi ke, Moziz nan sei ni nsiim nii ki jiin ni paamaa.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Le bi ke, Moziz nan wɔɔn ti ke uja li fir sɔb lipiiyiil a gbɔŋ ki yii u poo.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni tukangbir bu le nan che Moziz nan sɔb nsiim nmɔn ki sei ni.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr kaah nan naan dulinya npiinim ni, U nan naan uja nin upii le.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Mambu, le uja li jiɛn u baa nin u na le ki kɔɔn upii.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: wee binib bilee n lagin ki li ye tigbanann tiba, baa ji yin ye bininfob bilee, amaa bi ni yin ye uninfo uba le.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Kumanug a ninkpeeb, nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninkpeeb, nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim, nin Juu yaab a ninkpeeb biɛn nan lagin njakpanyiɛn, ki lol ilol, le ki baan Yisa tikulor ki ta u jon kitiŋ nmɔn a bɔr ninkpelu wan wee bi yi u ke Palati n ban.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Mambu, ban wee Uwonbɔr ta bi ki lagin ni, uninfosaal li nyi un choo bi ki bɔgir.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Le Palati baan u ke, Fin le sin ye Juu yaab a bɔru win naa? Le Yisa jiin ki ke, Ke ŋaah len man ni.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Baah nan nan jirin iniin n-yonn man nin, le u bɔɔnɔɔb nan baan u bɔr nmɔn a taai.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Le kumanug a ninkpeeb nmɔn pag ki bii u takpem.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Uja wan yii u poo wee ki kɔɔn upii uba ke, u tun iwanjaab toon na, waah kɔɔn upii ki kpiɛ u bu-n bu.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Le Palati ji baan u, Ŋaa yin jiin tiba, pelim baah pag ki biiŋ man.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Man mu jigin, upii yii u chal wee ki tan kun uja uba ke, u mu tun iwanjaab toon na.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Le Yisa le yii kaa jiin ni, le ni gar Palati.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Le bininfob biba nan ta bi buwaab ki dan Yisa ban, ki ke un ta u nɔɔ ki paan bi bu-u, amaa le u bɔɔnɔɔb fu bi bu.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Yuglagir a chiig a jim a yonn biɛn, Palati ti nyan uninfo uba le kuloondiigin, wan wee bininfobin ban, ki jaa.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Waah nan kan bi tann bi bu-n ni, le u ŋuul nan fii bi bu, le u bei bi ke, Ni che nbuwaab nmɔn n dan n ban ni, ki laa ŋɔɔ bi, nbuwaab nan kaah be man nan Uwonbɔr a niin a naamin ye bi yaan le.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn uja uba nan be kuloondiigin, le u yinl ye Barabasi. U ma nin bininfob biba le nan be kuloondiigin, bi mɔyiil lan wee bi nan jɔɔ kitiŋin yaan wee ki ku bininfobin bu.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, wan kaa gaa Uwonbɔr a naam ke kibigin ke, waa li kɔɔ mi ni.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Mambu le kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn nan lagin ki miɛ Palati ke un tun ke waah pii ti tun man ni.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Le ki gaa nbuwaab nmɔn, le ki ta u nɔɔ ki paan paan bi bu, ki kaab jaa bi bu.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ni ban n nyan Juu yaab a bɔru win lee.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyan ni yaan ki chaa nmɔn, le uja uba guun kenn u ki gbaan u nintogil, le ki baan u ke, Wan wɔɔn ŋan, n yin ŋa nii wee ki nan kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm aa.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Nan bu-n u nyi ibamɔn ke kininaŋ bu le kumanug a ninkpeeb ta Yisa ki ŋa u nɔɔn.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Le Yisa jiin ki bei u ke, Ba ŋa wee a yim uninfo ŋan naa? Uba kaa be ki ye uninfo ŋan ni, see Uwonbɔr a binba.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Le kumanug a ninkpeeb nmɔn nan mann pag ki bei kuninfogbaluŋin ke, bin bei u wee un nyan Barabasi sei bi.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: A pii nyi Uwonbɔr a siim kaah wɔɔn man, Laa ku uninfo; laa tun iwanchɔgin toon ni, laa su, laa ji tibɔkar a siɛra ki bii a kpiɛgi; laa ŋal bininfobi, ki li tenn a baa nin a na jilimau.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Le Palati mann baan bi ke, Ke wan wee ni yi u ke Juu yaab a bɔru nan n ŋa u nii.

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Le uja-n bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn, n pii chee ki tun ni niɛ nan n nbuwaan nin le.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Le bi tiɛn nin nmɔpɔɔn ki ke, Kpaa mu lidapunpunl bu.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Le Yisa lig u chimm nin njiɛm, le ki nan bei u ke, Niwann niba kpeŋileŋ li pariŋ; li kun na ki tan kɔɔr ŋaah jɔɔ niwann nan biɛn na, wee ki ta iligin ki sei faladama, wee ki nan kan njɔɔm Uwonbɔr a niin, wee ki ni li nɔɔm.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Le Palati baan bi ke, Babu? U bir jann ye ti laa? Le bi mann tiɛn papaab ki ke, Kpaa mu lidapunpunl bu.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Uja-n kaah nan gbir man nin, le u sufu nan bii takpem, le u nan jir ki kun nin npoobiin, u nan jɔɔ tiwann takpem pam.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Le Palati ban un penn kuninfogbaluŋin a sufu, mambu le u nyan Barabasi ki sei bi, le ki che bi boo Yisa, le ki ta u sei bi ke bin tan kpaa u lidapunpunl bu.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Le Yisa nan gbiɛ lig u bɔɔnɔɔbin, le ki bei bi ke, Ni li pɔɔ pam nin bijɔɔndam n nan kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a niin a naamin.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Le bitɔtogib nmɔn ta Yisa ki kɔɔ kitiŋ nmɔn a bɔru Palati a naakoon , ki yin bi kpiɛtiib biɛn.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: U bɔr nmɔn nan ŋa bi ligaril le; le u ji mann ki bei bi ke, N buwaab, ni pɔɔ pam nin uninfosaal n nan fir kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a niin a naamin.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Le bi choo u wanyeewann pir, ki ta daala maŋ ki yeen u, le ki ta ikunkon ki loo kiyuchigirig ki ta chigin u.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ni pɔɔ paa nin ujɔɔndaan n kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a niin a naamin ki jon nin alakoomi kaah li kɔɔ siɛrig a bolin na.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: le ki nin poor u ke baah ti poor u bɔr man nin, ki ke, Juu yaab a bɔru, a pɔɔ woo.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: U mɔleen nmɔn nan gar bi pam, le bi nin baan tab, Ke ni ye man ke ŋma pii li fir ŋmaraa.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Le ki ta likikagil ki fiɛb u yul, ki siin timɔsann jaar u bu, ki gbaa bi dun bu u titogil.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Le Yisa lig bi ki bei bi ke, Uninfosaal kaa li firi amaa Uwonbɔr ma li fir tun niwann biɛn.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Baah daa u nyɔɔrug ki diɛ nan, le bi choo daala maŋin ki pir le ki jiin u yaa wan yɔɔrin ki yeen u, le ki ta u ki nyan ke bin tan kpaa u lidapunpunl bu.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Le Piita bei u ke, Liga, tim jiɛn niwann biɛn le ki nɔɔŋ.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Baah nan be nsan bu ki chaa n-yonn man nin, le bi kenn u jaa uba le u yinl ye Simɔnn. Le bitɔtogib nmɔn mug u le u gaa Yisa a kum a dapunpunl nmɔn.

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, wan biɛn jiɛn u niin, bee u watiib nin u kpetiib, bee u ninsiɛb, bee u nam, bee u baam, bee u buwaab, bee u kpaab, n yinl bu nin tibɔŋann nin bu ke.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Le bi ta Yisa ki jon nan yaan wee bi yi ke Goligota.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: u na li kan tiwann ke tin jo waah nan jiɛn tiwann tan ni biɛn. U na li kan iniin, nin watiib nin kpetiib, nin ninsiɛb, nin nam, nin nbum, nin tikpaann, a kobig kobig kutingbɔŋ nan yaan, ki biɛ na li kan limugil ki kpiɛ; le n-yonn man chaan nan le un nan kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm nan.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Ni yaan le bininfob biba nan sei u ndaam man ŋmar nin nnyɔg man wee bi yi mi ke miɛr nan, le u yii kaa nyun ni.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Bisanliib na li ŋa bibɔɔnpaab, le bibɔɔnpaab mu na li ŋa bisanliib.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Le bi nin kpaa u lidapunpunl bu, le ki tɔn inaa ki por u wan yɔɔr.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan nyan nsan bu ki do Jerusalam, le u gaa bi sam ki liin, le ni gar bi, le bijawaam mu ji choo kuninfogbaluŋ kan nan pa bi bɔɔn nin. Le u yaan ti nan ta u tooi piig nin bilee-n ki nyan ngbaan, ki wɔɔn bi nan li ŋa u.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Bi nan kpaa u njakpanyiɛn le, tikur tiwiɛ a yonn.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: ke laa, Ti yin do Jerusalam le nan, nan yaan wee bi li ta Uninfosaal a Big ki ŋubin kumanug a yudam nin ban wɔɔnn nsiim a nɔɔn le bin ji u tibɔr ki ke bin ku u, le ki ta u ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan;.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Bi nan sɔb baah ke bi kan u bir tan nmɔn le ki tabin u dapunpunlin bu le ki ke, Juu yaab a bɔru n sɔ.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: le bi li laa u ki sii timɔsann ki ŋaa u ki boo u, ki nan ku u, amaa ni ween itaa daal u li fii nkumin.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Bi nan kpaa binaayigib bilee idapunpun bu ki kpiɛ u bu, le uba be u nɔɔjiim paam, le u kpiɛg mu be u nɔɔgam paam.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Iyaayoo le Jemisi nin Jɔnn, ban nan ye Zebedi a naachim nan, nan dan Yisa ban ki nan bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn, ti ban a tun niba le ki sei ti.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Le ban nyan niin nin chonn ki gugin bi yuli ki sugir u, ki ke, Tɔ, fin le ke a li fir ber iwonjiɛn niin nan ki ji ta iweehn itaa ki maa ki nna.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ni ban n tun ba sei naa.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: Man ke, nyan lidapunpunl nan a paab ki jiirin ki nan fee a ba ki jaau.

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Le bi jiin ki bei u ke, N-yonn man wee a nan jin a naamin ki kan npɔɔn ke, a che ti ni uba n kal a nɔɔjiim paam wee u miɛnin mu n kal a nɔɔgam paam.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Man le kumanug a ninkpeeb nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin mu sugir u, le ki bei tab ke, U ti feen u tab le ki jaar, kaa ji na li fir fee u ba ki jaa.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Le u jiin ki bei bi ke, Naa nyi naah miɛ niwann nan ni. Ni li fir nyu kiyiinyɔɔg kan wee n yin nyu nan naa? Bee ni li fir ki ji fala ke maah yin ji man nan naa?.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Juu yaab a bɔru wan ye Masiya nan, nya lidapunpunl nan a paab ki jirin daandaan nan, wee ti kan ki ŋa u yada. Le ban wee bi nan ta kpiɛ u bu ki kpaa n mu sugir u.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Le bi jiin ki ke, ti li fir. Le u bei bi ke, Ni sin li fir nyu kiyiinyɔɔg nmɔn yaan ki biɛ li fir ji fala ke maah yin ji man ni.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Nwosisiigin, le kutingbɔŋ biɛn nan bɔŋ ki tan kpaa kujɔɔg a kur titaa.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: Amaa naa ye min le li gann wan na yin kal nnɔɔjiim paam bee n nɔɔgami. Uwonbɔr le yin gann ban na yin kal nkaam nmɔn bu.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Ti kur titaa nmɔn a yonn, le Yisa kunn papaab ke, Eloi, Eloi, lema sabagtani? Ni taai le ye ke, N Wonbɔru, n Wonbɔru ba ŋa wee a fɔr ki jiɛn maa.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Le Yisa a tooi piigin kaah gbir man nin, le bi ŋuui fii Jemisi nin Jɔnn bu.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Le ban si niin nin gbir waah yi, le biba ke, Ni pela man u yi Elaja le.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Le Yisa yin bi biɛn ki lagin u ban, ki bei bi ke, N tɔɔl nyi ke bininfob ban yin biba dulinya nan a yudam nan don biba le; le ban mu jɔɔ tininkper nan mu ŋmag bininfob npɔɔn le.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Le bi ni uba guun ki tan yuur chiisɔgsu ki ta ŋa ndaam man mɔɔn nin ki ta sagin kudaag bu, le ki don tan sei u ke un nyu, ki ji len ke, Che ti lig njee Elaja li dan ki nan jiin u lidapunpunl nan a paab bee waa li dan naa.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Amaa ni laa be man ni. Ni ye uba ban tininkper ke, see ke u ta uba ki ŋa ni biɛn nan a yomu;.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Le Yisa mann kunn papaab, le ki nin kpo iyaayoo nmɔn.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Wan mu ban un ŋa uyudaan ke, see ke u ŋa u ba ni biɛn nan a yomu;.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: N-yonn nmɔn le likinkenl lan jɔn ki por iwonjiɛn niin nin choo bɔgir papaab ki tan jaa tum.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Uninfosaal a Big kaa dan ke bininfob n tun ki sei u le, amaa u dan ke u nan tun ki sei bininfob le, le ki ji ta u miɛl ki da bininfob takpem.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Le bitɔb a sanliiru nan si lidapunpunl nmɔn ban ki kan Yisa kaah kpo man nmɔn, le ki ke, Ibamɔn bu, uja nmɔn sin ye Uwonbɔr a Bija le.

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb dan Jeriko, ki nan nan nya, le bi ma nin bininfob takpem nɔɔ; le ujɔɔn uba nan be ki miɛ, u nan ka nsan gbaan le bi yi u ke Batimiyasi, u nan ye Timiyasi a bija le.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Le Meri wan nyan Magidalin nin Meri wan ye Jemisi big nin Josesi a na nan, nin Salomi, mu nan kpiɛ bipiib biba bu le bi si nan fɔg ki lig.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Waah nan nan gbir Yisa wan ye Nazarif a ninfo bu n-yonn man nin, le u yigin papaab ki ke, Yisa, Ubɔr Defid a yaabil, sam kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Bi pii nan nɔɔ Yisa, n-yonn man wee u la nan be Galilii n le, ki ter u. Bipiib tab takpem ban nan nɔɔ Yisa ki foo Jerusalam nan mu nan be niin.

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Le bininfob takpem nan tan u bu ke u ŋmin na. Le u ji mann tiɛn papaab ke, Defid a yaabil, sam kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Ni nan ye sili daal le, ni nan yin wonn njakpanyiɛn likpakɔɔl daal le. Lidaal nmɔn a jɔɔg.

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Le Yisa sir ki bei ke, Ni yin u ni. Le bi yin u, ki bei u ke, Che a sufu li pɔɔu. U yiŋ. Fii sira.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: le Arimatiya a tiŋ a jau wan a yinl ye Josef nan dan. U nan ye Juu yaab a bɔjiru a ninkpelu uba le, ki ye u buchinn nin ibamɔn, ki nan guu Uwonbɔr a naam n-yaa foonim. Mambu, le u nan jon Palati ban kaa san bijawaami ke un ta Yisa a gbanann ki sei u ni.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Le ujɔɔn nmɔn pir u daala takpoŋ ki bil, le ki fii sir, ki dan Yisa ban. Le Yisa baan u ke, A ban n tun ba ki seiŋaa.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Palati kaah pii gbir ke Yisa kpo nan, le ni gar u, le u yin bitɔb a sanliiru ki tan baan u ke, Yisa kaah kpe-n ni yuur waa.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Le u jiin ki ke, N baa, n ban a ligir n num le.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: Waah nan gbir bitɔb a sanliiru nmɔn a mɔnn diɛ, le u yin Josef nmɔn ki nan jaa u ke un tan yuur Yisa a gbanann nna.

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Tɔ, li chaau. Ŋaah ŋaam yada n bu le a num ligir. Libol nmɔn, le u num ligir, le u nɔɔ Yisa, le bi chaa.
