eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Josef pii nan daa kikinpeŋa le ki tan jɔnn Yisa a gbanann lidapunpunl n bu ki ta pɔbin niin, ki ta u piin kukaag kan wee bi nan jiɛ kutanpiig bu, ki ta litangbagtakpenl ki biin ki mɔbin.

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Likpakɔɔl daal kaah nan jon nin, le Meri wan nyan Magidalin, nin Salomi, nin Meri wan nan ye Jemisi a na nan, nan jon ki tan daa tulaali ke bin tan gbir ŋa Yisa a gbanann.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Le Meri wan nan nyan Magidalin nin Meri wan nan ye Josesi a na nan, bi nan kan baah pii Yisa a gbanann nan yaan na.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Nnyantoon a chinchinn a weem daal a jakpanyiɛn, ke nween kaa la pɔɔ, le bi chinn ki fii ki jon kukaag nmɔn yaan.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Baah la nan be nsan bu ki chaan, le bi baan tab ke, Ŋma tan yin bin litangbagil lan leg kukaag nan a mɔb ki sei taa.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Le bi tan mal niin, ki miin likalool nmɔn pii bin ki dɔ li binba le.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Le bi kɔɔ kukaag nmɔn yaan, ki kan unaachipɔɔn ka nnɔɔjiin paam ki yee daala peŋ fufɔbig. Le ni gar bi takpem.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Le unaachipɔɔn nmɔn bei bi ke, Ni laa che nin gar ni man ni. Ni ban Yisa wan ye Nazarif a tiŋ a ninfou wan wee bi nan kpaa u lidapunpunl bu n le. U fii nkumin na. Waa be do.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Ni li jir ki tan bei u bɔɔnɔɔb nin Piita ke u liin bi nintogil ki jon Galilii u; nin yaan le bi ta li kan u ke waah pii nan bei bi man ni.

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Le bipiibin a sufu nan ŋmarin, le bi gbanann jegil, le bi nan nyan kukaag nmɔn yaan, ki guun, le kaa bei uba, nan bu-n bijawaam le nan choo bi.

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Tɔ, Yisa kaah nan fii nkumin, Juu yaab a kpakɔɔl daal kaah jon ki won a jakpanyiɛn nmɔn, u nan chinn ta u ba wɔɔn Meri wan nan nyan Magidalin ni le, wan wee u nan nyan nnaanbiim milile u ni nan.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Le Meri nan jon bininfob ban nan nɔɔ Yisa nan ban. Ban nan jɔɔ npoobiin ki ka ki mɔɔr nan. Le ki bei bi ke u kan Yisa u, u fii nkumin na.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Baah gbir man nin, baa nan ŋa yada.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb bilee nan choon chaa ntingbaan. Le Yisa nan degir ki ta u ba wɔɔn bi.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Le bi jir kitiŋin yaan ki tan bei bi tabin, baah kan Yisa man, le baa ŋa yada.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu jigin le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin uba-n nan ka ki ji tijiir, le u nan degir bi bu, ki fuun bi bu nin bi yada kaah waar man, nin bi tuli kaah pɔɔ. Mambu, nin baah kaa ŋa ban kan waah fii nkumin ni yada-n bu.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Le u ji bei bi ke, Ni li chaa ki tan mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn ki wɔɔn uninfo biɛn, kutingbɔŋ nan yaan biɛn na.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Wan biɛn ŋa yada wee bi fun u Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin ke u li kan nŋmarim; amaa wan kaa ŋa yada ke Uwonbɔr na li bii u.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Amaa ban ŋaam yada nan li tun ligaril a toon; bi na li yin n yinl ki nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin, ki ji pag ileen pɔɔn.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: ni ye bi nan choo iwaa bee bi nan jin bi tijibir ke, naa li ŋa bi niba, bi ji nan ta bi nɔɔi ki paan bibaarib bu ke, bi li kan kutebug.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Tɔ, Yisa kaah nan pag tibɔr nmɔn ki diɛ n-yonn man nin, le Uwonbɔr ta u don U niin ki tan kaan u ma Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Man le u bɔɔnɔɔbin nan jon ki tan be ki mɔɔn u bɔŋann nmɔn ilog biɛn, le ti yudaau nmɔn be nin bi, ki che bi tun ligaril a toon, le bi toon nmɔn wɔɔn siɛra ke bi mɔɔn ni nmɔn ye ibamɔn le.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: Tɔ, iweehn ilee li nan gur nin bin tiɛr yuglagir a chiigin, ke idaa yan wee bi ti jɔɔ ŋma boroboro wan kaa ŋaan nnyɔgin a daai n piin, le kumanug a ninkpeeb nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin, ban baah li nɔɔ nsan man ki woon ki choo Yisa ki ku u, le ki bei tab ke.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Ni laa che ti choo u kuchiig nan yaan ni, n ba ke ti choo u iyaayoo ke, kuninfogbaluŋin li gaa tijar ti bu.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yisa nan be Betani, uja wan nan ye kikɔŋ wee bi yi u ke Simɔnn nan a niin le, ki ji tijiir. Le upii uba nan kɔɔn ki ŋmag tulaali kan jɔɔ kudaag pɔɔrug nan, bi yi tulaali nmɔn ke naar, le bi nan yi kululog nmɔn ke alabasita. Le upii nmɔn choo kululog nmɔn ki biir ki gbir tulaali nmɔn ki ŋa Yisa a yukper.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Le bininfob ban be niin nin pag nin liŋuul ki ke, Ba ŋa wee u bii tulaali nmɔn man naa?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Ti bi li fir kɔɔr ki kan ilig ke yin jo libinl a yɔɔr, ki ta ter bilanjiim nan. Le ki nan ŋulin u bu.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni jiɛn upii nmɔn na. Ni wan ŋanl le u tun ki seim nan, ke ba ŋa wee ni mugir u man naa?.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Bilanjiim le yin gbaa be ni ban le iyoo biɛn, le ni li fir ki ter bi n-yonn man wee ni ban; amaa min kaa yin gbaa be ni ban jigin ni.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Upii nmɔn tun nan wee u li fir tun seim le. Waah gbir tulaali wan ŋa n gbanann bu nan, u ŋaam ngbanann a piim a sili le nmɔn.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, nan yaan biɛn wee bi li mɔɔn n bɔŋann nin kutingbɔŋ biɛn nan yaan, bi li mɔɔn upii nan kaah ŋa nan ki seim nan bu, le ni li ye ntiɛrim ki jiin u bu.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Man le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee a ni u ba, wan wee bi yi u ke Judasi Isikariya nan, jon kumanug a ninkpeeb ban ke un kɔɔr Yisa.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Le bi pooi nan penn, waah bei bi man Yisa bu-n, le bi siin u limɔsiinl ke bi li yɔ ilig ni bu. Le u ni nan piin ki ban isan yan wee u li ta Yisa ki ŋubin bi nɔɔn.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Chinchinn a daal lan wee bi ti ŋma boroboro wan wee baa ŋaan nnyɔgin, wee ki ti ku npeebum iyaayoo ki ŋa Yuglagir a Tiɛrim a Chiig a jiirin, le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin baan u ke, A ban ti jo laa wee ki tan ŋa kuchiig nmɔn a jiir a sili ki sei ŋaa.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Le u tun bi ni bilee ki ke, Ni li chaa kitiŋ nan yaan man. Ni li kan uja uba wee u tu linyunbool, ni li nɔɔ wa man.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: ki li chaa iniin yan wee u ta li kɔɔ n-yaan, wee ki bei uniindaan nmɔn ke, Wan wɔɔn ke ti baaŋ ke, bicham a diig kan wee u ma ni u bɔɔnɔɔb yin ji yuglagir a tiɛrim a chiigin be laa.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Le u li wɔɔn ni kudiititɔrug kan yab wee bi pii ŋa ni wan biɛn a sili, wee ni ŋa tijiir nmɔn niin.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb bilee nmɔn jon kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, ki sin tan kan ke ni wan biɛn ŋa sili ke waah bi bei bi man ni na. Le bi ŋa kuchiig nmɔn a jiir.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee nmɔn nan dan kujɔɔg.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Baah ka ki ji tijiir n-yonn man nin, le u nan bei bi ke, Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, min nin nim kaah ka ki ji tijiir tan nan, ni ni uba li yin kɔɔrim.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Le ni bii bi sufu, le bi baan u uba ba uba ba ke, A len min lee.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni le ye ni piig nin bilee nan a ni uba wan wee min nin u ma lagin ki bɔr kisaŋ kiba ni nan le.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Uninfosaal a Big le sin yin kpo le ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin man nin, amaa tibɔbir li be wan yin kɔɔr u nan bu. Baa nan bi maa u daan gbaa ke, ni bi li sɔ u bu.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Baah nan ji tijiir n-yonn man nin, le Yisa yuur boroboro ki miɛ Uwonbɔr, le ki war war ki sei u bɔɔnɔɔb, ki bei bi ke, Ni gaa man. N gbanann le yen.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Man le u ji nan yuur kiyiinyɔɔg nin isiloon a daam ki miɛ Uwonbɔr, ki ta sei bi. Le bi biɛn nyun.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Le u bei bi ke, N cham le yen, le n cham li kpaar ki wur, bininfob takpem bu, ki wɔɔn ke Uwonbɔr poo tipoor pɔn na.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, maa ji li nyi ki nyu isiloon a daam nan ni see lidaal man wee n na yin nyu miwanpɔm Uwonbɔr a naamin ni.

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Le bi nan gaa iwonjiɛn lan, ki nyan ki jon Olif a siigi a jɔɔlin a paab.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni biɛn nan na li yiim, ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Uwonbɔr na li ku upeekpaarin, le ipee n guun ki yaal.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Amaa n nan fii nkumin ke, n li chinn ki liin ni sam ki jo Galilii.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Le Piita bei ke, N tab nan biɛn nan yiiŋ ke, min kaa li yiiŋi.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Ibamɔn le n beiŋ nan, kunyiɛg nan, a li niɛ itaa itaa ke ŋaa nyi mi le ukooja n ni mɔɔ milee.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Le Piita mann pag nin linunmanl ke, Hali ni ye min nin fin n lagin ki kpo gbaa ke, maa li nyi ki niɛ ke maa nyiŋi. Le u tabin mu len man.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Le bi jon nan yaan wee bi yi Gesemani nan. Nin yaan nmɔn le Yisa bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, Ni li ka doou wee n jo ki tan miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: U nan ta Piita le nin Jemisi, nin Jɔnn ki jon. Le u sufu bii ki be ke u yin kpo le-n;.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: le u bei bi ke, Npoobiin man choom nan li kum. Ni li ka do ki li lig man.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: Le ki fɔɔr ki jon nintogil wama, ki gbaan le u yutunl tan mar tum, ki miɛ Uwonbɔr, ke ni bi li fir ke, Uwonbɔr n bin che npoobiin man chaan u ban nan n bin jo lagir u bu, ki ji len ke.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: N baa, n baa, niba li kaa be le ŋaa li fir tun ni. Laa che n nyu kiyiinyɔɔg nan yaan ni; Amaa che a yaa bam le n tun, wee ni laa ye n yaa bami.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: Le u jirin u bɔɔnɔɔb bita n ban, ki miin bi jiɛn na. Le u baan Piita ke, Simɔnn, a jiɛn lee? Ŋaa li fir kal guurim wamaa.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Le ki bei bi ke, Ni ligir ki miɛ Uwonbɔr a wee ki laa kɔɔ nton nan yaan ni. Ni naam ma ban ni tun nan ŋan, amaa le ni gbanann kaa pɔɔ.

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Le ki ji jir u wonmiɛlin ki tan miɛ ke waah bi miɛ man chinchinn ni.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: ki ji nan jirin ki miin u bɔɔnɔɔbin jiɛn na, nan bu-n njiɛm le nan jɔɔ bi takpem. Le baa nyi baah li bei u man ni.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Waah nan jirin itaa itaa-n, le u baan bi ke, Ni le la biɛ ni dɔ jiɛn ki fooraa? Ni kpaan man na. N-yonn nmɔn foou. Bi ta Uninfosaal a Big ki ŋubin tibirdam a nɔɔn na.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Ni fiim wee ti jo. Lig wan kɔɔrimin le chaan nin.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Yisa kaah la be pagin, le u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee-n a ni uba wan wee bi yi u ke Judasi nan ŋmag kuninfogbaluŋ ban wee Uwonbɔr a toontum ninkpeeb nan tun bi, bi ma nan ye kumanug a ninkpeeb nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nan nin bininkpeeb, le biba ŋmag takoobi, le biba mu ŋmag ilangul.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: le Judasi wan kɔɔr Yisa nmɔn pii wɔɔn bi ke, Wan wee n sɔɔn ki poor u ke ni li nyi ke u ma yen. Wee ni choo u ki li ŋmag u nbamɔm ki li jɔɔ u chaau.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Judasi kaah nan foon n-yonn man nin, libol nmɔn le u jon Yisa ban ki yin u ke, Wan wɔɔn, le ki sɔɔn ki poor u.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Le bininfobin mu choo Yisa ki ŋmag u kenken.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin a ni uba nar u takoobi ki gaa kumanug a ninkpelu n-yaa yomu a tul ki par.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Le Yisa baan bi ke, N ye ban kɔnn bininkpeeb a sanliiru le ni dan nan choom a le ke n ye ufeer le naa?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: N nan ti be ni ban le iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan iyoo biɛn le ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr, le naa ti chorimi, amaa ni kpaa talar le ke Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ a bɔri tin gbeen.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin biɛn guun ki jiɛn u.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Le unaachipɔɔn uba yee likinkenl a binba ki nɔɔ Yisa a bɔɔn. Le bi nan bib u ki choo u kinkenl.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: le u jiɛn likinkenl nmɔn bi nɔɔn, le ki guun nin lijakpalomil.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Le bi ta Yisa ki jon kumanug a ninkpelu a niin, nan yaan wee kumanug a ninkpeeb, nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninkpeeb, nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin lagin.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Le Piita be Yisa a bɔɔn, nan fɔg hali nin baah tan kɔɔ kumanug a ninkpelu nmɔn a niin. Le Piita jon ki tan kal kpiɛ bi toontum bu, le bi wor nŋmi.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Le kumanug a ninkpeeb nmɔn nin bininkpeeb nmɔn biɛn pɔɔn bi ba ke bin gbir siɛradam a mɔnn, wee ki kan Yisa a bir wee ki ku u, le kaa kan u biri.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Siɛradam takpem nan por tibɔkar le ki tenn u, amaa le bi bɔkar nmɔn kaa nɔɔ nin tabi.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Le bijab biba fii ki sir ki por tibɔkar npaan ni ke.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: Ti gbir u bei ke, u li ber iwonjiɛn niin yan wee bininfob nan maa nan, ki ta iweehn itaa ki ji maa ki, wee nin laa ji ye bininfob li maa ki.

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Nin ni biɛn nan yɔli, bi bɔkar nmɔn mu kaa biɛ nan nɔɔ tabi.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Le Yisa nan nyan Jɔdann a mɔgil paam, ki gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ le Kinaaŋ nmɔn nan ta u don kukunkɔɔg bu.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Le kumanug a ninkpelu win nan fii ki baan Yisa ke, Tibɔr tan wee bijab nan pag ki jiin a paam nan a taai yaa? Ŋaa jɔɔ ti jiim maa.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: ki be niin iweehn pina, le ulinlaril nan bigin u. Waa nan jin niba iweehn nmɔn biɛn ni; le nkom nan nan choo u.

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Le Yisa ŋmin, kaa jiin tiba. Le u ji mann ki baan u ke, Fin le sin ye Masiya wan ye u ma Uwonbɔr a Bija n naa.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Le ulinlaril nmɔn bei u ke, Ni ye ke a sin ye Uwonbɔr a Bija ke, a bei litangbagil nan wee lin kpann tijiir.

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Nn min le yen. Ni na li kan Uninfosaal a Big kaah ka Utindaan Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam, ki ji na li kan u ki miin u be titalangbann ni le ki jiirin.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Uninfosaal kaa be tijiir a binba bu le. .

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Le kumanug a ninkpelu nmɔn tɔɔl choo u daala ki jiɛ, le ki ke, Taa ji ban siɛra dami!.

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le ulinlarilin ji yuur u ki ta u don lijɔɔl lan kper a paab, ki tan wɔɔn u kutingbɔŋ nan a wan yɔɔr biɛn.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Min tɔɔl kan waah sug Uwonbɔr man na. Ni tiɛr nii? Le bi biɛn chee ke u biiu, u kpaan nin nkum le.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: ki bei u ke, N li seiŋ tininkper a pɔɔm nin njɔɔm biɛn. Bi nan ta ni biɛn seima le n mu li fir ta sei wan wee n ban.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Le bi ni biba siin timɔsann jaar u bu; le ki piin u num paam le ki pebir u ntapam ki ji baan u ke, Biɛ njee ŋma pebiŋ ŋaa. Le ban guu win mu boo u.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Mambu a bi jiɛnim ke, ni biɛn nan bi yin ye a yaan le.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Piita la nan be nloob le ki wor nŋmi, le kumanug a ninkpelu a niin a sapɔɔn toontunn uba nan kan u.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Li jiɛn Uyudaan Uwonbɔr a binba, ki sin tun tenn u a binba. .

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: ki lig u chiir, ki nan bei u ke, A mu nan nɔɔ Nazarif a ninfou Yisa nmɔn le.

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Le ulinlaril ji nan yuur u ki jon Jerusalam, ki tan don iwonjiɛn niin a paab , le ki bei u ke, Ni ye a sin ye Uwonbɔr a Bija ke a yug do ki lu tuma.

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Le Piita niɛ ke, Min kaa nyi wi, min kaa nyi tibɔr tan wee a pag nan a taai, le ki fii nyan. Libol nmɔn le ukooja mɔɔ.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: Nan bu-n ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: U li pag sei u malakam a bu wee bin guuriŋ nbamɔm.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Le usapɔɔn nin ji kan u niin ki mann bei bininfob ban si niin nin ke, Uja nan mu ye u bɔɔnɔɔbin a ni uba le.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Le bi li yuuriŋ bi nɔɔn, wee a taal laa gbiɛr litangbagili. .

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Le Piita ji mann niɛ. Le ni kpiɛ wama le ban be niin nmɔn mann baan Piita ke, A sin ye bininfob nan a ni uba le, nan bu-n a ye Galilii a ninfou le.

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni len ke: Uba laa bigin u Wonbɔru wi. .

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Le Piita poo ke, Ni ye n por tibɔkar ke, Uwonbɔr n dar n tula, min kaa nyi naah pag uja wan nan a bɔri.

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Ulinlarilin kaah nan bigin Yisa diɛ n-yonn man nin, le u la fɔɔr ki sin jiɛn u ki guu iyoo yan kpaan.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Libol nmɔn le ukooja mɔɔ kpiɛ itaa ilee; le Piita nin tiɛr Yisa kaah bi bei u man ni ke, A li len ke ŋaa nyi mi itaa itaa le ukooja n nin mɔɔ. Iyaayoo nmɔn le Piita nin piin ti mɔɔr.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Binib takpem nan pɔɔn bi ba ki sɔb kugbɔŋ nan a poolin tibɔr tan nan ŋa ti sisiigin.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Yisa nan jir Galilii le Kinaaŋ pɔɔmin. Le u bɔr nan mɔɔn itingbaa biɛn.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: Binib ban kan tibɔr nan a piinim nin tidiɛmin nan mɔɔn ti le ki wɔɔn tim le ki ye ban tun tibɔr nmɔn a tooi.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: U nan mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr le bi wonjiɛndirin, le bininfob biɛn pag u.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Mambu, Min Lug, n baantɔɔl kaah biɛ tibɔr nan a piinim a bamɔn, ki tan kpaa ti diɛmin ni. Ni ma le n tiɛr ke ni ŋan n mu n sɔb ti taah piin ki tan sir man, ki sei fin, n ninkpelu Teofilu.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Le Yisa nan jon Nazarif, nan yaan wee bi nan wubiri u, u buwaan nin nan. Le likpakɔɔl daal nan foo, le u kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin, ke waah pii ti kɔɔ man nin, ki nan fii ke un karin Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: wee a mu n biɛ baah wɔɔniŋ tibɔr tan nan a dede.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: Le bi ta ulinlenn Azaya a gbɔŋ kpabkaag ki sei u. Waah kpar ki-n, le u kan nan yaan wee ni sɔb ke:.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: N-yonn man wee Herɔd nan ye Judiya a tingbɔŋ a bɔrninkpelu nan, le Uwonbɔr a minmanu uba nan be le bi yi u ke Zakariya. U nan nyan Abija a paabim ni le. U poo a yinl nan ye Elizabef. U mu nan ye Uwonbɔr a minmanu Arɔnn a maalim ni uba le.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: Uyudaan a Naaŋ be nim, nan bu-n U ma kpaar nkpam jaa n bu, ke n mɔɔn u bɔr ŋan nmɔn ki sei bilanjiim. U tunim ke n pag feeja a bɔr ki sei biloondiikɔɔb, wee ki ligir bijɔɔm a num, ki nyan limugildam bi mugilin.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Zakariya nin u poo biɛn nan ye bininfoŋam le Uwonbɔr ban, ki nɔɔ u kaal nin u siim biɛn.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: ki sin mɔɔn libinl lan wee Uyudaan jiɛn.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Amaa baa nan jɔɔ kibigi. Elizabef nan ye upiiŋɔɔb le, bi biɛn mu nan pora.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Le u kpab kugbɔŋin ki jiin sei kuwonjiɛndiig n-yaa toontunn uba, le ki sin kal. Le bininfob ban biɛn be kudiig nmɔn yaan nin lig u.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Man le weemba daal Zakariya nin u paabim a toontun yonn nan nan foo le u nan ke un tun u toon ki sei Uwonbɔr, ke waah pii ti tun ki tenn u man nin.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Le u piin ki pag tenn bi ke, Tibɔr tan wee n karin Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nan, dinan Uwonbɔr a mɔleen gbeen nin naah gbir yin na.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Le bi nan tɔn inaa , ke bin gann wan li kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin nin yaan ki li nyɔɔr nnyɔgkusim. Baah nan tɔn inaa-n nin, le Zakariya nan jin. Ni ma bu le u nan kɔɔ iniin ni yaan.

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Le bi biɛn pag u nin ligaril u nyunyunbasanl lan nyan u mɔnn ni. Le bi baan tab, Waa ye Josef a bija nnaa.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: le bininfob n biɛn nan par ki si nkaafiin ki miɛ Nween, n-yonn man wee u nan nyɔɔr nnyɔgkusimin.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, N pii nyi ke ni li gaam libɔŋal ke, Utebir, tebim a ba! Ŋa a tiŋ nan yaan ke taah gbir ke a ŋa Kapeniɛm man nin. .

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Iyaayoo le Uyudaan a malaka nan degir Zakariya bu, ki sir baah nyɔɔr nnyɔgkusim libinbinl lan bu-n nnɔɔjiim paam.

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Le u ji ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, baa cheel ulinlenn a mɔb u tɔɔl a tiŋin ni.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: Zakariya kaah nan nan kan u n-yonn man ni, le u lam nan yug, le bijawaam choo u.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Ibamɔn le n wɔɔn ni, ipukɔɔ takpem le nan be Iziral n-yonn man wee Elaja la nan be-n. N-yonn nmɔn wee utaal kaa nan nun ibin itaa nin iŋmal iloob, le nkom nan lu kitiŋ nmɔn yaab biɛn bu.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Le Uwonbɔr a malaka nmɔn bei u ke, Zakariya laa san bijawaami. Uwonbɔr pii gbir a miɛma, le a poo Elizabef li maa kibijag ki seiŋ, le a li yin u yinl ke Jɔnn.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr kaa nan tun Elaja bi ni uba ban ni see lipukɔɔl lan nan be Zarifa n-yaa binba ban. Zarifa be Sidonn a tiŋin le.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: Le ni na li mɔŋiŋ le a pool n penn u bu, le bininfob takpem mu a pooi na li penn u maalim bu.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Nkɔm takpem nan be Iziral a tiŋin ulinlenn Elasiya a yonn, amaa bi ni uba kaa nan kan kutebugi see Nemann a binba wan nan ye Siriya a ninfou nan.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: U nan yin ye lininfo gbingbemil le Uwonbɔr a nintogil, kaa biɛ nan yin nyu ndaam a booli biɛn ni . U na yin gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ le u maalimin na.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Le bininfob ban nan be kuwonjiɛndiigin a poolin kaah nan gbir Yisa a bɔr nmɔn nin, bi nan fiin bi ŋuui le u bu.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: Le u na li che Iziral yaab takpem n jir bi Yudaau Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: le bi fii ki ŋɔɔr u nyan kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, ki ta u jon ligungonl lan bu wee bi nan maa bi tiŋ nmɔn a gbaam, ke bin ta u ki jaa ligungonl nmɔn a tum.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: U li chinn Uyudaan, ki li jɔɔ Elaja a naaŋin a pɔɔm, U li kpenn baam a sufu ki jiin bi buwaabin nin binib ban kaa jɔɔ limɔcheel n kan bibuchim a lam, ki ŋa bininfob sili ki sei Uyudaan.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Amaa le u bɔgir bi siigin ki choon jon.

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Le Zakariya baan malaka n ke, N yin ŋa nii ki biɛ ke ŋaah len man nan sin li ŋaa? N pora, le n poo mu pora.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Le Yisa nan jir Kapeniɛm kan be Galilii a tiŋin nan. Le likpakɔɔl daal ke u wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a mɔleen;.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Le malaka nmɔn bei u ke, N ye Gabiriɛl le, min le be Uwonbɔr a nintogil le u tum a ban, ke n nan pag tibɔŋann nan ki wɔɔniŋ.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: le u wɔɔnim nmɔn nan gar bi, nan bu-n u wɔɔnim nmɔn kaah nan jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔmin bu.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: Le daandaan nan a li bir ki tan kpaa lidaal lan wee tibɔr nan na li ŋa ibamɔn. Ŋaah kaa ŋa n bɔr tan wee n pagin yada-n, tan na li ŋa ibamɔn n-yonn man kpaan.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Le uja wan wee kinaanbiig nan be u ban nan, nan be kuwonjiɛndiigin yaan ki tann nmɔpɔɔn ke.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Tɔ, bininfob ban nan paar si iniin ni, ni nan ŋa bi ligaril le, waah nan yuur iwonjiɛn niin a poolin nin.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Fin Yisa wan ye Nazarif a ninfo nan, a ban baa ti ban naa? Aah! A dan ke a nan bii tim lee? N nyi ŋa ye wan, a ye Uwonbɔr a kasi a toom le.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: Waah nan nan nyan n-yonn man nin, waa nan fir pagi, u nan pag libir le ki tenn bi, le bi biɛ ke u kan lijinjeel a wan le kudiig nmɔn yaan.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Le Yisa tiɛn kinaanbiig n bu ke, Ŋmim tifur, ki sin nya uja nmɔn yaan na. Le kinaanbiigin nyiɛ uja-n tum, bi sisiigin, le ki sin nyan u ni; waah nan nyiɛ u tumin, waa nan sei u kidaŋi.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: N-yonn man wee u toon nan nan diɛ iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn yaan nin, le u nan jir kun u niin.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Le ni nan ŋa bi biɛn ligaril, le bi baan tab ke, Ba a wɔɔm ye naa? U jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔn le nin npɔɔn ki bei nnaanbiim bimɔb le bi nyan bininfobin.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le u poo Elizabef nan choo lipool, le ki woon uba ndiin ki tan kpaa iŋmal iŋmu.

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Le bi nan mɔɔn Yisa a yinl kitiŋ nmɔn nin itingbaa biɛn.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: le ki nan nan bei ke, Ni niɛ nan le Uwonbɔr tun seim, ki sam kunyunyunbaag ki nyam ifa-n.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Le Yisa nan nyan kuwonjiɛndiig nmɔn yaan ki jon Simɔnn a niin, ki miin kugbanantoŋ le jɔɔ Simɔnn a chɔɔpii, le bi nan bei Yisa upii nmɔn a wiɛn bu.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Elizabef kaah nan choo lipool a ŋmal iloob, le Uwonbɔr nan tun u malaka Gabiriɛl ke un jo kitiŋ kan be Galilii a tingbɔŋin, wee bi yi kitiŋ nmɔn ke Nazarif nan.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Le Yisa nan bɔɔn upii-n bu ki fuun kugbanantoŋin bu, le ku jaau; le upii-n paa nan fii ki ŋa bi kuchɔŋ.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: ki tan pag sei usapɔɔn wan kaa nyi uja wee bi yi u ke Meri nan. Usapɔɔn nmɔn nan ye uja uba a jakpeŋ le, le u yinl ye Josef. Josef nan ye ubɔr Defid a yaabil le.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Nween kaah nan nan yin lu n-yonn man ni, le ban biɛn nan jɔɔ bibaarib bi niin yin ni nan ta bi ki dan Yisa ban; bi nan bu iwiɛn a booli a booli le u ta u nɔɔ ki ma ma bi, le bi kan kutebug.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Le malaka nan tan foo Meri ban ki bei u ke, N pooriŋ. Fin wan wee Uwonbɔr a nyɔɔr be a bu takpem nan, Uyudaan be niŋ.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: U ji nan che nnaanbiim nyan bininfobin takpem. Baah nan nyan diɛ nan le bi tiɛn nmɔpɔɔn ke, A ye Uwonbɔr a Bija le. Amaa le u nan fuun bi bu kaa ji nan che bi pag jigin ni, nan bu-n bi nan nyi waah ye Masiya.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Waah len man nan, le ni gar Meri. Le u ni nan tiɛr tibɔr nmɔn bu.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Naah nan won kutaagin le Yisa nan nyan kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ki kɔɔ kulɔŋin; le bininfobin nan choon ki ban ki tan kan u. Baah nan kan u nan, le bi nan gɔɔ u ki ke un laa yaar ki sin jiɛn bi.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Amaa le malaka ji bei u ke, Meri, laa san bijawaami. Uwonbɔr a nyɔɔr be a bu.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: Amaa le u ke, See ke n jon ntim man gurin yaan ki tan mɔɔn tibɔŋann ki jiin Uwonbɔr a naam paam nan, ni ma bu le Uwonbɔr tunim.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: A li choo lipool ki maa kibijabig, ki yin u ke Yisa.

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Ni ma bu le u nan jin Judiya a timin ki kɔɔ tiwonjiɛndirin ki wɔɔn bininfob tibɔr nmɔn.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: U na yin ye lininfo takpenl le, le bi li yin u ke Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan a Bija. Uyudaan Uwonbɔr li che un ji u yaaja Defid a naam.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: Le u na li ŋmag Jekɔb a niin hali nin n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmi; le u naam kaa li nyi diɛ.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Le Meri nan baan Malaka n ke, N ye usapɔɔn wan kaa nyi uja le, ni yin ŋa nii wee n choo lipool laa.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Le malaka n bei u ke, Kasi a Naaŋ li jiirin a bu, Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan a pɔɔm li biin a bu. Ni ma bu ŋaah li maa kibig kan nan yin ye kasi daan le, le bi li yin u Uwonbɔr a Bija.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Le ki bei u ke, Lig a nabig Elizabef, u mu jɔɔ kibijabig a pool le. Baah nan yi u ke upiiŋɔɔb nan, u jɔɔ iŋmal iloob a pool le.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Niba li kaa be ki pɔɔ Uwonbɔr ban ni.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Le Meri bei malaka nmɔn ke, N ye Uyudaan a yomu le. Che nin ŋa ke ŋaah len man nan. Le malaka-n nan fɔɔr ki jiɛn u.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Iyaayoo le Meri nan ŋa sili ki fii weem ki jon Judiya a yagil lan jɔɔ ijɔɔ nan a tiŋ kiba ni.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: Nan yaan wee u nan kɔɔ Zakariya a niin ki poor Elizabef.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: N-yonn man wee Elizabef gbir Meri a poori, le u big dam dam u poolin. N-yonn nmɔn le Kasi a Naaŋ gbeen Elizabef.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: Le u pag nin nmɔpɔɔn ke: Uwonbɔr a kaar be a bu ki jon bipiib biɛn na, ki be kibig kan be a poolin nan mu bu.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Amaa ba ŋa wee Uwonbɔr ŋaam tinyɔɔr man naa, ki che n Yudaau a na dan ke un nan gɔnnimaa?.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: Maah gbir a poori n-yonn man nin, le kibig kan be n poolin nin yug nin npopeen.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Tikaar be wan ŋa yada ke Uyudaan kaah bei u man nin le gbeen nin bu.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Le Meri len ke: N pagir Uyudaan n sufu ni le.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: Le ni mɔ n naaŋ, Uwonbɔr wan ye n feeru win ban.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: Nan bu-n u potiɛl be n ni min U yomu wan jɔɔ kunyunyunbaag. Lig! Naah nyan dinan ki chaa, n-yonn biɛn a ninfob li yim ke tikaar.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: Nan bu-n npɔɔndaan nan tun litoonl takpenl le ki seim. U yinl ye kasi le.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: U san bininfob ban biɛn san u nan kunyunyunbaag, naah nyan daandaan nan a ninfob ki jɔɔ chaa bi yaabili nin yaabisagira sagira.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: U ta u nɔɔ ki tun itoon gbingbem; ki worin kalanbaanidam baah tiɛr man bi sufu ni!.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: U nan jiin bibɔrtiib bi naangbann bu, amaa le ki don ban jiin biba tumin paab!.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: Binib ban wee nkom jɔɔ nan, U sei bi tijiŋann, le bi jin ki gbee. U che bijɔɔndam kun nin linɔɔpiɛŋmiɛl!.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: U ter u toontum Iziral yaab, ki tiɛr bi bu ki san bi kunyunyunbaag.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: Abiraham nin u yaabili nin n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ diɛmin hali nin waah nan pag ki sei ti baamin.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Meri nan be Elizabef ban le iŋmal itaa paam le ki nan jir kun.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Le isaŋ nan foo nin Elizabef nmaa, le u nan ma kibijag.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Le u kunkɔɔn tab nin u ninfob nan gbir Uwonbɔr kaah san u kunyunyunbaag man takpem, le bi biɛn nan lagin ki por u mɔm nmɔn.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Ni ween iniin daal le bi nan dan ke bin jii kibigin a punl, le ki nan ban bin ta u baa Zakariya a yinl ki yin u.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: Le u na kaa chee, ki bei bi ke, Aai, u yinl yin ye Jɔnn le.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Le bi bei u ke, Uninfo uba kaa jɔɔ liyinl nmɔn a bosu ni maalimin ni.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Le bi nan pag kibig nmɔn a baa libiril ki baan u ke u ban bi yin u bigin nii?.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Le u bei bi ke bin sei u kugbɔŋ ni. Le bi ta kugbɔŋ sei u, le u sɔb ke, U yinl ye Jɔnn le. Ni nan gar bi biɛn pam.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: Libol nmɔn yaan le Zakariya a mɔb yaar le u lanbil lɔgir. Le u piin ki pag, ki pagir Uwonbɔr.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Le bijawaam choo u kunkɔɔn tab biɛn, Le Judiya a tim man biɛn be igungon ni nan a ninfob nan pag tibɔr nmɔn.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Ni nan ŋa bininfob ban biɛn nan gbir nan ipotiɛ pam. Le bi baan ke, Kibig nmɔn yin ye ninfo bolin baa? Nan bu-n Uyudaan a nɔɔ paa u bu.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Le Kasi a Naaŋ nan gbeen Jɔnn a baa Zakariya, le u pag ilinlen leen ke.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: Ndom ye Uyudaan wan ye Iziral yaab a Wonbɔr u nan yaan le. Nan bu-n U dan ki nan sei u ninfob nkpenim a manuga.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: ki nan pee feeja a naatuŋ u toontunn Defid a niin a loob.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: Ke u li nyan tim ti dataam a nɔɔn, nin ban biɛn nan ti nan a nɔɔn.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: Ke u li san ti yaajam kunyunyunbaag, ki li tiɛr waah nan poo u kasi a poor tan nin bu.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: Man le u nan poo tipoor ki sei ti yaaja Abiraham.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: ke u li nyan tim ti dataam a nɔɔn, wee ti li nɔɔ u ki laa san bijawaami.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: ki li be kasi ki li jɔɔ nbuchin binben u ban iyoo biɛn.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: Fin, n big, bi li yiŋ ke Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan a linlenu. A li liin Uwonbɔr a sam, ki mann u sam ki sei u .

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: ki bei u ninfob baah li ŋa man ki kan nŋmarim waah jiɛn bi bir ki per bin bu.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Nan bu-n tim a Wonbɔr u a nyunyunbasanl bu, u li dan ti ban ke liwonpɔɔnl kaah nyann bɔɔn man nin.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: Ni na li wonn bininfob ban be libunbɔnlin ki san nkum a jawaam nan bu, Ni ma wɔɔn ti nsan man li sei tim nposɔɔn nan.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Le kibig nmɔn nan yabig ki gbee nin kinaaŋ, ki don kukunkɔɔg bu ki tan be hali ki nan kpaa iyoo kaah nan nan foo nin un kpiir u ba Iziral yaab bu.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le ubɔr yudaan Siiza Agusitu nan siin nsiim u tingbɔŋin ke bininfob biɛn n kun ki tan sɔb bi yin wee un lig bi kanl kaah kpaa man.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Le bininfobin biɛn nan kun bi tim, bin tan sɔb bi yin.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Josef mu nan ye ubɔr Defid a maalim ni uba le. Ni ma bu, u nan nyan Nazarif, kitiŋ kan be Galilii a tingbɔŋin nan, le ki jon ubɔr Defid a tiŋ, kan wee bi yi ki ke Betiliham, kan be Judiya a tingbɔŋin nin.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Le u kun ke un tan sɔb u ma nin u jakpeŋ Meri a yin; iyaayoo nmɔn ki miin Meri jɔɔ lipool le.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Baah la nan be Betiliham nan, le nmarwiɛn choo Meri.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: le u ma usapɔnmal kibijag, le ki ta u pɔbin likinkenlin, ki ta u ŋa kubulunkpoŋ a poolin; npaan kaa ji nan be bicham a diig n-yaan bin kɔɔ.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Bipeekpaab biba nan be kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ki kpaa ipee kunyiɛg kulɔŋ.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Le Uyudaan a malaka nan degir bi bu. Le Uyudaan nmɔn a pag a weem wonn bi bu. Le bijawaam nan choo bi takpem.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Le malaka nmɔn bei bi ke, Ni laa san bijawaami. N dan n nan bei ni tibɔr ŋan le, tan li sei bininfob biɛn nmɔn.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Dinan nan Defid a tiŋin bi ma ufeerin na, U ma ye Uyudaan Masiya.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Ni li kan kibig nmɔn kaah pɔb likinkenlin, ki dɔ tiwankor kaah ji kubulunkpoŋ kan yaan ni. Ni ma li ye limagil ki sei ni.

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le malakam takpem nan nyan paab ki jiirin ki nan kpiɛ malaka n bu ki pagir Uwonbɔr ke.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: Kupag ye Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan a yaan le. Nposɔɔn li be dulinya nan a ninfob ban bu wee U nyɔɔr be-n.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Le malakam nin nan fɔɔr bipeekpaab nan ban, ki jir don papaab. Le bipeekpaab nan bei tab ke, Ni che ti jo Betiliham ki tan lig niwann nan tun, wee ti Yudaau Uwonbɔr bei ti nan.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Ni ma bu le bi nan fii weem weem ki jon tan foo ki kan Meri nin Josef; le kibiliɛg n mu dɔ kubulunkpoŋ n-yaan poolin.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Baah nan tan foo ki kan u nan, le bi bei bininfob Uwonbɔr a malakam kaah bei bi man, ki jiin kibig nmɔn paam.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: le baah nan gbir bipeekpaab nan kaah len man nin, le ni gar bi biɛn.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Ubɔr yudaan Tiberiya Siiza kaah nan jin u naam ibin piig nin iŋmu-n wee Pontiɛ Palati mu ye Judiya a bɔru win, le Herɔd mu ye Galilii a bɔru, le u naal Filib mu ye Ituriya nin Tirakoniti a bɔru, Lisaniɛ mu ye Abilene a bɔru.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Amaa Meri ma nan tiɛr nan nan tun nmɔn biɛn, ki ŋmag ni u sufu ni.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: n-yonn man wee Anasi nin Kayafasi nan ye Uwonbɔr a minmanib a ninkpeebin, le Uwonbɔr a mɔleen foo Jɔnn wan ye Zakariya a bija-n kukunkɔɔg bu.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le bipeekpaab nan nan jir bi pee ban kulɔŋin. Baah nan chaa jir nmɔn le bi nan pagir ki donn Uwonbɔr a yinl, nin baah kan ki gbir nan biɛn nin bu; le ni sin ye le ke malaka kaah bei bi man nin.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Le u nan jin ntim man nan be Jɔdann ni biɛn ki mɔɔn ke bin jiɛn bi toonbira wee un fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful, wee bin kan tibir a jiɛn per.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: Baah nan ma u a bɔɔn a ween iniin daal le bi nan jii u punl le ki yin u yinl ke Yisa, liyinl lan wee malaka nan pur ke baa la choo u poolin.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: A li ke naah sɔb ulinlenn Azaya a gbɔŋin ke: Uba a kukɔl le be kukunkɔɔg bu ki yi, Mann Uyudaan a sama, mann u sam wee mi li tel ki sei u.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Ni nan nan foo nin Josef nin Meri n tun ke Moziz a siim kaah nan wɔɔn man nin, bin mann kumanug ki ŋa biba kasi Uwonbɔr ban. Ni ma bu le bi ni nan ta kibigin ki jon Jerusalam, ke bin tan taa u ki sei Uwonbɔr.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Nfatam biɛn na li gbeen, ijɔɔ nin igungon biɛn na li ber ki naa tum, tisangɔnn biɛn na li naar, tisanbogir biɛn piin.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Le bi ji nan mann kumanug ke Uyudaan kaah siin man nin, ke Bin ban kulɔŋ a lanjel ilee bee iniin a lanjebum milee ki mann kumanug nmɔn .

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Uninfosaal biɛn na li kan Uwonbɔr a feeja. .

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Uja uba le nan be Jerusalam, bi nan yi u ke Simiɛn. U nan ye uninfo buchinn le ki sin ŋmag Uwonbɔr a siimin nbamɔm, le Kasi a Naaŋ nan be u bu, ki lig lidaal man wee Uwonbɔr na li fee Iziral yaab ki jaa.

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Le Jɔnn bei kuninfogbaluŋ ban nan dan u ban ke un fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin ke, Nim iwaalab a bum, ŋma kpaan ni ke ni nya kiŋuufiiŋ kan chaan nan naa?.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: Kasi a Naaŋ pii nan chinn ki kar wɔɔn u ke waa li kpo see ke u kan Uyudaan a Masiya.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Ni li lo iloon yan wɔɔn ke ni sin kpenn ni binbema. Wee ki laa piin ki bei ni ba ke, Ti jɔɔ Abiraham wan ye ti yaaja. N bei ni le, Uwonbɔr li fir che itangbag yan be nan n kpann Abiraham a yaabili.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Kinaaŋin nan che Simiɛn kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan, ki kan Yisa a baa nin u na kaah ta u dan ke bin nan ŋa u nsiim kaah wɔɔn man nin.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Lilajal pii be isiig a nyagin, ki li gaa busub ban biɛn kaa lo iloon ŋan ki jaa tum ki ta bi gab nŋmi ni.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: le Simiɛn nan gaa kibigin u nɔɔ ki don Uwonbɔr ke.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn nan baan, Ke ti ni li ŋa nii.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: N yudaau, min wan ye a yomu nan, jaam nin nposɔɔn, Ke ŋaah pii nan siin limɔsiinl man nin.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Uninfo wan jɔɔ daala bilee ke, un nyan kiba ki sei wan kaa jɔɔu, le wan mu jɔɔ tijiir ke, un ŋa sei wan kaa jɔɔu.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Min tɔɔl a numbil kan a feegabin na.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Bilanpogaab mu nan dan bi nan gaa Uwonbɔr nyunful u ban. Le ki baan ke, Wan wɔɔn, ti mu yin ŋa nii.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: Wan wee a ŋaau sili ki siin ki sei bininfob biɛn nin.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni laa gaan ki lagir naah ye ni gaa man ni.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: U li ye nwonween ki kpiir a samin ki wɔɔn ban kaa ye Iziral yaab nan , Ki ji ta kupag ki sei a tɔɔl a ninfob Iziral yaab.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Le bitɔtob mu baan u, Ti mu yin ŋa nii? Le u jiin ke, Ni laa feen iligi, ki laa por tibɔkar ki paag bininfob bu; che ni num n gbee ni yɔr tan wee ni gaan nin.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Simiɛn kaah pag bi ki jiin kibig nmɔn paamin, le ni gar kibig nmɔn a ba nin ki na takpem.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Bininfobin kaah pii nan guu nin tama-n le bi tiɛr bi sufu ni ke Jɔnn le par ye Masiya nna.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Le Simiɛn nan kaab jaa bi bu, le ki sin bei kibigin a na Meri ke, Uwonbɔr gann kibig nmɔn ke u li che Iziral yaab takpem n lu yɔli u bu; le bininfob takpem mu li kan nŋmarim u bu. Uma nan yin ye limagil lan wee bininfob takpem li pag ki bii u nan;.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Le Jɔnn jiin bei bi biɛn ke, Min fun ni nnyum le, amaa wan jom chaan, wan a naatagil ŋmim wee maa kpaan n gbinn ni. U na li fu ni Kasi a naaŋ nin nŋmi ki ŋunn ni jaginn.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: ni ma le li kpiir naah tiɛr man ni sufu ni nan n nya npaan ni.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: U ŋmag u dilenn yikpeŋin le u nɔɔn ke un lenn nbamɔm, ki nyan ndibimin ki ŋuun u binbolin, amaa le ki sin ta kufoŋin ki jaa nŋmi man gaan kaa jɔɔ njunimin yaan.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Le upiininkpel uba nan be niin ki ye Uwonbɔr a linlenu, le bi yi u ke Ana, u nan ye Faniɛl a bisa le, ki ye Aser a bol a ninfou, u nan por takpema, ki be u chal ban ibin ilile a bɔɔn.

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Jɔnn nan ta imɔbon le takpem ki mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin ki kpaan bininfob.

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: le u chal kpo ki jiɛn u lipukɔɔl le ki nan ye ibin piniin nin ina; amaa waa nan per iwonjiɛn niin n-yaa kɔɔm nween nin kunyiɛgi, ki jiɛn nween ki lolig bimɔb ki miɛ u.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Amaa jɔnn kaah nan fuun ubɔr Herɔd, u waal a poo Herɔdiya bu-n, nin titoonbir tan wee u nan tun nin.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Waah nan foon n-yonn man nni, le u sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, ki nan pag kibigin a bɔr ki tenn bininfob ban biɛn nan guu n-yonn man wee Jerusalam yaab li kan nkpenim a manugin.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: Herɔd nan ta ni niɛ nan biɛn le ki kpiɛ: U nan loon Jɔnn le kuloondiigin.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Josef nin Meri kaah nan tun Uwonbɔr a kaal kaah wɔɔn bi man ki diɛ nan, le bi nan jir kun bitɔɔl a tiŋ Nazarif kitiŋ kan be Galilii a tingbɔŋin nin.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: N-yonn man wee bininfob biɛn nan gaa Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin, le Yisa mu nan gaa Uwonbɔr a nyunful. Le waah nan be miɛ Uwonbɔrin le paab loor.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Le kibigin nan yabig nin npɔɔn nin nbiɛm takpem, le Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl li be u bu.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: le Kasi a Naaŋ jiirin u bu nin tigbanann ki nann ulanjel. Le likukɔl nyan paab ki len ke, Fin le ye n bija, wan wee n jiɛ; fin le n num gbee.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Tɔ, ibin biɛn Yisa a baa nin u na ti chaa Jerusalam le ki tan jin yuglagir a chiig.

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: N-yonn man wee Yisa nan piin Uwonbɔr a toon yin, u nan jɔɔ ibin pita le. U baa nan ye, ke baah nan tiɛr man nin, Josef le, le Josef mu nan ye Heli a big.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: N-yonn man wee Yisa nan nan foo ibin piig nin ilee-n, le bi nan jon ke bin tan ji kuchiig ke bi kaal kaah wɔɔn man nin.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: le Heli mu ye Matat a big, le Matat mu ye Liifai a big, le Liifai mu ye Meliki a big, le Meliki mu ye Janai a big, le Janai mu ye Josef a big.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: Kuchiig nmɔn a daai kaah nan nan diɛ n-yonn man ni, le bi nan piin n kum, le Yisa paar be Jerusalam, amaa u yaab kaa nan nyi ke u paari.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: le Josef mu ye Matatiya a big, le Matatiya mu ye Emosi a big, le Emosi mu ye Nahum a big, le Nahum mu ye Esili a big, Le Esili mu ye Nagai a big.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Bi nan tiɛr ke u be kuninfobabug n-yaan le, ki nan choon lidamunl nmɔn biɛn. Ni bɔɔn bu le bi nan ban u bi niin yaab ban nin bijɔtiib ban.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: le Nagai mu ye Maaf a big, le Maaf mu ye Matatiya a big, le Matatiya mu ye Semiɛn a big, le Semiɛn mu ye Joseg a big, le Joseg mu ye Joda a big.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: amaa le kaa kan wi. Le bi nan jir Jerusalam ki tan ban niin.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: le Joda mu ye Joanann a big, le Joanann mu ye Resa a big, le Resa mu ye Zerubabel a big, le Zerubabel mu ye Sealitel a big, le Sealitel mu ye Neri a big.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: Ni ween itaa daal le bi tan kan u iwonjiɛn niin a poolin, ki miin u lagin ki ka nin ban wɔɔnn a ninkpeeb ki pelig bi bɔr le ki sin baan bi ibinbaan.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: le Neri mu ye Meliki a big, le Meliki mu ye Adi a big, le Adi mu ye Kosam a big, le Kosam mu ye Elimadam a big, le Elimadam mu ye Er a big.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Le ni nan gar ban biɛn nan pelig u binbaan yin, nin waah ŋa man ki jɔɔ nbiɛm ki pag jiin bi.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: le Er mu ye Josuwa a big, le Josuwa mu ye Eliɛzer a big, le Eliɛzer mu ye Jorim a big, le Jorim mu ye Matat a big.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: U baa nin u na kaah nan kan u niin nin, ni nan gar bi pam, le u na baan u ke, N big, ba ŋa a ŋa man naa? Ŋaa nyi ke min nin a baa kan npoobiin hali ki choon baŋ ŋaa.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: le Matat mu ye Liifai a big, le Liifai mu ye Simiɛn a big, le Simiɛn mu ye Juda a big, le Juda mu ye Josef a big, le Josef mu ye Jonam a big, le Jonam mu ye Elayakim a big.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Le u jiin ki baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni bam maa? Naa nyi ke ni ye n li be n Baa a niin lee.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: le Elayakim mu ye Meliya a big, le Meliya mu ye Mena a big, le Mena mu ye Matata a big, le Matata mu ye Netann a big, le Netann mu ye Defid a big.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Amaa baa nan biɛ u bɔr nmɔn a taai.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: le Defid mu ye Jesi a big, le Jesi mu ye Obed a big, le Obed mu ye Bɔɔzi a big, le Bɔɔzi mu ye Salimonn a big, le Salimonn mu ye Nasonn a big.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Le u nan nɔɔ bi ki jir Nazarif a tiŋin, ki chee bimɔb. Le u na ta nan biɛn ŋa nmɔn ki ŋa u sufu ni.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: le Nasonn mu ye Aminadab a big, le Aminadab mu ye Ram a big , le Ram mu ye Hezironn a big, le Hezironn mu ye Perisi a big, le Perisi mu ye Juda a big.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Le Yisa nan yabirig ki kpiɛg nin nlam, le Uwonbɔr nin bininfob a pool nan sɔɔn u bu.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: le Juda mu ye Jekɔb a big, le Jekɔb mu ye Azig a big, le Azig mu ye Abiraham a big, le Abiraham mu ye Tera a big, le Tera mu ye Naho a big.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: le Naho mu ye Serug a big, le Serug mu ye Reu a big, le Reu mu ye Peleg a big, le Peleg mu ye Iber a big, le Iber mu ye Sela a big.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: le Sela mu ye Kanann a big, le Kanann mu ye Afagzad a big, le Afagzad mu ye Sem a big, le Sem mu ye Nowa a big, le Nowa mu ye Lameg a big.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: le Lameg mu ye Metusela a big, le Metusela mu ye Enog a big, le Enog mu ye Jarid a big, le Jarid mu ye Mahalalel a big, le Mahalalel mu ye Kanann a big.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: le Kanann mu ye Adam a big, le Adam mu ye Uwonbɔr a big.

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Juu yaab a kpakɔɔl liba daal, le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan nɔɔ kɔɔ bukpaab biba a poolin. Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin choon ki gen iyoo ki nug bi nɔɔn ŋma.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Le Farasii yaab biba nan baan bi ke, Ba ŋa ni tun nan wee ti siim kaa che ti tun likpakɔɔl daalaa.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Le Yisa nan jiin ki baan bi ke, Naa nyi ki karin Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin, nkom kaah nan choo Defid nin u tab wee u ŋa man nin naa?.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: U nan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin ki yuur kasi a boroboro ki ŋman, ki ŋa sei u tab, amaa nsiim kaa nan wɔɔn nin uba nŋman boroboro nmɔn ni see biminmanib a binba.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Uninfosaal a Bija-n le ye likpakɔɔl daal nmɔn a yudaau.

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Juu yaab a kpakɔɔl liba daal jigin, le Yisa kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr. Le uja uba nan be niin le u nɔɔjiim kpo.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Le ban nan wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a siimin nin Farasii yaab nan be niin, ki ban bin lig njee Yisa li teb bininfob likpakɔɔl daalaa. Bi nan ban bin kan nan wee bi li ta bii u yinl le ki ke u bii likpakɔɔl daala.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Amaa Yisa nan nyi baah tiɛr man, le ki bei uja wan a nɔɔ kpo nan ke, Fii sir bininfob nan a nintogila. Le u fii ki tan sir niin.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Le Yisa baan bi ke, N baan ni le, ti siim wɔɔn ke nii likpakɔɔl daalaa: Ti ŋa nan ŋan bee nan biraa? Ti fee limiɛfol bee ti bii laa.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Le u nan lig bininfobin biɛn a num paam ki bei uja-n ke, Tann a nɔɔ nmɔn na. Le u tann ki miin mi jir pɔɔg biɛna.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Amaa le bi ŋuui nan fii Yisa bu takpem, le ki nan piin ki lolig baah li ŋaa Yisa man.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Le weemba daal le Yisa don lijɔɔl a paab, un tan miɛ Uwonbɔr, le ki miɛ ki tan wonn kutaag.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Naah nan wonn njakpanyiɛn ni, le u nan yin u bɔɔnɔɔb le ki gann binib piig nin bilee ki yin bi ke u tooi:.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: U nan gann Simɔnn nin Simɔnn a waal Anduru, nin Jemisi, nin Jɔnn, nin Filib, nin Batolomiu.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: nin Matiu, nin Tomasi, nin Jemisi wan ye Alifiu a bija nan; nin Simɔnn wan wee bi nan yi u ke Zealo ;.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: nin Judasi wan nan ye Jemisi a bija nan; nin Judasi Isikariya .

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan nɔɔ jiirin ki nan sir npaan nin. Le u bɔɔnɔɔb takpem mu nan be niin, le bininfob takpem nan be niin ki kpiɛ, bi nan nyan Judiya a tiŋin nin Jerusalam, nin nnyum takpem a timin Taya nin Sidonn a tingbaan yin le;.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: ban nan dan bin nan gbir u bɔr le ki sin kan liwiɛn tebilin. Ban wee nnaanbiim nan nɔɔn nan kan kutebuga.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Le bininfob biɛn nan ban bin maa Yisa a gbanann, npɔɔn kaah nan nya u gbanann nin ki che bininfob pɔɔgir n bu.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Le Yisa nan lig u bɔɔnɔɔbin ki bei ke: Tikaar be nim ban ye bilanjiimin bu, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a naam ye ni yaan le.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Tikaar be nim ban wee nkom jɔɔ ni daandaan nan, nan bu-n ni na li ji ki gbee. Tikaar be nim ban mɔr daandaan nan, nan bu-n ni na li la.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Tikaar be ni bu, n-yonn man wee bininfob nan ni, n-yonn man wee bininfob wiɛr ni, ki sugir ni, kaa ban ni yin tibir bu-n, Uninfosaal a Bigin bu.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Ni che ni li mɔn ni takpem lidaal nmɔn na, nan bu-n ni nyɔɔr tan be paabin yaba. Man a fala le bi baam nan jɔ ŋaan Uwonbɔr a linlem.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Amaa woo, nim bijɔɔndam, nan bu-n nim pii kan ni wiiba.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Woo, nim ban ji ki gbeeg daandaan, nan bu-n nkom na li choo ni. Woo, nim ban laa daandaan nan, nan bu-n ni na li mɔɔ tikpemɔr ki ji mɔɔ tinyinyinn.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Woo, nim ban wee bininfob biɛn pagir ni nan, nan bu-n man le bi baam nan pagir tibɔkar a linlem.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: N bei nim ban pelig n bɔr nan le: Ni li jiɛ ni dataam, ki li tun nan ŋan ki tenn ban nan ni.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Kaab ti kaar ki ŋa ban poog imɔɔŋ ki jaar ni bu nan ki li miɛ Uwonbɔr ki tenn ban bii ni.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Wan pebiŋ kitapag kiba ke, a labir kan gurin sei un peb. Wan yuur a daala takpeŋ ke, laa ke u laa yuur kuwan bigin ni.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Wan mu miɛŋ nan ke, a sei mu. Wan fee a wan yɔɔr ke, laa ji ban a gaa jiin ni.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Ni li tun tenn bininfob ke naah ban bi li tun tenn ni man nin na.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Ni ye ni jiɛ ban jiɛ ni a binba ke, ni ye ba a nyɔɔr ki sei naa? Hali tibirdam tɔɔl jiɛ ban jiɛ bi-n le.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Ni ye ke ni tun nan ŋan ki tenn ban tun nan ŋan ki tenn ni nan a binba ke, ni ye ba a nyɔɔr ki sei naa? Hali tibirdam tɔɔl ŋaan man le.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
