local: Ni ye ke ni peŋ ban wee ni ji na li ga-n ke, ni ye ba a nyɔɔr ki sei naa? Hali tibirdam tɔɔl peŋ man le ki tenn tibirdam, ki jin na gaa bi wan yɔɔr nmɔn a mɔb.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Amaa ni li jiɛ ni dataama, ki li tun nan ŋan ki tenn ba, ki li peŋ ba, ki laa tiɛr ke bi li yɔ jiin ni. Ni nyɔɔr na li yab, le ki ji na li ye Uwonbɔr wan be paab nan a buwaab, nan bu-n u ma tun nan ŋan ki tenn ban a num kaa gbee niba nin bipootondam.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Ni li jɔɔ linyunyunbasanl ke ni Baa kaah jɔɔ linyunyunbasanl man nin na.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: Ni laa ji bininfob tibɔr wee bi nan laa ji ni tibɔri. Ni laa bii bininfobi wee bi mu laa nan bii ni. Jiɛn per bininfoba wee bi mu nan jiɛn per ni.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Ni li pu, wee bi mu npu ni nin nmag ŋam man, ki nyii nyii, ki dam dam, wee li nan gbee nin imɔgban, ki nin nan wur, ni pan bu. Nan bu-n kiyiikpoŋ kan wee ni jɔɔ magir tiwann yɔɔr ki pu bininfob nan, bi mu li ta kima le ki mag ki pu ni.

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Le Yisa ji gaa bi libɔŋal ke, Ujɔɔn li fir da ujɔɔn kpiɛgaa? Bi biɛn kaa li lu libogilin naa?.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Wan tinn kaa jon wan wɔɔnn ni, ni ye wan tinn biɛ diɛ ke, u ni yin nann wan wɔɔnn le.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Ba ŋa wee a kan lidabul lan be a kpiɛg a numbil ni, wee kaa sin kan lidagenl lan be a baantɔɔl a numbil ni nan naa?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: A ŋa nii, ki bei a kpiɛg ke N jɔ, che n nyan lidabul lan be a numbil ni nan, le kaa sin gar lidagenl lan be a tɔɔl a numbil ni nan naa? Fin ulinlaril nan, choo ki nyan lidagenl lan be a numbil ni, wee ki nin fir kan nbamɔm ki nyan lidabul lan be a kpiɛg a numbil ni.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: Kisiŋaŋ kaa lo tiloonbiri, le kisibiig mu kaa lo iloon ŋan ni.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Bisib a loon le bi jɔɔ biɛr bi. Baa ker ikinkan bikunkom bu ki mu kaa ker igbiin kigansiig bu.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Uninfo ŋan tun itoon ŋan le, nan bu-n u sufu ni ŋan le, le kuninfobiig mu tun titoonbir, nan bu-n u sufu ni kaa ŋan ni. Nan bu-n nan gbee uninfosaal a sufu ni, ni ma le u pag.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: Ba ŋa wee ni yim Uyudaan, Uyudaan, kaa sin tun maah len man naa?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Wan chaan n ban ki pelig n bɔr ki chee ki tun maah ban man nin, n li wɔɔn ni waah nann man.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: U nann uja wan nan maa u niin nan le. U nan gbii le jiir tum ki tan pii kutangbapiig, le ki bil lifiɛl tɔɔn li bu ki maa. Ni bɔɔn bu le utaal dan ki nan nun takpem, le nkpen gbee, le kunyunmaŋ don ki nan tel iniin nmɔn, amaa yaa nan lu, waah nan maa i timaaŋann nin bu.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Amaa wan gbir n bɔr nan wee kaa chee nan, u nann uja wan mu nan maa u niin litanbiiril bu wee kaa nan gbii jiir tum ki bil lifiɛl nan le, nnyum nan gbee ki don ki nan tel iniin nmɔn le i lu ki ber pelpel.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Yisa kaah nan len man wee ni kɔɔ bininfob a tuli yin ni, le u jon Kapeniɛm a tiŋin.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Bitɔb a sanliiru nan be niin ki jɔɔ u yom wan wee u num nan gbee, le nwiɛn choo u, ni nan gur wama le nin un kpo.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le bitɔb a sanliiru win nan gbir Yisa bu, le ki tun juu yaab a ninkpeeb, ke bin tan bei u wee u dan ki nan che u yomu nan n pɔɔg.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Baah nan foo Yisa ban ni, bi nan balin u le nin kinimaŋ ki ke, Uja nmɔn kpaan nin a ŋa man sei wa.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: nan bu-n u jiɛ ti ninfob, ki ji maa kuwonjiɛndiig sei ta.

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Le Yisa nan fii ki nɔɔ bi. Waah nan tan mal iniin nmɔn yaan ni, le bitɔb a sanliiru win tun u jɔtiib ke bin bei Yisa ke: Uyudaan, laa bagir a ba ki chaan ni, maa kpaan nin a dan ki nan kɔɔ n niin nan yaan ni.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Ni ma bu le n lig ki miin maa kpaan nin n foo a ban ni. Amaa pagim a mɔbonl wee n yomu nan n pɔɔg.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: N baantɔɔl ye wan wee tininkper a pɔɔm ŋmagim le, le n mu ŋmag bitɔtogib. Ki li fir tiɛn uba bu ke Li chaau le un jo, ki tiɛn utɔ bu ke Dan, le un dan, le ki bei n yomu ke ŋa nan na, le un ŋa man.

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yisa kaah nan gbir man nin, uja nmɔn a bɔr nan gar u le, le u gbiɛ lig kininfogbalɔŋ kan nɔɔ win, le ki len ke, n bei ni le, maa laa nyi kan uninfo wan jɔ yada nan a bosu, hali nin Iziral yaabin gbaa.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le bijab ban u nan tun ni nan labir jir u niin, ki tan foo ki miin u yomu nmɔn pɔɔga.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Naa kan yuuri le Yisa nan jon kitiŋ kan wee bi yi ki ke Neyin ni, U bɔɔnɔɔb nin bininfob takpem le nan nɔɔ u.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Waah nan foo kitiŋin a timansanmɔnn n-yonn man ni, le u kan baah tu nkum chaa bin tan pii. U takpiir nmɔn nan ye lipukɔɔl liba a bigaŋ le, le kitiŋ nmɔn a ninfob takpem nan be lipukɔɔlin ban.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: Uyudaan kaah nan kan lipukɔɔl nan, le kunyunyunbaag choo u, le u bei u ke, Laa mɔri.

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: Le u jon ki tan maa nkumin, le ban nan tun nkumin sir. Le u ke, Unaachipɔɔn, n beiŋ le, fii paaba.

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Le utakpiirin nan fii kal paab, ki piin ki pag, le Yisa ta u ki ŋubin u na a nɔɔn.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Le bijawaam nan choo ban biɛn nan be niin nmɔn, le bi don Uwonbɔr a yinl. Ki len ke, Ulinlenn takpen le kpiirin ti sisiigin. Uwonbɔr le dan ke u nan ter u ninfob.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Yisa a bɔr nmɔn nan mɔɔn Judiya a tingbɔŋin nin ntim man nan mal nin yaan nmɔn biɛna.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Le Jɔnn a bɔɔnɔɔb nan bei u ni niɛ nan biɛn, le u yin u bɔɔnɔɔb bilee.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: ki tun bin tan baan ke, Fin li ye wan nan yin dan ni naa, bee ti la li lig u nsan buu.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Bijabin kaah nan foo Yisa ban ni le bi bei u ke, Jɔnn Wan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunful nan le tun ti ke ti nan baaniŋ ke, Fin le ye wan nan yin dan ni naa bee ti le la li lig u nsan buu.

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa nan che bibaarib takpem pɔɔg, le ki nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin ki ligir bijɔɔm takpem.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Le u bei Jɔnn a tooi ke, Ni li jir ki tan bei Jɔnn naah kan nan nin naah gbir man: Bijɔɔm ligir, le nwɔbim choon, le nkɔm kan kutebug, le bitukpaab gbir, le bitakpiib fii nkumin, le bi mɔɔn tibɔŋann ni ki sei bilanjiim.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Tikaar be wan kaa jɔɔ nbiɛnim n bu nan.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Jɔnn a tooi yin kaah nan jir a bɔɔn bu, le Yisa nan piin ki pag bininfob ki jiin Jɔnn paam ke: Naah nan jon lisagilin ni nan tan kan baa? Igbingbaan yan wee nfaam daarin bee?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Naa ye man ke, ni nan jon ni tan kan baa? Uninfo wan yee tiwann yɔɔr ŋanin bee? Aai, bininfob ban yee kudaapɔɔrug a wann ki be bi wiibin be ibɔrniin le.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Amaa ni nan jon ni tan kan baa? Ulinlenn naa? Nn, n bei ni le, u yab jon ulinlenn na.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: U bu le ni nan sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: N li tun n toom wee un liir a nintogil, Wan yin mann a dam a sam.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: N bei ni le, baa laa nyi ma uninfo uba wee u jon Jɔnn ni; amaa wan joor Uwonbɔr a naamin nan yab jon Jɔnn na.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Le bininfobin biɛn, hali nin bilanpogaab, baah nan gbir Yisa a mɔboi, nan chee ke Uwonbɔr a sam ŋan, nan bu-n Jɔnn kaah nan fun bi Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin bu.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Amaa Farasii yaab nin ban nyi Uwonbɔr a siim nan kaa nan ga Uwonbɔr a mɔleen kaah ye man ki sei bin ni, nan bu-n Jɔnn kaa nan fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful li.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Le Yisa nan jiin ki bei bi ke, Nim daandaan nan a ninfob, n yin ta ni ki bigin baa? Bi ye ninfo bol baa?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Bi nann nbuwaab kaah ka kinyaŋin ki yi tab ke: Ti pee iwol ki tenn ni, le naa waa. Ti mɔɔr tikpemɔr ki tenn ni, le naa fabin ni.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Nim daandaan nan a ninfob ni nann nbuwaab nmɔn le, Nan bu-n Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin nan dan kaa ji tijiir kaa nyu ndaami le ni ke, U jɔɔ kinaanbiig le.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Le Uninfosaal a Big nan dan ki ji ki nyu, le ni ji ke, Lig uja nan, u ye ujijir nin udanyur le, ki ye bilanpogaab nin tibirdam a jɔ.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr a lam wɔɔn miba ban ga mi ban le.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Farasii yaab uba le nan yin Yisa ke un dan ki nan ji kujɔɔg a jiir u niin, le Yisa nan jon uja nmɔn a niin, le bi nan lagin ki ka ji.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Upii uba mu nan be kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ki tun tibir. Waah nan nan gbir ke Yisa be iniin nmɔn yaan ki ji tijiir nan, le u nan dan niin ki ŋmag tulaali a lulog .

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa nan kɔɔ ntim takpemin nin ntim bumin ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a naamin kaah yin be man a bɔŋann, le u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee-n nan nɔɔ u.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: ki nan sir Yisa a bɔɔn u taafar paam le ki mɔr le u nyinyinn nan lur Yisa a taa far bu le u ta u yukper ki pir Yisa a taai, le ki ŋa tulaali ki ŋa u taai nmɔn.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: Bipiib ban wee u nan che nnaanbiim nyan bi ni nan nin waah nan teb ban a wiɛn nan mu nan be niin ki nɔɔ u, Meri nan ye u ba . Wan wee nnaanbiim milile nyan u ni nan.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Farasii a jau wan nan yin Yisa nan kaah nan kan man, u nan tiɛr u sufu ni le ke, Uja nan bi ye ulinlenn ke, u bi li biɛ upii wan maar u nan kaah ye wan, ke u ye tibir daan le.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: le biba mu ye Kuza a poo Joana nin Suzaana nin bipiib takpem. Kuza nan ye ubɔr Herɔd a niin a toontunn ninkpelu le, Le bi biɛn nan jɔɔ bi tɔɔl tɔɔl a wan yɔɔr ki tenn Yisa, ki ter u ma nin u bɔɔnɔɔbin biɛn.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, Simɔnn n jɔɔ limɔbonl liba le ki yin beiŋ, le Simɔnn jiin bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn, beim ni.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Bininfob nan nyan ntim ki dan Yisa ban. Baah nan nan yabig n-yonn man nin le Yisa gaa bi libɔŋal ke.

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Bijab bilee le nan jɔɔ wan peŋ bininfob ilig a pɔnl. Uba nan jɔɔ u pɔnl nkpalam kobiŋmu, le utɔ mu jɔɔ nkpalam piŋmu.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: Ukpaar uba le nan jon bukpaabin ke u tan yaal idi. Waah nan yaal nan, le iba lu nsanpoolin, le bininfob taan i bu, le nnɔɔm mu ji pii ki jin.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Bi bilee nmɔn bi ni uba kaa nan fir yɔn ni. Le u nan jiɛn per bi biɛn ke bi li chaau. Tɔ, a tiɛr ki miin bi bilee nan ŋma le ni nan yin jiɛ wan pen bi ilig nan a bɔr takpem maa.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Le iba mu lu kutintiɛg bu, ki pan weem le ki ji kpo, nan bu-n nin yaan kaa nan sɔɔn ni.

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Le Simɔnn jiin u ke, N tiɛr wan a pɔnl nan yaab wee u jiɛn per win le. Le Yisa bei u ke, A jɔɔ ibamɔn.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Le iba lu ikunkon a bol, ki lagin pan nin ikunkon nin, le i ŋuuri ku.

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Ki nan gbiɛ lig upii nmɔn, le ki bei Simɔnn ke, A kan upii nan waa? Maah kɔɔn a niin nan ŋaa seim nnyum wee n ŋunn n taai, amaa upii nan ma ta u nyinyinn le ki ŋunn n taai, le ki ji ta u yukper ki pir pir n taai.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Le iba mu lu ntanŋam bol, ki pan nbamɔm, ki yabig ki lon jon baah nan bun man ni nfim kokobig. Yisa kaah nan gaa bi libɔŋal nan ki diɛ n-yonn man ni, le u bei bi ke, Wan jɔɔ itul ke un gbirim.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: Ŋaa poor mi amaa u ma nan sɔɔn le ki poorim n-yonn man wee n kɔɔn a niin nan ki nan kpaa daandaan nan.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔb nan baan u libɔŋal nan a taai.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: Ŋaa biɛ nug n yukper nkpami amaa upii nan ma faan n taai le tulaali.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: Le u ke, Nim bu ke Uwonbɔr kpiir ki wɔɔn ni ki che nim biɛ tibɔr woon kaar ki jiin U naamin paam, amaa biba ma, n gaa bi ibɔŋal le, ke bi li lig, kaa yin kan ni, ke bi gbir kaa yin biɛ ni taai yi.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: Mambu N beiŋ le, n jiɛn u toonbir takpem ki per wa, ni ma bu li che u bam man takpem. Amaa wan wee n jiɛn u toonbir wama ki per u nan, u mu jiɛm wama le.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Libɔŋal nan a taai ye nan le: Ndibimin li ye Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Le Yisa nan bei upii-n ki ke, A kan tibir a jiɛn pera.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Le ndibim man lu nsan nan nann bininfob ban gbir n bɔr wee ulinlaril dan ki nan choo nyan bi sufu ni ke bi laa ŋaam yada wee ki nan kan fee jaa nan.

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Le bicham ban gur ki nan ka ji nan, nin baan tab ibinbaan ke, Ŋma sɔɔ, wee hali ki li fir jiɛn tibir ki per aa.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Man mu nan lu kutintiɛg nan bu mu nann bininfob ban gbir n bɔr nin npopeen, wee ta laa jiir bi sufu ni nbamɔm n le; bi ŋa Uwonbɔr yada n-yonn wama nan le, tɔ, le nton pii bi ke, le bi yii n nɔɔm.

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Le Yisa bei upii-n ke, A yada feeŋ gaba. Mambu li chaa nin nposɔɔn na.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Man mu lu ikunkon ni nan, nann bininfob ban gbir Uwonbɔr a bɔr wee ki sin ji che bi sufu be dulinya nan a njɔɔm nin linunchɔril bir lan be dulinya nan yaan ni, ti nyagin Uwonbɔr a bɔr n bu le bi sufu ni, le taa yin lo iloon ni.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Amaa man mu lu ntanŋam a bol nmu nann bininfob ban jɔɔ gbir sei nin ban a sufu ŋan, ban gbir tibɔr, ki ŋmag ti kenken, ki jɔɔ nnyiginim le ki lo iloon.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Uba kaa nyɔɔ fila wee ki ta kiyiikpoŋ chigin ki bu, bee ki ta siin gadu a lɔŋin ni. See ke u taa tɔɔn niwann nan kper bu, wee bininfob ban biɛn kɔɔn ke, bi kan nwonween nin.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Weem ba daal le Yisa nan si Genesareti a mɔgilin a gbaam, le kuninfogbaluŋ nan si ki jin u ki pelig Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Tibɔr tan biɛn woo nan, li nya npaan nin, le tiwann tan biɛn nan be libunbɔnlin li kpiir ki nya nwonween ni.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Tiŋalin tilee mu nan si limɔgil nmɔn a gbaam, le bijanchoob nan jiɛn ki sin jon niba paam ke bin tan ŋunn bi janlann. Yisa kaah nan kan tiŋalin nmɔn nin.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Mambu, ni li nyi naah gbir man nin. Tɔ, wan jɔɔ ke, u li mann gaa ki kpiɛ, amaa wan kaa jɔɔ ke, u li lann nan wee u tiɛr ke u jɔɔ-n gbaa.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: le u nan kɔɔ Simɔnn yaagin, ki bei u ke un tir ki jiin nnyumin wamau. Waah nan tir jiin nin, le Yisa nin ka kuŋaluŋ nmɔn yaan ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Iyaayoo le Yisa a watiib nin u na dan u ban, amaa le baa nan fir fo u ban ni, nan bu-n bininfob le nan yab.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: Waah nan pag ki diɛ n-yonn man nin, le u bei Simɔnn ke, Tir kuŋaluŋ nmɔn ki kɔɔn nnyum man yaan nyɔɔ wee ki jaa ni janlann wee ki choo ijan.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Le uba nan bei u ke, A watiib nin a na be nkaafiin ki ban bin kaŋ.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Le Simɔnn bei u ke N ninkpelu, ti won ban ijan kunyiɛmuŋ taa kan niba. Amaa ŋaah len man nin bu n li jaa tijanlann nmɔn.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ban gbir Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki chee wee ki tun nin ni samin bi ma ye n watiib nin n na.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Baah nan ŋa man nin, le bi choo ijan takpem, le bi janlann nin nan nan chinn.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Weemba daal le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin; le u bei bi ke, Ni che ti lɔŋ limɔgil nan a nan bu a dool, le bi piin bi sanchonl.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Ni ma bu le bi nan yin bi kpiɛtiib ban nan be kuŋaluŋ kumiɛn nin yaan ke bin dan nan ter bi. Le bi nan dan ki nan gbeen bi ŋalin tilee nmɔn biɛn le ti nan piin ki miig.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Baa nan piin ki chaa nan, le Yisa dɔɔn ki jiɛn. Le ntafapɔɔrim nan dan limɔgilin a poolin le nnyum ni fii ki kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin a poolin ki nan ban min gbee, ni nan ye bin kɔɔ kidaŋin le.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Simɔnn Piita kaah nan kan man nan, le u gbaan Yisa a taatum, ki bei u ke, Fɔɔr n ban ki li chaau, Uyudaan, n ye tibirdaan le.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin dan u ban ki nan fiin u njiɛmin ki ke, Uninkpel, uninkpel, ti li nɔɔ nnyum! Le u fii ki tiɛn ntafaamin nin kunyungbɔŋ nbu ke min ŋmin na, le mi ŋmin chii.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Nan bu-n ni nan gar u ma nin u tabin le, baah nan choo ijan ni man nin.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: Le u baan u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, Ni yadaa be laa? Le ni gar bi, le bijawaam nan choo bi. Le bi baan tab ibinbaan ke, Ninfo boliba yen naa? Wee u pag sei ntafaam nin kunyungbɔŋ wee mi chee u mɔb baa.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: ni nan ye man le ki sei Jemisi nin Jɔnn, Simɔnn a kpiɛtiib ban nan ye Zebedi a naachimin. Le Yisa bei Simɔnn ke, Laa san bijawaami. Naah nyan dinan ki jɔɔ chaa, ni yin mɔɔn bininfob le ki tenn Uwonbɔr.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan tan foo Gereza, kitiŋ kan be Galilii a biɛŋ a doolin.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Le bi nan tir bi ŋalin nmɔn don nkpengbaan, ki jiɛn bi wan yɔɔr biɛn nin yaan le ki nɔɔ Yisa.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Le Yisa nyan kuŋaluŋ a poolin, le Gereza a tiŋ a jau wan nan jɔɔ nnaanbiim nan, nan kenn u. Ni nan yuur ke waa yee tiwann yɔɔri, u mu kaa ji nan be iniin ni, amaa le ki sin dɔɔ tikarin.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Yisa nan be kitiŋ kiba ni-n, le uja uba dan u ban ki ye kikɔŋ. N-yonn man wee u kan Yisa, le u lu ki ta u num chigin tum ki balin u ke, Uyudaan, ni ye a ban ke, a li fir che n pɔɔg.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: Waah nan kan Yisa nan le u yigin nmɔpɔɔn ki gbaan Yisa a nintogil, ki tiɛn ke, Yisa wan ye Uwonbɔr wan be paabin a Bija, min nin fin babu? N baliŋ le, laa mugir mi.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Le Yisa tann u nɔɔ ki maa u, ki ke N ban man. A wiɛm diɛu! Libol nmɔn yaan, le u kan kutebug.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Nan bu-n Yisa pii nan pag nin tininkper a pɔɔm ki sei nnaanbiimin ke bi nya uja-n yaan na. Isaŋ takpem ni nan fii u bu le, hali nin baah nan lol u nɔɔi nin u taai nmɔn biɛn ni, u nan ti chinn tikulorin le, le nnaanbiim nmɔn nan ti taar u ki jo kukunkɔɔg bu.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Le Yisa bei u, Laa bei uba, amaa li chaa Uwonbɔr a minmanu ban ki tan ta a ba ki wɔɔn u, ki mann kumanug ki sei Uwonbɔr ke Moziz a kaal kaah wɔɔn man nin. Ni ma ye siɛra ki sei bi.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Le Yisa baan u ke, A yinl laa? Le u ke, N yinl ye Tiyaba, Nan bu-n nnaanbiim takpem le nan be u ni.

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Amaa Yisa a yinl nan mann mɔɔn le nin yaan biɛn. Le bininfobin biɛn nan lagin u ban ke bin gbir u bɔr, ki ban ke un che bibaarib ban be bi ban nin pɔɔg.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Iyaayoo le nnaanbiim nmɔn nan gbaa balin Yisa ke u laa che bin jiir kubonyɔɔŋ nan yaan ni.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Amaa le Yisa yaan ti nyan ki jon u binba a bol ki tan miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Idool takpem li nan be ligungonl a paab ki ji, le nnaanbiimin nan balin Yisa ke un che bi tan kɔɔ idoolin; le Yisa nan sei bi nsan.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Weemba daal Yisa kaah nan be ki wɔɔn le Farasii yaab nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim ban nan nyan Galilii a timin nin Judiya a timin nin Jerusalam a timin nan ka u ban. Le Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm nan be u ban le u che bibaarib kan kutebug.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Nnaanbiim kaah nan nyan uja-n ni ki nan tan kɔɔ idoolin; le idoolin biɛn guun burburbur, ki nyan ligungonl a paab, ki jiir limɔgilin, ki nɔɔ nnyum.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Le bijab biba mu nan dan ki tu ubaar wan a gbanann nan kpo, likinkenlin, ki ban bin ta u ki kɔɔn kudiig kan yaan wee Yisa nan be-n, ki tan ta u ki bil Yisa a nintogil.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Tɔ, bidookpaab nan kaah nan kan man nin, le bi nan guun libol nmɔn, ki tan mɔɔn tibɔrin nmɔn, kitiŋin nin kitingbaai.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: Amaa baa nan kan npaan ki li kɔɔ, bininfobin kaah nan yaabin bu, le bi nan ta u ki don kudiig a paab, ki feer limɔɔpinl le ki ta u nin u wan dɔɔnwaŋ jiir bininfobin a sisiigin ki tan bil Yisa a nintogil.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Le bininfob dan ke bin nan lig niwann nan ŋa nmɔn. Baah nan foo Yisa ban, bi nan kan uja wan wee nnaanbiim nan nyan u ni nan kaah ka Yisa ban, ki pɔɔ, le u binbem nan ŋan, le ki yee tiwann yɔɔr, le bijawaam choo bininfobin biɛn.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: N-yonn man wee Yisa kan bi yada-n, le U bei uja wan a gbanann kpe-n ke, N jɔ, n jiɛn a bir periŋa.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Le bininfob ban nan kan nin binumbimin bei bi Yisa kaah ŋa man wee uja wan nan jɔɔ nnaanbiimin kan kutebug.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Le Farasiim nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a samin tiɛr bi sufu ni ke, Ninfo boliba ye u ki sugir Uwonbɔr aa? Uba li fir jiɛn tibir per u kpiɛg kaa ye Uwonbɔr a binba.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Le Gereza a tiŋ a ninfob biɛn dan Yisa ban ki nan balin u ke u nya bi tiŋin na, bijawaam kaah nan choo bin bu. Le Yisa nan jir kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin ki fɔr.

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Yisa nan biɛ baah tiɛr man na, le ki baan bi, Ba ŋaa wee ni tiɛr man ni sufu ni naa?.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: Uja wan wee nnaanbiimin nan nyan u ni-n nan balin ke un nɔɔ Yisa, amaa le Yisa nan ŋɔɔr u, ki ke.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Maah bei ubaarin ke, N jiɛn u bir ki per waah nin maah li bei ke Fii ki li chaau. Ni la ba pɔɔ?.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: Li jir chaa kun a niin ki tan mɔɔn Uwonbɔr kaah tun nan seiŋa. Le u nan jir ki jin ntim nmɔn biɛn, ki mɔɔn litoonl takpenl lan wee Yisa tun ki sei u.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Amaa wee n che ni biɛ ke Uninfosaal a Big jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm dulinya nan yaan ki li jiɛn tibir per… Le u bei uja wan a gbanann kpe-n ke, N beiŋ le, Fiim, ki yuur a wan dɔɔn waŋ ki li chaa kun na.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: N-yonn man wee Yisa nan labir jir ki tan don limɔgilin ki miin kuninfogbaluŋ li too u nin npopeen, bi biɛn pii nan guu ki nyi u dam bu.

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Libol nmɔn le u fii bi numin, ki yuur u wan dɔɔn waŋ, ki chaa kun, le ki choon ki pagir Uwonbɔr.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Uja uba nan be niin le bi yi u ke Jairu; u nan ye kuwonjiɛndiig a ninkpelu le; le u dan ki nan gbaan Yisa a nintogil ki ke un dan u niin.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Le ni gar bininfobin biɛn. Le bi pag Uwonbɔr, ki san u bijawaam, ki bei ke, Dinan ti kan ligaril a toonla.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: nan bu-n u bisiɛg gaŋ wan nan jɔɔ ibin piig nin ilee nan li bu, ki ban un kpo. Yisa kaah nan chaa-n, le kuninfogbaluŋ nan mug ki nin nan tir u.

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa nyan iniin nmɔn yaan ki chaa, le ki tan kan ulanpo gaar ka bilanpogaab a nanbonn, bi nan yi u ke Liifai. Le Yisa bei u ke, Li nɔɔm ni.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Le upii wan nan kan tipiir ibin piig nin ilee wee naa diɛrin nan be niin. Uba kaa nan fir teb u pɔɔgi.

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Le Liifai fii ki jiɛn u wan yɔɔr biɛn le ki nɔɔ u.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: Le u dan ki nan sir Yisa a bɔɔn ki maa u daala a gbaam. Libol nmɔn yaan le u piirin diɛ.

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa nan yin u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee ki lagin tab, le ki sei bi npɔɔn nin tininkper a pɔɔm man wee bi li fir nyan nnaanbiim biɛn, ki sin ji li fir teb iwiɛn biɛn.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Le Liifai ŋa tijiir takpem u niin ki sei Yisa; le bilampo gaab nin bininfob takpem nan dan ki nan lagin jin nin bi.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Le Yisa baan ke, Ŋma maam maa? Le bininfob biɛn niɛ, le Piita bei u ke, Uninkpel, bininfobin kaah yabin bu le bi tiriŋ.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: U nan tun bi ke bi li choon ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a naamin a bɔra, ki li teb bibaarib ba.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Amaa le Farasii yaab nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin nan fu Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb ban ki ke, Ba ŋa wee ni lagin ki ji ki nyu nin bilampo gaab nin tibirdamaa.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Amaa le Yisa ke, Uba maa ma, n biɛ ke npɔɔn nyan ngbanann nin na.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: ki ji bei bi ke: Ni laa yuur niba ki ta choon nsan nmɔn ni, ni laa ŋmag njaangbiɛmi, ki laa ŋmag ipɔgi, ki laa ŋmag tijiiri, ki laa ŋmag iligi, ki laa ŋmag daala ilee mu.

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Le Yisa jiin bi ke, Ban pɔɔ kaa ban utebiri, see ban bu-n.

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Ni nan che upii nan kaa ji nan fir ki woo uba Yisa ban ni, le ki nan nyan npaan nin ki sin jegil, ki nan gbaan Yisa a nintogil, ki bei u bininfob biɛn a numbilin nan bu wee u maa u, nin waah kan kutebug weem man.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Ni kɔɔ iniin yan yaan ke ni li be niin na nin naah na li nya kitiŋ nmɔn yaan.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Maa dan ke n nan yin bibuchim le, amaa tibirdam wee bin kpenn bi binbem.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, N bisa, a yada li che a kan kutebug. Li chaa nin nposɔɔn na.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Kitiŋ kan yaab kaa chee gaa ni wee ni yin nya ke, ni pir ni taai a tann ki jaa niin na wee nin wɔɔn bi kaa che a siɛra.

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Le bi nan bei Yisa ke, Jɔnn a bɔɔnɔɔb lolig imɔɔŋ le ki miɛ Uwonbɔr iyoo biɛn man le Farasii yaab a bɔɔnɔɔb tɔɔl ŋaan, ke ba ŋaa wee iyoo biɛn a yaa bɔɔnɔɔbin ma ji ki nyuu.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yisa kaah la nan be pag man nin, le u ninfo uba nyan Jairu a niin ki ke, A bisa-n kpo, laa ji che wan wɔɔnn ji fala ki chaan a niin ni.

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Man le u bɔɔnɔɔbin sin nan nyan ki choon ntinbumin ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki sin teb bibaarib.

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Le Yisa jiin bi ke, Ni li fir che bicham ban dan bin nan ji Lipiichanil a jiir nan n lol bimɔb n-yonn man wee lipiichanil daan la be bi ban nan naa?.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Le Yisa gbir waah pag man, le ki bei Jairu ke, Laa san bijawaami. Fin len ŋam yada ke u li pɔɔg.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Galilii a bɔrbig Herɔd nan nan gbir niwann nan tun ni biɛn, le u sufu nan ŋmarin, nan bu-n bi ba nan ke Jɔnn li fii nkumin.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: N-yonn chaan, le bi na li nyan lipiichanil daan nin bi ban; iyaayoo le bi li lol bimɔb.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Yisa kaa nan foo Jairu a niin nin, waa nan chee uba n nɔɔ u ki kɔɔ, see Piita nin Jɔnn, nin Jemisi, nin kibigin a baa nin ki na.

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Le Yisa gaa bi libɔŋal ke, Uba kaa li chee ki jii litanl daala pɔŋin ki tan ta legin daala kpegi. Ni ye u ŋa man ke u chiɛ daala pɔŋin na, litanl pɔnl nin litanl kpegil kaa piɛ.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: Le bi ba mu ke Uwonbɔr a linlenu Elaja le degir u ba npaan ni, le bi ba mu ke iyoo yan jon ni a linlem uba li fii nkumin.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Le bininfob biɛn nan be mɔr kibig nmɔn a kpemɔr. Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni laa mɔri man. Waa kpo. U jiɛn le.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Uba mu kaa li ta ndapɔm ki ŋuun libokpegilin ni. Ni ye u ŋa man wee ndapɔmin nan mɔɔ ke, mi li pɔɔ liboolin, le ndaamin wur yɔli, le libool n mu bii.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Amaa le Herɔd bei ke, N nan che bi tugir Jɔnn a yula, ke ŋma a bɔr wee n ji gbir nan naa? Le ki nan gbar un kan u.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Aai see u ta ndapɔmin ki ŋuun liboopɔnlin.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Le bi laa u ki nyi waah kpo.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Wan mu nyun ndakpegim ke, waa ji ban ndapɔmi, u li ke, Ndakpegim mɔ ki jon ndapɔma. .

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le u tooi nan nan jirin ki nan bei u baah tan tun man. Le u nan ta bi le bi jon kitiŋ kan wee bi yi ki ke Befisada nan bi binba.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Amaa le Yisa choo kibigin a nɔɔ ki ke, N big fiim.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Le u foorug nan jirin, le u fii sir libol nmɔn. Le Yisa bei bi ke bin sei kibigin tijiir wee un ji.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Amaa le kuninfogbaluŋin nan nan gbir man, le ki nɔɔ u ki jon. Waah nan kan bi ki miin bi chaan n-yonn man nin, le u gaa bi ki wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr a naamin a bɔr, le ki che ban kaa nan pɔɔ kan kutebug.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: Le ni gar u baa nin u na takpem. Le Yisa kpaan bi ke bi li nyi bin bei uba nan tun nan bu.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Nween kaah nan nan ban min lu-n, le U bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee dan u ban ki nan ke, Che kuninfogbaluŋ nan jo ntinbumin nin tikpaann nin ki tan ban tijiir ki ji nin didɔɔbol, nan bu-n taah be nan yaan nan ye kulɔŋ le.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni tɔɔl n sei bi tijiir wee bin ji. Le bi bei u ke, Taa jɔɔ niba ki kpiɛ boroboro kon iŋmu nin ijan ilee nan bu, mambu ke ti li jo le ki tan daa tijiir ki nan jin kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: Amaa le u bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, Ni che bininfob nmɔn n por ki kal piŋmu piŋmuu.

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn nan che bi por ki kal ikur ikur.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Le u yuur boroboro kon iŋmu-n nin ijan ileen le ki lig paab ki sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, le ke war war ki sei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke bi li tol bininfobin na.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Le bi biɛn jin ki gbee. Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin nan lagin ti jiir tan wee bininfobin jin gbee ki gurin, ki gbeen tikachar piig nin tilee.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Weemba daal le Yisa nan be miɛ Uwonbɔr u binba, le u bɔɔnɔɔb nan be u ban; le u baan bi ke, Bininfob ke n ye ŋma.

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Le bi jiin u ke, Bi baa ke a ye Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin le, le biba mu ke Elaja le, le biba mu ji ke Uwonbɔr a linlem ban nan kpe-n a ni uba li fii nkumin.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Amaa nim ke n ye ŋma? Le Piita jiin u ke, A ye Uwonbɔr a Masiya le.

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Le Yisa nan sei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ikpaan nbamɔm ke bi laa bei uba tibɔr nmɔn ni.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: Le ki ji bei bi ke, Ni kpaa le ke min Uninfosaal a Big n ji fala takpem. Le Juu yaab a ninkpeeb nin kumanug a ninkpeeb nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nan li yiim, ki ji kum, amaa iweehn itaa daal, Uwonbɔr li fiinim nkumin.

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Le u bei bi biɛn ke, Uninfo wan ban un nɔɔ min ke, see ke, u yii u ba ki yuur u dapunpunl idaan biɛn ki li nɔɔm.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Wan jɔɔ u miɛfol ki debin nin, u na li lan li, amaa wan mu kaa debin u miɛfol min bu ke, u na li kan li.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Ni ye uninfo kan dulinya nan a wan yɔɔr biɛn wee ki sin lan u miɛl ke, ba a nyɔɔr wee u kan naa?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Wan sam ifa ki jiin n mɔboi nan paam ke, Uninfosaal a Big mu na li san u ifa lidaal lan wee u nan dan nin u pag nin u ba a pag nin kasi a malakamin.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, ban si do nan biba kaa li nyi kpo see ke bi nan kan Uwonbɔr a naamin.

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Waah nan pag man nmɔn, ni bɔɔn bu a ween iniin daal le u yuur Piita nin Jɔnn nin Jemisi, ki ta bi donn lijɔɔl a paab ke un tan miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: waah nan be miɛ Uwonbɔr nan le u num paam nan kpenn. Le u wan yɔɔr nan penn ki nyagin ke nŋmi peen ni.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Le bijab bilee, Moziz nin Elaja.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: nan degir nin linungbeel a pag, ki pag nin Yisa ki jiin u kum paam, nin waah na li kpo Jerusalam ki gbeen Uwonbɔr a bam man.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Amaa njiɛm nan jɔɔ Piita nin u tabin pam, le bi jiɛn ki nan jee ligir ki kan waah pag nin bijab bilee ban lagin si u ban nin.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Bijab nmɔn kaah nan fɔɔr Yisa ban ni, le Piita bei u ke, Uninkpel, ni ŋan nin ti li be do. Che ti ŋa tinanbonn titaa, kuba li ye a yaag, le kuba mu ye Moziz yaag wee kan guri mu li ye Elaja yaag.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: Waah la nan be pagin, le kitilangbɔŋ degir ki nan piin bi bu, le bijawaam choo Piita nin u tab nmɔn baah nan kɔ kitilangbɔŋin ni.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Le bi gbir likukɔl pag kitilangbɔŋin yaan ki ke, N bija wan wee ngann win li yen ni gbirim u mɔb.

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Likukɔl nan kaah nan pag diɛ-n, le bi kan Yisa a binba kaah paar ki si niin. Le bi nan ŋmag tibɔr nmɔn bi binba kaa bei uba iyaayoo nmɔn baah kan nan ni.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Naah nan won kutaag wee bi nan nyan ligungonlin a paab ki jiirin ni, le kuninfogbaluŋin nan too u.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Uja uba nan be kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn yaan, ki tiɛn ki yin Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn, n baliŋ le, nan lig n bija nan; u ma le ye n bigaŋ.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: kinaanbiig le fii u bu ki che u kuun ki nan lur likpikpiil ki nan bir, le kumɔsangbugbalug nya u mɔnn, ni le ŋaan u man le kaa ban nin jaa wi, ni na li bii u.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: N balin a bɔɔnɔɔb ke bin nyan kinaanbiig nmɔn, le bi walin gur.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni daandaan nan a ninfob ban kaa jɔɔ yada ki jɔɔ kuŋmaruŋ nan, n yin yuur ni ban nii ki li jɔɔ limɔril ni naa? Le ki nan bei uja-n ke, Taa a bija nmɔn dan do.

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: Hali kibigin kaah nan chaan Yisa ban n-yonn man nin, le kinaanbiig nmɔn nyiɛ u tum le u dɔ liɛr, iyaayoo le Yisa tiɛn kinaanbiig nmɔn bu, ki che kibigin kan kutebug, le u ta u ki sei u baa.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Ni nan gar bininfobin biɛn le nin Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm kaah yab man. Ni la nan gar bininfobin le waah tun litoonl lan nin bu, le Yisa bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: Ni loor ni tuli ki gbir nan wee n yin pag nan nbamɔm: Bi na li ta Uninfosaal a Big ki ŋubin bininfob a nɔɔn.

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Amaa baa nan biɛ naah ye man ni. Uwonbɔr le nan degin bi lam ke bi laa biɛ; le bi ji san bijawaam nin bi baan tibɔr nmɔn a taai.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Limɔkpel li nan dan u bɔɔnɔɔbin a sisiigin, le bi ke, ŋma ye wan jon ti biɛn ti sisiigin nan naa?.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Le Yisa biɛ baa tiɛr man. Le ki yuur kibig ki siin bagin u ba.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: Le ki bei bi ke, Wan gaa kibig nan n yinl bu ke, u gaa min le; le wan mu chee gaam ke, u gaa wan tunim n muu. Nan bu-n wan ye ulanjiin ni sisiigin ni u ma ye wan jon ni biɛn.

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Le Jɔnn bei u ke, Uninkpel, ti nan kan uja uba le u yi a yinl ki nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin, le ti bei u ke un jiɛn na, waah kaa ye ti ni uba-n bu.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Ni laa ŋɔɔ wi, nan bu-n wan biɛn kaa kɔnn ni ke, u si ni bɔɔn le.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: N-yonn kaah nan nan mal nin Yisa nin do Uwonbɔr a niin nan, le u nan pɔɔn u ba ki gaa Jerusalam a sam ke un jo.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Le ki tun biba le bi liir nsan ki jon Samariya a tiŋ big kiba ni, bin tan ŋa sili ki guur u.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: amaa kitiŋ big nmɔn a ninfob kaa nan gaa wi, nan bu-n u nan jo chaa Jerusalam le.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔb Jemisi nin Jɔnn kaah nan gbir man nan, le bi baan u ke, Ti yudaau, a ban ti yin nŋmi le nya paab ki nan gaa baa.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Le Yisa nan labir ki fuun bi bu.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: le u ma nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan jon kitiŋ kiba ni.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Baah nan be nsan bu ki chonn chaa-n, le uja uba nan bei Yisa ke, N li nɔɔŋ ki jo nan yaan biɛn wee a chaan.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Tiŋɔgir jɔɔ ifal yan yaan wee bi kɔɔ, le nnɔɔm man laan paab nan mu jɔɔ titiɛr tan yaan wee bi dɔɔ, amaa Uninfosaal a Big kaa jɔɔ waah dɔɔ nan yaan ni.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Le ki ji bei uja uba mu ke, Li nɔɔm ni. Le uja nmɔn bei u ke, Uyudaan, che n chinn ki jo tan pii n baa wee.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Che bitakpiibin pii bitakpiib tab. Amaa fin li chaa ki tan mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a naam nan a bɔr.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Le uba mu bei u ke, Uyudaau, n li nɔɔŋ, amaa che n chinn ki tan chabir n niin yaab wee ki nin jirin nan nɔɔŋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Uba kaa li ta u nɔɔ ki paan linaakuul bu ki ko wee ki sin ji gbiɛ lig u bɔɔn ni. Ni ye wan gbiɛ lig u bɔɔn man ke, waa ŋan nin u ŋa Uwonbɔr a naam nmɔn a toontun wi.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Ti yudaau nan gann bijab pilile nin bilee, ki tun bi bilee bilee wee bin chinn choon ntimin man yaan biɛn wee u li jo.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Ke bei bi ke, Idi beer ki yaba amaa le bidichiɛb kaa yabi. Mambu, ni li miɛ Uyudaan wee Un kpiɛn bidichiɛb, wee bin chiɛ idi nmɔn.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Ni li chaau man! N tun ni le, ke npeebumin igbingbel a sisiigin.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Ni laa ŋmag ilig a pɔgi, bee lipɔgil, bee inaatagi, le ki laa poor uba nsan bu.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Ni yin kɔɔ iniin yan yaan ke ni chinn ki len ke, Nposɔɔn li be iniin nan bu.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Ni ye nposɔɔn daan be iniin nmɔn ke, ni posɔɔm nmɔn li paar u bu, naa ye man ke ni nposɔɔn nmɔn ji li jirin ni ban.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Ni kɔɔ iniin nmɔn ke, ni li ji ki nyu nan biɛn wee bi li sei na. U toontunn ŋan nin un gaa u yɔr. Ni laa lɔɔl iniin ki dɔɔ.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Ni foo kitiŋ kan wee bi gaa ni ki sei ni tijiir tan ke, ni li ji timau;.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: ki che bibaarib npɔɔg kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, ki bei bi ke, Uwonbɔr a naamin mal na.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Amaa ni ye ni foo kitiŋ kan yaan wee baa gaa ni ke, ni nya kitiŋ nmɔn a sanjal bu ki len ke.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: Hali ti taai kaah tabin ni tiŋ nan a tann tan ni, le ti pir gabir ki chabir ni nan. Amaa ni li nyi ni nan: Uwonbɔr a naamin mala.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: N bei ni le, lidaal nmɔn Sodom yaab yaal na li sɔ kitiŋ nmɔn a ninfob yaal.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Woo, Korazin yaab! Woo, Befisada yaab! Nan bu-n ni ye ligaril a toon biɛn nan nan bi tun Taya nin Sidonn yaab a sisiigin ke naah tun ni sisiigin man nan ke, bi bi li jiɛn bi toonbir weem, ki yee npoobiin a wan yɔɔr, ki pug bi gbanann nfatam, wee ni wɔɔn ke bi jiɛn bi toonbira.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Amaa tibɔr a ji daal ke ni tudaril na li jo Taya nin Sodom a ninfob yaal.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Nim Kapeniɛm yaab, Uwonbɔr li don ni papaab baa? Aai, u na yin jiin ni Kitakpiin tiŋ le.

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Wan gbir ni bɔr ke u gbir n bɔra; wan yii kaa gbir ni bɔr ke, u yii n mu a bɔra; amaa wan mu yii n bɔr ke, u yii Uwonbɔr wan tunim a bɔra.

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Pilile nin bilee nan nan jirin nin npopeen le ki bei u ke, Uyudaan; hali nnaanbiim chee sei ti le a yinl bu.

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Le u jiin ke, N kan Sintani kaah nyan papaab ki lunn ke utaal li nyagin ni.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: N sei ni tininkper a pɔɔm man wee ni li taa iwaa nin inɔɔn bu, ki nyaŋ kidataag Sintani a pɔɔm biɛn; niba kaa li ŋaa ni.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Amaa ni laa che ni mɔ ni nnaanbiim kaah chee ni mɔbin a binba bu, ni che ni li mɔ na ni yin kaah sɔb dɔ Uwonbɔr a niin nin bu.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, Kasi a Naaŋ kaah nan gbeen Yisa ni nmɔn ni, le u ke, N Baa, paab nin tum a yudaau, n pagiŋ, nan bu-n ŋaah ta tibɔr nan ki woon bilandam nin bibiɛndam, amaa le ki sin kpiir wɔɔn nbuwaab finfim. Nn, n Baa, ŋaah ban nin tun man le yen.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: N Baa le ta ni nan biɛn ki seim; uba kaa nyi Bija-n kaah ye wan ni see Baa nmɔn a binba, le uba mu kaa nyi Baa nmɔn kaah ye wan ni see Bija-n nin ban mu wee Bija-n gann ki kpiir ki wɔɔn ni.

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Le Yisa nan gbiɛ lig u bɔɔnɔɔb le ki woon bei bi ke, Tikaar be inunbil yan kan nan wee ni ga nan bu.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Nan bu-n N bei ni le ke, bilinlem nin bibɔrtiib takpem nan ban bin kan tiwann tan wee ni kan nan le, amaa kaa kan ni. Bi nan ban bin gbir tibɔr tan wee ni gbir nan le, amaa le kaa gbiri.

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Kulaguŋ kiba ni le ban nyi nsiim chobchob nan a ni uba nan fii sir ki ban un mag Yisa, le ki baan u ke, Wan wɔɔn, n yin ŋa niin wee ki kan limiɛl lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm maa.

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Le U jiin ke, Ni sɔ siim a gbɔŋin ke nii? A karin ki nii.

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Le uja nmɔn jiin ke: Li jiɛ Uyudaan Uwonbɔr, ki li jiɛ U nin a sufu biɛn, nin a pɔɔm biɛn nin a potiɛm biɛn; ki li jiɛ a kunkɔɔn tau ke ŋaah jiɛ a ba man nin. .

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, A jiim ŋan, li tun man na, wee ki nan kan limiɛfol nmɔn.

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Amaa uja nan nan ban un wɔɔn ke waa jɔɔ tibiri; le ki baan Yisa ke, Tɔ ŋma lee nin ye n kunkɔɔn tau waa.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, Uja uba li nan nyan Jerusalam ki chaa Jeriko; le ban feen nan kenn u, le ki pir u wan yɔɔr ki fee u, ki boo u takpem, ki nan gur wama nin bin ku. Le ki jiɛn u.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le uminmann uba mu nan nɔɔ nsan nmɔn ki chaan ki nan kan uja nmɔn kaah dɔ, le u lɔg ki jon ki sin jiɛn u.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Man nmɔn jigin le Liifai a jau uba mu nan kan u, ki mu lɔg ki jon ki jiɛn u bil.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Amaa le Samariya a jau uba mu nɔɔ nsan nmɔn ki chaan, ki nan kan uja nmɔn. Waah nan kan u n-yonn man ni, le kunyunyunbaag choo u takpem.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: le u dan u ban ki nan ta nkpam nin nnyɔg ki ŋa u biɛi ki ta itan ki lol, le ki ta u paan u boŋ bu ki jon i chaan niin ki tan ŋmag u nbamɔm.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Le ni wonn kutaag le u ta ilig ki sei wan guu ichanniin nin ki ke, Li lig uja nan wee n nan jiirin ke n ji li yɔ ŋaah jin nan ki kpiɛ u bu. .

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Yisa kaah pag man ki diɛ-n, le u baan wan wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nan ke, Bininfob bi taa nan, u laba ye baah fee wan nan a kunkɔɔn tau waa.

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Le wan ŋma nsiim nbamɔmin jiin ke, Wan san u kunyunyunbaagin li ye u kunkɔɔn tau. Le Yisa bei u ke, Tɔ li chaa ki mu ta li tun man na.

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan chaa bi sam, ki nan tan foo kitinbig kiba ni, le upii wan bi nan yi u ke Maata nan, nan gaa Yisa tichann u niin.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: U nan jɔɔ u naal uba, bi yi u ke Meri, le Meri nmɔn nan kal tum ki mal Uyudaan a taai paam ki pelig u bɔr.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Amaa le Maata ma be ki gbaar nin kuchɔŋ a ŋaam, le ki nan dan nan bei Yisa ke, N yudaau, maah gbaar n ŋaaŋ kuchɔŋ n binba wee n naal nan kaa terim nan, a baga kaa be nii naa? Bei u wee u nan terim.

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Le Uyudaan jiin ke, Maata, Maata, n nyi ke a gbaar nin tiwann takpem n bu le, le ni mugiriŋ ki bii a sufu.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: amaa niwann niba li kpaa talar. Meri ma gann ni wan nan ŋan le, le uba kaa li fir nyan ni u ban ni.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Nweehn nba daal le Yisa nan be niba yaan ki miɛ Uwonbɔr. Waah nan miɛ diɛ n-yonn man, le u bɔɔnɔɔb n-yaa ni uba bei u ke, Uyudaau, wɔɔn ti ta li miɛ Uwonbɔr man ni, ke Jɔnn kaah nan wɔɔn u bɔɔnɔɔb man nin.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Le U bei bi ke, Ni yin miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, ni len ke: Ti Baa, che a yinl le be kasi, che a naam n foon.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Sei ti lidaal biɛn a jiir.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Jiɛn tibir ki per ta ke ti mu kaah jiɛn ban bii ti a bir ki per bi man nin ki laa liin ti ki kɔɔn libiginlin ni. .

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Le U bei bi ke, Ni ye ni ni uba jɔɔ u jɔ, wee ki jon u jɔ nmɔn ban kunyiɛgtasiig, ki tan bei u ke, N jɔ ni ye a jɔɔ kisaabaag ke, a seim ni.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: nan bu-n n jɔ uba le nyan lisanchonl ki foon n niin le n mu kaa jɔɔ tijiir tiba n sei wi.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Le a jɔ wan be ndiin ni beiŋ ke Laa mugirimi. N kpal linaanganla. Min nin n buwaab dɔ jiɛn le, maa ji li fir fii ki seiŋ niba.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, waa yin fii ki sei u tijiir nmɔn waah ye u jɔ-n bu, amaa waah kaa san ifa wee ki miɛ win, see ke u le nan fii ki sei u waah ban man.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Mambu n bei ni le: Ni li miɛu ke u li sei ni; Ni li ban na ke ni li kan naah ban nan, ni li kpaar linaanganla ke li li loor ki sei ni.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Nan bu-n wan biɛn miɛ ke u li gaa, le wan biɛn ban mu li kan, wan mu kpaar ke, linaanganl li loor ki sei u.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Nim bibaam, ni ye ke ni bija miɛ ni ujan ke ni ŋma li ta liwaalabil ki sei waa?.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Bee ŋma wee u li miɛ lijenl wee un ta unɔɔn ki sei waa?.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Ke ni ye nim tibirdam nyi naah li sei ni buwaab liwanŋanl man ke, ni Baa Uwonbɔr wan be papaab nan man kaa ni li ta Kasi a Naaŋ nan ki sei bininfob ban miɛ win naa.

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Yisa nan ŋɔɔr kinaanbiig kan ye kuburug le nyann. Kinaanbiigin kaah nan nan nyan ni, le uja wan nan jɔɔ kinaanbiig wee ki birin nan pag, le ni nan gar kuninfogbaluŋin.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Amaa le biba bei ke, Nnaanbiim a bɔru Beelizebul a npɔɔn le U nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin.

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Le biba mu nan mag Yisa, ki ban siɛra kan nyan paab.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Le Yisa mu biɛ baa tiɛr man, le ki bei bi ke, Nnaam a ninfob kɔn nin tab ke, nnaam nmɔn yin gab yɔli le; le iniin iba yaab mu kɔn nin tab ke, iniin nmɔn kaa yin siri.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Ni ye Sintani mu bɔgir ki kɔn nin uba ke, u naam yin ŋa nii ki sir aa? Nan bu n len man ni ye naah ke n jɔɔ Beelizebul a pɔɔm le ki ŋɔɔ nnaanbiim.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Ni ke Beelizebul a pɔɔm le n nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin ke, ke ni mu, ŋma a pɔɔm wee ni ninfob jɔɔ nyann baa? Man ke bi ma li na li ye ni bɔjib.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Amaa ni ye n ta Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm le n nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin ke, man ke Uwonbɔr a naamin pii foon ni ban na.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Lijawagidil ŋa u sili nin u jawann ki guu u niin ke, uba kaa li fir bii u wan yɔɔri.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: amaa wan pɔɔ jon u nan kɔn ki nyaŋ u ke, u yin gaa u jawann tan wee u bi jɔɔ ter uba-n le, ki ji ta u wan yɔɔr ki tol bininfob.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Uninfo wan kaa nɔɔ n bɔɔn ke, u ye n dundaau le; wan mu kaa lagin bininfob ki tenn nim ke, u ŋɔɔ bi le.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Ni ye kinaanbiig nyan uninfo ni ke, u choon yelin kukunkɔɔg a paab le, ki ban lifooril a bol, wee kaa kan ke, u ti nan bei u ba le ke, N li jir iniin yan yaan wee n nan nyan nin.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Ni ye u jir tan foo ki miin bi tigin iniin ni ki mann ni ke.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: u jir le weem ki tan yin nnaanbiim milile ban bir ki jon u ma-n le ki nan kpiɛ u bu, le bi ni lagin be; le uninfo nmɔn a binbem li bii ki jo waah nan be man chinchinn nin.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Yisa kaah pag man nmɔn, upii uba nan be kuninfogbaluŋin yaan le ki pag nmɔpɔɔn ke, Tikaar be upii wan maŋ ki mɔɔŋ libiil nan.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Le U jiin ke, Tikaar be bininfob ban gbir Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki chee ti nan bu.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn kaah nan kpiɛgin le Yisa len ke, Daandaan nan a ninfob ban ye tibir dam kaa nɔɔ Uwonbɔr nan ban bin kan ligaril a toonl le ki ni ŋaam yada; amaa maa yin tun ligaril a toonl liba ki sei bi; see Uwonbɔr a linlenu Jona a garil a toonlin a binba.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Jona kaah nan ye limagil ki sei Ninefa a tiŋ yaab man nin, man le min Uninfosaal a Big mu yin ye limagil ki tenn daandaan nan a ninfob.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Tibɔr a ji daal upiibɔr wan nan nyan nnɔɔjiin paam nan na li fii sir paab ki galin daandaan nan a ninfob ki bii bi, nan bu-n waah nyan nan yaan kutingbɔŋ nan ki dan u nan gbir ubɔr Solomɔnn a lam gɔr kaam a wɔɔm; amaa uba be do ki yab jon Solomɔnn na.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Tibɔr a ji daal, Ninefa a tiŋ a ninfob na li fii ki pag ki bii daandaan nan a ninfob; Jona kaah mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki sei bin bu, bi nan nyan bi toonbirin na; ke liga, daandaan nan wan jon Jona be do.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: U ba kaa yin nyɔɔ fila ki ta ki ŋa nan yaan wo, bee ki ta kiyiig chigin ki bu. See ke u ta ki siin le ki ween le bininfob ban kɔɔn nin kan ki wonweem.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: A numbil li ye a gbanann a fila. A numbili ŋan ke, a gbanann biɛn yin gbee ni nween le, amaa a numbili kaa ŋan ke, a gbanann biɛn yin be libunbɔnlin le.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Ni li nyi man wee ki laa che nwonween man be ni gbanann nin nan n kpann libunbɔnli.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: Ni ma bu ni ye a gbanann biɛn ween, wee niba yan kaa bɔɔn ke, ni yin wonn le nbamɔm ke fila kaah ti ween ki too binum man ni.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Yisa kaah nan pag diɛn, le Farasii yaab uba nan bei u ke un nɔɔ u wee bin tan ji tijiir. Le u nan nɔɔ u jon ki tan kɔɔ kal ke un ji.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Amaa le li ŋa Farasii yaab a ninfo nmɔn ligaril nin Yisa kaah kaa nir wee ki sin yin ji tijiir.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Le Uyudaau nmɔn bei u ke, Nim Farasii yaab nir tiyir nin nsam a bɔɔn a binba le, amaa le ni sufu ni gbee nin kujaŋuuŋ nin npooton.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Nim tininfojɔr nan! Naa ye wan nan naan ni bɔɔn nin, u ma naan ni poolin mu.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Amaa ni ta nan be ni yirin nin ni samin ni ki sei faladam ma wee ni bɔɔn nin ni poolin biɛn le ŋan.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
