local: Woo nim Farasii yaab, ni yagir tikpinfar tan kaa yabin gbaa, nfim piig a poolin nba le ki tenn Uwonbɔr, kaa sin ŋaan nan ŋan ni, kaa ban Uwonbɔr i. Ni ŋan ke ni li ŋaan nan ŋan, ki li nyann ni wan piig a poolin n nba ki tenn Uwonbɔr a.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Woo nim Farasii yaab, Ni ban ni li ka ininfogbingbem a kikaabol le kuwonjiɛndiigin ki ban bininfob li poor ni nin jilima kinyaŋin.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Woo ni nann tikar tan kaa daag wee bininfob choon ti bu kaa sin nyi nan le.

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Le ban nyi Uwonbɔr a siim nbamɔmin a ni uba jiin ke, Wan wɔɔn, ni ye a len man ke a sug ti muu.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Nim ban nyi Uwonbɔr a siim nbamɔm nan, woo, nan bu-n ni ta nsiim man pɔɔ takpem nan le, ki ta kpiɛ Uwonbɔr a siimin bu ki ta wɔɔn bininfob ke bi li ŋmag nsiim nmɔn nbamɔma. Ki mu kaa sin yin kpiɛ bi bu wee bi ŋmagi.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Woo, nan bu-n ni maa Uwonbɔr a linlem a kar nbamɔm le ki tenn bi; bi ma ye bilinlem ban wee ni yaajam nan ku-n.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Naah tun man nan, ni ye siɛra le ki wɔɔn ke ni chee ni yaajam kaah nan tun man na. Bi ma nan ku bilinlem nmɔn, le ni mu maa bi kar.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Mambu le Uwonbɔr nin U lamgɔrikaam bei ke, N li tun n linlem nin n tooi bi ban, bi li ku bi bam le ki mugir bitab.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Ni ma bu bi li galin n-yonn nan a ninfob bilinlem ban biɛn wee bi nan ku hali dulinya nan a piinim.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: baah nan ku Ebiɛl n-yonn man ki nan kpaa Zakariya, wan wee bi nan ku-n Uwonbɔr a manug a binbinl nin iwonjiɛn niin n-yaa sisiigin nin. N bei ni le, bi li galin n-yonn nan a ninfob bilinlem nan biɛn a kum bu.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Woo, nim ban nyi Uwonbɔr a siim nbamɔm nan, naah nyan nyinyim a samin bu. Ni baantɔɔl kaa nɔɔ mi, le ki bii ban ban bin kɔɔ nan a sam.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Yisa kaah fɔɔr niin nan, le ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin nin Farasii yaab a ŋuul nan fii takpem, le bi piin ki baan ibinbaan ki jiin u bɔr tan wee u pag takpem nmɔn paam.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: bi nan pen u lam le ki ban u pag ni ba.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Bininfob tuturil nan lagin tab ki dan ki nan mug Yisa ban hali ki ni nan taan tab a talangbin, le Yisa nan chinn pag nin u bɔɔnɔɔb ki ke, Ni li nyi Farasii yaab nan nin bi danyugbeel nan bu man, bi ye bilinlarib le.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Tibɔr tiba li kaa be ki woo wee kaa na li nya npaan ni ni; bee baah na li biɛ ti.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Mambu, tibɔr tan wee ni pag libunbɔnlin nan, see ke ti nan nyan npaan ni; tan mu wee ni lɔɔg ki pag tidirin kaa ban uninfo a tuli n gbir nan, bi wo li ta ti mɔɔn npaan ni wee bininfob biɛn n gbir.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: N jɔtiib, n bei ni le, ni laa san bininfob bijawaami, bi lee li fir ku ni gbanann a binba le; amaa le kaa ji li fir ŋa ni niba.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: Amaa n li wɔɔn ni naah li san wan. Ni li san Uwonbɔr man, wan li fir ku tigbanann, ki ji jɔɔ npɔɔn man li fir ta ŋa nŋmi man kaa jɔɔ njunim nan yaan. Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, ni li san waah man.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Naa ye ijiɛn iŋmu le bi kɔɔr koobu bilee, amaa Uwonbɔr kaah tam bi ni uba bu.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Ni laa san bijawaami man, ke Uwonbɔr nyi ni yukper a kanl, mambu, ni jɔɔ tinyɔɔr ki jon ijiɛn takpem ma.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: N bei ni le, uninfo wan lig kan min bininfob a nintogil, min Uninfosaal a Big mu na li lig kan u Uwonbɔr a malakam ban.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: amaa wan mu ke waa nyim bininfobin ke, min Uninfosaal a Big mu na li ke maa nyi wi Uwonbɔr a malakam ban.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Wan pag limɔbonl ki bii min Uninfosaal a Big ke, Uwonbɔr li jiɛn u bir ki per u; amaa wan pag ki sugir Kasi a Naaŋin ke Uwonbɔr kaa li jiɛn u daan a bir ki per wi.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Ni ye bi choo ni ki ta ni dan tiwonjiɛndirin, bee ntim a ninkpeeb ni tininkper a pɔɔm ban, bee bibɔjib ban ki ke, bin pag ni bɔr ke, ni laa baan ni sufu ni ke ti yin pag ke nii wee ki nyan tiba tibɔrin ni.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Nan bu-n n-yonn nmɔn foon ke, Kasi a naaŋin li wɔɔn ni naah li pag man.

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Uja uba nan be kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn yaan le ki bei Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn, bei n kpel nan wee u bɔgir ti baa a faalin ki seim ni.

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, N jɔ ŋma seim npɔɔn ke, n ji ni tibɔr ki bɔgir ni baa a faal ki sei naa.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Mambu le Yisa nan mann pag kuninfogbaluŋin yaan ke, Ni li nyi kujaŋuuŋ bu man, naa ye njɔɔm takpem bu li yin che uninfo nkan limiɛl lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmi.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Ni ma bu le u nan gaa bi libɔŋal ke, Ujɔɔndaan uba a kpaab li nan naan tijiir takpem;.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: le u nan tiɛr u sufu ni ke, N lee ni yin ŋa nii, tɔ maa ji jɔɔ npaan man wee n li ta n jiir nan biɛn ki ŋa.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le u ke, N yin ŋa nan le, n yin worin n naapɔr nan biɛn le, le ki mann tii tinaapɔr takpen. Ni yaan le n li ta n dii nin n jikaar biɛn ki ŋa.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Le ki nin bei n ba ke, A jɔɔ tijiir tan li kpaan ibin takpem a poolin; li dɔ foora, ki ji ki nyu ki li jɔɔ npopeen.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Le Uwonbɔr bei u ke, Lijajaril nan! Kunyiɛg nan a li kpo. Wee n lig n jee a jɔɔm nan ŋma nin ye maa.

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Wan lagin njɔɔm ki tenn uba, wee kaa sin jɔɔ njɔɔm Uwonbɔr ban ke, u mu nann kujajɔrug nan le.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Le Yisa nan bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, Ni laa che ni mugir ni, nin binbemin nin naah li ji niwann nan ni, ki mu laa che ni mugir ni, nin naah li yee tiwann yɔɔr tan ni.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: limiɛl jon tijiira, le tigbanann mu jon tiwann yeewann na.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Ni lig nnɔɔm: Baa bu tijiiri, ki mu kaa chiɛ, ki mu kaa jɔɔ tinaapɔr bee tinanbonn ni, amaa, Uwonbɔr li jinn bi, naa jɔɔ tinyɔɔr takpem ki jon nnɔɔm maa?.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ni ŋma li fir fɔbig u miɛl ni wann nan wee u potiɛl mugir u nan buu?.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Ke naah kaa li fir tun ki wan nyinyirig nan, ba ŋa wee ni che ni mugir ni nan gurin buu?.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Ni lig nmɔɔpuum nin mi ŋam, maa tun itoon iba bee min loo ikinken ki sei mi ba; amaa n bei ni le nan, nin ubɔr Solomɔnn kaah nan jɔɔ u wan yɔɔr ŋan tan nan, waa laa nyi ki yee tiwann yɔɔr wee ti ŋan ke nmɔɔpuum kaah ŋan man ni gbaa.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Timɔɔr tan be dinan, wee won ke bin nyɔɔ ti nŋmi nan, le Uwonbɔr mann ŋaŋir ti le kaa li fir yeen nim maa? Nim ban a yada waar nan!.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Le ni laa gbaar ban naah li ji nan bee naah li nyu nan ni, ki mu laa mugir ni potiɛli.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: nan bu-n, ban kaa jɔɔ Ween gu nɔɔ tiwann nan le, amaa nim ma, ni Baa Uwonbɔr pii nyi ke ni ban tiwann nan le.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Amaa ni li ban Uwonbɔr a naamin na, wee un ta tiwann yɔɔr nan biɛn ki sei ni.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: N ninfob ban kaa yab nan, ni laa san bijawaami, ni Baa Uwonbɔr jɔɔ npopeen nin waah yin jin ni nnaam n bu.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Ni kɔɔr ni wan yɔɔr tan wee ni jɔɔ-n ki ta ni ligi ki sei bilanjiim ma, ni ban ilig a bɔɔlim man kaa li chari, ki ban lifaal lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi ki be Uwonbɔr a niin ni. Nan yaan wee unaayig kaa li fir kɔɔ ki tan su, wee ikootug mu kaa li fir mɔɔ ki bii i.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Mambu nan yaan wee a faal be nan, a potiɛl mu be niin le.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Ni yeem ni wan yɔɔr ki ŋa sili nin itoon na, ki che ni filaam li gaan na.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: ki li be ke bitoontum kaah ti guu bi yudaau wan jon lipiikɔɔnl a jiir a jim kaah li foon man ni, wee ki nan kpaar linaanganl wee bi loor weem man ni.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Bitoontum ban wee bi yudaau li foon ki miin bi guu u kunim nan npopeen ye bi yaan le. N bei ni ibamɔn le, u li ŋa sili ni itoon, ki che u toontum nmɔn kal le un yuur tijiir ki nan sei bi, le bin ji.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: Ni ye bi yudaau nmɔn nan foon kunyiɛgtasiig bee kikojamɔɔŋ, ki miin bi la guu u ke npopeen yin ye bi yaan le.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Amaa ni biɛm ni nan man: Ni ye u niin daan nyi n-yonn man wee unaayig li kɔɔn ki nan su u ke, waa yin chee un kɔɔ; u yin guu le.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Ni mu ŋaam sili ki li guu, min Uninfosaal a Big nan yin jiirin n-yonn man wee naa nyi bu n le.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Le Piita baan Yisa ke, Uyudaau, a gaa tim le libɔŋal nan bee bininfob biɛn naa.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Le Uyudaan jiin ke, Utoontunn u la baa jɔɔ nlam ki tun ke u baa kaah ke u tum man naa? U ma ye wan wee u baa yin che u ŋa bitoontum nmɔn a ninkpelu, wee ki li lig u toontum ban biɛn be u niin nmɔn, ki li tenn bi tijiir n-yonn man kpaan nin bin ji.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Ni ye u yudaau n nan kun ki miin u la tun man ke, npopeen yin ye utoontunn nmɔn yaan le.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, u yudaau nmɔn yin ta u jɔɔm biɛn le ki ta ŋa utoontunn nmɔn a nɔɔn ke u li liga.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Amaa ni ye utoontunn nmɔn yii ki ke u yudaau nmɔn kaa yin jiirin weemin, wee ki piin ki boo u toontunn tab, bijab nin bipiib, wee ki ji ki nyu ndaam ki gbeeg ke.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: u yudaau nmɔn nan yin jiirin lidaal lan, nin n-yonn man wee utoontunn nan kaa yin nyi le. U yudaau nmɔn na li dar u tul pam, ki ŋa u ke baah ti ŋaan tukangbir dam man nin.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Utoontunn wan nyi u yudaan kaah ban un tun man, ki mu kaa ŋaa sili, ki mu kaa ji tun ke, bi li boo inɔɔkul takpem.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Utoontunn wan kaa nyi u yudaan kaah ban un tun man, wee ki tun nan kaa bi ye un tun ke, see ke bi boo u wama, le ki ta u jaa. Wan wee bi sei u wee ni yab nan, u ma le bi na yin baan ki gaa nan yab; amaa wan wee bi sei u wee ni yab takpem nan u ma mu le bi na yin baan ki gaa nan yab takpem.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: N dan n nan nyɔɔ nŋmi le dulinya nan yaan. N bi ban ke na mi pii gaan le!.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: Amaa n jɔɔ nnyum a fum n gaa, ni yin ŋaam npoobiin le hali nin naah na li diɛ.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Ni tiɛr ke n dan n nan sei dulinya nposɔɔn lee? Aai, n bei ni le, amaa nbɔgirim.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Naah nyan dinan ki chaa, bininfob biŋmu lagin ki be iniin iba a poolin ke bi na li yiɛr, le bita nan bii bilee, le bilee-n mu li bii bita nmɔn.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Bibaam na li bii bi buwaab, le nbuwaab mu na li bii bibaam, binam na li bii bi bisiɛm, le nbisiɛm mu na li bii binam; yaabisiɛm na li bii bi chapinyam, le chapinyam mu na li bii bi yaabisiɛm.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Le Yisa nan pag bei kuninfogbaluŋin ke, Naah ti nan kan kitilangbɔŋ do liwonlul paam min, ni ti pag le weem ke utaal yin nu le, le un sin nan nu.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Ni ti nan kan ntafaam daar ki nyan nnɔɔgan paam ke, ni bei le ke kutuntoŋ le be, le nin sin nan toŋ.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Nim bilinlarib nan, ni ŋaa nii ki biɛr utaal nin kutuntoŋ ki bɔgir wee kaa sin li fir biɛ isaŋ nan ma a bɔgirim maa?.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Ba ŋa wee naa li fir ji nitɔɔl a bɔr ki gann nan ŋan ki sei ni baa?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Fin nin uba jɔɔ tibɔr, wee u ban un ta ti ki jo ibɔrniin ke, naah la be nsan bu ki chaa nan, choo u dunl weema, wee un laa ta ti ki jo, naa ye man ke u yin dar ti foo bibɔjib ban le, le bibɔjib mu li taaŋ ki sei wan guu kuloondiigin, le wan guu kuloondiigin taaŋ ki loon.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: N bei ni le, ŋaa li nyi nya, see ke a nan yɔn ilig yaan wee bi ke a yɔ-n biɛn hali koobu kaa yin guri.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Bilanpogaab nin titoonbirdam takpem nan lagin Yisa ban ke bi nan pel u bɔr.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Amaa le Farasii yaab nin ban wɔɔnn nsiim ŋulin ki ke, Uja nan gaan titoonbirdam le ki ji bi ban.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le bininfob biba nan be, le ki nan bei Yisa Galilii yaab ban wee Palati nan kun ki ta bi cham ki ŋmarin kumanug a cham.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Le Yisa nan gaa bi libɔŋal nan:.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Galilii yaab nmɔn kaah jin fala man nan, ni tiɛr ke bi toonbir jon bi tab yaar le?.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: Ni ye ni ni uba jɔɔ ipee kobig wee upee uba bor ke, waah li jiɛn piwiɛ nin iwiɛn kulɔŋ ki tan ban uba-n hali nin waah na li kan waa?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: N bei ni le, aai! Amaa naa kpenn ni binbem ke, ni biɛn mu na yin kpo man le.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: U nan kan u ke, u li ta u ki paan u bɔsiper bu nin npopeen.

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Likpakɔɔl liba daal, le Yisa nan tan kɔɔ Farasii yaab a yudaau a niin ke un ji tijiir; le ban nan be niin nmɔn nan dibir u.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Bee ligonl fufɔbil kaah nan lu ki ku bininfob piig nin biniin Siloom a tiŋin ni, ni tiɛr ke bi galin nan yab jon ban biɛn nan be Jerusalam nlee?.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: ki ta u kun iniin. Le ki nin yin u jɔtiib nin u kunkɔɔn tab ki lagin tab ki bei bi ke, N peeu wan nan borin, n kan wa, che ti li jɔɔ npopeen na man.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Iyaayoo le ubaar uba be u nintogil, u taai nin u nɔɔi li nan pugin.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: N bei ni le, aai! Amaa naa kpenn ni binbem ke, ni biɛn mu nan yin kpo man le.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: N bei ni le ke ni ye man le, ni ye ke titoonbir daan uba kpenn u binbem ke npopeen takpem li be paab ki jon bibuchim piwiɛ nin biwiɛ ban wee naa ji kpaa talar nin bin kpenn bi binbem nan buu.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Le Yisa baan ban nyi Uwonbɔr a siimin nin Farasii yaabin ke, Nsiim chee ke ti li teb bininfob likpakɔɔl daal be ti la teb aa?.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Le Yisa gaa bi libɔŋal ke, Uja uba le nan jɔɔ kiduusiig u kpaabin, weemba daal le u jon ke u tan laa tiduur, le kaa tan kan ni.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Ni ye upii uba mu jɔɔ nligibim piig, wee nba bor ke, waa li nyɔɔ fila ki tigin u diig nbamɔm nin waah na li kan maa?.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Le ki bei u kpaguur win ke, Ibin itaa sɔ le n gbaa chaan kiduusiig nan yaan ke n nan laa tiduur, le kaa ga niba. Ga ki gab ba! Ba ŋa wee ki si ki bii ntam yɔlii.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Amaa le bi ŋmin. Le Yisa choo uja-n ki teb u ki jaa u le u jon.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: Ni ye u kan mi ke, u yin yin u jɔtiib le nin u kunkɔɔn tab ki lagin tab ki bei bi ke, N kan nligibim man nan borin na, ni che ti li jɔɔ npopeen na man.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Le ki baan bi ke, Ni ye ni ni uba jɔɔ kibig bee u naa wee u nyan lu libonl a poolin likpakɔɔl daal ke, waa li nyan u weem likpakɔɔl daal nmɔn naa?.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Le u kpaguur win jiin ke, Uyudaan, jiɛn na wee nin ŋa libinl liba ki kpiɛ, n li gbii ki tum ki piin nkpatam ŋam.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Ni ye man le, n bei ni le, npopeen be Uwonbɔr a malakam ban ni ye tibirdaan uba nan kpenn u binbem ke.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Le baa nan fir len niba.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: Ni ye ki lon libinl daal ke, ni ŋan! Naa ye man ke, a gaa ki gaba. .

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Le Yisa ji tugir ki ke: Uja uba le nan jɔɔ nbijabum milee.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan bichamin kaah kal bi jilima a kikaabol bin ji tijiirin le u gaa bi libɔŋal ke:.

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Juu yaab a kpakɔɔl daal le Yisa nan be wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr kuwonjiɛndiigin kiba yaan.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Le wan ye kibigin nan bei u baa ke, N baa, n ban a ta lifaal lan wee n na yin ga-n le ki seim; le u baa por lifaal nmɔn ki sei u.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: Bi yiŋ lipiichanil a jiir a jim ke, laa kal jilimadam a kikaaboli. Nba ke bi yin wan joŋa ke u chaan.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: le upii uba nan be kudiig nmɔn yaan, le kinaanbiig nan che u ŋa kiwɔbig ibin piig nin iniin. U nan bɔɔ le kaa li fir naar kolibi.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Naa nan yuuri, le kibigin ta u faalin ki yaar jon kitiŋ kan fɔgin. Le ki jin u ligin nmɔn yɔli yɔli le i biɛn diɛ.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: Ni ye man ke, udindaan wan yin ni bilee nmɔn li dan ki nan beiŋ ke, Ta a kikaabol ki sei uja nan na. Le ifa nchooŋ le a kal nan yaan kaa ye jilima a kikaabol li.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan win, le u yin u nyann nintogil le ki bei u ke, Upii, a wiɛn nmɔn jaa ŋa.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: Iligin biɛn kaah diɛ n-yonn man ni, le nkom lu kitiŋ nmɔn yaan takpem, le u nin piin ki ye wan chonn miɛ.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Amaa ni ye bi yiŋ ke, kalim nan yaan kaa jɔɔ jilima a bol, wee ni ye udindaan ni nan dan ke, u li beiŋ ke, N jɔ, fii ki tan kal nan yaan jɔɔ jilima a bola. Man le a chantab a num li gbeeŋ.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: Le ki ta u nɔɔ ki paan upii nmɔn bu. Libol nmɔn yaan le u naar u tɔɔl le ki pag Uwonbɔr.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Uninfo wan biɛn don uba li jiir tum; wan mu jiin uba tum, li do paab.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Le u nan jon ki tan ta uba ki sei kitiŋ nmɔn yaan a ninfou uba, le u ta u ŋa u kpaab ban yaan wee u goon idoolin, ke u li ŋaan idool nmɔn tijiir.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Yisa kaah nan teb upii-n nmɔn likpakɔɔl daalin bu le kuwonjiɛndiig a ninkpelu a ŋuul nan fii le u bei bininfob ke, Iweehn iloob li be ki ye itoon a ween. Mambu ni li chaan iweehn loobin a sisiigin ki nan gaa kutebug, amaa ki laa ji chaan likpakɔɔl daal li.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Le Yisa nan bei wan yin u ke un nan ji-n ke, A ŋaa tijiir ke, a laa yin a jɔtiib, bee a kpetiib, bee a maalim, bee a kunkɔɔn tab ban ye biwandamin ni, ni ye a ŋa man ke, bi mu li yiŋ ki yɔŋ.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: U ni nan ban un ji idool a jiirin le ki gbeen u pool, amaa uba kaa nan sei u niba.

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Le Uyudaan jiin u ke, Nim bilinlarib nan! Ni ŋma kaa loor ni naau bee ni boŋ ki ta jo nkpen ki tan nyun ki likpakɔɔl daal laa?.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Amaa ni ye a yin yin bininfob tijiir a jim ke, a yim faladam, nin nwɔbim, nin ndiɛm, nin bijɔɔm.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: U lam kaah nan nan jirin ni le u baan uba ke, N baa a toontum be bi ŋaa ki ji tijiir ki gbeeg ki sin gur, wee nkom yin ku min doo!.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Le upii wan ma ye Abiraham a yaabil nan, le Sintani ŋa u tiyomir ibin piig nin iniin, le naa bi kpaa nin n nyan u tiyomir nmɔn yaan likpakɔɔl daal laa.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: le Uwonbɔr a kaar li be a bu. Hali baah kaa li fir yɔŋ nan, Uwonbɔr li na yin yɔŋ, lidaal lan wee bibuchim na yin fii nkumin nin.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: N li jir kun n baa a niin, ki tan bei u ke: N baa, n galin paab ki ji galin a muu.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Waah nan pag man n-yonn man nin, le u dataam biɛn nan jin ifa, amaa le kuninfogbaluŋin biɛn nan jɔɔ npopeen nin waah nan tun ligaril a toonl yan biɛn nin bu.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Ban nan lagin ki ka ji nin Yisa nan a ni uba kaah nan gbir man ni, le u bei Yisa ke, Ban na yin ji tijiir nmɔn Uwonbɔr a niin nan, tikaar ye bi yaan le.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: Maa ji kpaan nin a yim a bija; ŋaam a toontumin a ni uba u.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Tɔ le Yisa nan baan ke, Uwonbɔr a naangbɔŋ nann baa? N yin ta mi ki naŋ baa?.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Uja uba le nan be le ki nan ŋaan sili nin kuchiig takpeŋ a jim, le ki yin bicham takpem.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Le u nan fii ki jir kun u baa ban. Waah laa nan be nan fɔg ki chaa kun nin, le u baa kan u, le kunyunyunbaag choo u takpem, le u guun ki too u nsanbu ki tan bib u ki poor u.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: Mi nann nnyiɛnbim man wee uninfo ta bun u kpaabin nin le. Le min yabig ki ŋa busub, wee nnɔɔm dan ki nan tiɛ bi tar ki bun le.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Kuchiigin a yoo kaah nan nan foo-n, le u tun u toontunnu ke un tan bei bininfob ban wee u nan yin bi-n ke, Ni dan ke tijiirin biɛn ŋa siliu.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Le kibigin bei u baa ke, N baa, n galin paab ki ji galin a muu. Maa ji kpaan nin a yim a bija.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Le U ji nan baan ke, N yin ta Uwonbɔr a naangbɔŋ nan ki bigin baa?.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Amaa le bi biɛn nan piin ki ban isan yan wee bi li yii ki laa jo. Le chinchinn a ninfou win bei u toontunn nin ke, N daa bikpapɔm le ki ban n jo ki tan lig baa be man, mambu n ŋa gafarau.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Amaa le u baa bei u toontumin ke, Ni ŋaam weem ki yuur daala ŋaŋ ki yeen mu, ki ta kigbiɛŋ ki yee u nɔɔbila, ki tagin u inaataga.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: Uwonbɔr a niin a naam nann boroboro a nyɔgim man wee upii ta ŋmarin boroboro a yom man yabin wee min fugir ki do-n le.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Le uba mu bei u ke, N daa inaaja kokaa piig le ki ban n tan bigin ki lig bukpaabin. N ŋa gafarau.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Wee ni taan kinaajakpamigin ki ku ka. Ni che ti ŋa tijiir ki ji kunaachuŋ.

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Le Yisa kaah nan be nsan bu ki chaa Jerusalam nan, u nan nɔɔ ntintakpem nin ntinbumin, ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr;.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Le uba mu bei u ke, N le la kɔɔn upii le daandaan nan, maa li fir dan ni.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Nan bu-n n big nan nan kpo le ki ji jirin nin limiɛfol; u nan bora le n ji nan kan u. Le bi piin kunaachuŋ a jim.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: le uba nan baan u ke, Uyudaan, bininfob wama le na yin kan feegabaa? Le U bei bi ke.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Ki miin u bija uninkpelin laa nan be bukpaabin. Waah nan nan mal iniin ni, le u gbir ilan nin kiwaag.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Le u toontunn nmɔn jir kun u yudaau n ban, ki tan bei u bi biɛn kaah len man. Le liŋuul choo u. Le u bei u toontum n ke, Ni li chaa kitiŋ poolin a sambu ki tan yin bilanjiim nin nwɔbim nin bijɔɔm nin ndiɛm ke bin dan.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: Ni pɔɔn ni ba wee ki nan kɔɔ linaanganl lan kaa yalin nan yaan. N bei ni le, bininfob takpem na li ban bin kɔɔ niin, le kaa yin fir kɔɔ.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Le u yin utoontunn uba ki nan baan u nan ŋaan iniin.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Le u toontunn nin bei u ke, Uninkpel, n tan tun ŋa wɔɔnim man nin na, amaa npaan la be.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Ni ye uniindaan nan fii ki loon linaanganl wee ni paar ki si nkaafiin ki kpaar linaanganl ki ke, Uninkpel, loora wee ti kɔɔ ke. Amaa u li ke, Maa nyi ni; kaa nyi naah nyan nan yaan ni.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Le u jiin ke, A waalin le kun, le a baa che bi ku kinaajakpamigin waah foon kaa kan kidaŋ kiba ki jɔɔ npotiɛŋamin bu.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Le u yudaau ni bei utoontunn ke, Nyam ki li chaa kitintakpeŋin a san bu nin n sanbumin, ki che bin kɔɔn, wee n niin nan ngbee.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Le ni na li ke, Tim nin fin nan lagin ki ji ki nyu na; ke a wɔɔn ti Uwonbɔr a bɔr ti timin na.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: N bei ni le, ban wee n nan yin ni a ni uba kaa li len n jiir nan ni. .

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Le u kpelin a ŋuul nan fii le u yii nloob a kɔɔm. Le u baa nyan ki nan balin u.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Amaa le u ji li jiin ke, Maa nyi ni, kaa nyi naah nyan nan yaan ni. Nim ti toonbir dam nan biɛn, ni fɔɔr n ban na.

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Bininfob takpem le nan nɔɔ Yisa, le u nan gbiɛ lig bi ki ke:.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Amaa le u jiin bei u baa ke, Lig! Min le bɔɔ koŋ ibin nan biɛn le kaa la nyi yii a mɔbi. Amaa ŋaa la nyi ki ta kiŋɔɔbig ki seim ke n ku ki yin njɔtiib wee ti ji kunaachuŋi.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Ti mɔr na li be niin, nin inyin a ŋmam, n-yonn man wee ni na kan Abiraham, nin Azig nin Jekɔb nin bilinlemin biɛn kaah be Uwonbɔr a naam ni, amaa nim ma bi li ta ni ki gab nkaafiin.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: Ni ye uninfo dan n ban, wee kaa nan u baa nin u na, nin u piib nin u buwaab, nin u kpetiib nin u watiib nin u ninsiɛb, hali nin u tɔɔl a miɛfol ke, waa li fir ŋa n bɔɔnɔɔru wi.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Amaa a bija wan bii a faal biɛn, ki choon nɔɔ ipiijagɔɔr kaah nan kunn ni, le a ku kinaajakpamig ki sei u.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Bininfob na li nya liwonpɔɔnl, nin liwonlul, nnɔɔjiin nin nnɔɔgan paam ki nan kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin ki kal bi kikaabol ki ji tijiir.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Le uninfo wan mu kaa yuur u kum a dapunpunl ki nɔɔ min ke, waa li fir ŋa n bɔɔnɔɔru wi.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Le u baa bei u ke, N big, fin le be n ban le iyoo biɛn; le nan biɛn wee n jɔɔ, ye a yaal le.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Ibamɔn bibɔɔnpaab na li ŋa bisanliib, le bisanliib mu li ŋa bi bɔɔn paab.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Amaa ni ye ti ji kunaachuŋ le ki li jɔɔ npopeen, nan bu-n a waal nan nan kpo le, amaa le ki ji jirin u miɛfolin, u nan bora, le ti ji nan kan u. .

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Ni che ti len ke ni ye ni ni uba ban un maa ligonl lan fɔg ke, waa li chinn kal ki bon kudaag ki jee u ligi li foo bee yaa li foo?.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Farasii yaab biba dan Yisa ban, ki nan bei u ke, Nya do ki li chaa niba paam ma, Ubɔr Herɔd ban un kuŋ le.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Nan bu-n ni ye ke u fiɛ lifiɛl wee kaa fir maa diɛ wee wan biɛn nan kan ke u li laa u.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Le U jiin ke, Ni li chaa ki tan bei kuŋɔg nmɔn ke, Dinan nin won le n yin nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin, ki che bibaarib n pɔɔg. Iweehn itaa daal le n toon yin diɛ.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: Ki ke, Uja nan nan piin ki ma le kaa nan fir diɛ.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Ni le ye man ke, ni kpaa le nin n jo Jerusalam dinan, nin won, nin daalib. Nan bu-n ulinlenn kaa kpeg niba yaan ni see Jerusalam!.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Bee ti len ke ubɔr uba li chaa un tan to u bɔr kpiɛg bitɔb. Waah li chinn ki kal ki bon jee u li fir ta bitɔtogib turpiig ki nyaŋ ubɔr wan jɔɔ bitɔtogib turpilee nan naa?.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Jerusalam, Jerusalam, nim ban ku Uwonbɔr a linlem, ki yɔgir ban wee u tun ni ban ni itangbag ki ku bi. Iyoo biɛn le n gbaar le ke n lagin ni nba, ke ukool kaah lagin u koobum ki ŋa u jiɛgin man ni, le naa ban man ni.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: Ni ye baa li fir to ke, see ke u tun ntoon le bin tan balin baah la be nan yaan fɔg nan.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Ni lig man, Uwonbɔr jiɛn ni niin ki per na. N bei ni le, naa ji li kam mi nin naah na li ke, Tikaar be uninfo wan choon Uwonbɔr a yinl bu-n. .

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Man li ye ni, ni ye ni ni uba kaa jiɛn waah jɔɔ nan biɛn ke, waa li fir ŋa n bɔɔnɔɔru wi.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: N-yaam ŋan; amaa ni ye mi mɔmin nan diɛ ke mi ji yin ŋa nii ki mɔɔ?.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: Mi ŋa yɔli le nmɔn. Maa ji yin jɔɔ niba a ter li. Maa ji yin ŋan ki sei titann ni, le kaa ji yin ŋan ki sei lifatanpol mu. Bi yin tami gab le. Wan jɔɔ litul ke un gbirim.

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Le Yisa bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, Ujɔɔndaan uba le nan jɔɔ u toontunnu, le bi nan nan bei ujɔɔndaan nin ke, u toontunnu win bii u faal le.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Le ujɔɔndaan nin nan yin u ki baan u ke, Ba a bɔr yen wee n gbir a bu man naa? Wɔɔm ŋaah ŋmag lifaalin man, nan bu-n ŋaa ji li fir ŋa utoontunn ninkpel jigin ni.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Le utoontunn ninkpelin ni nan tiɛr u sufu ni ke, N yudaau ban un ŋɔɔrim n toon nan yaan le. N ni yin ŋa nii? Maa jɔɔ npɔɔn ki li fir ko, le ki mu sin san ifa nin n li miɛ.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: n nyi maah li ŋa man wee bininfobin gaam bi niin, n-yonn man wee n yudaau nan nan yii mi.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Le ki yin uyudaau a pɔndamin ubaba ubaba. U nan baan chinchinn a pɔndaan win ke, A jɔɔ n yudaau a pɔnl iŋaa.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Le u jiin ke, Olif a siigi a kpam galam kobiniin. Le utoontunn ninkpelin bei ke, Yuurim a pɔnl a gbɔŋ, ki tan kal ki sɔb galam kobina.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Le u ji baan wan pa-n ke, A mu jɔɔ lipɔnl iŋaa? Le u jiin ke, Idi botu turil. Le u bei u ke, Yuurim a pɔnl a gbɔŋ wee ki sɔb idi botu piniin.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Le utoontunn ninkpelin a yudaau pag u, ki ke u jɔɔ nlam. Nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a ninfob kaa jɔɔ nlam nin bin ter tab Uwonbɔr a toon yin ke dulinya yaab kaah jɔɔ nlam nin bin ter tab man ni.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: N bei ni le, ni ta dulinya nan a jɔɔm ki ban jɔtiib, wee ni nan diɛ ke, ni li kan nkikaa ŋan man be Uwonbɔr a niin kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Uninfo wan tun kitoonbig nin yada-n, uma mu le li fir tun litoonl takpenl nin yada. Le uninfo wan mu kaa li fir tun kitoonbig nin yada ke, waa li fir tun litoonl takpenl mu nin yada.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Mambu ni ye baa ŋaaŋ yada nin ŋaah ŋmag dulinya nan a faal ke, ŋma li ŋaaŋ yada nin ibamɔn a faalin yaan naa?.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Le ni ye baah ŋaaŋ yada nin a ŋma uba a faal ke, ŋma yin ta a tɔɔl a faal ki sei ŋaa?.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Uyom uba kaa li fir tun ki tenn biyudam bilee. U yin nan uba le ki sin ban wan gurin; bee u yin ŋaan uba le tiŋann le ki sin yin wan guri yɔli. Ŋaa li fir nɔɔ Uwonbɔr ki sin ji nɔɔ iligi.

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Le Farasii yaab, ban ban ilig takpem nan gbir Yisa kaah len man ni, le ki laa u.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Nim ban wɔɔnn ni ba ke ni ye bibuchim bininfob ban nan, amaa Uwonbɔr nyi ni sufu ni. Nan wee uninfosaal tiɛr u sufu ni ke ni ye niwann ŋann tan kagin, amaa Uwonbɔr ban ni ye yɔli le.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Bi nan mɔɔn Nsiimin nin Bilinlem a bɔr le hali nin Jɔnn kaah nan nan foon. Naah nan nyan iyaayoo ki nan kpa daandaan nan, bi mɔɔn tibɔŋann tan pag ki jiin Uwonbɔr a niin a naam npaam le, le bininfob mugir biba ke bin nan kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a niin a naam nmɔn yaan.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: Papaab nin tum na li bor amaa Uwonbɔr a siim man sɔb dɔ-n a wan big kaa yin nyi bori.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Uja wan biɛn yii u poo wee ki ji kɔɔn upii uba ke u tun iwanchɔgin toon na, uja wan mu taa upii wan a chal yii u ke u mu tun iwanchɔgin toon na.

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Ujɔɔndaan uba le nan be, ki yee itan ŋan iyoo biɛn, ki ŋaan waah ban man idaa biɛn nin npopeen.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Le bi nan ti jɔɔn ulanjiin wan miɛ wee bi yi u ke Lazarusi, ki chaan ki nan kaan ujɔɔndaan ni a niin a timansanmɔnn. Umimiɛr nmɔn nan jɔɔ ibiɛ le u gbanann biɛn.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: le ki ban ke u li pii isaabul yan nya ujɔɔndaan nin a jibol ki ji. Hali ibɔ mu nan ti chaan ki nan liɛ u biɛ yin le.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Le faladaan nin nan kpo, le malakam nan ta u jon Abiraham ban. Le ujɔɔndaan nin mu nan kpo, le bi pii u.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: Waah nan be nŋmi-n nton nin ni, le ki nan nan waan lig ki kan Abiraham nan fɔg, le Lazarusi be u ban.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Le ujɔɔndaan nan yin u ki ke, Ti Baa Abiraham, sam kunyunyunbaag ni wee ki tun Lazarusi un tan ta u nɔɔbil ki bɔ nnyumin ki nan ma n lanbil wee lin sɔŋ, nan bu-n n be limugilin le nŋmi nan yaan.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Amaa le Abiraham nan jiin u ke, N big, li tiɛr ŋaah nan be a miɛfolin ni a nan gaa a wan ŋann na, le Lazarusi mu nan gaa nan kaa ŋan na, amaa daandaan nan u ni be lifooril ni le a mu be limugil ni.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Nan kpiɛ li ye libogil lan nyɔɔ li dɔ tim nin fin a sisiigin le uba kaa li fir nya ti ban ki jo a ban ni, le uba mu kaa li fir nya a ban ki dan ti ban ni.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Le ujɔɔndaan nan jiin ke, Man ke n balin le, baa, tum Lazarusi n baa a niin.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: nan bu-n n jɔɔ watiib biŋmu le. Che un tan kpaan ba wee bi mu laa nan dan nan yaan wee n be ki ji fala nan ni.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Le Abiraham jiin ke, Bi jɔɔ Moziz nin Bilinlem; che bin pel bi bɔr.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Le u ke, Aai baa Abiraham, amaa ni ye uba li bi fii nkumin ki jon bi ban ke, ni ma le bi bi li kpenn bi binbem.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Le Abiraham bei u ke, Ni ye baa pel Moziz nin Bilinlem a bɔr ke, uninfo fii nkumin wee ki tan pag sei bi ke baa le yin chee u mɔbi. .

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Le Yisa nan bei U bɔɔnɔɔb ke: Tiwann tan li che bininfob ntun tibir li dan, amaa ni li bir ki sei naah dan wan bu.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Bi bi ta linaal ki lol jɔn u sulum ki ta u jaa limɔgilin ke ni bi sɔ, nin un che nbuwaab nan a ni uba ntun tibir.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Ni choom ni bau man! Ni ye a nabig tun tibir ke, a kpaan mu; le ni ye u kpenn u binbem ke, a jiɛn u bir ki per wa.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: Ni ye u ŋaaŋ tibir itaa ilile lidaal a poolin, wee ki dan a ban itaa ilile ki ke, n kpenn n binbem ma ke, a jiɛn per wa. .

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Le itoon ni nin bei Uyudaan ke, Kpiɛ ti yada ni.

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni ye ni bi jɔɔ yada wee ki yaab foo nnyiɛnbim ke, ni bi li fir bei busub nan ke, Ŋab ki tan ŋuun limɔgilin na, le busub nmɔn bi li chee ni mɔb.

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Ni ye ni ni uba jɔɔ uyom wee u ko bukpaab bee u kpaa ipee, wee uyomin nan nyan bukpaabin ki chaa kun ke, a li bei u ke, Dan ni ki nan kal do ki ji tijiiraa.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Naa ye u yin len le ke, ŋa n nyiɛg a jiir a, ŋa sili ki li guum maah ji ki nyu n-yonn man ni na; ni bɔɔn bu a ban ke a mu li ji ki nyuu?.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: U li chee sei u toontunnu nmɔn kunɔɔpoog waah chee ki tun nan wee u bei u ke un tun ni naa?.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: Man li ye ni mu, n-yonn man wee ni tun Uwonbɔr a toon yan wee u ta sei ni nan diɛ ke, le ni ke Ni ye ti toon le, taa kpaan ni uba n pag ti.

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Yisa kaah nan chaa Jerusalam n-yonn man ni, le u nan nɔɔ Samariya nin Galilii a tinpaam a sisiigin.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: Waah nan tan foon kitiŋ kiba ni-n, le bijab piig ban ye nkɔm nan to u. Bi nan sir nan fɔg le.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: ki yin nkukɔpɔɔn ke, Yisa, Uninkpel, san ti kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan bi n-yonn man ni, le u ke, Ni li chaau, ni tan ta niba ki wɔɔn biminmaniba. Baah nan chaa-n le bi nan kan kutebug nsan bu.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Le bi ni uba kaah kan ke u kan kutebugin, le u nan jirin Yisa ban ki nan pag Uwonbɔr nin nkukɔpɔɔn.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: U nan gbaan Yisa a nintogil le ki sei u kunɔɔpoog. U nan ye Samariya a ninfou le.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Le Yisa baan ke, Bininfob piig le ma bi teb bin naa? Ke bininfob biwiɛ-n ma be laa?.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Uba kaa jirin ki nan sei Uwonbɔr kupag aa, ni paa ye uchaan nan a binba lee.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Le ki bei uja nmɔn ke, Fii ki li chaau, ke a yada bu a pɔɔga.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Farasii yaabin kaah nan baan Yisa n-yonn man wee Uwonbɔr a naam nmɔn yin fooni, le u bei bi ke, Uwonbɔr a naam kaa yin foon naah maan ni ba ki dibirin bu.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: bee uba kaa yin bei ke Lig, mi ma sɔ, bee Mi be nan do. Nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a naamin be ni ban.

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Le ki nan bei u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, N-yonn chaan le ni na li ban ni kan min Uninfosaal a Big a weehn yin a nba, amaa le kaa yin kan ni.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Bininfob na li bei ni ke, U be niin! Bee, U be do! Ni laa gu ki nɔɔ bi.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Nan bu-n Uninfosaal a Big a dam a daal na yin nann utaal kaah nyagin ki woon kitilangbɔŋ a yagi biɛn nin le.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Amaa ni kpaa le ke u na chinn ji fala niwann takpem ni, wee dulinya nan a ninfobin na yii u.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Ke naah nan ye man Nowa a yoo ni, ni ji na yin ye man le Uninfosaal a Big a dam a yonn.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Bininfob nan ji le, ki nyu, ki kɔɔn bipiib ki jɔɔ bi buwaab ki tenn bijab, ki nan nan kpaa lidaal lan wee Nowa nan nan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ. Le nnyum takpem nan dan ki nan jin bi biɛn.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: Ni nan ye man le Loti a yonn ni. Bininfob nan ji le ki nyu, ki daan ki kɔɔr, ki bu, ki maa.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: Amaa lidaal lan wee Loti nan nyan Sodom nan, nŋmi nin lichabil le nan nyan paab ki nan lu tum ki gaa bi biɛn.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Lidaal lan wee min Uninfosaal a Big na li kpiirin ni, ni na yin ye man le.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Lidaal nmɔn, uninfo wan nan be u niin limɔɔpinl a paab, wee u wann yɔɔr be poolin ke u laa ji ke un jiir ki tan yuuri; le wan mu be bukpaabin ke, u laa ji kun ke un tan yuur niba.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Li tiɛr Loti a poo a bɔr!.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Tɔ, uninfo wan ban un ŋma u miɛfol ke u li lann li, le wan lann u miɛfol ke u na li kan li.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: N bei ni le, lidaal nmɔn a nyiɛg, bininfob bilee na li lagin dɔ gadu bu, bi na li yuur uba ki jiɛn wan gurin.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Bipiib bilee na li lagin be ki naan idi, le bi na li yuur uba ki jiɛn wan gurin.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Le bi baan u, Laa, Uyudaan? Le u jiin ke, Nan yaan wee tiwannkpiin be-n, ni yaan le tijuur lagin.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Le Yisa nan kɔɔ Jeriko ki chaa.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Le uja uba be niin u yinl ye Zakiusi; u nan ye bilanpogaab a ninkpelu le ki ye uwandaan.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: U nan ban un kan Yisa kaah ye wan le, amaa waah nan ye kininfogingeŋin bu waa nan fir kan wi, kuninfogbaluŋin bu.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Ni ma bu le u nan chinn guun ki jon bi titogil ki tan don kikinkansiig ke un kan waah yin jo man, Yisa kaah nan nɔɔ man ki chaa-n bu.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Yisa kaah nan foon kikinkansiig nmɔn yaan nin, le ki waan lig u ki ke, Zakiusi jiirin weem ni. N yin n be a niin le dinan.

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Le u jiirin weem ki gaa u nin npopeen.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Bininfobin biɛn nan kan man le ki piin ki ŋulin ki ke, U jon un tan ŋa uchaan le tibir daan a niin.

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Amaa le Zakiusi nan sir paab ki bei Uyudaan ke, Lig, Uyudaan! Daandaan nan n li bɔgir njɔɔm a poolin biɛn ki sei faladam, le ni ye n nan jin uba dulim ke n li jiin udaan man a fim mina.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Dinan feegab foon iniin nan yaan na, nan bu-n uja nan mu ye Abiraham a yaabil le.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Nan bu-n Uninfosaal a Big dan ke u nan ban kan ban borin le, ki fee bi ki jaa.

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Baah nan pelig ni niɛ nan nin, le u nan gaa bi libɔŋal, nan bu-n u nan mal Jerusalam ma le bininfobin nan tiɛr ke Uwonbɔr a naamin paa yin foon le nba bu.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Le u ke, Ubuchin uba le nan jon kitiŋ kan fɔg, ke bin tan jin u nnaam wee un jirin.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Le u yin u toontum piig ki sei bi salima kon piig. Le ki bei bi ke, Ni ta ilig nan ki kpeŋa, hali nin maah na li jirin.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Amaa u bɔɔn yaab kaa nan ban ubɔri le ki tun ntoon bin tan bei ke, Taa ban uja nmɔn nŋa ti bɔru wi.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Amaa u nan jin u naam ma ki jir kun. Le u nan tun ki yin u toontum ban wee u nan ta iligin ki sei bin ke un kan baah ta kpeŋ ki kan ti nyɔɔr tan.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Le chinchinn yaa win dan u ban ki nan bei u ke, Uninkpel, a salima-n ŋa tinyɔɔr piig ki kpiɛu.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Le u ninkpelu nmɔn jiin ke, A ŋa choba, n toontunn ŋau! Ŋaah fir ki ŋmag niwann wama nbamɔm nan bu, gaam ntim piig a ninkper.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Le wan paan milee-n mu dan ki nan len ke, Uninkpel, a salima-n ŋa tinyɔɔr iŋmu ki kpiɛu.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Le u ninkpelu nmɔn jiin ke, Ŋaam ntim miŋmu a yudaau.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Le utoontunn uba dan ki nan bei u ke, Uninkpel, a salima-n sɔ; N nan ta gbin n nunpirigin le ki ta woon.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: N nan saŋ bijawaam le, nan bu-n a ye uja wan a bɔr pɔɔ le. A yuur niwann nan ŋaa ta bil le, ki chiɛ niwann nan wee ŋaa bun ni.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Le u ninkpelu win jiin ke, N li ta a baantɔɔl a mɔleen ki jiŋ tibɔr, fin utoontunn pootondaan nan! A nyi, bee ŋaa nyii, ke n ye uja wan a bɔr pɔɔ le, ki yuur nan wee maa ta bili, ki chiɛ nan wee maa bun naa?.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: Ba ŋa ŋaa nan ta n ligi nmɔn ki pen bininfob wee bi nan paan tinyɔɔr aa, wee maah jirin nan, maa bi li fir gaa nin tinyɔɔraa.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Le ki bei ban nan si nin yaan ke, Ni gaa u salima nmɔn u nɔɔn na, ki ta kpiɛ wan jɔɔ piigin na.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Le bi ke, Uninkpel u pii jɔɔ piig.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Le u nan jiin ki ke, N bei ni le ke wan biɛn jɔɔ ke, u li gaa takpem, amaa wan kaa jɔɔ niba-n, hali nan wee u jɔɔ wama-n, bi li gaa ni.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Amaa ndataam ban kaa nan ban ke n ŋaa bi bɔru win, ta bi ki dan n nintogil ki nan ku ba. .

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Yisa kaah nan len mam ni a bɔɔn le u nan jon ki don Jerusalam.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: Waah nan nan mal Befeg nin Betani a tim man be lijɔɔl lan wee bi yi li ke, Olif a Siigi a Jɔɔlin a paab nan, le Yisa tun u bɔɔnɔɔb bilee ki bei bi ke.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: Ni li chaa kitinbig kan be ni nintogil nan, ni tan kɔɔ kitiŋ nmɔn ke, ni li kan kibonjabig kaah lol, wee uba kaa la nyi jig ki, ni lor ki li jɔɔn n ban do.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Ni ye uba baan ni ke ba ŋa wee ni nan lor kiboŋ nmɔn ke ni bei ke, Uyudaan le ban ki. .

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Baah nan tun ban ni nan jon ki sin tan kan waah pii bei bi man ni le.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Baah nan be lor kiboŋ nmɔn n-yonn man nin, le kiboŋ nmɔn a daau kan ki baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni nan lor kiboŋ nmɔn naa.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Le bi jiin ke, Uyudaan li ban ki.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Le bi taan kiboŋ nmɔn dan Yisa ban, ki nan ta bi wann yeewann ki paan kiboŋin bu le Yisa jig.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: Waah nan jig kiboŋ nmɔn chaa-n le bininfob nan ta bi wann yeewann ki yar nsan bu.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Waah nan tan mal nan yaan wee nsan jon jir Olif a Siigi a Jɔɔl a paabin, le u bɔɔnɔɔb gbaluŋ nmɔn piin ki donn Uwonbɔr a yinl nin nkukɔpɔɔn nin npopeen baah kan ligaril a toonl lan ni.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Le ki ke: Tikaar ye ubɔr yudaan wan dan Uyudaan a yinl bu! Nposɔɔn nin kupag be paab le nin ndom.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Le Farasii ban nan be kuninfogbaluŋin yaan nin nan bei Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn, fuun a bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn buu.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Le u jiin ke, N bei ni le, bininfob nmɔn ŋmin ke, itangbag li kuun paab.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Yisa kaah nan tan mal Jerusalam ki kan kitiŋin, le u nan mɔɔ kitiŋ nmɔn bu.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: Le ki ke, Ni ye ke nim, hali nim, le bi nyi dinan, nan li taan nposɔɔn ki foon ni, amaa daandaan nan ni woo le ke ni numbil laa kan ni.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: N-yonn chaan, le ni dataam na li dan ki nan ma ligonl takpenl ki gɔɔn ni ki loon ni tim ilog nan biɛn.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Bi na li tir nim nin ni buwaab ban be ni niin ki jaa tum. Hali litangbagil liba gbaa kaa na li guri ki paa li tɔ bu. Nan bu-n naa biɛ Uwonbɔr a damin a yonn ni.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Le U nan tan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin, ki piin ki ŋɔɔr ban nan be niin ki kɔɔr tiwann ki nyan.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: Le ki bei bi ke, Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, N niin nan yin ye liwonmiɛl a niin le ; amaa ni ta ki ŋa ban feen a kikaabola. .

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Idaa biɛn u nan be iwonjiɛn niin le ki wɔɔn. Amaa le kumanug a ninkpeeb, nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim, nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninkpeeb nan lolig le baah li ŋa man ki ku u.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Amaa baa nan kan baah li nɔɔ isan yan ki ŋa man ni, nan bu-n bininfob biɛn a num nan man le bin gbir u bɔr.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Weemba daal waah nan be iwonjiɛn niin a loob ki wɔɔn bininfob ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr ŋann ni, le kumanug a ninkpeeb nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninkpeeb donn u ban.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Ki nan bei u ke, Bei ti nan a ninkper a pɔɔm wee a jɔɔ tun ŋaah tun man nan, Ŋma seiŋ man a ninkper a pɔɔm maa.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Le u jiin ke, N mu jɔɔ nbinbaan le ki yin baan ni, ni beim ni.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: Jɔnn a nyunfulin, ni nan nyan paab le bee uninfosaal ban naa.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Le bi nan nyan ngbaan ki tan gbeer tab ke, Ni ye ti ke, Ni nyan paab ke, u li baan ti ke, Ba ŋa wee naa ŋa u yadaa.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Amaa ti ke, Uninfosaal ban ke, bininfob ban biɛn be do nan li gabir ti itangbag, nan bu-n bi ŋa yada ke Jɔnn nan ye Uwonbɔr a linlenu le.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Mambu le bi nan jiin bei Yisa ke, Taa nyi naa nan nyan nan yaan ni.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Le Yisa ke, Tɔ, n mu kaa yin bei ni nan a ninkper a pɔɔm wee n tun maah tun man nan ni.

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Le Yisa nan gaa bininfob nmɔn libɔŋal nan, Uja le nan kon bukpaab, le ki ta bi ŋa bikpaakob biba a nɔɔn, le ki yaar jon niba paam ni yuur waah foon weemi.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Le lidichiɛl a yonn nan foo le u tun utoontunn uba ke un jo ki tan bei bikpaabin ke bin sei u bukpaabin a kpajiir ni. Amaa le bikpaabin tan choo u ki boo ki jaa u kun linɔɔpiɛŋmiɛl.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Le u ji tun utoontunn uba, le bikpaabin ji tan boo u mu ki jin u ifa le u mu kun linɔɔpiɛŋmiɛl.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Le ukpadaan nin ji tun uyom wan kpiɛ bita, le bi boo u ki nan sei u kidaŋ ki ta u gab.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Le ukpadaan nin ni bei ke, N ni yin ŋa nii? N yin tun n bija le, wan wee n jiɛ; ni ba ke bi li sei u jilima.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Amaa bikpaabin kaah nan nan kan win, le bi gbeer tab ke, Ufaajirin nsɔ, ni che ti ku u, wee lifaalin ŋa ti yaal.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Le bi nan choo u, ki ta u nyan nkpagbaan ki tan ku u. Tɔ, ukpadaan nan ni yin ŋa bikpaab nmɔn nii.?.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: U le dan ki nan ku bikpaab nmɔn ki ta bukpaabin sei bikpaab biba. Bininfobin kaah nan gbir man nin, le bi ke, Uwonbɔr laa che man nŋa.

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Le Yisa lig bi num paam chob le ki ke, Tɔ ke nan sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ni a taai aa? Kudaag kan wee bitanmaab yii ki n ni, ki ma nan ye kijaantelig ŋaŋ ?.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Uninfo wan biɛn lu litangbagil nan bu ke u li genn genn, amaa litangbagil nan mu lu wan bu ke, see li mar u.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Ban wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a siim, nin kumanug a yudam nan biɛ ke Yisa gaa libɔŋal nan ŋa bi ma le, mambu le bi nan ban bin choo u libol nmɔn yaan. Amaa le ki sin san kuninfogbaluŋin bijawaam.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Baah ni nan guu win, le bi nan tun bininfob penkaab, ban nan ta biba ŋa ke bipopendamin. Bi nan jɔɔ tama ke bin choo Yisa u mɔboi bu, wee ki ta u ki sei tininkper a pɔɔm nin npɔɔn man biɛn be ban ŋma kitiŋin a nɔɔn.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Le bininfob penkaab nmɔn baan u ke, Wan wɔɔn, ti nyi ke a pag ki wɔɔn nan ye ibamɔn, le a mu kaa gann ni, amaa ki wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr tan ye ibamɔn.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Ni ŋan nin ti li yɔ lampo ki tenn Siiza bee ti laa yɔɔ.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Le Yisa mu nan biɛ ke bi pen u lam le, le ki bei bi ke.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: Ni wɔɔm nligibim nmɔn ni. Ŋma a nunaŋ nin u yinl be nligibim nmɔn buu.

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Le bi jiin ke, Siiza. Le u bei bi ke Man ke, ni li tenn Siiza nan ye Siiza yaan na wee ki mu li tenn Uwonbɔr nan ye Uwonbɔr yaan na.

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Baa nan fir choo u waah nan len man kuninfogbaluŋin yaan ni, le u jiinim nan gar bi, le bi jir ŋmin.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Le Sadusi yaab, ban len ke uninfo kaa li fir fii nkumin nin, nan ta nbinbaan ki dan Yisa ban.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: Bi nan baan ke, Wan wɔɔn, Moziz nan sɔb nsiim sei ti ke, ni ye uja a kpel kpo ki jiɛn u poo wan kaa la jɔɔ kibig ke, ni kpa le nin uja nmɔn taa lipukɔɔlin ki maa nbum ki sei u kpel nmɔn.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Tɔ, bijab bilile le nan be, ki ye tinabir, le uninkpelin nan kɔɔn upii ki kpo kaa ma bigi.

eng: The second.
local: Le wan paa nan taa upii-n, ki mu kaa ma le ki kpo.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: le wan kpiɛ bita-n nan taah u, man le wan nan nan kpiɛ bilile-n nan kpo kaa ma kibigi.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le upii nmɔn mu nan kpo.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Ke nkum a fii daal ke, ŋma nin na yin ye upii nmɔn naa, bilile nan biɛn kaah nan kɔɔn u nan.

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Dulinya nan yaan bijab kɔɔn bipiib le bipiib mu kun bijab.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: Amaa ban kpaan nin dulinya pɔŋin nin nkum a fiimin, bijab kaa na li kɔɔn bipiib le bipiib mu kaa li kun bijabi.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: le baa ji na li kpo; nan bu-n bi na yin nann malakam le. Bi ye Uwonbɔr a buwaab le, baah ye nkum a fiim a buwaabin bu.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Amaa kimɔchiɛgin bu ke, hali Moziz nan wɔɔn ke bitakpiib li fii nkumin, nan bu-n u nan yin Uyudaan ke Abiraham a Weem, nin Azig a Weem, nin Jekɔb a Weem.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
