local: Waa ye ban kpo a Weemi, amaa ban fo a Weem, nan bu-n uma bu le bininfob biɛn be nin limiɛfol.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Le ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin a ni biba jiin ke, Wan wɔɔn, a len nan ŋan na.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Le uba kaa ji fir baan ibinbaan ni.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Le Yisa baan bi ke, Nii wee bi len ke Masiya ye Defid a yaabil aa?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Defid baan tɔɔl nyan pag Ilan a gbɔŋin ke, Uyudaan nan bei n yudaau ke: Kalim n nɔɔjiim paam.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: hali nin maah na li che a dataam nkpann a taai a tɔɔnbol. .

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Defid yin u ke Uyudaan. Ke niin wee u ji ye u yaabil aa.

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Bininfobin biɛn kaah nan peligin, le Yisa nan bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Ni li nyi ban wɔɔnn nsiimin bu. Bi ban bi li chonn yee daala fufɔgir le, ki jiɛ bi li poor bi nnyam ni, ki jiɛ bininkpeeb a kikaabol tiwonjiɛndirin nin jilima kuchiigin.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Bi feen ipukɔɔ a niin le paab, ki sin ji miɛ Uwonbɔr ki yuur ki wɔɔn biba. Man a ninfobol li kan litudaril lan yab.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Le Yisa gaa u bɔɔnɔɔb libɔŋal ki wɔɔn bi ke li ŋan ni bi li miɛ Uwonbɔr iyoo biɛn, wee ki laa bagir ki ta jiɛn ni.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: Le ki ke, Ubɔjir uba le nan be kitiŋ kiba ni le kaa san Uwonbɔr le u baga mu kaa be bininfob ni.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Lipukɔɔl mu nan be kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, ki gbaa chaan u ban ki nan bei u ke Seim ibamɔn ni, wee n nya kidataag kan mugirim nan a nɔɔn.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Iyonn iba u nan ti yiin le. Ni bɔɔn bu, le u nan tiɛr ke, Hali maah kaa le san Uwonbɔr wee n baga kaa be bininfobin ni.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: amaa lipukɔɔl nan kaah chaan ki nan mugirim iyoo biɛn nan see ke n sei u ibamɔn, wee u fɔɔr ki sin jiɛnim ke n miɛl fa wa. .

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Le Uyudaan len ke, Ni pel ubɔjir wan kaa ye ubamɔndaan nan kaah len man.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Ke Uwonbɔr kaa li sei u ninfogann kaab ban miɛ u nween nin kunyiɛgin ibamɔn naa? U li yii baa?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: N bei ni le, u yin sei bi ibamɔn le weem. Amaa Uninfosaal a Big nan foon n-yonn man ke u li kan yada daam kutingbɔŋ nan yaan naa.

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Le Yisa gaa libɔŋal ki sei ban tiɛr ke bi ma ye bibuchim ki jon bininfob biɛn nan ke:.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: Bijab bilee le nan tan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin ke bin miɛ Uwonbɔr, uba nan ye, Farasii le wan gurin mu ye ulanpogaar.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Le Farasii nmɔn sir ki miɛ u tɔɔl a miɛm ke: Uwonbɔr, n pooriŋ, maah kaa nann n tab ban ye, ban feen, ban ŋaan tibir, ban tun iwanchɔgin toon, bee ulanpogaar nan ni.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: N lolig bimɔb itaa ilee le iweehn ilile a poolin. Maah jɔɔ nan biɛn piig a poolin, n nyan liba le ki sei Uwonbɔr.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Le ulanpogaarin ma sir dindanl kaa chee un waan lig paabi. Le ki tibin u yul ki ke, Uwonbɔr, sam min tibirdaan kunyunyunbaag ni. .

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Le Yisa ji mann bei bi ke, N bei ni le nan, ulanpogaar n-yaa miɛm le Uwonbɔr nan chee le u nan kun, amaa naa ye Farasii a ninfou n ni. Uninfo wan biɛn don uba paab nan, le Uwonbɔr yin jiin u tum, le wan mu jiin uba tum nan, le Uwonbɔr yin don u paab.

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Bininfob nan jɔɔ nbuwaab finfim le chaan Yisa ban ke un ta u nɔɔ ki paan bi bu. U bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah kan man ni; le bi fuun bi bu.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Amaa le Yisa yin nbuwaabin ke bin dan u ban, le ki ke, Ni che nbuwaab finfim nmɔn dan n ban, ki laa ŋɔɔ bi, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a naamin ye bi yaa bosu nan yaan le.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, uninfo wan kaa chee un gaa Uwonbɔr a naamin ke kibig finfiŋ ke, waa yin kɔɔ u naam nmɔn yaan ni.

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Uninkpel uba nan baan Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn ki ŋan, n yin ŋa nii wee ki nan kan limiɛl lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm maa.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Le Yisa jiin ki baan u ke, Ba ŋa wee a yim wan ŋan naa? Uninfo ŋan kaa be see Uwonbɔr a binba.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: A nyi nsiimin: Laa tun iwanchɔgin toon ni, laa ku uninfo, laa su, laa ji tibɔr kaar ki siɛra, ŋa jilima ki sei a baa nin a na. .

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Le uja nmɔn jiin ki bei u ke, N pii ŋmag ni niɛ nan biɛn le nbuwaan nin na.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yisa kaah nan gbir man ni, le u bei u ke, Niba le laa pariŋ. Kɔɔr tiwann tan biɛn wee a jɔɔ-n na, wee ki ta sei faladama, wee ki li jɔɔ lifaal paab wee ki dan ki nan nɔɔm.

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Waah nan gbir man ni, le u sufu bii, nan bu-n u nan ye uja wan jɔɔ lifaal le takpem.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Le Yisa lig u ki len ke, Naah pɔɔ man ki sei nin bijɔɔndam nkɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin!.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ibamɔn, alakoomi kaah li kuur siɛrig a bonlin kaa pɔɔ foo nin ujɔɔndaan n fir kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin ni.

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Ban nan gbir man nin nan baan ke, Ŋma ni li fir ŋmaraa.

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Nan pɔɔ uninfosaal ban ni kaa pɔɔ Uwonbɔr ban ni.

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Le Piita bei u ke, Lig tim jiɛn nan biɛn wee ti jɔɔ-n le ki nɔɔŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, Uba kaa be wee ki jiɛn u niin bee u poo, bee u baa, bee u na, bee u kpetiib, bee u watiib, Uwonbɔr a naamin bu.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: wee ki laa kan limiɛfol takpem lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm n-yonn nan yaan nin iyoo yan chaan nan ni.

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Le Yisa nan yin u bɔɔnɔɔb piig nin bilee-n ki nyan ngbaan ki tan bei bi ke, Ti chaa do Jerusalam le nan, le niwann nan biɛn wee Bilinlemin nan sɔb ki jiin min Uninfosaal a Bigin paamin li gbeen.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Bi na li taa u ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaabin. Bi na li daar u nyɔɔrug, ki sugir u, ki siin timɔsann ki jaar u bu, ki fiɛb u ki nan ku u.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: Le ni ween itaa daal u li fii nkumin.

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn kaa nan biɛ u bɔr nmɔn a taai kolibi. Bi nan woon ni taai le bi ban, le baa nan biɛ Yisa kaah nan pag jiin nan paami.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yisa kaah nan tan mal Jeriko a tiŋin nan, le ujɔɔn uba nan ka nsangbaan ki miɛ.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: Waah nan gbir bininfob takpem kaah choon ni, le u baan nan ŋaan.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Le bi bei u ke, Yisa wan ye Nazarif a ninfo win le chaa jo.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Le u yin nmɔpɔɔn bu ke, Yisa, Defid a yaabil, sam kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Le bininfob ban nan lii Yisa a samin tiɛn u bu ke un ŋmin ma, amaa le u mann ki yin nmɔpɔɔn ke, Defid a yaabil, sam kunyunyunbaag ni.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Le Yisa sir, ki bei bi ke bin dar ujɔɔn nmɔn ki dan u ban ni. Waah nan chaan ki nan mal Yisa ban n-yonn man ni, le Yisa baan u ke.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: A ban n tun baa ki sei ŋaa? Le u jiin bei Yisa ke, U yudaau, n ban n ligir le.

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Ligira; a yada tebiŋa.

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Libol nmɔn le u numbili ligir le u nɔɔ Yisa, ki donn Uwonbɔr a yinl. Bininfobin biɛn kaah nan kan man nin, le bi mu nan pag Uwonbɔr.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Yisa kaah nan yaar ligin, le u kan bijɔɔndam kaah jɔɔ bi piin ki ŋaan kuwonjiɛndiig a piini a yiigin.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Ki ji kan lipukɔɔl lanjiin uba mu ta koobu ilee ki ŋa ipiin a yiigin.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Le u ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, lipukɔɔl lanjiin nan ŋa ipiin ki jon ni biɛn nan kaah ŋa man na.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: Bi biɛn nan sei ipiin bi jɔɔm bu le; amaa uma nin u fala nan biɛn ni, u ŋa waah jɔɔ nan biɛn le.

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔb biba nan be len iwonjiɛn niin a ŋam nin kaah jɔɔ itangbag ŋan, nin ipiin yan wee bininfob ta ŋa Uwonbɔr a yinl bu-n. Amaa le Yisa ke.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Naah kan tiwann tan biɛn do-n ma bu ke, n-yonn chaan le bi na li tir ni biɛn ki jaa tum, ke tangbagil liba kaa yin gur paa li tɔ bu.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔb baan u ke, Wan wɔɔn, iba yoo wee ni biɛn nan li ŋaa? Ba li ŋa limagil ki wɔɔn ke ni biɛn nan mala nin nin ŋaa.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni li nyi man, wee uba laa lar ni. Nan bu-n bininfob takpem na li dan n yinl bu, ki len ke, Min sɔ, bee Iyonn nmɔn mala. Ni laa nɔɔ bi.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Ni nan gbir itɔŋ nin ikɔn bu ke, ni laa che nin choo ni bijawaami. Ni nan biɛn na li chinn tun, amaa njoorim nmɔn kaa pa li dan nba bu man ni.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Tɔ le u bei bi ke: Ibol na li kɔn nin ibol, le nnaam mu li kɔn nin nnaam.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Le kutingbɔŋ na li dam takpem, le nkom nin iwiɛn na li be ntim ntim a poolin. Le niba li ŋa ki choo bininfob bijawaam le limagil takpenl na li nya papaab ki lunn.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Amaa ni nan biɛn nan yin ŋa ke, bi li choo ni ki mugir ni. Bi na li ta ni ki jo tiwonjiɛndirin nin tiloondirin, le ki ji li ta ni jo bibɔryudam nin kitiŋ a yudam ban, ki ji ni tibɔr n yinl bu.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Ni ma na yin sei ni npɔɔn man wee ni li fir taa pag n bɔr ki sei bi.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Amaa naah kaa la foo nan, ni bon ni sufu ni na wee ki laa che ni mugir ni naah na li pag nyan niba man ni.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Nan bu-n n na li sei ni imɔbon nin nlam man wee ni dataam kaa na li fir yii bee ki niɛ.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Hali ni baam nin ni nam, nin ni kpetiib, nin ni watiib, nin ni maali, nin ni jɔtiib na li kɔɔr ni ki ku ni biba.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Bininfob biɛn na li nan ni min bu.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Amaa hali ni yul a yukpobig kuba kaa li guri.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Ni si kenken ke ni li kan limiɛfol.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Ni nan kan ndataam kaah jin Jerusalam ki tan to ke, ni biɛm ke ki biim mal le nmɔn.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Iyaayoo ke, ni che ban be Judiya a tingbɔŋin li gu do ijɔɔ bu, wee ban mu be kitiŋ poolin mu li nya ki yaan na, ban mu be itingbaa nan mu laa ji kɔɔ kitiŋ poolin ni.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Nan bu-n n-yonn nan li ye litudaril ke nin ngbeen nan biɛn nan sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Ni na li bir idaa nmɔn ki sei bipiipoodam nin bipiib ban ŋmag nbiliɛm! Fala a jim na li be kitiŋ nmɔn ni, le Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ na li be bininfob nmɔn bu.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Bi na li ta takoobi ki ku biba, ki li choo bibam mu ki ŋa tiloondirin ntim biɛn ni. Le bininfob ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan li do taa Jerusalam bu hali nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab a yonn kaah na li ŋa ibamɔn.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Imag a siɛra na li be nween, nin uŋmal nin iŋmabil ni. Le kutingbɔŋ nan yaan, imɔg a nyum na li ŋa tinyungbann takpen, ki jɔɔ kufuug takpoŋ, ki sei ibol ipotiɛ.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Tininfobir a potiɛl a jawaam nan li dan dulinya nan yaan ki li che bininfob nfig, nan bu-n paab a wan naan wann na li dam dam le iŋmabil li lur.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le bi li kan Uninfosaal a Big kaah nyan kitilangbɔŋin ki chaan ni npɔɔn nin kupag takpeŋ.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Ni nan biɛn nan piin ki ŋaan ke, ni fii sir ki yaar ni yuli paaba, nan bu-n ni kpenim a manug a yoo mala.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Le u nan gaa bi libɔŋal nan: Lig bikinkam nin isiig biɛn.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Ni nan kan isiig nmɔn a far bun ke, ni tɔɔl li biɛ ke likpanyigil mal le nmɔn.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Ni ye man mu le, ni ye ni nan kan ni nan biɛn ŋaan ke, ni biɛ ke Uwonbɔr a niin a naamin mala.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Ibamɔn le n bei ni nan, daandaan nan a ninfob kaa li kpo biɛn ni le ni nan biɛn nŋa.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Paab nin tum na li jo amaa n mɔboi nan kaa li nyi ki jo.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Ni choom ni bau, naa ye man ke lidanyugbeel ni ipotiɛ bir li piin ni sufu bu, le ni nan ka kaa nyi le lidaal nmɔn nan piin ni bu ke kukurug pen kaagin.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: Nan bu-n lidaal nmɔn li dan ban biɛn be kutingbɔŋ nan biɛn bu.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Ni li guu nbamɔma, ki sin li miɛ Uwonbɔr wee ni fir guun ŋmar nan biɛn yin ŋa nan, wee ni nan fir ki sir uninfosaal a bija-n a nintogil.

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Idaa biɛn Yisa nan ti wɔɔn iwonjiɛn niin le, le kujɔɔg biɛn ke u ti nya ki tan dɔ lijɔɔl lan bi yi ki Olif a jɔɔlin kunyiɛg biɛn.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: le njakpiin ke bininfob nan lagin u ban iwonjiɛn niin ki nan pelig u bɔr.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Le ni nan nan mal nin bin ji ki chiig kan wee bi ŋma boroboro kan kaa jɔɔ nnyɔg wee bi yi ke yuglagir a chiigin.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: le Uwonbɔr a minmanib a yudam nin ban wɔɔnn nsiimin nan lolig baah li ŋa man ki ku Yisa, nan bu-n bi nan san bininfobin le bijawaam.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Le Sintani nan kɔɔ Judasi wan wee bi yi u ke, Isikariya nan, wee u nan kpiɛ Yisa a tooi piig nin bilee-n yaan nin.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Le Judasi nan jon ki tan pag nin biminmanib a yudam nin Iwonjiɛn niin a guguub waah li kɔɔr Yisa man.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Bi nan jɔɔ npopeen le ki chee ke bi li yɔ u ilig.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Le u nan chee, ki ni nan piin ki ban isan yan wee u li nɔɔ ki taah Yisa ki ŋubin bi nɔɔn, wee bininfob laa be niin ni.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Boroboro kan kaa jɔɔ nnyɔg a ji daal lan wee bi nan yin ta kipeebig ki mann kumanugin nan nan foo.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Le Yisa nan tun Piita nin Jɔnn kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, ki bei bi ke, Ni li chaa ki tan ŋa sili wee ti ji yuglagir a jiir.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Le bi baan u ke, La ban wee a ban ti tan ŋa silii.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni kɔɔ kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ke, uja uba li to ni nsan bu ki tu linyunbool. Ni li nɔɔ u ki chaa iniin yan yaan wee u ta yin kɔɔ-n.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: ki bei uniindaan nin ke, wan wɔɔnn ke ti baaŋ: Bi cham a diigin ye kula wee min nin n bɔɔnɔɔbin kɔɔ ki ji Yuglagir a jiir aa.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: U li wɔɔn ni paab a diig kan yab ki jɔɔ ijal. Ni ŋaam sili niin.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Le bi nan jon ki sin tan kan tiwann biɛn tan wee Yisa bei bi-n. Le bi nan ŋa Yuglagir a jiir.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Isaŋ nmɔn kaah nan nan foo-n, le Yisa nin u tooi kal bin ji.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Le u bei ke, N ban ni le nin linunmanl min nin nim nlagin ki ji Yuglagir a jiir wee n nin ji fala.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: Nan bu-n n bei ni le, maa ji li ji tijiir nmɔn ni hali nin taah na li kan ngbeem Uwonbɔr a naamin.

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Waah nan yuur kiyiinyɔɔgin, le u sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog le ki ke, Gaam ni nan ki por sei tab.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: Nan bu-n n bei ni le ke maa ji li nyu isiloon nan a daami hali nin Uwonbɔr a naamin kaah na li foon.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Le ki nan yuur boroboro, ki sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, ki war war sei bi, ki ke N gbanann le n jɔɔ tenn ni nan; ni li ŋaan man na, wee ki li tiɛr nbu.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Baah ji nan jin kunyiɛg a jiir ki diɛ a bɔɔn le u ji yuur kiyiinyɔɔgin, ki ke, Kiyiinyɔɔg nan li ye Uwonbɔr a poopɔnn tan be n chamin, ki kpaar sei ni nan.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Amaa n nɔɔ nin wan yin kɔɔrim a nɔɔ li lagin ki bɔr kisaŋin ki ji nan.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Uninfosaal a Big li kpo ke naah pii nan siin man nin, amaa tibɔbir li be wan kɔɔr win bu.

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Le bi nan piin ki baan tab wan li tun man.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Le limɔkpel ji nan dan u bɔɔnɔɔb a sisiigin bi wan jon u kpiɛg.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Le Yisa nan bei bi ke, ban kaa ye Juu yaab a bɔrtiib jɔɔ bi naamin mugir bininfob le; le ban jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm ki jon dulinya nan yaab yi biba Bititerib.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Amaa nim kaa ye ni li be man ni. Wan jon ni biɛn nan, ye u li be le ke wan ye ni biɛn a bigin, le wan ye uyudaan li be ke utoontunn nin.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Nan bu-n wan ka ji tijiir nin wan tol tijiir, ŋma ye uninkpel laa? Naa ye wan ka ki ji-n naa? Amaa n be ni sisiigin le ki nann utoontunn wan tol tijiirin.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Nim li ye ban nan si n chaŋ n fala a jimin.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: N Baa Uwonbɔr kaah jim nnaam man nin, mu yaa naam le n yin jin ni mu.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: wee ni li fir ji ki nyu n ban n naamin, ki nan kal tinaangbann bu ki ji Iziral yaab a bol piig nin ilee-n tibɔr.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Le Yisa len ke, Simɔnn, Simɔnn, lig, bi tunn Sintani le ke un nan lenn ni ke idi-n.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Amaa n miɛ Uwonbɔr, fin Simɔnn bu, ke a wonjiɛnl laa jiir tumi. Le a jirin n ban ke, a pɔɔg a nabum a gbanann na.

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Amaa le Piita jiin ke, Uyudaan, n ŋa sili nin n nɔɔŋ ki kɔɔ kuloondiigin bee nkumin na.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N beiŋ le, Piita, kunyiɛg nan, ukooja kaa laa yin mɔɔ le a niɛ nfimitaa ke ŋa nyimi.

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Le Yisa nan baan bi ke, Maah nan tun ni wee kaa sei ni kiligbɔɔlig bee lipɔgil bee inaatag, niba nan par ni waa? Le bi jiin ke, Naa nan par kolibi.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Amaa daandaan nan ni ye wan jɔɔ kiligbɔɔlig bee lipɔgil ke, un yuura; le ni ye wan kaa jɔɔ takoobi ke, un kɔɔr u daala ki daa ki bau.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Bi kan u kpiɛ titoonbirdamin na; le n bei ni le ke ni na li gbeen n ni. Nn, nan pii nan sɔb ki ta jiin n bu-n mal nin gbeen na.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, Uyudaan, lig, takoobi bilee sɔ. Le u jiin ke, Ni li kpaan.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Le Yisa nan nyan ki jon Olif a Jɔɔl a paab, ke waah pii ti ŋaan man nin, le u bɔɔnɔɔb nɔɔ u.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Waah nan tan foo niin nin, le u bei bi ke, Ni miɛ Uwonbɔr wee ki laa kɔɔ libiginlin ni.

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Le ki nan fɔɔr bi ban ki jon wama le ki tan gbaan tum ki miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: ki ke, N Baa, ni ye a bam ke, a laa che n nyu kiyiinyɔɔg nan yaan ni, ki mu laa ye min a bami, amaa ni li ye a yaa bam.

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Le malaka nan nyan paab ki jiirin u bu ki pɔɔg u gbanann.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Le waah nan be nton ni bu le u miɛ Uwonbɔr nin u pɔɔm biɛn. Le kutuntoŋ nan pɔɔ u bu ki nann ncham ki too tum.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: Le u nan fii liwonmiɛlin ki jir u bɔɔnɔɔb ban, ki miin bi dɔ jiɛn le, ki gbarig nin npoobiin.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni dɔ jiɛn naa? Ni fiim ki miɛ Uwonbɔr wee ki laa nan kɔɔ libiginl nan yaan ni.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Waah nan be ki pag nin u bɔɔnɔɔbin, le bi kan kuninfogbaluŋ chaan. Le Judasi wan nan ye u tooi piig nin bilee-n a ni uba, nan lii bi sam, le ki nan fɔɔr mal Yisa ke un poor u .

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: amaa le Yisa baan u, Judasi a dan a nan kɔɔr Uninfosaal a Big le nin ipooraa.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah nan biɛ nan yin tun nin, le bi ke, Uyudaan, ti ta ti takoobim ki li gaar bi waa.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Le bi ni uba gaa kumanug a yudaau a toontunnu a nɔɔjiim a tul ki par.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Amaa le Yisa jiin ke, Ni laa ji ŋa man ni! Le ki maa uja-n a tul le li jir ki tabin.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Le Yisa baan kumanug a ninkpeebin nin Iwonjiɛn niin a guguub nin Juu yaab a ninkpeeb ban dan bin nan choo win ke, N lii ban kɔnn nin bininkpeeb a sam le ni dan nin tijukpar nin njubum aa?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: N nan be nin ni le idaa biɛn iwonjiɛn niin, le naa ta ni nɔɔ ki ma mi. Amaa iyoo nan le ye ni yonni, n-yonn man wee libunbɔnl a pɔɔm ŋmag dulinya nan.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Le bi nan choo u, ki ta u jon kumanug a ninkpelu a niin. Le Piita nan tan be bɔɔn ki nɔɔ u chaa.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Amaa baah nan tan nyɔɔ nŋmi nlosisiigin ki nan lagin ka worin, le Piita nan kal kpiɛ bi bu.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Le kitoontun bisiɛg kiba nan kan Piita kaah ka kuŋmiweeŋ nmɔn yaan. Le ki lig u chimm ki nan ke, Uja nan ti be u ban.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Amaa le u niɛ. Ki ke, Upii, maa nyi wi.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Le naa kan yuuri le uba mu kan u le ki ke, A mu ye bi ni uba le. Le Piita jiin ke, Aai, uja nan, maa ye bi ni uba.

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Le kukurug kuba a bɔɔn le uba ji pag kpiɛ ke, Ibamɔn buu uninfo nan ti be u ban le, nan bu-n u nyan Galilii le.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Le Piita jiin ke, Uja nan, maah nyi nan wee a pag nan ni! Waah nan be pag man nin le ukooja mɔɔ.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Le Uyudaan gbiɛ lig Piita chimm. Le Piita nan tiɛr limɔbonl lan wee Uyudaan nan pag sei win: Kunyiɛg nan, ukooja kaa la yin mɔɔ le a niɛ nfimitaa ke ŋaa nyi mi.

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Le Piita labir ki nyan nkaafiin ki tan mɔɔ nbirim.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Bijab ban nan guu Yisa nan, nan piin ki daar u nyɔɔrug ki boo u.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: Bi nan piin u num le ki ji baan u ke, Bugira, ŋma fa ŋaa.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Le bi ji nan pag tisugir a leen takpem ki sei u.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Naah nan won kutaag njakpanyiɛn le Juu yaab a ninkpeeb nin kumanug a yudam nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim lagin, le bi taa Yisa jon bi nintogil.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Le bi ke, Ni ye fin li ye Masiya ke, bei ti ni. Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni ye n bei ni ke, naa li ŋaam yada.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: le n baan ni ke naa li jiin ni.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Amaa daandaan nan ki jɔɔ chaa, Uninfosaal a Big li kal npɔɔndaan Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Le bi biɛn baan, A nin ye Uwonbɔr a Bija lee? Le u jiin ke, Ni jɔɔ ibamɔn naah len ke min li ye-n.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Le bi ke, Ti ji ban siɛra ba buu? Ti pii gbir tibɔr nmɔn u tɔɔl a mɔboi yin na.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Nnyantoon a chinchinn a weem daal, kitaayɔg, le bipiibin nan taa tifar tan jɔɔ nbaam wee bi nan ban nin ki jon kukaagin yaan.

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Le kulaguŋin biɛn nan fii ki ta Yisa jon Palati ban.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Bi nan tan kan ki miin likaloolin choo loora.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: Le bi nan piin ki galin u ke, Ti kan uja nan le u be bei ti ninfob ke bi laa yɔ lampo ki tenn Siiza, ki ke u ma ye Masiya, ki ye ubɔr.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: amaa baah nan tan kɔɔ-n, baa nan kan Uyudaan Yisa a gbanann ni.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Le Palati baan Yisa ke, Fin le ye Juu yaab a bɔru win naa? Le Yisa jiin ke, Nn, ni ye ŋaah len man nan le.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Baah nan si kaa ji nyi baah tiɛr man nin, le libol nmɔn yaan bijab bilee ban nan yee tiwanpenn tan nyagin nan sir bi ban.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Le Palati mɔɔn ki bei kumanug a yudamin nin kuninfogbaluŋin yaan ke, Maa kan nan wee n li ta galin uja nan ni.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Le bijawaam nan choo bipiibin le bi gbaan tum ki tibin bi yuli, amaa le bijab bilee nmɔn baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni be ban wan fo bitakpiib a bol aa?.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Amaa le bi le yii ke, U wɔɔnim nmɔn che Judiya yaab a sufu fii le. U nan piin Galilii le ki nan kpa do.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: Waa be do; u fiiu! Ni li tiɛr waah nan bei ni man, n-yonn man wee u la nan be Galilii ni ban ni:.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Palati kaah nan gbir man nin, le u baan ke u ye Galilii a ninfo lee?.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: Ni kpaa le ke bin ta Uninfosaal a Big ki ŋa tibirdam a nɔɔn, le bi li kpaa u lidapunpunl bu, wee iween itaa daal ke un ji fii nkumin. .

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: Waah nan nan biɛ ke Yisa nyan Herɔd a tinpaamin nin, le u nan che bi ta u jon Herɔd ban, wan mu nan be Jerusalam iyaayoo nmɔn ni.

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Le bi nan tiɛr u mɔboi nmɔn.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Herɔd kaah nan kan Yisa iyoo man, le u pool nan penn takpem, nan bu-n ni nan yuura le u ban un kan u. Waah nan gbir Yisa bu man nin, le u nan jɔɔ tama ke un kan waah yin tun ligaril a toon man.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: Baah nan nyan kukaag nmɔn yaan ki jir kun ni, le bi nan ta ni niɛ biɛn ki bei Itoon piig nin uba-n nin ban biɛn nan be niin ni.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: U nan baan Yisa ibinbaan pam; amaa le waa nan jiin u tiba.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Meri wan nyan Magidalin, nin Joana, nin Meri wan nan ye Jemisi a na nan, nin ban nan be niin kpiɛ-n li nan tan bei Yisa a tooi nmɔn ni niɛ biɛn.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Kumanug a ninkpeeb nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin nan si niin, ki galin u nin inunman.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Amaa baa nan ŋa bipiibin yada, nan bu-n bi mɔboi nan nann tibɔyɔɔr le bi ban.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Le Herɔd nin u tɔtogib nan daar u nyɔɔrug. Le ki nan ta daala ŋaŋ kan peen ki nyagin ki yeen u ki ta u jiin Palati ban.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Amaa le Piita ma nan fii, ki guun ki jon kukaag nmɔn yaan. Waah nan tan bɔɔn ki ligin, le u kan ki miin kikinkenpeŋ nmɔn a binba li dɔ, le nan nan ŋa-n choo u ligaril, le u labir fɔr niin.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Lidaal nmɔn le Palati nin Herɔd choo tab kujɔtiig, amaa bi la nan ye ndataam le nin tab.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Lidaal nmɔn le bi bilee nan chaa kitiŋ kiba bi yi ki ke Emausi, ki ma nin Jerusalam nan ye maal ilile le.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Le Palati nan yin biminmanib a yudam nin Juu yaab a yudam nin bininfobin.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Bi nan choon ki pag nin tab niwann nan biɛn tun nin bu le.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: le ki bei bi ke, Ni ta uninfo nan dan ki ke u lar bininfob le bi kɔnn bininkpeeb a bɔr. N baan u tibɔr ni numin na le kaa kan igalin yan wee ni ke u tun nmɔn ni.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Baah nan chaa ki pag ni niɛ nan ni, le Yisa tɔɔl nan nan kpiir bi bu ki nan nɔɔ bi chaa;.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Herɔd mu kaa kan u galin yi. Ni ma bu le u jiin u ki tenn ti; ni kan ke waa ŋa niba, wee ni foo nin un kpo.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: amaa Uwonbɔr kaa nan wonn bi num wee bi biɛ wi.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Mambu n li dar u tul wee ki jaau.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ni choon ki pag baa a bɔr aa? Le bi baan sir, le bi num paam bii.

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋin tiɛn nmɔpɔɔn ke Kum uja nmɔn na! Wee ki nyan Barabasi ki sei ti.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Le bi ni uba, u yinl ye Kileopa, baan u ke, A ye uchaan le ki be Jerusalam nan ki ma kaa gbir niwann nan tun niin iweehn ilelee nan naa.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Palati kaah nan ban un nyan Yisa jaa-n le u ji nan balin bininfobin.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Le u baan ke, Wan ba ŋaa? Le bi jiin ke, Yisa wan nyan Nazarif nan a bɔr, U nan ye ulinlenn le, ke u mɔboi nin u toon jɔɔ npɔɔn Uwonbɔr a nintogil nin bininfob biɛn ban.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Amaa le bi le nan gbaa tann ke, Kpaamu lidapunpunl bu! Kpaamu lidapunpunl bu.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Le kumanug a yudam nin ti yudam nan ta u ki ŋa bibɔjib a nɔɔn ke bin ji u tibɔr ki ku u, le bi nan kpaa u lidapunpunl bu;.

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Le u ji nan pag kpiɛ nfim mitaa ke: Ba ŋaa? Uja nan le tun igalin ila baa? Maa kan nan wee u tun wee ni foo nkum a tudaril li. Mambu n li che bin dar u tul wee ki jaau.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: amaa ti nan jɔɔ tama ke uma yin ŋa nkpenim a manug ki sei Iziral yaab. Amaa baah ŋa u man nmɔn a ween itaa sɔ dinan.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Amaa le bi nan ta titintann ki yii ke bi le ban bin kpaa u lidapunpunl bu le, le bi tintann nmɔn nan nyaŋ Palati.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Nan ji kpiɛ ye, ti piib biba mu bei ti tibɔr tiba le ti ŋa ti ligaril. Bi jon kukaag nmɔn yaan le kutaayɔg nan.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Le u nan chee ki ŋa baah ban man nin.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: amaa le baa tan kan u gbanann ni. Bi jirin ki nan bei ti ke, bi tan kan zii le ni ye malakam, le malakam nmɔn ke u be nin u miɛfol.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Le ki nan nyan uja wan bininfob nan ban nin, wan bi nan ta jaa kuloondiigin u limɔyiil nin uninfo kul nbu-n, ki sin nan jaa Yisa ki sei bi ke bin ŋa u baah ban man.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Le ti ni biba jon kukaag nmɔn yaan, ki sin tan kan bipiibin kaah chinn ki bei ti man nin, amaa baa tan kan wi.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Baah nan jɔɔ u ki chaa-n, le bi nan choo Simɔnn wan nan nyan Sarene a tiŋin ki chaa Jerusalam nan, le ki ta Yisa a dapunpunl ki bugin u, le ki che u nɔɔ Yisa a bɔɔn.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ba ŋa ni jɔr man naa, wee kaa ŋa Uwonbɔr a linlem kaah nan pag tibɔr tan nin biɛn yadaa?.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Kuninfogbaluŋ mu nan nɔɔ Yisa a bɔɔn le, bipiib nan nɔɔ u ki mɔr tikpemɔr ki fabin u bu.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: Naa ye ni kpaa le ke Masiya n ji fala nmɔn, wee ki nan kɔɔ u pagin naa.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Le Yisa nan tan gbiɛ lig bi ki bei ke, Jerusalam a bisiɛm, ni laa mɔr n bu, ni li mɔr ni tɔɔl nin ni buwaab buu.

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Le waah nan piin Moziz a yonn ki tan kpaa Uwonbɔr a Linlemin, le u pag wɔɔn bi nan biɛn nan len nsɔbimin biɛn a poolin ki ta jiin u paam.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Nan bu-n iyoo chaan le ni na li ke, tikaar be bipiib ŋɔɔb, nin lipool lan kaa nyi ma kibig, nin ibii yan kaa la mɔɔn kibig bu.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Tɔ, baah nan tan mal kitinbig kan wee bi chaa-n, le Yisa ŋa ke u yin jo jo le-n.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Le bi ni na li bei ijɔɔ ke, Ber ki lu ti bu ni! ki bei igungon ke, Piin ti bu ni! .

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Amaa le bi nan bei u nin inunman ke, Paar ti ban na, nan bu-n nween lu; le ni nin be nin bɔŋ le. Le u nan nɔɔ bi ki kɔɔ ki tan be bi ban.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Nan bu-n ni ye bininfob ŋaan nan busub nan kaah la sɔɔn nan ke, bi ni nan koor ke ba ni yin ŋaa.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Dulinya a piinim ni ki miin wan wee bi yi u ke Limɔbonlin pii nan be le, le Limɔbonlin nan be nin Uwonbɔr, Limɔbonl nmɔn nan ye Uwonbɔr le.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Waah nan kal un ji bi ban nin, le u yuur boroboro ki sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, le ki nan piin ki war tenn bi.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Bi nan ta bininfob bilee, ban nan ye bi naayigib, mu kpiɛ u bu ke bin tan ku bi.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: U nan be nin Uwonbɔr le dulinya a piinimin.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Le bi num nin nan wonn le bi biɛ u. Le u bor bi ban.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: U bu le niwann biɛn nan naan; niba kaa be ki nan naan wee waa be niin ni.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Baah nan tan foo nan yaan wee bi yi ke, kuyukpabug nan, nin yaan le bi nan kpaa u ma nin binaayigibin lidapunpunl bu, uba be u nɔɔjiim paam le uba mu be u nɔɔgam paam.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: le bi nin bei tab ke, Waah bi pag nsann ki wɔɔn ti tibɔr tan sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin a taai yin, naa bi nann ke nŋmi le be ti sufu ni naa.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: U ni le limiɛfol nan be, limiɛfol nmɔn nan ye usaal a wonweem le.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Le Yisa ke, N Baa, jiɛn bi bir ki per ba, nan bu-n baa nyi baah tun nan ni. Le bi nan tɔn inaa ki por u wan yɔɔr.

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Le bi nan fii weem ki jir Jerusalam. Ki tan kan u tooi piig nin uba-n nin bibam kaah lagin ka.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Nwonween nin nan wonn libunbɔnlin, amaa le libunbɔnlin kaa nan biɛ mi.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Le bininfob nan si ki lig, hali ke biyudam daar u nyɔɔrug. Bi nan ke, U nan fee biba jaau; ni sin ye ke u ye Uwonbɔr a Masiya, Uninfo gannkaa-n, ke un fee u bau.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: le ki len ke, Ni ye ibamɔn le! Uyudaau sin fii nkumin na ki ta uba ki wɔɔn Simɔnn na.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Le Uwonbɔr nan tun uja uba, le u dan; u yinl le nan ye Jɔnn.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Le bitɔtogib mu nan donn ki nan daar u nyɔɔrug. Le ki sei u lidamunmɔŋil.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Le bininfob bilee-n mu bei bi nan tun nsann, nin baah nan biɛ u, waah nan war boroboro n yonn man ni.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: U nan dan ki ye siɛra jir le ki wɔɔn nwonween nmɔn bu, wee u ma bu uninfosaal n ŋa yada.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: le ki ke, Ni ye a ye Juu yaab a bɔru ke, a fee a ba ki jaau.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Baah la nan be pag ki jiin man paamin, le Yisa baantɔɔl nan nan sir bi sisiigin ki bei bi ke, Nposɔɔn li be ni bu.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: U ma baantɔɔl kaa nan ye nwonween nin ni; u le nan dan nwonween nin a siɛra a jim bu le.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Bi nan sɔb nsɔbim ki paan u yul bu, le ni karim ye: JUU YAAB A BƆR WIN SƆ.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Ibamɔn a wonweem man tenn uninfosaal biɛn nwonween nin nan chaan dulinya nan yaan le.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Le tiwar nin bijawaam choo bi, bi nan tiɛr ke bi kan utakpiir le.

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Le binaayigib ban nan jɔn niin nin a ni uba sugir Yisa ki ke, Ŋaa ye Masiya lee? Fee a baa jaau ki fee ti mu.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: U nan be dulinya nan yaan le, nin dulinya nan kaah le nan naan u bu nan yɔli dulinya kaa nan biɛ wi.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Le u baan bi ke, Ba ŋa wee ni che ni mug naa, ba ŋa wee ni che nbiɛnim be ni ban naa.

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Amaa le u kpiɛgin tiɛn ubu ki ke, Ŋaa san Uwonbɔr aa? Nin taah be litudaril liba ni nan naa?.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: U nan dan u yaabin le, amaa le u yaab nmɔn kaa nan gaa wi.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Ni lig n nɔɔi nin n taai man. Ni ye nbaantɔɔl le! Ni maam wee ki lig; U takpiir kaa jɔɔ tigbanann nin ikpab ke naah kam wee n jɔɔ man nan ni.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Tim kan litudaril nin ibamɔn le, nan bu-n bi jiin ti toon kaah kpaan ti gaa man le. Amaa uja nan ma kaa tun tibir ti ba.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: Waah nan pag man ki diɛ le u wɔɔn bi u nɔɔi nin u taai.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Nin ni ma biɛn ni, ban biɛn nan gaa win, nin ban nan ŋa yada u yinl bu-n, le u nan sei bi nsan ke bin nin ŋa Uwonbɔr a buwaab.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Le ki ke, Yisa, Iyoo man a nan kɔɔ a naam ni ke a tiɛr n bu.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Baah kaa la nan ŋa yada npopeen nin ligaril bu-n, le u nan baan bi ke, Ni jɔɔ tijiir doo ni ji.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Bi yin ye nbuwaab ban kaa nan ye ncham a maaŋ, bee tigbanann a bam, bee uja a bami, amaa Uwonbɔr a bam.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, N beiŋ ibamɔn le, dinan a li be n ban Uwonbɔr a niin.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Le bi yuur ujan ŋaakaa a wangenl ki ta sei u.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Limɔbonlin nan kpann tigbanann nin ncham le ki nan ŋa u kukɔɔbol ti ban. Ti nan kan u paga, kupag kiba kpeŋ kpeŋ, kan nan nyan ti Baa ban nin, ki gbee nin linyunyunbasanl nin ibamɔn nin.

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Ni nan ye nween a kurug piig nin tilee le, le libunbɔnl nan lu kitiŋ nmɔn biɛn bu ki tan kpaa kujɔɔg a kur titaa.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: le u gaa ki ŋman bi num min.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Jɔnn nan jin siɛra u bu ki sei bininfob. U nan tiɛn paab npɔɔn bu ke, U ma le n nan len ke, Wan yin dan n bɔɔn nin yab jon ma, nan bu-n u ma nan chinim. .

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
local: nan bu-n nween kaa ji nan jɔɔ nwonween ni. Le likinkenl lan nan jɔn iwonjiɛn niin nin, nan choo bɔgir nfim milee.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Le ki bei bi ke, Ni niɛ nan le n nan bei ni maah la nan be ni ban nin: Ni kpa le ke nan biɛn nan sɔb Moziz a siimin nin Bilinlem nin Ilan a gbɔŋin ni, ye nin gbeen le.

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Waah gbee nin linyunyunbasanlin bu le ti biɛn gaan tikaar ki kpiɛg.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Le Yisa nan tiɛn nmɔpɔɔn ki ke, N Baa, n ta n naaŋ ki ŋubin a nɔɔn na. Waah nan len man ki diɛ-n le u foor u diɛm a foorug.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Le u nan loor bi potiɛl ke bin biɛ nan sɔb Uwonbɔr a mɔleen yin a taai.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Uwonbɔr le nan che Moziz sei ti nsiim; le Yisa Masiya mu bu le ti kan linyunyunbasanl nin ibamɔn.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Bitɔb a sanliiru kaah nan kan ni wan nan tun nin, le u nan don Uwonbɔr a yinl ki ke, Ibamɔn uja nan ye ubuchin le.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: Le u bei ke: Nan wee bi nan sɔb sɔ: Masiya-n li ji fala ki fii nkumin iween itaa daal.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Bininfob ban nan lagin bi nan ji siɛra-n biɛn kaah nan kan niwann nan sin tun nin, le bi jir chonn sulim sulim ki kun.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Uba kaa nyi kan Uwonbɔr i, amaa U ma Uwonbɔr a binba, wan be Baa-n a logilin che bi biɛ wa.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: le nbinben a kpenim nin tibir a jiɛn per li mɔɔn u yinl bu ki piin Jerusalam ki sei ibol biɛn.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Amaa ban nan nyi win, nin bipiib ban nan nɔɔ u ki nyan Galilii, nan si nan fɔg le, ki lig ni niɛ nan biɛn.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: N-yonn man wee Juu yaab ban be Jerusalam nan tun biminmanib nin Liifai yaab ke bin tan baan Jɔnn waah ye wan nin a siɛra sɔ.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Nim li ye ni niɛ nan a siɛradam.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Uja uba nan be le u yinl ye Josef, u nan ye Juu yaab a laguŋ a ninfo wan ye ubamɔndaan le.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Waa nan walin un jiin guri, amaa le ki pag npaan ni u wiib ke, Maa ye Masiya nni.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: N yin sei ni nan wee n Baa nan siin limɔsiinl ke u li sei ni-n le; amaa ni laa nya kitiŋin yaan ni, ki li guu n-yonn man wee npɔɔn na li nya paab ki jiirin ki nan piin ni bu.

eng: He hadnt agreed with the plan and actions of the council. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea and eagerly anticipated Gods kingdom.
local: waa nan chee bi lol nin bi ŋaari. U nan nyan Juu yaab a tiŋ kan bi nan yi ki ke Arimatiya le, ki nan guu Uwonbɔr a naamin a foom le.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Le bi baan u ke, Ke a ni ye ŋmiɛ? A ye Elaja lee? Le u ke, Maa ye wi. A ye ulinlenn nin lee? Le u jiin ke, Aai.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Waah nan ta bi ki nyan kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ki tan mal Betani, le u nan don u nɔɔi paab ki kaab jaa bi bu.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: U nan jon Palati ban le ki tan gaa Yisa a gbanann.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Njoorimin le bi baan u ke, A ye ŋmiɛ? Sei ti njiim ni wee ti ta jir ban tun ti-n ban. A ke a ye ŋmiɛ.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: Waah nan be kaab ki jaar bi bu-n, le u nan fɔr ki jiɛn bi le Uwonbɔr nan yuur u ki don paab.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Le ki ta ti jiirin ki pɔbin ti kikinkenpeŋin, ki ta piin kikaapɔŋ kan wee bi nan chib ki kutangbapiigin wee baa la nyi ta uba pii niin nin.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Le bi nan jiɛ u ki jir Jerusalam ni npopeen takpem.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Le Jɔnn jiin ki len ulinlenn Azaya a mɔboi ni ke, N ye wan a kukɔl tann kukunkɔɔg bu-n le, Ŋa nsan mu li tel ki sei Uyudaan na. .

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Ni nan ye sili daal le, le likpakɔɔl nan be piin.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: le bi nan be iwonjiɛn niin iyoo biɛn, ki donn Uwonbɔr a yinl.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Le Farasiim ban wee bi nan tun nin.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Bipiib ban nan nyan Galilii ki nɔɔ Yisa dan nin nan nɔɔ Josef ki tan kan kukaagin nin baah pii u man.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: ni nan baan u ke, Ni ye ke ŋaa ye Masiya-n, kaa ye Elaja, bee Ulinlenn nin ke, ba ŋa wee a fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulaa.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Le bi nan jir kun ki tan ŋa sili ki ban tifar tan jɔɔ nbaam nin tulaali. Amaa le ki nan foor likpakɔɔl daal nmɔn, ke Uwonbɔr a siim kaah wɔɔn man nin.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Le Jɔnn jiin ke, N jɔɔ nnyum le ki fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunful, amaa uba si ki ŋmar ni ban le naa nyi wi.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: U ma ye wan nan yin ŋɔɔrin n bɔɔn, wan a naatagunn wee maa kpaan n lori.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Ni nan biɛn nan ŋa Betani, Jɔdann a kpengonl lan yaan wee Jɔnn nan fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin le.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Ni nan wonn le Jɔnn nan kan Yisa kaah chaan u ban le ki ke, Lig, Uwonbɔr a Peebig, wan nyann dulinya nan a birin!.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: U ma le n nan len, maah nan ke, Uja wan yin ŋɔɔrin n bɔɔn nin yab jon ma, nan bu-n u ma nan chinim.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: N baantɔɔl kaa nyi wi, amaa nan bu wee n nan dan ki nan fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin nan ye le ke un bin kpiir u ba ki sei Iziral yaab.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Tɔ le Jɔnn nan sei siɛra nan: N nan kan Kinaaŋ kaah nyan papaab ki jiirin ke ulanjelin le ki nan tɔɔn u bu.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: Maah bi li nyi wi, wee naa ye wan nan tunim ke n nan fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin le nan beim, Uja wan wee ni kan Kinaaŋ jiirin ki nan tɔɔn u bu-n le ye wan li ta Kasi a Naaŋ ki fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: N nan kan na, le ki jin siɛra ke Uwonbɔr a Bija-n sɔ.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Ni nan wonn kutaag le Jɔnn ji nan be niin nin u bɔɔnɔɔb bilee.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: Waah nan nan kan Yisa jo chaa-n le u ke, Lig, Uwonbɔr a peebigin.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔb bilee-n kaah nan gbir u len man nin, le bi nan nɔɔ Yisa.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Yisa kaah nan gbiɛ lig ki kan bi nɔɔ win le u baan bi ke, Ni ban baa? Le bi ke, Rabbi , Laban wee a kɔɔ.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Le u jiin ke, Ni dan, ni na li kan. Le bi nan jon ki tan kan waah kɔɔ nan yaan, le ki be u ban lidaal nmɔn. Ni nan ye kijɔɔg a kur tina paam le.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Anduru, wan ye Simɔnn Piita a waalin nan ye bininfob bilee ban nan gbir Jɔnn kaah nan pag man nin wee ki nan nɔɔ Yisa-n a ni uba le.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Anduru kaah nan ŋa man nan ye un chinn ban kan u kpel Simɔnn le ki bei u ke, Tim ban kan Masiya nna .

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Iweehn itaa daal le bi nan jɔɔ lipiichanil Kena, kitiŋ kan be Galilii a poolin nin. Yisa a na nan be niin.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Le ki taa u dan Yisa ban. Yisa nan lig u le ki ke, A ye Jɔnn a bija Simɔnn le. Bi ni li yiŋ ke Sifasi .

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: le bi nan bei Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb mu le bi tɔɔl nan dan lipiichanil nmɔn yaan.

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Ni nan wonn kutaag le Yisa nan bon ke un nya jo Galilii. Waah ban kan Filib nan, le u bei u ke, Li nɔɔm ni.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Ndaam kaah kaa ji nan be-n, le Yisa a na bei u ke, Baa ji jɔɔ ndaami.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Filib nan nyan Befisada a tiŋin le, u ma nin Anduru nin Piita.

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N na, ba ŋa a kɔɔn n nɔɔ niin naa? N yonn ni kaa laa foo.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Le Filib nan ban kan Nataniɛl le ki bei u ke, Tim ban kan Moziz kaah nan sɔb wan a bɔr nsiimin nin na, nin wan a bɔr wee bilinlem tɔɔl nan sɔbin, u ma ye Yisa wan nyan Nazarif, ki ye Josef a bija-n.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Le u na bei bitoontumin ke, N bija nan bei ni man ke ni ŋa man na.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Le Nataniɛl baan ke, Nazarif aa! Niwann ŋan li fir nyann niin naa? Le Filib len ke, Dan ki nan lig.

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Itangbag a nyunbunbol iloob nan si mal niin, i yaa bosu le Juu yaab nan ti jɔɔ ŋunn Uwonbɔr a kaal a ŋunnim kuchiig daal, kiba nan kɔɔ iboo iloob ki chaa ilile paam le.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan Nataniɛl be forin nin, le u bei u ke, Iziral a big bamɔŋ kan kaa jɔɔ igalin nin sɔ.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Le Yisa bei bitoontumin ke, Ni gbeen inyunbunbol nan nnyuma; le bi gbeen ki tan kpaan imɔgbeli.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Le Nataniɛl baan ke, A ŋaa nii ki nyi maa? Le Yisa jiin ke, N bi kaŋ ŋaah la bi ka bikinkamin a tum wee Filib nin bi yiŋin.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Tɔ ni gbil ki tan sei ban tol itolin a ninkpelu wa. Le bi ŋa man.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Le Nataniɛl nin len npaan ni ke, Wan wɔɔn, a ye Uwonbɔr a bija-n le; a ye Iziral a bɔr takpenl le.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: le uninkpelin nan len nnyum man kpann ndaamin. Waa nan biɛ ma nyan nan yaan ni, amaa bitoontum ban nan gbil nnyumin ma nan nyi. Le u nan yin lipiichanil daan nyan ngbaan.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Le Yisa ke, Nan bu wee a ŋa yada-n ye maah beiŋ ke n bi kan ŋaah ka bikinkam a tumin le. A li kan niwann nan jon man.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: le ki ke, Bininfob biɛn chinn tol ndaŋam le, le bicham nan gbee ke bi nin tol kidabirug, amaa fin ma woon man ŋan le, ki nin nan jɔɔ mi tol daandaan nan.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Le u ji len ke, N beiŋ ibamɔn le, a li kan papaab kaah loor, le Uwonbɔr a malakam do paab ki jiirin Uninfosaal a Bija-n bu.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Ni ma nan ye u chinchinn a garil a siɛra, lan wee Yisa nan ŋa Kena a tiŋ kan be Galilii a poolin. U nin nan kpiir ki wɔɔn u pag a toon kaah ye man, le u bɔɔnɔɔbin nan ŋa u yada.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le u ma nin u na ki kpiɛ u nabum nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan jir Kapeniɛm a tiŋin. Niin le bi nan tan be iweehn ilee.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Juu yaab a yuglagir a chiig kaah nan malin, le Yisa nan jon don Jerusalam.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: U nan kan bijab kaah kɔɔr inaa ki kpiɛ ipee nin ilanjel, le biba mu ka ijal bu ki kpenn ilig iwonjiɛn niin a loob.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Le u ban iŋmin a naalabil, le ki ŋɔɔr ipee nin inaa nin niwann biɛn iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn yaan; u nan yaal biligkpenibin a ligibim le ki labir bi jali ki chigin.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: Le ki bei bilanjel kɔɔribin ke, Ta i ki nya doo! Ni laa nyi ta n Baa a niin ki labir kinyaŋi.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin ni nan tiɛr ke ni nan sɔb le ke: A niin a jiɛm a nunmanl li kum.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Le Juu yaab nan baan u ke, Ba a garil a siɛra wee a li fir wɔɔn ti wee nin wɔɔn ke Uwonbɔr le seiŋ nsan ke a ŋa ni nan biɛn naa.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Le Yisa jiin bi ke, Ni worin iwonjiɛn niin nan ke, N ji li fiin i iweehn itaa daal.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Le Juu Yaab jiin ke, Bi nan taa ibin pina nin iloob le ki maa iwonjiɛn niin nan, le a li ta iweehn itaa ki fiin yaa.

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Amaa iwonjiɛn niin yan wee Yisa nan pagin nan ye u gbanann le.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Waah nan fii nkumin a bɔɔn, le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb ni nan tiɛr waah nan len man nin. Le bi nan ŋa Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ nin imɔleen yan wee u nan pagin yada.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Tɔ waah nin nan be Jerusalam yuglagir a chiigin ni, bininfob takpem nan kan ligaril a siɛram yan wee u nan ŋaan nin le ki ŋa u yinl yada.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Amaa Yisa kaa nan ta u ba ki sei bi, nan bu-n u nan nyi uninfo biɛn a potiɛl.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: Waa nan ban bininfob nji uninfosaal tab a siɛra, nan bu-n u nan nyi nan be uninfosaal ni.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Uja uba le nan be ki ye Farasii a ninfou le u yinl ye Nikodimosi, u nan ye Juu yaab a yudam a laguŋ a ni uba le.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Daal liba a nyiɛg le u nan dan Yisa ban le ki nan ke, Rabbi, ti nyi ke a ye wan wɔɔnn le ki nyann Uwonbɔr ban. Nan bu-n uba kaa li fir ŋa ligaril a siɛram yan wee a ŋaan nan wee naa ye Uwonbɔr le be u ban ni.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Le Yisa jiin ki bei u ke, N beiŋ ibamɔn le, uba kaa li fir kan Uwonbɔr a naami, see ke bi ji ma u. .

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Le Nikodimosi baan u ke, Bi yin ŋa nii ki ji maa uninkpelaa? Ibamɔn waah ji li fir jir kɔɔ u na a poolin wee bin ji maa wi.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N beiŋ ibamɔn le, uba kaa li fir kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin ni, see ke bi ma u nin nnyum nin Kasi a naaŋin.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Tigbanann le maa tigbanann, amaa le Kasi a naaŋ mu maa kinaaŋ.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Laa che nin gariŋ nin maah len ke, Ni kpaa le ke bi maa ni jigin nni.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Kutafaag daar chaa kaah ban nan paam le. A gbir ki fur le, amaa ŋaa li fir biɛ kaah nyan nan paam bee kaah chaa nan paami. Man le ye ni ki sei ban biɛn wee bi maa bi Kinaaŋin ni.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Le Nikodimosi baan ke, Ni li fir ŋaa nan naa.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Le Yisa ke, A ye Iziral yaab a wan wɔɔnn le, le ŋaa biɛ ni nan naa?.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: N beiŋ ibamɔn le, ti pag taah nyi nan le, le ki ji siɛra taah nan kan nan bu, amaa ni ma biɛn nan yɔli naa ŋa ti siɛra nmɔn yada.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: N pag nan ŋaan dulinya nan yaan le ki seiŋ le ŋaa ŋa yada; ni ye n nin pag nan ŋaan papaab ke a yin ŋa nii ki ŋa yadaa?.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Uba kaa be ki nyi don paabi, sei uninfosaal a bija wan nan nyann paabin.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Moziz kaah nan yuur liwaalabil paab kukunkɔɔgin man nin, ni kpaa le nin bi nan yuur uninfosaal a bija mu paab.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: wee wan biɛn ŋa u yada ke u li kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmi.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Mambu Uwonbɔr kaah jiɛ dulinya nan kagin bu le u ta u bija gaŋ sei ti, ke wan ŋa u yada ke waa li kpo, amaa u li kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmi.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Uwonbɔr kaa nan tunn u bija dulinya nan yaan ke un nan bii dulinya le, amaa u bu le dulinya yaab li kan feegab.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Waa yin bii wan biɛn ŋaa u yada, amaa wan biɛn kaa ŋaa yada pii si nbiimin le nan bu-n waa ŋa Uwonbɔr a Bija gaŋin yada.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Ni nan le ye Uwonbɔr kaah len man: Nwonween dan dulinya nan yaan na, amaa bininfob jiɛ libunbɔnl le ki jon nwonween, nan bu-n bi toon biɛn nan ye tibir le.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Wan biɛn tun tibir a toon ke u nan nwonween le, le kaa yin dan nwonween nin ni ki san ke u bir li nya npaan nin bu.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Amaa wan biɛn be nin ibamɔn nin ke u chaan nwonween nin le, wee bin kan npaan nin ke waah ŋa man nin u ŋa Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm bu le.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan jon Judiya a tiŋ paamin, nan yaan wee bi la nan tan be nin bi, ki fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
