local: Jɔnn tɔɔl nan fu bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunful le Anon kan mal Salim a tiŋin, nan bu-n nnyum takpem le nan be niin, le bininfob nan ti chaan u ban le u fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Limɔkpel le nan dan Jɔnn a bɔɔnɔɔb biba nin Juu a ninfo uba a sisiigin ki ta jiin liwonjiɛnl a kaal a ŋunnim paam.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Le bi nan dan Jɔnn ban ki nan bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn, uja wan nan be a ban Jɔdann a kpendoolin, wan a siɛra wee a nan jin nin, lig u nin fu bininfob le Uwonbɔr a nyunful, le bininfob biɛn chaa u ban.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Le Jɔnn jiin ki len ke, Uninfo le li fir gaa nan nyan paab ki tenn u le.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Nim baantɔɔl le fir ji siɛra ke n nan len ke, Maa ye Masiya-n ni, amaa Uwonbɔr nan chinn tun min le, le u nin ŋɔɔrin.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Lipiichanil daan li ye upii wan wee bi chann ni. Lipiichanil daan a jɔ wan dan u ban nin si guu u le ki sin pelig, le ki nan gbir Lipiichanil daan a kukɔl ke u pool nsɔŋ. Nposɔɔn nmɔn ye n yaan le, mambu n gbee nin nposɔɔn na.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Ni kpaa le ke u yinl ndon; ki kpaa le ke n mu a yinl njiir.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Wan nyann paabin le jon ni biɛn; wan nyann tumin ye tum yaau le, le ki pag ke wan nyann tumin. Wan nyann paabin le jon ni biɛn.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: U jin waah nan kan nan ki gbir nan a siɛra le, amaa uba kaa gaa u siɛra nmɔn ni.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Uja wan nan gaa siɛra-n u chee ke Uwonbɔr ye ubamɔndaan le.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Nan bu-n wan wee Uwonbɔr nan tun nin pag Uwonbɔr a mɔbonl le, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr gbeen u Kinaaŋ le.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Baa-n kaah jiɛ Bija-n bu le u ta niwann biɛn ki ŋa u nɔɔn.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Wan le ŋa Bija-n yada ke u li kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛm, amaa wan yii Bija-n ke waa li kan limiɛfoli, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ yin gbaa be udaan bu le.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Farasii a ninfob nan gbir ke Yisa nan ga bɔɔnɔɔb le ki jon Jɔnn le ki fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: although Jesus disciples were baptizing, not Jesus himself.
local: ni mu kaa nan ye Yisa le sin nan fu bi, amaa u bɔɔnɔɔb le nan fu bi.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: Uyudaan kaah nan gbir baah pag man u bu-n, le u fii Judiya le ki ji jir Galilii.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Ni nan ye un nɔɔ Samariya paam le.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Mambu u nan nan fo Samariya a tiŋ kiba bi yi ki ke Saikar, ki nan mal kukpakpiig kan wee ti yaaja Jekɔb nan ta sei u bija Josef nle.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Jekɔb a nyunbuŋ nan be niin, le Yisa kaah nan nyan lisanchonl ki bagin, le u nan kal ki mal kinyunbuŋ nmɔn. Ni nan ye nween a kur piig nin tilee le.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: Samariya a pii win kaah nan dan u nan luun nnyumin, le Yisa nan bei u ke, Ŋaa li seim nnyunyukaamaa.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Le Samariya a pii win bei u ke, A ye Juu a ninfou le n ye Samariya a piiu. Ni ŋa nii a miɛm nnyumaa.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, Ni ye ke a nyi Uwonbɔr a piin nin wan miɛŋ nnyunyukaam nan ke, a bi li baan u le u bin seiŋ limiɛfol a nyum.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Le upii-n baan u ke, Uninkpel, ŋaa jɔɔ nan li gbil nnyumi, le kinyunbuŋ nan mu nyɔɔ le. A yin kan limiɛfol a nyum nmɔn laa?.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: A yab jon ti baa Jekɔb, wan nan sei ti kinyunbuŋ nan wee ubaantɔɔl nin u naachim nin u wankor nan nyu niin ni waa.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Wan biɛn nyun nnyum nan ke nnyunyum ji li choo u.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: amaa wan biɛn nyun nnyum man wee n tenn win ke nnyunyum kaa ji li choo wi. Ibamɔn, nnyum man wee n tenn win yin kpann nnyunbunkaam le ki bun u poolin ki sei u limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Le upii-n bei u ke, Uninkpel, seim nnyum nmɔn ni wee nnyunyum laa ji jɔɔm wee n gbaa chaan do ki nan lu nnyumi.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Le u bei upii-n ke, Li chaau, tan yin a chal wee fin nin uma ndan.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Le u jiin ke, Maa jɔɔ chali. Le Yisa bei u ke, A jɔɔ ibamɔn nin ŋaah ke ŋaa jɔɔ chali.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: Ŋaah beim man nin ye ibamɔn le, a pii nan kan chiɛb biŋmu-u, le uja wan wee fin nin uma nin be-n kaa ye a chali. Ŋaah le len man nin ye ibamɔn le.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Le upii-n ke, Uninkpel, n kan ke a ye ulinlenn le.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Ti baam nan jiɛn Uwonbɔr lijɔɔl nan a paab le, amaa nim Juu yaab len ke ni kpaa le nin ti li jiɛn U Jerusalam.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Le Yisa pag npaan ni ke, Ŋa maah len man nin yadau, upii, n-yonn chaan le naa nan yin jiɛn Baa-n lijɔɔl nan a paab bee Jerusalami.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Nim Samariya yaab jiɛn nan wee naa nyi le; tim jiɛn nan wee ti nyi le, nan bu-n feegab nyann Juu yaabin le.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Ni ma biɛn nan n-yonn chaan le ki pii foon na le ban ye ibamɔn a wonjiɛmin li jiɛn Baa-n kinaaŋin nin ibamɔn nin, nan bu-n bi ma ye biwonjiɛm ban wee Baa-n ban.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Uwonbɔr ye kinaaŋ le, le ni kpaa le ban jiɛn Win li jiɛn U kinaaŋin nin ibamɔn.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Le upii-n ke, N nyi ke Masiya nmɔn chaan. U nan dan ke, u li mann pag ni biɛn ki wɔɔn ti.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Tɔ le Yisa pag npaan ni ke, Min wan pag tenniŋ nan li ye u.

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Libol nmɔn yaan le u bɔɔnɔɔb nan nyan kitiŋ poolin ki jirin ki nan kan u ma nin upii kaah be pag le ni gar bi. Amaa uba kaa nan baan ke, A ban baa? bee Ba ŋa wee fin nin u ma pagaa.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Le upii-n jiɛn u nyunbool le ki jir kitiŋ poolin ki tan bei bininfob ke;.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: Dan man, ki nan lig uja wan beim niwann nan biɛn wee n nan nyi ŋa. Ni li fir ye Masiya nnaa.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Le bi nan nyan kitiŋin a poolin ki chaan u ban.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Ki miin u bɔɔnɔɔbin gbeer u le ki ke, Wan wɔɔn, jim nibau.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Amaa le u bei bi ke, N jɔɔ tijiir tan n yin ji ke naa nyi ti bu.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin nin baan tab, Ke uba li ta tijiir ki nan sei u bee.

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Le Yisa len ke, N yaa jiir ye n tun wan nan tunim kaah ban man le ki sin diɛ itoon yan wee u seim ke n tun nin.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Libɔŋal kaa ke, Iŋmal ina li gur nin lidichiɛlaa? N bei ni le, ni ligir ni numa wee ki lig tikpaann ni! Idi beer ki foo nchiɛma.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Hali nin daandaan nan udichiɛrin gaan u yɔr le, hali daandaan nan u chiɛ idi-n nin limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛm bu le, wee nin che wan bu tiburin nin wan chiɛ idi-n nlagin kan nmɔn.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Mambu bininkpeeb kaah ke, Uba bu le utɔ chiɛ-n, ni ye ibamɔn le.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: N tuŋ a tan chiɛ idi yan wee ŋaa kon a baantɔɔl le. Biba li nan tun itoon pɔɔr, le fin nan jin bi tuntoŋin a nyɔɔr.

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Samariya yaab takpem ban nan nyan kitiŋ nmɔn yaan nan ŋaa Yisa yada upii-n a siɛra bu, waah len ke, U beim niwann biɛn nan wee n nan nyi ŋa.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Mambu Samariya yaab kaah nan dan u ban nin, le bi balin u ke un paar ki li be bi ban, le u nan be bi ban iweehn ilee.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Le u mɔboi bu bi takpem nan ŋa biwonjiɛm.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Le bi bei upii-n ke, Naa ye ŋaah len man nin a binba bu le ti ŋa yada; daandaan nan ti baantɔɔl gbira, le ki nyi ke uja nan sin ye dulinya nan a Feeru le.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Iweehn ilee daal a bɔɔn le u fii niin ki jon Galilii.

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Waah nan foo Galilii n, le Galilii yaab nan gaa u kuchɔŋ. Bi nan kan nan biɛn wee u nan ŋa Jerusalam yuglagir a chiigin yaan na, nan bu-n bi baantɔɔl nan be niin.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: U ji nan gɔɔn Kena kan be Galilii n ki kpiɛ, nan yaan wee u nan chinn taa nnyum ki kpann ndaamin. Le Kapeniɛm a bɔrniin a gbansɔbiru uba a bija dɔ bun.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Uja nan kaah nan gbir ke Yisa pii nan nyann Judiya ki foon Galilii n, le u jon u ban ki tan balin u ke un dan ki nan teb u bija wan nan mal nkumin.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Naa ye ni nan kan ligaril a siɛram nin ijenjee a toon ke, naa jɔɔ ni ŋa yada.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Le ibɔrniin a gbansɔbir win ke, Uninkpel, jiirin weem, ke naa ye man ke n bija nmɔn li kpo.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, A ban ke a li chaau. A bija nmɔn li pɔɔg. Le uja-n ŋa Yisa kaah pag man nin yada le ki fɔɔr u ban.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: Waah la nan be nsan bu-n, le u toontunnu too u le ki bei u ke u bija-n pɔɔga.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Waah nan baan n-yonn man wee u bija-n nan pɔɔgin, le bi bei u ke, Kugbanantuntoŋ nmɔn won jaa u nweehn a kurug kuba le.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Tɔ le baa-n biɛ ke n-yonn nmɔn totob li yen, man wee Yisa won bei u ke, A bija-n li pɔɔgin. Mambu le u ma nin u niin yaab biɛn nan ŋa Yisa yada.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Ni ma ye ligaril a siɛra lan kpiɛ milee wee Yisa nan ŋa, waah nan nyann Judiya ki jon Galilii n.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le Yisa nan choon gɔnn Galilii a timin, kaa nan ban u li be Judiya, nan bu-n Juu yaab ban nan be niin ni nan si guu le ke bin ku u.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Amaa Juu yaab a Nanbonn a Chiig a jim kaah nan nan malin.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: le Yisa a watiib nan bei u ke, Ni ye a nya doo le ki jo Judiya, wee a bɔɔnɔɔbin fir kan ligaril a siɛram yan wee a ŋaan.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Wan ban bininfobin biɛ waah ye wan kaa woog waah ŋaan nan ni. Ni ye a ŋaan man ke, ta a ba ki wɔɔn dulinyau.

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Nan bu-n u baantɔɔl a watiib kaa nan ŋaa u yada.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Mambu le Yisa nan bei bi ke, N-yonn man wee ni ye n ŋa-n kaa laa fo; nim bu ke n-yonn biɛn ŋan le.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Dulinya nan a ninfob kaa li fir nan ni, amaa bi nan min le, nan bu-n n ji siɛra ke bi toon ye titoonbir le.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Nim li chaa kuchiigin na. Min kaa laa ye n jo kuchiig nmɔn yaan ni, nan bu-n min bu ke, n yaa yonni kaa laa fo.

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: Waah nan len man nin, le u nan paar ki be Galilii.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: Amaa, u nabumin kaah nan jon kuchiigin a bɔɔn, le u mu nan woon ki jon, kaa ban bininfobin kan wi.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Tɔ kuchiig nmɔn yaan le Juu yaab nan choon ki ban u le ki baan ke, Uja nmɔn be laa.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Yisa a bɔr nan ŋuulin le kuninfogbaluŋ nmɔn yaan. Le biba ke, U ye uninfoŋan le. Le biba jiin ke, Aai, u lar bininfob le.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Amaa uba kaa nan pag wee bininfob gbiri, nan bu-n bi nan san Juu yaabin le.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Kuchiig nmɔn a jim nan nan por sisiigin le Yisa nin nan jon tan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin a loob ki be wɔɔn bininfob.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Ni nan gar Juu yaabin le bi baan ke, Uja nan ŋa niin ki kan nbiɛm nan wee kaa sin ye wan tinn aa.

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N yaa wɔɔmin kaa ye n yaan ni. Mi nya uma wan nan tunimin ban le.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Ni ye wan gann ke un tun Uwonbɔr a bam ke, u li biɛ ke n wɔɔnimin nya Uwonbɔr ban le bee ni ye n tɔɔl a biɛm lee.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Wan pag u tɔɔl a bɔr ke u pag man un kan u tɔɔl a jilima le, amaa wan tun ki ban Uwonbɔr wan nan tun win kan jilima ke u ye ubamɔndaan le; waa jɔɔ igalin iba.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Moziz kaa nan sei ni nsiimin naa? Amaa ni ni uba kaa nɔɔ nsiim nmɔn kaah len man ni. Ba ŋa ni ban ni ku maa.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Le kubabugin jiin ke, Kinaanbiig le jɔɔŋ. Ŋma gbaar un ku ŋaa.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Le Yisa nan bei bi ke, N nan ŋa ligaril a siɛra liba le, le ni biɛn jee.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Ni ma biɛn yɔli, Moziz kaah nan sei ni lipunjiil a siimin , le ni jiil kibig a punl likpakɔɔl daal.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Tɔ ni ye ke ni li fir jii kibig a punl likpakɔɔl daal wee Moziz a siimin laa bii ke, babu wee ni ŋuui fii n bu maah teb uninfomun likpakɔɔl daalin naa?.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Ni jiɛn kununaŋ a bɔjila, wee ki li jɔɔ ibamɔn a bɔjil.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Libol nmɔn yaan le Jerusalam a ninfob biba nan piin ki baan ke, Naa ye uja nan le bi gbaar ke bin ku naa?.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: U ma sɔ, ki si pag bininfobin npaan nin, le baa pag u niba. Ban jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔmin chee ke u ma sin ye Masiya waa?.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Amaa ti nyi uja nan kaah nyan kitiŋ kan; Masiya-n nan foon ke, uba kaa yin nyi waah nyan kitiŋ kan ni.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Yisa kaah la nan be iwonjiɛn niin nin, le u tiɛn paab npɔɔn bu ki ke, Nn, ni nyim, le ki nyi ma nyan nan yaan. Maa dan do n baantɔɔl a pɔɔm bu, amaa wan nan tunimin le ye ubamɔndaan. Nim kaa nyi wi.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: amaa min nyi u, nan bu-n uma nan tum le n nyan u ban le ki dan.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Ni nan bu le bininfob ban nan be niin ni nan gbaar ban bin choo u, amaa uba kaa nan taa u nɔɔ paan u bu, nan bu-n u yonni kaa laa nan foo.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Ni man biɛn nan, bininfob takpem ban nan be kulaguŋin yaan nan ŋa u yada. Le ki ke, Masiya-n nan foon ke, u li ŋa ligaril a siɛram wee nin jo uja nan kaah ŋa man naa.

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Farasiim nan gbir kuninfogbaluŋin kaah ŋulin Yisa nin u garil a siɛra bu-n, le biminmanib a yudaau nin Farasiim nan tun iwonjiɛn niin a guguubin ke bin tan choo u.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Le Yisa len ke, N yin be ni ban n-yonn man kaa fɔg le, ki nin jo wan nan tunimin ban.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ni na li ban n bol, amaa naa na li kami; maah nan yin be nan yaan nin, naa na li fir foon niin ni.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Le Juu yaabin nin baan tab ke, Uja nan yin jo laa wee taa li fir ban kan waa? U li jo nan yaan wee ti yaa ninfob yaal ki be Girig yaabin ni, ki tan li wɔɔn Girig yaabaa?.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Waah nan len ke, Ni na li ban n bol, amaa naa na li kami, maah nan yin be nan yaan nin naa na li fir foon niin ni a taai yaa.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Kuchiig nmɔn a joor daal lan ye lidaal takpenlin, le Yisa nan fii sir paab ki tiɛn npɔɔn bu ke, Ni ye nnyunyum jɔɔ wan ke, ni che un dan n ban ki nan nyu.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Wan biɛn ŋaam yada ke Uwonbɔr a mɔleen yin kaah nan len ke, ikpen yan jɔɔ limiɛfol a nyum li puu ki nyann u sufu ni.

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Yisa kaah nan pag man nin, u nan len Uwonbɔr a Kasi a Naaŋ kan wee ban ŋa u yada-n na yin ga-n le. Ki nan nan kpaa n-yonn nmɔn nin, Uwonbɔr kaa la nan ta Kinaaŋin ki sei ban ŋa u yada-n ni, nan bu-n Yisa kaa la nan kan kupagi.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Baah nan gbir u mɔleen yin, le bininfobin biba ke, Ibamɔn uja nan sin ye ulinlenn nin le.

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Le biba ke, U ye Masiya-n le. Amaa le biba ji nan baan ke, Masiya-n yin ŋa niin ki nyann Galilii?.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Uwonbɔr a mɔleen yin kaa ke Masiya-n na li nya ti baa Defid a niin yaabin nin Betiliham, kitiŋ kan wee Defid nan be-n naa.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Mambu bininfobin a potiɛm nan bɔgir le Yisa bu.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Biba nan ban bin choo u le, amaa uba kaa nan ta u nɔɔ ki maa wi.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Le iwonjiɛn niin a guguubin nan jir biminmanib a ninkpeeb nin Farasiimin ban, le bi baan bi ke, Ba ŋaa wee naa ta u kɔɔn naa.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Le iwonjiɛn niin a guguubin pag npaan nin ke, Uba kaa la nyi pag ke uja nan ni.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Le Farasiimin jiin ke, Ni ban ni ke u ji lar ni mu le bee?.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Biyudamin a ni uba bee Farasiim ŋa u yada waa?.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Aai! Amaa kuninfogbaluŋ ban kaa nyi nsiimin a niba-n, limɔpool be bi bu.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Le Nikodimosi, wan nan chinn jon Yisa ban nin wee ki nan ye bi ni a uba-n baan ke.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: Ti siim laa nyi ku wan wee taa chinn gbir u bɔr wee ki biɛ waah tun nan naa.

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Le bi jiin ke, A mu nyan Galilii lee? Lig ni poolin na, wee ki biɛ ke ulinlenn kaa nyann Galilii.

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Le bi biɛn nan jir kun bi baantɔɔl a niin yin.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Waah nan chaa-n, le u nan kan uja wan bi nan ma u nin tijɔɔn.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin baan u ke, Wan wɔɔn, ŋma nan ŋa tibira, uja nan bee u baa nin u naa, wee bi nan ma u nin tijɔɔn naa.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Le Yisa ke, Naa ye uja nan bee u baa nin u na le ŋa tibiri, amaa ni sei yaag le ke bininfob nkan Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm kaah yin tun man u miɛfolin.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Naah le la ye nweehn nan, ni ye ti tun wan nan tumin a toon le. Kunyiɛg chaan, le uba kaa li fir tun ni.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: Maah la be dulinya nan yaan nan, min le ye dulinya nan a wonweem.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Waah nan len nan nin, le u sii timɔsann ki jaa tum, le ki ŋmarin titann nin timɔsann nin, le ki taa ŋa uja-n a numbili.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: Le ki bei u ke, Li chaau, tan ŋunn a num Siloom a biɛŋin. . Le bi nan ta u jon ki tan ŋunn u num, le u jir kun ki nin ga.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Le u kunkɔɔn tab nin ban nan nyi kan waah miɛ-n nan baan, Naa ye uja wan nan ti ka ki miɛ-n sɔɔ.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Le biba nan ke uma le yen. Le biba mu ke, Aai, waa ye wan miɛ-n ni, u nann u le. Amaa le uja-n baantɔɔl yii ke, Min le yen.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Le bi baan ke, Ni ŋa nii a num ligiraa.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Le u jiin ke, Uja wan wee bi yi u ke Yisa-n le nan ta titansɔɔn ki ŋa n numbili yin. Le ki biɛm ke n li chaa Siloom ki tan ŋunn n numa. Le n jon ki tan ŋunn, tɔ le n nin gaa.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Le bi baan u ke, Uja nmɔn be laa? Le u ke, Maa nyi.

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Le bi nan ta uja wan nan jɔɔn nin ki dan Farasii yaab ban.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: Lidaal lan wee Yisa nan ta titansɔɔn ki ligir uja-n a numin nan ye likpakɔɔl daal le.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Mambu le Farasii yaabin mu nan baan uja-n naah nan ŋa man wee u num nan ligir. Le uja-n jiin ke, U nan ta titansɔɔn le ki ŋa n numbili yin, le n tan ŋunn, le ki ligir.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Le Farasii yaab biba len ke, Uja nan kaa nyan Uwonbɔr ban ni, nan bu-n waa ŋmag likpakɔɔl daali. Amaa le biba baan ke, Tibirdaan yin ŋa niin ki ŋa ligaril a siɛram nan naa? Le bi potiɛm nan bɔgir.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Ni joorim le bi ji nan labir ki baan ujɔɔn nin ke, Naah ye a yaa num le u ligirin, a yin ke baa ki jiin u paamaa? Le uja-n jiin ke, U ye ulinlenn le.

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Juu yaabin kaa le nan ŋa yada ke uja wan nin nan gaa-n nan ye ujɔɔn le, man ke baah nin nan tun yin u baa nin u na-n.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: Le bi nan baan bi ke, Ni bija sɔɔ? U ma le ni nan ke bi nan ma u nin tijɔɔn nin naa? Ni ŋa nii wee daandaan nan u ligiraa.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Le u baa nin u na jiin ke, Ti nyi waah ye ti bija, le ki ji nyi taah nan ma u nin tijɔɔn.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Amaa naah ŋa man wee u ligirin, bee wan ligir u numin ma, taa nyi. Baan u. U choo tininkpera; u li fir pag u baantɔɔl.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: Nan bu wee u baa nin u na nan len man nin, bi nan san Juu yaab ban pii nan bon ke wan biɛn chee ke Yisa le ye Masiya-n ke bi li nyan udaan kuwonjiɛndiigin.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Man bu le u baa nin u na nan ke, U choo tininkpera; baan u.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Le bi ji nan yin uja wan nan jɔɔn nin, le ki ke, Pag Uwonbɔr a ti nyi uja nan kaah ye tibir daan.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Le uja-n jiin ke, U ye tibir daan nin waah ye ke, maa nyi. Maah nyi nan ye, n nan jɔɔn na le daandaan nan n ligira.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Le bi baan u ke, U ŋaaŋ niin? U ligir a num niin.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Le u jiin ke, N pii bei na le naa peligi. Ba ŋa ni ji ban ni gbir aa? Bee ni mu ban ni ŋa u bɔɔnɔɔb lee.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Le bi sugir u ki ke, A ye uninfo nan a bɔɔnɔɔru le! Tim ye Moziz a bɔɔnɔɔb le!.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Ti nyi Uwonbɔr kaah nan pag sei Moziz, amaa uninfo nan ma, taa pii nyi waah nyan nan yaan ni.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Le uja-n jiin ke, Man ke ni ye nan wee bi nan li tiɛr le! Naa nyi waah nyan nan yaan ni, u mu li ligir n num.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Ti nyi ke Uwonbɔr kaa pelig tibirdam a wonmiɛli. U pelig wan nyi Uwonbɔr ki tun u bamin a wonmiɛl le.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Uba kaa la nyi gbir ke bi ligir uja wan bi nan ma u nin tijɔɔn a numi.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Ni ye ke uja nan kaa nan bi nyan Uwonbɔr ban ke, waa nan bi li fir ŋa niba.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Le bi jiin u ke, Fin wan bi nan ma nin tibir nan, a mu ye ŋmiɛ ki li wɔɔn taa! Le bi ŋɔɔr u kuwonjiɛndiig nmɔn yaan.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Yisa nan gbir ke bi ŋɔɔr wan a num nan ligirin kuwonjiɛndiigin na, le u ban kan u, le ki ke, A ŋa Usaal a Bija yada waa.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Le uja-n nan baan ke, Uyudaan, ŋma ye waa, beim ni wee n ŋa u yada.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Le Yisa ke, A nin kan wa, ibamɔn buu, u ma ye wan nin fin pag nan.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Tɔ le uja-n ke, Uyudaan, n ŋa yadau, le u gbaan Yisa a nintogil ki jiɛn u.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Le Yisa ke, Libɔjil bu le n dan dulinya nan yaan, wee bijɔɔm nligir le ban lig nlabir bijɔɔm.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Farasii yaab biba ban nan be u ban nin nan gbir waah len man le ki baan u ke, Baa? Ti mu ye bijɔɔm lee.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Le Yisa len ke, Ni bi ye bijɔɔm ke, naa bi li jɔɔ tibir a galin yi; amaa naah ke ni num ga nan, ni galin la be.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Amaa le Yisa nan jon Olif a Jɔɔlin.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Kutaayɔg paam le u ji nan kpiir uba iwonjiɛn niin a loob, niin le bininfob biɛn nan lagin ki ka jin u, le u nan kal ke un wɔɔn bi.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Ban wɔɔn nsiimin nin Farasii nan ŋmagin upii wan wee bi nan choo u iwanchɔgin toon nin ni. Bi nan che u sir kulaguŋin a nintogil le.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: le bi bei Yisa ke, Wan wɔɔn, bi choo upii nan le u be tun iwanchɔgin toon.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Nsiim a poolin Moziz nan pag sei ti ke ti gab li yaa piibol itangbaga. Daandaan nan a ke niin.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Bi nan ta nbinbaan nan ŋa kupeŋ le, baah li ta niwann nan ki galin u. Amaa le Yisa nan bɔɔn tum le ki nan piin ki jɔɔ u nɔɔbili ki sɔb tum.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Baah nan gbaa baan win, le u yaar paab le ki bei bi ke, Ni ye ke ni ni uba be kaa jɔɔ tibir ke, udaan nchinn gab upii nmɔn litangbagila.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Le u ji nan bɔɔn ki sɔb bil tum.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Waah nan len man nin, le ban nan gbirin piin ki fɔr uba ba, ban nan ye bininkpeebin le nan piin ki fɔr, hali ki nan jiɛn Yisa nin upii-n a binba nin paar si niin.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Le Yisa yaar u yul paab le ki baan ke, Upii, bi be laa? Uba kaa ji gur un bii ŋaa.

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Le upii-n ke, Uyudaan, uba kaa ji bii mi. Le Yisa nyan pag npaan ni ke, Tɔ man ke n tɔɔl kaa bii ŋi, li chaa daandaan nan na wee ki jiɛn tibir a binbema.

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Yisa kaah ji nan pag sei bininfobin, u nan ke, Min le ye dulinya nan a wonweem. Wan biɛn nɔɔm kaa li nyi chonn libunbɔnlin ni, amaa u li jɔɔ nwonween man tenn limiɛfol.

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Le Farasii yaab faa u limɔkpel ki ke, Fin sɔ, le ki ji a baantɔɔl a siɛra, a siɛra jil nmɔn kaa ye ibamɔn ni.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Le Yisa len ke, Hali ni ye ke n ji n baantɔɔl a siɛra ke, n siɛra jil nmɔn ye ibamɔn le, nan bu-n n nyi maah nyan nan yaan ki ji nyi maah chaa nan yaan. Amaa nim kaa biɛ maah nyan nan yaan bee maah chaa nan yaan ni.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Ni ji tigbanann a bɔjil le; min kaa ji uba tibɔri.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Amaa N jin tibɔr ke, maah len man nin ye ibamɔn le, nan bu-n maa be n binba. N si Baa, wan nan tumin ban le.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Ni tɔɔl a siim a poolin ni sɔb le ki wɔɔn ke bijab bilee nan jin siɛra niwann bu ke ni ye ibamɔn le.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: N ye wan jin n baantɔɔl a siɛra-n le, n siɛra jir wan gurin yin ye n Baa, wan nan tunim le.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Tɔ le bi baan u ke, A Baa be laa? Le Yisa jiin ke, Naa nyi min bee N Baa, ni bi nyi min ke, ni bi li nyi n Baa mu.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: U nan pag imɔbon nan le ki wɔɔn bininfob waah nan be iwonjiɛn niin a nan paam mal baah nan ta kumanug adaka siin nan yaan nin. Amaa uba kaa nan choo wi, nan bu-n u yonni kaa la nan fo.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Le Yisa ji bei bi taaliba ki kpiɛ ke, N yin fɔr le, ni na li ban n bol, ni na li kpo ni bir bu. Maah yin jo nan yaan nin, naa li fir dan niin ni.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Ni ma li nan che Juu yaabin baan tab ke, U yin ku u ba lee? Ni ma bu le u len ke, Maah yin jo nan yaan nin, naa li fir dan niin ni naa.

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Amaa le u ji tugir ki len ke, Ni nyan tum nan yaan le; min nyan paab le. Ni ye dulinya nan yaab le; min kaa ye dulinya nan yaa wi.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: N bei ni ke ni li kpo ni birin; ni ye ke naa ŋa yada ke min li ye maah ke n ye wan nin ke, ni le sin yin kpo ni birin le.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Le bi baan ke, A ye ŋmiɛ? Le Yisa jiin ke, Maah lee nan ke n ye wan npiinimin nin.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: Maah yin pag man ni bɔjilin yab takpema. Amaa maa yin pag n tɔɔl a bami sei ke wan nan tumin a bam, le maah gbir man u ban nin le n bei dulinya.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Baa nan biɛ ke u nan bei bi u Baa a bɔr le.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Mambu le Yisa nan len ke, Ni nan yuur Usaal a Big paab ke, tɔ ni li biɛ ke min le ye wan wee n ke n ye-n, le maa ŋaan niba n baantɔɔli amaa n le pag Baa-n kaah nan wɔɔnim man le.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: Wan nan tunimin be n ban; waa jaam n binba, nan bu-n n gbaa ŋaan nan gbee u num le.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Hali waah nan pagin, binib takpem ta bi yada ki ŋa u ni.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Le Yisa nan bei Juu yaab ban nan ŋa u yada-n ke, Ni ye ke ni ŋmag maah wɔɔn man nin ke, ni sin ye n bɔɔnɔɔb le.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: Ni li biɛ ibamɔn kaah ye nan, le ibamɔn nmɔn li che ni kan feegab.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Le bi jiin u ke, Ti ye Abiraham a yaabili le, ti mu kaa nyi ŋa uba a yomu wi. Ba ŋa a len ke ti li kan feegabaa.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, wan biɛn ŋa tibir ye tibir a yomu le.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Uyom kaa jɔɔ nan yaan wee u gbaa be iniin a poolin ni, amaa kiniinbijag ma ye iniin yaau le ki gbaa chaa.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Mambu ni ye ke Bija-n sei ni feegab ke, ni yin kan feegab le ibamɔn.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: N nyi ke ni ye Abiraham a yaabili le. Ni ma biɛn yɔli ni ŋa sili ni kum le, nan bu-n maah wɔɔn nan nin kaa kɔɔ ni tubomin ni.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: N bei ni maah nan kan man le Baa-n a nintogil, le nim ŋaan naah gbir nan ni baa ban.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Le bi len ke, Abiraham le ye ti baa. Le Yisa len ke, Ni bi ye Abiraham a buwaab ke, ni bi li ŋa Abiraham kaah nan ŋaan man ni.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: A li ke naah ye man nin, ni gbaar le ni kum, uja wan bei ni ibamɔn yan wee n gbir Uwonbɔr ban nin. Abiraham kaa nan ŋaan man ni.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Ni ŋaan nan wee ni tɔɔl a baa ŋaan le. Le bi yii u mɔboi nmɔn ki ke, Taa ye njagɔɔrbumi, taah jɔɔ Baa wan nin ye Uwonbɔr baantɔɔl le.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni ye Uwonbɔr le ye ni Baa ke, ni bi li jiɛm, nan bu-n n nyann Uwonbɔr ban le ki be do daandaan nan. Maa dan n baantɔɔl a pɔɔmin ni; amaa u ma nan tum.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Ni ŋa wee naa biɛr n mɔboi yaa? Nan bu-n naa gbir maah len man ni.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Ni ye ni baa, ulinlaril, a buwaab le, ki ban ni li ŋaan ni baa kaah ban man. U pii nan ye uninfokur le npiinimin, kaa ŋmag ibamɔn ni, nan bu-n ibamɔn kaa be u ban ni. U nan por tibɔkar ke, u pag u bol a leen le, nan bu-n u ye tibɔkar daan le ki ji ye tibɔkar a baa.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Ni ma biɛn yɔli, maah bei ni ibamɔn nin bu, naa ŋaam yada!.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Ni ŋma li fir nyan wɔɔn ke n jɔɔ tibiraa? Ni ye ke n len ibamɔn ke, ba ŋaa naa ŋaam yadaa?.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Wan ye Uwonbɔr yaau gbir Uwonbɔr kaah len man le. Nan bu wee naa gbirin ye le ke naa ye Uwonbɔr yaabi.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Le Juu yaab jiin u ke, Taa jɔɔ ibamɔn nin taah ke a ye Samariya a ninfo le ki jɔɔ kinaanbiigaa.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Le Yisa ke, Maa jɔɔ kinaanbiigi, amaa n tenn n Baa le jilima le nim kaa tenn min jilima.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: Maa gbaar ban n tɔɔl a pagi, amaa uba li be ki ban ki, u ma ye ubɔjir.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, ni ye ke wan biɛn ŋmag maah wɔɔn man ni ke, waa li nyi kpo.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Ni nan bu le Juu yaab nan waag ki ke, Daandaan nan ti biɛ ke a jɔɔ kinaanbiig! Abiraham nan kpou nin bilinlem tɔɔla, amaa a ke wan biɛn ŋmag ŋaah wɔɔn man nin ke waa li nyi kan nkumi.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: A yaab jon ti baa Abiraham waa? U nan kpou, le bilinlemin mu nan kpou. A tiɛr ke a ye ŋmiɛ?.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni ye n pag n ba ke, n pag nmɔn ye yɔli le. N Baa, wan wee ni ke u ye ni Wonbɔr win, u ma ye wan pagirim.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: Hali naah kaa le nyi win, min nyi u. Ni ye n ke maa nyi u ke, n li ŋa tibɔkardaan ke nimin, amaa n nyi u le ki ŋmag u mɔleen.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: Ni nan mɔ ni baa Abiraham le ke u nan kan n dam dulinya nan yaan; u nan kan na le u pool sɔŋ.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Le Juu yaab bei u ke, Ŋaa la fo ibin piŋmu, le a nan kan Abiraham aa.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, Abiraham a na kaah nan maa win, ki miin n pii ye le.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Mambu le bi nan pii itangbag ke bin gab u, amaa le Yisa woon Iwonjiɛn niin a guguubin ban ki nyan.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: N-yonn nba a bɔɔn, le Yisa nan jon don Jerusalam ke un tan ji Juu yaab a chiig.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Tɔ le Jerusalam a poolin kubiɛŋ nan be ki mal ntimansanmɔnn man wee bi yi ki ke Ipee a Timansanmɔmin, kubiɛŋ kan wee Juu yaab a leen Aramayig bi yi ki ke Befesida wee ki jɔɔ nnaadam miŋmu man jin kubiɛŋin ki too-n.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Doo le kuninfogbaluŋ ban ye bibaarib nan ti dɔɔ, bijɔɔm, ndiɛm nin nwɔbim.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Ubaar wan nan be niin ni nan ŋa ibin piita nin iniin kaa fir choon ni.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan u dɔ niin wee ki sin gbir ke u binbem nmɔn yuurin, le u baan u ke, A ban a kan kutebugaa.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Le ubaarin jiin ke, Uninkpel, nnyum nan nan dam dam ke maa jɔɔ wan li terim wee n jiiri. N gbaar n jiir ke uba chinn jiirim le.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Tɔ le Yisa bei u ke, Fii paaba! Yuur a wandɔɔnwaŋ ki li choon na.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Libonl nmɔn le uja-n nan kan kutebug; le ki yuur u wandɔɔnwaŋ le ki nan choon. Lidaal lan wee man nmɔn nan ŋa-n nan ye likpakɔɔl daal le.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: mambu le Juu yaabin nan bei uja wan nan kan kutebugin ke, Ni ye likpakɔɔl daal le; nsiimin kaa chee nin a tu a wandɔɔnwaŋin.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Ni nan a bɔɔn, le Yisa nan don Galilii a mɔgil a gbaam nan fɔg .

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Amaa le u jiin ke, Uja wan che n pɔɔgin beim ke, Yuur a wandɔɔnwaŋ ki li choon na. .

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: le kuninfobabug nan nɔɔ u, nan bu-n bi nan kan ligaril a siɛram yan wee u nan tun ki sei bibaaribin le.

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Mambu le bi baan u ke, Udaan ye ŋmiɛ wee ki beiŋ ke a taa a wandɔɔnwaŋ ki li choon naa.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Le Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan don lijɔɔl a paab ki tan kal.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Uja wan nan kan kutebugin kaa nan nyi wan ye wi, nan bu-n Yisa nan ŋmarin kubabug nmɔn yan ki bora.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Juu yaab a Yuglagir a Chiigin nan mala.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Lidaal nmɔn a bɔɔn le Yisa nan ban kan u iwonjiɛn niin le ki bei u ke, Lig, a pɔɔga. Jiɛn tibir a tuma, naa ye man ke nan bir jon man li fir kaŋ.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Yisa kaah nan nan lig ki kan kuninfobabugin kaah chaan u ban nin, le u bei Filib ke, Ti yin da boroboro laa ki sei bininfob nan njii.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Le uja-n nan fɔr niin le ki nan tan bei Juu yaab ke Yisa li nan che u kan kutebug.

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: U nan baan man ke un mag u le, amaa u pii nan nyi u poolin waah nan yin ŋa man.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Yisa kaah nan teb uja-n likpakɔɔl daalin bu, le Juu yaab nan piin ki mugir u.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Le Filib jiin u ke, Iŋmal iniin a yɔr kaa li fir da boroboro wee i yin yab ki foo wee bi biɛn pii nyiri.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, N baa le tun U toon le ki nan kpaa dinan, le n mu, tɔɔl, tun le.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Le Anduru wan ye u bɔɔnɔɔb uba ki ye Simɔnn Piita a waalin nan pag ke.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Yisa kaah nan len man nin bu le Juu yaab nan walin npɔɔn bu ke bin ku u; baa nan yin ku u ke waah bii likpakɔɔl daal a siimin a binba bu, amaa ke u le ji yi Uwonbɔr le ke u Baa, ki ke uma nin Uwonbɔr kpaa le.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: Kibig kiba sɔ ki jɔɔ boroboro bum miŋmu kpeŋ kpeŋ nin njanbum milee, amaa ni yin ŋa nii ki tol foo kuninfogbaluŋ nan naa.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Le Yisa nan jiin ki bei bi ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, Bija-n kaa li fir ŋa niba u baantɔɔli, u li fir ŋa nan wee u kan u Baa-n ŋaan le, nan bu-n niwann nan biɛn wee Baa-n ŋaan nin Bija-n mu ŋaan ni ma le.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Le Yisa len ke, Che bininfob nmɔn nkala. Timɔɔr takpem le nan be niin yaan, le bijabin nan kal, bi nan foo bijab turiŋmuu.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Baa-n kaah jiɛ Bija-n bu le u wɔɔn u nan biɛn u ŋaan. Nn, u li wɔɔn u niwann nan jon man hali ke nin gar ni.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Le Yisa nan yuur boroboro-n, ki poor Uwonbɔr i bu, le ki nan che bi por i ki sei ban nan ka-n le bi biɛn gaa baah ban man. U ji nan ŋa man le ijan nin bu.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Nan bu-n Baa-n kaah le fiin ban kpo nkumin ki tenn bi limiɛfol man nin, man le Bija-n mu tenn ban wee u num gbee limiɛfol.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Bi biɛn kaah nan nan jin gbee-n, le u nan bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, Ni lagin baah jin gur yan nin na. Ni laa che niba npaar yɔli.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: Nan kpiɛ ye le ke, Baa-n kaa ji uba tibɔri, amaa le ki ta libɔjil ki ŋubin Bija-n a nɔɔn.

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Le bi nan lagin lagin baah nan jin ki gur boroboro biŋmu-n a poolin nin le ki gbeen tikachar piig nin tilee.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: ke bininfob biɛn li tenn Bija-n jilima ke baah tenn Baa-n jilima nan nin. Wan kaa sei Bija-n jilima ke waa sei Baa wan nan tun win, jilima.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Bininfobin kaah nan kan ligaril a siɛra yan wee Yisa nan tun nin a bɔɔn, le bi nin piin ki len ke, Ibamɔn, ulinlenn wan yin dan dulinya nan yaan nin sɔ.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, wan biɛn gbir n mɔleen wee ki ŋa Uwonbɔr wan nan tumin yada li kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛm wee kaa li kpo; u lagir nkum bu ki kan limiɛfola.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Yisa kaah nan biɛ ke bi lol le ke bin dan ki nan mug ŋa u ubɔrin, le u ji nan woon nyan niin ki don lijɔɔl a paab u binba.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, n-yonn chaan le n-yonn nmɔn pii foon na le ban kpo li gbir Bija-n a kukɔl le ban gbir waah len man nin li kan limiɛfol.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Kujɔɔg kaah nan foo-n, le u bɔɔnɔɔb nan jiir limɔgilin.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Ke Baa-n kaah jɔɔ limiɛfol u baantɔɔl man nin, man le u sei Bija-n npɔɔn ke u mu li jɔɔ limiɛfol u baantɔɔla.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: nin yaan le bi nan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki don limɔgilin ki jon Kapeniɛm. N-yonn nmɔn ni nan bɔŋa, le Yisa kaa la nan dan bi ban ni.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: Le u sei u tininkper a pɔɔm ke u li ji tibɔra nan bu-n u ye Uninfosaal a Bija le.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Ntafapɔɔrim le nan daar le tinyungbann nan fii.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Ni laa che nin ŋa ni ligarili, ke n-yonn chaan le ban biɛn be bi kaarin li gbir u kukɔl.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Baah nan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki don maal itaa bee itaa nin li genlin, le bi nan kan Yisa chonn nnyum bu ki forin kuŋaluŋin ban, le nin nan ŋa bi bijawaam le bi jegil.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: le ki nyann, ban ŋa nan ŋan li fii nin limiɛfol, le ban ŋa titoonbir li fii ki kpo.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Amaa le u nan bei bi ke, Ni ye min le; ni laa san bijawaami.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: N binba kaa li fir ŋa niba; n ji tibɔr maah gbir man le, le n bɔjil ye ibamɔn le, nan bu-n maa ŋaan n wiib kaah ban man ni, amaa n ŋaan wan nan tumin kaah ban n ŋa man le.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Tɔ le bi ni nan ban bin ta u kɔɔn kuŋaluŋin a poolin, le libol nmɔn le kuŋaluŋin foo likpengonl lan yaan wee bi chaa-n.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Ni ye ke n jin n baantɔɔl a siɛra ke, uba kaa li chee ke n bɔr nmɔn ye ibamɔn ni.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Kutaag kaah nan won nin le kuninfogbaluŋ ban nan paar be limɔgil a doolin nan nan biɛ ke kuŋaluŋ kiba le nan be niin, le bi nan biɛ ke Yisa kaa nan nɔɔ u bɔɔnɔɔb ki kɔɔ ki, amaa bi nan jon bi binba le.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Uba li be kpiɛ le ki ji siɛra maah ban man, le n nyi ke u siɛra jil ki jiin n paam ye ibamɔn le.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Tɔ le tiŋalin tiba nyan Tiberiya ki nan sir ki mal nan yaan wee Uyudaan nin nan poor Uwonbɔr ki sei boroboro-n wee bininfobin nan jin nin.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Ni nan tun ki tan baan Jɔnn na, le u nan jin n siɛra ibamɔn bu.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Nin yaan le kuninfogbaluŋin nan biɛ ke Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔbin kaa nan be niin ni, le bi kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki jon Kapeniɛm ki tan chonn ban u bol.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Naa ye ke n gaan uninfosaal a siɛra le; amaa n pag Jɔnn a siɛra le wee ni kan feegab.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Baah nan nan ban kan u limɔgil a nanbu a doolin, le bi baan u, Wan wɔɔn, ibayoo a foon doo.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Jɔnn le nan ye fila kan gaan ki sei ni nwonween, le ni nan gann n-yonn nba ki ji nwonween nmɔn a mɔm.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, ni chonn bam le, naa ye naah kan ligaril a siɛra bu, amaa naah ŋman boroboro le ki gbee-n bu.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: N jɔɔ n baantɔɔl a siɛra jil ke ki kpiɛg ki jon Jɔnn kaah jin n siɛrau. Nan bu-n, litoonl lan wee Baa-n ta seim ke n tun diɛ-n, le n be tun nin, le jin siɛra ke Baa-n li nan tunim.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Ni laa tun ki ban tijiir tan li bii, amaa ni li gbaar ban tijiir tan li che ni kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛm, tan wee Uninfosaal a Bigin yin sei ni-n. Uwonbɔr wan ye Baa-n le ta u cheem a daagim ki paan u bu.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: Le Baa wan nan tunimin baantɔɔl nan jin siɛra n buu. Naa la nyi gbir u kukɔl bee ki kan u nunaŋi.

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Tɔ le bi baan u ke, Ti yin ŋa nii ki tun Uwonbɔr kaah ban itoon yan naa.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: le u mɔleen mu kaa kɔɔ ni sufu ni, nan bu-n naa ŋa wan u nan tun nin yada.

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Uwonbɔr a toonl ye nan le: Ŋaam wan wee u nan tun nin yada.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Ni tinn Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin le linunmanl ki tiɛr ke nsɔbimin bu ni li kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi. Nsɔbim nan mu li wɔɔn siɛra maah ye wan.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Mambu le bi baan u ke, Baa a garil a siɛra wee a yin ŋa sei ti wee ti kan ki ŋaaŋ yadaa?.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: amaa nin ni ma biɛn yɔli, ni yii le ke naa yin dan n ban ki nan kan limiɛfoli.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Ti yaajam nan ŋman kulɔŋ a boroboro kukunkɔɔg bu; ke na pii nan sɔb man nin: U nan sei bi boroboro kan nyan paabin le bi jin. .

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: Maa ban kupag bininfob ban ni.

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, naa ye Moziz le nan sei ni boroboro kan nan nyan paabin ni, amaa n Baa le tenn boroboro bamɔn kan nyan paabin.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: amaa n nyi naah ye lininfobol lan. N nyi ke naa jiɛ Uwonbɔr ni sufu ni.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a boroboro ye min wan nyan paab ki jiirin ki nan tenn dulinya nan a ninfob limiɛfolin le.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: N ta n Baa a yinl le ki dan, le naa gaami; amaa ni ye ke uba ma ta u tɔɔl a yinl ki dan ke, ni li gaa u.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Le bi ke, Uyudaan, naah nyan dinan ki jɔɔ chaa li tenn ti boroboro nan na.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Ni ye ke ni ban tab a pagim ke, ni yin ŋa niin ki ŋa yadaa, wee kaa sin pɔɔg ni ba ke ni kan kupag kan nyan Uwonbɔr uba-n ban naa?.

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Le Yisa ni nyan pag npaan ni ke, Min li ye limiɛfol a boroboro. Wan dan n ban ke nkom kaa ji li choo wi, le wan ŋaam yada ke nnyunyum kaa li nyi choo wi.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Amaa ni laa tiɛr ke n li galin ni Baa-n a nintogili. Wan galin ni ye Moziz, wan bu wee ni tama be-n le.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Amaa ke maah bei ni man nin, ni kan ma amaa le kaa ŋa yada.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Ni ye ni nan bi ŋa Moziz yada ke, ni bi li ŋaam yada, nan bu-n u nan sɔb n bɔra.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Nan biɛn wee Baa-n ta seim li dan n ban, le wan biɛn dan n ban ke maa li ŋɔɔr wi.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Amaa naah kaa nan ŋa waah nan sɔb nan ni yada-n, ni yin ŋa nii ki ŋa maah len man nan yadaa.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Nan bu-n maa nan nyan paab jiirin ke n nan tun n wiib kaah ban man le amaa ke n nan tun Uwonbɔr wan nan tunimin a wiib kaah ban man.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Le Uwonbɔr wan nan tunimin a wiib kaah ban man sɔ, ke n laa lann nan biɛn wee u ta seimin ni, amaa ke lidajoorikaalin ke n don bi paab.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Baa-n kaah ban man ye le ke wan biɛn lig kan Bija-n ki ŋa u yada ke u li kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmin, le lidajoorikaalin n li don u paab.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Ni nan bu le Juu yaab nin nan piin ki ŋulin, waah nan len ke, Min le ye boroboro kan nan nyan paab ki jiirin nin.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Bi nan ke, Naa ye Yisa, Josef a bija-n sɔɔ? Wan a baa nin u naa ti nyi-n naa? U nin yin ŋaa nii ki len ke, N nyan paab le ki jiirin naa.

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni jiɛn iŋulin tab ban na.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Uba kaa li fir dan n ban wee naa ye Baa wan tunimin li ta u dan nin, le lidajoorikaalin ke n li don u paab.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Ni sɔb bilinlem a gbɔŋin ke: Uwonbɔr na li wɔɔn bi biɛn. Ban biɛn pelig Baa-n kaah len man nin wee ki tinn u ban nin li dan n ban.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Uba kaa la nyi kan Baa-n ni, man ke wan nyan Uwonbɔr ban nin; u yaa binba le nan kan Baa-n.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, wan ŋa yada jɔɔ limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛm.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Min le ye limiɛfol a boroboro.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Ni yaajam nan ŋman kulɔŋ a boroboro kukunkɔɔgin le, amaa bi nan kpou.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Amaa boroboro kan nyann paab ki jiirin nsɔ, ki ma le uninfo li fir ŋman le kaa li kpo.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Min le ye boroboro kan fo-n wee ki nyan paabin. Ni ye uninfo wan ŋman boroboro nan ke, u yin be le ki gbaa be. Boroboro nan le ye n gbanann, tan wee n yin ta sei dulinya, dulinya nan a miɛfol bu.

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Nin yaan le Juu yaab a ŋuui nan fii le bi nan piin ki far tab imɔkpel ke, Uja nan yin ŋa nii ki tenn ti u gbanann wee ti ŋmaa.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, naa ye ni ŋman Uninfosaal a Bija a gbanann ki nyun u cham ke, naa jɔɔ limiɛfol ni ban ni.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Wan biɛn ŋman n gbanann wee ki ji nyun n cham ke u jɔɔ limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi, le lidajoorikaal ke n li don u paab.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Nan bu-n n gbanann ye tijiir le ibamɔn le n cham ye nan nyu le ibamɔn.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Wan biɛn ŋman n gbanann ki nyun n cham ke u be n ban, le n mu be u ban.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: Ke Baa wan fo-n kaah nan tum wee n be ki jɔɔ limiɛfol Baa-n bu-n, man mu le wan ŋman n gbanann li jɔɔ limiɛfol n bu.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Boroboro kan nyan paab ki jiirin nin sɔ. Ni yaajam nan ŋman kulɔŋ a boroboro le ki kpo, amaa wan ŋman boroboro nan ke u li be ki gbaa be.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Waah nan be Kapeniɛm a wonjiɛndiigin ki wɔɔn nin le u nan len man.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: U bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah nan gbir man nin, le bi takpem len ke, Nwɔɔnim nmɔn pɔɔ hali, ŋma li fir gaa maa.

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Yisa kaah nan biɛ ke u bɔɔnɔɔbin ŋulin le waah pag man nin bu-n, le u baan bi ke, Ni bii ni sufu lee?.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Ke ni ye ke ni kan uninfosaal a bija-n kaah don waah nan nyan nan yaan nin ke!.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Kinaaŋin le tenn limiɛfol; tigbanann ma be yɔli le. Imɔbon yan wee n pag tenn ni nan ye kinaaŋ le, ki ji ye limiɛfol.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Amaa ni biba be kaa ŋa yada. Npiinimin, Yisa pii nan nyi bi ma nmɔn wan kaa nan ŋa yada nin wan na li kɔɔr u.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Le u ji len ke, Mambu le n nan bei ni ke uba kaa li fir dan n ban ni, man ke Baa-n li sei u nsan.

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: N-yonn nmɔn ki jɔɔ chaa le u bɔɔnɔɔb takpem labir ki jir le kaa ji nɔɔ wi.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Le Yisa baan itoon Piig nin bilee-n ke, Nim kaa li ban ni fɔri, bee ni li fɔraa.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita jiin u ke, Uyudaan, ti ni yin jo ŋma ban naa? A jɔɔ limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmin a mɔboi le.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Ti ŋa yada ki nyi ke a ye Uwonbɔr a Kasi Daan le.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Maa nan gann ni Piig nin bilee lee? Nin ni ma biɛn nin, ni ni uba ye ulinlaril le.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, uja wan kaa nɔɔ ntimansanmɔnn ki kɔɔ ipee a kpinkpenlin ni, amaa ki yug lagir niba paam ki kɔɔ ke, u ye unaayig le nin wan feen.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Uja wan nɔɔ ntimansanmɔnn ki kɔɔ-n le ye u pee yin a kpaaru.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Wan dɔ guu-n li loor ntimansanmɔnn ki sei u, le ipee-n pelig u kukɔl. U yi u pee yin a yin le ki ta i nya.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: U nan ta u tɔɔl yaai nmɔn biɛn nyan ke, u liin bi sam le bi paa u bɔɔn, nan bu wee u pee yin nɔɔ win ye baah gbir u kukɔlin le.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Amaa baa li nɔɔ uchaan ni; ibamɔn buu, bi li guun jiɛn u, nan bu-n baa biɛr uchaan nin a kukɔli.

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yisa nan pag nin ibɔŋal le, amaa baa nan biɛ waah nan bei bi man ni.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Man bu le Yisa ji nan ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, min le ye ipee-n a diig a timansanmɔnn.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Ban nan chinn dan nin min nin nan ye binaayigib le nin ban feen, amaa ipee-n kaa nan pelig bi bɔri.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Min le ye ntimansanmɔnn; wan biɛn nɔɔm ki kɔɔ li kan feegab. U li nɔɔm kɔɔ ki nɔɔm nya, ki tan kan timɔɔr sɔɔn.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Unaayigin dan le u nan su ki ku ki bii; min dan le ke bin kan limiɛfol, ki kan li nin linungbeel.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Min le ye upeekpaar ŋan nin. Upeekpaar ŋan nin le taa u miɛfol ki kpenn ipee.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Wan kpaa ipee nmɔn ki gaan tiyɔr kaa ye upee daan ni. Mambu u nan kan ligbingbel chaan ke, u jaar ipee-n le ki guun. Le ligbingbelin nan leg ipee-n ki worin.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Nan bu wee u guun nin, u ye wan kpaa ki gaan tiyɔr le mambu u poobil kaa wii ipee-n bu.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Min li ye upeekpaar ŋan nin; n nyi n pee le n pee yin mu nyim.

eng: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. I give up my life for the sheep.
local: a li ke Baa-n kaah nyim man nin wee n mu nyi Baa-n, le n ta n miɛfol kpenn ipee-n.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: N jɔɔ ipee iba yaa ye likpinkpenl nan a peei. Ni kpaa le n taan itɔɔl ni. I mu li pel n kukɔl, wee i nlabir ipee iba yan nɔɔ tab ki jɔɔ upeekpaar uba.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: Nan bu wee n Baa-n jiɛm ye maah ta n miɛfol ki kpenn, wee ki jin nan gaa li-n.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Uba kaa fee n miɛfolin nmɔn n ban ni, amaa n tɔɔl a wiib le n ta bil bi. N jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm ki li fir taa ki bil nin tininkper a pɔɔm ki ji li fir gaa li. N nan gaa nsiim nan n Baa-n ban le.

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Imɔbon nmɔn nan che Juu yaabin ji nan bɔgira.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Bi pam nan ke, Kinaanbiig li jɔɔ u le u waar. Ba ŋa ni pelig u bɔraa.

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Amaa le biba ke, Ileen nan kaa ye kinaanbiig kaah jɔɔ wan a leen yi. Kinaanbiig li fir ligir ujɔɔn a numaa.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Le Kuchiig kan tiɛr Jerusalam a wonjiɛn niin a yonn nan nan foo. Ni nan ye tiwar a yonn le.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: le Yisa nan chonn ki jin iwonjiɛn niin a nan paam bi nan yi ki ke Solomɔnn a dɔŋin.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Le Juu yaab nan lagin ki jin u, ki bei u ke, A ban ti li biɛnn ti potiɛlin ki tan kpaa ibayoo? Ni ye fin li ye Masiya-n ke, bei ti npaan ni na.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N nan bei na, amaa le naa ban ni ŋa yada. Ligaril a siɛram yan wee n ŋa n Baa a yinl bu-n li pag seim.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: amaa naah kaa ye n pee yin bu le naa ŋa yada.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: N yaa pee pelig n kukɔl le; n nyi i, le i nɔɔm.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: N sei i limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛm le, le yaa jɔɔ yin buri; uba kaa li fir fee i n nɔɔn ni.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: N Baa, wan ta i sei mi, yab jon ni biɛn na; uba kaa li fir fee i n Baa a nɔɔn ni.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Min nin n Baa ye nba le.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Le Juu yaabin ji pii itangbag ke bin gabir u ki ku.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: amaa le Yisa bei bi ke, N ta ligaril a siɛram takpem yan nyan Baa-n ban ki wɔɔn na. Ke i la baa bu wee ni gabir itangbag ni ku maa.
