eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Le Juu yaabin jiin ke, Taa gabiriŋ itangbag i ma bu, ŋaah sugir Uwonbɔr nbu, nan bu-n fin, usaal mu, nan ke fin ye Uwonbɔr.

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Le Yisa jiin bei bi ke, Naah sɔb dɔ ni siimin naa? N nan len ke ni ye iween le.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Ni ye ke Uwonbɔr yin bima ban wee U mɔboi foon nin iween ke, le Uwonbɔr a sɔbim mu kaa kpenn ke.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: ke wan wee Baa-n nan gann wee u ye u yaa win ki tunn u dulinya nan yaan nin? Ba ŋa wee ni galim ke n sugir Uwonbɔr maah ke N ye Uwonbɔr a Bija-n buu?.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Ni laa ŋa min yada see ke n nan tun n Baa kaah tun man nin.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Amaa ni ye ke n ŋa ni ke, naah kaa le ŋa min yada nan, ni ŋa ligarilin yadau, wee ni mann biɛ ke Baa-n be n ni, le n be Baa-n ni.

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Le bi ji nan ban bin choo u, amaa le u nan ŋmar bi nɔɔn.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Tɔ le Yisa nan jir don Jɔdann a kpem ki jon nan yaan wee Jɔnn pii nan chinn fun Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin. Niin le u nan be.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: le bininfob takpem nan dan u ban ki nan ke, Nin Jɔnn kaah kaa le nan ŋa ligaril a siɛra liba-n gbaa, waah nan len man uja nan bu-n nan ye ibamɔn le.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Nin yaan le bininfob takpem nan ŋa Yisa yada.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Tɔ le uja wan bi nan yi u Lazarusi nan nan bun. U nan nyan Betani le, kitiŋ kan wee u ma nin u ninsiɛb Meri nin Maata nan nyan ni.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: Meri, wan a ninja bun nan, u ma nan ye wan nan kpaar tulaali ki jaa Uyudaan bu ki ji ta u tɔɔl a yukper ki ŋmir U taafar.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Mambu le u ninsiɛbin nan tun bei Yisa ke, Uyudaan, ŋaah jiɛ wan nin bun le.

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Yisa kaah nan gbir man nin, le U ke, Iwiɛn nan kaa li joor nin nkumi. Aai, ni ye Uwonbɔr a pag le wee nin che Uwonbɔr a Bija-n kan npagim ni ma bu.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Yisa nan jiɛ Lazarusi nin u ninsiɛb Maata nin Meri.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: Amaa waah nan nan gbir ke Lazarusi bun nin, u nan paar waah nan be nan yaan nin le ki tan kpaa iween ilee.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Tɔ le ki nin nan bei u bɔɔnɔɔb ke, Ni che ti jir Judiya.

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Le bi nan bei ke, Amaa wan wɔɔnn, naah laa yuuri, le Juu yaab nan ban bin gabiriŋ itangbag, le a ji ban a jir niin naa.

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Naa ye tikur piig nin tilee li be nweehn naa? Usaal wan chonn nween kaa gbiɛri, nan bu-n, u ga dulinya nan a wonweem le.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: U chonn kunyiɛg le ke u gbiɛr, nan bu-n waah jɔɔ nwonween ni.

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Waah nan len man a bɔɔn, le u ji nan piin ki wɔɔn bi ke, Ti jɔ Lazarusi dɔɔn jiɛn le; amaa n chaa niin le n tan ligir u.

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin jiin ke, Uyudaan, ni ye u jiɛn li ke u li pɔɔg.

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Yisa nan pag Lazarusi a kum le, amaa u bɔɔnɔɔbin ma nan nyi ke u len kuwonaaŋ a jiɛm le.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Mambu le u nin bei bi npaan ni ke, Lazarusi kpou.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: nim bu n pool penn nin maah kaa bi be niin ni, nan bu-n ni li ŋa yada. Amaa ni che ti jo u ban.

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Le Tomasi , bei u bɔɔnɔɔtabin ke, Ni che ti mu njo, wee ni tan ye tim nin u ma nkpo ke ti kpo.

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yisa kaah nan foon nin, le u nan gbir ke Lazarusi kaah kpo wee bi pii u kukaagin a ween inaa sɔ.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Nya Batani ki chaa Jerusalam kaa nan foo maal ilee.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: le Juu yaab pam nan dan bin nan poor Maata nin Meri bi ninja kaah kpe-n a ninbɔŋ.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Maata kaah nan gbir ke Yisa chaan nin, le u nyan ki too u nsan bu, amaa Meri ma nan paar iniin le kaa nyan ni.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Le Maata bei Yisa ke, Uyudaan, ni ye ke a nan bi be ke, n ninja kaa nan bi li kpo.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Amaa n nyi ke hali daandaan nan a baan nan biɛn bu ke Uwonbɔr li ta ni ki seiŋ.

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, A ninja-n li fii nkumin jigin.

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Le Maata ke, N nyi ke u ji li fii lidajoorikaal a kum a fiimin.

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Min le ye nkum a fiim nin limiɛfol. Wan ŋaam yada li kan limiɛfol, hali nin waah le kpe-n biɛn na;.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: le wan biɛn jɔɔ limiɛfol wee ki ŋaam yada kaa jɔɔ un kpo. A ŋa ni nan yada waa.

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Le u bei u ke, Nn, Uyudaan, n nyi ke a ye Masiya-n le, Uwonbɔr a Bija-n, wan nan ye un dan dulinya nan yaan nin.

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Waah nan len man diɛ-n, le u nan jir tan yin u waal Meri ki nyan ngbaan. Le ki bei u ke, Wan wɔɔn nin be do, le ki baan a bu.

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Meri kaah nan gbir man nin, le u nan fii weem ki jon u ban.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: N-yonn nmɔn Yisa kaa la nan kɔɔ kitiŋin a poolin ni, amaa u la nan si nan yaan wee Maata nan nan to win le.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Juu yaab ban nan be Meri ban iniin, ki sar u sufu-n, kaah nan kan waah fii weem ki nyan man nin, le bi nyan nɔɔ u, ki tiɛr ke u nan chaa kukaagin yaan le un tan mɔɔ.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Meri kaah nan foo nan yaan wee Yisa nan be-n wee ki kan win, le u gbaan Yisa a taatum le ki ke, Uyudaan, a nan bi be do ke, n ninja nan kaa nan bi li kpo.

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yisa kaah nan kan Meri mɔrin, wee Juu yaab ban nan nɔɔ win mu nan mɔrin, le ni nan mug u kinaaŋin.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: Le u baan ke, Ni pii u laa? Le bi jiin ke, Uyudaan, dan ki nan lig.

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Le Yisa nan mɔɔ.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Le Juu yaabin len ke, Lig waah jiɛ u man.

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Amaa le bibam ke, U ma wan nan ligir ujɔɔn a numin kaa bi li fir guur uja nan wee u laa kpoo.

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Kinaaŋin kaah ji nan mug Yisa-n, le u dan kukaagin yaan. Ki nan ye libonl lan be litangbagilin, le litangbag takpenl loo ki mɔb.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Le Yisa len ke, Ni yuur litangbagilin na. Le Maata, wan ye utakpiirin a ninsa len ke, Amaa, Uyudaan, n-yonn nan ni li nu pam, nan bu-n waah be niin a ween inaa sɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Le Yisa ke, Maa beiŋ ke ni ye ke a ŋa yada ke, a li kan Uwonbɔr a pagaa.

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Le bi loor litangbagilin. Le Yisa lig paab le ki ke, Baa, N pooriŋ pam nin ŋaah gbir ki gaa n wonmiɛl.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: N nyi ŋaah gbaa gbir n bɔr, amaa n len man le bininfob ban si do nan nbiɛ, wee ki ŋa yada ke fin le tunim.

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: Waah nan len man ki diɛ-n, le Yisa yin nin nkukɔ pɔɔn ke, Lazarusi, nyann.

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Le utakpiirin nan nyann, u nɔɔi nin u taai nan bon nkum a kinkenl le ki ji piin u num bu. Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni nyan nkum a kinkenlin na wee ki che u li chaa kun na.

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Mambu Juu yaab pam ban nan dan bi nan poor Meri, wee ki nan kan Yisa kaah ŋa man nin, le bi ŋa u yada.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Amaa le bi ni biba nan jon Farasiim ban ki tan bei bi Yisa kaah ŋa man.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Le biminmanib a yudaau nin Farasiim nan yin Juu yaab a yudam a laguŋ. Le bi baan ke, Ti ka ŋaan baa? Ni lig uja nan kaah ŋaan ligaril a siɛram takpem man.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Ni ye ke ti che u ŋaan man ke, uninfo biɛn li ŋa u yada, tɔ le Rom yaabin li dan ki nan fee ti kasi a bol nan nin ti ninfob.

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Le bi ni uba, wan a yinl nan ye Kayafasi, ki nan ye biminmanib a yudaau libinl nmɔn nin nan pag paab ke, Naa nyi niba kolibi!.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Naa nyi ke uninfo uba kpo bininfob bu ke ni ŋan sei ni ki jon Juu yaab biɛn kaah li bur.

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: Waa nan len ni nan u tɔɔl a wiibi, amaa waah nan ye biminmanib a yudaau libinl nmɔn nin bu le u nan pag ilinlen leen ke Yisa na li kpo Juu yaab bu.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: le naa ye bi bol a binba bu, amaa Uwonbɔr a bum ban yaalin, ke un lagin bi bogil liba wee bi ŋa ninfo uba.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Mambu lidaal nmɔn le bi piin ki lolig ke bin nyan u miɛfol.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Mambu le Yisa kaa ji nan chonn kubabugin Juu yaab kaah be nan yaan nin. Amaa u nan nyan le ki jon kitiŋ kan bi yi ke Efiram, wee kaa fɔg nin kukunkɔɔgin ni, le ki nan tan be nin u bɔɔnɔɔb.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Juu yaab a Yuglagir a chiig kaah nan nan malin, le bininfob takpem nan nyan ntimin ki jon Jerusalam bin tan kan likaal a ŋunnim wee Yuglagir a chiig n nin foo.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Le bi nan be ki ban Yisa a bol, baah nan si iwonjiɛn niin nin paamin le bi baan tab, Ni tiɛr ke niin? Waa chaan un nan ji kuchiig nan le bee.

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Amaa biminmanib a yudam nin Farasiim nan pag sei le ke wan ban kan Yisa kaah be nan yaan ke, ni ye un bei bi le wee bin tan choo u.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: Yuglagir a chiig kaah nan nan gur iween iloobin, le Yisa nan foon Batani, nan yaan wee Lazarusi, wan wee Yisa nan fiin nkuminin be-n.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Do le kitiŋ nmɔn yaab nan sei Yisa jilima ki ŋa kunyiɛg a jiir ki sei u ma nin u bɔɔnɔɔb. Maata li nan por ti, le Lazarusi nan jin Yisa nin u bɔɔnɔɔb ban.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Le Meri nan ta tulaali pɔŋ, kan jɔɔ nbaam wee ki daag pɔɔ-n a lulog muŋ; le ki kpaar jaa Yisa a taai bu le ki ta u yukper ki ŋmir ŋmir u taai nmɔn. Le tulaali-n yaa baam fii gaa iniin nmɔn.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Amaa le u bɔɔnɔɔbin uba, Judasi Isikariya, wan nan nan yin kɔɔr Yisa-n, nan yii ki ke.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: Ba ŋaa wee tulaali nan kaa nan bi li kɔɔr ki ta iligin ki sei bilanjiimaa? Mi daag yab foo libinl munl a yɔra.

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: Naa ye u baga le nan be bilanjiim bu le u nan len man ni, amaa u nan ye unaayig le; nan bu-n waah nan gu ilig a pɔgilin, u nan ti nyann nan be niin nin le ki jɔɔ ter u ba.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni jiɛn wa, ni ye Uwonbɔr a lol le ke un woon mi le ki gu baah na li piim lidaal lan.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Ni yin gbaa jɔɔ bilanjiim le ni ban, amaa min kaa yin gbaa be ni ban ni.

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Juu yaab a ninfogbaluŋ kaah nan biɛ ke Yisa be niin nin, le bi nan dan, naa nan ye u binba a kam bu amaa ke bin nan kan Lazarusi, wan wee u nan fiin nkumin nin.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Mambu biminmanib a yudam nan lol Lazarusi tɔɔl a kum le.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: nan bu-n u kum a fiim bu le Juu yaab pam nan labir chaa Yisa ban le ki ŋaan u yada.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Lidaal lan nan paa-n le kuninfogbaluŋ kan nan dan kuchiig a jimin nan gbir ke Yisa be nsan bu ki chaa Jerusalam.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Le bi nan gen gen tikpikpagbann ki nan too u nsan bu, le ki tann ke, Jilima daan! Tikaar be wan dan Uyudaan a yinl bu-n! Tikaar be Iziral yaab a bɔru win bu.

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Le Yisa ban kibonpɔliŋ ki kal ki bu, ke naah pii nan sɔb man nin.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: Zayɔnn yaabaa, ni laa san niba a jawaami; Ni lig, ni bɔru le chaan nin, ki ka kibonpɔliŋ bu.

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Chinchinn u bɔɔnɔɔb kaa nan biɛ ni niɛ biɛn nan ni. See Yisa a pag a bɔɔn le bi nin nan biɛ ke nan sɔb nan, nan sɔb u bu le, le bi nan tun ni niɛ nan biɛn ki sei u.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Tɔ le waah nan fiin Lazarusi nkumin ki yin u nyan kukaagin ni, le kuninfogbaluŋ ban nan be niin ni nan mɔɔn tibɔr nmɔn.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Bininfob pam kaah nan gbir waah ŋa ligaril a siɛra man nin, le bi nan nyan ki too u nsan bu.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Ni ma bu le Farasiimin nan pag bei tab ke, Lig, naa kpiɛ ti niba. Lig dulinya nan yaab biɛn kaah nɔɔ u man.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Girig yaab biba nan ŋmar ban nan jon yuglagir a chiigin yaan bin tan jiɛn Uwonbɔr.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Bi nan dan Filib, wan nan nyan Befisada kan be Galilii-n ban le ki baan u ke, Uninkpel, ti bi ban ti kan Yisa le.

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Le Filib nan jon tan bei Anduru; le Anduru nin Filib nan labir jon ki tan bei Yisa.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N-yonn foo nin uninfosaal a Bija-n kan kupaga.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, ndibim kaa nan lu tum ki bur ke, mu lee la ye mu ba le. Amaa mu bur ke, mu li naan kidibin gbaluŋ.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Uja wan ban u miɛfol li lann li, le wan mu nan u miɛfol dulinya nan yaan li jɔɔ li nin n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Wan ban un tun terim ke ni ye u li nɔɔm le, le n be nan yaan ke, n toontunnu mu li be niin. N Baa li sei wan tun tenn min jilima.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Daandaan nan n binbem ye limugil le, ke n yin len ke niin? N Baa, feem gab n-yonn man wee n be nan naa? Aai, ni nan bu le n nan dan.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: Baa, che a yinl li jɔɔ kupaga! Le likukɔl nyann paab ki jiirin ki ke, N nan sei li kupaga, le n ji li sei li kupag.

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Kubabug kan nan be niin wee ki gbir likukɔlin nan len ke utaal le tiɛn; le biba mu len ke malaka le pag ki sei u.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Le Yisa len ke, Likukɔl nan ye nim a nyɔɔr le, naa ye min ni.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Daandaan nan ni foo Uwonbɔr nji dulinya nan a ninfob a bɔra; daandaan nan bi li ŋɔɔr dulinya nan a bɔrbig nnyan.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Amaa min, bi nan yuurim kutingbɔŋ nan yaan paab ke, n li dar bininfob biɛn dan n ban.

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: U nan len nan ki wɔɔn li kumbol lan wee u yin kpo le.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋin pag paab ke, Ti gbir Nsiimin ke Masiya-n na yin gbaa be le, ni ŋa niin wee a len ke, Ni ye bin yuur usaal a Bija le paabaa? Ŋma ye, Usaal a bija nmɔn naa.

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Naa li yuuri nwonweenin li be ni ban. Nwonween kaah la be ni ban nin, ni li chonn na wee libunbɔnlin laa pii ni. Usaal wan chonn libunbɔnlin kaa nyi waah chaa nan yaan ni.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Ni ta ni yada ki ŋa wan ye nwonween wee ki la be ni ban ni na, wee ni nan kpann nwonween a bum. Yisa kaah nan pag man ki diɛ-n, le u fɔɔr bi ban ki tan woon.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Hali nin Yisa kaah nan ŋa ligaril a siɛram bi nintogil a bɔɔn, baa le la nan ŋa u yada.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Ni nan biɛn nan ŋa le ke ulinlenn Azaya kaah nan len man nin ŋa ibamɔn, waah nan ke: Uyudaan, ŋma nan ŋa taah len man nin yada ŋmam bu wee Uyudaan a nɔɔ kpiiraa.

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Mambu baa nan fir ŋa yada, nan bu-n, ke Azaya kaah nan len man niba paam ke:.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: U jɔɔn bi nuŋi ya le ki ku bi sufuu, wee bi laa ga nin bi nuŋi yi, ki laa biɛr nin bi sufu ki laa labir n ban, wee n sei bi kutebugi.

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Nan bu wee Azaya nan len man nin, u nan kan Yisa a paga le ki pag u bu ki wɔɔn.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Ni ma biɛn yɔli n-yonn nmɔn bisanliibin pam nan ŋa yadau. Amaa Farasiimin bu bi nan san bijawaam le ke bi li nyan bi kuwonjiɛndiigin ni, mambu baa nan nyan bi yada ki wɔɔn ni.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: nan bu-n bi nan jiɛ bininfob a dom le ki jon Uwonbɔr a dom.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Le Yisa tiɛn paab ke, Uninfo ŋa min yada ke, waa ŋa n binba le yada, amaa u ŋa wan nan tunimin le yada.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: U lig n paam ke, u ga wan nan tunimin le.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: N dan dulinya nan yaan le ke nwonween nin, wee uba laa ŋa min yada wee ki sin ji be libunbɔnlin ni.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Ni ye wan gbir n bɔr wee kaa ŋa maah len man nin ke, maa ji u tibɔri. Nan bu-n maa dan n nan ji dulinya nan a ninfob tibɔr le, amaa n dan ke bin kan feegab le.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Wan yii min kaa ŋaan maah len man nin ke u daan a bɔjiru be; nan li ku u idajoorikaa ye n mɔleen yan n pagin le.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Nan bu-n maa pag n tɔɔl a pɔɔmin, amaa Baa wan tumin le beim maah li len man nin maah li pag man.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: N nyi ke waah beim man nin li jɔɔ limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm chaan. Mambu n pag Baa-n kaah ke n pag man le.

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Ni laa che ni mug ni sufu. Ni gaa Uwonbɔr jiu; ki gaa n mu jiu.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Tidir takpem le be n Baa a niin; naa bi ye man ke, n bi li bei ni. N chaa niin le n tan mann npaan ki guur ni.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: Ni ye ke n jon tan mann npaan ke, n li jirin ki nan ta ni wee nitɔɔl tan li be n ban nan yaan wee n be-n.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Ni nyi nsan man chaa maah yin jo nan yaan nin.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Le Tomasi bei u ke, Uyudaan, taa nyi ŋaa chaa nan yaan ni, mambu ti yin ŋa niin ki biɛ nsan naa.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Min le ye nsan nin ibamɔn nin limiɛfol. Uba kaa chaan Baa-n ban ni wee naa ye min bu.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Ni ye ke a sin nan biɛm ke, a li biɛ n Baa-n baantɔɔl. Daandaan nan ki jɔɔ chaa, a num kan wa le ki biɛ wa.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Le Filib len ke, Uyudaan, wɔɔn ti Baa-n ni wee nin kpaan ti.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Naa nyi maa, Filib, nin maah le nan be ni ban ni yuur man nan a bɔɔn naa? Wan biɛn kam ke u kan Baa-n na. Ni yin ŋa niin ki len ke, Wɔɔn ti Baa-n naa?.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Naa ŋa yada ke n be Baa-n ni le, le Baa-n be n nii? Imɔbon yan n pag sei ni kaa ye n tɔɔl a bɔri. Amaa, ti ye Baa, wan be n ni-n, le tun u toon.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Ŋaam yadau maah len ke n be nin Baa-n wee Baa-n be nin min nin, bee ŋa ligaril a siɛramin yadau.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, wan biɛn ŋaam yada ke u li tun maah tun man nin. U li tun tiwann tan jon man, nan bu-n n chaa Baa-n ban le.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Le n li ŋa naah li miɛ nan biɛn n yinl bu, wee Baa-n kan kupag Bija-n bu.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Ni ye ni miɛ nan n yinl bu ke, n li sei ni.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Ni ye ni jiɛm ke, ni li chee maah bei ni man.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: Le n li baan Baa-n, wee un ji sei ni utiter uba wan na li gbaa be nin ni n-yonn biɛn.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: Ni ma ye ibamɔn a Naaŋin. Dulinya nan a ninfob kaa li fir gaa wi, nan bu-n baa nyi kan wi, laa baan bin biɛ wi. Amaa ni nyi u, nan bu-n u be nin ni le ki li be ni ni.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: Maa li jiɛn ni wee ni be ni binba ke nkpeebimi; n li dan ni ban.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: Naa li yuuri, dulinya nan yaab kaa ji li kami, amaa nim li kam. Nan bu-n maah yin gbaa be nin n miɛfolin, ni mu yin be le nin limiɛfol.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: Lidaal nmɔn ni na li biɛ ke n be nin n Baa le, le ni be nin min, le n be nin ni.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Wan biɛn chee ki ŋmag n siimin kaah ban man ke, u ma ye wan jiɛm. Wan jiɛm ke u li kan njiɛm n Baa ban, le n mu li jiɛ u ki ta nba ki wɔɔn u.

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Le Judasi len ke, Amaa, Uyudaan, ba ŋa wee a yin ta a ba ki wɔɔn ti wee kaa yin wɔɔn dulinya yaabaa.

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni ye wan jiɛm ke, u li ŋa maah wɔɔn man. N Baa li jiɛ u, le n Baa nin min li dan u ban ki nan be u ban.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Wan kaa jiɛm kaa li ŋa maah wɔɔn man nin ni. Imɔbon yan ni gbir nan kaa ye n yaa mɔboi yi; i ye Baa wan nan tumin a mɔboi le.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: N pag ni nan biɛn maah la be ni ban nin le.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Amaa Utiter, Kasi a Naaŋ, wan wee Baa-n yin tun u n yinl bu-n, li wɔɔn ni niwann biɛn ki tiɛr ni niwann nan biɛn wee n nan bei ni.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Nposɔɔn li be ni ni; nposɔɔn man be n nin le n ta sei ni. Maa sei ni ke dulinya kaah tenn man nin ni. Ni laa che ni mug ni sufu ki laa san bijawaami.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Ni gbir maah ke, N yin fɔɔr le ki ji jirin ni ban. Ni ye ke a jiɛm ke, a li jɔɔ nmɔn na, maah chaa Baa-n ban nin, nan bu-n Baa-n li yab ki jom.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: Daandaan nan n bei ni le wee nin ni ŋa, wee ni nan ŋa ke ni li ŋa yada.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: Maa ji li pag nin ni ki yuuri, nan bu-n dulinya nan a bɔrbig le chaan nin. Waa jɔɔ n pɔɔmi.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: amaa ni kpaa le nin dulinya nan n tinn ki biɛ ke n jiɛ Baa-n le ki tun waah wɔɔnim man nin dede. Ni che ti fɔɔr do; daandaan nan.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Ni nan yin wonn kutaag Yuglagir a Chiigin le. Le Yisa nan nyi ke iyoo foo nin u nya dulinya nan yaan ki jo Baa-n ban. Waah pii nan jiɛ u yaab ban be dulinya nan yaan nin, le u nin nan ta u jiɛm man gbee-n kaah kpaa man ki wɔɔn bi.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Baah nan nan be ji kunyiɛg a jiirin, ki miin ulinlaril pii nan kɔɔ Judasi Isikariya, wan ye Simɔnn a bija-n, ke un kɔɔr Yisa.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyi ke Baa-n ta niwann biɛn ki ŋa u nɔɔn nin, le ki ke u nan nyann Uwonbɔr ban le ki ji yin jir Uwonbɔr ban;.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: le u nan fii sir paab tijiir a ji bol nmɔn yaan, le ki nan pir u daali kan wee u yee paan ni, le ki ta kunaapoog ki gbin u chaŋ.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Ni bɔɔn, le u minn nnyum ki ŋa kusɔŋin le ki ŋunn ŋunn u bɔɔnɔɔb a taafar, le ki ta kunaapoog kan gbin u chaŋin ki ŋmir i koor.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: U nan dan Simɔnn Piita ban, le u bei u ke, Uyudaan, a yin ŋunn n taafar lee.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Ŋaa la biɛ maah ŋaan nan nin ni, amaa a na li biɛ ni bɔɔn bu.

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Le Piita ke, Aai, ŋaa jɔɔ a ŋunn n taafari. Le Yisa jiin ke, Ni ye maa ŋunniŋ ke, ŋaa kpiɛ n ban ni.

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita jiin ke, Tɔ man ke, laa ŋunn n taafar a binba, amaa ŋunn n nɔɔi nin n yul ki kpiɛu.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, Uninfo wan fun nin kpaan u ŋunn u taai le; u gbanann biɛn kaa jɔɔ tijaginn ni. Le nim kaa jɔɔ tijaginn ni, amaa naa ye nim biɛn ni.

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Nan bu-n u nan nyi wan na yin kɔɔr u, mambu le u nan ke naa ye bininfob biɛn le kaa jɔɔ tijaginn ni.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: Waah nan ŋunn bi taafar ki diɛ-n, le u nan yee u wann yeewaŋ le ki jir u binbe bol. Le ki baan bi ke, Ni biɛ maah ŋa nan ki sei ni nan waa?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Ni yim Wan wɔɔn le nin Uyudaan, le ni ye ibamɔn, nan bu-n maah ye wan le yen.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Daandaan nan maah ye ni yudaau nin wan wɔɔnn wee ki ŋunn ni taafar nan, ni baantɔɔl li ŋunn tab a taafara.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: N wɔɔn ni limagil le wee ni li ŋaan ke maah ŋa nan sei ni-n.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Min le ye busub bamɔm, le n Baa le ye ukpadaan.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, utoontunn kaa be ki jon u daan wi, le ntoon mu kaa jon wan tun wi.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: U gaar ibon yan biɛn be nni wee kaa lo iloon nin le ki jaar, le ki mann gbar ibon yan biɛn lo iloon nin wee yin mann lo iloon.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: Ni niɛ biɛn, N nan bei na wee nin che ni laa kɔɔ kulɔŋi.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Daandaan nan naah biɛ ni niɛ nan, ni ye ke ni ŋa man ke ni li kan tikaar.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Nim pii be le chasi, nan bu-n maah nan bei ni imɔbon yan nin bu.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Bi li nyann ni kuwonjiɛndiigin; ibamɔn buu, n-yonn chaan le wan biɛn yin kuŋ le tiɛr ke u tun itoon le ki tenn Uwonbɔr.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: Maa pag nan ki tenn ni biɛn ni; n nyi ban wee n nan gann. Amaa ni nan le yin che nan sɔb dɔ Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin ŋa ibamɔn: Min nin wan por n jiirin le nan lig ziɛnim.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Ni li be nin ma, wee n mu li be nin ni. Nbon kaa li fir lo iloon mitɔɔli; ni kpaan le mi li tɔ busub a nyagil bu. Ni mu kaa li fir lo iloon ni see ke ni be nin min.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Bi li tun ni nan biɛn, nan bu-n baa nyi Baa-n bee min ni.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: N bei ni le daandaan nan wee ni nin ŋa, ni nan ŋa ke ni ŋa yada ke min le yen.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Min le ye linyagil; le ni ye ibon. Ni ye ke uninfosaal be nin min wee n be nin u ke, u li lo iloon takpem; naa ye n bu ke naa li fir ŋa niba.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: N nan bei ni ni nan le, wee n-yonn nmɔn nan foo ke ni li tiɛr maah nan kpaan nin. Maah la nan be ni ban nin bu le maa nan bei ni man ni.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, wan chee maah tunn wan biɛn a bɔr ke u chee n bɔr le; le wan chee n bɔr ke u chee wan nan tunimin a bɔr le.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Ni ye wan biɛn kaa lagin be nin min ke, u nann nbon man bi gaa jaa wee mi koor le; ibon nmɔn a bosu le bi pii, ki jaar nŋmi-n le yin gaa boor.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Daandaan nan n chaa wan nan tumin ban le, amaa ni ni uba kaa baam ke, A chaa laa.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Waah nan len nan a bɔɔn, le Yisa a pool nan bii ki naaŋin le u len npaan ni ke, N bei ni ibamɔn le, ni ni u ba le yin kɔɔrim.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Maah len man nin bu, ni pooi bii le.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Ni ye ke ni be nin min wee n mɔleen be nin ni ke, ni baan naah ban nan biɛn bu ke, ni li kan ni.

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin nan gbiɛ lig tab, ki nin biɛnn waah len wan.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Amaa n bei ni ibamɔn le: N fɔr ke ni yin kpiɛ nim le. Maa jon ke, Utiterin kaa yin dan ni ban ni; amaa n jon ke, n li tun u ni ban.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Ni nan li ye n Baa a pag, ke ni lo iloon takpem, ki wɔɔn ni ba ke ni ye n bɔɔnɔɔb le.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Ubɔɔnɔɔr wan wee Yisa nan jiɛ-n, li nan ka Yisa a logil.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: U nan dan ke, u li ji dulinya tibɔr le dulinya nlu, nin Uwonbɔr a buchin binbem, nin U bɔjil ki wɔɔn:.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Ke Baa-n kaah nan jiɛm man nin, man le n mu jiɛ ni. Daandaan nan li be nin n jiɛmin yaan na.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita nan gabin ubɔɔnɔɔr nmɔn ki ke, Baan u waah len wan na.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: Ki jiin tibir paam bi galin na, nan bu-n bininfob kaa ŋam yada;.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Ni ye ke ni chee sei n siimin ke, ni li be nin n jiɛmin yaan, ke maah chee sei n Baa a siimin man wee ki be u jiɛmin yaan man nin.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Le u nan gbeen jir Yisa paam, le ki baan u ke, Uyudaan, u ye ulaa.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: le ki jiin nbuchin binbem paam bi galin na, nan bu-n n chaa Baa-n ban le, nan yaan wee naa ji li kami;.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: N bei ni ni nan le ke n mɔm li be ni ban wee ni mɔm ngbeen.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, U ma ye maah yin ŋu lisaabɔl nan kisaŋin ki sei wan nin. Le ki ŋu lisaabɔlin kisaŋin, le ki taa sei Judasi Isikariya, wan ye Simɔnn a bija-n.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: le ki jiin libɔjil paam bi galin na, nan bu-n daandaan nan dulinya a bɔrbigin pii biiu.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: N mɔsiinl ye nan le: Ni li jiɛ tab ke maah jiɛ ni man nin.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Libol lan wee Judasi nan gaa lisaabɔlin, le Sintani nan kɔɔ u poolin. Le Yisa bei u ke, Ŋaah yin ŋa nan nin, ŋa ni weema.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: N jɔɔ tibɔr pam n pag sei ni, tan yab jon ni tuli kaah li gbir man.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Njiɛm takpem kaa be ki jon nin uninfosaal nta u miɛfol ki kpenn u jɔtiibi.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Amaa ban nan be tijiirin yaan nin uba kaa nan biɛ nan bu wee Yisa nan len man ki sei wi.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Amaa u ma, wan ye ibamɔn a naaŋin, nan dan ke, u li che ni biɛ ibamɔn biɛn. Waa li pag u tɔɔl a pɔɔm bu; u li pag waah gbir tan nin a binba le, le u li bei ni niwann nan la chaan nin ŋa.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Ni ye ni ŋa n siimin kaah len man ke ni ye n jɔtiib le.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Judasi kaah nan guu iligin, bibam nan tiɛr ke Yisa bei u waah li da nan le kuchiigin bu, bee ki ta niba ki sei bilanjiim.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: U li ta kupag ki seim ki ta nan ye n yann nin ki wɔɔn ni.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: Maa ji yin yi ni bitoontumi, nan bu-n utoontunn kaa nyi u daau a toon yi. Amaa, n yi ni n jɔtiib le, nan bu-n maah biɛ nan n Baa ban nin n che ni mu biɛ na.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Libol lan wee Judasi nan gaa lisaabɔlin, le u nan nyan. Le kunyiɛg nan nan ŋmiɛ.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Niwann nan biɛn wee Baa-n ye-n ye n yann le. Ni ma bu le n ke kinaaŋin li ta maah ye nan ki wɔɔn ni wee ni biɛ.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Nim li kaa nan gann mi, amaa min li nan gann ni ke ni lo iloon yan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi. Wee Baa-n li sei ni niwann nan biɛn wee ni baan u n yinl bu.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Waah nan jon nin, le Yisa ke, Daandaan nan le Usaal a Bija-n yin kan kupag le Uwonbɔr yin kan kupag u ni.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: Ni kpiɛ wama ke naa ji yin gami, le ni bɔɔn wama mu ni ji li kam.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Ni ye ke Uwonbɔr kan kupag u ni ke, Uwonbɔr li sei u Bija wan be u ni-n kupag, le ki li sei u kupag nba bu.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: N siim sɔ: Ni li jiɛ taba.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Le u bɔɔnɔɔbin biba baan tab ke, Ba ŋa wee u len ke, Ni kpiɛ wama ke naa ji yin gami, le ni bɔɔn bu ke ni ji li kam. Le ki ji len ke, Nan bu-n n chaa Baa-n ban lee.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: N buwaab, maa yin be ni ban nin yuuri. Ni na li bam, n yin bei ni le daandaan nan, ke maah nan bei Juu yaab man nin: Nan yaan wee n chaa-n, naa li fir dan niin ni.

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Ni ye dulinya nan yaab nan ni ke, ni li nyi ke bi nan chinn nan min le.

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Le bi gbaa baan tab ke, Waah ke ni kpiɛ wama ke nmɔn, ni taai ye baa? Taa biɛ nan wee u pag nmɔn ni.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: N sei ni nsiim pɔm le: Ni li jiɛ taba. Ni kpaa le ni li jiɛ tab ke maah jiɛ ni man nin.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Ni ye ke ni ye dulinya nan yaab ke, ki li jiɛ ni ke ki yaabin. Naah ye man nin, naa ye dulinya nan yaabi, amaa n nan gann ni ki nyan ni dulinya nan yaan na. Mambu le dulinya nan nan ni.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Le Yisa nan kan ke bi ban bin baan u le ni taai, le u bei bi ke, Ni baan tab le maah len ke Ni kpiɛ wama ke naa ji yin gami, le ni bɔɔn bu ke ni li kamin a taai yaa?.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Ni ye ke ni jiɛ tab ke, ni ma le li che uninfo biɛn biɛ ke ni ye n bɔɔnɔɔb le.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Yisa kaah nan len man nmɔn, ni bɔɔn bu le U yaar lig paab le ki miɛ Uwonbɔr ke: N baa, n-yonn nmɔn foo u. Che A bija n kan kupag wee A bija mu n pagiŋ.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Ni li tiɛr imɔbon yan wee n nan pag ki sei nin na: Utoontunn kaa yab jon u daau wi. Ni ye bi nan mugirim ke, bi li mugir ni mu. Ni ye bi chee sei n wɔɔm ke, bi li chee sei ni mu a wɔɔm.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: N bei ni ibamɔn le, ni li mɔɔ ki fabin amaa le ni mɔ dulinya yaab. Ni sufu li bii, amaa ni sufu biil nmɔn li kpann nmɔn.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita nan baan u ke, Uyudaan, a chaa laa? Le Yisa jiin ke, Nan yaan wee N chaa-n, ŋaa li fir nɔɔ daandaan nan ni, amaa ni ŋa wama ke a li nɔɔ.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Nan bu-n fin le sei u tininkper a pɔɔm le u jon bininfob biɛn ke un sei ban biɛn wee A ta sei win limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Bi yin ŋaa ni nan le n yinl bu, nan bu-n baa nyi Wan nan tunimi.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Upii be maa kibig ke ni wu u le nan bu-n u yonni li foo; amaa ni ye u ma u bigin ki diɛ ke u tamir nwunwun nin bu le ni mɔ u waah ma kibig ŋa dulinya nin bu.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Le Piita baan ke, Uyudaan, ba ŋa wee maa li fir nɔɔŋ daandaan nan naa? Ni ye nkum ke n li kpo a bu.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Daandaan nan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm nmɔn ye nan le: Ke bin biɛ fin wan ye ibamɔn a Wonbɔru win, nin Yisa Masiya, wan wee a tun nin.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Maa nan bi dan ki nan pag sei bi ke, baa bi li jɔɔ tibir a galin yi, amaa, daandaan nan, baa ji li fir ke baa nyi bi bir bu.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Man le ni ye ki sei ni: Daandaan nan le ye ni sufu biil a yonni, amaa n ji li kan ni le ni jɔɔ nmɔn, le uba kaa li fir ta ni mɔm nmɔn ki sin jiɛn ni.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Tɔ le Yisa jiin ke, A sin li kpo n buu? N beiŋ ibamɔn le, a li niɛ ke ŋaa nyimi itaa itaa, le ukooja nni mɔɔ!.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: N ŋmagin kupag nan dulinya-n le n nan seiŋ maah diɛ litoonl lan wee A nan ta seim ke n tun nin.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Wan nam ke u nan n Baa baantɔɔl le.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: Lidaal nmɔn nan foo ke naa ji na yin baanim niba. N bei ni ibamɔn le, n Baa li sei ni nan biɛn wee ni baan n yinlin bu.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: Le daandaan nan, n Baa, seim kupag A nintogil ni, kupag kan wee n nan kan A ban n-yonn man wee dulinya kaa la nan naan nin.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: Ni ye ke maa nan bi ŋa niwann nan wee uba kaa fir ŋa bi sisiigin ke, baa bi jɔɔ tibir a galin yi. Amaa daandaan nan bi nan kan ligaril a siɛrama, le ni biɛn yɔli bi nan min nin n Baa biɛn le.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: N kpiiriŋ ki wɔɔn bininfob ban wee A nan ta seim dulinya nan yaan nin na. Bi nan ye A yaab le; Fin le ta bi seim le bi chee A mɔleen.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Naah yuur ki nan kpaa daandaan nan naa la baan ni ba n yinl bu. Ni baan na wee ki gaa, wee ni mɔm nmann gbeen.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Amaa ni nan li ye nin gbeen nsiimin kaah nan sɔb man nin: Bi nam le laa jɔɔ bu.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: Maah le gbaa pag tenn ni pag woon a poolin nin, n-yonn chaan le maa ji na li pag ileen nmɔn a booli yi amaa n yin pag npaan nin le ki jiin n Baa-n paam.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Daandaan nan bi nyi ke niwann nan biɛn wee A ta seimin nyann Fin ban le.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: Utiter, wan wee n yin tun u wee un nyann Baa-n ban nin, ki ye ibamɔn a naaŋ kan nyann Baa-n ban nin dan ke, u li ji n siɛra.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Lidaal nmɔn ni na li baan naah ban nan n yinl bu. Maa ke min le na yin baan Baa-n ki sei ni.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Nan bu-n n ta imɔleen yan wee A ta seimin ki sei ba, le bi gaa i. Bi nyi ibamɔn ke n nyan Fin ban le, ki ŋa yada ke Fin le tunim.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Ni ye ni mu nji n siɛra le, nan bu-n ni be n ban le npiinimin na.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: Aai, Baa-n tɔɔl jiɛ ni nan bu-n naah jiɛm wee ki ŋaam yada ke n nyann Uwonbɔr ban nin bu.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: N miɛ le ki tenn bi. Maa miɛ tenn dulinya, amaa ban wee A ta bi seimin, nan bu-n bi ye A yaab le.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: N nyann Baa-n ban le ki kɔɔn dulinya ni; daandaan nan n nya dulinya ni le ki chaa jir Baa-n ban.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Maah jɔɔ ban ye A yaab le, le Ŋaah jɔɔ ban nin biɛn ye n yaab. Le n kan kupag bi ban.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin ke, Daandaan nan a pag ni ween le kaa ji pag liwool a poolin ni.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Maa ji yin be dulinya nan yaan ni, amaa bi ma la be dulinya nan yaan, min chaan A ban le. Baa wan be kasi, guur bi A yinlin a pɔɔm bu, liyinl lan wee A ta sei min bu, naah li ŋa man wee bin ŋa nba ke taah ye nba man nin.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Daandaan nan ti kan ke a nyi niwann biɛn hali kaa ji ban u ba n baaniŋ ibinbaan ni. Ni ma che ti ŋaaŋ yada ke a nyann Uwonbɔr ban le.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Maah la nan be bi ban nin, n nan guu bi le ki ŋmag bi A yinl lan wee A nan ta seim nmɔn a pɔɔm bu. Bi ni uba kaa bori see gab yɔli a ninfo win naah li ŋa man wee nin gbeen naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Le Yisa bei ke, Ni ŋa yada njoorimin le!.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: N chaan A ban le daandaan nan, amaa n pag ni niɛ nan n-yonn man wee n la be dulinya nan yaan ni le, naah li ŋa man wee bin gbee nin n mɔmin bi sufu ni.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Amaa n-yonn chaan, le ki pii foon na, le ni na li worin, ki kun ni niin yin uba ba uba ba. Ni biɛn na yin jiɛn nbinba le, ni biɛn yɔli maa be nbinba, nan bu-n n Baa be nim.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Min le ta A mɔleen yin ki sei bi le dulinya yaab nan bi, nan bu-n baa ji ye dulinya yaab jigin ke maah kaa ye dulinya a ninfou man nin.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: N bei ni ni niɛ nan, naah li ŋa man wee ni li jɔɔ nposɔɔn nni. Dulinya nan yaan ni li kan tibɔmugkaar. Amaa ni che ni sufu li pɔɔu! N nyaŋ dulinyau.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: N wonmiɛl kaa ye A nyan bi dulinya nan yaan ni, amaa n miɛ le ke A guur bi ki nyan bi tibir daan a nɔɔn.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Baa ye dulinya yaabi, hali a li ke maah kaa ye dulinya a ninfou man nin.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Ŋa bi kasi ibamɔn nin buu; A mɔleen yin ye ibamɔn le.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Ŋaah nan tum dulinya-n man nin, man le n tun bi dulinya-n.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yisa kaah nan nan miɛ Uwonbɔr ki diɛ-n, le uma nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan lɔŋ Kidironn a fataŋ. Lidool limiɛnin olif a siigi nan be niin, le uma nin u bɔɔnɔɔb nan tan kɔɔ niin.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: Bi ma bu le n ŋa nba kasi, ke ni che bi mu n ŋa kasi ibamɔn.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Le Judasi, wan nan kɔɔr Yisa nmu nan nyi bukpaab nmɔn yaan, nan bu-n Yisa nan ti gbaa lagin nin u bɔɔnɔɔb niin le.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: N wonmiɛl kaa tenn bi binba. N miɛ ki tenn ban mu la yin ŋaam yada bi mɔleen bu-n le.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Le Judasi nan dan isiig nmɔn yaan, ki taan bitɔtogib biba nin iwonjiɛn niin a guguub bibam, ban nan nyan biminmanib a yudam nin Farasiim ban nin. Bi nan ŋmag imumɔl le nin filaam nin tijawann.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: ke bi biɛn n ŋa nba; ke Ŋaah be nni wee n mu be A ni man nin. Che bi mu li be nin tima wee dulinya yaab n ŋa yada ke Fin le tunim ni.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Yisa, kaah nan nyi niwann nan biɛn li ŋa win, le u nan nyan sir ki too bi le ki baan ke, Ŋma wee ni ban naa.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: N ta kupag kan wee A nan ta seimin ki sei ba, ke nin che bin ŋa nba ke taah ye nba man nin:.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Le bi jiin ke, Yisa wan nyan Nazarif. Le Yisa ke, Min le yen.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: Le n li be nin bi wee A li be nim. Che bi li jɔɔ nmɔkpaa bamɔn na. Wee nin che dulinya yaab nbiɛ ke Fin le tunim le ki jiɛ bi hali ke ŋaah jiɛm man nin.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Yisa kaah nan ke, Min le yen nin, le bi nan sul jir bɔɔn ki lu lu tum.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: N Baa, n ban bininfob ban wee A ta seimin na li be n ban le maah na yin be nan yaan nin. Wee bin kan npagim, kupag kan wee A nan ta seimin, nan bu-n Ŋaah jiɛm n-yonn man wee dulinya kaa la nan naan nin.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Le Yisa ji nan baan bi ke, Ŋma wee ni ban naa? Le bi ke, Yisa wan nyan Nazarif.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: N Baa Wan ye tibuchinn daan, hali dulinya yaab kaah kaa le nyiŋin, min nyiŋ, le bi nyi ke Fin le tunim.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Le Yisa jiin ke, N bei ni ke min le yen. Ni ye ni ban min le ke, ni che bijab nan li chaau.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: N che bi biɛŋa, le ki li che bin mann biɛŋ naah li ŋa man wee Ŋaah jiɛm man nin li be nin bi wee n mu tɔɔl li be nin bi.

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: Ni niɛ nan ŋa ke nin che imɔbon yan wee u nan pagin gbeen le: Ban wee a nan ta seimin bi ni uba kaa bori.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita, wan nan ŋmag takoobi-n , dar fɔɔ le ki gaa biminmanib a yudaau a toontunnu a nɔɔjiim a tul ki par.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Le Yisa nan bei Piita ke, Ta a takoobi-n jiin kutɔɔgin na! N laa nyu kiyiig kan wee n Baa ta seimin yaan naa.

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Le bitɔtogib ban wee Judasi nan taan nin, nin bi sanliiru nin Juu yaab a ninkpeeb choo Yisa. Ki lol u.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: le ki nan chinn ta u dan Anasi ban, wan a chɔɔl nan ye Kayafasi nan, wee u nan ye biminmanib a yudaau libinl nmɔn nin.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Kayafasi nmɔn le pii nan kpaan Juu yaab ke, ni bi li ŋan ni ye uninfo uba bi kpo ki sei bininfob biɛn ke.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Simɔnn Piita nin ubɔɔnɔɔr uba nan nɔɔ Yisa. Nan bu-n biminmanib a yudaau win nan nyi ubɔɔnɔɔrin, le u nan nɔɔ Yisa ki kɔɔ biminmanib a yudaau win a niin a loob.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: amaa Piita ma nan paar si nkaafiin le ki guu. Le ubɔɔnɔɔr wan wee biminmanib a yudaau nan nyi-n, nan jirin ki nan pag sei kibisiɛg kan nan be itoon nin lidaal nmɔn nin, ki che Piita kɔɔn.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Le kibisiɛg kan nan be ntimansanmɔnn nin baan Piita ke, Ŋaa ye u bɔɔnɔɔru lee? Le Piita jiin ke, Maa ye.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Tiwar li nan be, le bitoontumin nin bininkpeebin nan si ki gɔɔn nŋmi man wee bi nan mann ki worin. Piita tɔɔl nan si ki kpiɛ bi bu le ki wor.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, le biminmanib a yudaau win nan baan Yisa ki jiin u bɔɔnɔɔbin nin u wɔɔnimin paam.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Le Yisa nan jiin ke, N pag npaan ni le ki sei dulinya, n gbaa wɔɔn tiwonjiɛn dirin bee iwonjiɛn niin le, nan yaan wee Juu yaab biɛn lagin tab. Maa la nyi pag niba ki woon ni.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Ba ŋa wee a baanim ibinbaan naa? Baan bininfob ban gbir maah pag man nin. Ibamɔn buu bi nyi maah len man.

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Yisa kaah len man nin, le bininkpeebin a ni uba wan mal win peb u kitapag ki ke, Ŋaah yin pag man ki sei biminmanib a yudaau le yen naa.

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, Ni ye n pag nan bir ke, a bin jim ni siɛra. Amaa ni ye n pag ibamɔn ke, ba ŋa wee a peb maa.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Le Anasi ta Yisa ki chiɛn biminmanib a yudaau Kayafasi ban ke iŋmin la lol u.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Simɔnn Piita kaah nan si wor nŋmi-n, le bi baan u ke, Ŋaa ye uja nan a bɔɔnɔɔbin a ni u ba lee? Le u niɛ, ki ke, Maa ye.

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Le biminmanib a yudaau a toontunnu wan a maalim ye Piita kaah gaa wan a tul ki parin, kpeen u ki ke, Maa bi kan fin nin uma olif a kpaabin yaan naa.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Le Piita ji niɛ, libol nmɔn le ukooja piin ki mɔr.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Le Juu yaab nyan Yisa Kayafasi ban ki ta u jon Rom yaab a bɔryudaau a naakoon. Ni nan ye njakpiin le, Juu yaab kaah nan yin ji yuglagir a chiigin nin; baa nan chee kɔɔ ubɔr a naakoon wee ki ŋa bi ba tijaginn ke bi kaal kaah wɔɔn man nni.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Ni bu le Palati nan nyann bi ban ki nan baan bi ke, Uja nan a galin ye ba wee ni jɔɔ galin waa.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Le bi jiin u ke, Ni ye waa bi ye wan galin ke, taa bi yin ta u ki nan seiŋi.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Le Palati ke, Ni ta u ni tɔɔl ki tan ji u tibɔr ni siim kaah wɔɔn man na. Le Juu yaabin jiin ke, Nsan kaa jaa sei ti nin ti ku uninfo.

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: Ni niɛ nan tun ke nin gbeen Yisa kaah nan pag ke waah na yin kpo nkum man a bosu nle.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Le Palati nan jir kɔɔ nnaakoon, ki tan yin Yisa ki baan u ke, Fin le ye Juu yaab a bɔru win naa.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Le Yisa jiin ki baan u ke, Ni ye a tɔɔl a potiɛl le bee biba le beiŋ n bɔraa.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Le Palati mu ji baan u ke, N ye Juu a ninfou lee, a tɔɔl a ninfob nin biminmanib le taaŋ seim. A tun baa.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, N naam kaa ye dulinya nan a naami, ni bi ye man ke, n toontum nan bi li kɔn n bu ke Juu yaab kaa li fir choomi. Amaa daandaan nan n naamin yin nyann niba paam le.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Le Palati ke, Tɔ a ye ubɔr le! Le Yisa jiin u ke, A jɔɔ ibamɔn nin ŋaah pag ke n ye ubɔr nmɔn. Ibamɔn buu, ni ma bu le bi mam, ni ma bu le n dan dulinya nan yaan, n nan ji ibamɔn a siɛra. Le ban be ibamɔn paamin pelig n bɔr.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Le Palati baan u ke, Ba ye ibamɔn naa? Imɔbon nan a bɔɔn bu le Palati ji jir nyan Juu yaabin ban ki tan bei bi ke, Maa kan nan bu wee n li galin wi.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Amaa ni ye ni kaal le ke n nyan uloondiikɔɔr uba ki sei ni Yuglagir a chiig a yonn. Ni ban n nyan Juu yaab a bɔru nle ki sei naa.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Le bi tiɛn ki jiin ke, Aai, naa ye uma! Nyan Barabasi ki sei ti! Barabasi nan ye ban bii tininkper a pɔɔmin a ni uba le.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Le Palati nan ta Yisa sei ki che bi fiɛb u.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Le bitɔtogib nan ta ikunkon ki loo kiyuchigirig ki ta chigin u yul. Le ki ta daala maŋ geuŋ ki yeen u.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: le ki le gbaa chaa u ban ki tan bei u ke, Ti poor Juu yaab a bɔru! Le ki peb u kitapag.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Le Palati ji nyan ki tan bei Juu yaabin ke, Lig, n yin ta u nyann le ki nan sei ni wee ni biɛ ke maa kan nan wee n li ta galin wi.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Yisa kaah nan nyann ki chig ikunkon a yuchigirig nin daala maŋin, le Palati bei bi ke, Uja nmɔn sɔ.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Libol nmɔn biminmanib a yudam nin ban guu iwonjiɛn niin nin kaah kan win, le bi tiɛn ki ke, Ni kpaa mu! Ni kpaa mu! Amaa le Palati jiin ki bei bi ke, Ni nan ta u ki tan kpaa mu. Min bu ke, maa kan nan wee n li ta galin wi.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Le Juu yaab le yii ki ke, Ti jɔɔ nsiim, le nsiim nmɔn wɔɔn ke ni ye un kpo le, nan bu-n u ke u ye Uwonbɔr a Bija le.

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Palati kaah gbir man nin, bijawaam nan mann choo u le.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: le u jir kɔɔ u niin. Le ki baan Yisa ke, A nyan la ban naa? Amaa Yisa kaa nan jiin u tibɔri.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Le Palati ke, A yii le kaa yin pag gar n paam maa? Ŋaa tiɛr ke n jɔɔ npɔɔn man li fir feeŋ jaa bee ki kpa ŋaa.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Le Yisa jiin ki bei u ke, Ŋaa bi li fir jɔɔ npɔɔn ki jon mi wee naa ye mi nyann paab le. Mambu wan taam ki ŋa a nɔɔn nin jɔɔ tibir takpem.

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, le Palati nin gbaar ban waah li ŋa man ki fee Yisa jaa, le Juu yaab mu le mann tann ki ke, A jaa uja nan jon ke, ŋaa ji ye Siiza a jɔ. Wan bei ke u ye ubɔr ke udaan yii Siiza le nmɔn.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Palati kaah gbir man nin, le u ta Yisa nyann ki nan kal ubɔjir a binbinl bu nan yaan wee bi yi ke kutangbapiig a loobin .

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: Ni nan ye sili a daal le, ni wonn wee bin ji Yuglagir a chiig a nyantoom le, tikur piig nin tilee a yonn. Le Palati nan bei Juu yaabin ke, Ni bɔru sɔ.

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Amaa le bi tiɛn ki ke, Ta u fɔra! Ta u fɔra! Kpaa mu! Le Palati baan bi ke, N kpaa ni bɔru waa? Le biminmanib a yudam bei ke, Taa jɔɔ ubɔri see Siiza a binba.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Ni joorimin le Palati nan ta u sei bi ke bin tan kpa mu. Le bitɔtogibin nan gaa Yisa.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: Le ki che Yisa tu u tɔɔl a dapunpunl, ki nyan jon nan yaan wee bi yi ke kuyukpabugin .

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Niin le bi nan kpa u, le ki kpaa bijab bilee mu ki kpiɛ u bu, uba be nan bu a logil le utɔ mu be nan bu a logil le Yisa be sisiigin.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Le Palati nan che bi sɔb nsɔbim ki tabin lidapunpunlin bu. Nsɔbim nmɔn nan karin nan le, YISA WAN NYAN NAZARIF, KI YE JUU YAAB A BƆRU WIN.

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Juu yaab takpem le nan karin nsɔbim nmɔn. Nan yaan wee bi nan kpaa Yisa nmɔn nan mal kitiŋ poolin le, nsɔbimin nan sɔb Aramayig nin Latinn nin Girig a leen yin le.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Le biminmanib a yudam kaa chee Palati bu ki ke, Laa sɔb ke Juu yaab a bɔru wi, amaa sɔb ke uja nan le ke u ye Juu yaab a bɔru win.

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Le Palati nan jiin ki bei bi ke, Maah sɔb man nin, n pii sɔba.

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Bitɔtogibin kaah nan kpaa Yisa n-yonn man nin, le bi por u wann yɔɔr nfimina bu, ki sei tab uba ba, ki nin gur u daala big kan nan be poolin nin. Daala big nmɔn kaa nan jɔɔ nŋalimi, bi nan ta litanmunl liba le ki loo ki nyan paab ki tan kpaa tum.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Le bitɔtogibin bei tab ke, Ni laa che ti choo chiɛ. Ni che ti tɔ inaa ki lig wan li ji daala nmɔn. Ni niɛ nan nan ŋa ke nin gbeen nan nan sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Bi por n daalam ki sei tab le ki tɔn inaa n wann yeewann bu. Man le bitɔtogibin nan ŋa.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Le Yisa a na, nin u nawa, nin Meri Kolopasi a poo, nin Meri wan nyan Magidalin nan si mal Yisa a dapunpunlin.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Yisa kaah nan lig kan u na niin nin, nin ubɔɔnɔɔr wan wee u jiɛ-n kaah si ki malin, le u bei u na ke, Upii nan, a bija sɔ.

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: le ki bei ubɔɔnɔɔrin ke, A na sɔ. Iyaayoo le ubɔɔnɔɔr nmɔn nan taa Meri ki kun u niin.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, Yisa kaah nan nan biɛ ke niwann biɛn diɛ, ki li gbeen naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin, le u len ke, Nnyunyum le jɔɔm.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Lidamunmɔŋil nan si kiyiigin niin, le bi ta itakpiinduuŋ a faar ki lon niin, le ki ta sagin kudaag ki yuur ki paab ki ta tan ŋa Yisa a mɔnn.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Yisa kaah nan gaa nyun nin, le ki bei ke, Ni diɛu. Le ki tibin u yul tum le ki kpo.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Ni nan ye sili a ŋa daal le, kutaag kan nan yin wonn paan nin nan ye likpakɔɔl gannkaal le. Juu yaab kaah kaa nan ban tigbanann nin li be lidapunpunl bu-n, le bi nan bei Palati ke un che bin genn bi taai wee ki lagir bi jiin nin.
