eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Mambu le bitɔtogibin nan jon ki tan gen uja uba wan wee bi nan chinn kpa u lidapunpunl bu nin Yisa-n a taai le ki ji gen wan gurin a taai.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Amaa baah nan nan foon Yisa ban ki miin u pii kpe-n baa ji nan gen u taai yi.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Le bitɔtogibin a ni uba ta likpanl ki ŋu Yisa a logil, libol nmɔn le ncham nin nnyum nyann.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Uja wan nan kan man nmɔn nan jin siɛra le, u siɛra nmɔn nan ye ibamɔn le. U nyi ke u pag ibamɔn le, u jin siɛra ke ni mu n ŋa yada le.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Ni niɛ nan ŋa ke nin gbeen naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: U kpabil liba kaa yin gen ni.

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: ke Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ni ba yaan kaah len ke, Bi li lig wan wee bi nan ŋuu-n.

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le Josef wan nan nyan Arimatiya n baan Palati Yisa a gbanann bu, Josef nan ye Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔru le, u nan woog le waah nan san Juu yaabin bu. Le Palati sei u nsan le u tan nyan Yisa a gbanann ki ta jon.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Nikodimosi le nan chiɛn u, uja wan pii nan jon Yisa ban kunyiɛgin. Nikodimosi nan ta kisiig a nyinyinn nin nnyɔgkusim nle ki ŋmarin tab, mi kpikpiɛl nan foo botu munla.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Le bininfob bilee nmɔn nan ta tiwann nunumir nmɔn ki nug Yisa a gbanann ki ta ti pɔbin litanl penlin. Bi nan ŋa Juu yaab a kaalin baah ŋaan man ki pii bi kumin le.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Nan yaan wee bi nan kpa Yisa-n nan ye bukpaab le, bukpaab nmɔn yaan kukaapɔŋ kan wee baa la nyi pii uninfo nan be niin.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Nan bu-n baah nan be ŋaan sili nin wonn likpakɔɔlin wee libol mu kaa ji nan fɔgin bu le bi nan pii Yisa nin yaan.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Nyantoom a chinchinn a daal a jakpanyiɛn, wee ni la nan bɔɔn nin, le Meri wan nyan Magidalin nan jon kukaagin yaan ki tan foo ki miin litangbagil lan nan loo kukaagin a mɔbin choo loora.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Le u nan guun dan Simɔnn Piita nin ubɔɔnɔɔr wan wee Yisa nan jiɛ-n ban, ki bei bi ke, Bi nyan Uyudaan kukaagin a poolin na, taa nyi baah ta u ŋa nan yaan ni.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Ni ma bu le Piita nin ubɔɔnɔɔr umiɛnin piin lisanchonl ki chaa kukaagin yaan.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Bi bilee nmɔn biɛn nan gu le, amaa ubɔɔnɔɔr umiɛnin nan guun ki jon jiɛn Piita le ki nan chinn foo kukaagin yaan.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: Le ki bɔɔn ki lig poolin ki kan litanl penlin kaah dɔ niin amaa le kaa kɔɔ poolin ni.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita, wan nan paa u bɔɔn nin, foon ki nan jon kɔɔ kukaagin a poolin. U nan kan litanl penlin kaah dɔ man na.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: ki kpiɛ nkum a kinkenl lan nan lol Yisa a yulin. Likinkenl nmɔn nan kpab li tɔɔl le, ki dɔ li binba ki jiɛn litanl penlin.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Ni joorim ubɔɔnɔɔr umiɛnin, wan nan chinn foo kukaagin yaan nin, mu kɔɔ poolin. Le ki kan ki ŋa yada.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Le bibɔɔnɔɔbin nan jir kun bi niin yin.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Amaa Meri nan si kukaagin a paab le ki mɔr. Waah nan si ki mɔrin, le ki nan bɔɔn ki lig kukaagin a poolin.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: le ki kan malakam bilee kaah yee tiwann penn, ki ka nan yaan wee Yisa a gbanann nan dɔ-n, le uba ka u yul paam, le uba mu ka u taai paam.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Le malakam nmɔn baan u ke, Upii ba ŋa wee a mɔraa? Le u ke, Bi yuur n yudaau a gbanann le, maa nyi baah ta u ŋa nan yaan ni.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: Nin yaan nmɔn, le u gbiɛ lig ki kan Yisa kaah si niin, amaa waa nan biɛ ke Yisa le yen ni.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Le Yisa baan u ke, Upii ba ŋa wee a mɔraa? Ŋma wee a ban naa? Meri wan nyan Magidalin ma nan tiɛr ke ukpaadaan ni le yen, le ki ke, Uninkpel, ni ye a ta u tan ŋa nan yaan ke, a wɔɔnim ŋaah ta U ŋa nan yaan nmɔn ni, wee n jo ki tan yuur u.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Le Yisa yin u ke, Meri. Le Meri gbiɛ lig U paam ki kuun nkukɔpɔɔn Juu yaab a leen yin Aramayig ke, Rabboni .

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Laa bibim ki ŋmag mi, nan bu-n maa la jir Baa-n ban ni. Amaa li chaa n nabumin ban ki tan bei bi ke, N chaa jir N Baa nin ni Baa, N Wonbɔru nin ni Wonbɔru win ban le. .

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Le Meri wan nyan Magidalin nan ta tibɔr nmɔn ki jon bibɔɔnɔɔbin ban ki tan bei bi ke, N kan Uyudaan na! Le ki bei bi waah bi ke un bei bi man nin.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Nnyantoon a chinchinn a weem a daal a jɔɔg, le bi bɔɔnɔɔbin nan lagin be kudiig kiba a poolin, bi nan kpal inaangan le baah nan san Juu yaab bijawaamin bu, le Yisa dan ki nan sir bi sisiigin ki ke, Nposɔɔn li be nin ni.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: Waah nan len man nmɔn a bɔɔn, le u nan ta U nɔɔi nin U logi ki wɔɔn bi. Ni nan mɔŋ bibɔɔnɔɔbin le takpem baah nan kan Uyudaan nin.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Le Yisa ji nan bei bi ke, Nposɔɔn li be nin ni! N Baa kaah nan tum man nin, man le n mu tun ni.

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Le ki nan foor ki jaa bi bu ki ke, Ni gaam Kasi a Naaŋ.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Ni ye ni jiɛn ban biɛn a bir ki per bi ke, bi kan jiɛn pera; ni ye naa jiɛn per bi ke, baa kan jiɛn peri.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Tomasi , ki ye bibɔɔnɔɔbin a ni u baa-n kaa nan be bi ban Yisa kaah nan dan nin ni.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Le bibɔɔnɔɔb bimiɛn nin nan bei u ke, Ti kan Uyudaan na! Amaa le u nan bei bi ke, See ke n kan ikpaagin a bon U nɔɔi ni wee ki ta n nɔɔbil ki bɔ nan yaan wee ikpaagin nan kpaa-n, ki ta n nɔɔ ki bɔ U logi yin, naa ye man ke maa yin ŋa yada.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Iween ilile a bɔɔn U bɔɔnɔɔb ji nan be iniin le, le Tomasi nan be bi ban. Hali inaangan nan kpal le, amaa le Yisa kɔɔn ki nan sir bi sisiigin ki bei bi ke, Nposɔɔn li be nin ni.

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Le ki bei Tomasi ke, Ta a nɔɔbil ki nan bɔ doou; lig n nɔɔi. Tann a nɔɔ ki bɔ n logi a boi nan yaan na ki jiɛn nbiɛnim wee ki ŋa yada.

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Le Tomasi bei u ke, N yudaau nin n Wonbɔru.

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Le Yisa bei u ke, Nan bu-n ŋaah kamin bu, le a ŋa yada; tikaar be ban kaa kan wee ki sin ŋa yada-n bu.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yisa nan tun ligaril a siɛra ibam takpem u bɔɔnɔɔb a numin, le naa sɔb dɔ kugbɔŋ nan a poolin ni.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Amaa nsɔbim nan ye ni ŋa mi yada le ke Yisa le ye Masiya, Uwonbɔr a Bija, le ni ŋa u yada ke ni li kan limiɛfol u yinlin.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, Yisa ji nan degir u bɔɔnɔɔb bu Tiberiya a mɔgilin. Naah nan tun man sɔ:.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Simɔnn Piita, nin Tomasi , nin Nataniɛl , nin Zebedi a naachimin, ki kpiɛ Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb bilee bi biɛn nan lagin be le.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita bei bi ke, N chaa jan chool le. Le bi jiin bei u ke, Ti li nɔɔŋ jo. Le bi nyan ki kɔɔ kubulunkpoŋin, amaa kunyiɛmuŋ nmɔn biɛn baa nan choo niba.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Le njakpanyiɛn, le Yisa sir nnyum a gbaam, amaa bibɔɔnɔɔbin kaa nan biɛ ke Yisa le yen ni.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Le u baan bi ke, N jɔtiib, naa jɔɔ ijan naa? Le bi jiin u ke, Aai.

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni ta ni janlaaŋin ki jaa kubulunkpoŋin a jiim paama, wee ki choo ijan. Baah ta bi janlaaŋin ki jaa nnyumin nin le kaa ji li fir daar ki kɔɔn kubulunkpoŋin yaan ni, nan bu-n bi nan choo ijan le takpem.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Le ubɔɔnɔɔr wan wee Yisa jiɛ-n bei Piita ke, Uyudaan le yen! Piita kaah gbir ke Uyudaan le yen nin, le u yuur u kinkenl gab le ki yug lu nnyumin.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Le bibɔɔnɔɔb bi miɛnin ta kubulunkpoŋ ki ŋɔɔr u taai, ki dar kujanlaaŋin ke ki gbee nin ijan, nan bu-n bi bol kaa nan fɔg nin likpengonli, ni nan ye itaadann kobig paam le.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Baah nan don likpengonlin, le bi kan iŋmisakɔɔn a ŋmiim le ijan pul niin, nin boroboro.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni li jɔɔn ijan yan wee ni choo-n iba ni.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Le Simɔnn Piita don kubulunkpoŋin a poolin ki dar kujanlaaŋin donn likpengonl. Ki nan gbee nin ijan gbingbem le, i biɛn nan lagin ye ijan kobig nin piŋmu nin itaa le, amaa hali nin yaah le nan yab man nmɔn biɛn na kujanlaaŋin kaa nan chiɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Le Yisa bei bi ke, Ni dan nan chann ni mɔb. U bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn a ni uba kaa nan baan u ke, A ye ŋmiɛ? Bi nan nyi waah ye Uyudaan.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Le Yisa dan, ki nan yuur boroboro ki ta sei bi, ki ji yuur ijan nin mu ki sei bi.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Ni niɛ nan le nan kpiɛ nfimitaa le Yisa degir u baa u bɔɔnɔɔb ban, waah nan fii nkumin a bɔɔn.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Baah nan jin diɛ-n, le Yisa baan Simɔnn Piita ke, Simɔnn Jɔnn a bija, a jiɛm ibamɔn ki jon bi niɛ waa? Le u jiin ki bei u ke, Nn n Yudaau, a nyi ke n jiɛŋ. Le Yisa ke, Li jinn n peebum nan na.

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Le Yisa ji len ke, Simɔnn Jɔnn a bija, a jiɛm ibamɔn naa? Le u jiin ke, Nn, n Yudaau, a nyi ke n jiɛŋ. Le Yisa len ke, Li guu n peei nan na.

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Nan nan kpiɛ nfimitaa-n le u bei u ke, Simɔnn Jɔnn a bija, a jiɛm maa? Piita a sufu nan bii le nan bu-n Yisa kaah nan baan u ki tan kpaa nfimitaa ke, A jiɛ maa? Le u ke, Uyudaan, a nyi niwann biɛn; a nyi ke n jiɛŋ. Le Yisa ke, Li jinn n peei nan na.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: N beiŋ ibamɔn le, ŋaah nan pɔɔn nin, a ti yee a wan yɔɔr a tɔɔl le ki jo ŋaah ban nan paam; amaa ni ye a por ke a yin tann a nɔɔi le, le uba n ta a wann yɔɔr ki yeeŋ ki taaŋ jo ŋaah kaa ban a jo nan paam.

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Yisa nan len man ki wɔɔn nkum man a bol wee Piita yin kpo wee ki ta kupag sei Uwonbɔr nle. Le ki bei u ke, Li nɔɔm ni.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Le Piita gbiɛ lig ki miin ubɔɔnɔɔr wan wee Yisa jiɛ-n nɔɔ bi le.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Piita kaah kan win, le u baan u ke, Uyudaan, ke u ma.

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Le Yisa jiin u ke, Ni ye n ban u li be nin u miɛfol ki nan kpaa n-yonn man wee n li jirin ke, ni ye ba ki sei ŋaa? Ni kpaa le nin a nɔɔm.

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu, le tibɔr nan mɔɔn u bɔɔnɔɔbin a sisiigin ke ubɔɔnɔɔr uniɛ ma kaa yin kpo. Amaa Yisa kaa ke waa yin kpo; u len le ke, Ni ye n ban u li be nin u miɛfol ki nan kpaa lidaal lan wee n na li jirin ke, ni ye ba ki sei ŋaa.

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Ubɔɔnɔɔr nmɔn le jin tiwann nan a siɛra le ki sɔb ni bil. Ti nyi ke u siɛra nmɔn ye ibamɔn le.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Daandaan nan, Yisa ji nan tun tiwann takpem tibama, ni ye ni biɛn bi sɔb dɔ ke, n tiɛr ke hali dulinya munl nan kaa bi li kan npaan man wee bi li ta tigbann tan wee bin sɔb nan ŋa.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Kuchiig kan wee bi yi ki ke Pentikosi a daal kaah nan nan foo-n, bi nan lagin be bogiliba le.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Libol nmɔn le kufuug nyan paab, ki fu ke ntafapɔɔrim le daarin. Ki jiirin nan gbeen kudiig kan wee bi ka-n.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Le bi kan ni ba le ni nann nŋmipeen a dann ki yar tɔɔ tɔɔ bininfob biɛn bu.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Le bi biɛn nan gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ ki piin ki pag ileen pɔɔn kinaaŋin a pɔɔmin.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Juu yaab ban nan san Uwonbɔr nan be Jerusalam iyaayoo nmɔn, bi nan nyann dulinya nan a timin biɛn le.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Baah nan gbir kufuug n-yonn man ni, le kuninfogbaluŋ lagin niin, le ni ŋa bi nmɔn, nan bu-n bi uba ba nan gbir biwonjiɛmin kaah pag u bol a leen le.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Le ni ŋa bi ligaril, le bi baan tab ke: Bininfob ban pag nan biɛn kaa ye Galilii yaab lee?.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Ke ni ŋa nii wee ti biɛn ji gbir bi pag ti boli a leen yaa?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Ti ni biba nyan Patiya, nin Medisi nin Elam; nin Mesopotamiya, nin Judiya, nin Kapadosiya, nin Pontusi nin Asiya.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: Ferijiya nin Pamfiliya, Ijibir nin Libiya a yagil lan mal Sarene a tiŋin; le tim bibam mu nyan Rom.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: le biba mu ji nyan Kereti nin Arabiya, le ti biɛn gbir baah pag ti boli a leen yin, ki donn Uwonbɔr a yinl nin lijinjeel a toon yan wee U tun nin.

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Le ni ŋa bi ligaril nin lipotiɛl, le bi nin baan tab ke, Ni niɛ nan a taai ye baa.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Le biba mu laa bi, ki ke, Bi nyun ndaam le ki gbee.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Le Piita nin u tab piig nin uba-n fii ki sir kuninfogbaluŋin a nintogil, le u pag nniɛn pɔɔn ke: N nabum Juu yaab, nin nim ban biɛn be Jerusalam nan, ni che n pag tibɔr nan wɔɔn ni; ni pel nbamɔma wee ki gbir maah yin pag man nmɔn.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Bijab nan kaa ye bidanyugbeeb ke naah tiɛr man nni. Ni la ye njakpanyiɛn a kur tiwiɛ le!.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Aai, ulinlenn Jowel pii nan pag ni nan buu:.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: Uwonbɔr nan len ke, dulinya nan a joorimin, n na li kpaar n naaŋin ki jaa bininfob biɛn bu. Le ni bijam nin ni bisiɛm na li pag ilinlen leen, le ni naachipɔm na li kan zii, le ni janinkpeeb mu na li dɔgin tidindɔgin.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn hali ban ye n toontum, bijab nin bipiib biɛn, n na li kpaar n naaŋin ki jaa bi bu le bin pag ilinlen leen.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: N na li tun lijinjeel a toon paab, nin ligaril a siɛra dulinya nan yaan. Ncham nin nŋmi nin kukpunyibɔŋ.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Nween na li kpenn ki bɔŋ, le uŋmal mu li kpenn ki mann ke nchamin, ni na yin ŋa man le, wee Uyudaan a pag takpeŋin daalin ni foon.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Le wan biɛn yin Uyudaan a yinl ke u li kan nŋmarim.

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Le Piita ji mann len ke, Iziral yaab, ni gbirim nan wee n yin pag bei ni ki jiin Yisa wan ye Nazarif a ninfou npaam, ni tɔɔl nyi ke Uwonbɔr le nan che u tun ligaril nin lijinjeel a toon nin siɛra ni sisiigin.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Uwonbɔr li nan ta uja nan ŋa ni nɔɔn, ki pii nan bon ki nyi nan na yin ŋa; le ni che tibirdam kpa u lidapunpunl bu le u kpo.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Amaa, le Uwonbɔr fiin u nkumin ki nyan u nkum a nan wii ni, nan bu-n nkum kaa jɔɔ npɔɔn man li fir ŋmag wi.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Defid nan pag Yisa a bɔr ke, N kan Uyudaan kaah be nim iyoo biɛn. Nan bu-n U be n nɔɔjiim paam, Le maa ji yin be limugilin ni.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Mambu ni mɔm n sufu ni, le n pag npopeen a bɔr; n gbanann li be nin tama.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: nan bu-n ŋaa yin che n paar kukaagin ni, kaa ji yin che a kasi daau a gbanann n buri.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: A wɔɔm limiɛfol a nsan ma; Ki che ni mɔm ŋaah be n ban ni bu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: Le Piita ji mann len ke, N na a buwaab n li fir pag nin tidindann ke ti yaaja ubɔr Defid kpo le bi pii u, ke u kaag la be nin dinan.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Amaa waah nan ye ulinlenn nin u nyi Uwonbɔr kaah siin limɔsiinl ki sei u, ke u na li che u yaabil uba nji nnaam ki kal ubɔr gbann ni bu.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Defid kaah pii nan kan nan yin tun nin bu, le u nan pag Masiya a kum a fiim bu, ki ke Masiya nmɔn kaa na yin paar kitakpiin tiŋi, le u gbanann mu kaa na yin buri.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Yisa nmɔn le Uwonbɔr nan fiin u nkumin, le ti biɛn mu le ye u kum a fiim a siɛradam.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Le Uwonbɔr ji ta u kaan U nɔɔjiim paam. Waah kal n-yonn man nin, le u gaa Kasi a Naaŋ kan wee Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinl ke U na li sei win, Kasi a Naaŋ nmɔn le u ta sei ti, le ti gbir ki kan.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Defid ma kaa nan don paabi, amaa le ki ke, Uyudaan Uwonbɔr nan bei n Yudaau ke, kalim n nɔɔjiim paam.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: hali nin maah na li che a dataam n kpann a taai a tɔɔnbol.

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Mambu ni che Iziral yaab biɛn n biɛ ni niɛ: Yisa wan wee ni nan kpa u lidapunpunl bu-n, U ma le Uwonbɔr ŋa u Uyudaan nin Masiya.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Bininfobin kaah nan gbir man ni, le bi sufu nan bii takpem, le bi baan Piita nin u tooi tabin ke, Ti na a buwaab ti yin ŋa nii.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Le Piita bei bi ke, Ni jiɛn ni toonbir a tuma, wee ti fu ni biɛn nan Uwonbɔr a nyunful, Yisa Masiya a yinl bu, wee Uwonbɔr njiɛn ni bir ki per ni, ki sei ni ipiin yan ye Kasi a Naaŋin.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Nan bu-n U siin limɔsiinlin sei nim nin ni buwaab, nin ban a tim fɔg, nin ban wee ti Yudaau Uwonbɔr na li yin nin.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Le Piita sei bi ikpaan takpem, le ki ji balin bi ke, Ni fee niba gaba wee ki nyan dulinya nan a ŋmaruŋ nan yaan na.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Ban nan chee u bɔr nmɔn ni le bi nan fun bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful. Ban nan kpiɛ bi bu lidaal nmɔn nan foo bininfob turita paama.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Le bi nan ta bi saŋi ki tinn itoon nmɔn a wɔɔm ki jɔɔ nɔɔ nin tab, ki lagin ji tijiir, ki lagin tab ki miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Le bijawaam nan choo bi biɛn, nan bu-n itoon nin nan tun lijinjeel a toon le nin ligaril a siɛra.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Le biwonjiɛm nmɔn biɛn nan jɔɔ nɔɔ nin tab le ki jɔɔ bi wan yɔɔr ki por tenn tab.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Ki kɔɔr baah jɔɔ nan biɛn ki por ni ligi sei tab, uninfo biɛn nin waah ye un gaa man, wee nin kpaan u.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Lidaal biɛn bi lagin iwonjiɛn niin a loob le ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr, ki lagin tab bi niin ki ji tijiir, ki jɔɔ nmɔn nin ibamɔn nin tab.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: ki pag ki don Uwonbɔr a yinl, le uninfo biɛn a sufu sɔɔn bi bu. Le idaa biɛn ke Uyudaan che bininfob mann ga nŋmarim kpiɛg bi bu.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Weemba a ween tikur titaa a yoo le Piita nin Jɔnn nan chaa iwonjiɛn niin liwonmiɛl a yoo.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Le bi nan kan baah tu uja wan pii nan wɔb u maalim nin ki tan bil iwonjiɛn niin a timansanmɔnn man wee bi yi ntimansanmɔnn ŋan nin. Bi nan tug u tan bilig man le iweehn biɛn, ke u li miɛ ilig ban chaan iwonjiɛn niin a loobin ban.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: N-yonn man wee u nan kan Piita nin Jɔnn wee bi yin kɔɔn, le u miɛ bi ilig.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Le Piita nin Jɔnn lig u chimm, le Piita nan bei u ke Lig ta.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Le u ni mir lig bi, ki nyi ke u yin kan niba le bi ban.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Amaa le Piita bei u ke, Maa jɔɔ iligi, amaa maah jɔɔ nan le n yin ta seiŋ nan. Yisa Masiya wan nyan Nazarif nan a yinl bu, fii choon na.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Le ki choo u nɔɔjiim ki fiin u, libol nmɔn le uja-n a taafar nin u talangbin pɔɔg.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: Le u yug sir u taai bu, le ki piin ki choon jin. Le ki nɔɔ bi kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin ni, ki choon ki yugir ki mabir, ki pag don Uwonbɔr.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Bininfobin biɛn kaah nan kan waah choon ki pagir Uwonbɔr man nin.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: le bi biɛ ke uma ye uja wan nan ka iwonjiɛn niin n-yaa timansanmɔnn ŋan n-yaan ki miɛ-n, le ni ŋaa bi lijinjeel nin ligaril naah ŋa wee u nan chonn.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Umiɛr nmɔn kaah la nan ŋmag Piita nin Jɔnn nin, le ni ŋa bininfobin biɛn ligaril, le bi guun lagin bi ban, baah nan lagin tab nan yaan nmɔn le bi yi ke Solomɔnn a dɔŋin.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Piita kaah kan bininfobin n-yonn man nin, le u bei bi ke: Nim Iziral yaab, ba ŋa wee ni gar ni uninfo nan buu? Ba ŋa wee ni lig ti, a li ke tim a pɔɔm bee ti binben ŋam bu le che kuwɔbig nmɔn fii ki choon naa?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: Uwonbɔr wan nan ye Abiraham nin Azig nin Jekɔb nin ti yaajam a Wonbɔru nan, U ma ye wan don U toon tun, Yisa le ni ta u ki ŋubin bi yudam a nɔɔn ke bin ku u, le Palati nan bigin un jaa u, le ni ji yii u Palati a nintogil.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Ni yii Kasi daan nin Ubuchin daan le ki bei Palati ke un nyan uninfo kurin ki sei ni.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Nim le ku limiɛfol daan nmɔn, amaa le Uwonbɔr fiin u nkumin. Le tim le ye ni siɛradam.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Yada nin Yisa a yinl bu le uja wan wee ni kan ki nyi u nan pɔɔg ki fin choon, waah ŋa Yisa a yinlin yada n bu. Yada kan wee u jɔɔn le che u kan kutebug ni biɛn a numin.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Tɔ, n nabum, n nyi ke nim nin ni sanliibin biɛn nan tun nan wee naa nyi le.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Amaa ni ye nan wee Uwonbɔr nan tun ki gbeen nan wee u nan chinn che u linlemin biɛn pag ke U Masiya nan li ji fala-n.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Mambu, ni kpenn ni binbema ki dan Uwonbɔr ban, wee un ŋunn ni toonbir, ni ŋa man na wee npɔŋim a yonn nnya Uyudaan ban ki foon.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: U li tun Masiya ni ban, wan wee u pii nan gann u ki sei ni nan, u ma ye Yisa.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Yisa nmɔn la yin be Uwonbɔr a niin le, hali nin iyoo kaah na li foon, le Uwonbɔr n mann tiwann biɛn, ke waah nan che U kasi a linlem pag iyoo yan nan jon nin.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Nan bu-n Moziz len ke, Uyudaan wan ye ni Uwonbɔr win li nyan ulinlenn uba wan be ke min nan ni tɔɔl a bolin; ki sei ni, ni pelim nan biɛn wee u li pag ki sei ni nan.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Le uninfo wan kaa pelig u bɔr ke, Uwonbɔr li nyan udaan u ninfobin.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Ibamɔn, ulinlenn Samɔɔl, nin bilinlem ban ŋɔɔrin u bɔɔn, nan biɛn nan pag nan tun daandaan nan na.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Nim le ye bilinlem nmɔn a naachim nin tipoor tan wee Uwonbɔr nan poo sei ni yaajamin. U nan bei Abiraham ke, A yaabil uba bu tikaar npii dulinya nan yaab biɛn.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah nan gann U toon tun wi, u nan chinn tun u ni ban le, ke un kpenn ni pootom a binbem wee u kaar n nin fir pii ni.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Piita nin Jɔnn kaah la nan si ki pag tenn bininfobin ni, le biminmanib nin ban guu iwonjiɛn niin a ninkpelu nin Sadusi yaab dan bi ban.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Bi ŋuui nan fii itoon ni bu le takpem baah wɔɔn bininfobin ke, Yisa nan fii nkumin ni.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Le bi nan choo bi, naah nan ye kujɔɔg n bu le bi nan kɔɔn bi kuloondiigin ke ni wonn wee.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Amaa le bininfob takpem ban gbir Piita a bɔr nmɔn nan ŋa yada, le biwonjiɛm a kanl nan fii ke turiŋmu paam.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Naah nan won kutaag, le Juu yaab a yudam nin bininkpeeb nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim nan lagin tab Jerusalam.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Ban nan lagin nmɔn yaan biminmanib a ninkpelu Anasi nan be niin, nin Kayafasi, nin Jɔnn nin Alɛgzanda nin kumanug a yudaau nmɔn a niin yaab bibam.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Le ki che bi ta Piita nin Jɔnn dan bi ban, le bi baan bi ibinbaan ke, Npɔɔn mi la ba bee ŋma a yinl wee ni ta fiin kiwɔbim nan naa.

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Le Piita gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ le ki bei bi ke: Nim Juu yaab a yudam nin bininkpeeb!.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: Ni ye ni yin ti dinan ki nan ji ti tibɔr ki jiin litoonl ŋanl lan wee ti tun sei kiwɔbig nan, ki baan ti taah ŋa man wee u nan kan kutebug ke.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: ni ŋan nin nim Iziral yaab biɛn mu biɛ ke: Yisa Masiya wan ye Nazarif a ninfou win a yinl bu, le uja wan si ni numin nan kan kutebug. Yisa nmɔn le ni nan che bi kpaa u lidapunpunl bu-n, amaa le Uwonbɔr fiin u nkumin.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: U ma le Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ pag u bu ke, Kudaag kan wee nim bitanmaab yiin, ki ma nan yin ye kijaantelig ŋaŋ.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Nŋmarim kaa be u ba ban ni, le liyinl liba le kaa be dulinya nan yaan wee Uwonbɔr ta sei bininfob ke bin yin ki kan nŋmarimi, see Yisa a binba.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Biyudam nmɔn kaah nan kan Piita nin Jɔnn kaah jɔɔ sufu pɔɔril man nmɔn, ki biɛ ke bi ye bininfob ban kaa jon ntinnim nbamɔmi, ni nan gar bi le, le bi biɛ ke bijab nan nyi be nin Yisa u.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Amaa baah nan kan uja wan kan kutebugin si Piita nin Jɔnn ban nin, le baa ji kan bimɔb ban li pag ki bii bi.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Le bi nan che bi nyan Juu yaab a yudam a laguŋ a diig nmɔn yaan, amaa le bi paar ki gbeer tab.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: Le ki baan tab ke, Ti yin ŋa bijab nmɔn nii? Bininfob ban biɛn be Jerusalam nan pii nyi ligaril a toon yan wee bi tun, mambu ni che taa ji li fir niɛ bi.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Amaa taah li ŋa man wee tibɔr nan laa mɔɔn ki foo bininfob biɛn ye nan le, ni che ti kpaan bi, wee bi laa ji yi Yisa a yinl ki tenn bininfobi.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Mambu le bi yin bi kɔɔ ndiin le ki bei bi ke, bi li nyi bin ta Yisa a yinl ki pag bee ki wɔɔn uba.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Amaa le Piita nin Jɔnn jiin bi ke, Ni ŋan nin Uwonbɔr ban nin ti chee ni mɔb bee Uwonbɔr aa, ni jiin sei ni bau.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Nan bu-n tim kaa li fir jiɛn ki laa pag nan wee ti kan ki gbir n bu.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Baah nan kɔɔn bi bijawaam takpem a bɔɔn, le bi jaa bi ke bi li chaau, baa nan kan nsan man bu wee bi li nɔɔ ki dar bi tuli yi, nan bu-n bininfobin biɛn nan pagir Uwonbɔr le, ligaril a toon lan tun nin bu.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Uja wan nan kan ligaril a tebug nmɔn nan por jon ibin pinau.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Baah jaa Piita nin Jɔnn ni, le bi jir kun bitab ban, ki tan bei bi kumanug a yudam nin bininkpeebin kaah bei bi man.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Bi mu kaah gbir man nin, le bi biɛn lagin ki miɛ Uwonbɔr nin nmɔpɔɔn ki ke, Npɔɔn takpen daan, fin le naan paab nin tum, nin imɔg nin nan biɛn be i yaan.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: A nan che A Kasi a Naaŋin gbeen ti yaaja Defid, wan ye A toon tun win le u pag ke, Ba ŋa wee ibol nan pel nin iŋuu, le bininfob lolig ilol yan kaa ŋan naa?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Le dulinya nan a bɔrtiib ŋa bi sili le biyudam lagin tab ke bin kɔnn Uwonbɔr nin U ninfo gannkaau Masiya.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Ni ye ibamɔn le ke Herɔd, nin Pontiɛ Palati, nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab nin Iziral yaab sin nan lagin kitiŋ nan yaan le, ki lol ilol ke bin ku A kasi a yomu Yisa, wan wee A gann wee u ye A Masiya nan.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Le bi tun nan wee A pii nan tiɛr A bamin nin A pɔɔmin ke ni na li ŋaan.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Daandaan nan, Uyudaan lig baah fann ti man wee ki ter tim ban ye A toon tun nan wee ti fir pag A bɔr nmɔn nin lipoobil.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: Tann A nɔɔ paan bininfob bu ki che bin kan kutebug, tun ligaril a siɛra nin lijinjeel a toon, A kasi a toon tun Yisa a yinlin a pɔɔn n bu.

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Baah miɛ Uwonbɔr ki diɛ nan, le baah be kudiig kan a poolin nin degin. Le Kasi a Naaŋ gbeen bi biɛn nan, le bi pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr nin lipoobil.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Biwonjiɛmin biɛn nan jɔɔ sufu uba nin potiɛl liba le. Bi ni uba kaa nan ŋmag waah jɔɔ nan ke ni ye u binba yaali, amaa bi nan por baah jɔɔ nan nmɔn tenn tab le.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le itoon nmɔn nan jin Uyudaan Yisa a kum a fiim a siɛra, nin npɔɔn takpen le Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl takpem nan be bi biɛn bu.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Uba kaa nan be niin ki ye fala daan ni, nan bu-n, ban jɔɔ bi tɔɔl tɔɔl a paan nin bi niin ti kɔɔr le, ki gaa iligi ki ta dan.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: ki nan sei itoon nin, le bin por ki sei uninfo biɛn nin waah kpaan un gaa man.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Uja uba nan be niin le u yinl ye Josef, u nan ye Liifai yaab a bol a ninfou le, wan wee bi ma u Saipuru a tiŋin nan, itoon nin yi u ke Banabasi .

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: le u nan kɔɔr u paam ki gaa mi ligi ki ta dan ki nan sei itoon nin.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: N ninkpelu Teofilusi, n gbɔŋkpog n-yaan, n nan sɔb nan biɛn wee Yisa nan piin ki tun, nin waah nan wɔɔn man.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: ki nan kpaa lidaal lan wee bi nan nan ta u don paabin, Kasi a Naaŋin kaah nan che u wɔɔn u tooi yan wee u gann nin, le Uwonbɔr nin nan ta u don paab.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: U fala a jim a bɔɔn, le u ta u baa ki wɔɔn bi ki jin siɛra takpem ke u fo. U nan degir bi bu ki be nin bi iweehn pina ki wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr a naamin a bɔr.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: Kulaguŋ kuba ni, le u nan lagin ki ka ji nin bi, le ki nan sei bi limɔsiinl nan: Ni laa nya Jerusalami, amaa ni li guu ipiin yan wee n baa nan siin limɔsiinl lan wee ni gbir n pag li bɔrin.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Nan bu-n Jɔnn fun ni nnyum le, amaa naa li yuuri ni li gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Yisa nin u tooi kaah nan lagin tab n-yonn man nin, le bi baan u ke, Uyudaan daandaan nan le a yin jiin Iziral yaab a naamin ki sei baa.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Naa ye ni li nyi n-yonn man bee idaa yan wee n Baa siin ke nin tun man u tɔɔl a ninkper a pɔɔmin ni.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Amaa ni li kan npɔɔn n-yonn man wee Kasi a Naaŋin li jiirin ni bu-n, le ni na li ji n siɛra Jerusalam nin Judiya a timin biɛn nin Samariya nin dulinya nan a logi nan biɛn.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Waah pag man ki diɛ a bɔɔn, le Uwonbɔr ta u don paab bi numbimin, le kitilangbɔŋ degin u bu le baa ji ga wi.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Amaa le bi paar si ki ta bi num kpaa ki lig papaab kitilangbɔŋin waah chaa do man, libol nmɔn ki miin bijab bilee yee tiwanpenn, ki nan sir bi ban.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: Ki baan bi ke, Nim Galilii yaab, ba ŋa wee ni si ki lig papaab man naa? Yisa wan nyan ni ban ki don paabin, u le ji na yin jirin man le ke naah le kan u wee u don paab man nin.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Yisa a tooi yin nan nyan ligungonl lan wee bi yi ke Olif a Siigi a Jɔɔlin a paab ki chaa jir Jerusalam. Nin yaan nin Jerusalam kaa nan foo maal kiba.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Baah foo Jerusalam n-yonn man nin, le bi jon tan kɔɔ iniin yan yaan wee bi ti be-n, i nan ye tidititɔɔr le. Ban nan be nin yaan le ye Piita, Jɔnn, Jemisi nin Anduru; Filib nin Tomasi, Batolomiu nin Matiu; Jemisi wan nan ye Alifiu a bija-n, nin Simɔnn wan wee bi nan yi u ke zealo , nin Judasi wan ye Jemisi a bija-n.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Bi biɛn nan ti lagin tab le nin bipiib biba nin Yisa a na Meri, nin Yisa a watiib, ki miɛ Uwonbɔr nin potiɛl liba kaa peri.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Piita nan fii sir biwonjiɛm ban nan lagin a sisiigin.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: ki len ke, N nabum, Uwonbɔr a leen yan wee Kasi a Naaŋ nan che Defid pag Judasi a bɔr n-yonn man nan jon nni, ye nin gbeen le. U ma nan liin nsan ki tan wɔɔn le bi choo Yisa.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: U nan ye ti kpiɛg uba le, ki kpiɛ ti bu itoon nan yaan.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Judasi a toonbir a ligi yan wee u kan nin, le u ta ki daa bukpaab; nin yaan nmɔn le u ji nan lu le u yul chaan tum, le u pool pɔɔ le u niɛr biɛn nyan.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Jerusalam yaab biɛn nan gbir tibɔr nmɔn na, le ki yin bukpaab nmɔn bi leen yin ke, Akelidama, ni taai le ye, Ncham a Kpaab.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Ke Piita kaah nan len man ni, Ke ni sɔb Ilan a gbɔŋin ke, Che u niin n sir kulangboŋ; wee uninfo laa kɔɔ niin ni. Amaa Ni ŋan nin uba nsir u toon tun bol.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Mambu ni kpaa le nin ti gann uja uba wan be ni ti iyoo yan biɛn wee Uyudaan Yisa nan jɔɔ ti choon nin.

eng: beginning from the baptism of John until the day when Jesus was taken from us. This person must become along with us a witness to his resurrection.
local: naah nan nyan Jɔnn Wan nan Fu Bininfob Uwonbɔr a Nyunfulin ki tan kpaa iyoo yan wee Uwonbɔr nan nan ta Yisa ki don papaab ki sin jiɛn ti-n. U daan le ye wan nan jin Yisa a kum a fiim a siɛra ki kpiɛ ti bu.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Le bi nyan bijab bilee: Josef wan wee bi nan yi u ke Basabasi, nin Matiyasi.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Le ki miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, Uyudaan, fin le nyi uninfo biɛn a sufu ni. Wɔɔn ti bininfob bilee nan a sisiigin wan wee a gann.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: wee un ŋa a toom ki gaa a toon yan wee Judasi ta jiɛn ki sin jon tan be nan yaan kpaan nin win.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Le bi tɔn inaa, le Matiyasi jin, le bi ta u ki kpiɛ itoon piig nin uba-n bu.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Uja uba mu nan be le u yinl ye Ananiɛsi, le u poo mu a yinl mu ye Safira, le bi nan kɔɔr bi paam ki gaa mi ligi.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Le ki nyan ilig iba ki woon, ke u poo nyi i bu, amaa le ki ta yan gurin dan ki nan sei itoon nin.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Le Piita baan u ke, Ananiɛsi, ba ŋa wee a che Sintani kɔɔ a sufu ni, wee a por tibɔkar ki sei Kasi a Naaŋ ki nyan ilig yan wee a kɔɔr a paamin ki woon naa?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Ŋaah nan kɔɔr npaan nin ni, maa nan ye a yaan lee? Ŋaah ji kɔɔr ki gaa ilig yan nin, yaa be a nɔɔn lee? Ke ba ŋa wee a tiɛr man a sufu ni naa? Naa ye uninfo le a por tibɔkar nmɔn ki sei, amaa ni ye Uwonbɔr le.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananiɛsi kaah gbir man n-yonn man ni, le u nyiɛr lu tum ki kpo. Le bijawaam choo ban biɛn nan gbir nan tun nmɔn.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Le binaachipɔm yuur u ki pɔbin likinkenlin ki ta u nyan ki tan pii u.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Le ni kpiɛ wama, le u poo mu kɔɔn, kaa la nyi nan ŋa.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Le Piita baan u ke, Beim ni, fin nin a chal a paam man wee ni nan kɔɔrin a ligi a biɛn sɔɔ? Le u jiin ke, Nn i biɛn le yen.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Le Piita baan u ke, Ba ŋa wee ni kpaag bimɔb, ki mag Uyudaan a Naaŋ aa? Lig! Ban ta a chal ki tan pii-n a taai li dɔ ntimansanmɔnn nan, bi ji li yuur a mu.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Libol nmɔn, le u mu nyiɛr lu u tum ki kpo. Le binaachipɔmin kɔɔn ki nan kan waah kpo dɔ, le bi ji yuur u mu nyan ki tan pii u bagin u chal.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Le bijawaam nan mann choo biwonjiɛm a laguŋin nin ban biɛn nan gbir nan tun nmɔn takpem.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Itoon nin nan tun ligaril a siɛra nin lijinjeel a toon le takpem bininfobin a sisiigin. Biwonjiɛm biɛn nan ti lagin tab Solomɔnn a dɔŋin le.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Hali nin bininfobin kaa nan jɔɔ li nungbeel bi ni-n, baa nan fir lagin bi ban ni, amaa le ki sin nan pag ki don Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin takpem.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Nin nima biɛn ni, le bijab nin bipiib takpem nan ŋa Uyudaan yada le nin kpiɛ bi kanlin.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Mambu le bininfob nan nyan nin bibaarib, ki paan bi gadum nin tiwandɔɔn wann bu, nsan gbaan ke Piita nan chaa jo n-yonn man ke, u jinjiim li lu bibam bu.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Le bininfob ban be Jerusalam a tinbumin mu tu bibaarib, nin ban wee nnaanbiim mugir bin ki dan, le bi biɛn nan kan kutebug.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Le biminmanib a yudaau nin u tab biɛn ban ye Sadusi yaab nan jɔɔ lipiiponl nin itoon nin.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: Le bi choo itoon nin ki kɔɔn bi kuloondiig kan bi loog bininfob biɛn.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Amaa lidaal a nyiɛg le Uyudaan a malaka jiirin ki nan loor kuloondiigin a naanganl, ki nyan itoon nmɔn niin.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: Le ki bei bi ke, Ni li chaa iwonjiɛn niin nan yaan, ki tan sir, ki bei bininfobin limiɛfol pɔnl nan a bɔr biɛn na.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Baah gbir u bɔr nmɔn, le bi jon ki tan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn yaan, njakpanyiɛn ki piin ki wɔɔn bininfob. Biminmanib a yudaau nin u tabin kaah nan dan ni, le bi yin Iziral yaab a yudamin biɛn, le ki tun ke bin tan nyan Yisa a toon yin kuloondiigin ni ki ta bi dan.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Amaa baah nan tan fo kuloondiig nmɔn yaan ki tan lig, kaa nan kan bi, le ki jir bisanliibin ban ki tan bei bi ke.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: Ti foo kuloondiigin ki miin inaangan nin kpal le; le ban guun si ki guu, amaa le ti loor kaa kan uba kuloondiigin a poolin ni.

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Ban guu iwonjiɛn niin a ninkpelu nin biminmanib a yudamin kaah gbir man nin, le ni ŋa bi kuŋmaruŋ nin lijinjeel, nan na li ŋa.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Le uba kɔɔn, ki nan bei bi ke, Ni lig! Bijab ban wee ni ta ŋa kuloondiigin nin, bi si iwonjiɛn niin ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le ban guu iwonjiɛn niin nin a ninkpelu nin u tabin jon ki tan ta itoon nin ki jirin. Baa nan nyan bi nin npɔɔn ni, nan bu-n bi nan san bijawaam le ke bininfobin na li gabir bi itangbag.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Baah nan ta itoon nin jirin n-yonn man nin, le bi che bi sir Juu yaab a yudam a nintogil, wee biminmanib a yudaan win baan bi ibinbaan.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: Le u len ke, Ti nan sei ni bimɔb nin inunman, ke ni laa wɔɔn bininfob li yinl nan ni, ke lig naah ta nwɔɔnim nmɔn ki yar Jerusalam a gbanann biɛn, wee ki che Yisa a cham nan yin be ti nɔɔn.

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Le Piita nin u tooi tabin jiin ke: Ni kpaa le nin ti chee Uwonbɔr a mɔb le ki jiɛn uninfosaal a mɔb!.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Yisa wan wee ni pii nan kpa u lidapunpunl bu wee u kpe-n, Uwonbɔr wan ye ti Wonbɔru nan fiin u nkumin na.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Le ki don u ki tan kaan U baan tɔɔl a nɔɔjiim paam, ki ŋa u ki Bɔrbig nin Ufeer, ke Iziral yaabin kpenn bi binbema, wee un jiɛn bi toonbir ki per bi.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Tim nin Kasi a Naaŋin le ye ni nan a siɛradam, le Uwonbɔr ta U Kasi a Naaŋ nmɔn sei ban cheel U mɔb.

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Juu yaab a yudamin kaah gbir man n-yonn man nin, le bi ŋuui fii, le bi ban bin ku bi.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Amaa Farasiim a ninfou uba nan be niin, le u yinl ye Gamaliɛl, u nan ye wan wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim le, le bininfob biɛn tenn u jilima, le u fii sir Juu yaab a yudam a laguŋ nmɔn yaan, ki len ke bin laan ta itoon nmɔn ki nyan nkaafiin wamau;.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: le ki sin bei biyudam nmɔn ke, Hei Iziral yaab, ni li nyi naah ke ni ŋa bininfob nan man nan fa.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Iyoo yan nan jon nin, Tiudiɛ nan wɔɔn u ba le ke u ye lininfo takpenl le, le bijab kobina nan nɔɔ u bɔɔn, amaa baah nan nan ku win, u bɔɔnɔɔbin biɛn nan worin na, le ni biɛn ŋa yɔli.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: U kum a bɔɔn, le Judasi wan nan ye Galilii a ninfo win mu nyan ki sir lininfo kanl a yonn, ki wɔɔn u ba, ki kpenn bininfob takpem a lam le bi nɔɔ u. Le bininfob ji nan ku u, le u bɔɔnɔɔbin biɛn worin.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Mambu, n kpaan ni le ke ni paan jaa bininfob nmɔn na! Ki che bi li chaau! Wee ni ye bi toon nmɔn ye bininfosaalib yaan le ke, naa yin tun ni.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Amaa ni ye ni nyan Uwonbɔr ban ke, naa li fir gɔɔ bi. Ni li kan ke ni kɔnn Uwonbɔr le.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Le biyudam nmɔn nin chee Gamaliɛl a mɔb ki yin itoon nin ki kɔɔn, ki che bi boo bi ki ta bi jaa, ki sei bi bimɔb ke, bi li nyi ki laa mɔɔn Yisa a yinl nmɔn ni.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Le itoon nin nan nyan Juu yaab a yudamin ban nin nmɔn, nin baah kpaan nin bin ji ifa Yisa a yinl bu-n.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Lidaal biɛn ke bi chaa iwonjiɛn niin ni le, le ki jin mɔɔn Yisa a yinl bininfob a niin yin, ke Yisa le ye Masiya, ki ji mɔɔn u bɔŋann nin.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: N-yonn man wee Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb nan mann kpiɛgin, le Juu yaab ban pag Girig a leen yin, nan ŋulin Juu yaab ban pag Hiiburu a leen yin bu, nan bu-n bi pukɔɔi kaa nan gaan tijiir naah bi ye bi li gaan man ni ni.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Le itoon piig nin bilee-n yin Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin biɛn ki lagin tab, ki bei bi ke, Naa ŋan nin ti jiɛn Uwonbɔr a bɔr a mɔɔnim ki paar tijiir a tolimin ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: N nabum, ni lig wee ki gann bijab bilile ni sisiigin, ban wee ni nyi ke bi gbee nin Kinaaŋin nin nlam gɔr kaam, wee ti ta man a toon ki sei bi.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: wee tim nta ti tɔɔl a saŋi ki ŋa liwonmiɛlin, nin Uwonbɔr a bɔrin a wɔɔnimin.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Ni nan gbee bininfobin biɛn a numa, le bi gann Sitifin, wan gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ ki jɔɔ yada nan, nin Filib, Pirokorusi, nin Nikanor, nin Timonn, nin Pamenasi, nin Nikolasi, wan nan nyan Antiɛg wee ki kpenn u wonjiɛnl ki nɔɔ Juu yaab a wonjiɛnlin.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Le bi ta bi jon itoon nan ban, le bi ta bi nɔɔi ki paan bi bu ki miɛ Uwonbɔr ki sei bi.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Le bi mann mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔrin. Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb ban be Jerusalam nan a kanl nan kpiɛ weem weem, le Uwonbɔr a minmanib takpem chee Yisa a bɔŋann nin ki ŋa u yada.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Daandaan nan, Sitifin uja wan wee Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl nin u pɔɔm nan be u bu-n, u nan tun ligaril a siɛra nin lijinjeel a toon le bininfobin a sisiigin.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Le bininfob biba nan fii ki yii Sitifin a bɔr, bi nan ye Yemba le ki nyan ban nan kan nŋmarim ki be Juu yaab a wonjiɛn bolin bi nan nyan Sarene nin Alɛgzandira nin Silisiya, nin Asiya le. Le bi biɛn nan piin ki far limɔkpel nin Sitifin.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Amaa baa nan fir pag nyaŋ Sitifin ni, nan bu-n u na pag nin nlam gɔr kaam le nin Kinaaŋ a pɔɔm.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Le bi kɔɔ bininfob biba bu ki gbaa bi ke bin len ke, Ti gbir waah pag imɔbon iba ki sugir Moziz nin Uwonbɔr.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Baah len man n-yonn man nin, le bininfobin, nin bininkpeebin, nin ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siimin a ŋuui fii, le bi fii ki choo Sitifin, ki ta u jon Juu yaab a yudamin ban.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Le bi ji ŋmag bininfob biba ni, ki ke bin por tibɔkar ke, Iyoo biɛn uja nan le choon le ki pag ki bii iwonjiɛn niin nin Moziz a siimin.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Nan bu-n ti gbir u ke Yisa wan ye Nazarif a ninfo win li ber iwonjiɛn niin ki ji kpenn Moziz a kaal lan wee u nan ta sei ti nan.

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Le ban nan lagin ka Juu yaab a yudam a laguŋ nmɔn yaan biɛn nan ta bi numbili ki kpaa Sitifin, ki nan kan u num paam kaah nann Malaka a num.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Le biminmanib a yudaau baan Sitifin ke, Baah galiŋ man nan sin ye ibamɔn naa.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Le Sitifin jiin ke, N nabum nin n baam, ni pel n bɔr nan na man! N-yonn man wee ti yaaja Abiraham nan be Mesopotamiya a tingbɔŋin, iyaayoo ki miin waa la jon Haran ni, le Uwonbɔr a pag degir u bu.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: Ki bei u ke, Nya a tiŋin nin a ninfobin; wee ki li chaa kitiŋ kan wee n li wɔɔn niŋ nan.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le u fii ki jiɛn Chalidiɛn a tiŋ nmɔn, le ki jon ki tan kal Haran. U baa a kum a bɔɔn le Uwonbɔr che u fii niin, ki dan kitiŋ kan yaan wee ni nin be nan.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Waa nan sei u kitiŋ a faali, hali waa nan ta kitiŋ nmɔn a bɔgil wama ki sei wi. Amaa le ki siin limɔsiinl ke u li sei, u ma nin u yaabili ki tiŋ nmɔn, hali nin Abiraham kaah kaa la nan jɔɔ kibig nan.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Uwonbɔr nan bei u nan le: A yaabili na yin ye bicham le kitiŋ kan kaa ye bi yaagi, le bi li ŋa bi biyom niin, ki kan limugil takpem, ki tan kpaa ibin kobina.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Amaa N li dar libol lan che bi tun tenn bi ke biyom nan a tul. Ni bɔɔn bu, N li nyan bi kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, ki dan kitiŋ nan yaan, ki nan jiɛnim.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Uwonbɔr nan siin lipunjiil a mɔsiinl le ki sei Abiraham. Le Abiraham nan ma Azig, le ki nan jii u punl iween itaa daal. Le Azig mu nan ma Jekɔb, le Jekɔb mu nan ma u naachim piig nin bilee-n, ban ye ti yaajaninkpeebin.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Jekɔb a naachim nmɔn kaah nan jɔɔ lipiiponl bi waal Josef bu-n, le bi nan choo u kɔɔr sei Ijibir yaab ke uyomin. Amaa Uwonbɔr nan be nin u.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: le ki nyan u limugil nmɔn biɛn nin. Ki sei Josef nlam, ki che Ijibir a bɔr yudaau Faro ban u bɔr; ki ŋa u uyudaan Ijibir a tiŋin nin u niin biɛn.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Le nkom nan lu Ijibir a tiŋin nin Kenn a tiŋin biɛn, le fala choo bininfob ke ni bir, le ti yaajam a jiir par.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Jekɔb kaah nan gbir ke tijiir be Ijibir n-yonn man ni, le u tun u buwaab niin. Ni ma nan ye bi chinchinn a chaam Ijibir.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Bi chaam man nan kpiɛ milee-n, le Josef nin che u nabum nmɔn biɛ u, le Faro nin biɛ Josef a niin yaab.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le Josef nin tun bi ke bin tan ta u baa Jekɔb nin u niin yaab dan Ijibir ni, bi nan ye bininfob pilile nin bi ŋmu le.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Le Jekɔb nin u niin yaab fii jon Ijibir ki tan kal, bi nan be niin le u ma nin ti yaajam biɛn nan kpo.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Le bi ta bi gbanann kun Sekim ki tan pii kukaag kan wee Abiraham nan ta ilig ki daa ki Hamor a naachim ban nin.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: N-yonn kaah nan nan mal nin Uwonbɔr ngbeen U mɔsiinl lan wee U nan siin sei Abiraham nan, ki miin ti ninfob Iziral yaab ban nan be Ijibir ni nan yab pam.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le ubɔr uba nan jin Ijibir a naam, u mu kaa nan nyi Josef bu.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: Le ubɔr nmɔn nan ŋmag ti ninfobin nŋmag birim, ki to bi bu takpem, ki ji mug ti yaajamin ke bin nyan bi biliɛm bi niin yin ki ta bi gaba wee bin kpo.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Iyaayoo le bi nan nan ma Moziz, le waah nann u biliɛtabi, ki be u baa a niin iŋmal itaa.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: Baah nan nyan u iniin n-yonn man nin, le u bɔrin a bisa kan u ki ta u ki wubir u, ke u tɔɔl a bija-n.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Le bi nan wɔɔn Moziz nlam man biɛn wee Ijibir yaab jɔɔ, le u ŋa uninfoja u leen yin nin u toon yin.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Moziz kaah nan nan foo ibin pina n-yonn man nin, le u nan bon ke un gɔnn u bol a ninfob Iziral yaab.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Waah nan choon gɔnn bi nan, le ki nan kan Ijibir a ninfou kaah mugir Iziral a ninfou, le u dan ki nan fee u ki jaa, ki ku Ijibir a ninfo win.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: Moziz nan tiɛr ke u tɔɔl a ninfob Iziral yaab li biɛ ke Uwonbɔr ta u ma le ke un fee bi jaa, amaa le baa biɛ.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Le ni won kutaag, le u ji kan Iziral yaab bilee kaah be kɔn. Le u ban waah li ŋa bi man wee bin jir ki mɔŋ nin tab, le ki bei bi ke, Ni lig ni ye nnabum le nin tab; ke ba ŋa wee ni boo tab baa.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Amaa le wan mugir u kpiɛgin tir Moziz ki jaa ngbaan ki baan u, Ŋma ŋaaŋ ti yudaau, nin ti bɔjiru waa?.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: A yin kum le ke ŋaah won ku Ijibir a ninfo win man nin naa.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Moziz kaah gbir man n-yonn man nin, le u guun ki nyan Ijibir, ki jon Midiya a tiŋin ki tan be niin ki ye uchaan, ki nan kɔɔn upii ki ma nbijam milee.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Ibin pina a bɔɔn, le malaka degir Moziz bu ki nann nŋmipeen ki gaan kukunkɔɔg bu ki mal Sina a jɔɔl bu.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Moziz kaah kan man nin, le ni ŋa u ligaril. Le u fɔɔr mal ke un mann lig, le ki gbir Uyudaan a kukɔl ke.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: Min le ye ni yaajam a Weem, ki ye Abiraham a Weem, ki ye Azig nin Jekɔb a Weem. Le bijawaam kɔɔ Moziz, le u nin si ki jegil kaa ji fir lig niin ni.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Le Uyudaan bei u ke, Pir a naatagiya; ke ŋaah si nan yaan nmɔn ye kasi a bol le.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: N kan Ijibir yaab kaah mugir N tɔɔl a ninfob Iziral yaab Ijibir a tiŋin man na, ki ji gbir bi fabin ya. Man le N jiirin ke N nan fee bi ki jaa. Mambu, dan wee N tuŋ wee a jir Ijibir.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Moziz nmɔn le Iziral yaab nan yii u ki baan u ke, Ŋma ŋaaŋ ti yudaau nin ti bɔjiru waa? Amaa u ma le Uwonbɔr gann ke u le ye bi yudaau nin bi feeru, le u ma Uwonbɔr che Malaka pag sei Moziz kumɔɔchiɛg a ŋmiimin, ki tun u niin.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Le u nan nyan bi Ijibir, ki tun ligaril nin lijinjeel a toon Ijibir a tiŋ nmɔn yaan nin limɔgmanlin nin baah ji nan be kukunkɔɔg bu ibin pina-n.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: U yaa Moziz le ji nan bei Iziral yaab ke, Uwonbɔr na li tun ulinlenn uba ni ban ke waah tunim man nin, u yin nya ni tɔɔl a ninfobin le.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Moziz nmɔn ji nan be nin Iziral yaab kaah lagin kukunkɔɔg a paab, nin malaka wan nan pag sei u Sinai a jɔɔl bu-n, nin ti yaajam; le u nan gaa limiɛfol a mɔboi ki ta sei ti.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Amaa le ti yaajam kaa nan chee u mɔbi. Mambu le che bi yii u, le ki tiɛr bi sufu ni ke bin ji jir Ijibir a tiŋin.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Le bi bei Moziz a waal Arɔnn ke, Maa ibawol ki sei ti ni, wee ti li nɔɔ i. Ke, Moziz wan nyan ti Ijibir a tiŋin nan taa nyi nan ŋa wi.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Iyaayoo le bi nan maa litingbanl lan nann kinaabig a dann, ki mann kumanug li bu, ki ŋa kuchiig ki donn nan wee bi ta bi nɔɔi maa-n.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
