local: Amaa le Uwonbɔr nyan U num bi ban, le ki jaa bi le bi nin jiɛn paab a wann naan wann. Ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a linlem a gbɔŋin ke: Nim Iziral yaab naah nan mann kumanug kukunkɔɔg bu ibin pina-n, ni nan mann sei min lee?.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Ni yuur litingbanl lan wee bi yi li ke Moleg n yaa nanboŋ le paab nin litingbanl Refann lan ye liŋmabil nan, le i ye ibawol yan wee ni maa ki jiɛn. Mambu, n li ta ni ki tan ŋa tiyomirin kitiŋ kan fɔg ki jon Babilɔnn.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Ti yaajam nan jɔɔ kuwonjiɛn nanboŋ le kukunkɔɔg bu, le ki ye siɛra ki wɔɔn ke Uwonbɔr be bi ban. Uwonbɔr le nan wɔɔn Moziz baah li ŋa ki man, le bi ŋa ki ke Uwonbɔr kaah wɔɔn Moziz man nin.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Ti yaajam mu nan ta Tinanbonn nmɔn le ki sei bi buwaab, le bi mu tug ki chaa, le bi ma nin Josuwa nan foon do nan, ki fee kitiŋ kan wee boliba yaab la nan gaa ki ŋmagin. Uwonbɔr le nan ŋɔɔr bininfob nmɔn ki nyan bi ti a yaajam a numin, le kunanboŋin nin be nin yaan digdig nin Defid mu a yonn.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Uwonbɔr a num nan gbee Defid nmɔn na, le u nan miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, un che un maa kudiig ki sei u ma Uwonbɔr wan ye u yaaja Jekɔb a Weemin.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Amaa Solomɔnn le nan maa kudiigin ki sei u.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr wan kaa jɔɔ kpiɛg nan kaa be iniin yan yaan wee bininfob maa nni. A li ke Uwonbɔr a linlenu kaah nan len ke:.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: Paab le ye N bɔrjal, le tum ye N taai kaah tɔɔ nan bu. Ba a niin wee ni yin maa sei maa? Uyudaan le len man. Bee ba a paam wee ni yin ta seim wee n li be ki fooraa?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Naa ye min le naan tiwann nmɔn biɛn naa.

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Tɔ, le Sitifin ji nan len ke, Ni tuli pɔɔ pam, naa nɔɔ Uwonbɔr a sam nin ni sufu, kaa pelig u bɔri! Iyoo biɛn ni yiin Uwonbɔr a Kasi a Naaŋin le, ke ni yaajam kaah nan yii u man ni!.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Uwonbɔr a linlenu le nan be le ni yaajam kaa mugiru waa? Hali bi ji nan ku ban nan len ke uninfo buchin Masiya na li dan nin na. Daandaan nan nim le ye ban nyan u bɔɔn ki ku u.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: nim le ye ban nan gaa nsiim man wee malakam nan ta sei nin, amaa le kaa sin ji chee mi.

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Sitifin kaah len man nin, le liŋuul choo bininfobin takpem u bu. Le bi ŋman bi nyin.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Amaa le Sitifin gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ, le ki waan lig papaab, ki kan Uwonbɔr a pag, le Yisa si Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Le u len ke, Lig, n kan paab kaah loor wee Uninfosaal a Big si Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam.

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Le bininfob kunn paab, ki piig bi tuli, le bi biɛn guun ki dan u ban ke bin choo u.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: le ki dar u nyan kitiŋ nmɔn a gbaam, ki tan gabir u itangbag. Ki miin siɛra jibin ta bi wan yeer ki bil unaachipɔɔn wan wee bi yi u ke Sɔɔl nan a tatum, le ni wɔɔn ke nkum nmɔn tɔɔ u yaa yul bu le.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Baah nan be gabir win, le Sitifin miɛ Uwonbɔr ke, Uyudaan Yisa gaa n naaŋ.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Le ki gbaan le ki yin nkukɔpɔɔn ke, Uyudaan, jiɛn bi bir ki per ba. U len man le ki sin kpo.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Sɔɔl le la nan ŋma tijar le ki ke u li ku Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb. Le ki jon biminmanib a yudaau ban.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Uja uba le nan be Sizariya, le u yinl ye Kɔnelu. U nan ye Rom yaab a tɔtogib a sanliiru le, ki ŋmag bitɔtogib kobig, ki be Itali a tɔtogib a paabimin.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Iyaayoo Yisa a tooi nin biwonjiɛm ban be Judiya a tingbɔŋin biɛn gbir ke ban kaa ye Juu yaab n mu gaa Uwonbɔr a bɔrin na.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: ke un gaa tigbann ki jo Damasiko a wonjiɛndirin ki tan choo ban nɔɔ Yisa a samin bijab nin bipiib ki ta bi jirin Jerusalam ki nan ŋa bi kuloondiigin.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: Kɔnelu nin u niin yaab biɛn nan ye biwonsam le. U nan ter bilanjiim pam, ki miɛ Uwonbɔr iyoo biɛn.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Ni bu Piita kaah nan jon Jerusalam nan, le ban tiɛr ke lipunl a jiim kpaa ki sei biwonjiɛm biɛn ni, nan pag bii u.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: N-yonn man wee u nan chaa ki tan mal Damasiko nan, le nwonween nyan paab, ki jiirin ki nan wonn ki linn u.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Le weemba daal a jɔɔg tikur titaa a yonn, le u kan zii. U nan kan Uwonbɔr a malaka le u dan u ban ki nan ke, Kɔnelu.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: ki bei u ke, A kɔɔ ban kaa jii bi pun a niin le, ki ji lagin jin nin bi.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Le u nyiɛr lu tum, ki gbir likukɔl yi u ke, Sɔɔl Sɔɔl, ba ŋa wee a mugirim man naa.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Kɔnelu kaah kan malaka nmɔn nin, le bijawaam choo u, le u baan u ke, Uyudaan baa yen naa? Le malaka nmɔn jiin ke, A miɛm nin a teril lan wee a jɔɔ ter bilanjiimin ye litiɛril a piin le Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Le Piita piin ki pag wɔɔn bi a li ke naah tun man nin:.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Le Sɔɔl baan u ke, Uyudaan a ye ŋmiɛ? Le likukɔlin jiin ke, Min le ye Yisa wan wee a mugirin.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Daandaan nan tun bininfob biba Jopa wee bin tan yin uja wan wee bi yi u ke Simɔnn, wee u yinl liba mu ji ye Piita nni.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Ubɔr Herɔd nan choo biwonjiɛm bibam, ki ban un mugir bi.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: N nan be Jopa a tiŋin le ki miɛ Uwonbɔr, le ki kan zii, le n kan niwann niba nyan papaab. Ni nan nann likinkenl le, ki peen, ki jɔɔ imɔgban ina ki jiirin ki nan sir n ban.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Daandaan nan fiim ki li chaa kitiŋ nmɔn yaan na, bi li beiŋ nan kpaa talar wee a ŋa.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: U be ugbanjab wan wee bi yi u ke Simɔnn n-yaa niin le, u niin nmɔn mal limɔgil le.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: U nan che bi ta takoobi le ki ku Jemisi wan ye Jɔnn a kpelin.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Le n lig ki kan tiwankor tan jɔɔ itaa ina, nin ipiɛl, nin tiwanfuul wann nin nnɔɔm man laan paab.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le bijab ban chiɛn Sɔɔl nan, nan paar sir soon kaa ji pagi. Bi nan gbir likukɔl kaah paga, amaa le kaa kan wan pagi.

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Malaka wan nan pag sei win kaah nan jon nin, le Kɔnelu yin u niin a toontum bilee nin u tɔtogir uba, wan nan be u ban idaa biɛn ki tun itoon ki tenn win.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: Waah ŋa man, ki biɛ ke Juu yaab a sufu penn nin, le u che bi choo Piita mu. Ni nan ŋa ki yegin yuglagir a chiig kan wee bi ŋma boroboro kan kaa jɔɔ nnyɔgin a yonn le.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Le n gbir likukɔl len ke, Piita fiiu, ki ku ki ŋman.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Le Sɔɔl nan nan fii paab, ki ligir u numbili, amaa le kaa ji ga ni ba. Le bi nan da u nɔɔ ki ta u jon Damasiko.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: Ki bei bi niwann nan biɛn ŋa, le ki tun bi Jopa a tiŋin.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: Baah choo Piita nan, ni bɔɔn bu le bi ta u ŋa kuloondiigin le ki che bitɔtogib bina na a kerim mina guu u ki kpenn tab. Ubɔr Herɔd nan lol ke yuglagir a chiig a jiir a jim nmɔn a yonn jon ke, u li nyan Piita kuloondiigin, ki ta u siin kuninfogbaluŋ a numbimin ki ji u tibɔr.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Le n ke, Ibamɔn buu Uyudaan! Maa la nyi jin niwann nan kaa be kasi bee niwann nan kaa ŋan ni.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Iweehn itaa waa nan ga, kaa jin ni, kaa nyun mu.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Naah nan won kutaag wee bi chaan, ki nan tan mal Jopa nween paamin, le Piita don kudiititɔrug a paab, ke un tan miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Piita kaah nan be kuloondiigin nin, amaa le biwonjiɛm a laguŋ mu nan miɛ Uwonbɔr linunmanl ki tenn u.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Le likukɔl ji mann nyan paab ki len ke, Laa yin nan wee Uwonbɔr ŋa ni kasi, ke naa ŋan ni.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔru uba nan be Damasiko nmɔn yaan, bi nan yi u ke Ananiɛsi. Le uyudaan yin u zii a poolin le ki bei u ke, Ananiɛsi! Le u jiin ke, Min sɔ, n Yudaau.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Le nkom nan choo u le u ni ban tijiir un ji. Baah nan be ŋaan tijiir nmɔn ni, le u kan zii.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Herɔd nan lol ke ni won njakpanyiɛn ke, u li nyan Piita ki ji u tibɔr, le Piita dɔ biguguub bilee a sisiigin ki jiɛn le bi ta tikulor tilee ki baan u, le ban guun nmɔn a ni bibam mu guu kuloondiigin a timansanmɔnn.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Ni nan ŋa man le ki tan kpaa nfimitaa, le ki ji jir don paab.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Le Uyudaan bei u ke, Fiim ki li chaa ki tan nɔɔ nsan man tel ki chaa Judasi a niin ni, ki baan uja wan nyan Tarsusi, wee u yinl ye Sɔɔl nbu. Lig, daandaan nan u be miɛ Uwonbɔr le.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: Le u kan paab loor le niba nann likinkenl takpenl ki nyan paab ki jiirin nin imɔgban ina.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Libol nmɔn le Uyudaan a malaka degirin, le nwonween wonn kuloondiig nmɔn yaan. Le u dam dam Piita a bɔsipar ki fiin u, ki bei u ke, Fii weema. Le tikulorin pir ki lu.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Libol nmɔn le baah nan tun bijab bita ban n ban nin, nan sir maah be iniin yan yaan nin. Bi nan nyan Sizariya a tiŋin le.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: Le ki kan uja uba zii nin, wan a yinl ye Ananiɛsi, le u kɔɔn ki ta u nɔɔi ki paan u bu, ke un ligir.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Le u kan tiwankor tan jɔɔ itaa ina nin tan be nnyumin nin tan fuul tum nin nnɔɔm likinkenl n-yaan.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Le malaka n bei u ke, Yeem a wan yɔɔr ki tag a naatagiya. Le Piita ŋa man. Le malaka n ji bei u ke, Ta a kinkenl gaba ki li nɔɔm ni.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Le Kinaaŋ beim ke n nɔɔ bi ki laa yii. Le biwonjiɛm ban be Jopa a tiŋin nin, bi biloob mu nan chiɛnim le ti jon Sizariya, le ti biɛn foo ki kɔɔ Kɔnelu a niin.

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Le Ananiɛsi jiin ke, Uyudaan, bininfob takpem le pag uja nmɔn bu, ki jiin niwann nan biɛn wee u tun ki jin a tɔɔl a ninfob fala Jerusalam man.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Le u gbir likukɔl yin u ke, Piita fiiu wee ki ku ŋman.

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Le u nɔɔ u, le bi nyan kuloondiig n-yaan, amaa le kaa biɛ ke malaka-n kaah tun nan nin ye ibamɔn le, ki nyi ke u kan zii le.

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Le Piita ke, Uyudaan! Ibamɔn buu, maa la nyi jin niwann nan kaa ŋan bee nan ye tijaginn ni.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: Le u dan nan do nin tininkper a pɔɔm man wee u gaa biminmanib a yudam ban nin, ke un nan choo ban biɛn yi a yinl.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: Le u bei ti malaka kaah foon u niin man ki nan bei u ke un tun uba Jopa, wee un tan yin uja wan wee bi yi u ke Simɔnn, wee u yinl liba ji ye Piita nan.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Le bi nan jon lagir chinchinn a guguuru n bu, le ki ji lagir umiɛn nin bu, ki nin tan foo tikur a naanganl lan leg ntimansanmɔnn man chaa kitiŋ poolin nin. Le linaanganl nmɔn loor litɔɔl ki sei bi, le bi n-yaan nkaafiin. Ki nɔɔ nsan ki chaa, le malaka-n bor ki jiɛn Piita.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Le likukɔl nmɔn ji mann bei u ke, Laa yin nan wee Uwonbɔr ŋa ni kasi ke naa ŋan ni.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Amaa le Uyudaan bei u ke, Fin li chaau, uma ye wan wee N gann u ke un mɔɔn N yinl ki bei ban kaa ye Juu yaab nin bi bɔrtiib nin Juu yaab.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: U li pag tibɔr ki seiŋ wee fin nin a niin yaab biɛn n kan feeja.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Le Piita a num wonn, le u len ke, Daandaan nan le n nin biɛ ke ni sin ye ibamɔn le ke Uwonbɔr le tun u malaka le u dan nan nyam Herɔd a nɔɔn, ki ji nyam Juu yaab kaah bi ban bin ŋam man nin.

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Likukɔl nmɔn nan len le ki tan kpaa nfim mitaa, libol nmɔn le bi ta likinkenl nmɔn don paab.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: N li wɔɔn u naah kpaa talar nin un ji fala N yinlin bu man.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Maah piin tibɔr nan a pagim n-yonn man nin, le Kasi a Naaŋ jiirin bi bu, ke waah pii nan jiirin ti bu npiinimin man nin.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: Waah biɛ man nin bu, le u jon Jɔnn Mag a na Meri a niin, ki miin bininfob takpem pii lagin niin ki miɛ Uwonbɔr le.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Piita kaah la nan be tiɛr waah kan zii kan nin a taai-yin, iyaayoo le Kɔnelu kaah tun ban nin, baan ki biɛ Simɔnn a niin kaah ye yan ki sir ntimansanmɔnn.

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Le Ananiɛsi nan jon ki tan kɔɔ iniin nmɔn yaan, ki ta u nɔɔi ki paan Sɔɔl bu, le ki bei u ke, N nabig Sɔɔl, n Yudaau Yisa wan degir a bu nsan n-yonn man wee a nan chaan do nan ni, u ma tunim ke n nan ligir a num wee a gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Le n tiɛr Uyudaan kaah nan len man nin, Jɔnn nan fun ni Uwonbɔr a nyunful le, amaa nim ma yin gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ le.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: Le Piita kpaar kpaar nkaafiin a naanganl, le usapɔɔn toontunn, wan a yinl ye Roda dan linaanganl nmɔn ban ke un jee ŋma yen naa.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Le ki yin ki baan njee uba be do wee u yinl ye Simɔnn Pitaa?.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Libol nmɔn, le niba nyan u numbili yin ki lu, ki nann tijanpɔgir, le u ligir ki fii sir, le bi fun u Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Ke, ni ye Uwonbɔr sin pu bi ke waah pu tim ban ŋa Uyudaan Masiya yada-n ke, n mu ye ŋma wee ki ke n yii wee Uwonbɔr kaah len man n laa tun naa.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: Le ki biɛ Piita a niɛn, ki guun jir bininfobin ban nin nmɔn takpen, le kaa ji loor linaanganlin ni, ki tan bei bi ke, Piita be nkaafiin.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Piita le la nan be tiɛr waah kan zii kan nin bu le, le Kinaaŋ bei u ke, Simɔnn bijab bita choon baŋ.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: le u jin tijiir le ki kan npɔɔn. Iyaayoo le Sɔɔl nan lagin be nin Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin iweehn ilee Damasiko.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Baah nan gbir man n-yonn man nin, le bi jiɛn tifur, le ki don Uwonbɔr a yinl, ki len ke, Uwonbɔr che ban kaa ye Juu yaabin kpenn bi binbema, ke bi mu n kan limiɛfol.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Le bi bei u ke, A waar le. Waah nan gbaa pag bi ke ni sin ye man nin, le bi ke, Ni li ye u tinaaŋ le.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Ni bu fii ki jiir tuma, ki laa biɛn njee a nɔɔ bi bee a laa nɔɔ, nan bu-n min le tum bi.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: Libol nmɔn le u piin ki mɔɔn Yisa a bɔr tiwonjiɛndirin, ke Yisa ye Uwonbɔr a Big le.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb ban nan kan limugil Sitifin a kum a yonn nin, bi nan worin le ki jon ntim man fɔg, ki tan kpaa Fonisiya, nin Saipuru, nin Antiɛg ki jɔɔ Yisa a bɔr ki mɔɔn tenn Juu yaab a binba.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Amaa le Piita le si ki kpaar linaanganl, le bi nan loor linaanganl nmɔn ki kan u, le ni gar bi.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Le Piita jiirin tum ki nan bei bijabin ke, Min le ni ban nmɔn. Babu wee ni dan naa.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Ban biɛn nan gbir u mɔɔn ni nmɔn, ni nan gar bi le, le bi baan ke, Uja nan le kaa nan ku ban yi Yisa a yinl Jerusalam nnaa? Waah yaan ti dan le ke un nan choo Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb ki lol bi ki ta bi jir tan sei kumanug a yudam lee.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb biba nyan Saipuru, nin Sarene ki jon Antiɛg, ki tan be mɔɔn ti Uyudaan Yisa a bɔŋann nin ki tenn ban kaa ye Juu yaabin.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Le u don u nɔɔ ke bin ŋmin tifura, le ki wɔɔn bi Uyudaan kaah nyan u kuloondiigin man. Le u bei bi ke, Ni li chaa ki tan bei Jemisi nin biwonjiɛmin nan tun nan a bɔr, le u fɔɔr jiɛn bi ki yaar jon niba paam.

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Le bi jiin ke, Kɔnelu le tun ti, u ye Rom yaab a tɔtogib a sanliiru le, ki ye uninfo buchin, ki san Uwonbɔr, le Juu yaab biɛn tenn u jilima. Kasi a malaka le dan ki nan bei u ke un yiŋ wee a dan u niin, wee un gbir nan wee a li pag sei u.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Le Sɔɔl a mɔɔni nmɔn nan mann ki kpiɛ nin Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm, le u bɔr nmɔn wɔɔn ke Yisa sin ye Masiya le, le Juu yaab ban be Damasiko nan a potiɛl nan ŋmarin.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Le Uyudaan a pɔɔm be nin bi, le bininfob takpem ŋa yada ki kpenn bi binbem ki sei Uyudaan.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Naah nan wonn kutaag, le ni mug ban guu kuloondiigin, kaa ye wama, nan bu-n baa nyi Piita kaah be nan yaan ni.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Le Piita yin bijabin ki ta bi kun u niin le ki ŋa bi u cham. Ni won kutaag le Piita fii ki nɔɔ bi, le biwonjiɛm ban be Jopa n-yaa ni bibaam chiɛn u.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Antiɛg a tiŋin, biwonjiɛm a laguŋ nan jɔɔ bilinlem nin ban wɔɔnn: Bi nan yi bi ke Banabasi nin Simiɛn wan a yinl ji nan ye Naga, nin Lusiɛsi, wan nan nyan Sarene, nin Manann nin Sɔɔl.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le Juu yaab nan lagin tab ki lol ke bin ku Sɔɔl.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Biwonjiɛm a laguŋ kan be Jerusalam nan gbir tibɔr nmɔn le ki tun Banabasi jon Antiɛg a tiŋin.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Le Herɔd sei bimɔb ke bin ban u ni, le bi ban ban kaa kan wi, le u baan ban guu kuloondiigin ibinbaan, ki sei nsan ke bin ku ba. Ni bɔɔn bu le Herɔd nyan Judiya ki jiir Sizariya, ki tan be nin yaan wama.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Le ni ŋmiɛ ki won kutaag le u foo Sizariya, ki miin Kɔnelu pii yin u maali nin u jɔtiib, le bi biɛn dan ki nan ka guu Piita a foom.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Baah nan be jiɛn Uwonbɔr ki lolig imɔɔŋin, le Kasi a Naaŋ nan bei bi ke, Ni nyam Banabasi nin Sɔɔl, wee bin tun itoon yan wee N yin bi ke bin tun ni.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: nween nin kunyiɛg ke bi guu kitiŋ nmɔn a timansanmɔnn le nbamɔm ke bin ku u. Le u biɛ baah lolig u bu man.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Waah nan tan foo niin ki kan Uwonbɔr kaah sei bi literil man nin, le ni mɔŋ u, le u kɔɔn bi biɛn lipoobil, ki bei bi ke bi li nɔɔ Uyudaan nin bi sufu biɛn nin ibamɔn.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Herɔd nan kɔnn Taya nin Sidom yaab le; le bi lagin tab ki ban nsan bin tan kan u. Baah nan kan nsan nmɔn Basitusi wan ye ubɔr Herɔd a toontunn wan wee u nan ŋau yada nban nin, le bi faa u tisandann, nan bu-n bi jiir nan nya ubɔr n yaa tiŋin le.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Piita kaah nan foon ki yin kɔɔ n-yonn man ni, le Kɔnelu kan u ntimansanmɔnn le ki gbaan tum ki sei u jilima.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Le bi lol imɔɔŋ ki miɛ Uwonbɔr, ni bɔɔn bu le bi ta bi nɔɔi paan bi bu, le ki che bi jon.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Kitiŋ nmɔn a gonl nan jɔɔ libonl. Le Sɔɔl a bɔɔnɔɔb yuur u kunyiɛg ki ŋa kukachiɛg takpeŋin, ki ta u nyan kitiŋ nmɔn a gonl a bonlin yaan.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Banabasi nan ye uninfoŋan le, ki gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ nin yada, le ki nan ta-n bininfob takpem ki sei Uyudaan.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Le ubɔr Herɔd gann lidaal liba, ki yee u naam a daala ki nyan nan kal u naangbɔŋ bu, le ki pag sei bininfobin.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Amaa le Piita bei u ke, Fiiu, min tɔɔl ye uninfo le. Le ki fiin u.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: N-yonn nmɔn le Kasi a Naaŋ nan ta bininfob bilee nmɔn ki nyan nsan bu, le ki che bi jon Selesiya ki tan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ niin ki jon Saipuru.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Le Sɔɔl jon Jerusalam ki tan pɔɔn u ba ke un lagin nin Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb, le baa ŋa u yada ke u ye u bɔɔnɔɔri ki san u bijawaam.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Le Banabasi jon Tarsusi a tiŋin, ke un tan ban Sɔɔl.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Le bi tiɛn ke, Likukɔl nan ye nween a kukɔl le, naa ye uninfosaali.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Waah nan pag nin Kɔnelu nan, le ki kɔɔ iniin nmɔn yaan ki miin bininfob takpem le lagin ki ka.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Baah nan foo Salami nan, le bi kɔɔ Juu yaab a wonjiɛndirin le ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki tenn bi. Jɔnn le nan be bi ban ki ter bi.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Amaa le Banabasi dan ki nan ta u jon Yisa a toon-yin ban. Ki bei bi Sɔɔl kaah nan kan Uyudaan nsan bu man, nin Uyudaan kaah nan pag sei u man, nin Sɔɔl kaah nan mɔɔn Yisa a yinl nin lipoobil man Damasiko a tiŋin.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: waah nan tan kan win, le u ta u jirin Antiɛg. Libinmunl le Banabasi nin Sɔɔl nan be nin biwonjiɛm a laguŋ ki wɔɔn bininfob takpem. Bi bɔɔnɔɔb nmɔn le nan ye chinchinn a wonjiɛm Antiɛg a tiŋin.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Libol nmɔn le Uyudaan a malaka che kuwiɛnbirug choo Herɔd le igoor jin u le u kpo, waah kaa nan pagir Uwonbɔr nbu.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Le u bei bi ke, Ni tɔɔl nyi ke tim Juu yaab a siim kaa chee ke ti li chaa ban kaa ye Juu yaab ban ni, bee ki ŋmarin bi ni. Amaa Uwonbɔr wɔɔm ke n laa yin u ba ke waa be kasi bee waa ŋan ni.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Le bi choon ligungonl lan be nnyum a sisiigin nmɔn ki tan foo Pafosi. Nin yaan le bi nan kan unyɔgdaan u ba, u yinl nan ye Bar-Yisa, u nan ye Juu yaab a ninfou le ki mal ke u ye Uwonbɔr a linlenu le.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Le Sɔɔl lagin be nin bi ki choon u wiib ki gɔnn Jerusalam, ki mɔɔn Uyudaan a yinl nin lipoobil.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le bilinlem biba nyan Jerusalam, ki dan Antiɛg a tiŋin.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Amaa le Uwonbɔr a bɔr mann mɔɔn ki yan kɔɔ bininfobin.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Mambu, naah yim n bu le n dan kaa yii. Ni yim ba bu.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: u nan be ubɔr yudaan ban le, ubɔr nmɔn a yinl nan ye Seregu Pɔɔlu, u nan ye ulandaan le, le ki yin Banabasi nin Sɔɔl ke bin nan pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr wee un gbir.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: U ma nin Juu yaab ban pag Girig a leen yin nan fa limɔkpel le, mambu le bi nan ban baah li ŋa man ki ku u.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Bi ni uba a yinl le nan ye Agabu. Le Kinaaŋ nan che u len ke nkom takpem li lu Rom yaab a tingbɔŋin. .

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: N-yonn man wee Banabasi nin Sɔɔl diɛ bi toon yan wee bi nan jon ke bin tan tun nin, le bi ta Jɔnn Mag ki nyan Jerusalam ki jir kun Antiɛg.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Le Kɔnelu jiin u ke, Ni ween ina sɔ dinan, le n nan be miɛ Uwonbɔr n niin nan a yoo bu, njɔween paam a kur titaa. Libol nmɔn le n kan uja uba le u yee tiwann yɔɔr le ti nyagin ki nan sir n nintogil.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Amaa le unyɔgdaan nmɔn, wan a yinl ji ye Elimasi, Girig a leen yin nin, nan pɔɔn u ba ke un ŋɔɔr ubɔrin wee un la ŋa yada.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: N-yonn man wee n nabumin nan nan biɛ man ni, le bi ta Sɔɔl jon Sizariya a tiŋin, le ki che u kun Tarsusi.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin lol ke bin ter biwonjiɛn tab ban be Judiya a tiŋin uninfo biɛn kaah li fir man a tarig.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: ki len ke, Kɔnelu, Uwonbɔr gbir a miɛm ma, ki li tiɛr a piini yan wee a pu bilanjiimin.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Judiya nin Galilii nin Samariya a wonjiɛm a laguŋ kan nposɔɔn n-yonn nmɔn. Kasi a Naaŋ a teril bu le bi kan ngbanann pɔɔn, le bi kanl kpiɛ, le ki be ki san Uyudaan.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Le bi sin nan ŋa man, ki ta bi teril nmɔn tun Banabasi nin Sɔɔl, ke bin ta tan sei biwonjiɛn sanliib n na.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Le Sɔɔl wan a yinl ji ye Pɔɔl nan gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ, le ki lig unyɔgdaan nin chiim ki ke.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Ni tun uba Jopa, wee un tan yin wan wee bi yi u ke Simɔnn, wee u yinl liba ji ye Piita nni. U ye uchaan le ki be Simɔnn wan ye ugbanjab n-yaa niin wee i mal limɔgilin.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Piita kaah nan choon jin kutingbɔŋin, le ki nan nan jon un tan gɔnn Uwonbɔr a ninfo gann kaab ban be Liida a tiŋin ni.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Pɔɔl nin Banabasi kaah nan foo Ikoniɛm nan, le bi kɔɔ Juu yaab a wonjiɛndirin, ke baah pii ti ŋaan man nin. Niin le bi nan pag nbamɔm, le Juu yaab nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab takpem ŋa yada.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: A ye ulinlar a big le, ki ye nan ŋan biɛn a dataag! A gbee nin kularug le iyoo biɛn nin nbiin. A li jiɛn ki laa labir Uyudaan a bamɔn yin ki ŋa ti tibɔkaraa?.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Ni ma bu le n che bi yiŋ ni weem, ŋaah dan nan, ni ŋan. Daandaan nan ti biɛn be do Uwonbɔr a nintogil. Ti ban ti gbir tibɔr tan wee ti Yudaau wɔɔŋ ke a bei ti n le.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: Nin yaan le u nan tan kan uja uba, wan wee bi yi u ke Aniɛsi, wee u ye tigbanann kpiindaan nin, wee u nan dɔɔn u wan dɔɔn waŋ bu ki tan kpaa ibin iniin nin.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Amaa Juu yaab ban kaa nan chee ŋa yada-n nan bii ban kaa nan ye Juu yaabin a sufu le. Le bi ŋuui fii biwonjiɛmin bu.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Daandaan nan Uyudaan li dar a tul. U li che a jɔɔn ki nin yuur ke ŋaa la kan nween ni. Libol nmɔn, le liŋunl nin libunbɔnl jiirin u bu, le u nin choon tulin, ki ban u ba n da u.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Le Piita piin ki bei bi ke, N nin biɛ ke ni ye nbamɔm le ke Uwonbɔr kaa gann ni.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Le Piita bei u ke, Aniɛsi, Yisa che a kan kutebuga, fiiu ki kpab a wan dɔɔn waŋa. Libol nmɔn yaan le Aniɛsi fii.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Pɔɔl nin Banabasi nan be niin ki yuura, ki jɔɔ lipoobil ki pag Uyudaan a bɔr ki tenn bininfob, le Uyudaan nmɔn sei bi npɔɔn le bi pag u nyunyunbasanl a bɔr, ki tun ligaril a siɛra nin lijinjeel a toon.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Ubɔr uyudaan nin kaah kan nan tun nin, le u ŋa yada, nan bu-n waah nan kan ntinnim man nyan Uyudaan ban nin le ni ŋa u ligaril.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Le kitiŋ nmɔn a ninfob bɔgir nin tab; biba nan si Juu yaabin a chaŋ le, le biba mu si Yisa a tooi yin a chaŋ.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Le bininfob ban biɛn nan be Liida a tiŋin nin Saron a yagil nan kan u, le ki kpenn bi binbem ki ŋa Uyudaan yada.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: Amaa U gaan ibol biɛn ban san u, ki tun nan ŋan nin le.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Baah nan nyan Pafosi nan, le Pɔɔl nin u ninɔɔtab kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki jon Pega kan be Pamfiliya a tiŋin nin, nin yaan le Jɔnn jiɛn bi ki jir Jerusalam.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Le Juu yaab nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin, nin bi tiŋ nmɔn a yudam lagin tab, ki lol ke bin jin itoon nin fala ki gabir bi itangbag.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Upii wonjiɛn uba mu nan be Jopa, le u yinl ye Tabita, . U nan tun itoon ŋan le iyoo biɛn ki tenn faladam.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Ni nyi tibɔr tan wee Uwonbɔr nan pag sei Iziral yaab ki wɔɔn bi tibɔŋann tan ye nposɔɔn Yisa Masiya bu, u ma ye ti biɛn a Yudaau.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Le bi nyan Pega, ki jon Antiɛg kan be Pisidiya a tiŋin nin. Le likpakɔɔl daal foo le bi kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin ki ka.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Amaa baah nan biɛ ke bin yin gabir bi itangbagin, le bi guun jon Lisitira nin Deba, nin ntim bum man be Likoniya a tiŋin nin.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le u nan buŋ ki kpo, le bi fun u ki ta u don kudiititɔrug a paab ki tan bil.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Ni nyi nan ŋa Judiya a tiŋin, ni nan piin Galilii le Jɔnn kaah nan pag linyunful lan a bɔrin.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Baah karin Moziz a siim nin Uwonbɔr a linlem a sɔbim n-yaan a bɔɔn bu, le kuwonjiɛndiig nmɔn a ninkpeeb bei Pɔɔl nin Banabasi ke, Ti na a bum, ni ye ke ni jɔɔ tibɔŋann tan wee ni yin pag pɔɔg ti gbanann ke, ni paga.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: nin yaan le bi ji nan tan tugir ki mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Jopa nin Liida kaa nan fɔg nin tabi, le Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔb ban be Jopa gbir ke Piita be Liida, le bi tun bijab bilee u ban ke bin tan balin u ke Ti baliŋ le ŋa weema ki dan.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: ni biɛn nyi Uwonbɔr kaah gbeen Yisa wan nyan Nazarif nan U pɔɔm man, ki sei u Kasi a Naaŋ nin npɔɔn nin waah nan choon ki tun itoon ŋan ki teb ban biɛn nan be ulinlarin a pɔɔmin ni, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr le nan be u ban.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Le Pɔɔl fii sir ki don u nɔɔ le ki piin pag ke, Nim Iziral yaab nin nim ban kaa ye Juu yaab ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr nan, ni pelim man!.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Le uja u ba nan be Lisitira ki ye kiwɔbig, bi nan ma u ki miin u ye kiwɔbig le, kaa nyi chonn ni.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Le u fii ki nɔɔ bi, waah nan tan foon, le bi ta u ki don paab a diig n-yaan. Le ipukɔɔ yan biɛn nan be kudiig nmɔn yaan fii ki sir gɔɔn Piita ki mɔr, ki ta bi tan nin bi daalam ki wɔɔn u, ki ke, Dokasi kaah nan be u miɛfolin ki ŋal nan teen ti sɔ.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Tim le ye u siɛra dam nin waah tun nan biɛn Juu yaab a tingbɔŋin nin Jerusalam. Le bi nan kpa u lidapunpunl bu le u kpo.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Iziral yaab, a Wonbɔru le gann ti yaajamin ki che bi pɔr takpem, n-yonn man wee bi la nan be Ijibir a tiŋin nin, le Uwonbɔr ta u pɔɔm ki nyan bi kitiŋ nmɔn yaan.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Le u nan pelig Pɔɔl a mɔleen yin. Le Pɔɔl lig u chiim, ki biɛ ke u jɔɔ yada kan li sei u kutebug.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Le Piita nyan bi biɛn kudiig nmɔn yaan; le ki gbaan ki miɛ Uwonbɔr. Le ki nan jir utakpiir n ban, ki yin u, Tabita, fiiu. Le u ligir ki kan Piita, le ki fii kal.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: amaa le iweehn itaa daal, le Uwonbɔr fiin u nkumin, le ti kan u.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: Le Uwonbɔr nan ta limɔril ki ŋmag bi kukunkɔɔg bu ibin pina.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: le u yin u nmɔpɔɔn ke, Fii sir a taai buu! Le u yug sir paab ki nin choon.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Le Piita choo u nɔɔ, ki ter u, le u fii sir. Le u yin biwonjiɛmin nin ipukɔɔ-n, ki ta u ki sei bi, ke u fo.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: Amaa naa ye bininfob biɛn le nan kan wi, amaa ban wee Uwonbɔr pii gann bi wee bi ye siɛra damin le nan kan u, nin tim ban nan lagin ki jin ki nyun nin u kum a fiim a bɔɔn nin.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: le u bii ibol ilile yan be Kenann a tiŋin nin, le ki che u tɔɔl a ninfob gaa kitiŋ nmɔn le ki kpann bi faal.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Bininfobin kaah nan kan Pɔɔl kaah tun man nin, le bi nin piin ki pag papaab Likoniya yaab a leen yin ke, Itingban le kpann uninfosaal ki jiirin ti ban.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Le bininfob ban biɛn nan be Jopa a tiŋin nan gbir tibɔr nmɔn, le bininfob takpem nan ŋa Uyudaan yada.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: Le u sei ti nsan ke ti li mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nmɔn ki tenn bininfob, ki ji ti siɛra ke u ma le Uwonbɔr nyan ke u li ye ban fo nin ban kpo a bɔjiru.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Ibin kobina nin piŋmu a sisiigin le ni niɛ biɛn nan tun. Ni bɔɔn bu le Uwonbɔr sei bi bibɔjib le bi ŋmag ki tan kpaa ulinlenn Samɔɔl a yonn.

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Le bi yin Banabasi a yinl ke Ziɛu, ki yin Pɔɔl mu a yinl ke Hemesi, nan bu-n u ma nan ye wan pag tibɔr.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Piita nan paar be Jopa a tiŋin, ki jin isaŋa, un nan be uja wan nan ye ugbanjab n-yaa niin le, wan wee bi yi u ke Simɔnn nin.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Bilinlem biɛn nan jin u siɛra ke wan biɛn ŋa u yada ke u kan tibir a jiɛn pera u yaa yinlin bu.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Le bininfob nan nan bei Uwonbɔr ke un sei bi ubɔr, le Uwonbɔr gann Sɔɔl, wan ye Kisi a bija nan, u nan ye Bɛnjamɛnn a bol a ninfou le, le u ŋmag nnaam nmɔn ki tan kpaa ibin pina.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Le bi tingbanl lan wee bi yi ke Ziɛu wee u diig si kitiŋ nmɔn a gbaamin a minmanu nan ŋmag inaaja nin tifar ki nan sir kitiŋ nmɔn a timansanmɔnn, nan bu-n u ma nin kuninfogbaluŋin nan ban bin mann kumanug le ki sei Pɔɔl nin Banabasi.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Piita kaah la nan be pagin, le Kasi a Naaŋ jiirin ki nan gbeen ban biɛn nan gbir tibɔr nmɔn nin.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Amaa Banabasi nin Pɔɔl kaah nan gbir baah len man nmɔn, le bi sufu bii, le bi dar bi daalam par par, ki guun kɔɔ kuninfogbaluŋin yaan, le ki tann ke:.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le u nan pir Sɔɔl a naam nmɔn, le ki jin Defid. U nan pag Defid a bɔr ke: N kan Defid wan ye Jesi a bigin le N num gbee; nan bu-n u li tun nan biɛn wee n ban.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Le ni gar biwonjiɛm ban nan jii bi pun ki chiɛn Piita nan, Kasi a Naaŋ a piin kaah kpaar wur ban kaa ye Juu yaab n mu bu-n.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: Bininfob nan, ba ŋa wee ni tun man naa? Ti tɔɔl le ye bininfob le ke naah ye man nin. Ti ŋmagin tibɔŋann le ti nan sei ni wee ni jiɛn dulinya nan a wann yɔɔra wee ki li nɔɔ Uwonbɔr Wan fo-n, Wan naan paab nin tum nin imɔg nin nan biɛn be i yaan.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: Uja nan a yaabili ni, le Uwonbɔr nyan Yisa wan ye ufeerin, ke un fee Iziral yaab ki jaa, ke waah nan siin limɔsiinl man nin.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Nan bu-n bi gbir baah pag ileen pɔɔn ki donn Uwonbɔr a yinl. Le Piita ke.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Yaayoo nna, U nan che ibol biɛn ŋaan baah ban man le.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Amaa Jɔnn le nan chinn dan ki nan mɔɔn ki sei Iziral yaab biɛn ke bin kpenn bi binbema, wee un fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Uba li fir len ke bininfob nan n laa gaa Uwonbɔr a nyunful laa? Bi gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ ke ti mu kaah nan gaa man nin le.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: Nin man biɛn ni, Waa nan ŋa niba wee naa jin siɛra, U wɔɔn U binbenŋam le ki che u taal nu ki che tiwanbukaar biɛn lo iyoo biɛn ki tenn ni tijiir takpem le ki che ni mɔ ni.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Jɔnn kaah nan nan be diɛr u toon nni, le u baan bi ke, Ni tiɛr ke n ye ŋmiɛ? Maa ye Uma. Aai, amaa Uwonbɔr a Masiya wan yin ŋɔɔrin n bɔɔn ki dan nin, maa kpaan nin n gbin u naatagi a ŋmimi.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Le ki ke bin fu bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful Yisa Masiya a yinlin buu. Le bi gɔɔ Piita ke un paar bi ban iweehn ilee.

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Hali Pɔɔl nin Banabasi kaah le pag man ni nmɔn biɛn na, ni nan pɔɔ le nin bin fir gɔɔ kuninfogbaluŋin nmɔn ke bi laa mann kumanug ki sei bi.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: Le Pɔɔl ji len ke, N nabum, nim ban ye Abiraham a yaabili, nin nim ban kaa ye Juu yaab wee ki san Uwonbɔr nan, tim bu le bi ta feegab a bɔrin ki sein.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Le Juu yaab biba nyan Antiɛg kan be Pisidiya a tiŋin, nin Ikoniɛm a tiŋin, ki dan ki nan pag ki sei kuninfogbaluŋin ki bii Pɔɔl nin Banabasi. Le bi gabir Pɔɔl itangbag ki fuul u nyan kitiŋ nmɔn a gbaam, ki tan gab ki nyi ke u kpo le.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Jerusalam yaab nin biyudam kaa nan biɛ Yisa, nin nima biɛn ni, bi nan ku u le ni gbeen bilinlem a bɔr tan wee bi jɔɔ karin likpakɔɔl daal biɛn nin.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Amaa bi bɔɔnɔɔbin kaah nan lagin ki gɔɔn win a bɔɔn le u fii ki jir kitiŋ nmɔn yaan. Le ni won kutaag le uma nin Banabasi jon Deba.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Hali baah kaa le nan kan u bir tan kpaan nin bin ku win, le bi bei Palati ke un ku wa.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Bi nan mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn le kitiŋ nmɔn yaan le bininfob takpem ŋa bi bɔɔnɔɔb. Le bi jir jon Lisitira, nin Ikoniɛm nin Antiɛg.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Baah nan ŋa Yisa man ki diɛ ke Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ kaah wɔɔn man nin, le bi tabir u lidapunpunl bu, ki ta u pii kukaagin.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: ki pɔɔgir bi bɔɔnɔɔbin a gbanann nin bi sufu ke bi mann sir bi yada bu nin ibamɔn na. Ni kpaa le nin ti kɔɔ tibɔmug kaarin takpem wee ki nin nan kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Amaa le Uwonbɔr fiin u nkumin.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Le Pɔɔl nin Banabasi nan gann biwonjiɛm a laguŋ ni biba le ki ŋa bi bisanliib tiwonjiɛndir biɛn nin, le ki lol bimɔb ki miɛ Uwonbɔr, ki ta bi ŋa Uyudaan wan wee bi gaa u jin nan a nɔɔn.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: le ban nan nɔɔ u ki nyan Galilii ki chaa Jerusalam nan, ni nan ga u le idaa biɛn. Daandaan nan bi ma nin ye u siɛra jib ki sei ti ninfob.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Baah nan choon Pisidiya a timin ki diɛ-n, ni bɔɔn bu le bi jon Pamfiliya a tiŋin.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Ti bei ni tibɔŋann nin le: Tan wee Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinl ki sei ti yaajamin.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: baah nan pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr Pega nan, le bi nyan niin ki jon Ataliɛn.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: U gbeen limɔsiinl nmɔn ki sei tim ban ye bi yaabili n na ki fiin Yisa nkumin na. Ke naah sɔb ilan yan kpiɛ milee n yaan ke: Fin le ye N bija; dinan le N ŋa a baa.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: Le bi nyan Ataliɛn ki kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki jir Antiɛg, nin yaan le bi nan ta bi ŋa Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanlin, nin baah tun itoon ki diɛ nbu.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Ibamɔn ke Uwonbɔr nan fiin u nkumin na, le waa buri, ni piin imɔbon nan yaan le: N yin ta tikaar tan ye kasi a kaar tan wee N nan siin limɔsiinl ki sei Defid nan le.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Baah nan foo Antiɛg nan, le bi bei biwonjiɛm a laguŋin Uwonbɔr kaah ta bi tun man, nin waah loor nsan man ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaab wee bi ŋa yada man.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Man le ni ji nan sɔb kugbɔŋ nan a niba yaan ki len ke: Ŋaa li che a poopendaau a gbanann n bur kukaagin ni.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Le bi nan paar nin yaan ki be nin bi bɔɔnɔɔbin ki yuur takpem.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Defid nan tun nan wee Uwonbɔr wɔɔn u ke un tun u tɔɔl a yonn nin le, ni bɔɔn bu le u kpo; le bi pii u u yaajam a karin, le u gbanann bur.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Amaa wan wee Uwonbɔr fiin u nkumin nin, u gbanann kaa buri.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Mambu, n nabum n ban ni li nyi ke Yisa bu le tibir a jiɛn per a mɔleen yin pag sei ni.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: Wan ŋa Yisa yada ke, u daan kan nŋmarima, amaa Moziz a siim n ma, kaa li fir sei uninfo nŋmarimi.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Ni li guu ni ba man, ki laa che bilinlem nan kaah len man nin n ji ni.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Ni lig man, nim bininfob ban yɔɔl nan, Ni che nin gar ni wee ni gbanann n kpo, N yin tun niwann niba le idaa nan yaan, le naa yin ŋa ni yada, hali ni ye uba le bei ni ke gbaa.

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Pɔɔl nin Banabasi kaah nyan kuwonjiɛndiig nmɔn yaan nin, le bininfob nmɔn balin bi ke bin pɔɔn biba wee ki nan jirin likpakɔɔl daal lan chaan nin, ki nan wɔɔn bi tibɔr nmɔn ki kpiɛ.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Bininfobin kaah nan worin nin, le Juu yaab takpem nin ibol yan nan jiɛn bi kaal wee ki nɔɔ Juu yaab a kaalin, ni nan nɔɔ Pɔɔl nin Banabasi. Le bi pag nin bi ki balin bi ke, bi li nɔɔ Uwonbɔr nin u nyunyunbasanlin.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Likpakɔɔl daal lan paan nin a daal kaah nan nan foo, ni nan gur wama le nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninfob biɛn n dan ki nan gbir Uyudaan a bɔr.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Juu yaab kaah nan kan kuninfogbaluŋin le ni ŋa bi lipiiponl, le bi pag bii Pɔɔl a wɔɔmin.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Le Pɔɔl nin Banabasi le pag nin lipoobil ke, Ti chinn pag tibɔr nan ki sei nim Juu yaab le. Amaa naah yii tibɔr nmɔn, ki ji tiɛr ke naa ŋan nin ni kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin bu, ni ma che ti jiɛn ni, le ki chaa ban kaa ye Juu yaab ban.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Uyudaan kaah ke ti ŋa man sɔ: Min le ŋa ni ban kaa ye Juu yaab n yaa weem, ke nim bu le Uwonbɔr yin fee dulinya yaab ki jaa.

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Ban kaa nan ye Juu yaabin kaah gbir man nin, le ni mɔŋ bi, le bi sei Uyudaan a bɔr nmɔn jilima. Le ban wee Uwonbɔr gann ke bin kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmin ŋa yada.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Le Uyudaan a bɔr nmɔn mann mɔɔn kitiŋ nmɔn a logil biɛn.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Amaa le Juu yaab fiin bipiib ban ye biwonsam ki ye biyudam nin kitiŋ nmɔn a yudam. Le bi mugir Pɔɔl nin Banabasi ki ŋɔɔr bi ki nyan bi tiŋin.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Le Pɔɔl nin Banabasi pir pir bi nɔɔi, ki sin yaar jon Ikoniɛm.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Le ni mɔŋ bibɔɔnɔɔbin le bi gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Bijab biba le nan nyan Judiya, ki dan Antiɛg, le ki nan wɔɔn biwonjiɛmin ke: See ke ni jii ni pun, ke Moziz a siimin kaah wɔɔn man nin, naa ye man ke, naa li kan nŋmarimi.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Le Pɔɔl nin Banabasi fa bi limɔkpel nin kinimaŋ. Le bi gann Pɔɔl nin Banabasi nin biwonjiɛm bibam, ke bin jo Jerusalam, ki tan kan itoon nin nin bisanliibin ki jiin nbinbaan nmɔn paam.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Le biwonjiɛm a laguŋin nan tun bi, le baah nan choon ki nɔɔ Fonisiya nin Samariya nan, le bi bei bi ban kaa ye Juu yaabin kaah kpenn bi binbem ki ta biba ki sei Uwonbɔr man. Tibɔr nmɔn nan mɔŋ biwonjiɛm pam.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: N-yonn man wee bi foon Jerusalam nan, le biwonjiɛm a laguŋ nin Yisa a toon nin bisanliibin gaa bi kuchɔŋ. Le Pɔɔl nin Banabasi bei bi niwann nan biɛn wee Uwonbɔr che bi tun.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Le biwonjiɛm ban nan si Farasii yaab a chaŋin fii sir ki ke, Ni kpaa le nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin jii bi pun, ki chee sei Moziz a siimin.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Le itoon nin nin bisanliibin lagin tab ki pag ki jiin nbinbaan nmɔn paam.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Bi nan pag ti ni yuur le Piita nan nan fii sir ki ke, N nabum, ni nyi ke iyoo yan jon nin, Uwonbɔr nan gannim le ni poolin ke n pag tibɔŋann nin ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaab, wee bin gbir ki ŋa yada.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Uwonbɔr wan nyi uninfosaal a sufu ni nin, u ma gaa ban kaa ye Juu yaab ki sei bi Kasi a Naaŋ, ke waah nan sei ti man nin.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: Waa wiɛr tim nin bima, nan bu-n U ŋunn bi sufu le bi yada bu.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Ke daandaan nan, ba ŋa wee ni ban ni mag Uwonbɔr aa? Wee ki ban ni ta kunaajadaag kan wee tim bee ti baam kaa fir tun ki ta tun biwonjiɛm nan naa?.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: Aai! Tim ŋa yada ke ti Yudaau Yisa a nyunyunbasanl bu le ti kan nŋmarim, ke bi mu kaah kan man nin.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Le kulaguŋin nmɔn yaab jir ŋmin soon, ki nin pelig Banabasi nin Pɔɔl kaah pag Uwonbɔr kaah ta bi tun ligaril a siɛra nin lijinjeel a toonl man, ban kaa ye Juu yaabin a sisiigin.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Baah nan pag ki diɛ n-yonn man nin, le Jemisi pag paab ke: N nabum, ni pelim man.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Sɔɔl tɔɔl nan chee Sitifin a kum buu. Lidaal nmɔn le limugil takpenl nan dan biwonjiɛm a laguŋ kan nan be Jerusalam n bu, le bi biɛn nan nyan ki yal jon Judiya nin Samariya a tinbumin, ki jiɛn Yisa a toon n yaa binba nin yaan.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Simɔnn bei ti Uwonbɔr a baga kaah be ban kaa ye Juu yaabin bu man na, nin waah nyan bi ni bibam wee bi ye u tɔɔl yaab man na.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Le bininfob ban san Uwonbɔr nan ta Sitifin ki tan pii, le ki mɔr ki fabin takpem u bu.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Bilinlem a mɔleen yin chee sei ke naah sɔb man nin na:.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Amaa le Sɔɔl ma piin ki bii biwonjiɛm a laguŋ. Ki kɔɔ bi niin yin ki chor bijab nin bipiib, ki tan loog bi kuloondiigin.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: Ni bɔɔn bu N li jirin, Ki mann ŋuun Defid a nanboŋ kan lu-n. Ki mann tan bii-n, ki che min sir.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Biwonjiɛmin kaah nan yalin, bi ni nan mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin le nan yaan biɛn wee bi jon.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: ni bu bininfob ban gur le ban Uyudaan, nin ban biɛn kaa ye Juu yaab wee ki jɔɔ n yinlin, Uyudaan wan tun man nmɔn le len man.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Le Filib jon Samariya a tiŋ kiba ni ki tan mɔɔn Masiya a bɔr ki sei bi.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: nan wee ti pii nyi ibin yan jon.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋin kaah gbir Filib a bɔrin ki ji kan ligaril a siɛra a toon yan wee u tun ni, le bi biɛn ni nan mann pel u bɔr nmɔn.

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Le Jemisi ji mann bei bi ke, Mambu min len le ke ti laa ta nan pɔɔ ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaab wee ki ban bin ta biba ki jirin Uwonbɔr ban nni.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: U na nyan nnaanbiim takpem bininfobin, le bi yigin papaab ki sin nyan, ki ji teb tigbanann kpiindam nin nwɔbim takpem kan kutebug.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Amaa ti li sɔb kugbɔŋ le ki sei bi, ki bei bi wee bi laa ji ibawol a jiiri, ki laa tun iwanchɔgin toon lipiibanlin bee lijabanlin ni, ki laa ŋma kuwankobug kan wee bi ŋmaan ŋmaan ki ku a nann ni, ki laa ji nchami.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Mambu nmɔn takpem nan be kitiŋ nmɔn yaan.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Nan bu-n Moziz a siimin, ti yaajam a yonn le u nan mɔɔn nsiimin kitiŋ biɛn nin, le bi karin nsiim nmɔn tiwonjiɛndirin likpakɔɔl daal biɛn.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Le uja u ba mu nan be kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, ki ye unyɔgdaan, le u yinl ye Simɔnn, u nan tun ligaril a toonl le ni gar Samariya yaab biɛn. U nan far u gbingbanl ke u ye uninfoja le.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Le itoon nin bisanliibin, nin biwonjiɛmin biɛn lol ki nyan bibam bi ni, ki tun bi ma nin Pɔɔl nin Banabasi le bi jon Antiɛg. Le bi nan gann Judasi, nin Silasi. Bijab bilee nmɔn le nan ye biwonjiɛm nmɔn a sanliib.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: bininfob pɔɔndam nin bilanjiim biɛn nan pelig u bɔr le ki len ke, Uja nan jɔɔ nween a pɔɔm le Npɔɔn Takpen.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Le bi nan sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tun bi: Yisa a tooi nin biwonjiɛm a sanliib nin ban ye ni nabum le sɔb kugbɔŋ nan, Ki tenn nim yadadam ban kaa ye Juu yaab wee ki be Antiɛg nin Siriya nin Silisiya: Ti poor ni.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Nan bu-n u nyɔgi a toon nmɔn nan ŋa bi ligaril le ke ni yuur, ni bu le che bi nan chee sei u ki nɔɔ u.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Ti gbir ke ti ni biba dan ni ban le naa ye tim le tun bi, ki jɔɔ tibɔr ti ba ki pag ni, le ni mugir ni ki ŋmarin ni potiɛl.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Amaa Filib kaah dan ki nan wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr a naam a bɔŋann nin, nin Yisa Masiya a yinlin, bijab nin bipiib nan ŋa u yada, le u fun bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Ni bu le ti chee ki gann bininfob biba bi ma nin ti jɔtiib Banabasi nin Pɔɔl ke bin dan ni ban.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Simɔnn tɔɔl nan ŋa u yadau, le u fun u nnyumin. Le u nɔɔ Filib ki choon nan yaan biɛn wee u chaa ki ga ligaril a siɛra a toon gbingbemin yan wee u ŋaan nin, le ni gar u.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: ban junn bi miɛfol ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a yinl bu-n.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Yisa a toon ban be Jerusalam nan kaah gbir ke Samariya yaab gaa Uwonbɔr a bɔrin ni, le bi tun Piita nin Jɔnn bi ban.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Nima bu le ti tun Judasi nin Silasi ke bin nan ta bi mɔb ki pag bei ni ta sɔb nan.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Baah nan tan foon, bi nan miɛ Uwonbɔr le ki sei bi ke bin gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Ni ŋan ki sei Kasi a Naaŋ nin tim wee ti laa che nin mug ni ki jo nsiim man sɔb nan ni:.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: nan bu-n Kasi a Naaŋin kaa la nan jiirin bi ni u ba bu; bi nan fun Uwonbɔr a nyunful Uyudaan Yisa a yinlin a binba le.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Ni laa ji tijiir tan wee bi ta mann ibawoli, ni laa ji nchami, ni laa ŋma kuwankobug kan wee bi ŋmaan ŋmaan ku a nann ni, wee ki laa tun iwanjaab toon lipiibanlin bee lijabanlin ni. Ni pɔɔn niba wee ki nya ni niɛ nan yaan na. Ni laan paara.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Le Piita nin Jɔnn nan ta bi nɔɔi ki paan bi bu, le bi gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Le bi tun bi, le bi jon jir Antiɛg, nin yaan le bi lagin biwonjiɛm a laguŋin biɛn ki sei be kugbɔŋin.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Simɔnn kaah nan kan Yisa a toon n kaah ta bi nɔɔi ki paan bi bu, wee Uwonbɔr sei bi Kinaaŋin, le u sei bi ilig.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Baah karin kugbɔŋin nin, le ni ŋa bi nmɔn nin baah ta pɔɔninba a kpaan yan ki sei bin bu.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: ki bei bi ke, Ni seim npɔɔn nmɔn ni, wee n mu ti ta n nɔɔi ki paan wan bu ke un gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Judasi nin Silasi ban mu nan ye bilinlemin, nan pag takpem le ki pɔɔg biwonjiɛm nmɔn a gbanann nin bi sufu.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Amaa le Piita jiin u ke, A tug ke a li fir ta ilig ki daa Uwonbɔr a piin yaa? Fin nin a ligi nmɔn n gabim yɔli!.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Baah nan yuur nin yaan wama-n, a bɔɔn bu, le biwonjiɛmin nan chiɛn bi nsan bu nin tikaar nin nposɔɔn, le bi jir kun ban tun bin ban.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Ŋaa tun niba ti toon nan yaan ni, nan bu-n a sufu kaa be nbamɔm nin Uwonbɔr i.

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Amaa le Pɔɔl nin Banabasi nan paar Antiɛg, le bi ma nin bi tab takpem nan mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin ki sei bininfob ki ji wɔɔn bi Uyudaan a bɔr.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Ni bu jiɛn a potiɛbir nmɔn na, ki kpenn a binbem wee ki miɛ Uyudaan. Ni ba ke u li jiɛn a potiɛbir nmɔn ki periŋ.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Ni nan yuur wamau le Pɔɔl nan bei Banabasi ke, Che ti jir tan gɔnn ti nabum ban biɛn be ntim man yaan wee ti nan mɔɔn Uyudaau a bɔr ki sei bi-n, ki kan baah tun man.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Nan bu-n n kan ki miin a gbee nin npooton le, ki ji ye tibir a yomu.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Le Banabasi nan ban bin ta Jɔnn wan a yinl ji ye Mag ki kpiɛ bi bu.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Le Simɔnn bei bi ke, Ni miɛ Uyudaan ki seim ni, wee nan wee ni len nan n laa kan mi.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: amaa le Pɔɔl kaa chee ke bin ta Jɔnn Mag ki kpiɛ, nan bu-n u nan jiɛn bi le Pamfiliya ki labir kun kaa ji kpiɛg bi bu itoon nin yaan ni.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
