local: Ni bɔɔn bu Piita nin Jɔnn kaah nan mɔɔn Uyudaan a bɔrin ni, le bi jir Jerusalam. Baah nan chaan bi nan mɔɔn Yisa a bɔŋann n le, Samariya a tinbumin.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Bi mɔkpel nmɔn kaah nan pɔɔn bu le nan che Pɔɔl nin Banabasi yiɛr tab. Le Banabasi ta Jɔnn Mag, le bi kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki jon Saipuru.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: N-yonn nmɔn le Uyudaan a malaka bei Filib ke, Fii ki li chaa ki tan nɔɔ a nɔɔgam paam a sam man nyan Jerusalam ki chaa Gaza a tiŋin ni.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Amaa le Pɔɔl mu gann Silasi. Le biwonjiɛmin ta bi ki ŋa Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanlin le bi jon.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Le Filib fii ki nyan ki piin u sanchonl, le ki to Itopiya a jau uba wan wee bi faarin ki miin u mu nyan lisanchonl le ki chaa kun. Uja nmɔn nan ye Itopiya a piibɔru wan a naam a yinl nan ye Kandasi a lig ŋmagib a gbansɔbib a yudaau le, u nan jon Jerusalam le un tan jiɛn Uwonbɔr.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Le u nɔɔ Siriya nin Silisiya a timin ki pɔɔgir biwonjiɛm a laginn a gbanann.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: ki nan chaa jir kun ki ka u toroko a poolin, le itaan dar u. Waah nan chaa n-yonn man nin, le u be karin ulinlenn Azaya kaah sɔb kugbɔŋ kan nin.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Le Kinaaŋ bei Filib ke, Fɔɔr ki mal toroko nan ban na.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Le u guun jon niin, ki gbir ujan kaah karin Uwonbɔr a linlenu Azaya a gbɔŋin. Le Filib baan u ke, A biɛr nan wee a karin nan a taai yaa.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Le u jiin ki ke, N yin ŋa nii ki biɛ ni taai yaa, wee naa ye uba le wɔɔm maa? Le u yin Filib ke un kɔɔn ki nan kal kpiɛ u bu.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin tibɔr tan wee lijafaalin nan be karin nmɔn ye nan le, Le u chee ki jon a li ke baah ti dar upee chaa bin tan ku man nin, Ki ŋmin ke baah chiɛ kipeebig a kor ke ki ŋmin man nin, kaa yaar u mɔbi.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: U fai a jimin, bi nan mɔu ibamɔn na. Ki junn u miɛfol dulinya nan yaan. Ke ŋma ji li fir pag u yaabili a bɔraa.

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Le lijafaal nmɔn baan Filib ke, Beim ni, ŋma wee ulinlenn nmɔn pagaa? U pag ki jiin u tɔɔl paam le bee uba paam maa.

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Le Filib piin nan yaan wee lijafaal nmɔn bi karin chaan, ki bei u Yisa a bɔŋann.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Baah nan nɔɔ nsan nmɔn ki chaa n-yonn man nin, le bi tan kan nnyum nba kaah dɔ, le lijafaal n bei Filib ke, Lig nnyum sɔ. Ba yin ŋa wee ŋaa li fum Uwonbɔr a nyunfulaa.

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Le lijafaalin che itaan nin sir, le u ma nin Filib jiir nnyumin yaan, le Filib fun u Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Le bi nan nyan nnyumin yaan ki don, le Uyudaan a Naaŋ ta Filib yaar jon, le lijafaalin kaa ji kan wi, le ki kɔɔ u toroko a poolin ki kun nin nmɔn.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Filib nin nan tan be Azotusi le, ki jin ntim ntim biɛn ki mɔɔn Yisa a bɔŋann hali nin waah nan nan foo Sizariya.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Le Pɔɔl foon Deba, le ki jon foo Lisitira, nan yaan wee u kan ubɔɔnɔɔr wan wee bi yi u ke Timoti. Wan a na nan ye Juu a ninfou ki ye yada daan ni, amaa le u baa ye Girig a ninfou.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Nnabum ban be Lisitira nin Ikoniɛm a timin nan pag Timoti pam.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Pɔɔl nan ban un ta Timoti le jo nsan nmɔn, ni ma bu le u nan jii u punl Juu yaab ban nan be niin nin bu, nan bu-n bi biɛn nan nyi Timoti a baa kaah ye Girig a jau.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Baah nan choon ntimin nin, le bi nan mɔɔn tibɔr tan wee itoon nin bisanliib ban be Jerusalam nan pag choo ki wɔɔn ke bi li nɔɔ-n.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Le biwonjiɛmin a yada nan pɔɔgir le bi kanl kpiɛg lidaal biɛn.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Kasi a Naaŋ kaah kaa nan chee ke bin mɔɔn tibɔrin nmɔn Asiya a yerin nin ni, le Pɔɔl nin u ninɔɔtabin nan nɔɔ Ferijiya nin Galantiya a tingbɔŋin.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Baah nan tan foo Misiya a timin a tarigin, bi ni nan ban bin kɔɔ Batiniya a timin le, amaa le Yisa a Naaŋ kaa chee ke bin kɔɔ.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Mambu che bi nan lagir Misiya a tiŋ bu ki jon Tirosi.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Kunyiɛg kaah nan foo-n le Pɔɔl kan zii, u zii nmɔn a poolin le Masedoniya a jau uba si ki balin u ke, Dan Masedoniya ki nan ter ti ni.

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Pɔɔl kaah kan zii nmɔn a bɔɔn, le ti ŋa sili weem ke ti jo Masedoniya, nan bu-n ti nan biɛ ke Uwonbɔr le ke ti jo nin yaan ki tan mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin ki sei nin yaan a ninfob.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Taah nan nyan Tirosi a tiŋin, ti nan jon limɔgilin le ki tan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki jon Samotara, le ni won kutaag le ti foo Niɛpolisi.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: Ti nan nyan nin yaan ki jon Filipi, kitiŋ nmɔn nan be Rom yaab a nɔɔn le ki ye kitiŋ takpeŋ Masedoniya a yerin nin. Ti nan be niin ki yuur wamau.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Likpakɔɔl daal le ti nɔɔ nyan kitiŋ nmɔn a timansanmɔnn ki jon nkpenn, ti nan ban nan yaan wee ti li miɛ Uwonbɔr le. Le ti kal ki piin ki pag tenn bipiib ban nan lagin niin nmɔn.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Bipiib ban nan ka pelig tibɔr nmɔn, bi ni u ba a yinl le nan ye Lidiya, u nan nyan Tayatira le, ki choon kɔɔr ikinken man yan a daag pɔɔ, ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr. Le Uyudaan loor u sufu le u chee tibɔr tan wee Pɔɔl pagin.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Taah nan fun u ma nin u niin yaab Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin, le u gɔɔ ti ke ti jo u niin. Le ki ke, Ni ye ni sin nyi ke n ŋa ti Yudaau yada ke, ni li chaan ki nan be n niin. Le u balin ti le ti jon.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Weemba le ti nan chaa baah miɛ Uwonbɔr nan yaan nin, taah nan chaa-n, le kibisiɛg ki ba to ti nsan, u nan ye uyom le. Le kinaaŋ be u bu le ki ŋa u uninligir. Waah nan ye uninligir nbu, u nan ga ilig takpem ki tenn u dam.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Le kibisiɛgin nɔɔ Pɔɔl nin tim ban gurin a bɔɔn ki tann papaab ke, Bininfob nan ye Uwonbɔr wan kaa jɔɔ kpiɛg nan a toontum le, le ki bei ni naah li ŋa man ki kan nŋmarim.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: U le nan ŋaan man le lidaal biɛn. Waah ŋaan man ni le Pɔɔl a ŋuul nan fii, le u gbiɛ lig u le ki bei kinaaŋ kan be u ni nmɔn ke, Yisa Masiya a yinl bu, n ke a nyam u ni. Libol nmɔn le kinaaŋin nyan u ni.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Kiyom bisiɛg nmɔn a dam kaah nan biɛ ke baa ji li kan ilig kibisiɛgin ban nin, le bi choo Pɔɔl nin Silasi ki dar bi jon kitiŋ nmɔn a bɔjib ban kinyaŋin ban jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm ban.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Baah ta bi foo ubɔjir ban n-yonn man nin, le bi bei u ke, Bininfob nan ye Juu yaab le, ki jɔɔ kuŋmaruŋ ti tiŋ nan yaan.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: ki wɔɔn ke ti chee ki li ŋaan likaal lan wee Rom yaab a siim kaa chee.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋin kpiɛ bibɔjibin bu, le bi bei bi ke bin pir Pɔɔl nin Silasi ki boo ba.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Baah nan boo bi nmɔn a bɔɔn le bi ta bi ki ŋa kuloondiigin, le ki bei uloondiiguurin ke u li mann guu bi nbamɔma.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Waah nan gbir man nin, le u ta bi ŋa kuloondiig kan be poolinpoolin nin, le ki baan bi tibaandaar.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Le kunyiɛgtasiig le Pɔɔl nin Silasi nan miɛ Uwonbɔr ki gaa liwonpagil a lai-n ki pagir Uwonbɔr, le ban loo kpiɛ bi bu-n nin pelig.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Libol nmɔn le kitiŋ dam, le kuloondiig nmɔn a fiɛl tɔɔl nan dam dam. Le tidir a timansanmɔɔŋi loor nba bu, le baah nan baan bi tibaandaar tan ni choo gɔgir lu.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Iyaayoo le wan guu kuloondiigin fii njiɛmin, ki miin tidir biɛn a timansanmɔɔŋi loor le ki yaa yaa, le u nyi ke baah loon ban nin biɛn guun na, le u nan fɔɔ u tɔb a jug ke un ku uba.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Amaa le Pɔɔl tiɛn ki ke, Laa sei a baa kidaŋi! Ti biɛn be doo.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Le uloondiiguurin ke bin sei nŋmi ni, le ki guun kɔɔ weem ki jegil, ki tan gbaan tum Pɔɔl nin Silasi a nintogil.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Le u nan ta bi ki nyan tan baan bi, N yin ŋa ni wee ki kan feegabaa.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Le bi jiin bei u ke, Ŋaam Uyudaan Yisa yada, wee ki kan feegab, fin nin a niin yaab biɛn.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Le bi nan pag Uyudaan a bɔr ki sei u ma nin ban be u niin nmɔn biɛn.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Lidaal a nyiɛg, le uloondiiguurin nan ŋunn bi biɛi; le u ma nin u niin yaab biɛn nan gaa Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Le u nan ta Pɔɔl nin Silasi ki kɔɔ u niin le ki sei bi tijiir; ni nan mɔŋ u le, nan bu-n u ma nin u niin yaab biɛn kaah nan ŋa Uwonbɔr yada nbu.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Naah nan won kutaagin, le Rom yaab a bɔjib nan tun bi tooi uloondiiguurin ban ke: Nyan bininfob nmɔn ki jaau.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Le uloondiiguurin bei Pɔɔl ke, Bibɔjib tun bi tooi ni ke n che fin nin Silasi nya kuloondiigin n-yaan na, ni li fir n nya. Ni li chaa nin nposɔɔn na.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Amaa le Pɔɔl bei bi ke: Baa la jin ti tibɔri, amaa bi won boo ti kuninfogbaluŋ a numin na, hali nin taah le ye Rom yaab nna, le ki won ta ti ki ŋa kuloondiigin, le daandaan nan bi nin ban bin woon ki nyan taa? Aai! See ke bi baantɔɔl dan ki nan nyan ti.

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Le itoon nin nan jir ki tan bei bibɔjibin man, baah nan gbir ke Pɔɔl nin Silasi ye Rom yaabin le bijawaam choo bi.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Le bi jon Pɔɔl nin Silasi ban ki tan fa bi tisandann, le ki nyan bi kuloondiigin, ki balin bi ke, bin nya bi tiŋin na.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Pɔɔl nin Silasi kaah nan nyan kuloondiig nmɔn a bɔɔn, bi nan jon tan kɔɔ Lidiya a niin le. Nin yaan le bi nan lagin biwonjiɛm ki pɔɔg bi sufu. Le bi nin fɔɔr kitiŋ nmɔn yaan.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Tɔ, le Pɔɔl nin Silasi nɔɔ Amfipolisi a tiŋin nin Apoloniya a tiŋin ki tan foo Tesalonika. Nin yaan le Juu yaab a wonjiɛndiig nan be.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Le Pɔɔl tan kɔɔ niin ke waah pii ti kɔɔ man ni. U nan ta ipkakɔɔ itaa le ki be niin, ki wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin a taai.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: ki wɔɔn ni taai ke ni kpaa talar le ke Masiya nji fala ki kpo ki fii nkumin, Yisa wan wee n pag u bɔr nan, u ma ye Masiya nmɔn.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: U nan pag ki kpenn Juu yaab biba a lam, le bi nin dan ki nan kpiɛ u ma Pɔɔl nin Silasi bu. Le Girig yaab ban jiɛn Uwonbɔr, nin bipiib ban jɔɔ jilima takpem nan kpiɛ bi bu.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Amaa Juu yaabin nan ŋa u lipiiponl le; ki jin ki lagin kinyaŋ a ninfobir, ki nan mug kitiŋ nmɔn yaan nin tifur, ki tan leg Jasonn a niin, ki ban Pɔɔl nin Silasi, ke bin nyan bi dan kuninfogbaluŋin ban.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Amaa baah kaa nan kan bi n-yonn man ni, le bi choo Jasonn nin nnabum biba, ki dar bi dan kitiŋin a ninkpeeb ban, ki tiɛn ke Bininfob nan le nan mugir bininfob ntim biɛn nin, le ki nan dan do nan mu.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: Le Jasonn gaa bi ki ŋa u niin. Le bi biɛn nan choon ki bii ubɔr Siiza a siim, ki ke ubɔr uba mu ji be le bi yi u ke Yisa.

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: Baah nan gbir man n-yonn man nin, le ni bii kuninfogbaluŋin nin biyudamin a sufu takpem.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Le biyudam nmɔn che Jasonn nin bi miɛnin yɔn ilig le bi ta bi jaa.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Naah nan bɔŋ n-yonn man nin, le biwonjiɛmin nan chabir Pɔɔl nin Silasi le bi jon Beriya. Baah nan foo-n le bi kɔɔ Juu yaab a wonjiɛndiigin.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Beriya a tiŋ a ninfob pel Pɔɔl a bɔrin ki jon Tesalonika yaaba, le ki gaa tibɔrin nin njiɛm takpem ki dibir imɔleen nmɔn lidaal biɛn ki lig njee Pɔɔl kaah pag nan nmɔn sin ye ibamɔn naa.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: Le Juu yaab takpem nan ŋa yada ki kpiɛ Girig a piib ban jɔɔ liyinl nin Girig a jab takpem bu.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Juu yaab ban be Tesalonika nan kaah gbir ke Pɔɔl be mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr le Beriya a tiŋin nin, le bi tɔɔl jon niin ki tan pag ki bii Pɔɔl, ki fiin kuninfogbaluŋin a ŋuui, ke bin ŋɔɔr wa.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Libonl nmɔn le biwonjiɛmin nan chiɛn Pɔɔl nin biba ki tan jaa bi limɔgil a gbaam, amaa le Silasi nin Timoti ma paar Beriya.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Bininfob ban nan chiɛn Pɔɔl nan ta u foo Atensi le, ki nin jir kun, le Pɔɔl bei bi ke bin tan bei Silasi nin Timoti ke bin dan u ban weem.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Pɔɔl kaah la nan be Atensi ki guu Silasi nin Timoti nni, le u kan kitiŋin kaah gbee nin itingban, le ni bii u sufu takpem.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Le u kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin ki pag tenn Juu yaab nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr nan tibɔr, ki ji chaa bi nyamin idaa biɛn, ki pag nin ban chaan nin yaan nmɔn tibɔr.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Le ban wɔɔnn wee bi yi biba ke Epikurusi, ki yi biba mu Sitoog nan, mu nan dan ki nan far limɔkpel nin Pɔɔl. Le bi ni biba nan pag nin tab ke, Wan be jabin nan mu ke baa? Le biba jiin ke, Ni nann ke u pag iweenchaan iba a bɔr le-n. Bi nan len man babu, Pɔɔl nan mɔɔn Yisa a bɔŋann nin le nin u kum a fiim.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Le bi ta Pɔɔl ki jon bibɔjib a kikaabol nan yaan wee bi yi ke, Aropagusi nan, ki bei Pɔɔl ke, Ti li fir biɛ tibɔchann tan wee a wɔɔn nan naa?.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: A pag tibɔchan le ti ban, le ti ban ti biɛ ti taai.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Le Pɔɔl fii sir Aropagusi a laguŋ nmɔn yaan, ki bei ke: Atensi yaab! N kan ni binbemin, ni ban liwonjiɛnl pam.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Nan bu-n maah gɔnn ni tiŋ nan, n dibir tiwann tan wee ni jiɛn nin le nbamɔm, hali ki nan kan libinbinl liba le ni sɔb libu ke, libinbinl nan si NWEEN MAN WEE TAA NYI nan a taalin le. Daandaan nan n ban n wɔɔn ni nween man wee ni jiɛn wee kaa sin nyi Win yaa bɔr le.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Nween man naan dulinya nin niwann nan biɛn be niin nin, u ma ye paab nin tum a yudaau, waa be tiwonjiɛndir tan wee bi ta bi nɔɔi maa yaan ni.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Uninfosaal kaa li fir ta u nɔɔ ki tun niba sei Wi, a li ke U ban niba le nni, nan bu-n U ma tɔɔl le tenn uninfosaal biɛn limiɛfol nin kufoorug nin niwann nan biɛn gur.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Uninfo uba ni le u che ibol biɛn nyan, ki che bi be dulinya nan a gbanann biɛn nin, le u lol n-yonn man wee un nan siin bi nin baah na li be nan yaan.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: Uwonbɔr nan ŋa man le ke bininfobin ban u, nba ke bi ban u ke bi li kan u. Amaa u bol kaa fɔg nin ti ni uba ban ni.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Nan bu-n U ma bu le ti fo ki choon ki be. Ke ni tɔɔl a landam biba kaah nan len ke, Tim ye Uwonbɔr a buwaab le.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Mambu taah ye Uwonbɔr a buwaab nan, ti laa tiɛr ke Uwonbɔr ye kuwan naan waŋ ke salima, bee tikur ninann bee litangbagil lan wee uninfo ta u lam bee u biɛm ki naan li nni.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Iyoo yan nan jon nin, wee bininfob kaa nan nyi Uwonbɔr nan, waa nan gar bi bir bu. Amaa daandaan nan, u ke uninfo biɛn nkpenn u binbema.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Nan bu-n, u pii siin lidaal lan wee u na yin che uninfo gannkaau nmɔn nji dulinya yaab tibɔr ibamɔn a sam bu. U jin ni siɛra ki sei uninfo biɛn na le ki fiin u nkumin.

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Baah gbir Pɔɔl pag nkum a fiim a bɔrin, le biba sɔɔl u, amaa le biba mu ke, Ti ban ti mann gbir nan wee a wɔɔn nan le jigin.

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Pɔɔl fii ki jiɛn bi kulaguŋ nmɔn yaan.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Bijab wama nan le nan ŋa Pɔɔl a bɔrin yada, le ki ŋa u bɔɔnɔɔb. Bi ni uba nan ye Dionasi, u nan ye bibɔjibin a ni uba le. Ki kpiɛ upii uba mu nan ŋa yada, le u yinl ye Damarisi, biba mu nan be ki kpiɛ.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le Pɔɔl nyan Atensi ki jon Korenti a tiŋin.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: Nin yaan le u nan kan Juu yaab a ninfo uba, le u yinl ye Akula, amaa le bi nan ma u Pɔntusi, le u ma nin u poo Pirisila la nyan Itali ki dan Korenti, nan bu-n ubɔr yudaan Kuladiyasi le nan sei bi bimɔb ke Juu yaab biɛn nya Rom a tiŋin na. Le Pɔɔl jon ke un tan kan bi.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: u mu kaah nan ye ugbanjab ke baah pii ti tun man nin, mambu le che u nan paar bi ban ki tun nin bi.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Le likpakɔɔl daal biɛn ke Pɔɔl n kɔɔ kuwonjiɛndiigin, ki far Juu yaab nin Girig yaab limɔkpel ki ban bin chee u bɔr tan wee u pag tenn bin.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Le Silasi nin Timoti nan nyan Masedoniya ki dan. Le Pɔɔl nin ta u toon yin ki jiɛn ki nin ta u saŋi biɛn ki sei Yisa a bɔr n-yaa mɔɔnim, ki wɔɔn Juu yaab ke Yisa le ye Masiya.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Amaa le Juu yaabin kaa chee Pɔɔl a bɔr nmɔn ni, le ki sugir u. Le Pɔɔl gbagbag u wan yeewaŋ, le ki ke, Ni cham li be ni tɔɔl a yuli bu! N tun maah ye n tun man le. Naah nyan dinan ki chaa, n yin jo ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ban le.

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Le Pɔɔl nyan kuwonjiɛndiig nmɔn yaan ki jon tan kɔɔ Tatusi Jasitusi a niin, u nan ye uwonjiɛn le.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Le kuwonjiɛndiig nmɔn a yudaau Kerepusi nin u niin yaab biɛn ŋa Uyudaan yada; le Korenti yaab takpem mu gbir u bɔr nmɔn le ki ŋa yada. Le bi fun bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Weemba a nyiɛg le Pɔɔl nan kan zii le Uyudaan pag nin u ke: Laa san bijawaami; wee ki li mɔɔn n bɔŋann nmɔn na, ki laa ŋmin ni.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: N be niŋ, le uba kaa be ki li legiŋ ki seiŋ kidaŋi, nan bu-n n jɔɔ bininfob takpem kitiŋ nan yaan.

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Mambu Pɔɔl nan be niin le libinl nin iŋmal iloob, ki wɔɔn bininfob Uwonbɔr a bɔr.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: N-yonn man wee Galio nan ye Akaya a tingbɔŋ a bɔr yudaau win, le Juu yaab nan lagin tab ki tan leg Pɔɔl, ki ta u jon bibɔjib ban.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: Ki tan bei ke, Uja nan le lar bininfob le bi jiɛn Uwonbɔr nin nsan man kaa nɔɔ ti siimi.

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Pɔɔl kaah ke un pag n-yonn man nin, le Galio bei Juu yaab nmɔn ke, Nim Juu yaab, ni ye tibɔr nan ye tibɔbir bee limɔyiil le ke, ni bi ŋan n mann pel.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Amaa naah ye ibinbaan ki jiin ni tɔɔl a mɔleen, nin ni yin, nin ni tɔɔl a siim paam nan ma, ni tɔɔl nmann tibɔr nmɔn na, maa li fir ji tibɔr nmɔn ni.

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Le ki che bi ŋɔɔr bi nyan tibɔr a ji bol nmɔn.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Le bi biɛn choo Sositenesi, wan ye kuwonjiɛndiig a yudaau ki boo u tibɔr a jibol, amaa le Galio a baga kaa be niin ni.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Pɔɔl nan be Korenti a tiŋin iyoo takpem. Le ki nan chabir biwonjiɛmin ki kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki jon Siriya. Le Pirisila nin Akula chiɛn u. Waah kaa la nan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin nin, le u koo u yul Senkariɛ a tiŋin nan bu-n waah poo tipoor tan nin bu.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Le bi nan chaa ki tan foo Efesusi, nan yaan wee Pɔɔl nan jaa Pirisila nin Akula nan, le u tɔɔl jon ki tan kɔɔ Juu yaab a wonjiɛndiigin, ki pag sei Juu yaab nmɔn.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Le bi nan bei u ke un laan paar bi ban iweehn ilee, amaa le waa chee.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: Amaa waah nan chabir bi n-yonn man nin, le u bei bi ke, Ni ye Uwonbɔr chee ke n ji li jirin ni ban. Le ki kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki nyan Efesusi.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: Waah tan foo Sizariya n-yonn man nin, le u don tan poor biwonjiɛm a laguŋ, le ki ji jiir Antiɛg.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: Waah jin n-yonn wama Antiɛg nmɔn a bɔɔn bu, le Pɔɔl ji nan nyan nin yaan ki choon ntim ntim Galantiya nin Ferijiya a tinbumin ki pɔɔgir bi bɔɔnɔɔbin a sufu.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Juu a jau uba le nan be, le u yinl ye Apolosi, amaa bi nan ma u Alɛgzandira le, le u nan dan Efesusi. U nan ye ubiɛndaan le, ki nyi Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ nbamɔm.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Bi pii nan wɔɔn u Uyudaan a sam ma, le u nan pag Yisa paam ke ni yeg, hali waah le nan nyi Jɔnn kaah nan fun bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin a binba-n nni.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Le u piin ki pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr nin lipoobil, Juu yaab a wonjiɛndiigin. Le Pirisila nin Akula gbir u bɔr nmɔn, le ki nan ta u kun ki tan mann wɔɔn u Uwonbɔr a sam nbamɔm.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Apolosi kaah nan ban un jo Akaya nan le biwonjiɛmin pɔɔg u gbanann ki sɔb kugbɔŋ ki sei bi bɔɔnɔɔb ban nan be niin nin ke, bin gaa mu kuchɔŋ. Waah tan foo niin n-yonn man nin, u sin nan jiir ban ŋa yada, Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl nbu le ki ye literil ki sei bi.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Nan bu-n uma nin Juu yaab kaah nan far limɔkpel nin tabin, le u yuur Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ, ki wɔɔn bi ke Yisa le sin ye Masiya, ki pag nin npɔɔn, ki nyaŋ bi kuninfogbaluŋin yaan.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Apolosi kaah la na be Korenti nan, le Pɔɔl nan nyan niin ki nɔɔ kisanbig ki jon Efesusi. Nin yaan le u kan biwonjiɛm biba.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: le ki baan bi ke, Naah nan ŋa yada-n ni nan gaa Kasi a Naaŋin waa? Le bi jiin ke, Aai. Taa pii la nyi ki gbir ke Kasi a Naaŋ be gbaa.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Le Pɔɔl baan bi ke, Ke bi nan fun ni Uwonbɔr a nyunful nii? Le bi bei u ke, Bi nan fun ti Jɔnn a nyunfulin le.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Le Pɔɔl bei bi ke, Jɔnn kaah nan fun bininfob Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin, ni wɔɔn ke bin kpenn bi binbem ki jiɛn bi toonbira. Le ki sin bei bi ke bin ŋaam wan yin ŋɔɔrin u bɔɔn nin yada, u ma ye Yisa.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Baah gbir man nin, le bi nin fun bi Uwonbɔr a nyunful, Uyudaan Yisa a yinl bu.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Le Pɔɔl ta u nɔɔi ki paan bi bu, le Kasi a Naaŋ jiirin bi bu, le bi nin pag ileen pɔɔn, ki ji pag ilinlen leen.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Bijab nmɔn biɛn nan ŋa bininfob piig nin bileeu.

eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Pɔɔl nan be Juu yaab a wonjiɛndiigin iŋmal itaa le, ki pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki tenn bi nin lipoobil, ki pɔɔn uba ke un kpenn bi potiɛl ki jiin Uwonbɔr a naamin paam.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Amaa bi ni biba nan ye tukangbirdam le; kaa chee Yisa a sam, ki pag tibɔbir kuninfogbaluŋin yaan ki jiin Uyudaan nmɔn paam. Le Pɔɔl fɔɔr jiɛn bi. Le ki ta biwonjiɛmin jon Tiranusi a wɔɔnim a diigin, le idaa biɛn ke u wɔɔn bi niin.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: Ni nan ye man le ki tan kpaa ibin ilee, mambu che Juu yaab nin Girig yaab ban biɛn nan be Asiya a tingbɔŋin nan gbir Uyudaan a bɔr nmɔn na.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Le Uwonbɔr nan che Pɔɔl tun ligaril a toon gbingbemin yan be i binba.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: le bininfob yuur u numpirim nin u wan yɔɔr tan wee u yee gbaa, ki paag bibaarib bu le bi wiɛn diɛr, le nnaanbiim nyan ki jiɛn bi.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn Juu yaab biba mu nan be, ki choon nyan nnaanbiim bininfobin, bi nan yi Uyudaan Yisa a yinl le ke bin nyan nnaanbiim, ki bei nnaanbiimin ke, Tim le sei ni bimɔb, Yisa wan wee Pɔɔl nan mɔɔn u bɔrin a yinlin bu, ni nyau.

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Nbijabum bilile ban nan ye Juu yaab a minmanib a yudaau Sikefa a naachimin le nan tun man.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Le weemba daal, le kinaanbiig nmɔn baan bi ke, N nyi Yisa, ki nyi Pɔɔl, ke ni mu, ni ye ŋmam maa.

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Le uja wan wee kinaanbiig be u bu nmɔn, fii ki choo bi, ki boo bi ki nyaŋ bi biɛn, le bi nan guun nyan iniin nmɔn yaan nin igbananbil le bi gbanann nya ncham.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: N-yonn man wee Juu yaab nin Girig yaab ban be Efesusi a tiŋin nin biɛn nan gbir man nin, le bijawaam choo bi, le bi sei Uyudaan Yisa a yinl jilima.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Le ban nan ye biwonjiɛmin takpem nan nyan npaan ni ki nan kpiir bi toonbir.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: Le binyɔgdam nin ta bi sabi ki dan nan nyɔɔ nŋmi kuninfogbaluŋ n-yaan, le bi bigin isab nmɔn a ligi ki miin i ye tikur a ligibim turpiŋmu le.

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: Ni ma nan che Uyudaan a bɔr nmɔn mann mɔɔn ki kɔɔ bininfobin, ki kpiɛ npɔɔn.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn kaah tun jon a bɔɔn, le Pɔɔl lol ke un nɔɔ Masedoniya nin Akaya ki jo Jerusalam. Waah nan be niin nmɔn a bɔɔn bu, le Pɔɔl bei u ba ke, See ke n gɔnn Rom mu.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: U nan tun Timoti nin Erasitusi ban ye u toon terib bilee-n le, Masedoniya, amaa le u paar Asiya a tiŋin.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le Efesusi yaab nan gaa tijar ki jiin Uyudaan Yisa a samin paam.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Uja uba nan be le u yinl ye Dimitirusi, u ti jɔɔ tikur ninann le ki maa bi tigbann yan wee bi yi ke Atemisi nan a dir ki kɔɔr tenn bitoontumin. Le bitoontum n mu ta itoon nmɔn ki kan tinyɔɔr takpem.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Le Dimitirusi nan yin bi biɛn ki lagin tab, nin ban biɛn tun itoon nmɔn a booli biɛn ki bei bi ke, N jɔtiib, ni nyi taah ga tinyɔɔr tan itoon nan yaan.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Ni tɔɔl kan ki gbir Pɔɔl nan a bol kaah kpenn bininfob takpem a lam ki lar bi ki kɔɔn kulɔŋ man Efesusi nin Asiya a tingbɔŋin biɛn. U ke tiwann naan wann tan wee bininfob maa-n kaa ye itingban ni.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: U ŋaan kidaŋ le u mɔleen nmɔn yaan, naa ye kidaŋ kan li bii ti nyaŋ a binba, amaa ti tingbanl takpenlin Atemisi a diigin a jilima yin war le, le litingbanlin tɔɔl, lan wee bi jiɛn Asiya a tingbɔŋin nin dulinya nan yaan a jilima li bii.

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Baah gbir man nmɔn, le bi ŋuui fii, le bi piin ki tann ki ke: Tim Efesusi yaab a tingbanl Atemisi ye litingbanl takpenl le.

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Naa nan yuuri le kitiŋ nmɔn a ninfob fu, ki gaan tijar. Le ki bib Pɔɔl a ninɔɔtab bilee Gayɔsi nin Arisitakusi ban nyan Masedoniya nan, le kuninfogbaluŋin kpab tab ke ninfo uba a danin ki kɔɔ kinaakoog takpoŋin.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Pɔɔl nan ban un kɔɔ kuninfogbaluŋin yaan le, amaa le biwonjiɛmin kaa nan chee un kɔɔ.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Hali kitiŋ nmɔn a yudam nin Pɔɔl a jɔtiib nan tun itoon le ki balin u ke Pɔɔl nmɔra, ki laa kɔɔ kuninfogbaluŋin yaan ni.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Iyaayoo kulaguŋ nmɔn nan jɔɔ kuŋmaruŋ le: Biba nan tann niba bu le biba mu tann nitɔ bu. Bininfob takpem kaa nan nyi nan bu wee bi be niin ni.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Le Juu yaab nyan uja uba le bi yi u ke Alɛgzanda, ki siin u bi nintogil ke un pag bi bu, le Alɛgzanda don u nɔɔi papaab, ki ban un pag ki sei kuninfogbaluŋin, ki nyan bi limɔkpel nmɔn yaan.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Amaa baah nan nan biɛ ke u ye Juu a ninfou le-n, le bi biɛn tann ki pag bɔr tiba ke, Tim Efesusi yaab a tingbanl Atemisi ye litingbanl takpenl le! Bi nan tiɛn man ki tan kpaa tikur tilee le gbaa.

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Ni joorimin le kitiŋ nmɔn a gbansɔbiru uba nan che kuninfogbaluŋin ŋmin, le u baan bi ke: Nim Efesusi yaab, uba be kutingbɔŋ nan yaan wee kaa nyi ke nim Efesusi yaab le guu Atemisi a diig nan nin litingbanl lan nyan papaab ki nan lu nan naa.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Nima bu uba kaa be ki li fir niɛ ni nan a bamɔn bu. Ni ŋan nin ni mɔr ki laa ban jegjegi.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Ni ta bininfob nan dan doo, amaa baa sun ti wonjiɛndiig a wan ni, bee ki sug ti tingbanl li.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Ni ye Dimitirusi nin u toontun tabin jɔɔ tibɔr nin uba ke, tibɔr a ji bol be, bibɔjib mu be. Bi li fir jo ki tan poor tab bi ban.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: Amaa ni ye ni jɔɔ tibɔr tan kaa ye ni ta ti jo bibɔjib nmɔn ban ke, ni ŋan nin ni jo ni tɔɔl a yudam ban.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Ke naah ye man nin, ti be kidaŋin le, bi li ji tim tibɔr kufuug kan wee ti ŋa dinan nan bu. Le taa yin fir wɔɔn kufuug nmɔn a taai, taa yin nyi taah li len man ni.

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: Waah len man nmɔn, le u che bi biɛn fɔɔr.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Kufuug nmɔn kaah nan ŋmin nin, le Pɔɔl yin biwonjiɛm, le ki pɔɔg bi gbanann, ki chabir bi, le ki jon Masedoniya a tingbɔŋin.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: U nan chonn ntim man be niin nmɔn le, ki wɔɔn biwonjiɛm ban be niin nmɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr takpem, ki pɔɔg bi sufu, ni joorimin le u tan foo Giresi.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: ki be niin iŋmal itaa. U nan ŋa u sili le ke un jo Siriya, amaa le ki gbir ke Juu yaab lolig nlolbirim le u bu, le u lol ke, un jir nɔɔ Masedoniya paam le.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Pirusi wan nyan Beriya a bija Sopata, nin Arisitakusi nin Sekondusi, ban nyan Tesalonika, nin Gayɔsi wan nyan Deba, nin Timoti nin Tachikusi nin Tirofimusi ban nyan Asiya a tingbɔŋin nin, bi ma nan nɔɔ Pɔɔl ki jon.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Le bijab nan, nan liir nsan ki tan guur ti Tirosi a tiŋin.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Amaa Kuchiig kan wee bi ti ŋma boroboro kan kaa jɔɔ nnyɔg nni a bɔɔn bu le ti kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ, ki nyan Filipi ki chaa Tirosi. Ni ween iŋmu daal le ti foo bi ban Tirosi, ki paar be niin ki tan kpaa iweehn ilile.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Likpakɔɔl a weemba daal le ti lagin tab, ke ti ji ti yudaau a jiir. Le Pɔɔl pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr digdig nin kunyiɛgtasiig, nan bu-n u nan ban ni won ke un jo ni ba yaan le.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Bi nan nyɔɔ filaam le takpem kudiititɔrug kan yaan wee ti nan lagin nin a poolin.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Le Unaachipɔɔn uba nan be niin, le u yinl ye Itikusi, le u ka kudiititɔrug a takorobonl bu. Le Pɔɔl pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki yuur, le u ka pelig, le njiɛm nan choo u, le u nyan kudiititɔrug kan kpiɛ titaa-n a paab ki tan lu tum. Le bi jiir ki tan yuur u, ki miin u kpou.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Le Pɔɔl nyan kudiititɔrugin a paab, ki jiir ki nan wugin u bu, ki ta u nɔɔi bib u, ki len ke, Ni laa che ni sufu n bii, u fo.

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Le ki jir don kudiititɔrugin a paab ki tan jin tijiir. Ni bɔɔn bu le u ji pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr hali nin naah nan won, le u jaa bi ki jon.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Le bi nan yuur unaachipɔɔn nmɔn ke u fo, le bi ta u ki kun nin npopeen takpem.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Le ti chinn jon ki tan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ, ki jon Asosi. Nin yaan le ti nan tan yin yuur Pɔɔl, u nan ŋa man a sili le, nan bu-n, u nan ban un choon tum le ki foo niin.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: Waah nan kenn ti Asosi n-yonn man ni, le ti yuur u ki ŋa kuŋaluŋin yaan ki jon Mitilene.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Le ni won kutaag le ti nyan niin ki foo Kiosi. Le ni nan won kutaag le ti foo Samosi, ki ji won kutaag le ti foo Miletusi.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Pɔɔl kaa nan ban un sir Efesusi, nan bu-n waa nan ban un yuur Asiya a tiŋin ni, le ki ban weem ke u bi li fir ke, un bin foo Jerusalam, lidaal lan wee bi yi ke, Pentikosi nan a daal.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Le Pɔɔl tun ntoon, ke bin nya Miletusi a tiŋin, ki jo Efesusi ki tan yin biwonjiɛn ninkpeebin ni.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Baah tan foo n-yonn man nin, le u bei bi ke: Ni nyi n binbem kaah nan ye man, maah nan be ni ban nin, maah foon Asiya a tingbɔŋ nan yaan lidaal lan ki nan kpaa dinan.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: N tun Uwonbɔr a toon le nin jiininba takpem nin tinyinyinn, nin nima biɛn ni n kan limagil takpem Juu yaab ban na.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Ni nyi maah pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr, ki wɔɔn ni bininfobin nin ni niin yin. N nan pag ni tibɔr tan biɛn li ter ni le kaa gur tiba.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: N bei Juu yaab nin Girig yaab biɛn ke bin kpenn bi binbema ki jir Uwonbɔr ban, ki ŋa ti Yudaau Yisa yada.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Tɔ, daandaan nan Kinaaŋin le che n chaa Jerusalam, amaa maa nyi nan ta li ŋaam nin yaan ni.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: Amaa maah choon ntim man biɛn nin, Kasi a Naaŋ ti chinn beim le ke kuloondiig nin fala be n nintogil ki guum.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Amaa n tiɛr ki n miɛfol ye yɔli ki seim, amaa nan kpaa le ye n tun itoon yan wee Uyudaan Yisa nan ta seimin ki diɛ, itoon nmɔn le ye n wɔɔn bininfob tibɔŋann nmɔn Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: Daandaan nan n nyi ke maah nan choon ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a naam a bɔrin ki tenn nim ban nin, ni ni uba kaa ji li kan mi.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Mambu le n bei ni dinan ke, nnɔɔ kaa be uninfosaal biɛn a chamin ni.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Nan bu-n n pag ni Uwonbɔr a bam a bɔrin biɛn ki wɔɔn na, kaa gur tiba.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Ni li ŋmag niba nbamɔm nin ipee yan biɛn wee Kasi a Naaŋ ta ŋubin ni nɔɔn ke ni li guu-n. Ni li guu Uwonbɔr a ninfobin na, ban wee U ta U cham ki daa-n.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: N nyi ke maa nan be a bɔɔn ke, tibɔkardam ban nann igbingbelin na li dan ni sisiigin, ki bii Uwonbɔr a ninfob.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: N-yonn chaan le ni ni biba mu na li nya ni tɔɔl a gbanann nin, ki por tibɔkar ki jiin ibamɔn a wɔɔnimin paam, ki lar biwonjiɛm ki che bin nɔɔ bi.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Ni bu, ni li guu ni ba! Ki li tiɛr ke ibin itaa sɔ le n le kpaan ni biɛn nan nin tinyinyinn, nween nin kunyiɛg.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: Daandaan nan, n jɔɔ ni ki ŋubin Uwonbɔr a nɔɔn nin U nyunyunbasanl a mɔleen yin bu, U bɔr nmɔn le li fir che ni nɔɔ nbamɔm, ki li fir sei nim ban biɛn wee u ŋa ni u kasi a ninfobin lifaal.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: Maah nan be ni ban nin, maa nan ban uba a kurninann ni bee salima kaa ban uba a wanyeewaŋi.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Ni tɔɔl nyi ke min tɔɔl a nɔɔi le n jɔɔ tun, ki lig nba nin n ninɔɔtab.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Maah tun man nmɔn n wɔɔn ni le ke see ke ti tun takpem wee ki ter bilanjiim, ni li tiɛr Uyudaan Yisa kaah nan len ke: Tikaar be wan pu bu ki jon wan gaan na. .

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: Pɔɔl kaah nan pag u bɔr nmɔn ki diɛ n-yonn man nin, le uma nin bi ma biɛn lagin ki gbaan tum ki miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Bi biɛn nan mɔr le, ki yuur bi nɔɔi paab ki poor u.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: Nan nan bii bi sufu ye waah nan len ke baa ji li nyi ki kan u num paamin bu. Le bi chiɛn u ki jon limɔgilin ke un tan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Baah nan chabir ti a bɔɔn bu, le ti kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki don limɔgil ki tan foo Kosi. Le ni won kutaag le ti jon Rodesi, ki ji nyan niin ki jon Patara.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Niin le ti kan kuŋaluŋ kan chaa Fonisiya, le ti kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin ki chaa.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Taah nan tan ga Saipuru n-yonn man ni, le ti nɔɔ nnɔɔganpaam ki jon Siriya. Le ki tan foo Taya nan yaan wee bi nan tan yin nyan kuŋaluŋin a wan yɔɔr.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Niin le ti kan biwonjiɛm, le ki paar bi ban ki tan kpaa iweehn ilile. Le Kinaaŋ che bi balin Pɔɔl ke, un laa jo Jerusalami.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Amaa le ti chaam a yonn nan foo, le biwonjiɛmin nin bipiib nin bi buwaab chiɛn ti ki tan nyan kitiŋ n yaan. Le ti biɛn gbaan limɔgilin a gbaam ki miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: Taah nan chabir tab a bɔɔn, le ti jon tan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin ki chaa, le bi biɛn jir kun.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: Le ti nyan Taya ki foo Tolemasi, ki jon tan poor biwonjiɛmin, le ki paar bi ban weemba.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Le ni won kutaag le ti nyan Tolemasi, ki foo Sizariya, ki jon Filib wan ye umɔleenmɔɔn a niin ki tan be. U nan ye bijab bilile ban wee bi nan gann bi ke bi li tol tijiir n-yaa ni u ba le.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: U nan jɔɔ nbisiɛm mina le ke maa la kun bijabi, le bi pag ilinlen leen.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Ti nan be niin ki yuur wamau, ni bɔɔn bu le Uwonbɔr a linlenu wan wee bi yi u ke Agabu nan nyan Judiya ki dan.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Le ki nan ta Pɔɔl a changbam, ki lol u tɔɔl a taai nin u nɔɔi, le ki len ke, Kasi a Naaŋ len ke, Juu yaab ban be Jerusalam yin lol nchangban nan daan man le ki ta u ŋubin ban kaa ye Juu yaab a nɔɔn.

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: Taah nan gbir man nmɔn, le tim nin ban biɛn nan be niin nin, balin Pɔɔl ke u laa ji chaa Jerusalami.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Amaa le Pɔɔl jiin ke, Ba ŋa wee ni mɔr ki ku n gbanann naa? Naa ye ba tan yin lolimin a binba bu le n chaa, amaa n ŋa sili nin n tan kpo Jerusalam Uyudaan Yisa a yinl buu.

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: Waah len man ni, le ti jir ŋmin, amaa le ki len ke, Che Uyudaan a bam n tun.

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le ti ŋa sili ki jon Jerusalam.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Le biwonjiɛm ban nyan Sizariya a ni biba chiɛn ti jon Minason a niin nan yaan wee ti nan tan yin be. Minason nmɔn mu pii nan ye Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔru le, u nan ye Saipuru a ninfou le ki ye chinchinn a wonjiɛmin a ni uba.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: Taah foo Jerusalam n-yonn man nin, le biwonjiɛm ban nan be niin nin gaa ti nin npopeen.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Le ni won kutaag, le Pɔɔl nin tim ban gurin jon ke ti tan poor Jemisi. Biwonjiɛn ninkpeebin mu nan be niin.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Le Pɔɔl poor bi, ki wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr kaah tun nan ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaabin u ma Pɔɔl a toon yin.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: Baah gbir u bɔr nmɔn nin, le bi biɛn pag Uwonbɔr. Le ki bei Pɔɔl ke: Ti na a big, a kan Juu yaab turtur kaah ŋa yada man na, bi biɛn ji ŋmag nsiimin le kenken.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Biba nan bei bi ke, a wɔɔn Juu yaab ban be ban kaa ye Juu yaab a timin nin, ke bin yii Moziz a siimin na, ki ji bei bi ke, bi laa jiil bi buwaab a pun yi, ki laa nɔɔ Juu yaab a kaali.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Ti yin ŋa nii? Bi le li gbir ke a foon na.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: ni bu ti beiŋ man ke, a tum man. Bijab bina le be ti ban do ki poo tipoor.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Ta bijab nan wee fin nin bi ma li chaa ki tan mann ni bau, wee a yɔ baah jin lipɔnl lan biɛn, wee bin koo bi yuli, wee bininfob biɛn biɛ ke baa pag man ki jiin a paamin kaa ye ibamɔn ni, nan bu-n, fin tɔɔl nɔɔ nsiimin le.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: Amaa ban kaa ye Juu yaab wee ki ye biwonjiɛm nan ma, ti pii sɔb kugbɔŋ sei bi ki wɔɔn bi ke, bi laa ji tijiir tan wee bi ta mann libawoli, bee ki ŋman nchami, bee kuwankobug kan wee bi ŋmaan ŋmaan ku, ki laa tun iwanjaab toon lipiibanlin bee lijabanlin ni.

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Le ni won kutaag le Pɔɔl ta bijab nmɔn, le u ma nin bi ma mann biba, le ki kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin, ke bin wɔɔn bi idaa yan wee bi yin be ki tan kpaa lidaal lan wee biba a manim na li diɛ wee bin mann kumanug ki sei bi biɛn.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: Iweehn ilile nmɔn kaah nan nan mal ndiɛmin, le Juu yaab ban nyan Asiya ki dan nin kan Pɔɔl iwonjiɛn niin. Le ki fiin kuninfogbaluŋin a ŋuui Pɔɔl bu le ki choo u.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: le ki ji tann ke Iziral yaab, ni ter ti ni! Uja nan le choon ilog biɛn, ki pag ki bii ti ninfob ki bii ti siim nin iwonjiɛn niin nin paam, nan ji kpiɛ ye ti jɔɔ ban kaa ye Juu yaabin chaan tiwonjiɛn niin nan yaan, ki bii ti kasi a niin nmɔn.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: N nabum nin n baam, ni gbirim n siɛra nan.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Le kitiŋin yaab biɛn a ŋuui nan fii, le bininfobin biɛn guun ki nyan ilog biɛn. Ki nan leg Pɔɔl, ki choo u, ki dar u nyan iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan. Libol nmɔn le bi loon iwonjiɛn niin ni a timansanmɔɔŋi.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Baah gbir u pag bi tɔɔl a leen yin ni, le bi ŋmin soon. Le Pɔɔl len ke.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Le bininfobin nin ban bin ku Pɔɔl, iyaayoo le Rom a tɔtogib a yudaau wan nan ŋmag bitɔtogib tur nan gbir ke Jerusalam yaab biɛn be gaan tijar le.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: N ye Juu a ninfou le, bi mam Tarsusi kan be Silisiya a tingbɔŋin nin le, amaa n nan ŋa uninfo Jerusalam nan le. Gamaliɛl le nan wɔɔnim. U nan wɔɔnim ti yaajam a siim le chib chib, le n nɔɔ Uwonbɔr nin linunmanl, ke ni biɛn nan kaah nɔɔ U dinan man nan.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Le u ŋa weem ki fii ki ta bitɔtogib ninkpeeb biba nin bitɔtogib, le bi daa jon kuninfogbaluŋin ban. Bininfobin kaah kan u ma nin bitɔtogibin n-yonn man ni, le bi jiɛn Pɔɔl a boor.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: N nan mugir ki ku ban nɔɔ Yisa a samin le, ki ji chor bijab nin bipiib ki loog kuloondiigin.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Le bitɔtogib nmɔn a yudaau jon ki tan choo Pɔɔl, ki sei bi mɔb ke bin ta tikulor tilee ki baan mu. Le ki baan bi uninfo nmɔn waah ye wan nin waah galin nan.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: a li ke kumanug a yudaau nin ti sanliib biɛn kaah li fir ŋa maah pag tan nan biɛn a siɛradam ke ni sin ye ibamɔn le-n. Hali gbaa n nan tan gaa tigbann bi ban ki ta tan sei bi nabum ban be Damasiko nan, ke n tan choo ban be niin ki nɔɔ Yisa nan, ki ta bi jirin Jerusalam ki nan ŋa bi kuloondiigin wee bin dar bi tuli.

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋin yaan biba tann Pɔɔl bu balbal, balbal, bi ŋmaruŋ nmɔn bu che bitɔtogib a yudaau win kaa nan biɛ ni taai nbamɔmi. Mambu le u sei bimɔb ke bin ta u ŋa bitɔtogib a niin na.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: Maah chaa ki tan mal Damasiko nwosisiigin le nwonween takpem nyan papaab ki nan won n bu ki jim.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Baah nan tan foo ntisamɔn a nan yaan wee bi tɔɔg kɔɔ-n, le bitɔtogibin yuur Pɔɔl papaab, nan bu-n, kuninfogbaluŋin nan ban bin ku u le.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Le n lu tum, ki gbir likukɔl yin ke, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, ba ŋa wee a mugirim man naa.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Le bi nin nɔɔ bitɔtogib a bɔɔn ki tann ke, Kum uja nan na.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Le n jiin ki baan u ke, Uyudaan a ye ŋmiɛ? Le u beim ke, Min Nazarif a ninfou Yisa le a mugir nmɔn.

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Bitɔtogibin kaah nan tan yin ta u kɔɔ bi niin n-yonn man nin, le u baan bitɔtogib nmɔn a yudaau ke, Npaan be nin n pag niba ki sei ŋaa? Le u jiin ki baan u ke, A fin pag Girig yaab a leen yaa.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Bininfob ban nan chiɛmin nan kan nwonween nin na, amaa baa nan biɛ wan nan pag ki tenn min a niɛn ni.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Ŋaa ye Ijibir a ninfou wan che bininfob ta ikɔn dan kitiŋ nan yaan ki ta bitɔtogib turina, ki jon kulɔŋ ki tan be n-yonn man nan jon n lee.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Le n baan u ke, Uyudaau n yin ŋa nii? Le Uyudaan beim ke, Fiim paab ba ki li chaa Damasiko. Bi ta li wɔɔniŋ nan biɛn wee Uwonbɔr ke a ŋa.

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Le Pɔɔl jiin u ke, N ye Juu a ninfou le, n nyan Tarsusi kan be Silisiya a tingbɔŋin nin le, n ye kitintakpeŋ a ninfou le, n baliŋ le, che n pag ki sei bininfob nan.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: N ninɔɔtab le nan ŋmag n nɔɔ ki dam foo Damasiko, nan bu-n nwonween man nan nyagir n num paamin nan jɔɔn ma.

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Le bitɔtogib a yudaan win sei npaan. Le Pɔɔl sir bitɔtogibin a diig a tisanmɔnn nan yaan wee bi tɔɔg ki kɔɔ-n, ki don u nɔɔ bininfobin bu ke bin ŋmin na. Baah ŋmin n-yonn man nin, le u pag sei bi Juu yaab a leen yin, ke:.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: Uja uba mu a yinl ye Ananiɛsi nan dan ke un nan kam. U nan ŋmag Uwonbɔr a siimin nbamɔm, ni bu Juu yaab ban nan be Damasiko nan tenn u jilima le takpem.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Le u nan sir n ban ki ke, N nabig, Sɔɔl, ligira. Libonl nmɔn le n ligir ki kan u.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Le u len ke, Uwonbɔr wan ye ti yaajam a Weemin pii ganiŋ le, ke a biɛ u bam, ki kan U ninfo Buchiu, ki ji gbir u mu a mɔnn.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: A li ŋa u siɛradaau, ki li pag tenn bininfob biɛn ŋaah kan nan nin ŋaah gbir nan.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: A ji ka ki guu baa? Fiim, wee n fuŋ Uwonbɔr a nyunful, wee a yin Yisa a yinl wee un ŋunn a bir.

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: Maah nan jir Jerusalam, ki tan kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan, ki miɛ Uwonbɔr nan le n kan zii.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: zii nmɔn yaan le n kan Uyudaan, le u beim ke. Ŋa weema! Nya Jerusalam libol libol nan na, nan bu-n ni ye a bei Jerusalam yaab n bɔr ke, baa yin gaa tibɔr nmɔn ni.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Le n jiin u ke, Uyudaau, bininfob pii nyi ke min le nan chonn kɔɔ tiwonjiɛndirin ki chor bininfob ban nɔɔ a samin, ki boo bi ki kɔɔn bi tiloondirin.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: Le baah nan ke bin ku a siɛradaau Sitifin nin, n tɔɔl nan be niin, ki chee ke bin ku mu. Min le ji nan ŋmag ban nan ku win a wan yɔɔr.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Le Uyudaan beim ke, Fin le li chaau; min le yin tuŋ nan fɔg bininfob ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ban. .

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: Kuninfogbaluŋin le maan biba ki pel waah pag man nin nmɔn, nin waah nan nan len ni niɛ. Le bi don bi likukɔl ki tann papaab ke, Uja nan kaa ŋan nin u li be kutingbɔŋ nan yaan ni. Ni ku mu man.

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Baah nan be tann ki pir bi daalam gabir, ki liɛr titann ki kur kutangbaag jaar paabin.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: le bitɔtogib a yudaau n sei bimɔb ke bin ta Pɔɔl ki kɔɔn bitɔtogib a niin na ki ji bei bi ke, bin boo mu, wee ki baan u wee win biɛ tibir tan wee u tun wee Juu yaab tann titintann nmɔn.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Le bi tann u ki ke bin boo, le Pɔɔl baan bitɔtogib a sanliiru wan nan ŋmag bitɔtogib kobig wee ki si u ban nin ke, Ni ŋan nin ni boo Rom a tinbig wee baa la jin u tibɔr ki kan u galin yaa.

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Bitɔtogib a ninkpelu nmɔn kaah gbir man ni, le u jon bi yudaau n ban, ki tan baan u ke, A ni yin ŋa nii? Rom a ninfou le ye uja nmɔn.

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Le bitɔtogib a yudaau nmɔn mu fii, ki tan baan Pɔɔl ke, Beim ni a ye Rom a ninfou lee? Le Pɔɔl chee ke Nn.

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Le bitɔtogib a yudaau nmɔn bei u ke, Min nan yɔn ilig takpen le, ki kpann Rom a ninfou. Amaa le Pɔɔl bei u ke, Min bu ke n ye Rom a tinbig le.

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Libol nmɔn le bitɔtogib ban nan ban bin baan u ibinbaan nin fɔr ki jir bi bɔɔn. Bitɔtogib a yudaau win tɔɔl a lam nan yug le, waah nan nan biɛ ke Pɔɔl ye Rom a tinbig wee u che bi lol u tikulorin bu.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: Naah nan won kutaag wee bitɔtogib a yudaau nmɔn nan ban un biɛ igalin yan wee Juu yaab pag ki jiin Pɔɔl paam nan a bamɔn chib chibin, le ni nan won kutaag, le u che bi lor u, le ki sin sei bimɔb ke, kumanug a ninkpeeb nin Juu yaab a yudamin biɛn lagin ni, le ki che Pɔɔl dan ki nan sir bi nintogil.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Le Pɔɔl lig Juu yaab a yudam nmɔn chiir ki nan len ke, N nabum, maah be ki nan kpaa dinan, n biɛ n sufu ni ke n tun ki gbeen n toon kaah ye man Uwonbɔr ban na.

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Waah len man nmɔn, le kumanug a yudaau Ananiɛsi bei ban si ki mal Pɔɔl ke, bin faa Pɔɔl a mɔba.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Le Pɔɔl bei u ke, Uwonbɔr li faaŋ, a be le ke ligonl lan kaa ŋan wee bi ta nfatam pɔn li nan le! A ka do le ki ji min tibɔr, nsiim kaah wɔɔn man, amaa le fin tɔɔl bii nsiim nmɔn ki ke bin faam.

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Le ban nan si mal Pɔɔl n nan tiɛn u bu ke, A ye ŋmiɛ ki nan sugir kumanug a yudaau waa.

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Le Pɔɔl jiin bi ke, N nabum, maa nyi ke u ye kumanug a yudaau wi. Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Naa ŋan nin a pag tibɔbir ki jiin bininfob a yudaau paami. .

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Pɔɔl kaah biɛ ke Juu yaab a yudam nmɔn biba ye Sadusim yaab, wee biba mu ye Farasiimin, le u pag npɔɔn ke, N nabum, n ye Farasii a ninfou le, le ki ye Farasii a big. Le baah jim tibɔr tan bu ye maah jɔɔ tama ke bitakpiib naah li fii nkumin nin bu.

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: Waah pag man nmɔn le limɔkpel dan Sadusi yaab nin Farasii yaabin a sisiigin, le bi laguŋ nmɔn bɔgir.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Le bi tintann nin nan mann ki do ki kpiɛg. Le ban wɔɔnn Uwonbɔr a siim ki ye Farasiim nan a ni biba fii sir, ki pag nin linunmanl ke, Taa kan uja nan a biri. Naa nyi ke nba ke kinaaŋ bee malaka le pag ki sei waa.

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Le limɔkpel nmɔn nan fii takpem, hali ke bijawaam nan choo bitɔtogib a yudaau, u nan nyi ke bin yin choo Pɔɔl dar bɔgir le. Mambu le u sei bimɔb ke bitɔtogibin tan nyan Pɔɔl npɔɔn bi ban ni wee ki nan ta u ŋa bi niin na.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: Le niwonn ki nan be kunyiɛg, le Uyudaan dan nan sir Pɔɔl ban ki ke, Che a sufu li pɔɔu, ke ŋaah pii jin siɛra man Jerusalam, man le a yin ji n siɛra Rom mu.

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Naah nan won kutaag, le Juu yaab biba lagin tab ki kal ki lol, ki poo ke baa yin ji bee ki nyu niba, see ke bi nan ku Pɔɔl.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Ban nan lol man nmɔn nan yab jon bininfob pinau.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Le bi jon kumanug a yudam nin bininkpeeb ban ki bei bi ke, Ti poo tipoor tan pɔɔ le ke taa yin ji niba see ke ti nan ku Pɔɔl.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Daandaan nan ti ban nim le n sir Juu yaab a yudam a taalin ki tun u ba wee un jo bitɔtogib a yudaau n ban, ki tan bei u ke un ta Pɔɔl dan ni ban, ke ni ban ni baan u le ki mann biɛ u bɔr nmɔn nbamɔm. Amaa ti li ku u ke waa la foon do.

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Amaa Pɔɔl a ninsa a big kaah gbir bi lol nmɔn nin, le u jon bitɔtogib a niin nmɔn yaan ki tan bei Pɔɔl.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Le Pɔɔl yin bitɔtogib a sanliiru uba, ki bei u ke, Ta Unaachipɔɔn nan ki jo ni yudaau ban, u jɔɔ tibɔr tiba le ki yin bei u.

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Le u ta u jon bitɔtogib a yudaau ban. Le ki tan bei u ke, Pɔɔl wan ye uloondiikɔɔrin le ke n ta Unaachipɔɔn nan ki dan a ban ke u jɔɔ tibɔr tiba le ki yin beiŋ.

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
