local: Le bitɔtogib a yudaau choo Unaachipɔɔn nin a nɔɔ ki ta u nyan ngbaan, ki tan baan u ke, Ba a bɔr wee a yin beim maa.

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Le u ke: Juu yaab le kpaag bimɔb ki ban a won nyan Pɔɔl ki che bin ta u ki dan Juu yaab a yudam ban, ke bi ban bin mann ki baan u le ki biɛ u bɔr a sam nbamɔm.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Laa gar bi paam mi, nan bu-n Juu yaab a jab biba bi yab jon bijab pinau le ki woo kulɔŋ ki guu u. Bi biɛn poo tipoor le ke baa yin ji bee ki nyu niba see ke bi nan ku Pɔɔl. Bi pii ŋa sili le ki guu ŋaah li jiin bi man.

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Le bitɔtogib a yudaau nan chabir Unaachipɔɔn nmɔn ki kpaan u ke, Laa bei u ba ke a poor tibɔr nan ki seim mi.

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Le bitɔtogib a yudaau win yin u sanliib bilee ki bei bi ke, Ni ta bitɔtogib kobilee nin bitanjigib pilile, ki ta bikpantɔgib mu kobilee, ki ŋa sili ki jo Sizariya. Ni nya tikur tiwiɛ a yonn dinan a nyiɛg nan ki jo.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Ni ta utaan ki sei Pɔɔl wee un jig, ki ta u ki jo ubɔr Fɛlisi ban soon ki laa che un kan kidaŋi.

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: U nan sɔb kugbɔŋ le ki sei bi ke bin ta tan sei ubɔr Fɛlisi, le kugbɔŋ nmɔn wɔɔn ke.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: Min Kuladiyasi Lisiɛ le sɔb kugbɔŋ nan, ki poor jilimadaan ubɔr Fɛlisi.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Juu yaab le nan choo uja nan, ki yin ku u, le n gbir ke u ye Rom a ninfou le. Ni ma bu le n ta bitɔtogib ki jon tan fee u.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: N nan ban n biɛ nan bu wee bi choo u le, mambu le n nan ta u ki jon bi ma Juu yaa a yudam ban.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: N biɛ ke ni ye bi tɔɔl a siim bu le bi galin u nmɔn, amaa naa ye u galin niba le wee ni kpaan nin un kpo, bee bin loon u n diin ni.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: Le bi beim ke Juu yaab lolig le ke bin ku u, mambu le n ke bitɔtogib nan n ta u ki dan a ban weem, n bei ban galin u nmɔn ke, bin dan a ban ki nan wɔɔŋ u bir kaah ye tan.

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Le bitɔtogib nmɔn nan tun ke bi yudaau kaah wɔɔn bi man ni, ki ta Pɔɔl kunyiɛg nmɔn ki jon kitiŋ kan wee bi yi ki ke Antipatirisi nan.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Le ni won kutaag, le bitanjigibin ta u ki jon, le ban nan choon tumin ma nan jir kun bi niin.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Bitanjigibin kaah tan foo Sizariya n-yonn man nin, le bi ta kugbɔŋin sei ubɔr, ki ta Pɔɔl ŋubin u.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Le ubɔr karin kugbɔŋin le ki baan u waah nyan nan yaan. Le ki biɛ ke u nyan Silisiya le.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: Le u bei u ke, Ban galiŋin foon n-yonn man ke, n li gbir a bɔr nmɔn. Le ki sei bimɔb ke ban guun li ŋmag u ubɔr Herɔd a bɔr naakoon na.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: Iweehn iŋmu daal, le kumanug a yudaau Ananiɛsi, nin bininkpeeb, nin Rom yaab a bɔjiru wan wee bi yi u ke Tetulusi nan, foo Sizariya ki pag galin Pɔɔl ubɔr Fɛlisi ban.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Baah nan yin Pɔɔl kɔɔn n-yonn man nin, le Tetulusi nan pag Pɔɔl a bɔr ki wɔɔn Fɛlisi ke: Ubɔr Fɛlisi fin bu le ti kan nposɔɔn kitiŋ nan, nin ŋaah tiɛr wonbu le a jɔɔ ki mann ki tiŋ nan.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Jilima daan Fɛlisi, ti jɔɔ npopeen nan yaan biɛn nin niwann biɛn nin, ki tenniŋ kunɔɔpoog takpem ni bu.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: Amaa maa ban n bii a saŋiyi, n baliŋ le ke a mɔr ki gbir ti bɔr nan wamau.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Ti kan uja nan le n miin u ye kuŋmaruŋ daan le, ki jɔɔ limɔyiil Juu yaab a tingbɔŋ nan yaan biɛn. U ye bikɔnbam a sanliiru le ki be Nazarif yaab a wonjiɛmin.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: hali u nan ban un bii iwonjiɛn niin nin le. Ni bu le ti choo u.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: Ni ye a tɔɔl baan uja nan ke, a li biɛ nan biɛn bu wee ti pag ki galin u nan a bamɔn.

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Le Juu yaab nmɔn kpiɛ u bu ki ke waah pag man nmɔn sin ye ibamɔn le.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Ubɔr nmɔn kaah wɔɔn Pɔɔl nnɔɔ ke un fii ki pagin: Le Pɔɔl ke, N nyi ke ŋaah ŋa ubɔjir ti tingbɔŋ nan yaan, ni ŋa ibin takpem ma. Mambu, ni ŋaam npopeen pam nin maah yin ji min tɔɔl a siɛra a ban.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: A li fir ki feen ki biɛ ke naah laa jon iweehn piig nin ilee le n nan jon Jerusalam ke n tan jiɛn Uwonbɔr.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Ban galin min kaa kan maah far bininfob limɔkpel iwonjiɛn niin man ni, bee ki fiin bi ŋuui tiwonjiɛndirin ni, bee kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ni.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Fetusi kaah nan foo Sizariya iweehn itaa daal le u nyan nin yaan ki jon Jerusalam.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: Le bi mu kaa ji li fir pag wɔɔniŋ n galin kaah ye nan ni.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: niin le biminmanib a yudam nin Juu yaab a yudam dan Fetusi ban ki nan pag ki galin Pɔɔl.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Amaa, n che ke, n jiɛn Uwonbɔr wan ye ti yaajam a weemin le, ki nɔɔ u siimin, man wee bi ke maa ye ibamɔn nin. N ŋa nan sɔb nsiim a gbɔŋin nin nan sɔb Uwonbɔr a linlem a gbann nin yaan biɛn yadau.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: Le ki balin Fetusi nin linunmanl ke un chee ki tun baah ban man nin sei ba wee ki nyan Pɔɔl Sizariya a tiŋin ki ta u dan Jerusalam, amaa bi nan ban bin woon ku Pɔɔl le nsann.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: le n jɔɔ tama uba le nin Uwonbɔr ke baah jɔɔ man nin, ke nkum a fiim na li be ki sei bininfoŋam nin bipootondam.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Le Fetusi jiin bi ke, Bi la ŋmag Pɔɔl Sizariya a tiŋin, min tɔɔl chaa niin le daandaan nan.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Mambu, n pɔɔn n baa le iyoo biɛn ke n tun nan kpaan, wee n sufu li be nbamɔm nin Uwonbɔr nin uninfosaal ban.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Che ni sanliib biba n nɔɔm ki jo, ni ye u tan jɔɔ igalin ke ni galin mu.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: Maah nyan Jerusalam ni yuur pam, le n nan jir niin ki ŋmag ipiin ke n ta sei n ninfob ban ye bilanjiimin, ki sin mann kumanug.

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Fetusi kaah nan be bi ban ki tan kpaa iweehn iniin bee piig nan, ni bɔɔn bu le u jir Sizariya, ki won kutaag, le u kal u bɔr binbinl bu ki che bi ta Pɔɔl dan u ban.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: Bi nan nan kam ki miin n nan mann nba le ki be kasi iwonjiɛn niin ki mann kumanug nmɔn. Iyaayoo nmɔn bininfob takpem kaa nan be niin ni, le tifur mu kaa nan be.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Pɔɔl kaah foon n-yonn man nin, le Juu yaab ban nan nyan Jerusalam nan fii sir ki gɔɔn u, ki pag galin Pɔɔl tibɔr tan wee baa li fir ji ti siɛra.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Amaa Juu yaab biba nan nyan Asiya a tingbɔŋin ki be iwonjiɛn niin nan yaan. Ni bi ye bi kan n bir ke, ni kpaa talar le nin bin dan ki nan pag biim.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Le Pɔɔl jin uba a siɛra ki ke, Maa tun niba ki bii Juu yaab a siimi, bee ki bii iwonjiɛn niin ni bee ubɔr Siiza.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Bee ban be do nan, che bin wɔɔn tibir tan wee bi nan kan n tun, maah nan sir Juu yaab a yudam a nintogilin.

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Fetusi kaah nan ban un sɔŋ Juu yaab a sufu nni, le u baan Pɔɔl ke, A li ban a jo Jerusalam wee n tan ji a bɔr tan wee bi pag ki galiŋ nan niin naa.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: See limɔbonl liba lan wee n nan tiɛn ki pag bi sisiigin ke, Bitakpiib na li fii nkumin nin bu le n si a nintogil dinan nan.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Le Pɔɔl jiin ke, N le la si ubɔr Siiza a nintogil le u naakoon, nan yaan ŋan ki kpaan ni ji n bɔr. Maa tun niba ki bii Juu yaabi, ke fin tɔɔl kaah nyi ni nbamɔm man nin.

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: Fɛlisi ma kaah nan nyi Yisa a san nmɔn a nɔɔm nbamɔmin bu le u jiɛn tibɔrin, le ki ke, Bitɔtogib a yudaau Lasiya nan foon n-yonn man ke, n li ji ni bɔr nmɔn.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: Ni ye n bi jɔɔ niba a galin yan wee n tun wee ni kpaan nin n kpo ke, maa yii nkumi. Amaa ni ye bi por tibɔkar le seim ke, uba kaa li fir taam ki ŋubin bi nɔɔn ni. N balin ubɔr Siiza le ke un ji n bɔr nmɔn.

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: Le ki nan bei bitɔtogib a sanliiru uba ke u li guu Pɔɔl, ki che u li be u wiiba, ki che Pɔɔl a jɔtiib li tenn u nan wee u ban na.

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Le Fetusi ta tibɔr nmɔn ki gbeer u ninkpeeb, le ki bei Pɔɔl ke, A balin ubɔr Siiza ke Siiza ban le a li jo.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, le Fɛlisi dan tibɔr a ji bol, u ma nin u poo Dirusila, wan nan ye Juu a bisiɛg nan, le ki che bi yin Pɔɔl ki dan bi ban. Le Pɔɔl pag uninfo kaah li ŋa Yisa Masiya yada man, le u pelig.

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: Le ni ŋa iweehn ilee le ubɔr Agiripa nin Bɛnisi dan Sizariya ke bin nan poor ubɔr Fetusi.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Pɔɔl kaah nan pag ki wɔɔn nbuchin binben nin ba a ŋmagim nin tibɔr a jidaal a yonn kaah yin foon man nin, le bijawaam choo Fɛlisi, le u ke, Ni li kpaan man daandaan nan! Li chaau. Amaa ni ye n kan isaŋ ke n ji li yiŋ.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: Baah nan be niin idaa takpem nmɔn nan che Fetusi nan wɔɔn ubɔr Agiripa Pɔɔl a bɔr kaah ye man na, ki ke: Uja uba be do le Fɛlisi nan jiɛn u ŋa kuloondiigin.

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: Fɛlisi nan ban Pɔɔl n kɔɔ u bu nin ilig le, ni ma bu le u nan gbaa yi u ki pag nin u.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: Maah nan jon Jerusalam n-yonn man ni, le biminmanib a yudam nin Juu yaab a ninkpeeb nan pag ki galin u, ki ke u kpaan nin nkum le.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Ibin ilee kaah jon a bɔɔn le Posiɛ Fetusi mu gaa Fɛlisi a ninkper nmɔn. Fɛlisi kaah nan yin fii lijalin bu-n, u nan ban un penn Juu yaab a pooi le, mambu le u che Pɔɔl be kuloondiigin.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Le n bei bi ke, Tim Rom yaab a kaal kaa chee man ni, ni ye bi pag ki galin uninfo uba ke, ti che bi lagin tab le ki gbir baah pag ki galin u nan bu, ki che u mu n len u bamɔn kaah ye man.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Baah dan n ban nin tibɔr nmɔn, maa bii isaŋi, ni nan won kutaag le n che bi lagin n naakoon, le ki che bi ta Pɔɔl kɔɔn.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: Ban nan pag ki galin u nan kaah fii bin pag, baa nan fir pag ki galin u ki jiin tibir tan paam wee n bi tiɛrin ni.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: Amaa bi le far u limɔkpel le ki jiin bi wonjiɛnl paam, nin utakpiir wan wee bi nan yi u ke Yisa nan, wee Pɔɔl ke u be u miɛfolin nin bu.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: N sufu nan ŋmarin le maah li ji tibɔr nmɔn man; mambu le che n baan u ke un bin chee ki jo Jerusalam wee n tan ji u bɔr nmɔn niin.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Le Pɔɔl balin ke bi li ŋmag u ki nan kpaa lidaal lan wee Siiza na li ji u bɔr. Mambu le n che bi guu u ke n nan ta u jo ubɔr Siiza ban.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Le ubɔr Agiripa bei Fetusi ke, Min tɔɔl ban n gbir uja nmɔn a mɔn. Le u jiin bei u ke, Won nan, a wo li gbir u mɔn.

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Le ni won kutaag, le ubɔr Agiripa nin Bɛnisi nan ji kunaachuŋ ki tan kɔɔ kulaguŋ a diigin le bitɔtob a ninkpeeb nin kitiŋ nmɔn a ninkpeeb nɔɔ u. Le Fetusi che bi ta Pɔɔl kɔɔn.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Le Fetusi ke: Ubɔr Agiripa, nin nim ban biɛn be ti ban nan, ni kan uja nan na! U ma le Juu yaab ban be Jerusalam nin ban biɛn be Sizariya nan dan n ban nin u bɔr, ki galin u, ki fu u bu, ki ke naa kpaan nin un be.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: Amaa min kaa kan tibir tan wee u tun ni wee ni kpaan nin un kpo, amaa waah balin ubɔr yudaan nin, n yin ta u le jo Rom.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Amaa maa nyi ma yin sɔb kugbɔŋ ki pag u bɔr man ki sei ubɔr yudaan ni. Ni ma che n ŋmag u dan ni biɛn ban, nin ubɔr Agiripa ban diginn. Ubɔr Agiripa kaah li baan u ibinbaan ki feen u a bɔɔn le n ni li kan maah li sɔb nan ki sei u.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Nan bu-n n tiɛr ki miin naa yin jɔɔ itaa nin n che uloondiikɔɔr n jo ubɔr yudaan ban, wee kaa li fir wɔɔn waah bii nan ni.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Baah nan nan bei ke ti kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ ki jo Itali nan, le bi nan ta Pɔɔl ki kpiɛ biloondiikɔɔb biba bu ki sei bitɔtogib a sanliiru wan a yinl ye Juliɛsi nan, wee u be bibɔrtiib a yudaau a paabim n yaan nin.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Le ti biɛn kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ kan nan nyan Adiramatum, ki pii ŋa sili nin kin jo Asiya a tingbɔŋin nin ntim man mal limɔgilin; le kuŋaluŋin fii. Masedoniya a ninfou Arisitakusi, wan nan nyan Tesalonika n nan be ti ban.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Le ni wonn kutaag, le ti foo Sidonn, le bitɔtogib a sanliiru Juliɛsi kaah ŋmag Pɔɔl nbamɔm man nin bu, le u jaa u nsan ke u li chaa u jɔtiib ban be niin nin ban wee bin ter u waah ban nan.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Le ti nyan nin yaan ki chaa, le nfaam daar ki too ti, mambu le che ti nan nɔɔ Saipuru ligungonl lan be nnyum a sisiigin liwonpɔɔnl paam ki jon.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: Taah lɔŋ limɔgil nni, le ti nɔɔ Silisiya nin Pamfiliya, ki tan foo Mara, Lisiya a tingbɔŋin.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Nin yaan le bitɔtogib a sanliiru win kan kuŋaluŋ kan nyan Alɛgzandira ki chaa Itali a tiŋin, le u che ti kɔɔ ki ma.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: Ki chaa soon soon, ti nan yuur nsan wamau, ki tan foo Kenidusi a tiŋin ban. Nfaamin kaa nan che ti nɔɔ taah nan ban nan paamin ni, ni bu che ti ni nan nɔɔ ligungonl lan be Kereti a tiŋ kan be nnyum a sisiigin Salmone a logil paamin le.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: Ti nan kpag limɔgil a gbaam le, ni nan pɔɔ pam ki sei ti nin ta tan foo kitiŋ kan wee bi yi ke, sisibol ŋanl nan, nin yaan kaa fɔg nin Lasiya a tiŋin ni.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Ti nan yuur takpem limɔgil nmɔn yaan na, ni che Juu yaab a mɔlol a chiigin nan nan jon na, n-yonn man wee kidaŋ ti be limɔgil nmɔn yaan nin. Le Pɔɔl sei bi ikpaan ke.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: N jɔtiib, n nyi ke ti li kan kidaŋ ti sanchonl nan yaan, naa ye tiwann yɔɔr tan be kuŋaluŋ nan yaan a binba bu bee kuŋaluŋin a binba bu, amaa ti baantɔɔl a miɛfol be kidaŋin.

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Amaa le bitɔtob a sanliiru nan kaa nan chee Pɔɔl kaah len man nni le ki sin nɔɔ wan donn kuŋaluŋin nin kuŋaluŋin daan a bɔr.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: Sisibol ŋanl kaa nan be nin kuŋaluŋ n sir wee kufaag a yonn n jo, mambu le che bininfob takpem nan len ke ti pɔɔn ti ba ki foo Fonisi kitiŋ kan be Kereti a tingbɔŋin nin, Fonisi nmɔn jɔɔ tiŋalin kaah sii nan yaan, ni nan too nnɔɔgan nin nnɔɔjiin a wonlul paam le.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Le ntafaam nan fii nnɔɔgan paam limɔgil nmɔn yaan ki daar soon, soon, le bi tiɛr ke bi nyaŋ le nmɔn; le ki choo tikulor tan baag kuŋaluŋ siigin lor, le ki gaa nsan, ki kpag Kereti a mɔgil a gbaam.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: Naa yuuri le ntafapɔɔrim fii n nnɔɔjiin liwonpɔɔnl paam ki daar chaan ligungonl lan be nnyum a sisiigin nin.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: Le ki jɔɔ kuŋaluŋin yeŋ, ni che taa ji na li fir too nfaamin ni. Uba kaa ji nan fir ŋmag kuŋaluŋin ni, le ti le ni nan chee sei nfaam nmɔn le mi daar kuŋaluŋin chaa.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Le mi ta ti ki tan foo kugungonbig kan be nyum a sisiigin wee bi yi ke Kɔda, le ti gbaa ki yuur kubulunkpoŋ kan wee kuŋaluŋin darin ki ŋa kuŋaluŋ n yaa poolin.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: Le bi nin dar kubulunkpoŋin ki kɔɔn kuŋaluŋin yaan, le ki ta nŋmin ki lol kuŋaluŋin kenken. Bi nan san bijawaam ki nyi ke bi tan li par fel Libiya a tiŋ ban nan yaan a nyum kaa nyɔɔ-n. Mambu le nan che bi jiin kuŋaluŋin a kulorin le ntafaam nin daar bi ki chaa.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: Ntafaam la nan daar ki pɔɔ, ni che kuŋaluŋin nan yeŋ le, le ni won kutaag le bi nyan tiwann yɔɔr tiba kuŋaluŋin yaan ki ta jaa nnyumin.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: Ni ween itaa daal le bi ji nyan kuŋaluŋ nmɔn a wan yɔɔr tan kpiɛg ki ji gab n nyumin.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Taah nan be ntafaam nmɔn a poolin ki chaa, ni nan jin idaa takpem ke taa kan nween bee iŋmabili, ni che taa ji nan jɔɔ tama ke ti li ŋmari.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Ni nan yuur takpem ke bininfobin kaa jin ni, le Pɔɔl nan fii sir ki bei bi ke, N jɔtiib, ni nan bi chee n mɔb, wee kaa nan bi nyan Kereti a tiŋin ke, ni bi li ŋan, naa bi li kan kidaŋ nin kininbɔŋ nan a bosu.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Amaa daandaan nan, n balin ni le, ni che ni sufu li dɔɔu, ke ni ni uba kaa yin lan u miɛfoli, amaa kuŋaluŋ nan a binba le nɔɔ li bii.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Kunyiɛg n-yaan le n yudaau Uwonbɔr wan yem wee n jiɛn u nan a malaka won jiirin ki nan sir n ban.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: le ki beim ke, Laa san bijawaami, Pɔɔl. A li foo ubɔr Siiza a nintogil, Uwonbɔr li fee fin nin bininfob ban biɛn be kuŋaluŋ nan yaan nan a miɛfol ki jaa fin bu.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Mambu n jɔtiib ni che ni sufu li pɔɔu man, n ŋa Uwonbɔr yada ke ni li tun ke waah len man nin.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Amaa nin ni ma biɛn ni, ntafaam nan li che ti do ligungonl lan wee nnyum lin li nan bu.

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: Ni nan ŋa tinyiɛmunn piig nin tina ke ntafaamin le la daar ti ki chaa limɔgil lan wee bi yi li ke Adarati a mɔgilin yaan, le kunyiɛgtasiig le bi ŋalindomin ni nan biɛ ke bi be malig nyum a tarig le.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Le bi ta nan wee bi jɔɔ bigin nnyum a nyunyɔɔmin ki bigin, ki miin mi nyunyɔɔm ye itaa kobig nin itaa pilee le, ki ji fɔr wama le ki bigin lig ki miin ni ye itaa piwiɛ le.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Le bijawaam kɔɔ ti, ti nan tiɛr ke n ba ke kuŋaluŋ n tan par do itangbag bu, ni ma che ti ni nan ta tikur tan kpiɛg tina tan chor kuŋaluŋ ki siigin, ki jaa nnyumin, ki ta lol choo kuŋaluŋin a bɔɔn, le ki sin miɛ Uwonbɔr ke kutaag nwonn ti.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Amaa biŋalindomin nan ban bin ŋmiɛr le ki jiɛn kuŋaluŋin, le ki lor kubulunkpoŋin jiin nnyumin, le ki nin kpalin ke bi ban bin ta tikur tan kpiɛg n le ki lol kuŋaluŋin a titogilin.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Le Pɔɔl bei bitɔtogib a sanliiru win nin bitɔtogibin ke, Ni ye biŋalindom nan kaa be kuŋaluŋ nan yaan ke ni ni uba kaa yin ŋmari.

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Le bitɔtogibin nan jii iŋmin yan wee bi ta lol kubulunkpoŋin ki chi chi, ki che ku lu.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Le ki be kutaayɔg paam, le Pɔɔl balin bi biɛn ke bin jim tijiir, le ki ke Nan bu-n dinan yin kpiɛ iweehn piig nin ina le nan, le bijawaam che naa jin niba.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Daandaan nan n balin ni le ke ni pɔɔn ni ba ki ji tijiira. Ni ma li che ti kan npɔɔn. Ni ni uba kaa yin lan u yukpobig u yul bu.

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: Waah len man nmɔn a bɔɔn, le u yuur boroboro ki poor Uwonbɔr bi biɛn nan a numin. Le ki choo war war ki piin nŋmam.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Bi biɛn nan a sufu nan pɔɔg le bi jin tijiir bi baantɔɔl.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: Tim ban nan be kuŋaluŋ nmɔn yaan nan ye bininfob kobilee nin pilile nin biloob le.

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Baah jin ki kpaan baah ban man a tarigin, le bi ta tijiir tan gurin jaa nnyumin, le kuŋaluŋin kaa ji kpiɛgi.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Kutaag kaah won diɛ-n le bi kan litanbiiril, le biŋalindomin kaa biɛ ke kitiŋ le yen ni, amaa le ki nin gbeer ke ni bi li fir ke, bin bi ta kuŋaluŋin kɔɔ niin wee kun fel titann nin.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Le bi jii iŋmin nin chinn, le tikur tan kpiɛgin lu nnyumin, le ki ji lor iŋmin yan lol tidaar tan wee bi jɔɔ don kuŋaluŋin ni, le ki nin don likinkenl kuŋaluŋin a titogil, ke ntafaam li daar bi chaa, le ki tan mal likpengonl.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Le kuŋaluŋin a titogil fel titann nin, kaa ji li fir jo, le kuŋaluŋin sir le kunyungbɔŋ nin chaan ki nan liɛr kuŋaluŋin a bɔɔn le ku nan choo bɔgir bɔgir.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Le bitɔtogibin nan ban bin ku ban wee bi nan ta ŋa kuloondiigin nin biɛn, ke ni ba ke bi ni uba n nan wo nnyum do ki guun ŋmar.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Amaa le bitɔtogibin a sanliiru n ma nan ban un ŋmiɛn Pɔɔl le, le kaa chee bi lol nmɔn ni. Amaa le ki sin jaa bi ke wan nyi linyunwol wee ki li fir wo do ke, un chinn yug ki lu ki li wo chaa likpengonla.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: Ban gur kaa nyi linyunwol nan mu n choo kuŋaluŋ a daag bee iŋalinbɔg ki kpab ki wo do. Ti biɛn nan ŋa man le ki ŋmar.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Iyaayoo le Ubɔr Agiripa nan bei Pɔɔl ke, N jaa ŋa, a li fir pag a bɔr kaah ye man ki sei ti. Le Pɔɔl yuur u nɔɔ ki poor u ki piin u bɔr:.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: Ubɔr Agiripa, ni ye liyul ŋanl le ki seim nin maah si a nintogil dinan ke n pag nbamɔm ki wɔɔniŋ, nin Juu yaab kaah pag ki galinim man biɛn.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: diginn, n nyi ke a jɔɔ nbiɛm ki nyi Juu yaab a kaal nin bi mɔkpel kaah ye man. Ni ma bu n baliŋ le a mɔr ki gbir n bɔr nan ki seim.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: Juu yaab biɛn nyi maah nan be man n-yonn man wee n la nan ye kibigin, npiinimin n binbem kaah nan ye man ntɔɔl a tiŋin, nin Jerusalam.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Bi nyim ke ni fɔg ki bi li fir ji n siɛra, ni ye bi ban ke, bi li fir wɔɔniŋ ke n nan ŋmag Farasii yaab a siim man pɔɔn le ki jɔɔ Farasii yaab a binbem.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: Amaa daandaan nan maah nan si wee ni jim tibɔr nan, ni nyan n tama wan wee Uwonbɔr nan siin ti yaajam limɔsiinl lan nin bu le.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Limɔsiinl nmɔn bu le che tim Juu yaab a bol piig nin ilee nan jɔɔ tidindann ke li na li gbeen, ni ma che bi jiɛn Uwonbɔr nween nin kunyiɛg. Ubɔr aa, tama wan wee n jɔɔ nan bu le che Juu yaab galinim nan.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Babu che ni li pɔɔ ni ban nin ni ŋa yada ke Uwonbɔr fiin bitakpiibaa?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Chinchinn, min tɔɔl nan tiɛr ke ni kpaa talar nin n tun maah li fir tun man biɛn ki bii Yisa wan ye Nazarif a ninfou nan a yinl.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Man nmɔn le n sin nan tun Jerusalam. Kumanug a yudam le nan seim nsan le n chor biwonjiɛm ki kɔɔn bi tiloondirin, le baah ti ji bi tibɔr ki nan diɛ ki ke bin ku bi nan, min tɔɔl ti kpiɛg ban ke bin ku bin bu le.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Iyoo biɛn n ti kɔɔ tiwonjiɛndirin le ki dar bi tuli, ki mug bi ki che bin sug bi yudaau. N nan fiin n ŋuul bi bu ki tan lagira, mambu che n ni nan chaa itindoo le ki tan mugir bi.

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: Nsan chonl nmɔn ni, le n ti nan fii ki chaa Damasiko ke n tan tun ke kumanug a yudam kaah seim nsan ki wɔɔnim man nin.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Ubɔr aa, nwosisiigin le n nan be nsann bu ki chaa, le ki kan nwonween kaah nyan paab, ki nyagin ki jon nween, ki nan nyagir ki jin min nin n ninɔɔtabin bu.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Le ti biɛn lu tum, le n gbir likukɔl pag ki beim tim Juu yaab a leen yin ke, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, ba ŋa wee a mugirim maa? Ni pɔɔ nin a ta a taal liɛ ipiinh.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Le n baan u ke, Uyudaau, a ye ŋmiɛ? Le Uyudaan jiin ke, Min Yisa le a mugir nmɔn.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Fii ki sir a taai buu. N degir a bu le ki ganiŋ ke a li ye N toontunnu, ki ye siɛra ki wɔɔn bininfob ŋaah kam man, nin nan wee n la yin kpiir ki wɔɔniŋ.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: N li nyaŋ a tɔɔl a ninfob a nɔɔn, nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan a nɔɔn. N yin tuŋ le bi ban.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: a tan ligir bi num ki nyan bi libunbɔnlin ki kpenn bi ŋa nwonween ni na, ki nyan bi Sintani a pɔɔmin ki sei bi Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm, wee bin kan tibir a jiɛn per, nin npaan n ban wee Uwonbɔr n ta bi ŋa U yaab ban be kasi-n a sisiigin baah ŋaam yada n bu.

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: Man bu Ubɔr Agiripa, maa ŋa tukangbirdaan maah kan zii kan paabin ni.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: Chinchinn, n mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr nmɔn le ki sei Damasiko yaab, nin Jerusalam yaab, nin Judiya a tingbɔŋin biɛn. Le ki mɔɔn ti sei ban kaa ye Juu yaab mu. N wɔɔn bi le ke bin kpenn bi binbem wee ki ta biba ki sei Uwonbɔr a, ki li be nbinbem man sin li wɔɔn ke bi kpenn bi binbema.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Ni ma bu le Juu yaab choom, iwonjiɛn niin a poolin ki ban bin kum.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: Amaa n kan Uwonbɔr a teril le ki nan kpaa dinan, le n be ki ji ni siɛra ki tenn ban gbee bininfob nin ban kaa gbee bininfob. Maah len man nan kaa lagir Uwonbɔr a linlem nin Moziz kaah nan len ke ni li tun man nin ni.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: ke Masiya li ji fala, ki li ye chinchinn a ninfo wan yin fii nkumin, ki wɔɔn u tɔɔl a ninfob nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan tibɔr tan li wonn bi num.

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Pɔɔl kaah pag man nmɔn le Fetusi tiɛn u bu ke, Pɔɔl; a waara. A biɛm takpem nan bu le che a waar.

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Le Pɔɔl jiin u ke, Jilimadaan Fetusi, maa waari, tibɔr tan wee n pag nan ye ibamɔn le ki jɔɔ itaa.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: Ubɔr Agiripa nyi tibɔr nmɔn, ni ma bu le n pag n wiib ki tenn u. N gaa jin ke tibɔr tan wee n pag nan kaa ye tibɔwoon kaari, nan bu-n taa nan pag ki woon ni.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: Ubɔr Agiripa, a ŋa Uwonbɔr a linlemin yada waa? N nyi ke a ŋa bi yadau.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Le Ubɔr Agiripa jiin Pɔɔl ke, A tiɛr ke a li fir che n ŋa uwonjiɛn n-yonn wama nan yaan naa.

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Le Pɔɔl jiin u ke, Ni baga kaa be n-yonn wama bee takpemi, min miɛ Uwonbɔr le ni laa ye a binba amaa fin nin bininfob ban biɛn pelig n bɔr dinan nan li ŋa biwonjiɛm ke maah ye man nan, tikulor tan wee bi lolim nan a binba n choo nyan.

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Le ubɔr Agiripa, nin uyudaan Fetusi, nin Bɛnisi, nin bininfob ban biɛn nan be niin nmɔn fii.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: Ki nyan kudiig nmɔn yaan, le ki nin pag ki gbeer tab ke, Uja nan kaah tun nan nan kaa kpaan nin un kpo bee un kɔɔ kuloondiigin ni.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Le Agiripa bei Fetusi ke, Uja nan kaa nan bi balin Siiza ke, ti bi li fir jaa u.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Pɔɔl, wan ye Masiya a toontunnu, wee U yim ki gannim ki ŋaam ntoon ke n mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: tibɔŋann tan wee U nan chinn siin limɔsiinl lan nyan u linlemin ban wee ki sɔb Uwonbɔr a kasi a Gbɔŋin nin.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: tibɔŋann nmɔn wɔɔn U bija-n a bɔr le, ki ta jiin u ninforin paam, ke u nan ye ubɔr Defid a yaabil le.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: ki jiin u wonaaŋ paam mu ke, Uwonbɔr a Kasi a naaŋin nan wɔɔn nin npɔɔn ke u ye Uwonbɔr a bija le u kum a fiimin bu: U ma ye ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: U ma nin U yinlin bu, le Uwonbɔr san ti kunyunyunbaag, le ki ŋa ti itoon ke ti yin bininfob ban kaa ye Juu yaabin a booli biɛn ke bi li jɔɔ limɔcheel bi yada kan wee bi jɔɔ-n bu.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Le ni mu kpiɛ ban wee Yisa Masiya yin bin a kanlin na.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: N poor nim ban biɛn be Rom wee Uwonbɔr jiɛ ni ki yin ni wee ni ye U ninfogann kaabin: Ti Baa Uwonbɔr nin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be nin ni.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Chinchinn, Yisa Masiya bu le n poor n Wonbɔru ni biɛn nan bu, nan bu-n dulinya nan yaan biɛn bi pag ni yada buu.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: Uwonbɔr wan wee n jiɛn nin n sufu biɛn ki mɔɔn U bija-n a bɔŋann nin, U ma ye n siɛra daau ki jiin maah tiɛr ni bu man isaŋ biɛn.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: n wonmiɛlin n-yonn biɛn; n miɛ Uwonbɔr le ke ni ye U bam ke Un loor nsan seim wee n dan ni ban.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: N ban n kan ni le wee ki ta kinaaŋin a piini ibam ki sei ni wee yin sei ni npɔɔn.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: ni ma ye, nan li pɔɔg tab a yada nim nin min a gbanann pɔɔmin.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: N nabum, maa ban ni laa nyi ke iyoo takpem n lolig le ke n dan ni ban naah li ŋa man wee n fir ki kan bininfob takpem ni sisiigin, ke maah pii kan man ban kaa ye Juu yaabin a sisiigin biba-n.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Ni kpaa le nin n pag tibɔŋann nan ki sei Girig yaab nin ban kaa ye Girig yaab, ki pag ti ki sei bilandam nin tijɔri.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Ni ma bu le n numan ke n mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn ki sei nim ban be Rom nan mu.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Ifa kaa jɔɔm tibɔŋann nin a mɔɔnim bu, nan bu-n ti ye Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm le ki sei ban biɛn ŋa yada ke bin kan feegab: Juu yaab li yin chinn kan feegab le ban kaa ye Juu yaabin mu nin paan.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Tibɔŋann nin yaan le nbuchin binben man nyan Uwonbɔr ban nin kpiir npaan ni, nbuchin binben man nyan ti yada-n npiinimin nin ndiɛmin, ke naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Ubuchin li kan limiɛfol yada bu.

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ nyan papaab ki kpiir ban kaa jɔɔ Uwonbɔr nin bipootondam buu, ban jɔɔ bipootom ki deginn ibamɔn nin bu-n.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: bininfosaalib kaah bi ye bi li nyi Uwonbɔr man nin ni dɔ npaan ni le ki sei bi, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr le che ni dɔɔn npaan ni ki sei bi.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah nan naan dulinya n-yonn man nin, le bininfob kan Uwonbɔr wan kaa ga-n a binbem, nin U Pɔɔm man kaa jɔɔ diɛmin, le U wonaaŋin ji che bininfob biɛ U, ni bu baa jɔɔ nan wee bi li ta pag ke baa nyi.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Nin ni ma biɛn ni bi nyi Uwonbɔr, le kaa tenn U jilima kaa tenn u kunɔɔpoog mu, amaa le baa tiɛr nan ye nlam a wan ni, le bi sufu gbee nin ijɔrig nin libunbɔnl.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Hali nin baah pag ke bi ye bilandam nan biɛn ni, bi kpann tijɔri le.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: ni ma che bi kpenn Uwonbɔr wan kaa kpegin a jilima ki ta sei tiwann mann wan tan wee bi mann wee ti nann uninfo wan kpeg nin nnɔɔm nin tiwankor nin tiwann tan fuul.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Mambu che Uwonbɔr nyan U num le ki jiɛn bi le bi tun bi sufu a bam a toon, yan ye lipiibanl bee lijabanl lan kaa be kasi ki ye ifa a wan ki tenn tab bi tɔɔl a gbanann nin.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Bi kpenn Uwonbɔr a bamɔn n le ki ŋa ti tibɔkar, le ki jiɛn tiwann naan kaar ki tun ki tenn ti, le ki yii kaa jiɛn Uninaan nin tɔɔl li, U ma kpaan npagim iyoo biɛn. Ibamɔn.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu, Uwonbɔr nyan U num ki jiɛn bi per bi fai a kɔlisa nmɔn. Hali gbaa bi yaa piib kpenn le ki dɔɔ nin bi piitab ki jiɛn bijab.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Man mu jigin le bijab mu yii kaa dɔɔ nin bipiibi le ki jɔɔ bi kɔlisa nmɔn dɔɔ nin bijatab. Bijab tun tijaginn a toonl le nin bijatab, le ki gaa litudaril lan kpaan bi ki jiin bi toonbirin bu.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Nan kpiɛ, baah yii kaa ban bi li nyi Uwonbɔr a bamɔn yin bu le U nyan U num ki jiɛn bi per bi potiɛkpiinn nin, le bi tun nan kaa kpaan nin bin tun ni.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Bi sufu gbee nin npooton a booli le, nin titoonbir nin kujaŋuuŋ nin nan kaa jɔɔ bu. Bi gbee nin linunchɔril le, nin lininfo kul, ki kɔnn nin tab, ki lar tab, ki nan tab. Bi ye bi bɔɔn sub le.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: nin bitingbiib, ki nan Uwonbɔr, ki sugir tab, ki jɔɔ kalanbaani, ki far igbingban; ki tiɛr baah li ŋa man ki mann tun titoonbir; ki yiin bi baam nin bi nam a mɔɔŋi;.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: Baa jɔɔ nlami, baa ye ban wee bi li fir ŋa bi yada, bi sufu kaa be bi tabin ni, kaa san tab kunyunyunbaagi.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Bi nyi Uwonbɔr a buchin siimin kaah len ke bininfob ban tun man nmɔn kpaan nin nkum le, baa nan tun man a binba amaa le ki sin chee sei bitab ke bi li tun man na.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Mambu, fin, wan jin a kpiɛ tibɔr nan, ŋaa jɔɔ nsan man wee a li nɔɔ ki ke ŋaa nyi, ni ma bu ŋaah nɔɔ nsan man bu wee ki jin a kpiɛg tibɔrin, a tɔɔl jɔɔ igalin, nan bu-n fin wan jin a kpiɛg tibɔrin tɔɔl tun man le.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Daandaan nan ti nyi ke Uwonbɔr kaah ji ban tun man nmɔn tibɔrin ni nɔɔ ibamɔn a sam bu le.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Ke ni ye fin, uninfosaal mu, jin bi tibɔr wee ki mu ji tun man ke, a tiɛr ke a li fir ŋmar Uwonbɔr a bɔjilin naa?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Bee a ziɛn Uwonbɔr a binben ŋamin a yabim nin U chee sei nin U mɔril nlee, kaa nyi ke Uwonbɔr kaah jɔɔ nbinben ŋan man nin ban a kpenn a binbem lee?.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Amaa ni tukangbiril nin naah kaa ban ni kpenn ni sufu-n bu, le ni kpiɛg U ŋuufiiŋ lan yab ki tenn ni ba lidaal man wee Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ nin U buchin bɔjilin na yin degirin nin.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Uwonbɔr Na li sei uninfo biɛn nin waah nan tun man.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Le ban si iyoo biɛn ki tun itoon ŋan ki ban kupag nin jilima a binbem nin limiɛfol lan kaa kpegin, U li sei bi limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Juu yaab ma ta ba ki jon ban kaa ye Juu yaabin naa, bee lipunjiil a nyɔɔr ye baa?.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Amaa ban ban niwann biɛn li ye bi yaan, wee ki yii ibamɔn wee ki nɔɔ titoonbir a samin, Uwonbɔr a ŋuul yin fii bi bu le.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Juu yaab jɔɔ isan a booli takpem ni wan biɛn ni; chinchinn, Uwonbɔr nan ta U bɔrin ŋubin Juu yaab a nɔɔn le.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Tibɔmugkaar nin nton li dan bininfob ban biɛn tun titoonbir nan bu. Chinchinn ni yin foo Juu yaab ban le ki nin foo ban kaa ye Juu yaab n mu ban;.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Ni ye bi ni bibam kaa si bogiliba ke, bi kaa si bogiliba nmɔn li fir bii Uwonbɔr mu wee U laa si bogilibaa?.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: amaa kupag nin jilima nin nposɔɔn le Uwonbɔr yin ta sei ban tun nan ŋan nin: Chinchinn ni na yin foon Juu yaab ban le wee ki nin foo ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ban.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Naa ye man ni! Che Uwonbɔr ma le ye ubamɔndaan na, wee uninfosaal biɛn mu le ye tibɔkardaan na. Ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin man nin: Na li ŋa man wee bin ke fin Uwonbɔr a bɔr ŋan ni ye a pag ke le uba mu kaa yin fir faaŋ limɔkpel a bɔr a jimin ni.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Nan bu-n, Uwonbɔr kaa gann ni.

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Amaa ni ye ti kaa jɔɔ nbuchin binben bu che Uwonbɔr a buchin binbemin nyan npaan nin ke, ti ji yin len ke baa? Ti li fir len ke Uwonbɔr galin nin waah fiin U ŋuul ti buu?.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Ban biɛn kaa jɔɔ Uwonbɔr a siim wee ki tun titoonbirin, bi yin kpo le kaa jɔɔ nsiimi, ban biɛn jɔɔ Uwonbɔr a siimin, amaa wee ki tun titoonbirin, Uwonbɔr yin ta nsiim nmɔn le ki ji bi tibɔr.

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Naa ye man ni! Ni bi ye man ke, Uwonbɔr na yin ŋa nii ki ji dulinya yaab tibɔraa?.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Nan bu-n naa ye ban gbir nsiimin le ye bibuchim Uwonbɔr ban ni, amaa ban chee sei nsiimin bi ma le bi na li yin bi bibuchim.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Nba ke uba ma li faam limɔkpel, Ke ni ye n bɔkarin kpiir Uwonbɔr a bamɔn nbu ki che Uwonbɔr a jilima mann kpiɛ ke, ke ni ŋa ni wee Uwonbɔr ji yin jim tibɔr ki ke n ye tibirdaan naa.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Ibamɔn, ban kaa ye Juu yaab, wee kaa jɔɔ nsiimin ni, amaa ni ye bi tun nsiimin kaah wɔɔn man nin bi tɔɔl a lamin ke, ni wɔɔn ke bi baan tɔɔl jɔɔ nsiim, hali nin baah kaa jɔɔ nsiim nmɔn biɛn na.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Ba ŋa taa li fir len, ke bitingbiib kaah len man wee bibam sin chee-n, ke, Ni che ti tun tibir wee niwann ŋann nnyan naa? Bi li ji bi tibɔr naah kpaan man.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Baah tun man nin le wɔɔn ke nsiimin kaah wɔɔn man nin be bi sufu ni, le bi potiɛl mu jin siɛra ki wɔɔn nan ŋan nin nan kaa ŋan ni, nan bu-n, isaŋ iba bi potiɛl ti nan cheel tenn bi le ke bi toon ŋan, le isaŋ iba mu ke bi potiɛl n ji len ke bi toon iba kaa ŋan ni.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Ni joorimin ti yin len ke nii? Tim pii ŋan jon bi lee? Naa ye man ni! Ti pii wɔɔn ni ke Juu yaab nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin biɛn be tibir a poolin.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Ni niɛ nan na yin tun lidaal lan wee Uwonbɔr na yin ji bininfob a bɔwoonkaar Yisa Masiya bu, ke n bɔŋann nin kaah wɔɔn man nin.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: Ke naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin: Uba kaa be ki ye ubuchin Uwonbɔr ban ni, hali uba kaa be;.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Daandaan nan fin, ni ye a ke a ye Juu a ninfou; wee ki nɔɔ nsiimin, ki far a gbingbanl ŋaah jɔɔ nɔɔ nin tab nin Uwonbɔr man;.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Uba kaa be ki biɛ ni taai yi, uba kaa be ki ban Uwonbɔr a boli.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: ni ye a nyi Uwonbɔr a bam, wee ki chee sei nan jon ni biɛn nan bu-n nsiimin kaah wɔɔn ni man ni-n;.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Bi biɛn labir bi bɔɔn kpaa Uwonbɔr ki jon na, bi biɛn tun ki bur Uwonbɔr a samin na; uba kaa be ki tun nan ŋan ni, hali uba kaa be.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: ni ye a ke a ye wan wɔɔnn bijɔɔm le nsan, ki ye nwonween ki sei ban be libunbɔnlin.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Bi mɔɔŋi yaa le ke tikar tan logir ki yaa-n; le bi lanbili pag nan kaa ye ibamɔn ni. Le bi mɔgbai gbee nin liwaataal a labir.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: ki ye wan kpaan tijɔri, ki ye wan wɔɔnn nbuwaab tirim, nan bu-n ni jɔɔ nnyinyin nin ibamɔn nin kaah sɔb man nsiimin a gbɔŋin.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Bi mɔɔŋi gbee nin imɔpoo le nin npooton.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: fin wan wɔɔnn a tabin, ba ŋa wee ŋaa wɔɔn a baa? A wɔɔn bi ke bi laa su, ke fin suu?.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Le bi taai ton nin ncham a nyam;.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Fin wan wɔɔnn ke bininfob laa tun iwanchɔgin toon nin, a tun iwanchɔgin toon naa? Fin wan kaa ban libawomanil a bɔrin ŋaa su kuwonjiɛndiig a wan naa?.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Le bi ŋaar biɛn ke, bi tenn bininfob ndam ki che bin ji fala.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Fin wan jɔɔ nsiimin ki far ligbingbanlin, ŋaa tenn Uwonbɔr le jilima le ki bii U siimin naa?.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: le kaa nyi nposɔɔn a ŋaari.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin man nin: Ban kaa ye Juu yaabin sugir Uwonbɔr le nim Juu yaab bu.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Le baa san Uwonbɔr bijawaami.

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Lipunjiil jɔɔ bu ni ye ni ŋmag nsiimin ke, amaa ni ye ni bii nsiimin ke, ni yin be le ke ban kaa jii-n.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Daandaan nan ti nyi Uwonbɔr a siimin a poolin kaah len man biɛn, mi pag tenn bininfob ban nɔɔ nsiim nmɔn le, wee uba nlaa nan kan bimɔb ki fir pag niba, wee Uwonbɔr n nan ji dulinya biɛn tibɔr.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Ni ye ban kaa jii bi pun yin ŋmag nsiimin kaah wɔɔn man nin ke, Uwonbɔr kaa yin ŋmag bi le ke ban jii bi pun yin naa?.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Ni ma bu uba kaa li fir len ke waah ŋmag Uwonbɔr a siimin bu u ye ubuchin Uwonbɔr ban ni; amaa nsiimin le che ti biɛ tibir kaah ye nan.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Wan kaa jii u punl tigbanann nin amaa wee ki sin ŋmag nsiimin li fir pag ki biiŋ, hali nin ŋaah le jɔɔ nsiim nmɔn wee min sɔb dɔ nmɔn biɛn nin lipunjiilin, a ye wan bii nsiim le.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Amaa daandaan nan nbuchin binben man nyan Uwonbɔr ban, wee U che ti biɛ mi-n, maa nyan nsiim a ŋmagimin ni, ni ma le nsiim a gbɔŋ nin bilinlem jin mi siɛra-n.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Wan ye Juu a ninfou npaan ni a binba kaa ye Juu a ninfo wi, le lipunjiil bamɔnl mu kaa ye nan ŋaan tigbanann nin a binba le.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: Nbuchin binben man nyan Uwonbɔr ban nmɔn nyan yada kan wee bi jɔɔ nin Yisa Masiya nle, ki tenn ban biɛn ŋa u yada-n. Naa jɔɔ nbɔgirimi.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Aai wan ye Juu a ninfou ke u ye Juu a ninfou u sufu ni le, le lipunjiil bamɔnl mu be sufu ni le, ki ye kinaaŋin, naa ye nan sɔb dɔ kugbɔŋin nin a binba, u yaa daan gaa u pag Uwonbɔr ban le naa ye uninfo ban ni.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: nan bu-n, ti biɛn nan ye tibirdam le ki par Uwonbɔr a pag.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: le Masiya daa ti ki che ti be ti wiib U nyunyunbasanlin bu ki ke tibɔr ŋan.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Uwonbɔr nan ta U le ki mann tibir a manug, baah ŋa u chamin yada-n bu. U tun man ki ban un wɔɔn u bamɔn le, nan bu-n, U nan jɔɔ limɔril, kaa dar bininfob ban tun tibir n-yonn man wee Yisa Masiya kaa la nan dan dulinya nan yaan nin a tuli yi.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: U nan ŋa man ki wɔɔn U bamɔn le n-yonn man wee ti be nan, ke U li ye ubamɔndaan wee ki ŋa ban ŋa Yisa yada-n mu bibamɔndam.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Ligbingbanfaal ye baa? Laa kpiɛ niin ni. Ba bu che naa kpiɛ niin naa? Taah ŋmag nsiimin buu? Aai, amaa taah ŋa yada-n bu.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Ni ma bu tim le si le ke uninfosaal a yada le che u bɔr ŋan Uwonbɔr ban amaa naa ye waah ŋmag nsiimin bu.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Uwonbɔr ye Juu yaab a binba a Uwonbɔr u lee? Waa ye ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ma a Uwonbɔr waa? Nn U ye ban kaa ye Juu yaab tɔɔl a weem le.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Uwonbɔr ye u ba le, u ma na yin bei ban jii bi pun ke, bi bɔr ŋan bi yada bu, ki jin bei ban mu kaa jii bi pun ke, bi mu a bɔr ŋan bi yada bu.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Ti wɔɔn ke yada a ŋaam bii nsiimin lee? Aai naa ye man ni! Ti le mann ŋmag nsiimin le.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Ti yin pag ke nii ki jiin ti yaaja Abiraham nin waah nan kan nan nin paam maa?.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Ni ye Abiraham a toon bu le Uwonbɔr nan ke u bɔr ŋan ke, u nan jɔɔ nan wee u li ta faa u gbingbanl, amaa naa ye Uwonbɔr a nintogili.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin len ke nii? Abiraham ŋa Uwonbɔr le yada, le Uwonbɔr kan u yada nmɔn kpiɛ u buchin binbem bu.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Ni ye uninfo tun litoonl ki gaa tiyɔr ke, baa kaan u yɔr nmɔn ke ti ye ipiin ni, amaa ni kpaan un gaa le.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Amaa, uninfo wan kaa nɔɔ itoon ŋan a tum bu, wee ki gaa Uwonbɔr wan che bipootondam kpann bibuchim jin nin, u yada nmɔn bu le Uwonbɔr ke u bɔr ŋan u buchin binbem bu.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Taah nan gbaa don limɔgilin a dool ki diɛ, ni bɔɔn bu le ti biɛ ke ligungonl lan be nnyum a sisiigin nin a yinl ye Malita le.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: Defid tɔɔl nan len man le, waah nan pag ke Uwonbɔr a kaar be uninfo bu u buchin binbem bu ki kpiɛ u toon bu-n:.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Bininfob ban a niin nan be niin nmɔn nan ŋmag ti nbamɔm pam. Bi nan mann nŋmi le ki sei ti ke ti li wora le ki sin poor ti nin ti foom, nan bu-n utaal le nan nun le tiwar be.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: Tikaar be ban galin wee Uwonbɔr jiɛn per bin bu, ban a bir bu wee U degin nin.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Le Pɔɔl tan pii tidagingann ki piɛn pii kijageŋ ki nan ŋa nŋmi-n, le nŋmi-n a tom gbir uwa-n le u nyan ki nan finfin Pɔɔl a nɔɔ ki junn ki jɔn.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Tikaar be uninfo wan wee Uwonbɔr kaa li nyi kan u bir ki bil guun win bu.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Ligungonl nmɔn a paab a ninfob kaah kan uwa-n kaah jɔn Pɔɔl a nɔɔ bu man nin, le bi bei tab ke, Uja nan ye uninfokur le, nin waah le ŋmar limɔgil nan a poolin nan biɛn yɔli, bitakpiib kaa yin jiɛn un be u miɛfolin ni.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Tikaar nan ye ban jii bi pun a binba yaar le bee, ban kaa jii n mu kpiɛ nii waa? Ti le gbaa pag le ke Abiraham a yada-n nan wɔɔn u buchin binbem le.

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Le Pɔɔl gbag u nɔɔ le uwa-n lu nŋmi-n yaan, amaa le naa ŋa Pɔɔl niba hali wama.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Ba nan che Uwonbɔr nan ŋa Abiraham ubuchin nmɔn naa? Waah nan jii u punlin a bɔɔn bee waah kaa la nan jii n naa? Naa ye waah nan jii-n a bɔɔn ni, amaa waah kaa la nan jii-n le Uwonbɔr nan ŋa u ubuchin!.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Le bininfobin ni nan ka ki tiɛr ke u nɔɔ li duun bee u li nyan lu ki kpo. Baah pel pel ki nan bag kaa kan niba ŋa Pɔɔl nan, le bi ji kpenn bi potiɛl ki ke u ye litingbanl le.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: U punjiilin nan ye siɛra le ki wɔɔn ke u pii ŋa Uwonbɔr yadau, le Uwonbɔr ta U buchin binbem a daagim ki daag u, u yada bu, n-yonn man wee waah laa nan jii u punlin. Le ki ŋa u yadadam ban kaa jii bi pun biɛn a baa, na li ŋa man wee bin kanh bi kpiɛ ban jɔɔ nbuchin binben nin.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Nin yaan kaa nan fɔg nin ubɔr Pubilisi a kpaabin ni, u ma nan ye ligungonl nmɔn a bɔru. Le u gaa ti kuchɔŋ ki tan kpaa iweehn itaa.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: U ma ji ye ban jii bi pun wee kaa be lipunjiil a binba bu amaa wee ki nɔɔ yada a tabogi yan wee ti baa Abiraham nan jɔɔ n-yonn man wee waah laa nan jii u punlin.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Kugbanantuntoŋ nin kupoomarug le nan jɔɔ Pubilisi a baa le u dɔ. Le Pɔɔl kɔɔ u ban ndiin ki tan ta u nɔɔ ki paan u bu ki miɛ Uwonbɔr sei u le u pɔɔg.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Naa nan ye nsiimin a ŋmagim bu le Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinl ki sei Abiraham nin u yaabili ke bi li ji dulinya nan a faalin ni, amaa nbuchin binben man nyan yada-n nin bu.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Naah nan tun man nmɔn le bibaarib ban nan gur ki be ligungonl nmɔn yaan biɛn dan le u teb bi.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: Ni ye Uwonbɔr bi ke ban ŋmag nsiimin le yin ye dulinya nan a faajib ke, yada a ŋaam kaa bi jɔɔ tinyɔɔri le limɔsiinl kaa jɔɔ tinyɔɔri.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Bi nan pu ti ipiin takpema, le taah ji nan nan be ŋaan sili nin ti fii kɔɔ kuŋaluŋin n-yonn man nin, le bi ji nan ta nan biɛn wee ti li ban nsann bu-n, ki kɔɔn kuŋaluŋin yaan ki sei ti.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: nan bu-n nsiimin a biim le jɔɔ Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ chaan. Nan yaan wee nsiim kaa be-n uba mu kaa jɔɔ igalin ni.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Ti nan be Malita iŋmal itaa le, ni bɔɔn bu le ti kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ kan nan be niin tiwar nin kufaag a yonn nin, kuŋaluŋ nmɔn nan nyan kitiŋ kan wee bi yi ki ke, Alɛgzandira nle, bi nan yi kuŋaluŋ nmɔn ke, Tijaann a weem. Ti nan kɔɔ kuŋaluŋ nmɔn le ki jon.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Mambu Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinlin nɔɔ nin yada le, ki ye Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl ki si guu Abiraham a yaabili biɛn, naa ye ban ŋmag nsiimin a binba, amaa ban jɔɔ Abiraham a yada-n. U ma ye ti biɛn nan a baa.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Ki tan foo kitiŋ kiba bi yi ki ke, Sarakuse, ti nan be niin iweehn itaa le.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: Ke naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin: Min le ŋaaŋ ibol takpem a baa. U ye ti baa le Uwonbɔr ban, wan wee u ŋa u yada, U ma ye Uwonbɔr wan tenn bitakpiib limiɛfol wee ki che tiwann tan kaa nan be-n nin be ke ti pii nan be le-n.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: Le ki tɔɔr jon Regiɛm a tiŋin. Le ni won kutaag le ntafaam yaan ti nan nyan nnɔɔganpaam ki daar ti, le iweehn ilee daal le ti foo Putoli a tiŋin.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Nin tama kaah kaa le nan be nmɔn biɛn ni, Abiraham nan jɔɔ tama le ki ŋa Uwonbɔr yada le ki nan ŋa ibol takpem a baa ke Uwonbɔr kaah nan bei ke, A yaabili na yin yab man le.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Nin yaan le ti kan nnabum biba. Le bi bei ti ke, ti li be bi ban ki tan kpaa iweehn ilile. Le ti be bi ban ki nan nyan jon Rom.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Abiraham nan kan ke u por ki mal nkuma, u nan ye ibin kobig paam le, le u poo Sɛra mu nan por ki lagir nmaala, amaa naa nan che u yada kan wee u jɔɔ-n diŋi.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Nnabum man be niin nin nan gbir taah chaan na, le bi fii ki kenn ti Apiu a nyaŋin, le bi ba mu kenn ti ichanniin yan kpiɛ itaa ki mal Rom a tiŋin ni. Pɔɔl kaah kan bi n-yonn man ni, le ni sei u ngbananpɔɔn, le u poor Uwonbɔr.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Nin ni ma biɛn ni, waa nan biɛnn Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinlin ni, amaa u yada nan mann sei u npɔɔn le u sei Uwonbɔr kupag.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Taah foo Rom n-yonn man nin, le bi jaa Pɔɔl le u be iniin le utɔtogir guu u.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: U num nan gbee ke Uwonbɔr jɔɔ npɔɔn ki li fir tun waah nan siin limɔsiinl ke U li tun man nin.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Ni ween itaa a bɔɔn le Pɔɔl yin Juu yaab a yudam ki lagin tab. Baah lagin tab n-yonn man ni, le Pɔɔl bei bi ke: N nabum, maa tun niba ki bii ti ninfob bee ti yaajam a kaali, amaa le bi nan choom ki taam ŋa kuloondiigin Jerusalam, le ki nan taam ŋubin Rom yaab a nɔɔn.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: U yada nmɔn bu le Uwonbɔr nan kan u ki kpiɛ ban jɔɔ nbuchin binben nin.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Le bi baanim ibinbaan, ki nan ban bin jaam, nan bu-n baa nan kan n galin yan kpaan nin bin ku mi.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: Imɔbon yan len ke, Bi nan kan u kpiɛ-n, kaa nan sɔb dɔ u binba bu le.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Amaa le Juu yaab ban kaa nan chee bin jaamin bu, le n nan ke n balin ubɔr Siiza le, amaa naa ye n ban n pag galin n ninfob le.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: amaa tim tɔɔl, ban wee Uwonbɔr li kan kpiɛ ban jɔɔ nbuchin binben nin. Taah ŋa yada nin U ma Uwonbɔr wan fiin ti yudaau Yisa nkumin nin bu.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Mambu le n ke bin che n kan ni ki bei ni tibɔr nmɔn. Iziral yaab biɛn kaah jɔɔ tama wan nin bu le bi lolim tikulor nan.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Ti toon bir bu le Uwonbɔr nan ta Masiya sei nkum amaa le U fiin u nkumin ke Un ŋa ti bininfoŋam Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Le bi jiin u ke, Taa kan tigbann nyan Judiya ki pag a bɔri, le ti nabum ban nyan niin nan kaa pag niba ti ban ki bii ŋi.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Amaa ti ban ti biɛ a potiɛl le, ti pii nyi ke nwonjiɛn san man wee a nɔɔ nan, bininfob takpem li pag ki bii mi iyag biɛn.

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Le bi nan siin lidaal lan wee bi li jir u ban, le bininfob takpem nan lagin u niin kɔɔ kaai nmɔn yaan le u piin ki wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr a naam a bɔr njakpanyiɛn ki tan kpaa kujɔɔg, ki wɔɔn bi Yisa a bɔr tan be Moziz a siimin yaan, nin Uwonbɔr a linlem a sɔbimin kaah ye man ki gbaar un kpenn bi potiɛl wee bin ŋa Yisa yada.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Biba a potiɛl nan kpenn u bɔr nmɔn buu, amaa biba mu kaa nan ŋa ti yada.
