eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Le limɔkpel nan dan bi sisiigin le bi piin ki worin, baah gbir Pɔɔl a mɔboi joor kaai n-yaa bɔɔn ke. Kasi a Naaŋ nan pag ibamɔn le ki sei ulinlenn Azaya, le u pag ki sei ni Yaajam:.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: Li chaa ki tan bei bininfob nan ke, Ni le yin gbir le kaa yin biɛ ni taai yi; ni le yin lig le kaa sin yin kan ni.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Bininfobin a sufu le pɔɔ; le bi kpaa bi tuli ke bi laa gbiri, ki ji dabin bi num. Naa bi ye man ke, bi bi li kan nin bi num, ki gbir nin bi tuli, ki biɛ ni taai bi sufu ni, ki kpenn bi binbem wee n teb bi.

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Mambu n ban ni biɛ le ke, Uwonbɔr a feeja a bɔr foon ban kaa ye Juu yaab n ban na, le bi li gbir ti.

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Pɔɔl nan be kudiig nmɔn yaan ibin ilee le ki yɔ ku bu. Le uninfo wan biɛn dan u ban ke un gaa u nbamɔm.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: U nan mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a naamin a bɔr le, ki wɔɔn bininfob Uyudaan Yisa Masiya a bɔr nin lipoobil le uba kaa ŋɔɔr wi.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mambu, ti yada kan che ti ŋa bininfoŋam Uwonbɔr ban nin, li che ti be Uwonbɔr ban nin nposɔɔn ti yudaau Yisa Masiya bu.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: wan a yada bu wee ti kan Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl lan yaan wee ti nin be nan. Le ni mɔ ti nin tama kan wee ti jɔɔ-n ke ti na li por Uwonbɔr a pag.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: Naa ye man a binba, amaa ti fala a jimin gbaa ni mɔ ti le, nan bu-n fala a jim le jɔɔ nnyiginim chaan;.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: nnyiginim li tenn ti nbinben le nbinben tenn ti tama.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Le tama kaa li che ti ji ifa, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr nan che U Kasi a Naaŋ kan wee U ta sei ti-n le kpaar U jiɛmin ki ŋa ti sufu ni.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Ni kan na, Uwonbɔr kaah gann isaŋ yan, taah kaa la nan jɔɔ npɔɔn nin bu, le Masiya dan ki nan kpo sei ban kaa jɔɔ nween ni.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: Ni li pɔɔ takpem nin uninfo uba n chee ki kpo uninfo buchin bu, amaa niba ke uba li chee ki kpo uninfo ŋan bu.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr kaah mag ki wɔɔn ti U tɔɔl a jiɛm man wee U jiɛ ti-n ye nan le: Taah le la nan ye tibirdamin, le Masiya kpo ki sei ti.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Waah ta U chamin ki ŋunn ti ki ŋa ti bininfoŋam nan, nan jon man le ye, U bu le ti yin kan nŋmarim ki nya Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋin yaan;.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Taah nan ye Uwonbɔr a duntiibin, le U bija a kumin bu tim nin U ma jir ki mɔŋ nin tab, nan jon man le ye taah jir mɔŋ nin Uwonbɔr nan, U bija a miɛfolin bu ti li kan fee gab.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Naa ye ni yaa binba, amaa ni ye nmɔn le ki sei ti Uwonbɔr ban nin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya wan che tim nin Uwonbɔr jir ki mɔŋin bu.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Mambu, tibir kaah nan nɔɔ uninfo uba bu ki kɔɔn dulinya nan yaan nin, wee u bir nmɔn ta nkum ki dan nin, ni ma che nkum dan bininfob biɛn bu, nan bu-n ti biɛn tun tibira.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Le nsiim nan nan foon ki miin tibir pii be dulinya ni. Amaa baah kaan tibir nan yaan wee nsiim kaa be.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Amaa nin ni ma biɛn ni, nkum li le ŋmag na nyan Adam a yonn ki nan kpaa Moziz a yonn, hali ki nyaŋ ban kaa tun tibir ke Adam kaah nan tun tibir ki nan bii Uwonbɔr a siimin, Adam nan jɔɔ wan yin ŋɔɔrin nin a nunaŋ le.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr a piin yin kaa nann Adam a birin ni. Ni ye bininfob takpem kpo uninfo uba nmɔn a bir bu ke, man ke Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanlin ma li jo man, le U piin yan nyann uninfo uba wan ye Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanlin nin ma li yab ki tan lagir le Un ta sei bininfob takpem!.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Jigin, Uwonbɔr a piin yin kaa nann uninfo uba a birin ni: Tibɔr a jim nɔɔ uba a bir bu le ki ta nkum dan, amaa ipiin yan wee Uwonbɔr ta sei ti-n ma nɔɔ bininfob takpem a toonbir bu le, le Uwonbɔr ke ti bɔr ŋan.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Ni ye uninfo uba a bir bu, le nkum nɔɔ uninfo uba nmɔn bu ki dan ke, man ke Uwonbɔr a buchin piin yan yab ki san ti kunyunyunbaagin ma jon man na, U ma ye Yisa Masiya wan sei ti limiɛfolin.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Mambu uninfo uba a bir bu kaah nan che Uwonbɔr bii bininfob biɛn nin, man mu le uninfo uba a buchin binbem bu mu le che Uwonbɔr bei bininfob biɛn ke bi bɔr ŋan le ki sei bi limiɛfol.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: A li ke naah nan ye uninfo uba a mɔyiil bu le nan che bininfob takpem kpann tibirdamin, man mu le uninfo uba a mɔcheel bu mu yin che bininfob takpem n kpann bibuchim.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Nsiimin nan kpiɛ le ki che titoonbir pɔr. Amaa le titoonbir kaah pɔr man nmɔn linyunyunbasanlin pɔr jon man na.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: naah ŋa man wee titoonbir che nkum nyaŋ bininfob biɛn nin, man mu le nbuchin binben mu yin che linyunyunbasanl mu n sei ti limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmi ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya bu.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Ti ni yin len ke nii? Ti le li mann tun tibira wee nin che Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl npɔr bee?.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Naa li fir ye man ni! Tibir bu ke, ti kpou; ke ni yin ŋa nii wee ti gbaa be tibir nmɔn yaan naa?.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Bee naa nyi ke tim ban biɛn nan fun Uwonbɔr a nyunful Masiya bu-n nan fun u kum a nyunful mu waa?.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Mambu ti nan kpo nin Yisa le bi pii ti nin u ma, Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin bu, a li ke Masiya kaah nan fii nkumin nin u Baa a pagin, man le ti mu yin be limiɛfol pɔnlin yaan.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Ni ye tim nin Yisa lagin kpo man ke, man le ti le ji na yin lagin fii nin U.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Ti nyi ke ti binben kpegimin nan lagin kpaa nin Masiya u, na li ŋa man ki bii tibir a gbanann, wee ti laa ji be tibir a yomirin ni;.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Nan bu-n, wan biɛn kpo ke, u nyan tibir a yomirin na.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Daandaan nan ni ye ti kpo nin Masiya ke, ti jɔɔ yada ke ti na li lagin be nin u.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Nan bu-n ti nyi ke Masiya kaah nan fii nkumin nin, waa ji yin kpo jigin ni; nkum kaa jɔɔ npɔɔn ki li fir nyaŋ wi.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: Nkum man wee u kpe-n, ti yaa bir bu le u kpo taaliba ki sei ti biɛn; amaa limiɛfol lan wee u nin jɔɔ-n, u nin be ki sei Uwonbɔr le.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Man nmɔn jigin, ni kan ni ba ki kpiɛ ban kpo ki sei tibirin na, amaa Masiya ni, ni jɔɔ limiɛfol ki sei Uwonbɔr.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Mambu ni laa ji che tibir ŋmag ni gbanann tan kpeg nan ni, ki laa chee sei tibir a gbanann nmɔn a bami.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Laa ta a gbanann a bɔgi ki sei tibir ke npooton a toontunn tiɛri, amaa ta a baa ki sei Uwonbɔr, ki li be ke bininfob ban nyan nkumin ki be limiɛfolin nin; wee ki nin ta a gbanann a bɔgi ki sei Uwonbɔr ke tibuchinn a toontunn tiɛrin.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Tibir kaa ji yin ye a yudaau wi, nan bu-n, nsiim kaa ŋmag ŋi, amaa Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanlin bu le a be.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Ni nin ye nii? Ti li tun tibir ki ke nsiim kaah kaa ji ŋmag ti wee ti be Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanlin nin buu? Naa li fir ye man ni!.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Naa nyi ke a ta a baa ki sei uninfo uba ki ye u yomu ki cheel u mɔb ke, a sin ye uyom le ki sei wan a mɔb wee a cheel nmɔn, ni ye a chee sei titoonbir le ke a ye titoonbir a yomu le, le ni joorim nan ye nkum le, bee a mu ji jɔɔ limɔcheel ke, ni ma yin che a ŋa uninfo buchin naa.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Amaa n tenn Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, nin nim ban nan ye titoonbir a yomi wee ki nan nɔɔ nwɔɔnim man wee ni nan gaa nin ni sufu biɛn nin bu.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Ni ŋmar ki nyan tibirin na le ki nin ŋa nbuchin binben a yomi.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: N ta bininfosaalib a binbem le ki pag nan bu-n ni tɔɔl a won naaŋin ni jɔɔ idaburig le. A li ke naah nan jɔɔ ni gbanann a bɔgi ki ŋaan tijaginn a toon a yomirin, nin npooton man mann kpiɛgin, daandaan nan ni ta ni gbanann ki ŋa nbuchin binben a yomirin na wee ki li be kasi.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Naah nan be tibir a yomirin nin, ni nan be ni wiib le nbuchin binben kaa nan ŋmag ni.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Ba a nyɔɔr wee ni kan n-yonn nmɔn nin naah ni nan san nan ifa daandaan nan naa? Ni ma biɛn nan a joorim nan ye nkum le!.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Amaa daandaan nan wee Uwonbɔr fee ni jaa ni tibirin wee ni nin ye Uwonbɔr a yomi nan, tinyɔɔr tan wee ni kan ye kasi a binbem le, le ni joorim ye limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Titoonbir a nyɔɔr ye nkum le, amaa le Uwonbɔr a piin ye limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm ti Yudaau Masiya ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: N nabum, n pag ki tenn bininfob ban nyi nsiimin le, naa nyi ke nsiimin jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm ki ŋmag uninfo n-yonn man wee u la fo be nlee?.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Ni che n mag wɔɔn ni, nsiim ŋmag chaldaan n-yonn man wee u chal la be nle, amaa ni ye u chal kpo ke, lijakunl nmɔn a siim kaa ji ŋmag wi.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Ni bu, ni ye u kun uja uba wee u chal la be ke, bi yi u kuwanchɔgiŋ le. Amaa ni ye u chal kpo ke, nsiim nmɔn kaa ji ŋmag wi, le u mu kaa ye kuwanchɔgiŋi, hali nin waah le ji kpenn kun uja uba nmɔn biɛn ni.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: Ni bu, n nabum, ni tɔɔl kpo ki sei nsiimin na, Masiya a gbanann nin bu, ke ni fir ŋa Masiya wan nan fii nkumin nin kaah ye ban, na li ŋa man wee ti lo iloon ŋan ki sei Uwonbɔr.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Tigbanann a bam kaah la nan ŋmag ti n-yonn man nin, le nsiimin nan che kɔlisa birug fii ti gbanann nin, ki che ti lo iloon ki sei nkum.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Amaa daandaan nan, taah nan kpo ki sei niwann nan nan ŋmag ti, ti nyan nsiimin yaan na, ti ni yin tun ki sei Kinaaŋ a binben pɔŋin le, naa ji ye nsiim kpegim man nan sɔb dɔ-n ni.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Ti ni yin len ke nii? Nsiimin ye tibir lee? Naa ye man ni! Ibamɔn buu maa bi yin nyi tibir kaah ye nan ni, nsiimin le che n biɛ. Maa bi yin nyi linunchɔril kaah jann ye nan ni, ni ye nsiimin kaa bi be ke, Laa jɔɔ linunchɔrili.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Amaa le tibir kan npɔɔn nsiimin yaan, ki che linunchɔril a booli pɔr n sufu ni. Ni bu nsiimin kaa bi be ke, tibir ma bi kpou.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Maah kaa la nan nyi nsiimin n-yonn man nin; n nan jɔɔ limiɛfol, amaa nsiim kaah nan nan foo nin, le tibir fii ki kan npɔɔn n gbanann nin le n kpo.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: Le n kan ke nsiim man bi ye min sei limiɛfolin, nin ta nkum le dan.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Tibir kan npɔɔn nsiimin yaan le ki larim, le nsiim nan bu, le tibir kum.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Ni bu nsiimin be kasi, le maah wɔɔn man nin be kasi, ki jɔɔ tibuchinn ki ŋan.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Ke ni wɔɔn ke niwann ŋan ke, ni labir nkum le ki dan n buu? Aai naa ye man ni! Amaa tibir nɔɔ nan ŋan bu le ki che nkum pɔr nni, na li ŋa man wee ti biɛ tibir kaah ye nan, nsiimin le che ti biɛ tibir kaah bir kaa tan jɔɔ magim man.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Ti nyi nsiimin ye kinaaŋ a binbem le; amaa min le jɔɔ tigbanann a bam, le bi kɔɔrim sei tibir ke uyomin.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: Maa biɛ nan n tun nmɔn a taai yi. Nan bu-n, nan wee n bi ban n tun nin maa tun ni ma, amaa niwann nan wee n nan nin, ni ma le n tun.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Tɔ le ni ye n tun nan wee maa bi ban n tun nin ke, n chee ke nsiimin ŋan.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Naah ye man nmɔn, naa ji ye min tɔɔl le tun man ni, amaa tibir tan be n sufu-n nin, le tun man.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: N nyi ke niwaŋann niba kaa be n sufu-n ni, ti ma ye n binben biimin yaan. Nan bu-n, n bi ban n tun nan ŋan, amaa maa yin fir tun ni.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: Nan bu-n, maah tun nan nin kaa ye niwaŋann nan wee n bi ban n tun nin; aai, nan kaa ŋan wee maa bi ban n tun nin, ni ma le n le gbaa tun.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Daandaan nan ni ye n tun maah kaa bi ban n tun man nin le ke, naa ji ye min le tun ni, amaa tibir tan be n sufu-n nin le tun man.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: N kan ke nsiim nmɔn tun itoon: Ni ye n ban n tun nan ŋan ke, nan bir be n ban.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: Nan bu-n, n potiɛmin ni mɔm le nin Uwonbɔr a siim;.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: amaa n kan nsiim miba mu kaah be n gbanann nin, ki kɔnn nsiim man be n potiɛmin ni, ki ŋaam tibir tan be n gbanann ni a bɔgi a loondikɔɔru.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Ba a nyunyunbaag a ninfobol ye maa! Ŋma yin feem ki nyam nkum a gbanann nan yaan naa?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: N tenn Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya bu! Ni ma bu, min tɔɔl a potiɛmin n ye Uwonbɔr a siimin a yomu le, amaa le n binben biimin mu ye tibir a siimin a yomu.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Mambu, daandaan nan Uwonbɔr kaa yin bii bininfob ban be nin Masiya nni.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: nan bu-n, Masiya bu le limiɛfol a naaŋ a siim feem ki jaa tibirin nin nkumin a siimin.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Uwonbɔr tun nan wee nsiimin kaa fir tun nna nan bu-n uninfosaal a gbanann kaa nan fir ŋmag nsiim nmɔn ni, le U tun U tɔɔl a big le ki nann uninfosaal wan ye tibirdaan nin ke un nan mann tibir a manug. Le ki bii tibir tan be uninfosaal a gbanann nin ni-n.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: na li ŋa man wee nin gbeen ti buchin a binbem kaah ye man nsiimin yaan, nan bu-n taah kaa ji be tigbanann a wiib a bamin wee ki nin be Uwonbɔr a Kasi a naaŋ kaah ye ti man nin.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Ban nɔɔ tigbanann a bamin a potiɛl be tigbanann nmɔn kaah ban man nin yaan le; amaa ban nɔɔ Kasi a Naaŋ a bamin mu a potiɛl be Kinaaŋin a bamin yaan le.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Wan ta u potiɛl ŋa tigbanann a bamin yaan ke ni ye nkum le, amaa lipotiɛl lan mu wee Kasi a Naaŋin ŋmagin ye limiɛfol le nin nposɔɔn;.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: wan ta u potiɛl ki ŋa tigbanann a bamin ke, u ye Uwonbɔr a dataag le, nan bu-n waa nɔɔ Uwonbɔr a siimin ni, hali kaa sin li fir nɔɔ mi.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Ban wee tigbanann a bam ŋmagin kaa li fir gbee Uwonbɔr a numi.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Nim, ma ke, tigbanann a bamin kaa ji ŋmag ni amaa Kinaaŋin le ni ŋmag ni, ni ye Uwonbɔr a Naaŋ be ni ni ke. Wan mu kaa jɔɔ Masiya a Naaŋin ke, waa ye Masiya yaa wi.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Amaa ni ye Masiya be ni ni ke, ni gbanann kpo tibir buu, nin ni ma biɛn ni, ni naaŋin fo nbuchin binben bu.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Ni ye wan a Naaŋ fiin Yisa nkumin nin be ni ni ke, wan nan fiin Masiya nkumin nin mu li sei ni gbanann tan kpegin limiɛfol u Naaŋin a pɔɔm man be ni nin bu.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Mambu, n nabum, ti jɔɔ naah ye ti tun man le amaa naa ye tigbanann a bami, ki li be Uwonbɔr a Naaŋin kaah wɔɔn ti man.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Ni ye ni nɔɔ tigbanann a bamin kaah wɔɔn ni man nin ke, ni li kpo; amaa ni che Uwonbɔr a Naaŋin ku tigbanann a bam a toon yin ke, ni li fo.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: nan bu-n, ban wee Uwonbɔr a Naaŋin lii bi samin, bi ye Uwonbɔr a naachim le.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah ta U Naaŋ kan sei ni-n kaa ye Kinaaŋ kan li ŋa ni bi yom wee ni ji li san bijawaami, amaa U Naaŋ nmɔn bu le che ni kpann U naachim. Uma bu le ti kuun ki yi ke, N Dindaau N Baa.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Uwonbɔr a Naaŋin tɔɔl le jin siɛra ti naaŋin ke ti sin ye Uwonbɔr a buwaab le.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Daandaan nan ni ye ti sin ye Uwonbɔr a buwaab ke, man ke ti ye U faajib le, tim nin Masiya le lagin ki ye Uwonbɔr a faajib, ni ye tim nin Masiya lagin ki jin U fala-n ke tim nin U ma ji na yin lagin kan U pag.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: N chee ke fala kan wee ti ji daandaan nan kaa foo ti ta bigin kupag kan wee Uwonbɔr na yin kpiir ti ni nni.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Niwann nan wee Uwonbɔr naan nan biɛn le guu le bi sufu ŋa sili ki lig lidaal lan wee Uwonbɔr na yin kpiir U naachimin ni.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Tiwann naan wann nmɔn biɛn a pool bii le taah kaa fir tun naah bi ye ti-n tun nan nin, naa ye tiwann naan wann nmɔn a wiibi, Uwonbɔr wan naan ti-n kaah la ban ti li be man le yen, amaa le ki sei ti tama.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: ke tiwann naan wann tɔɔl li nya nburim a yomirin, ki kɔɔ Uwonbɔr kaah ta wiib kan jɔɔ kupag ki sei U buwaabin yaan.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Ti nyi ke tiwann naan wann biɛn le dun le ke nmarwiɛn kaah wu man nin hali nin daandaan nan.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Naa ye ni yaa binba, amaa tim ban wee Uwonbɔr chinn sei ti U Kasi a Naaŋin a looi yin tɔɔl dun le ti sufu ni ki guu n-yonn man wee Uwonbɔr na yin ta ti kpann U tɔɔl a buwaab, ki sei ti nkpenim a manug ti gbanann nin.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Tama nan bu le ti kan feegab. Amaa tama kan wee ti ga-n kaa ye tama kolibi. Ŋma a tama be niwann nan wee u pii jɔɔ-n buu?.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Amaa ni ye ti jɔɔ tama taah kaa la jɔɔ tiwann tan nin bu ke, ti jɔɔ limɔril le ki guu ti.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Man le ye ni, Kinaaŋin ter ti le ti daburigi yin. Taa nyi taah yin miɛ Uwonbɔr ki ban nan ni, amaa Kasi a Naaŋin tɔɔl le gaa ki miɛ Uwonbɔr ki tenn ti nin ndundun man wee imɔleen kaa li fir pag wɔɔn ni.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Le Uwonbɔr wan feen ki lig ti sufu-n nin nyi Kasi a Naaŋin a potiɛl, nan bu-n Kasi a Naaŋin le gaan ki miɛ tenn Uwonbɔr a ninfob gannkaabin Uwonbɔr a bam kaah wɔɔn man.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Le ti nyi ke niwann biɛn nin, Uwonbɔr tun le ki tenn bininfob ban jiɛ win naah li ŋan ki sei bi man, U jɔɔ nan bu wee U yin bi.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Ban wee Uwonbɔr pii nan nyi ki bon ke bi na li chee ki kpenn ki li be ke u bija-n kaah be man nin, wee nin che U bija nmɔn nŋa kibininkpeeg u nabum takpem a sisiigin.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Ban wee Uwonbɔr bon bi bu-n, bi ma le u yin; ban wee U yin nmɔn, bi ma le U ke bi bɔr ŋan; ban wee U ke bi bɔr ŋan nin nmɔn, bi ma le u ji che bi kan kupag.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Ti ni yin len ke nii ki jiin ni niɛ nan paam maa? Ni ye Uwonbɔr si ti chaŋ ke, ŋma li fir kɔnn taa?.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Uwonbɔr wan kaa nan yii U tɔɔl a bija a kum bu, amaa le ki ta u mann kumanug ki sei ti biɛn, ke ni yin ŋa nii wee waa ji li fir ta U ki kpiɛ tiwann tan biɛn gurin bu ki sei taa?.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Ŋma li fir galin bininfob ban wee Uwonbɔr gann nan naa? Uwonbɔr le len ke bi bɔr ŋan.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Ŋma li fir bii baa? Masiya le nan kpo, le nan jon man le ye u nan fii nkumin na, ki ka Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam ki miɛ Uwonbɔr ki tenn ti.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Ŋma li fir bɔgir ti nyan Masiya a jiɛm nan yaan naa? Tibɔmugkaar bee nton bee limugil bee nkom bee nnan bee kidaŋ bee bitɔb a jugaa?.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: A li ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Fin bu le ti be nkum a daŋin lidaal biɛn; ki nann upee wan wee bi ŋa sili bin kɔrin.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Aai nin ni ma biɛn ni, ti jon ban nyaŋa Masiya a jiɛmin bu.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: N nyi kaa biɛn ni ke naa ye nkum bee limiɛfol, kaa ye malakam bee npɔniimi, kaa ye tiwann yɔɔr tan be daandaan nan bee tiwann yɔɔr tan la yin dan ni, kaa ye ipɔɔn iba.

eng: or height or depth, or any other thing that is created.
local: kaa ye nkpikper bee nnyunyɔɔni, kaa ye tiwann yɔɔr tan be tiwann naan wann nin biɛn ni, ni ba kaa be ki li fir bɔgir ti nyan Uwonbɔr a jiɛm man be ti Yudaau Masiya ni-n yaan ni.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: N pag ibamɔn le Masiya bu, maa por tibɔkari, le n potiɛl jin siɛra Kasi a naaŋin.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: n jɔɔ npoobiin le nin nan wu kan kaa jɔɔ diɛm n sufu ni.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: N bi ban ke Uwonbɔr a mɔpoolin bin jim wee min nin Masiya n yiɛr nin tab min tɔɔl a nabumin bu, ban wee min nin bi ma piɛ libolin.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: bi ma ye Iziral yaab. Bi ma le Uwonbɔr nan ta bi kpann U bimarim; ki che bi jɔɔ U tɔɔl a pag, ki poo tipoopɔnn sei bi, ki sei bi U siim, ki wɔɔn bi baah li jiɛn U man iwonjiɛn niin, le ki siin bi limɔsiinl.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Bi yaab le ye ti yaajam ninkpeebin, ban nyan ti yaajamin a bolin nin Masiya tɔɔl nyan niin le u ninforin nin bu, U ma ye Uwonbɔr wan jon niwann biɛn, U ma kpaan npagim man kaa jɔɔ diɛm! Ibamɔn.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: Naa wɔɔn ke Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinlin kaa gbeen ni. Nan bu-n naa ye ban nyan Iziral a bolin nin le ye Iziral yaabi.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: Ni mu kaa wɔɔn ke ban ye u yaabili yin biɛn le ye Abiraham a buwaabi. Amaa Uwonbɔr nan bei Abiraham ke, Azig a maalim ni li na yin ye a yaabili.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: Ni ma wɔɔn ke Abiraham a yaabili yan wee bi ma bi tininforin ni-n kaa ye Uwonbɔr a buwaabi, amaa nbuwaab ban wee Uwonbɔr nan poo tipoor ke Abiraham li maa-n bi ma ye u maalim.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Limɔsiinl nmɔn kaah nan len man sɔ: Jirib a nayonanbu N li jirin ki miin a poo Sɛra ŋmag kibijag le.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Naa ye ni yaa binba, Ribiɛka a buwaabin nan ye baa uba a buwaab le, u ma ye ti baa Azig.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Nin ni ma biɛn ni, baah kaa la nan ma njaanbum nmɔn nin, bee nin nbuwaab nmɔn n nan tun nan ŋan bee nan birin, ni ye Uwonbɔr kaah ban ki gann nan le n sir.

eng: It wasnt because of what was done but because of Gods call. This was said to her: The older child will be a slave to the younger one .
local: Naa ye itoon ni amaa ni ye wan yin nin le, Uwonbɔr le nan bei nbuwaab nmɔn a na ke, Uninkpel le na yin tun ki tenn kibig.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: A li ke naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin: Jekɔb le N jiɛ amaa Esɔɔ le N nan.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Ti ni yin len ke nii? Uwonbɔr kaa tun nan kpaan naa? Naa ye man ni!.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Uwonbɔr nan bei Moziz ke, Ban wee N ban N san bi kunyunyunbaagin bi ma le N yin san, ban wee N ban N jogirin bi ma le N yin jogir.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Mambu naa ye uninfosaal a bamin bee u pɔɔninba bu, amaa ni ye Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu le.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin u nan bei ubɔr Faro ke, N jiŋ nnaam ni niɛ nan bu le, ke N ta N pɔɔm ki ŋa a ni ki che N yinlin mɔɔn dulinya nan a gbanann biɛn.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: N nabum n potiɛl nin maah miɛ Uwonbɔr ki ban man ye le ke Iziral yaab n kan feegab.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Mambu Uwonbɔr san ban wee U ban Un san kunyunyunbaag nle kunyunyunbaag, ki mann pɔɔg ban a tuli pɔɔ-n a tuli.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: N li fir ji bi siɛra ke bi num man Uwonbɔr a toonl bu, amaa bi numan nmɔn kaa nɔɔ baah nyi U man ni.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Ni ni uba li beim ke: Ke ni ŋa nii wee Uwonbɔr le la biɛn nin galin taa? Ŋma li fir yii Uwonbɔr kaah ban man naa.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Baah kaa nyi nbuchin binben man nyan Uwonbɔr ban nin wee ki ban bi tɔɔl kaah li nɔɔ man ki li jɔɔ nbuchin binben nin, ni bu che baa chee sei Uwonbɔr a buchin binbemin ni.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Amaa ke a mu ye ŋmaa, uninfosaalaa, ki pag jiin Uwonbɔr aa? Niwann nan wee a naan nan li fir bei fin wan naan nin ke, Ba ŋa wee a naanim man naa? .

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Masiya le ye nsiimin a diɛm le ban biɛn ŋa u yada ke bi li jɔɔ nbuchin binben.

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Uyɔgmaar kaa jɔɔ npɔɔn nin un ta liyɔgkonl liba ki maa kuchiig daal a bool ki jin ta liyɔgkonl nmɔn ki maa libool lan wee bi jɔɔ tun itoon idaa biɛn naa?.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Moziz nan sɔb ki wɔɔn nbuchin binben man be nsiimin yaan kaah wɔɔn man ke: Uninfo wan ŋmag nsiim nan kaah wɔɔn man nin ke, u li be nin nsiimin.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Ke ni ye Uwonbɔr gann ki wɔɔn U ŋuufiiŋ, ki che U pɔɔm nyan npaan ni, wee ki jɔɔ limɔril nin tiwann tan fiin U ŋuul wee ki bi kpaan nin Un bii ti-n naa?.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Amaa nbuchin binben man nɔɔ nin yada n ma wɔɔn ke: Laa pag a sufu ni ke, Ŋma na yin do paab baa? .

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Ke ni ye U ŋa man ki ban bin biɛ U pag takpeŋ kan wee U ta ŋa bi ni-n, ban wee U nan chinn ŋa bi sili ki guun kupag nmɔn nin.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: bee, Ŋma na yin jiir kitakpiin tiŋaa? .

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: hali tim, ban wee U yin nin mu, naa ye ban nyan Juu yaab a bolin nin a binba, amaa ban nyan ban kaa ye Juu yaab a bolin nin mu?.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Amaa ni len ke baa? Limɔbonlin mal a ban na; li be a mɔnn nin a sufu ni, li ma ye, yada a mɔbonl lan wee ti mɔɔn nin:.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: A li ke waah len man Hozaya a gbɔŋin nin: N li yin ban kaa ye n ninfobin ke, n ninfob; le ki yin maah kaa jiɛ libol lan nin Maah jiɛ libol lan.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Ke ni ye a yaar a mɔb ki len ke, Yisa le ye Uyudaan, wee ki ŋa yada a sufu ni ke Uwonbɔr fiin U nkumin ke, a li kan feegab.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Le, Ni li ŋa ke kitiŋ kan a ninfob wee N nan ke, Naa ye N ninfobin, bi li yin bi ke, Uwonbɔr wan fo-n a buwaab.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Nan bu-n a sufu ni le a ŋa yada le Uwonbɔr ke a bɔr ŋan, a mɔnn le a yaar ki pag le ki kan feegab.

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Azaya nan mɔr Iziral yaab bu le: Nin Iziral yaab kaah le yab ke limɔgil a tanbiidil nmɔn biɛn ni, bi ni wama nan le nan yin ŋmar.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: A li ke Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ kaah len man nin, Wan gaa U ki jin ke, waa li nyi ji ifa.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Nan bu-n Uyudaan li dar dulinya nan yaab a tul ki diɛ weem.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Juu yaab nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab kaa jɔɔ nbɔgirimi, uyudaan uba nmɔn le ye bi biɛn nan a yudaau le u kaar takpem be ban biɛn yi win nbu.

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: Ni nann ke Azaya kaah pii nan len ke: Naa ye ipɔɔn biɛn daan Uyudaan kaah nan guur U yaabili yin ke, ti bi yin be le ke Sodom, Ti bi yin be le ke Gomoriya n.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Ni ma bu Wan biɛn yin Uyudaan a yinl ke u li kan feegab.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Ti yin len ke nii? Ti yin len le ke ban kaa ye Juu yaab, wee kaa nan nɔɔ nbuchin binben, bi nin kan nbuchin binben nin na, nbuchin binben man nyan yada kan wee bi jɔɔ-n.

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Ni yin ŋa nii wee bin yin baah kaa ŋa wan yada nan a yinl laa? Ke bi yin ŋa nii ki ŋa baah kaa gbir wan a bɔr nan yadaa? Ke bi yin ŋa nii ki gbir wee naa ye uba li mɔɔn ti ki tenn baa?.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Amaa Iziral yaab, ban nɔɔ nsiimin a buchin binbem bu-n, kaa kan mi.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Ke bi yin ŋa nii ki mɔɔn ti wee naa ye bi tun bi lee? A li ke naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin, Ban mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin tenn bininfobin tenn bi tibɔmɔnn le.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Ba ŋa wee baa kan naa? Nan bu-n baa nan nɔɔ ke bi yada le yin che bin kan ni, amaa bi nan ŋaan le ke bi toon yin li yin che bin kan nin. Le ki gbiɛr litangbagil gbiɛrikaal.

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Amaa naa ye bi biɛn le chee sei tibɔŋann nmɔn ni. Ni ma bu le Azaya len ke, Uyudaan, ŋma ŋa ti mɔleen nan yadaa.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: A li ke naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin: Lig, N ta litangbagil le bil Zayɔnn lan li che bininfob ngbiɛr litangbagil takpenl lan li che bininfob nlu, le wan gaa U ki jin ke waa li nyi ki ji ifa.

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Ni ma bu yada a ŋaam nya baah gbir imɔleen nin yaan le, imɔleen nin mu a gbirim nya Masiya a bɔrin a mɔɔnimin le.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Amaa le n baan ke, Baa gbiraa? Ibamɔn bi gbira: Bininfob gbir bi kukɔl kutingbɔŋ nan a gbanann biɛn na, bi bɔr foo dulinya nan a tariga.

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: Le n ji baan ke: Iziral yaab kaa biɛ ni taai yaa? Chinchinn Moziz nan len ke, N li che ni jɔɔ linunchɔril ban kaa ye ni bol yaabin bu; ki che ni ŋuulin fii libol lan yaab kaa biɛr niwann a taai yin bu.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Le Azaya ŋa lipoobil ki len ke, Bininfob ban kaa nan choon ban min kan ma; N kpiir N ba ki wɔɔn ban kaa nan baan n bu-n na.

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Amaa ki jiin Iziral yaab paam, U len ke, Iyoo biɛn N tann N nɔɔ le ke N bib ban ye tukangbirdam nin limɔyiil damin.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: Le n baan bi ke, Uwonbɔr yii U ninfob lee? Naa ye man ni! Min tɔɔl ye Iziral a ninfou le, Abiraham a yaabil le yem, n nyan Bɛnjamɛnn a bolin le.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Uwonbɔr kaa yii U ninfobi, ban wee U pii nan nyi-n. Naa nyi ke Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ni sɔb ki jiin Elaja paam, waah nan galin Iziral yaab Uwonbɔr ban ki ke:.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: N yudaau, bi ku a linlemin biɛn ki ber a manug a binbin jaa tuma; N yaa binba le gur, le bi ji gbaar bin kumaa?.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Ke Uwonbɔr nan jiin u ke nii? U nan jiin u ke, N la jɔɔ bijab turilile ban gur kaa la gbaan bi dun bu ki jiɛn litingbanl Baal li.

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Man le ye ni, taah be n-yonn man nan, bininfob wama nan le Uwonbɔr gann U nyunyunbasanl bu.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: Ke ni ye U nyunyunbasanl bu le U gann bi ke, man ke naa ye baah tun nan bu; ni bi ye baah tun nan bu ke, U nyunyunbasanl nmɔn kaa ji bi yin ye linyunyunbasanli.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Ba ji kpiɛ? Iziral yaab kaah nan ban bin kan nan nin inunman nin baa kan ni, amaa Uwonbɔr kaah gann ban nin bi ma kan. Ban gurin a sufu nan pɔɔ le.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: a li ke naah sɔb man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin: Uwonbɔr le ta kinaaŋ kpiiŋ ki sei bi, ki sei bi inunbil yan kaa ga nin itul yan kaa gbiri, hali ki nan kpaa dinan.

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Le ubɔr Defid len ke: Che bi jijibol n kpann kupeŋ ki chor bi, nin litangbagil gbiɛrikaal nin litudaril ki sei bi.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: Che bi num paam n bɔŋ wee bi laa ga, ki che bi fala n kpann litugil lan kpiɛg ki nyag bi bu iyoo biɛn.

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: Le n ji baan ke: Bi nan gbiɛr ke bin lu ki laa ji fii bee? Naa ye man ni! Amaa baah nan burin bu, ni ma che ban kaa ye Juu yaab kan nŋmarim na li ŋa man wee Juu yaab li jɔɔ bi linunchɔril.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Amaa ni ye bi burim nmɔn bu le dulinya yaab kan njɔɔm, le baah lann nan nin che ban kaa ye Juu yaabin kan njɔɔm ke, man ke Juu yaab mu nan jiɛn bi bir ki kan nŋmarim ke, dulinya nan yaab li kan njɔɔm nin jo man.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: N pag bei nim ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan le. Maah le ye ntoon ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan man, n jɔɔ linunmanl n toon nmɔn bu.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: ki jɔɔ tama ke n bi li fir ke n bin che min tɔɔl a ninfob li jɔɔ linunchɔril wee bi ni biba nkan feeja.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Ni ye Uwonbɔr a yimin ye jir mɔŋ nin dulinya le ke, ni ye U ji nan jir gaa bi ke ni nin yin be nii? Ni naah yin ye limiɛfol lan nyann nkumin le.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Ni ye boroboro a yom wama man wee bi yin chinn ta sei Uwonbɔr nan be kasi ke, boroboro n biɛn be kasi le; ni ye kisiig a sibonl be kasi ke, ki bon tɔɔl be kasi le.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Ni ye Uwonbɔr gaa kisiig nmɔn a bon iba par ke, wee nim ban ye olif a siigin yan pan itɔɔl wee bi gaa isiig iba ki nan tugin ni wee ni nyu ni pɔɔm nya olif a siigin yaan nin.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: ni laa far igbingban ibon nmɔn bu. Ni ye ni far igbingban ibon nmɔn bu ke, ni li nyi ke: Nim ban ye ibon nin kaa ter lisibonlin ni, amaa lisibonlin li ter ni.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Ni ba ke a li len ke, Uwonbɔr nan gaa ibon iba le par le ki taam tugin.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Ni ye ibamɔn le. Baah kaa nan ŋa yada-n bu le Uwonbɔr nan gaa bi par gab, amaa le fin si a yada bu. Laa jɔɔ kalanbaani amaa li san Uwonbɔr.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Ni bu ni ye Uwonbɔr kaa nan jaa isiig a bon ke waa yin jaa a mu.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: Lig Uwonbɔr kaah jɔɔ nbinben ŋan nin inunkoon man: U nunkoon yin tɔɔ ban lu-n bu le, amaa le u binben ŋamin tɔɔ a bu, ni ye a le laa be nin U binben ŋam nmɔn ke. Naa ye man ke U li gaa fin tɔɔl gab.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Hali ni ye ban nan lu-n jiɛn nbiɛnim ke, Uwonbɔr li jiin bi tugin, nan bu-n u jɔɔ npɔɔn man ji li fir jiin bi tugin.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Ni biɛn nan a bɔɔn, ni ye Uwonbɔr nan bi gaa fin wan ye kulɔŋ a olif wee ki ji gaa bukpaab a olif ki ta tugin tab ke, li nyi ke naa pɔɔ sei U nin Un gaa bukpasib a bon ki ji jiin mi tugin mi tɔɔl a sib bu!.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: N nabum, maa ban ni li be kaa nyi tibɔwoonkaar nan ni, tan li che ni laa donn niba naah nyi nan bu: Iziral yaab a tupɔɔm man bi ba bu-n kaa yin yuuri, amaa le ni yin be man le hali nin ban kaa ye Iziral yaabin kaah na li dan Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Ni ma bu Iziral yaab biɛn li ŋmar, a li ke naa sɔb man Uwonbɔr gbɔŋin nin: Ufeerin yin nya Zayɔnn le; U li nyan nan kaa ye liwonjiɛnl Jekɔb a maalimin ki gab.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Ni ma ye tipoor tan wee N poo sei bi n-yonn man wee N na yin nyan bi tibirin nin.

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Ki jiin ban yii tibɔŋann nin paam ke, bi ye Uwonbɔr a dataam le nim bu; amaa ki jiin nganim paam ke, bi ye Uwonbɔr kaah jiɛ ban le bi yaajamin bu.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Uwonbɔr a piin a seim nin U yim kaa kpenn ni.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: A li ke nim ban kaa la nan chee Uwonbɔr a mɔb isaŋ iba wee Uwonbɔr nan san ni kunyunyunbaag Iziral yaab a mɔyiilin bu-n.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: mambu che bi mu yii Uwonbɔr a mɔb le ke nin che Uwonbɔr n san bi kunyunyunbaag ke waah san ni man nin.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Uwonbɔr ŋa uninfo biɛn uloondiikɔɔr le bi mɔyiilin bu, naah li ŋa man wee Un fir san bi biɛn nan kunyunyunbaag.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Uwonbɔr-a, U lam nin U biɛm nyɔɔ le ki yab lagir! U bɔr a jim pɔɔ nin uninfo n biɛ, le uba kaa li fir nɔɔ U sami!.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: Ŋma nyi Uyudaan a potiɛl laa? Bee ŋma ye wan gbeer waa.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Ŋma la nyi ta ni ba sei Uwonbɔr a, ki ke Uwonbɔr n nan yɔ u jiin naa.

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Nan bu-n niwann biɛn nyan U ma ban le; le niwann biɛn be U bu le U ye niwann biɛn. U ma kpaan npagim hali n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmi! Ibamɔn.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Mambu, n balin ni le n nabum, Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu, ni ta ni gbanann ki ŋa liwanfol a manug, kan be kasi ki gbee Uwonbɔr a num, ni ma ye liwonjiɛnl a toonl kinaaŋin.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Ni laa chee sei dulinya nan yaab a binbem jigin ni, amaa ni mann pɔŋ ni potiɛla wee ni binbemin kpenn. Ni ma le ni li fir mag ki chee Uwonbɔr a bam kaah ye man. U bam man ŋan ki jɔɔ linungbeel ki be chasi-n.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah sam kunyunyunbaag kan nin bu le n bei ni biɛn nan ke: Uba laa tiɛr ke u ye lininfo gbingbemil ki jon waah be man nni, amaa che uninfo biɛn li tiɛr ibamɔn a bɔjil ki tenn u bau, ke yada kan wee Uwonbɔr ta sei win kaah kpaa man nin.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Ni kpaa le nin uninfo biɛn n chee sei ubɔr yudaan a ninkper a pɔɔm kaah wɔɔn man, nan bu-n tininkper a pɔɔm li kaa be kaa ye Uwonbɔr kaah siin man ni. Tininkper a pɔɔm man be-n Uwonbɔr le ta mi ŋa.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: A li ke ti biɛn nan kaah jɔɔ gbanann tiba wee ti jɔɔ ibɔg man ni, igbanann bɔg nmɔn biɛn kaa lagin tun litoonl liba.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Ni ma bu, wan yii tininkper a pɔɔm a siimin a nɔɔm ke u yii Uwonbɔr kaah siin man nin le, bininfob ban ŋa man ke bi li ban litudaril jaa biba bu.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: man mu le ye ni Masiya ni, ti ninfo gbaluŋ le lagin ki ŋa tigbanann tiba, le ligbanann bɔgil liba ye igbanann bɔg yan gurin yaan le.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Ni ma bu ban ŋmag tininkper a pɔɔmin kaa ye kuwansanwaŋ ki sei bininfob ban tun nan ŋan ni, amaa ban kaa tun nan ŋan nin. A ban a li be a wiib ki laa san wan ŋmag tininkper a pɔɔmin naa? Man ke li tun nan ŋan na wee un pagiŋ.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Ti jɔɔ ipiin balbal le, ke Uwonbɔr kaah sei ti U nyunyunbasanl lan nin. Ni ye wan a piin ye ilinlen leen a pagim ke, che un ta tun u yada kaah kpaan man.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Ni ma bu u ye Uwonbɔr a toontunnu wan li tun nan ŋan le ki sei ni. Amaa ni ye a tun nan kaa ŋan ke, a li san bijawaama, nan bu-n waa ŋmag takoobi yɔli. U ye Uwonbɔr a toontunnu le, wan wee U ta u siin U ŋuufiiŋin yaan ke un dar wan tun titoonbir a tul.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Ni ye wan a piin ye un tun ke, che un tun; wan a piin ye u li wɔɔn ke, che un wɔɔn;.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Mambu, ni kpaa le nin ti li jɔɔ limɔcheel ki tenn ban ŋmag tininkper a pɔɔm, wee ni laa ye taah li ŋa man ki nya litudarilin a binba, amaa naah li ŋa man wee ni potiɛl nlaa galin ni.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: ni ye wan a piin ye u li pɔɔgir u tab a gbanann ke, che u li pɔɔgira; ni ye wan a piin ye u li chaar tijiir ki ter bitab bi bɔr mugkaarin ke, che u li pu man na; ni ye wan a piin ye un ŋa usanliir le ke, che u li jɔɔ nsaliibinben nbamɔm; ni ye wan a piin ye u li san bininfob kunyunyunbaag le ke, che u li ŋaan man nin npopeen na.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu le ni yɔ lampo, ni ma bu ban ŋmag tininkper a pɔɔm ye Uwonbɔr a toon tum le, ban ta bi saŋi biɛn sei tininkper a pɔɔm a siimin a ŋmagim.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Ni kpaa le nin njiɛm le ye ibamɔn. Ni li nan nan ye tibir; ki li ŋmag nan ŋan nin kenken.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Tun sei uninfo biɛn naah kpaan a tun sei u man na: Ni ye lampo a pɔnl le nɔɔŋ ke, a yɔ lampo nmɔn na; ni ye nchaarim a pɔnl le nɔɔŋ ke, a chaara; wan kpaan nin a sei u jilima ke, a sei mu, wan kpaan a don u ke a don mu.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Ni ta ni baga ki ŋa kunabitiig a jiɛmin yaan. Ki li tenn tab jilima ki jon ni tɔɔl.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Ni laa che lipɔnl liba ngur dɔ kaa yɔn ni, man ke lipɔnl lan le gbaa be ki ye ti li jiɛ tabin a binba, ni ma bu wan jiɛ uninfosaal kpiɛg ke u gbeen nsiimin na.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Ni laa san ligbanyagili, amaa wee ki li ŋmag kinaaŋin ni sufu ni, ki li tun tenn Uyudaan na.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Nsiim man ye, Laa tun iwanchɔgin toon ni, Laa ku, Laa su, Laa jɔɔ linunchɔril a kpiɛg a wann bu, nin nsiim man gurin biɛn, i lagin ki be lisiinjal liba nan yaan le: Li jiɛ a kunkɔɔn tab ke ŋaah jiɛ a ba man nin.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Ni che ni li mɔ na ni tama ni, ki li jɔɔ limɔril ni bɔr mugkaarin, ki li jɔɔ yada ni wonmiɛlin.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Njiɛm kaa tenn kunkɔɔn tab kidaŋi mambu njiɛm ye nan gbeen nsiimin le.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Ni li por naah jɔɔ nan ki tenn Uwonbɔr a ninfob ban ye bilanjiimin na. Ki li ŋaan bininfob tiŋann na.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Ni ŋaam nan, ni biɛm taah be n-yonn man daandaan nan a taai ya. Isaŋ foo nin ni ligir ni jiɛmin na, nan bu-n ti feegabin mal ti ki jon taah nan chinn ŋa yada n-yonn man nin na.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Ni li kaab tikaar jaar ban mugir ni buu; ni li kaaba ki laa poog imɔɔŋ ki ŋaan bi.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Kutaag a wonim mala; le nweehn be forin le nmɔn. Ni ma bu ni che ti ta libunbɔnl a toon yin ki bil ngbaan wee ki ta nwonween a gbaginu ki yee.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Ni li jɔɔ nmɔn nin naah mɔ ban nin na; ki li wii nin ban wii-n na.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Ni che ti binbem li ween na, a li ke ti be nweehn le-n, ki laa ye ban nyu ndaam a booli biɛn bee bidanyugbeebi, ki laa tun iwanchɔgin toon ti piibanlin bee ti jabanlin nin iwanjaab toon a boolin yi, ki laa jɔɔ imɔkpel nin lipiiponli.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Ni li be nin tab nin nmɔn na. Ni laa jɔɔ kalanbaani; amaa ni chee ki li be nin bilanjiima. Ni laa wɔɔn ni biɛmi.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Amaa ni ta Uyudaan Yisa Masiya ki yee ke kuwan yee waŋin, ki laa ban nsan man wee ni li nɔɔ ki tun tigbanann a kɔlisa birugin a bami.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Ni laa ta uba a bir ki yɔ wi. Maan ni ba ki tun nan ŋan uninfo biɛn a numin.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Ni ye a bi li fir ke, naah tɔɔ a bu nan, li be nin nposɔɔn nin uninfo biɛn na.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: Ni laa ta tibir ki yɔ tibiri, n jɔtiib, amaa ŋa npaan sei Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋin, ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Ni ye min le n jiin; min le yin yɔ bi, Uyudaan le len man.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Amaa: Ni ye nkom jɔɔ a dataag ke, a jim mu; ni ye nnyunyum jɔɔ u ke, a sei mu nnyum wee un nyu. Ni ye a tun ni niɛ nan ke, a yin jin u ifa le.

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Ni laa che tibir n nyaŋ ni, amaa ni ta nan ŋan ki nyaŋ tibira.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Tim ban a yada pɔɔ-n ye ti ter ban a yada kaa pɔɔ-n bi tugi a tumin le, wee ki laa ban ti tun nan li gbee ti tɔɔl a numi.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Che ti biɛn nan ntun nan li gbee a kunkɔɔn tab a num u tɔɔl a ŋann bu, wee u yada n mann yabig.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Ni ma bu hali Masiya kaa nan tun nan li gbee u tɔɔl a numi, amaa a li ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Tisugir tan wee bi sugiriŋin lu min bu le.

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Nan biɛn wee bi nan sɔb bil n-yonn man nan jon nin bi nan sɔb bil ti yaa wɔɔnim bu le, wee ti li jɔɔ limɔril nin ngbananpɔɔn nin tama imɔleen nin bu.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: Uwonbɔr wan tenn limɔril nin ngbananpɔɔn nin un sei ni nmɔkpaa a tinaaŋ wee ni li nɔɔ Masiya.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: wee ni ye ni jɔɔ sufu uba nin mɔbiba ke ni li fir pag Uwonbɔr wan ye Uyudaan Yisa Masiya a baa-n.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Ni gaam tab, ke Masiya kaah gaa ni man nin, naah li ŋa man wee ni fir ta npagim ki sei Uwonbɔr.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: Ni ma bu le min bei ni ke Masiya ye Juu yaab a toontunn u Uwonbɔr a bamɔn bu le, ke nin gbeen U mɔsiinl lan nan siin sei ti yaajamin.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: wee ban kaa ye Juu yaabin pag Uwonbɔr U nyunyunbasanl bu, ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Mambu n li pag fin Uwonbɔr a yinl ban kaa ye Juu yaabin a sisiigin; n li gaah liwonpagil a lai ki don a yinl.

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: Jigin Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ len ke, Ni che ni li mɔ ni nin U ninfob nan na, nim ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Ni gaam wan a yada kaa pɔɔ-n na, wee ki laa ji tab tibɔr tan ye limɔkpel a bɔri.

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: Le jigin, Pagim Uyudaan, nim ban biɛn kaa ye Juu yaab nan, wee ki gaah ilan ki pag U, nim bininfob nan biɛn.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Uninfo uba a yada bu li fir che un ji tijiir a booli biɛn, amaa uninfo wan a yada kaa pɔɔ-n, u ji tikpinfar a binba le.

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: Le jigin, Azaya a gbɔŋin ni sɔb ke, Jesi a sibonlin yin pɔɔ le ki pa donn, wan yin fii ki gaa kutingbɔŋ nan ki ŋmagin; ban kaa ye Juu yaabin na yin jɔɔ tama u ma bu le.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Wan ji tijiir a booli biɛn nin laa yin wan kaa ji tijiir biɛn nin yɔli, wan mu kaa ji tijiir biɛn nin mu laa galin wan ji tijiir a booli biɛn nin ni, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr gaa u daan na.

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: Che tama a Wonbɔru win gbeen ni nmɔn biɛn nin nposɔɔn naah ga U jin nin bu, wee ni li jɔɔ tama kan yab ki tan lagir, Kasi a Naaŋin a pɔɔm bu.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: A ye ŋma wee ki li fir galin uba a toontunnu waa? U tɔɔl a yudaau ban le u sisiim bee u lum yin nya. U li sir, Uyudaan li fir che un sir.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: N nabum, min tɔɔl kaa biɛni, ni tɔɔl gbee nin nbinben ŋan, ki jɔɔ nbiɛm takpem, ki li fir wɔɔn tab nbamɔm.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Uninfo uba tiɛr ke lidaal liba ŋan jon yan gurin na, le uba mu tiɛr ke idaa biɛn ye nba le. Che uninfo biɛn n nɔɔ waah tiɛr man u potiɛlin.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: Maah nan sɔb lipoobil a bɔr tan ki sei ni-n nann le ke n ji tiɛr ni le-n, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr kaah sam kunyunyunbaagin bu.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Wan ŋa lidaal liba kasi ki tiɛr ke li ŋan jon idaa yan gurin, u ŋaan man ki tenn Uyudaan le jilima. Uninfo wan kaa kɔ tijiir tiba nan, u ji ti ki tenn Uyudaan le jilima, ni ma bu u poor Uwonbɔr le ki nin ji; wan mu kɔ tijiir tibam nan, u kɔ ti ki tenn Uyudaan le jilima, le ki poor Uwonbɔr.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: ke n ŋa Masiya a toontunnu ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ki ta Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin tun itoon ke uminmanirin, wee Uwonbɔr n gaa ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ke kumanug kan wee Kasi a Naaŋ ŋa ki kasi-n.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Ti ni uba kaa fo be u binba bu, le ti ni uba kaa kpeg u binba bu.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Mambu n jɔɔ ligbingbanfaal Masiya bu nin maah tun litoonl lan ki tenn Uwonbɔr.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Ni ye ti fo be ke, ti be ki sei Uyudaan le, ni ye ti ji kpo ke, ti kpo ki sei Uyudaan le. Ni ma bu ti fo bee ti kpo ke, Uyudaan le ye ti.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: Maa li nyi ki pag niba wee naa ye Masiya kaah taam tun litoonl lan ni, ke n liin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin a sam ki wɔɔn bi wee bin chee Uwonbɔr a mɔb nin maah wɔɔn bi man nin maah tun man.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu, le Masiya nan kpo ki fii nkumin naah li ŋa man wee U li ye ban fo nin ban kpo a Yudaau.

eng: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of Gods Spirit. So Ive completed the circuit of preaching Christs gospel from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum.
local: Le ni ma ye siɛra nin ligaril a toon a pɔɔm nin Kinaaŋin a pɔɔmin. Ni bu naah nyan Jerusalam ki tan kpaa Dalimasiya a yerin , n mɔɔn Masiya a bɔŋann nin ki diɛ niin na.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: Ke fin, ba ŋa wee a ji a nabig tibɔraa? Bee ba ŋa wee a yin a nabig yɔlii? Nan bu-n ti biɛn na li sir Uwonbɔr a nintogil tibɔr a ji bol.

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: N pag ti ninsa Fiibi wan tun Uwonbɔr a toon Sechariya a wonjiɛm a laguŋin sei na.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Maah ye Uwonbɔr wan fo-n, Uyudaan le len, idun biɛn na li gbaan N nintogil; ilanbil biɛn na li kpiir ki bei Uwonbɔr.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: N le gbaa boŋ le ke n mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin nan yaan wee bininfob kaa la nyi gbir Masiya a bɔrin ni, wee ni laa ye ke n maa tɔɔn uba a fiɛl bu le.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: N bei ni le ke u dan ke ni gaam u ke naah kpaan Uyudaan a ninfob li gaan bi tab man Uyudaan a yinl bu, wee ki ter u waah li ban literil lan ni ban, nan bu-n u ter bininfob takpem pam, nin min gbaa tɔɔl.

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Ni ma bu, ti biɛn nan na li pag ki wɔɔn Uwonbɔr ti toon kaah ye man.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: Amaa ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Baah kaa nan bei ban u bu-n li kan, le ban kaa nan gbir li biɛ ni taai.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: Poor Pirisila nin Akula, ban ye n toon tun tab Yisa ni-n.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Mambu ni che ti ta tab a bɔr a jim jiɛn na. Ki che ni li be ni sufu ni ke naa yin ye litangbag gbiɛrikaal bee naa yin leg ni nabig a sami.

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Ni ma nan gbaa leg ki che maa kan nsan ki li dan ni ban ni.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: Bi nan ta bi miɛfol ŋa kidaŋin le min bu. Naa ye n yaa binba amaa biwonjiɛm a laginn ban kaa ye Juu yaabin biɛn a pool sɔɔn bi bu.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Maah be Uyudaan Yisa ni-n bu, maa biɛn ni, tijiir tiba kaa be ki ye tijaginn ti baan tɔɔli. Amaa ni ye uba tiɛr ke tijiir tiba ye tijaginn ke, man ke u ma ban le ti ye tijaginn.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: Amaa daandaan nan niba yaan kaah kaa ji be n tun tiyerin nan yaan nan, wee n mu a potiɛl le be ni ban ibin takpem ke n kan ni nan.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: Poor biwonjiɛm a laguŋ ban lagin tab bi niin yin nin mu. Poor n jɔ kpaarimɔɔn Epenetusi, wan nan chinn ŋa Masiya yada Asiya a yerin nin.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Ni ye a nabig a sufu bii ŋaah ji tijiir tan nin bu ke, man ke ŋaa ji nɔɔ njiɛm a sami. Laa che ŋaah ji tijiir tan nin bii a nabig kan wee Masiya nan kpo u bu-n ni.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: n bon le ke n na yin jo Sipenn ke n li kan ni. N jɔɔ tama ke n naah yin jo jo ke n li gɔnn ni, wee ki che ni terim n jo n sanchonlin, ni bɔɔn bu ki miin n pii jin taah lagin be-n a mɔm wamau.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Poor Meri wan nan tun itoon takpem ki ter ni-n.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Laa che bininfob n pag ki bii nan wee a chee ke ni ŋanin ni.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: Daandaan nan ma, n be nsan bu le ki chaa Jerusalam n tan tun ter biwonjiɛm ban be niin nin.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: Poor Anduroniku nin Juniɛsi ban ye n bol yaab ki ji ye n loondiig kɔɔn tabin. Bi jɔɔ liyinl Masiya a tooi yin ban, bi nan chinn ŋa Masiya yada nin ma.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a naamin kaa ye tijikaar nin tiwann nyuwann a bɔri, amaa mi ye nbuchin binben le, nin nposɔɔn, nin nmɔn Kasi a Naaŋin yaan.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Nan bu-n Masedoniya nin Akaya yaab nan jɔɔ nmɔn ke bin chaar ki sei biwonjiɛm ban nan ye faladam ki be Jerusalam.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Poor Ampilatusi, wan wee n jiɛ Uyudaan ni-n.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: Ni nan mɔ bi le ke bin tun man, le ibamɔn ni sin kpaan nin bin chaar man ki sei bi. Ni ma bu ni ye ban kaa ye Juu yaabin lagin por nin Juu yaabin Kinaaŋ a kaarin ke, ni kpaan nin bin ji lagin por nin Juu yaab bi wann yɔɔr a kaar.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: nan bu-n uninfo wan ye Masiya a toontunn u wee ki tun man ke ni gbeeg Uwonbɔr a num le, le bininfob chee sei ke ni ŋan.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: Poor Uribanusi, wan ye ti toon tun kpiɛg Masiya ni-n, nin n jɔ kpaarimɔɔn Sitachisi.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Mambu ni che ti pɔɔn ti ba ki tun nan li ta nposɔɔn dan nin nan li pɔɔg tab a sufu ti yada-n.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
