local: Ni bɔɔn bu ni ye n tan tun diɛ wee ki kan ke bi gaa ipiin nmɔn ke, n li nɔɔn nan gɔnn ni wee ki jo jo Sipenn.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Poor Apelesi, wan a binbem wee bi mag lig ki miin u si bogiliba Masiya ni-n. Poor Aritobulusi a niin yaab biɛn na.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
local: N nyi ke n nan dan ni ban ke, n na yin ŋmag Masiya a kaarin le takpem ki dan.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Laa bii Uwonbɔr a toonl tijikaar bu. Tijiir biɛn ŋan, amaa naa ŋan nin uninfo n ji tijiir tan li che u kpiɛg n gbiɛr ki lu.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Poor Herodiyon, wan ye n bol a ninfou win na. Poor Narisisu a niin yaab ban be Uyudaan nin ni-n na.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: N nabum, n balin ni le, ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya nin Kinaaŋin a jiɛm bu, ni kpiɛ n bu n gbaalin ki miɛ Uwonbɔr sei ma.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: Naa ŋan nin a ŋman tinann bee a nyu ndaam bee a tun nan li che a nabig n lu.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Poor Tirafena nin Tirafosa, bipiib ban tun itoon nin linunmanl ki sei Uyudaan nin na. Poor n jɔ kpaarimɔɔn Perisi, upii wan mu ji nan tun itoon nin linunmanl ki sei Uyudaan nin na.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: Ni li miɛ Uwonbɔr ki tenn ma wee n fir ŋmar ki nya Judiya yaab ban kaa ye biwonjiɛmin a nɔɔn wee Uwonbɔr a ninfob ban be Jerusalam nan a num ngbee maah yin tun nan nin.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: Poor Rufusi, wan ye uninfo gann kaa Uyudaan nin, nin u na wan ye min tɔɔl a na-n na.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Mambu ŋaah ŋa tiwann nan yada man a sufu ni li be fin nin Uwonbɔr a sisiigin na. Tikaar be uninfo wan kaa galin uba waah chee sei nan nin bu.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: wee Uwonbɔr a bam bu n fir dan ni ban nin nmɔn wee min nin nim n mann pɔŋ.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Poor Asinkiritusi, nin Filigonn, nin Hemesi, nin Patirobasi, nin Hemasi, ki nin kpiɛ n nabum ban be bi ban nin na.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Amaa wan jɔɔ nbiɛnim ki jiin waah ji tijiir tan nin paam wee ki sin jin ti ke u bii u bau. Nan bu-n waah jin nan nin kaa nyan u yada-n ni; nan mu wee a tun wee naa nyan a yada ni ke ni ye tibir le.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Nposɔɔn a Wonbɔru li be nin ni biɛn. Ibamɔn.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: Poor Filogusi, nin Juliya, nin Neresi nin u ninsa, nin Olimpasi, ki nin kpiɛ biwonjiɛm ban be bi ban nin na.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Ni poor tab nin jilimau. Biwonjiɛm a laginn nin biɛn poor ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: N nabum, n balin ni le, ni li dibir bininfob ban jɔɔ nbɔgirim chaan ki nan legin ni samin wee naa chee sei nwɔɔm man wee ni tinn nin. Ni li lɔg ba.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: Bininfob nmɔn a booli kaa tun tenn ti Yudaau Masiya, amaa bi tun bi tɔɔl a kɔlisa bu le. Bi jɔɔ kulandiiŋ nin nmɔmɔm le ki lar bininfob ban a lam par bi-n.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Bininfob biɛn gbir limɔcheel lan wee ni jɔɔ-n buu, ni bu che ni mɔm le takpem ni bu; amaa n ban ni che ni num li ween le niwann nan ŋan bu, wee ki laa che ni nɔɔ be titoonbirin ni.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Nposɔɔn daan Uwonbɔr li taah Sintani ki mar ni taatum ke naa yuuri. Ti Yudaau Yisa a nyunyunbasanl li be nin ni.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: Timoti, wan ye n toon tun kpiɛgin, poor ni, le Lusiɛsi, nin Jasonn, nin Sosipata ban ye n bol yaabin mu poor ni.

eng: Im Tertius, and Im writing this letter to you in the Lord—hello!.
local: Min, Teritusi wan kpiɛ Pɔɔl wee ti sɔb kugbɔŋ nan poor ni Uyudaan a yinl bu.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: Gayɔsi, wan gaa min nin biwonjiɛm a laguŋin biɛn kuchɔŋin wee ni mɔ ti-n poor ni. Erasitusi wan ye kitiŋ poolin a lig ŋmagir win, nin ti nabig Kuratusi poor ni.

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
local: Daandaan nan che ti pag Uwonbɔr wan fir ta ni siin tibɔŋann nin bu nin maah mɔɔn Yisa Masiya wan nin bu, ki ta jiin tibɔkpiirikaar tan nan woo hali yaayoo-n nin.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: amaa daandaan nan ti kpiir ki dɔ npaan nin le, ilinlen leen a sɔbimin yaan, le Uwonbɔr wan kaa jɔɔ diɛm nan a siimin bu, wee ibol biɛn nŋa U yada ki chee U mɔb.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: ni pagim Uwonbɔr wan a binba ye ulandaan nan, hali nin n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ diɛm! Ibamɔn.

eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: Pɔɔl wan wee Masiya nan yin u ki ta u ŋa u toom Uwonbɔr a bam bu-n, nin ti na a big Sositenesi.

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: le sɔb ki poor nim biwonjiɛm a laginn ban be Korenti a tiŋin, nin ban wee Uwonbɔr ŋa bi u yaab Masiya bu-n, le u yin bi ki ŋa bi kasi nin ban biɛn be nan yaan biɛn ki yi ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a yinl ke bi yudaau nin ti mu a yudaau win.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti Baa Uwonbɔr nin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be ni bu.

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: N poor Uwonbɔr iyoo biɛn ni bu, nin waah san ni kunyunyunbaag Masiya bu.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: Naah be nin Yisa-n ni ŋa biwandam le niwann biɛn ni, nin ni pipagir nin ni biɛmin.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: Masiya a bɔr tan wee ti nan wɔɔn ni-n, ni gaa ti nbamɔma.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: Ni bu naa lann Uwonbɔr a Naaŋ a piini ke naah ŋa sili ki guu ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a jirimin.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: U li ŋmag ni nbamɔm hali nin ndiɛm, wee ni laa nan jɔɔ igalin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a jirin daali.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Uwonbɔr wan yin ni ke ni li jɔɔ nɔɔnintab nin U bija Yisa Masiya wan ye ti Yudaau win, U ye yada daan le.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: N nabum, n balin ni le ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a yinlin, ke ni biɛn chee sei taba wee nbɔgirim laa be ni sisiigin ni, wee ni li jɔɔ nmɔkpaa man pɔɔ nin lipotiɛl liba.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: N nabum, Kiloe a niin yaab biba beim ke ni jɔɔ limɔkpel le nin tab.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: Nan wee n pag nmɔn ye nan le: Ni ni uba ke Min nɔɔ Pɔɔl le, le uba mu ke Min nɔɔ Apolosi le; le uba mu ji ke Min nɔɔ Piita le; le utɔ mu ke Min nɔɔ Masiya le.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Masiya nmɔn bɔgir lee? Bi nan kpaa Pɔɔl le lidapunpunl bu ni buu? Bee bi fun ni Uwonbɔr a nyunful Pɔɔl a yinl buu?.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: N poor Uwonbɔr nin maah kaa fun ni ni uba Uwonbɔr a nyunfuli see Kerepusi nin Gayɔsi a binba.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: Ni bu, uba kaa li fir len ke bi fun u Uwonbɔr a nyunful n yinlin ni.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Nan bu-n Masiya kaa tum ke n na li fu bininfob le, amaa u tum ke n nan mɔɔn u bɔŋann nle. Tan kaa ye uninfosaal a biɛmi, ni ye man ke Masiya a kum lidapunpunl bu li ŋa yɔli.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Masiya a kum lidapunpunlin bu-n a bɔr ye ijɔrig le ki sei ban borin, amaa tim ban kan feegabin n ma ni ye Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm le.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, N li bii bilandam a lam ki che bibiɛndam a biɛm npaar yɔli.

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Ni ye man ke, ulandaan nin be laa? Bee ubiɛndaan nin be laa? Bee ban jɔɔ nnyinyin dulinya nan yaan nin be laa? Naah ye Uwonbɔr taa dulinya nan a lam le ki labir ijɔrig aa?.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Uwonbɔr tɔɔl a lamin bu che dulinya nan yaab a lam kaa fir biɛ wi. Ni nan gbee Uwonbɔr a num nin baah nan ke nan ye ijɔr leen nin kaah nan mɔɔn wee bininfob ban nan ŋa yada-n kan feeja-n bu.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: Juu yaab ban bin kan ligaril a toonl le, le Girig yaab mu ban nlam.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: amaa ti mɔɔn Masiya a kum lidapunpunl bu nle: Tibɔr nmɔn ye litangbag gbiɛrikaal le Juu yaab ban, le ki ji ye ijɔr leen ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ban.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: Amaa ban wee Uwonbɔr yin bin, Juu yaab nin Girig yaabin, bi ma nyi Masiya kaah ye Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm nin u lam.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Uwonbɔr a jɔrigi jɔɔ nlam jon uninfosaal a lama, le Uwonbɔr a daburigi mu pɔɔ jon uninfosaal a pɔɔma.

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: N nabum, maah nan dan ni ban ni, maa nan mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki tenn ni nin nmɔmɔn bee nbiɛn takpen ni.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: N nabum, ni li tiɛr naah nan ye ban, n-yonn man wee Uwonbɔr nan yin ni. Ni takpem kaa nan ye ban jɔɔ nlam tininforin nin; takpem mu kaa nan ye jilima a ninfobi; le ni takpem mu kaa nan ye nnaam a ninfobi.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Amaa maah nan be ni ban ni, maa nan ban n biɛ niba ki ji kpiɛ see Yisa Masiya nin u kum lidapunpunl nbu.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr gann ban wee dulinya nan yaab yi bi ke tijɔri nle ke bin jin ban ke bi ye dulinya nan a landamin ifa; ki ji gann ban wee dulinya yaab yi bi ndaburimin ke bin jin ban ye bipɔɔndam dulinya nan yaan nin ifa.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: N nan dan ni ban nin idaburig, le bijawaam jɔɔm le n gbanann jegil.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: U gann nim ban kaa ye nnaam a ninfob dulinya nan yaan nin nim ban kaa nan ye niba-n, amaa le ki ŋa nan wee dulinya yaab tiɛr ke ni ye niba-n yɔli.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: Naa ye bininfosaalib a biɛm le n jɔɔ mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔrin ki tenn ni, amaa ni nyan Kasi a Naaŋin le nin u pɔɔmin.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: U ŋa man le ke uba nlaa fir fa ligbingbanl U nintogili.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: Naah li ŋa man wee ni yada laa be bininfosaalib a biɛm bu, amaa wee ki li be Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm bu.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: U ma Uwonbɔr bu le nim be nin Masiya, le u tenn ti U tɔɔl a lam. U ma ŋa ti bibuchim, ki ŋa ti kasi, le ki sei ti nkpenim a manug.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: Ti pag nlam a bɔr ki tenn binunwondam ban chegir Uwonbɔr a samin nin le, amaa naa ye dulinya nan a lam man wee bi jɔɔ wɔɔn nni bee dulinya nan a bɔrtiib ban kaa na yin jɔɔ tinyɔɔr kaah ŋmag nlam man n ni.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: Ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin man ni, ke, Wan far u gbingbanl ke, u li far u gbingbanl Uyudaan kaah tun nan nin bu.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Aai, ti pag Uwonbɔr a lam man woo n-yaa bɔr le, amaa le Uwonbɔr pii nan chinn ŋa nlam man li sei ti jilima nmɔn a sili ki guun ti le mi saŋi nin foo.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Dulinya nan a a bɔrtiib, uba kaa be ki nyi nlam nmɔn ni. Bi bi nyi mi ke, baah nan bi li kpaa ti Yudaau, wan ye jilimadaan nni.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: Ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Linunbil kaa kan ni, Litul mu kaa gbiri, le uninfosaal a sufu mu kaa nyi, nan wee Uwonbɔr ŋa ni sili ki guun ban ban win ni.

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr ma che U Naaŋin kpiir ki wɔɔn ta, U Naaŋ nmɔn le feen niwann biɛn, hali nin Uwonbɔr kaah woon tibɔr tan gbaa.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Ni ŋma nyi Uninfosaal a potiɛl wee naa ye uninfosaal nmɔn a naaŋ kan be u ni nnaa? Man le ye ni, uba kaa nyi Uwonbɔr a potiɛli see Uwonbɔr a Naaŋin a binba.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: Kinaaŋ kan wee ti gaa-n, kaa ye dulinya nan a naaŋi, amaa ki ye Kinaaŋ kan nyan Uwonbɔr ban le. U li che U naaŋin ter ti wee ti biɛ u piini nmɔn.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: Taah pag imɔleen yan nmɔn kaa nyan Uninfosaal a wɔɔnim a lamin ni, amaa imɔleen nmɔn nyan Kinaaŋin le, yan ye kinaaŋ a mɔleen ki wɔɔn ibamɔn ni.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Wan kaa jɔɔ Kinaaŋ nmɔn kaa cheel tibɔr tan nya Uwonbɔr a Naaŋin ban ni, tibɔr nmɔn ye ijɔr leen le ki sei u, le waa li fir biɛ ti taai yi, nan bu-n ti ye kinaaŋ a leen le.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Uninfo wan jɔɔ Kinaaŋ nmɔn man feen niwann biɛn le ki biɛ ni taai, amaa uninfo uba kaa li fir biɛ u yaa binbem.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: A li ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Ŋma nyi Uwonbɔr a potiɛlaa wee ki li fir wɔɔn waa? Amaa tim ma jɔɔ Masiya a potiɛl.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Man bu ni ŋan bininfob li nyi ke ti ye Masiya a toontum le, Uwonbɔr ta U bɔwoonkaar ki ŋa ti nɔɔn ke ti ta pag ki bei bininfob.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Daandaan nan ni kpaa talar le ke ban wee bi ŋa bi yada-n n tun bi toon nbamɔm.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: N baga kaa be naah ji n bɔr man bee dulinya a ninfob kaah ji n bɔr man ni, maa ji n ba a bɔr gbaa.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: Maa tiɛr n galin yi, amaa naa wɔɔn ke maa tun tibiri, Uyudaan le ji n bɔr.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Ni bu ni laa ji uba tibɔr daandaan nan wee n-yonn kaa la foo, ni li guu n-yonn man wee Uyudaan na li foon. U ma na li nyan nan be libunbɔnlin nween ni, ki kpiir tibir tan be uninfo a sufu-n npaan ni, iyaayoo bininfob biɛn na li kan bi pɔg Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: Daandaan nan, n nabum, nim a nyɔɔr bu le n bigin ni niɛ ki sei min nin Apolosi, ke ni tinn ti ban n wɔɔm man ke, Ni la tun ki lagir nan sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin bu wee nin che ni laa jɔɔ kalanbaani uba bu ki jon u kpiɛgi.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Ŋma che a jɔɔ nbɔgirim a tab ban naa? Ba wee a jɔɔ wee naa ye bi seiŋ lee? Ke ni ye bi seiŋ le ke, ba ŋa wee ni far igbingban ke baa pu ni lee?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: See ni pii jɔɔ nan biɛn wee ni ban! See ni pii ye bijɔɔndam le! See ni pii ye bibɔrtiib le! Amaa naa ye timi. N sin bi ban ke na ni ŋa bibɔrtiiba, wee ti mu n ŋa bibɔrtiib ki kpiɛ ni bu.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: Min ban ke, n tiɛr ke Uwonbɔr gann tim ban ye itoon nan le ki jiin ti bɔɔn ke taa kpaa talar le liwonjiɛnlin ni. Ki nann bininfob ban wee bi li ku ti npaan ni ki ŋa ti ligaril a ninfob ban wee bi pag ki galin ti wee dulinya nan a ninfob nin Malakam ligin.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Tim ye tijɔri le ki sei Masiya, amaa nim ye bilandam le Masiya ban! Tim ye ndaburim le, amaa nim ye bipɔɔndam le! Ni jɔɔ jilima, amaa tim kaa jɔɔ jilima!.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Ki nan kpaa iniɛ a yoo nkom nin nnyunyum jɔɔ ti le, ti yee tiwanchar le, le bi boo ti, le ti choon yelin kaa jɔɔ binbeboli.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: Ti nɔɔi le ti jɔɔ tun itoon nin inunman. Bi poo imɔɔŋ ŋa ti ke, ti mu kaab ikaab ŋan le ki tenn bi, bi mugir ti le amaa le ti mu chee sei bi.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: Ni ye bi jɔɔ ti kutingbiig ke ti jiin tibɔŋann le. Ki nan kpaa daandaan nan ti nann tiwanchɔgin le dulinya nan yaan, ki ji nan dulinya nan a fatanpol a mɔɔr.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: Maa sɔb ni niɛ ke n jin ni ifa le, amaa n kpaan ni le, naah ye nbibankaam nbu.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Nin naah le jɔɔ ban wɔɔnn ni Masiya a bɔr turpiig nan biɛn na, naa jɔɔ baam takpemi, Masiya a bɔŋann nbu le n nan ŋa ni baa.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: Mambu n balin ni le, ni li nɔɔ n binbem ma.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Mambu le n tun Timoti, n bija wan wee n ban wee u si ibamɔn Uyudaan a samin ni. U li tiɛr ni maah jɔɔ nbinben man Masiya-n wee ni nɔɔ nan wee n wɔɔn nan yaan biɛn wee n be nin ni tiwonjiɛndirin.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Ni ni biba tiɛr ke maa yin dan nan gɔnn ni, ni ma bu le bi jɔɔ kalanbaani.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Ni ye Uyudaan chee ke, n li gɔnn ni daandaan nan, naa ye kalanbaanidam nan a pipagir a binba bu le n na yin ligi, amaa n ban n kan npɔɔn man wee bi jɔɔ le.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Uwonbɔr a naamin kaa ye tipipagir a binba amaa mi ye npɔɔn le.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Ni ban ni laa? Ni ban n dan ki nan dar ni tul le bee n dan nin njiɛm a Naaŋ nin jiininbaa?.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Ni ye ibamɔn le ke iwanjaab toon be ni sisiigin, nan wee ban kaa ye biwonjiɛm kaa li chee tun ni: N gbir ke uba be do ki dɔɔ nin u baa a poo.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Le ni jɔɔ kalanbaani! Naa bi ye ni li mɔr le ki sin nyan uja wan tun iwanjaab toon nmɔn ni gbanann nin naa?.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Hali nin maah kaa be ni ban nin tigbanann nan biɛn ni, amaa n be ni ban kinaaŋin. Le ki paa chinn jin wan tun iwanjaab toon nan tibɔr a li ke n be niin len.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: Ni ye ni nan lagin tab Uyudaan Yisa a yinlin bu wee n be ni ban nin Kinaaŋin ke, wee ti Yudaau Yisa a pɔɔmin be nin ti ke.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: ni ta uninfo wan tun man nmɔn ki ŋubin Sintani a nɔɔn na wee un bii u binben biim a gbanann nmɔn, wee u naaŋ n nan kan nŋmarim Uyudaan a dan daal.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Naa ŋan nin ni li far ligbingbanli, naa nyi ke boroboro a nyɔgim wama le che boroboro yom fugir ki do nnaa?.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Ni bu, ni jiɛn ni binben kpegim n na, ki ŋa bininfob pɔm ke naah pii be man ni. Bi ta Masiya wan ye ti yuglagir a peebigin mann kumanuga.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Ni bu ni che ti ji yuglagir a chiig wee ki laa jɔɔ nbinben kpeg man ye titoonbir nin npooton, amaa ki li kpenn ki ŋa bininfob pɔm ki li si nbamɔm nin ibamɔn na.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: N gbɔŋ kan wee n sɔb ki sei ni-n, n pii bei ni ke ni laa nɔɔ bininfob ban tun iwanjaab toon nni.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Naah nan sɔb kugbɔŋ seim tibɔr tan bu-n, ke ni ŋan nin uja nlaa kɔɔn upii-n.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: Naa ye ni nyan niba dulinya nan a ninfob ban tun iwanjaab toon, bee linunchɔril bee ban ji bitab dulim bee ban mann ibawol nle. Ni ye man ke, see ke ni nyan ni ba dulinya nan yaan.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: Amaa iwanjaab toon kaah yab nbu, ni ŋan nin uja biɛn li jɔɔ u poo, le upii biɛn mu li jɔɔ u chal.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: Amaa n sɔb ki bei ni ke ni laa jɔɔ nɔɔ nin tab nin ban yi biba ke biwonjiɛm wee ki sin chon tun iwanchɔgin toon lipiibanl bee lijabanl, bee kujaŋuuŋ bee libawomanil bee kutingbiig bee bidanyub bee ban ji bitab dulim. Ni laa lagin ki ka nin u yaadaan ki ji tijiiri.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: Ni ŋan nin uja nta uba ki sei u poo, le upii mu nta uba ki sei u chal.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Ni ye n toonl le ke n ji ban kaa ye biwonjiɛm tibɔraa? Naa ye ti ji ban ye biwonjiɛmin nlee?.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Upii a binba kaa ye u gbanann nmɔn ni, amaa u chal mu le ye u gbanann nmɔn, uja mu a binba kaa ye u gbanann ni, amaa u poo mu le ye ti.

eng: God will judge outsiders. Expel the evil one from among you! .
local: Uwonbɔr le yin ji ban kaa ye biwonjiɛmin tibɔr. Ni ŋɔɔr bipootondamin ni ban na.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Ni laa yiin tab a dɔɔmi, man ke ni nan chee sei tab ke ni la yin ta isaŋ iba le ki miɛ Uwonbɔr. Wee ni bɔɔn bu ke ni jir ki lagin tab wee Sintani nlaa fir bigin ni n yonn man wee ni nan walin ni ŋmag ni baa guri.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: N bei ni ni niɛ le, amaa naa ye nsiimi.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: Min bi ban ke uninfo biɛn li be ke maah be man nan le, amaa Uwonbɔr sei uninfo biɛn nin u piini le, uba a piin be iba ki jiɛn utɔyaan.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: N pag ki tenn ban kaa la kɔɔn bipiib, nin ban kaa la kun bijab nin ipukɔɔ nle, ke bi bi jiɛn lipiikɔɔnl bee lijakunl wee ki bi be ke maah be man nan ke, ni bi ŋan.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Amaa ni ye baa li fir choo biba ke, ni ŋan nin bin kɔɔn bipiib bee bin kun bijab, nin bi li jɔɔ nmɔsanyunsɔɔm wee bi sufu gaan nŋmi.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Bipiidam nin biniinpiib le n siig nsiim nan ki tenn ni : Naa ŋan nin upii nnya ki jiɛn u chali.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: Amaa ni ye u nyan u chal ban ke, un la ji kun uja uba, amaa un jirim u chal nmɔn a niin. Uja mu nlaa yii u poo.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: Nim ban gurin mu le n bei nan, : Ni ye ni ni uba kɔɔn upii wan kaa ye uwonjiɛn ni, wee ki fir be nin u chal ke, u la yii wi.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Ni ye upii mu jɔɔ chal wee u chal nmɔn kaa ye uwonjiɛn ke, wee ki li fir be nin u chal ke u la yii wi.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Uja wan kaa ye uwonjiɛn nmɔn labir Uwonbɔr a ninfou u poo nbuu, le upii wan kaa ye uwonjiɛn nmu labir Uwonbɔr a ninfou u chal nbuu. Naa bi ye man ke ni buwaab kaa bi li fir be kasi, amaa ibamɔn buu, Uwonbɔr gaa bi le ki ŋa bi kasi.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Amaa ni ye wan kaa ye uwonjiɛn ban un yii u chal bee u poo ke ni che u li chaau. Naa ye limugil le ke u li be nin wan kaa ye uwonjiɛn nmɔn ni.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Fin upii nan yin ŋa nii ki biɛ ke ŋaa li fir fee a chal ki jaa? Fin uja mu a yin ŋa nii ki biɛ ke ŋaa li fir fee a poo ki jaa?.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Nin ni ma biɛn ni, che uninfo biɛn li ŋmag Uyudaan kaah ban u li be man nin, ke waah pii nan be man wee Uwonbɔr yin win. Nsiim man wee n ta sei biwonjiɛm a laginn biɛn le yen.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Uja u la ba nan jii u punl wee Uwonbɔr nin yin waa? Ni che u li ŋmag man na wee ki laa ji jiri ye wan kaa jii u punli. Uja ula kaa nan jii u punl wee Uwonbɔr yin waa? Ni che u li ŋmag man na wee ki laa ji jii.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: A jii ke ni ye yɔli le, ŋaa ti ji jii ke ni ye yɔli le. Nan kpaa talar ye ni ŋmag Uwonbɔr a siimin le.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Ni che uninfo biɛn li be ke waah nan be man wee Uwonbɔr yin win.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: A nan ye uyom le Uwonbɔr yin ŋaa? Laa che nin mugiriŋi, amaa a nyi ŋaah li ŋa man ki nya tiyomirin ke, a ŋaam.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Wan nan ye uyom wee Uyudaan yin u ke, waa ji ye uyom Uyudaan ban ni, man mu le wan mu nan ye uba wee Uyudaan yin u ke, u nin ye Masiya a yomu le.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Uwonbɔr daa ni le ki yɔn; Mambu ni laa ji ŋa bininfob a yomi yi.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: N nabum, ni ma bu ni che uninfo biɛn li be nin Uwonbɔr a, ke waah nan be man wee Uwonbɔr yin win.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Daandaan nan, nim ban kaa la nyi bipiib nin ban kaa la nyi bijab nan man, Uyudaan kaa seim nsan ke n bei ni niba, amaa Uyudaan a nyunyunbasanl bu le n ji ni bɔr ke uninfobamɔn nin.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Daandaan nan a bɔr mugkaar nbu, n tiɛr ke ni ŋan nin ni li be ke naah be man nan.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: A kɔɔn upii waa? Laa ji gbaar a yii wi. Ŋaa la kɔɔn upii? Laa ji ban upii a kɔɔn ni.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Amaa ni ye a kɔɔn upii ke naa ye tibiri, usapɔɔn wan kaa la nyi uja mu kun uja ke, naa ye tibiri, amaa ban jɔɔ bipiib bee chiɛb li kan tibɔmugkaar bi binbemin, le n ban nnyan bi tibɔmugkaar nmɔn yaan.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: Maah ban n wɔɔn ni nan sɔ, n nabum, n-yonn kaa ji yabi, naah nyan daandaan nan ki jɔɔ chaa, ban jɔɔ bipiib li be ke ban kaa jɔɔ bipiibin;.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: ban mɔr, li be ke baa mɔr len, le ban jɔɔ npopeen li be ke baa jɔɔ npopeen len, le ban daag tiwann yɔɔr li be ke tiwann yɔɔr nmɔn kaa ye bi yaan len;.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: ban jɔɔ dulinya nan a wan yɔɔr ki jɔɔ tun bi toon yin, bi li be ke bi baga kaa be niin len. Nan bu-n dulinya kaah be man daandaan nan ni, naa li yuuri ni li fɔɔr ki jo.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: N ban ni li be ni wiib le ki laa che niba mugir ni, ujakpag a baga be u Yudaau a toon yin le, le u gbaar waah li ŋa man wee nin gbee Uyudaan a num.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Amaa upiidaan ma a baga be dulinya a wan yɔɔrin le, waah li ŋa man wee nin gbee u poo a num.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: ni bu u potiɛl bɔgir le, upii wan kaa kun uja bee usapɔɔn wan kaa nyi uja mu a baga be Uyudaan a toon ni le, nin waah li ŋa man ki ta u gbanann nin u naaŋ ki sei Uyudaan nbamɔm. Amaa wan jɔɔ chal ma lig dulinya nan a wan yɔɔr le, nin waah li ŋa man wee nin gbee u chal a num.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: N pag ni niɛ nan ni yaa ŋam bu le, naa ye n ban n mug ni le amaa naah li ŋa man wee ni laa jɔɔ nbɔgirim Uyudaan ban ni.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Ni ye uja jɔɔ kijakpeŋ wee u nan gbee upii, wee uja nmɔn jɔɔ lipotiɛl u bu kaa li fir choo uba ke, che un tun waah ban man, ni ŋan nin bin kɔɔn tab, waa tun tibiri.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Amaa uja wan mu ŋmag uba, wee naa mugir u ke un kɔɔn upii wan wee u lol u tɔɔl tɔɔl a sufu ni ke, waa yin kɔɔn upii wan wee bi ta sei u nke, u tun nan ŋan na.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Ni bu wan kɔɔn u jakpeŋ ke ni ŋan, amaa wan mu kaa kɔɔn u jakpeŋ ke, u tun nan ŋan ki jon wan kɔɔn u jakpeŋa.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Upii jɔɔ u chal wee u la be u miɛfolin ke, u chal nmɔn le ye u. Amaa ni ye u chal nmɔn kpo ke, u li fir kun uja wan wee u ban, amaa waah yin kun wan nmɔn li ye wan nɔɔ Uyudaan.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: Amaa min ban ma ke, ni ye u be kaa ji kun uja ke, ni li mɔ u ki jon nin waah li kun ujau, Uwonbɔr a Naaŋ le che n pag man.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: N nabum, maa li fir pag ki sei ni ke maah bi li pag ki sei kinaaŋ a ninfob man nni, amaa n yin pag ki sei ni ke maah bi li pag ki sei dulinya nan yaab man nle, ke bininfob ban nann nbuwaab finfim ban la biɛn nwonjiɛn binben nle.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: N tenn ni nbiim le, naa ye bisaabi, naa la nan kpaan nin bisaabi. Hali daandaan nan naa la kpaan nin bisaabi.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: Ni le la be le ke dulinya yaabin, ni la jɔɔ lipiiponl le nin limɔkpel ni sisiigin, ni toon kaa be le ke dulinya yaab a toon yaa?.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Ni ye uba ke u nɔɔ Pɔɔl le, wee uba mu ke u nɔɔ Apolosi ke, naa tun le ke dulinya yaab kaah tun man nin nnaa?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Nin ni ma biɛn nan a bɔɔn, ŋma ye Apolosi? Ke ŋma ye Pɔɔl aa? Ti le ye bitoontum le, ban wee Uyudaan nan ta itoon ki ŋa uninfo uba biɛn a nɔɔn wee ni ŋa yada-n.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Min le nan bun iwanbool, le Apolosi mu wur nnyum amaa Uwonbɔr le che ni pan ki yabig.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Wan bun nin wan wur nnyum kaa ye niba, Uwonbɔr le ye niba, nan bu-n U ma che ni pan ki yabirig chaa.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Wan bun nin wan wur nnyum ye nba le, bi biɛn yin gaa tiyɔr bi toon kaah ye man le.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Tim le ye Uwonbɔr a toon tun tab; nim le ye Uwonbɔr a kpaab ki ji ye Uwonbɔr a diig.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah sam kunyunyunbaag nbu, n bil lifiɛl le ke litanma kpegilin, le uba maa tɔɔg li bu, amaa uninfo biɛn li nyi waah maa ki tɔɔg li bu man.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Lifiɛl liba kaa ji be wee uba li fir fiɛ wee kaa ye lifiɛl lan pii chinn be nni, li ma ye Yisa Masiya.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Ni ye uba yin maa tɔɔn lifiɛl nmɔn bu wee ki jɔɔ salima, bee tikur ninann bee itangbag pɔɔr bee tidaar bee timɔɔr bee ikikag ke.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: u toonl nmɔn li wɔɔn liba la ye lan, nan bu-n Yisa a jirin daal nŋmi li na yin kpiir li ki mag uninfo biɛn a toonl ki lig li ŋam kaah ye man.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Ni ye nŋmi kaa gaa waah maa nan nin ke u li kan u yɔr.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Wan yaal gaa nŋmi ke, u li lann u yɔr, amaa u baantɔɔl ma li kan nŋmarim, le ki nin be ke uninfo wan ŋmar ki nyan nŋmi ni.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Naa nyi ke nim le ye Uwonbɔr a wonjiɛndiig nnaa, le Uwonbɔr a Naaŋ be ni nii?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Ni ye, wan bii Uwonbɔr a wonjiɛndiig n ke, Uwonbɔr mu li bii u yaadaan; Uwonbɔr a wonjiɛndiigin ye kasi a diig le, le ni mu le ye kudiig nmɔn.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Ni laa lar niba. Ni ye uba tiɛr ke u ye ulandaan le dulinya nan yaan ke, un ŋaam u ba kujɔrug wee ki fir ŋa ulandaan.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Dulinya nan yaab a lam ye ijɔrig le Uwonbɔr ban. Ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Uwonbɔr ta bilandam a lam le ki pen choo bi.

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: ki ji sɔb niba yaan ke, Ti yudaau nyi bilandam a potiɛri kaah ye yɔli.

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Ni bu uba la far u gbingbanl uninfosaal bu! Niwann biɛn ye ni yaal le.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: Naah ye Pɔɔl, bee Apolosi, bee Piita, bee dulinya nan, bee limiɛfol, bee nkum, bee daandaan nan nin iyoo yan chaan ni, nim le ye ni biɛn.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: Masiya le ye ni, le Uwonbɔr mu ye Masiya.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: Ki jiin tibawojiir paam: Ti nyi ke ti biɛn jɔɔ nbiɛm. Nbiɛm mu che uninfo far ligbingbanl le, amaa njiɛm ma pɔɔgir uninfo le.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Wan tug ke u nyi niba ke, u yaadaan kaa la nyi waah bi ye u li nyi man ni.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Amaa uninfo wan ban Uwonbɔrin, u ma le Uwonbɔr nyi.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Tɔ, ki ta jiin ibawol a jiir a jim paam: Ti nyi ke ibawol ye yɔli le dulinya nan yaan, le nweenba kaa ji be ki kpiɛ Uwonbɔr uba nmɔn bu.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Hali baah yi nan iween nin i be papaab bee dulinya nan yaan ke ni, iween takpem nin biyudam takpem sin be.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: tim ban ma ke, Uwonbɔr uba kpeŋileŋ le be, u ma ye ti Baa Uwonbɔr wan nan naan niwann biɛn, le Uyudaan uba kpeŋileŋ mu le be u ma ye Yisa Masiya, u ma bu le tiwann biɛn nan naan, le ti mu be u ma bu le.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Amaa naa ye uninfo biɛn le nyi man ni. Bininfob biba la jɔɔ libawol manil a potiɛl le bi sufu ni, ni bu ni ye bi jin man a jiir ke, ti nann tibawojiir le, bi yada mu kaah kaa pɔɔ-n, bi ban ni ye tibir le.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Amaa tijiir kaa li fir che ti mal Uwonbɔr ban ni; taa jin ti ke naa yin kpiɛ ti niba, ti mu ji jin ti ke naa yin war ti niba.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Amaa ni li nyi man, wee ki laa che ni wiib a binbem nkpann litangbag gbiɛrikaal ndaburim a samin ni.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Ni ye uninfo wan a yada waar kan fin wan jɔɔ nbiɛm nan be ji tijiir nbawodiin ke, naah li pɔɔg u sufu wee un ji tibawojiiraa?.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: A biɛm nmɔn bu le che a na big wan a yada kaa pɔɔ-n, wee Masiya nan kpo u bu-n ngab yɔli.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Ni ye a tun man a bir ki sei a nabum ban a yada kaa pɔɔ nan ki che idaburig choo bi ke, Masiya le a ŋa tibir.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Ni bu, ni ye nan wee n ji-n li che n na big n tun tibir ke, maa ji li ŋman tinann ni, wee ki che un lu.

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Ni ye ni ni uba jɔɔ limɔkpel nin u kpiɛg wee ki ta ti jon ban kaa ye biwonjiɛmin ban ke bin tan ji ti ke, naa bi ye un ta jo ban ye biwonjiɛmin ban lee?.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Naa nyi ke biwonjiɛm le na yin ji dulinya nan yaab tibɔraa? Ke ni ye nim le na yin ji dulinya nan yaab tibɔr ke, ba ŋa naa kpaan nin ni mann nbɔfinfim nan naa?.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Naa nyi ke ti na li ji Malakam tibɔraa? Ke ŋma nin chaa baan dulinya nan yaab a binbem maa!.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Ni ma bu ni ye ni jɔɔ limɔkpel nin tibɔr nmɔn a bol ke, ni ta tibɔr nmɔn ki ŋa ban kaa jɔɔ tininkper biwonjiɛmin wee bin ji ti.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: N jin ni ifa nin maah len man nmɔn na. Limɔkpel dan nim a sisiigin ke, nim kaa jɔɔ ulandaan wan li fir mann tibɔr nmɔn naa?.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: Le ni tug tab ki chaa bibɔjib ban, hali nin ban kaa ye biwonjiɛmin, le bi ji ni bɔr!.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Ibamɔn buu, naah ji tab a bɔr man ni pii wɔɔn ke ni luu. Ba ŋa wee ŋaa ban a chee igalin naa? Ba ŋa wee ŋaa chee bin jiŋ dulim maa?.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Nan bu-n, ni tɔɔl ji dulim ki galin le ki ŋaan ni na a buwaab mu man.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Naa nyi ke bipootondam kaa na li kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin naa? Ni laa lar niba: Bininfob ban tun iwanchɔgin toon lipiibanl bee lijabanl bee bibawomanib, bee iwanchɔgin toon bee bijab ban chon tun iwanjaab toon bee bijab ban taar bijatab ki kpann bipiibin.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: bee binaayigib, bee kujaŋuuŋ, bee bidanyub bee bitingbiib, bee ban ji tab dulim bi niɛ nan biɛn kaa na yin kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin ni.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Nim bibam nan ye man le. Amaa le Uwonbɔr ŋunn ni bir, ki ŋa ni kasi, ki ke ni bɔr ŋan Uyudaan Yisa Masiya a yinlin nin Uwonbɔr a Kasi a Naaŋin bu.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Ni ni biba len ke, Niwann biɛn kaa bi kɔ. Amaa naa ye niwann biɛn le li ter ti. Ni ke, Niwann biɛn kaa bi kɔ. Amaa maa yin che niba nŋaam tiyomiri.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Tijiir ye lipool yaal le, le lipool ye tijiir yaan. Ni ye ibamɔn le, amaa Uwonbɔr li bii niwann nilee nmɔn biɛn. Tigbanann kaa ye nan wee ti li jɔɔ tun iwanjaab toon ni, amaa ti ye Uyudaan yaan le, le Uyudaan mu ye tigbanann.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm bu le fiin Uyudaan nkumin, U na li fiin ti mu nkumin.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Naa nyi ke ni gbanann tug nin Masiya a gbanann lee? Ke n ta Masiya a gbanann nmɔn ki lagin nin wan tun iwanjaab toon naa? Naa li firi.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Bee naa nyi ke wan ta uba ki tugin nin wan tun iwanjaab toon ke, bi nin ye gbanann tiba lee? Ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Bi bilee nmɔn le ŋa gbanann tiba-n.

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Amaa wan ta uba ki tugin nin Uyudaan ke, udaan nin Uyudaan nin jɔɔ Kinaaŋ kiba le.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Ni guun nya iwanjaab toon nin na man. Tibir tan biɛn wee uninfosaal tun kaa be u gbanann ni, amaa wan tun iwanjaab toon ke, u tun ki bii u tɔɔl a gbanann le.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Naa nyi ke ni gbanann ye Kasi a Naaŋ a diig lee? Naa ye u ma le Uwonbɔr ta u ki sei ni nnaa? Naa ye niba.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: Uwonbɔr le daa ni kudaapɔɔrug, ni ma bu ni che ni gbanann le jɔɔ jilima ki tenn Uwonbɔr a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: N nabum maa ban ni laa nyi ibamɔn nni, ke ti yaajam biɛn nan lagin be kitilangbɔŋ a lɔŋin le, ki lagin don limɔgil.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: Bi nan fun bi nnyum le ke kitilangbɔŋ kaah nan piin Moziz bu nin baah nan tan don limɔgilin man ni.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: Bi biɛn nan lagin jin kinaaŋ a jiir nna.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: ki ji nan lagin nyun kinaaŋ a nnyumin; bi nan nyun nnyum man nan nyan kinaaŋ a tangbapiig kan nɔɔn bi nle, kutangbapiig nmɔn nan ye Masiya le.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Ni biɛn yɔli, Uwonbɔr a sufu kaa nan sɔɔn bi ni takpem bu! Le bi nan kpo ki yal dɔ dɔ kukunkɔɔg bu.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Tibɔr tan be nan ye limagil le ki sei ti, ke ti laa tiɛr nan bir ke baah nan be man nni.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Ni laa nɔɔ itingban ke bi ni bibam kaah nan ŋaan man nni, ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Bininfobin nan kal le ki jin ki nyun, le ki fii ki gel itingban.

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Ti laa tun iwanjaab toon ke bi ni bibam kaah nan tun man nni, wee weemba daal bininfob turpilee nin bita nan kpo nni.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Ti laa mag Uyudaan ni, ke bi ni bibam kaah nan bigin U man wee iwaalab nan junn bi ku nni.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Ni laa ŋulin ni, ke bi ni bibam kaah nan ŋulin man wee nkum a malaka nan dan ki nan ku bi nni.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn nan tun ki ye limagil le ki sei bi, ni nan ye ikpaan le ki sɔb dɔ ki sei tim ban nan joor bɔɔn dulinya nan a diɛmin.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Ni bu wan nyi ke u si nbamɔm ke, un choo u bau wee ki laa lu.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Nbigim le kaa be ki nyaŋ ni see nbigim man be nin uninfosaal biɛn n yaa binba. Uwonbɔr ye u bamɔndaan le, waa li che nbigim n foon ni ban ki jo ni pɔɔm a tarigi. Amaa ni ye nbigim foon ni ban ke, u ji li fir naan nsan ki sei ni wee ni nɔɔ nya ki mann sir mi bu.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Man bu n jɔtiib, ni nyan ni ba libawol manilin na.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: N pag tenn bilandam le, ni mu n tug ki jiin nan wee n pag nan paam ma.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Naa ye kiyiinyɔɔg kan wee ti tenn Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog wee ki nyu ki poolin ni ye Masiya a cham lee? Boroboro kan wee ti war war ki ŋman ni, kaa ye Masiya a gbanann lee?.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Ni ye boroboro konl liba le, amaa tim yaba, le ki lagin ki ŋa gbanann tiba, nan bu-n taah lagin ki ŋman boroboro konl liba n bu.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Lig Iziral yaab: Naa ye bi ma lagin ki ŋma kumanug a nann tan wee bi mann libinbinl bu nna?.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: Ke n wɔɔn ke kumanug ye niba lee, bee libawol ye niba lee?.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Aai kumanug kan wee ban kaa ye biwonjiɛm mann ni, bi mann tenn nnaanbiim le amaa naa ye Uwonbɔr i, n mu kaa ban ni li mann ki tenn nnaanbiimi.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Naa li fir nyu Uyudaan a yiinyɔɔgin, ki jin nyu nnaanbiim a yiinyɔɔgin ni. Naa li fir ji Uyudaan a jiir wee ki ji nnaanbiim a jiiri.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Ti ban ti fiin ti Yudaau a piiponl lee? Ti pɔɔ ki jon u waa?.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Ni ni biba len ke, Niwann biɛn kaa kɔ amaa naa ye niwann biɛn le jɔɔ tinyɔɔri. Niwann biɛn kaa kɔ amaa naa ye niwann biɛn le jɔɔ literili.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Laa tun ki jiin a baantɔɔl a bam bu, amaa li tun ki jiin a tab a ŋam bu.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Li ŋma tinann tan wee bi kɔɔr kinyaŋin na, wee ki laa baan ibinbaan yan li mugir a potiɛli.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Uyudaan le ye kutingbɔŋ nan nin niwann nan biɛn be ki yaan.

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Wan kaa ye uwonjiɛn baaniŋ tijiir wee a yin ji ke, li ji nan biɛn wee u ta siin nna ki laa baan ibinbaan yan li mugir a potiɛli.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Amaa ni ye uba beiŋ ke, N mann libawol le ke, ni ye man ke a laa ji, nan bu-n uninfo wan beiŋ man nbu nin u potiɛl a mugil bu.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: uninfo u miɛnin a potiɛl bu le n pag nan, naa ye fin ni. Ni bi yin ŋa nii wee n wiib a binbem nmugir uba a potiɛlaa?.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Ni ye n sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog n jiirin bu ke, ni yin ŋa niin wee uba n pag ki bii n yinl tijiir tan bu wee n sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog n buu?.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: Ni ye ni jin, bee ni nyun bee ni ŋa nan biɛn ke, ni che nin don Uwonbɔr a yinl.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Ni laa che uba nlu waah ye Juu a ninfou, bee Girig a ninfou, bee uwonjiɛn ni.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Hali nin maah ban uninfo biɛn a num n gbeem nin niwann biɛn nin ni. Maa gbaar ban n yaa binba a ŋam bu amaa n gbaar ban bininfob biɛn a ŋam bu le, naah li ŋa man wee bin kan nŋmarim.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: Maa ye n baa? Maa ye ntoon naa? Maa kan ti Yudaau Yisa nmɔn naa? Naa ye nim le ye n toon nmɔn a nyɔɔr ti Yudaau nnaa?.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Ni ye maa ye Yisa a toom biba ban ke, amaa nim ban man nbiɛnim kaa be, n ye ntoon le ni ban, nim le ye ndaagim man wɔɔn ke n sin ye ntoon le Uyudaan ban.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: Maah li ta nan ki kɔn nba bu ban li ji n bɔrin n sɔ.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: Nsan kaa be nin ti li ji ki nyuu?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Nsan kaa be nin ti ta uwonjiɛnpii ki che u li nɔɔ ti choon ke ti toontuntab nin ti Yudaau a watiib nin Piita kaah ŋaan man n naa?.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Bee ni ye min nin Banabasi a binba le ye ti tun ki jin tibaa?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Utɔtogir ula ba tog bitɔb wee ki ji jinn ubaa? Bee ŋma li ŋu isiig yan lo iloon wee kaa li ji isiig nmɔn a loon yaa? Bee ŋma kpaa inaa wee kaa li nyu nnaabiim maa?.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: Uninfosaal a lam le n ta pag man naa? Naa ye man le nsiimin len naa?.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: Ni sɔb Moziz a siim n-yaan ke, Laa ta niba ki bon kinaajag a mɔb, n-yonn man wee u be kuur idi. Uwonbɔr a baga be inaa a binba ni lee?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Ibamɔn tim le u pag ki sei, naa ye man naa? Nn, ni sɔb sei tim le, nan bu-n wan ko nin wan kuur biɛn jɔɔ tama uba le ke bi naah li por idi nmɔn.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Taah li gaa dulinya nan a wan yɔɔr tan ni ban nin yab ki jon Kinaaŋ a bim man wee ti ta bu-n ni sufu-n ni waa?.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Ni ye biba jɔɔ nsan nin bin gaa literil ni ban ke, tim kaa jɔɔ nsan nmɔn ki jon baa? Amaa, taa ta nsan nmɔn tun mi toon yi. Amaa le ki ji fala a booli biɛn ta kaa ban niba nlegin Masiya-n a bɔŋann ni a mɔɔnimin bu.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Naa nyi ke ban tun Uwonbɔr a wonjiɛn niin n-yaan, bi jiir nya niin lee, le ban mu mann kumanug Uwonbɔr a binbinl bu-n mu por nan wee bi mann libinbinl bu-n lee?.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Mambu le Uyudaan sei bimɔb ke ban mɔɔn u bɔŋann nin a jiir nya tibɔŋann nmɔn yaan le.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Amaa maa ta nsan nmɔn ki tun itoon ni. Kaa ji sɔb ni niɛ ke ni seim niba. N kpo ke ni sɔ nin uba n che n gbingbanfaal n ŋa yɔli.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: Hali n mɔɔn tibɔŋannin ke, maa li fir fa n gbingbanli, amaa ni kpaa talar le ke n mɔɔn ti. Ni li bir ki seim ni ye maa mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn ke.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: Ni ye n bi tun nin n tɔɔl a wiib ke, n bi kpaan nin n ga tiyɔra, amaa naa ye min tɔɔl a wiib bu ke, man ke Uwonbɔr kaah siinim ke n tun man le yen.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Ke n yɔr le ye ni laa? Ni ye nan le: Tibɔŋannin nmɔn a mɔɔnimin ni ye n mɔɔn ti le ki tenn bininfob le bin gbir ki laa yɔ niba, n jɔɔ nsan nin n gaa niba bi ban amaa n mu kaa gaan ni.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: Hali maah ye nba wee uba a yomu kaa yem nan, n ta nba le ki kpann uyom ki sei bininfob biɛn, ke n che bininfob takpem ndan Yisa ban.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Ni ye n jon Juu yaab ban ke, n be le ke Juu a ninfou win, naah li ŋa man wee Juu yaab n kan nŋmarim. N ji jon ban wee nsiimin ŋmag bin ban ke, n be le ke nsiim nmɔn kaah ŋmag ban ni, , naah li ŋa man wee nsiim nmɔn kaah ŋmag ban ni n kan nŋmarim.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: Ni ye n be ban kaa nɔɔ nsiimin ban ke, n ta nba le ŋa wan kaa nɔɔ nsiim, ke bin kan nŋmarim. .

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Maah nan be nin ban a yada waarin, n nan be le ke wan a yada waarin, naah li ŋa man wee bin kan nŋmarim. N che n binbem be le ke bininfob biɛn a binbem kaah be man ni, naah li ŋa man wee n fir fee bininfob bibam ki jaa.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: Tibɔŋann nbu le n tun ni niɛ nan biɛn, ke n nan ji ni nyɔɔr.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Naa nyi ke bitaaguum gu ke, utaaguun uba kpeŋileŋ le yin guun ki ji ki gaa tiyɔraa? Ni bu, ni li gu nin inunman na wee ki gaa tiyɔr.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Bitaaguum biɛn ŋmag biba le ntinnim biɛn ni. Bi ŋaan man ki ban bin gaa tiyɔr tan kaa yuur le, amaa tim ŋaan man ki ban ti ga tiyɔr tan yuur kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm le.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Mambu min kaa be ke uninfo wan gu yɔli yɔli n ni, n mu kaa be ke unɔɔkufaar wan far ki lan nni.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: Aai, n mug n gbanann le ki ŋa ti tiyomir, ke maah mɔɔn tibɔŋannin ki tenn n tab man ni, ni bɔɔn bu min tɔɔl n laa nan lann n yɔri.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Ni li be ke maah be man nin nna, ke n mu kaah be ke Yisa kaah be man ni.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: N pagir ni nin naah tiɛr nbu ki la biɛn nin nɔɔ maah nan wɔɔn ni nan biɛn ni.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Daandaan nan n ban ni biɛ ke uja biɛn a Yudaau ye Masiya le, upii mu a Yudaau ye u chal le, le Masiya mu a Yudaau ye Uwonbɔr.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Uja wan chig kiyuchigirig wee ki miɛ Uwonbɔr bee ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔr ke, u yaa daan kaa sei u yul nmɔn jilima.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Le upii wan mu kaa yil sadiga wee ki miɛ Uwonbɔr bee ki len ilinlen leen ke, waa sei u yul nmɔn jilima, le ni nann ke u koo u yul nmɔn le-n.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Ni ye upii kaa ban un yil sadiga ke, ni ŋan nin un chiɛ u yul bee ki koo u yukper nmɔn, man mu ji ye ifa ke, see ke u yil sadiga.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Uja kaa kpaan un chig kiyuchigirig kuwonjiɛndiigin ni, nan bu-n waah jɔɔ Uwonbɔr a nunaŋ wee ki ji ye npagim ki sei Uwonbɔr nbu; amaa upii ma ye npagim le ki sei uja.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Nan bu-n, naa ye upii a gbanann le bi ta naan uja, amaa uja a gbanann le bi ta naan upii;.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Naa ye uja le bi nan naan ki sei upii, amaa upii le bi nan naan ki sei uja.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu nin malakam bu, ni kpaan nin upii li jɔɔ siɛra a wan u yul bu, wee nin wɔɔn ke u chal jɔɔ u pɔɔm.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: Amaa Uyudaan ma ban ke, uja kaa li fir be u ba bu ki jiɛn upii, upii mu kaa li fir be u ba bu ki jiɛn uja.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Upii kaah nan nyan uja a gbanann nin ni, man le upii mu ma uja. Amaa niwann biɛn nya Uwonbɔr ban le.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Ni baan ni bau njee ni ŋan nin upii li miɛ Uwonbɔr wee kaa yil sadigaa?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Ti binbemin ni pii wɔɔn ke uja jɔɔ tiyukper fufɔgir ke ni ye ifa le ki sei u.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: upii mu jɔɔ tiyukper fufɔgir ke, naa ye u jilima le yen naa? Tiyukper fufɔgir nmɔn le bi ta sei u ke u li jɔɔ piig u yul.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Amaa ni ye uba ban un fa limɔkpel ki jiin tibɔr nan bu ke, tim nin tiwonjiɛn tab kaa jɔɔ nan wee ti li tun ki kpiɛ ni niɛ bu.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Maah yin wɔɔn ni nan nan, maa yin pag ni, naah ti lagin tab ki pag nan nin kaa mann ni, amaa ni bii ni le.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Chinchinn n gbir ke ni ye ni lagin tab biwonjiɛm a laguŋ ke, nbɔgirim be ni sisiigin le, le n sin kan ke ni ye ibamɔn le.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Naa jɔɔ nbiɛnimi, ni ye ni li jɔɔ nbɔgirim le ni sisiigin wee nin wɔɔn ban ye bibamɔndamin kaah ye ban ni sisiigin.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Ni ye ni lagin tab ke naa ye Uyudaan a jiirin le ni jii.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: naah be ji n-yonn man ni, ni ji mɔnn tab le, uba kaa guu u kpiɛgi. Uba mɔɔr nkom le, le uba mu nyun ki gbee.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Naa jɔɔ ni niin yan yaan wee ni li be ki ji ki nyuu? Bee ni ziɛn biwonjiɛmin le ki jin bilanjiimin ifaa? N yin pag ni ke baa? N li fir pag ni nan be nan buu! Aai maa yin pag ni!.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Maah nan gaa nan Uyudaan ban ni, ni ma le n mu ta sei ni: Kunyiɛg kan wee bi nan kɔɔr Yisa-n, le u nan yuur boroboro.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: waah sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog ki diɛ n yonn man ni, le u war war le ki ke, N gbanann sɔ, tan ye ni yaan, ni li ŋaan man wee ki li tiɛr nbu.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Man nmɔn jigin baah nan jin diɛ a bɔɔn le u ji yuur kiyiinyɔɔg, ki len ke, Kiyiinyɔɔg kan ye Uwonbɔr a poopɔnn tan ye n yaa chamin nsɔ, ni li tun man n-yonn man biɛn wee nin nan nyun ke, ni li tiɛr nbu.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Iyoo yan biɛn wee ni ŋman boroboro nan wee ki nyun kiyiinyɔɔg nan yaan ke, ni mɔɔn Uyudaan a kumin le nin waah na li jirin.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Mambu wan ŋman boroboro nan bee ki nyun u yiinyɔɔg n-yaan wee kaa sin nɔɔ nsan man li sei Uwonbɔr a yinlin jilima ke, u yaa daan tun tibir ki sei u Yudaau a gbanann nin u chamin na.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Uninfo biɛn ye un mann dibir uba le nbamɔm, wee ki nin fir ŋman boroboro-n wee ki nyu kiyiinyɔɔg n-yaan.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Nan bu-n, wan ŋman wee ki nyun wee kaa sin feen ki biɛ Uyudaan a gbanann nmɔn a taai ke, u jin ki nyun litudaril le ki jaa uba bu.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Mambu le ni ni bibam gbarig ki buŋ le ni ni bibam kpeg.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Amaa ni ye ti bi ji ti ba a bɔr ke taa ji bi na li kɔɔ tibɔr a jimin ni.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Amaa ni ye Uyudaan jin ti tibɔr ke, ti ga litudaril le na li ŋa man wee ti laa won nan kpo nin dulinya yaabi.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Mambu n nabum, ni ye ni nan lagin ni ji ke ni guur taba.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Ni ye nkom jɔɔ wan ke un chinn ji u niin na, wee ni lagin tab ke nin laa ta tibɔr a jim foon ni. Tibɔr tan gurin, n nan dan ke n li wɔɔn ni.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Daandaan nan n ban n pag ki jiin ipiin yan li tenn biwonjiɛmin paam le: Ni ŋaam ke maah wɔɔn Galantiya yaab a wonjiɛm a laginn ke bi li ŋaan man ni.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Likpakɔɔl daal biɛn ke, ni biɛn li nyan ŋaah tun ki kan man a piin ki biliga, wee n nan foon ke ipiin a chaarim n laa ji be.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Wee ni ye n foon ke, n li sɔb kugbɔŋ, kan ta li wɔɔn bininfob ban wee ni gann ni, wee bin ta ni piini yan wee ni ŋaan ki chiɛn Jerusalam.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: Ni ye n nan lig ki miin ni kpaa talar nin n mu jo ke, bi li chiɛn nim.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: Ni ye n jon Masedoniya ke, ni bɔɔn bu n li dan ni ban n yin nɔɔ Masedoniya paam le.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: Nba ke n na li be ni ban wama, bee nin kufaag a yonn, wee ni terim nin n sanchon nan yaan biɛn wee n chaa.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: Maa ban n dan nan kan ni daandaan nan wee ni li ye paab a gɔnni yi, n jɔɔ tama nin n nan ji isaŋ ni ban ni ye Uyudaan chee ke.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: N li be Efesusi a tiŋin hali ki tan kpaa Pentikosi a daal.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: nan bu-n itoon takpem a naanganl loor le ki seim nin n tun, amaa le bininfob takpem mu kaa chee seim nin n tun ni.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Timoti nan dan ni ban ke, ni che u li be ni ban na wee ki laa che u lam yugiri, nan bu-n, u tun ti Yudaau a toon yin le ke maah tun man ni.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Uba laa yii ki laa gaa wi. Ni che u nan nya ni ban ki jirin n ban nin nposɔɔn. N lig u ma nin ti nabumin nsan bu.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: Daandaan nan ki jiin ti na a big Apolosi paam: N balin u le ke u ma nin ti nabumin nnɔɔ dan ni ban, le waa ban un jo daandaan nan ni. Amaa ni ye u nan kan nsan ke u li jo.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Ni li guu niba man, ki che ni yada li pɔɔu, ki li ye bininfob ban jɔɔ lipoobil nin npɔɔn.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Ni li tun niwann biɛn nin njiɛma.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Ni nyi Sitefanasi nin u niin yaab le nan ye chinchinn a ninfob ban kpenn bi binbem ki ŋa biwonjiɛm Akaya a tiŋin, bi ta biba le ki sei itoon nin ki tun ki tenn Uwonbɔr a ninfob. N nabum, n balin ni le.
