eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: ni chee sei bininfob nmɔn a bosuu, nin bi toontunn nunman damin.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: N nan jɔɔ npopeen Sitefanasi nin Fotunatusi, nin Akakusi kaah nan foon nin, nan bu-n bi nan ŋa nan wee ni bi li ŋa seimin na.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Bi pɔɔg min nin nim a sufuu. Man a ninfob kpaan nin ni gaa ba.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Biwonjiɛm a laginn ban be Asiya a yerin nin poor ni. Akula nin Pirisila nin biwonjiɛm ban lagin ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr bi niin yin ni poor ni pam Uyudaan a yinlin.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: N nabum ban biɛn be do nan poor ni. Ni li poor tab nin jilimau.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Min Pɔɔl le ta n tɔɔl a nɔɔ ki sɔb ipoor nan.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Wan kaa ban ti Yudaau ke, limɔpool be u bu. Dan ti Yudaau.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Ti yudaau Yisa a nyunyunbasanl li be ni bu.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: N ban ni biɛn Masiya a yinl bu. Ibamɔn.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Min Pɔɔl wan wee Uwonbɔr a bam bu wee n ŋa Masiya a toom nan, min nin ti na a big Timoti, le sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tenn Uwonbɔr a ninfob ban be Korenti, nin u ninfob gannkaab ban biɛn be Akaya a tingbɔŋin biɛn.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti Baa Uwonbɔr nin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be ni bu.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: N pagir Uwonbɔr wan ye Yisa Masiya a baa-n; ti Baa wan san ti kunyunyunbaag ki jɔɔ nposɔɔn biɛn.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Ti Baa wan sɔŋir ti sufu a bɔmugkaar biɛn ni, wee ti mu li fir sɔŋir ban be limugilin a sufu, lisufusɔŋil lan wee ti nan gaa Uwonbɔr ban ni.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Ni li nɔɔ njiɛm ki sin li ban Kinaaŋ a piini, ni nunman li ye ilinlen leen a piin.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Ti fala a jim Masiya a yinl bu kaah yab man nin, man le ti sufu sɔŋil mu na yin yabig ki tan lagir u ma Masiya bu.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Wan pag ileen pɔɔn ke, waa pag tenn uninfosaali, amaa u pag tenn Uwonbɔr le. Uba kaa biɛr i taai amaa u pag liwool a bɔr nmɔn kinaaŋ a pɔɔmin le.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Ti jin fala ke, ni ye ni yaa sufu sɔŋil le nin feegab; ni ye ti kan lisufusɔŋil ke, ni ye nim a sufu sɔŋil le lan li sei ni limɔril ki che ni fir ji fala ke taah ji man ni.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Amaa wan pag ilinlen leen ke u pag tenn bininfob le npɔɔn, ki pɔɔgir bi gbanann nin ibalin.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Mambu ti jɔɔ tama ni bu ke ni pɔɔ, nan bu-n ti nyi ke naah le ji ti fala man ni, man mu le ni yin kan ti sufu sɔŋil.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Wan pag ileen pɔɔn ke, u pag pɔɔgir uba le, amaa wan pag ilinlen leen ke u pag ter biwonjiɛm le.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: N nabum, maa ban ni laa nyi fala wan wee ti jin Asiya a yerin paamin ni. Ti nan kan limugil takpenl lan yab ki jon ti pɔɔm a tariga, ki che ti jaa tama ti tɔɔl a miɛfol bu.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: N bi ban ni biɛn li pag ileen pɔɔn, amaa n jann ban ni pag ilinlen leen le. Wan pag ilinlen leen jon wan pag ileen pɔɔn na, see u kar wɔɔn le nin ter biwonjiɛm.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Nan bu-n, ti nan tiɛr ke nkum kaa ji fɔg nin ti, amaa ni niɛ nan tun ki wɔɔn ti ke ti laa far ti baantɔɔl a gbingbanli, amaa wee ki li far ligbingbanl Uwonbɔr wan fiin bitakpiib nkumin ni bu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: N nabum, n ban n tiɛr ni maah nan mɔɔn tibɔŋann tan ki sei ni wee ni gaa ti ki ŋmag nle.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: N nabum, ni ye n dan ni ban ki nan pag ileen pɔɔn ke, ni ye baa a ŋan ki sei naa, wee naa ye n ta tibɔkpiirikaar dan ni ban bee nbiɛm bee ilinlen leen bee nwɔɔnim a mɔbonl laa?.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: U ma nyan ti nkum a daŋ takpeŋ n-yaan, U li nyan ti. U ma bu le ti jɔɔ tama ke U yin gbaa nyan ti le.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Tibɔŋann nan bu ni ŋmara, ni ye ni ŋmag tibɔr tan wee n nan mɔɔn sei ni nbamɔm ke. Naa ye man ke ni yada li gab yɔli.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: Hali ni ye niwann nan kaa fo wee ki jɔɔ kufuug ke liwol bee kubiɛg a dann, wee kaa mɔɔ gɔr ke, a yin ŋa niin ki bɔgir liwol a mɔr bee kubiɛg a mɔr wee naa ye ki mɔɔ ki gɔr lee?.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: a li ke naah ter ti nin liwonmiɛlin man ni, ni ma yin che bininfob takpem n sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog tim bu, waah san ti kunyunyunbaag nbu le u jiin bininfob takpem a miɛm.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Nan wee n gaa-n ni ma le n mu ta sei ni le ni ye nan kpaa talar chinchinn: Ke Masiya kpo ti yaa bir bu le ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ man ni.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Wan pee bitɔb a wol wee kaa pee yin bitɔtob ke, ŋma yin ŋa bitɔb a silii?.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: le bi nan pii u, le u fii nkumin iween itaa daal ke naah sɔb man ni.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Daandaan nan taah jɔɔ ligbingbanfaal lan sɔ: Ti potiɛl jin ti siɛra ti binbem dulinya nan a ninfobin, nin ni baantɔɔl a sisiigin, ti kasi a binbem nin ibamɔn nin nya Uwonbɔr ban le. Naa ye dulinya nan a lam le che ti tun man ni, amaa ni ye Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu le.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Ni ye man le, see ke a pag ileen pɔɔn wee i ween, naa ye man ke bi yin ŋa niin ki biɛ? A le yin pag ki tenn nfaam le.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: u nan degirin Piita bu, le ki ji degir u tooi piig nin bilee-n mu bu.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Taa sɔb nan wee naa li fir karin ni bee naa li fir biɛ. Amaa n jɔɔ tama ke.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Naa jɔɔ nbiɛnimi ileen a booli be dulinya nan yaan, amaa ileen nmɔn biɛn jɔɔ bu.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le u nan degir u nabum ban nan yab ki jon bininfob kobiŋmu-n bu n-yonn nmɔn, bi ni takpem la be bi miɛfolin, amaa biba ma kpou.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: naah pii biɛ ti wama tiwann man ni, ni na li biɛ ti takpem wee ki fir fa ligbingbanl ti bu, ke ti mu kaah na li jɔɔ ligbingbanfaal ni bu man ti Yudaau Yisa a jirin daalin.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Ni ye maa nyi uba kaah pag tibɔr tan ki tenn nim ke, n ye libobanl le udaan ban le u mu ye libobanl n mu ban.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: U nan degir Jemisi bu, le ki nan degir u tooi nbiɛn bu.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Maah nan jɔɔ tidindann ni niɛ nan n bu, ni ma che n lol ke n chinn gɔnn ni wee ni ji tinyɔɔr nfim milee.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Ni ye man le. Ni sufu kaah ban Kasi a Naaŋ a piini nbu-n, ni che ni sufu mu li be ipiin yan li ter biwonjiɛm ma.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Ni biɛn nan a joorim, le u degir min tɔɔl bu, min wan wee u nan yim ke uyom a dann ni.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: N nan lol le ke, n nan chaa Masedoniya ke, n burin ki nan gɔnn ni, wee ki ji nan nyan Masedoniya ke, n jirin ni ban, wee ni terim wee n jo Judiya.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Min le nan joor bɔɔn itoon nmɔn yaan kaa bi kpaan nin bi li yim ntoon ni, nan bu-n n nan mugir Uwonbɔr a ninfob le.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Daandaan nan n nabum ki jiin kasi a Naaŋ a piini paam maa ban ni li be kaa nyi.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Mambu wan pag ileen pɔɔn ke un miɛ Uwonbɔr wee un che un fir ki wɔɔn nan wee u pag nmɔn a taai.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: Maah lol man nmɔn n lol jagilangbiɛ le, a li ke dulinya yaab kaah ŋaan man ni, wee n ke, Nn, nn, ki ji ke, Aai, aai kufoorug kiba a poolin naa?.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu le n ye maah ye wan nan, le u nyunyunbasanl lan be n ni nmɔn kaa ye yɔli. Nan bu-n n tun ki jon itoon nin biɛn na, amaa naa ye min le tun ni Uwonbɔr nyunyunbasanl lan nan be n ban ni-n le tun.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Ni nyi ke naah kaa nan ye biwonjiɛm n-yonn man ni, ni nan che bi ta ibawol yan kaa fin pagin ki lar ni kɔɔn kulɔŋ.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Ni ye n miɛ Uwonbɔr ileen pɔɔn nin ke, n naaŋin le n miɛ, amaa n sufu ni naa jɔɔ tinyɔɔri.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Amaa ni ye ibamɔn le ke Uwonbɔr kaah ye yada daan nin bu, tibɔr tan wee ti jɔɔ tenn ni nan kaa ye Nn nin Aai le.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Ke naah ye min bee bi miɛ ni, tibɔŋann nan le ti mɔɔn, tibɔŋann nmɔn le ni mu ŋa ti yada.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: Mambu le n bei ni ke, uninfo wan jɔɔ kasi a Naaŋ a pɔɔm ki pag kaa li fir poo bimɔb ki ŋa Yisa, uba mu kaa li fir ke, Yisa ye Uyudaan ni, see kinaaŋin be u ni.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Ke n ni yin ŋa nii? N li miɛ Uwonbɔr n naaŋin, le ki ji miɛ u n sufu ni, n li gaah ilan n naaŋin ki ji gaah n sufu ni.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr a bija Yisa Masiya wan wee min nin Silasi nin Timoti nan mɔɔn u bɔr ki sei nin, waa ye Nn nin Aai, amaa u ma bu ke iyoo biɛn u Nn le ye Nn le.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Amaa ni ye tibɔrin mɔɔn ke Masiya nan fii nkumin na ke, niin wee nim bibam ke nkum a fiim kaa bee?.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Kasi a Naaŋ a piini be le balbal, amaa Kasi a Naaŋ ye kiba le.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Ni ye a pagir Uwonbɔr a naaŋin a binba ke, ni yin ŋa niin wee uninfo wan be ni sisiigin kaa nyi ŋa len nan ni n len ke Ibamɔn naa?.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Ni ma bu ni baga kaa be nin Uwonbɔr kaah siin imɔsiin yan biɛn nni, i ye Nn le, Masiya bu. Le u ma Masiya bu Ibamɔn yan wee ti pagin, i ye kupag le ki donn Uwonbɔr.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Ni ye nkum a fiim kaa be ke, Mambu ke Masiya gbaa tɔɔl kaa nan fii nkumin ni.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Itoon ni be le balbal amaa le Uyudaan ye u ba.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Nba ke a wonpagil nmɔn ŋan amaa naa ye nan pɔɔg uba a sufu.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Daandaan nan Uwonbɔr le che tim nin nim si nbamɔm Masiya ni. U ma gann ti le ti jɔɔ nbɔgirim.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Masiya kaa nan bi fii nkumin ke, taah mɔɔn tibɔŋann tan ni ye yɔli le, nin ni yada wan wee ni ŋaan.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Itoon a tum mu be le balbal, amaa Uwonbɔr uba le che ni niɛ nan tun bininfobin.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: N poor Uwonbɔr nin maah fir pag ileen pɔɔn a booli ki jon ni biɛn.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: ki daag ti waah ye ti a daagim, ki ta u naaŋin ŋa ti sufu ni, le ni wɔɔn ke u li sei ti nan biɛn wee U siin limɔsiinlin.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: Mambu ke ti ŋa tibɔkar a siɛradam ki sei Uwonbɔr a, nan bu-n ti jin siɛra ke Uwonbɔr nan fiin Masiya nkumin na. Amaa nkum a fiim kaa be ke, man ke Uwonbɔr mu kaa nan fiin Masiya nkumin ni.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Uninfo biɛn nan gaa Kasi a Naaŋin kaah sei u ipiin yan ni, bininfob biɛn a teril bu le.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Amaa ni ye n be biwonjiɛmin ke, n nɔɔ li pag imɔbon ŋan iŋmu yan li ter n tab ki jon nin n pag ileen pɔɔn a booli imɔbon turpiiga.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Uwonbɔr le ye n siɛradaan ke nin nan ŋan nin n ŋmiɛ ni maah kaa ji nan jirin Korenti a tiŋin ni.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Ni ye nkum a fiim kaa be ke, man ke Masiya tɔɔl kaa nan fiin nkumin ni.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Kasi a Naaŋ che biba pag nlam a bɔr, Kasi a Naaŋ nmɔn a piini le che biba pag nin nbiɛm Kinaaŋ kiba nmɔn a pɔɔmin.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: N nabum, ni laa tiɛr ke nbuwaabi. Amaa tibir ma paam ke ni li be ke nbiliɛmin, amaa ni potiɛlin ni li be ke binunwondama.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: Taa li fir mugir ni ki wɔɔn ni naah li ŋa yada man ni, amaa ti tun nin ni le ni yaa mɔm bu, nan bu-n naah jɔɔ yada nbu le ni si kenken.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Masiya kaa nan bi fii nkumin ke, ni yada bi ye yɔli le, le tibir la be ni bu.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: Kasi a Naaŋ nmɔn le che biba jɔɔ yada, le ki sei biba mu ipiin yan wee bi jɔɔ teb bibaarib, Kinaaŋ kiba nmɔn a pɔɔm bu.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: Nsiimin yaan ni sɔb ke, N yin ta ban pag ileen pɔɔn a booli a booli nle nin bininfocham a mɔgbai le ki pag sei bininfob nan, amaa nin ni ma biɛn na, baa yin pel n bɔr nmɔn ni, Uyudaan le len man.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: Ni bi wɔɔn ke ban kpo nin Masiya-n bora.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Kinaaŋ kiba nmɔn le sei uba ligaril a toon a pɔɔm, ki sei uba ilinlen leen a pagim a piini, ki sei uba npɔɔn le u biɛr nnaam a bɔgirim, ki sei uba mu ileen pɔɔn a booli a pagim, le ki sei uba mu ileen pɔɔn nmɔn a kar wɔɔn a pɔɔm.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Ileen pɔɔn ye siɛra le ki sei ban kaa ŋa yada-n amaa naa ye yadadami; ilinlen leen ma pag tenn yadadam le naa ye ban kaa ŋa yada nni.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Ni ye taah be ti miɛfolin nin bu le ti jɔɔ tama nin Masiya ke, man ke ti jɔɔ kunyunyunbaag ki jon bininfob biɛn na.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn ye Kinaaŋ kiba nmɔn a toonl le, le u ta sei uninfo biɛn waah ban man.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Ni ye biwonjiɛm biɛn lagin tab, wee bi biɛn piin ki pag ileen pɔɔn, wee biba kaa biɛ ni taai bee ban kaa ŋa yada-n yaa ni biba kɔɔn ke, baah li len ke ni waar lee?.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Amaa ni ye ibamɔn le, Masiya sin nan fii nkumin na, u ma ye chinchinn a ninfou wan fii nkumin.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Tigbanann ye nba le, hali nin taah le jɔɔ ibɔg ibɔg takpem nmɔn, hali nin ibɔg nmɔn biɛn kaah le yab nmɔn na ti lagin ye gbanann tiba le, Yisa mu ye man le.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Amaa ni ye biwonjiɛm lagin tab ki pag ilinlen leen wee wan kaa ŋa yada bee wan kaa biɛ ni taai kɔɔn ki gbir ke, u li ji uba tibɔr ni biɛn bu ke u ye tibirdaan le.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Uninfo kaah nan ta nkum ki dan dulinya nan yaan ni, man mu le uninfo ji ta nkum a fiim ki dan.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Ti biɛn nan fun Kasi a Naaŋ nmɔn le nin kinaaŋ kiba nin gbanann tiba, ŋaah ye Juu a ninfou bee Girig a ninfou wi, ŋaah ye uyom bee ŋaah ye a ba, Kinaaŋ kiba nmɔn le bi nan ta sei ti nyun.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Iyaayoo le u sufu ni a bɔwoonkaar biɛn li nyan npaan ni, le un gbaan tum ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr ki len ke, Uwonbɔr sin be nin ni.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Adam bu le bininfob biɛn kpeg, Masiya mu bu bininfob biɛn li kan limiɛfol.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Tigbanann nmɔn kaa lagin ye gbanann bɔgil liba, amaa ti jɔɔ ibɔg takpem le.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: N nabum, ti ji yin len ke baa? Ni lagin tab ni jiɛn Uwonbɔr ke, uba jɔɔ ilan, bee nwɔɔnim, le utɔ mu jɔɔ tibɔkpiirikaar, le uba mu pag ileen pɔɔn a booli, le uba mu jɔɔ karwɔɔn. Ni niɛ nan biɛn ye nin tun le ki sei biwonjiɛm npɔɔn.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Uninfo biɛn nin u kum a fii yoo, Masiya le chinn fii, le ban ye u ninfob mu na li fii u jirin daal.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Ni ye litaal len ke, Maah kaa ye nnɔɔ-n, maa kpiɛ tigbanann nin ke, man kaa li fir che un nya tigbanann nin ni.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Ni ye uba pag ileen pɔɔn ke, ni ninfob bilee bee bita li fir pag amaa ni ye bi li pag le ki tenn tab le uba mu n karwɔɔn.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Litul mu len ke, Maah kaa ye nnumbimin ni bu maa kpiɛ tigbanann nin ke, man kaa li fir che un nya tigbanann nin ni.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le dulinya a diɛm nfoon, u nan nyaŋ nnaam kaah ye niwann nan biɛn nin mi pɔɔm ke ni bɔɔn bu u yin ta nnaamin ki ŋa u Baa Uwonbɔr a nɔɔn le.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Ni ye karwɔɔn kaa be ke, wan pag ileen pɔɔn nmɔn nŋmi na, wee ki pag sei u baantɔɔl nin Uwonbɔr.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Tigbanann biɛn bi lagin ki ye nnumbim ke, ni bi yin ŋa niin wee tigbanann li gbiraa? Ke tigbanann bi lagin ki ye litul ke, ni bi yin ŋa niin wee tin gbir nbaamaa?.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Masiya le yin ŋmag niwann biɛn ki tan kpaa iyoo yan wee Uwonbɔr na li nyaŋ u dataam biɛn.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Bilinlem bilee bee bita li fir pag wee ban gurin mu n dibir nbamɔm ki biɛ tibɔr nmɔn kaa len man.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr lagin igbanann bɔg nmɔn biɛn nba le waah ban ti man.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: Kidataag kan wee u na li bii joor ye nkum le.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Amaa ni ye tibɔkpiirikaar dan nim ban kan n yaa ni uba ban wee u yin pag ke, wan bi chinn be pagin nŋmi-n na.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Ni ye igbanann bɔg nmɔn biɛn bi ye ligbanann bɔgil liba ke, tigbanann nmɔn biɛn bi yin be laa?.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: U ta niwann biɛn ki ŋa u nɔɔn na. Amaa naa wɔɔn ke Uwonbɔr tɔɔl kpiɛ niin ni, U ma ta niwann biɛn ki ŋa Masiya a nɔɔn.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Ni biɛn li fir pag ilinlen leen ki tenn tab wee nin che ni biɛn n kan ntinnim ki ji pɔɔg ni gbanann.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: A le ke naah ye man ni, tigbanann jɔɔ ibɔg ibɔg le, amaa ti ye nba le.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Ni ye U tun ni niɛ nan ke, bija-n tɔɔl yin be u ma wan ta niwann biɛn ŋa u nɔɔn n ban le, le Uwonbɔr nin ye niwann biɛn ki ŋmag niwann biɛn.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Bilinlem le ye bi naam a pɔɔm ki fir ŋmag mi.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Daandaan nan ni ye, nkum a fiim kaa be ke, baah fun ban bitakpiib a nyunfulin yin ŋa nii? Ni ye bitakpiib kaa fii nkumin ke, ba ŋa wee bininfob fu nnyum ki tenn baa?.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Nnumbim kaa li fir bei nnɔɔ ke, Maa baŋi! Le liyul mu kaa li fir bei itaa ke, Maa ban ni.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Uwonbɔr kaa ye kuŋmaruŋ a Wonbɔru wi. Nin tiwann tan ye yɔli, amaa U ye wan tun nin ni sam bu le. Ke naah be man biwonjiɛm biɛn a sisiigin ni.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Ke babu wee ti le be kininbɔŋin iyoo biɛn naa?.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Amaa igbanann bɔg yan be kaa jɔɔ npɔɔn nin kaa bi be ke, tigbanann kaa bi li fir tun ni.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: bipiib nŋmi-n bi mɔɔŋi soon n-yonn man wee ni be ni jiɛn Uwonbɔr a. Nsan kaa be nin bin pag tibɔri, naa kpaan nin bin ŋa bisanliib ke Uwonbɔr a siim kaah wɔɔn man nin.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: N nabum, n be nkum a daŋin le lidaal biɛn, n jɔɔ ligbingbanfaal nin taah jɔɔ nbinben man ti Yudaau Masiya-n nin bu, ni ma bu le n len man.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: le igbanann bɔg yan wee ti tiɛr ke yaa jɔɔ jilima takpemin, ti ma le ti ŋmag ti nbamɔm. Le igbanann bɔg yan kaa ŋan nin i li be npaan ni nin, ti ma le ti ŋmag ti nbamɔm.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Ni ye bi ban bin biɛ niba ke, bin baan bi chiɛb bi niin yin; ni ye ifa le nin upii n pag tibɔr kuwonjiɛndiigin.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: Ke ni ye n kɔn Efesusi a tiŋ a ninfob ban nann kulɔŋ a wankorin bininfob a potiɛri bu ke, ba a nyɔɔr wee n kan naa? Ni ye nkum a fiim kaa be ke, Ni che ti ji ki nyu, wee won ke ti won kpo.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Igbanann bɔg yan ŋan ni, kaa ban nterimi. Amaa Uwonbɔr le ta igbanann bɔg nmɔn, ki tugin tab le ki ta jilima takpem sei ligbanann bɔgil lan kaa jɔɔ jilima nmɔn.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Uwonbɔr a bɔrin piin nim ban lee? Bee ti foon ni yaa binba ban lee?.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Ni laa che uba nlar ni. Kujɔtiibirug bii nbinben ŋan le.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: Ni bu naa kpaan tigbanann li jɔɔ nbɔgirimi. Amaa wee igbanann bɔg nmɔn a baga li be tabin.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Ni ye uba tiɛr ke u ye ulinlenn bee u jɔɔ Kinaaŋ a piini ke, ni ŋan nin u yaadaan n biɛ ke nan wee n sɔb ki sei ni nan ni ye Uyudaan a siim le.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Ni che ni lam njirin, wee ni li be ke naah bi ye ni li be man ni, wee ki laa ji tun tibiri; nim bibam kaa nyi Uwonbɔr i. N len man le ke n jin ni ifa.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Ni ye ligbanann bɔgil liba ji fala ke, igbanann bɔg yan gurin biɛn mu ji fala nmɔn le, ni ye ligbanann bɔgil liba kan jilima ke, ni mɔ igbanann bɔg yan gurin le.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Ni ye u yii ni niɛ ke, ni mu a baga laa be u ni.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Amaa uba li baan ke, Ban kpo na yin fii nkumin niin? Bi gbanann na yin be niin.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Daandaan nan ni ye Masiya a gbanann le, ni biɛn le ye tigbanann nmɔn a bɔgi.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Ni ma bu n nabum, ni che ni sufu li be ilinlen leen a pagimin, ki laa kɔɔg ileen pɔɔn a booli a pagimi.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Kujɔrugaa! Ŋaah bun nan ni kaa li pa, see ke ni kpo.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Biwonjiɛmin, Uwonbɔr nan gann itoon le chinchinn, le ki paan bilinlem, ban kpiɛ mitaa le u gann ban wɔɔnn, le ki nin paan ban tun ligaril a toon, le ki ji paan ban jɔɔ kutebug a piini, nin ban li fir ter bitab, le ki gann bitoontum sanliib, nin ban pag ileen pɔɔn a booli.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Amaa ki che niwann biɛn li tun ki nɔɔ tab nin ni sam bu-u.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Ni ye a bun niba ke, ŋaa ŋu ligbaamil lan yin be nni, amaa ni le yin ye biwanboolib le, nba ke idi bee niba.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Ti biɛn le ye itoon naa? Ti biɛn le ye bilinlem maa? Ti biɛn le ye ban wɔɔnn naa? Ti biɛn le tun ligaril a toon yaa?.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr le tenn tigbanann waah ban man, le ki tenn biwanboolib biɛn mi tɔɔl a gbanann.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Ti biɛn le jɔɔ kutebug a piin yaa? Ti biɛn le pag ileen pɔɔn naa? Ti biɛn le kar wɔɔn naa.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Tigbanann biɛn kaa piɛ, bininfob a gbanann be tiba le, le tiwankor a gbanann be tiba, le nnɔɔm a gbanann be tiba, le ijan mu yaal be tiba.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Amaa ni li ban ipiin yan jon i biɛn na. Ke n ji li wɔɔn ni nsan man ŋan jon niwann biɛn.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Paab a gbanann be le, ki jiɛn tum a gbanann, amaa paab a gbanann a ŋam be nbinba le, ki jiɛn tum a gbanann a ŋam.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Nween a ŋam be nbinba le, le uŋmal a ŋam be nbinba, le iŋmabil mu a ŋam mu be i binba, iŋmabil a ŋam kaa piɛ nin tabi.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Nkum a fiim mu na yin be man le, tigbanann tan wee bi ta pii-n bur le, amaa ti nan fii ke, taa ji yin buri;.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Baa pii ti nin tibuchinn ni, amaa le ti fii nin tibuchinn, bi pii ti ndaburim a piim le amaa le ti fii nin npɔɔn;.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Bi pii ti dulinya a gbanann le, amaa le ti fii nin Kinaaŋ a gbanann, dulinya nan a gbanann be ke, kinaaŋ mu a gbanann be.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Man le ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Chinchinn a ninfou Adam nan ye wan fo le, le Adam wan joorin mu ye wan tenn kinaaŋ a miɛfol.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: Kinaaŋ kaa nan chinn ki dan ni, dulinya nan a gbanann le nan chinn ki dan, ni bɔɔn bu le Kinaaŋ a gbanann mu ŋɔɔrin ni.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Chinchinn a ninfo win nan nyan titann nin le, le uninfo wan kpiɛ milee n mu nyan paab.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Ni ye n pag bininfosaalib a leen nin Malakam a leen, amaa wee kaa jɔɔ njiɛm ke, n nann kileŋ le bee libulonl ki mɔr yɔli.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Dulinya nan a ninfob be le ke uja wan nan nyan titann ni nin le, le ban ye paab yaabin mu be ke uja wan nyan paabin.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: Ni ye n jɔɔ ilinlen leen a piini, ki fin biɛr tibɔwoonkaar biɛn, ki jɔɔ nbiɛm biɛn, hali ki jɔɔ yada kan wee n li fir pag wee ijɔɔ nfii, amaa wee kaa sin jɔɔ njiɛm ke n ye yɔli le.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: A li ke taah jɔɔ uja wan nan nyan titann nin nan a nunaŋin, man mu le ti ji yin jɔɔ uja wan nyan paab nan mu a nunaŋ.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Ni ye n ta maah jɔɔ nan biɛn ki sei faladam, hali ki nan ta n gbanann sei bin nyɔɔ nŋmi wee kaa sin jɔɔ njiɛm ke, maa jɔɔ tinyɔɔri.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: N nabum, n bei ni le ke, tigbanann nin ncham kaa li fir kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin ni, le nan bur mu kaa li fir kɔɔ nan kaa bur a naamin ni.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Njiɛm ye limɔril le, tininforin le ye njiɛm , maa ye lipiiponli, maa ye ligbingbanl faali, maa ye kalanbaani.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: Ni pelim man, n yin wɔɔn ni tibɔwoonkaar le. Ti biɛn nan kaa yin kpo, amaa ti biɛn na li kpenn.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Njiɛm kaa ye wɔɔnimba, kaa ye kujaŋuuŋi, kaa ji ye kiŋuufiiŋ yɔli yɔli, kaa sɔb tibɔbir bilig sufu ni.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: Lidaal nmɔn, binum a gabinim nin bi ligirim kunaatuŋ joor kaagin li mɔ, kunaatuŋ nmɔn nan mɔ ke, bitakpiib na li fii nkumin nin tigbanann ŋan, le ti mu n kpenn.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Njiɛm kaa ye li jɔɔ npopeen a kpiɛg a toonbir bu amaa ibamɔn nin bu le ni mɔ ni.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Ni kpaa talar le ke tigbanann tan burin nkpann tigbanann tan kaa li buri, le tigbanann tan kpegin, mu nkpenn tigbanann tan kaa kpegi.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Mi guu iyoo biɛn le ki ye gaa ji iyoo biɛn, ki ye tama iyoo biɛn ki jɔɔ limɔril iyoo biɛn.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Ti piin ki pagir ti ba le jigin naa? Ni kpaa talar le ke uba n sɔb ti siɛra a gbɔŋ ki sei ni bee ti gaa siɛra a gbɔŋ ni ban ke biba kaah ŋaan man n naa?.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Ni ye nan bur kpann nan kaa burin wee nan kpeg kpann nan kaa kpegin ke, ileen yan pag ki sɔb bilin yin ŋa ibamɔn le, Nkum kaa kan nnyaŋimi.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Nim baantɔɔl le ye ti siɛra a gbɔŋ, ki sɔb dɔ ti sufu ni le bininfob biɛn karin ki nyi.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Njiɛm kaa wiiri. Amaa ilinlen leen na li diɛ; le ileen pɔɔn a pagim na li jo, le nbiɛm a tarig na li kpaa.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Nkum a nyaŋim be laa? Nkum, a pɔɔm man wee a jɔɔ junn nin be laa.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Nim le wɔɔn ke ni nann kugbɔŋ kan nyann Masiya ban le, ki ye ti toon a siɛra, naa ye ngbansɔbmɔɔn le bi ta sɔbi amaa Uwonbɔr wan fo-n a Naaŋ, naa ye litangbagil bu le bi sɔb paan ni, amaa bininfob a sufu ni.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Ti nyi tibɔr nmɔn wama le, ti pag ilinlen leen wama le.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Nkum a jum ye tibir le, le tibir mu a pɔɔm nya nsiimin le.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Amaa nnyinyin man gbeen nin nan dan ke, nnyinyin man kaa gbeen nin biɛn li fɔɔr.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Tidindann nan a bosu ye ti yaan le Masiya bu Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr le ye kunɔɔpoog; U ma sei ti n nyaŋim ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya bu.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Maah nan ye kibigin, n nan pag le ke kibigin, ki tiɛr ke kibigin, ki biɛr ke kibigin, amaa maah nin nan gbee uninfo nan n ta linaakpaanlin le ki jaa.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: Taa wɔɔn tiba ke ti li fir tun niba nin ti tɔɔl a pɔɔmi, amaa taah fir tun nan ni nyan Uwonbɔr ban le.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Mambu n nabum, ni mann sir nbamɔma. Ni laa che niba n boon ni, ki li tun u yudaan a toon yin iyoo biɛn na, nan bu-n ni nyi ke ni toon yan wee ni tun ki tenn u yudaan ni kaa yin gab yɔli.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Daandaan nan ti ga amaa nin nann taah lig niɛnu le, taa ga nbamɔmi; amaa n-yonn chaan le ti na li kan nbamɔm nin nnumbim. Daandaan nan n nyi wama le amaa n-yonn chaan le n na li biɛ ni biɛn ke Uwonbɔr kaah nyim biɛn man ni.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: U che ti ye bitoontunŋam ban fir mɔɔn tipoopɔnn ni le, naa ye nan sɔb dɔ, amaa ti nyan Kinaaŋin le; nan sɔb dɔ-n ma ku le amaa le Kinaaŋ ma tenn limiɛfol.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Daandaan nan tiwann titaa nan le be, ti ma ye Yada, Tama nin Njiɛm , amaa njiɛm mu le jon ni biɛn.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Ni ye itoon nan le che nkum foon ke, wee bi nan sɔb litangbagil bu-n, iyaayoo le Uwonbɔr nmɔn a weem takpem jiirin ki nan won ki nyagin Moziz a num paam, nwonween nmɔn kaah nan nyagin man ni n bu, Iziral yaab kaa nan fir ligi, ni ye itoon nmɔn le nan che nkum foon ki biɛ che nwonween takpen foon ke.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: Kasi a Naaŋ a toon-yin kaa li jɔɔ jilima nin nwonween ki jo man naa?.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Ni ye itoon yan ku bininfobin che jilima foon ke, ibuchin toon yan che jilima chaan nin ma li jo man!.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Nan nan ye jilima-n kaa ji jɔɔ jilima daandaan nan ni, nin ti ta ki mag nwonween takpen man kaa jɔɔ diɛm nan ni.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Ni ye nan wiir nan che jilima foon ke, nan kaa wiir ma a jilima li jo man.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Mambu taah jɔɔ tama kan nin bu le che ti jɔɔ lipoobil.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Taa ye Moziz wan nan ta likinkenl ki piin u num paam ke Iziral yaabin laa kan nwonween man yin jo nbu.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Amaa bi le laa ye tukangbirdam le, hali nin dinan, ni ye bi karin Moziz a gbann nin ke, likinkenl nmɔn la deg bi num paam le. Laa la degiri. Nan bu-n Masiya a binba bu le li degir.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Hali nin dinan, bi karin Moziz a a gbann nmɔn ke, likinkenl nmɔn le la deg bi sufu le.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Amaa ni ye uba kpenn ki nɔɔ Uyudaan ke, likinkenl nmɔn degir u num paam ma.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Uwonbɔr ye kinaaŋ le, Uwonbɔr a Naaŋ kaah be nan yaan ni, ye nbaa mu be niin le.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Amaa tim ban wee likinkenl kaa deg ti num paamin, le ti kan ti Yudaau kaah jɔɔ tiŋann man takpem, le ni kpenn ti le ti nann u, le ti yaa ŋam nmɔn kpiɛg, le tiŋam nmɔn nyan Uwonbɔr wan ye kinaaŋin ban le.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: Mambu le n nin bon n sufu ni ke, maa ji yin gɔnn ni, wee ni kan nan wi.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Ni ye n bii ni sufu ke, ŋma ji yin penn n sufu kaa ye nim ban wee n bii ni sufu nnaa?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Ni bu le n nan sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ke, maa ban n dan ki nan kan npoobiin ban bi li che ni li mɔm n ban ni. N jɔɔ tidindann ni biɛn nan bu, ke ni biɛn na li ji n mɔm.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Maah nan sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tenn ni, n nan sɔb nin npoobiin le takpem nin tinyinyinn, naa ye ke n nan ban n bii ni sufu le, amaa n nan ban ni biɛ maah ban ni takpem man le.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Ni ye uba ta npoobiin ki foon ke, waa bii n yaa binba a sufu, amaa u bii ni biɛn a sufuu gbaa, maa ban n ŋa li kinimaŋ tan lagiri.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Ni takpem kaah dar u tul man ni, ni kpaan wa.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Amaa daandaan nan ni ŋan nin ni jiɛn per u, amaa wee ki sɔŋir u sufu, wee ki laa che npoobiim n kɔɔ ki tan lagiri.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: N balin ni le, ni che ni bam nmann pɔŋ nin u.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Ni nan bu le n sɔb ki mag ni njee ni sin chee n mɔb biɛn waa?.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Ni ye ni jiɛn wan a bir ki per u ke, n mu jiɛn per wa. Le nan wee n jiɛn perin, hali ni ye niba le n jiɛn per ke, nim bu le n jiɛn per u Masiya a nintogil.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: wee Sintani n laa kan npɔɔn ki lar ti. Nan bu-n ti nyi u san kaah ye man.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Maah nan foo Tirosi a tiŋin ke n mɔɔn Masiya a bɔŋann ni, le n biɛ ke Uyudaan sin loor u toon a naanganl ki sei ma.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: Amaa maah kaa nan kan n na a big Tatusi nin yaan ni bu che n sufu kaa nan dɔ, nibu le n chabir nin yaan yaab, le ki jon Masedoniya.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr le ye kunɔɔpoog, nan bu-n iyoo biɛn U lii ti sam ki pee nnyaŋim a naatuŋ Masiya a pɔɔm bu. U bɔr mɔ le ke tulaali a baam kaah mɔ man ni, le u che ti mɔɔn ti ilog biɛn.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Tim le ye Masiya a tulaali ki sei Uwonbɔr ban kan feegab nin ban kaa kan feegab a sisiigin.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Ban kaa kan feegabin ma ban, ti jɔɔ nbaam man che nkum forin le, amaa ban kan feegabin ma ban ti jɔɔ nbaam man che limiɛfol forin le. Ŋma kpaan nin itoon nmɔn naa?.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Taa ye bininfob takpem ban ŋmag Uwonbɔr a bɔr nmɔn wee ti ye kpoŋkpoŋ ki sei bin ni, amaa Uwonbɔr kaah tun ti n bu le ti jɔɔ ibamɔn a pipagir ki be u nintogil ki pag Yisa a pɔɔm bu.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Naah ye Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu le u ta itoon nan ki ŋa ti nɔɔn ni, nibu che naa gbɔɔ ti gbanann ni.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Amaa ti yii tibɔwoonkaar nin ifa a tooi le, taa lar uba, bee ki bii Uwonbɔr a bɔri. Amaa ibamɔn a wɔɔm a poolin le ti ta tiba ki wɔɔn uninfo biɛn a potiɛl kaah wɔɔn u ke un nɔɔ man wee nin gbee Uwonbɔr a num.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Nibu, ni ye Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin pag ki woon ke, ni pag woon ban bor Uwonbɔr a samin nin le.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Dulinya nan a weem jɔɔn ban kaa ye biwonjiɛm a potiɛriya, ni bu baa ga Masiya a bɔŋann nin a wonweemi, u ma ye Uwonbɔr a nunaŋ.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Naa ye ti mɔɔn ti baantɔɔl a bɔr le, amaa ti mɔɔn Yisa Masiya a bɔr le, ke u ma ye ti Yudaau, le tim ye ni toontum, Yisa bu.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah nan len ke, Che nwonween nwonn libunbɔnlin. U ma che u wonweem ween ti sufu ni, ke un sei ti nbiɛm a weem man li sei Uwonbɔr kupag Masiya a num paam.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Amaa ti jɔɔ njɔɔm man be tiyɔgir a binboli yin, le ni wɔɔn ke npɔɔn man jon niwann biɛn nyan Uwonbɔr ban le, amaa naa ye tim ban ni.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Ti kan nton a booli biɛn, amaa naa nyaŋ ti. Ti kan limugil takpem amaa tigbanann kaa gbɔɔ.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Bi mugir ti takpem, amaa Uwonbɔr kaa jiɛn ti. Bi fa ti jaa tum, amaa kaa ku ti.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Ti jɔɔ Yisa a kum ti gbanann nin iyoo biɛn, wee nin che u miɛfolin mu n nyan npaan ni ti gbanann nin.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Tim ban fo nan, ti be nkum a nɔɔn le Yisa bu, naah li ŋa man wee u miɛfol n kpiir ti gbanann nin.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Ni bu nkum be mi toon ni le ti ni, amaa le limiɛfol mu be mi toon yin ni bu.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, N ŋa yadau; ni ma bu le n pag. Kinaaŋ kiba nmɔn le ti mu ŋa yada le ki pag.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: nan bu-n ti nyi wan nan fiin Yisa nkumin, u ma na yin fiin tim ke waah nan fiin Yisa man ni, ki ta tim nin nim ki jo u baan tɔɔl ban.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn ye tinyɔɔr le ki sei ni, wee Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl n foo bininfob takpem, wee bin sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog takpem nin kupag.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Mambu che naa gbɔɔ ti gbanann ni. Hali ti gbanann kaah le bagirin gbaa, amaa ti naaŋin ma mann pɔŋir le iween biɛn.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Nan bu-n fala kan kaa kpiɛg wee ti jin ki wama nan, ni ma yin che ti kan kupag kan yab ki jon ni biɛn nan.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Naa ye ti num paa nan be npaan ni nin bu le, amaa ti num paa nan woo-n bu le. Nan be npaan ni nin kaa yin yuuri, amaa nan woo-n le yin yuur.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Ti nyi ke dulinya nan a gbanann tan nann kunanboŋ wee ti be nan ber ke, ti jɔɔ iniin yan wee Uwonbɔr maa, iniin yan kaa jɔɔ nlumi ki be Uwonbɔr a niin, iniin yan wee uninfosaal kaa maa yi.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Ni bu ti nin jegil le ki guu lidaal lan wee Uwonbɔr naah li ta ti ki kɔɔn iniin nmɔn yaan.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Nan bu-n ti nan kɔɔ iniin nmɔn yaan ke, taa na yin gaan ni.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Amaa taah be tigbanann tan nann kunanboŋin nin, ti dun le ki fabin. Naa ye ke taa ji ban tigbanann nin a binbem le, amaa ti ban Uwonbɔr a niin le, wee lidaal nan foo ke limiɛfol n nan niɛ tigbanann tan kpegin wee nin diɛ.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Uwonbɔr le ŋa ti sili ki guun nan be nan paam, ki ta u Naaŋ ki sei ti le ni ye siɛra ke u li sei ti nan biɛn wee U siin limɔsiinlin.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Ni ma bu, ti jɔɔ tidindann le iyoo biɛn ki nyi ke taah be tigbanann a niin yin ni, taa be Uyudaan ban ni.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: Yada bu le ti be, taa be ti num kaah ga nan nin bu.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: N len na, ti jɔɔ tidindann, n bi li ban ke na ti pii bi nyan tigbanann a niin nan yaan na ki kun Uyudaan ban na.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Nibu, ti num man le nin ti penn u sufu, taah be tigbanann a niin nan yaan, bee taah kaa be ti yaan ni.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Nan bu-n ti biɛn na li sir Masiya a nintogil tibɔr a ji daal; uninfo biɛn na li gaa u yɔr u toon kaah nan ye man waah nan be tigbanann nan yaan ni, naah ŋan naa, naa kaa ŋan naa u yɔr le yen.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Mambu, ti nyi naah ye ti li san Uwonbɔr man, nibu le ti pɔɔgir bininfob a gbanann baah li ta biba ki jirin u ban man. Uwonbɔr nyi ti binbem kaah be man, le n jɔɔ tama ke ni mu nyi n sufu ni naah ye man.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Naa ye ti pagir tiba le ni ban jigin ni, amaa ti tenn ni nsan le, wee ni fir faa igbingban ti bu, naah li ŋa man wee ni fir kan njiim ki sei ban far igbingban baah ga nan nbu ki jon nan be sufu nin bu.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Ni ye ti yelig le ke, Uwonbɔr bu le ti ye man; ti mu kaa ji yelig ke, nim bu le ti ye man.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Masiya a bamin le ŋmag ti, taah biɛ ke uninfo uba le kpo ki sei bininfob biɛn ni, ni bu bininfob biɛn kpeg le.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: Bininfob biɛn bu le u nan kpo, ke ban fo-n la ji be bi ba bu, amaa ki li be ke wan nan gaa bininfob biɛn a kum ki kpo ki ji fiin nkumin ni.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Daandaan nan ki jɔɔ chaa, taa ji lig uba ke dulinya nan a ninfob kaah lig u man nni. Hali taah nan lig Yisa man ni, taa ji lig u man ni.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Ni bu uninfo wan be Masiya ban ke, udaan nin ye uninfopɔɔn le, u binben kpegim nbiɛn jon na, le nbinben pɔɔn dan!.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Ni niɛ biɛn nyan Uwonbɔr ban le, u ma che ti jir mɔŋ nin U Masiya bu, le ki sei ti jir mɔŋ a toon.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Ke Masiya bu le Uwonbɔr jir ki mɔŋir nin dulinya a ninfob, kaa ji kaan bininfob a bir tan wee bi tun ni. Le ki ta jir mɔŋ a bɔr ki sei ti.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Mambu ti ye ban si Masiya a taalin le, le Uwonbɔr be nin ti ki che ti yi bininfob. Yisa bu le ti balin ni: Ni jir ki mɔŋ nin Uwonbɔr a.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Tim bu le Uwonbɔr nan che wan kaa jɔɔ tibir labir tibirdaan, u ma bu le ti na yin ŋa Uwonbɔr a buchim.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Tim ban ye Uwonbɔr a toontunn tab, ti balin ni le, ni laa che u nyunyunbasanlin n gab yɔli.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: U len ke, Iyoo kaah nan nan foo nin n gbir ni miɛmin, n nan pel ni miɛma, Iyoo kaah nan nan foo nin bininfob n kan feegabin, n nan ter na. N bei ni le, daandaan nan le ye Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl, daandaan nan le ye feegab a daal.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Taa ta litangbagil gbiɛrikaal ki bil uba a samin ni, mambu uba kaa li fir kan ti galin ti toon yin ni.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Ti ga fala nin limugil nin tibɔr, amaa le ki jɔɔ limɔril takpenl ki ta tiba ki sei itoon nin nin ni wan biɛn ni, taah ye Uwonbɔr a toontumin nbu.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: le bi boo ti, ki kɔɔn ti tiloondirin, ki gaan tijar ti bu, le ti le tun itoon nmɔn nin inunman nin nkom kaa jiɛn nyiɛgi;.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: ti ye bipopendam le, ki jɔɔ nnyinyin, limɔril nin nbinben ŋan ki gbee nin Kasi a Naaŋ nin njiɛm.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: Ibamɔn a pipagir nin Uwonbɔr a pɔɔmin; nin tibuchinn a kunkɔntiɛr tan be ti nɔɔjiim nin ti nɔɔgam paamin;.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: biba tenn ti jilima le biba mu kaa tenn ti jilima, biba pag ki mann ti le biba mu pag ki bii ti; ti ye bibamɔn pagib le amaa le bi yin ti tibɔkardam;.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: bi nyi ti, amaa le ti be ke baa nyi ti le-n; ti kpeg le amaa le ki sin fo, bi boo ti le amaa le kaa ku ti;.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: ti jɔɔ npoobiin, amaa le iyoo biɛn ke ni mɔ ti, ti ye faladam le, amaa le ti che bininfob takpem kpann bijɔɔndam; taa jɔɔ niba, amaa le ki sin jɔɔ niwann biɛn.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Ti pii pag ti poolin a bɔr biɛn ki bei nim Korenti yaaba, ki loor ti sufu ki sei na.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Taa ŋmiɛ ni ti bami, amaa nim le ŋmiɛ ti ni bam.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: Ni ŋaam ke taah ŋaan man ki tenn nin, n pag tenn ni ke n baantɔɔl a buwaab le-n, ni mu nloor ni sufuu man.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Ni laa por kudaag kuba a kpikpiɛl nin ban kaa ye biwonjiɛmin ke inaaja ilee kaah por man nni. Nmɔkpaa mi la be bibuchim nin bipootondam a sisiigin naa? Bee nween nin libunbɔnl jɔɔ ba a nɔɔ nin tab baa?.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Yisa nin Sintani li fir lagin tab baa? Uwonjiɛn nin wan kaa ye uwonjiɛn li fir jɔɔ nmɔkpaa?.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Kuwonjiɛndiig nin kutingbandiig jɔɔ baa a mɔcheel nin tab baa? Tim le ye Uwonbɔr wan fo-n a diig. A li ke Uwonbɔr kaah len ke, N li be nin bi, ki li choon nin bi, Ki li ye bi Wonbɔru, wee bi mu ye n ninfob.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Man bu ni nya bi ban na, ki li jɔɔ nbɔgirim, Uyudaan le len man. Ni laa maa niwann nan ye tijaginn ni, wee N gaa ni.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: Wee N li ye ni Baa, le ni mu li ye N naachim nin N bisiɛm, Uyudaan wan jɔɔ npɔɔn le len man.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Daandaan nan ti nabum, ti ban ni biɛ Uwonbɔr kaah san Masedoniya a wonjiɛm a laginn kunyunyunbaag man le.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: Nin bi fala nin bi bigim takpem a poolin, ni biɛn yɔli, ni nan mɔŋ bi le takpem le bi nan pu Uwonbɔr ipiin nin npopeen.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: Nan bu-n n ji bi siɛra ke bi sei baah jɔɔ nan le, ke nin jon bi pɔɔm a tarig. Bi tɔɔl a bam bu.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: bi nan balin ti ke ti sei bi nsan wee bin ta bi nɔɔi ki kɔɔn biwonjiɛm a teril nmɔn yaan weem.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Bi nan ŋa ki jon taah bi tiɛr ke bi li fir ŋa man na, amaa bi nan chinn ta biba le ki sei uyudaan; le ki nin ta biba ki sei ti le ti ŋmag Uwonbɔr a bam.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: Mambu le ti nan kpaan Tatusi ke taa pii nan piin ki tun man ni, u li tun man na ki diɛ linyunyunbasanl a toonl nan ni tɔɔl ban na.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Amaa naah jɔɔ nan yaba: Ni yada, nin ni pipagir, nin ni nyinyim, nin linunmanl nin njiɛm wee ni jɔɔ nin ti, ni mu che ni nyunyunbasanl a piini li yaba.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: Maa siin nsiim le ki sei ni, amaa n wɔɔn ni kpiɛtiib kaah tun nin linunmanl man le. Ke n mag ni jiɛm kaah ye man.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Ni nyi ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanl kaah ye man. U nan ye uwandaan le, amaa nim bu le u kpann faladaan, naah li ŋa man wee u fala nmɔn n che ni kpann biwandam.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: N kpaani sɔ, nan li ŋan ki sei ni tibɔr nan yaan: Jirib n-yaan nim le nan chinn ki sei ni piini kaah ye nan, naa ye naah li pu n-yaa binba amaa ni wɔɔn naah jɔɔ kɔlisa nin ni tun man le.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: Daandaan nan ni gbaar itoon nmɔn na, wee naah jɔɔ kɔlisa ki jiɛ itoon yan nin nfir nɔɔ tab, naah li fir ki ŋa man.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Ni ye a sei Uwonbɔr nin nbam ke, Uwonbɔr li gaa ipiin nmɔn ŋaah jɔɔ nan, kaa ye ŋaah kaa jɔɔ nan ni.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: Ti boni kaa ye ti tun ni itugi wee ni kpiɛtiib ma li foori, amaa ni biɛn a tugi li kpaa dede.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: Naah jɔɔ wee ni yab daandaan nan ni, ni ŋan nin ni ter ban kaa jɔɔ nna, wee ni ye ke ni mu kaa nan jɔɔ ke, bi mu li ter ni, ni niɛ le li che ni biɛn li kpaa nin tab.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: a li ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Wan nan chiɛ takpemin, waa nan jɔɔ ki tan lagiri, le wan nan chiɛ wama-n, naa nan waari.

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: Uwonbɔr n gaa kunɔɔpoog nin waah che Tatusi a baga be ni ban, ke n baga kaah be ni ban man ni.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Nan bu-n taah balin u ke un jo ni ban ni, u cheeu, le u baantɔɔl a num man nin un dan ni ban, nin u bam.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: Ti li che ti na big kiba nnɔɔ u ki dan ni ban. Ti na a big nmɔn kaah mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin man ni bu che biwonjiɛm a laginn biɛn pagir u le.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: Nan kpiɛ, biwonjiɛm a laginn nin le ji gann u ke u li nɔɔ ta wee ti tu ipiin yan wee bi nan ŋaan wee yin don uyudaan baantɔɔl a yinl, ki ji wɔɔn taah jɔɔ linunmanl nin ti ter man.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Ti ban taah li ŋa man le wee bininfob nlaa bii ti yinl ki ke ti ban niba ipiin nmɔn yaan ni.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Ti boni kaa ye ti tun nan ŋan Uwonbɔr a numbimin a binba le, amaa ti ban ti tun nan ŋan le bininfobin tɔɔl a numin.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Nan kpiɛ, ti yin che ti na a big kiba le nnɔɔ bi ki dan, wan wɔɔn ti waah jɔɔ kɔlisa man nin niwann biɛn ni nin iyoo biɛn, waah jɔɔ tidindann takpem ni bu nbu.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: Tatusi ma bu ke, u ye n toontunkpiɛg le ni sisiigin; amaa ti na bum n ma si biwonjiɛm a laginn a taai-n le, ki ye jilima sei Masiya.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Mambu ni wɔɔn bijab nan na ban bi man, nin nan bu wee ti jɔɔ kalanbaani ni bu, wee biwonjiɛm a laginn nin kan.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: A li ke taah jɔɔ limɔsiinl man ni, n jɔtiib, ni che ti ŋa tiba kasi ki nya nan ye tijaginn ni ki sei tigbanann nin kinaaŋ nna, wee ki li be nbamɔm ki li jɔɔ kasi a binbem nin jilima ki tenn Uwonbɔr.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Ni loor ni sufu ki sei ta man. Taa tun ki bii uba, taa ta kuŋmaruŋ dan uba a binbemin ni, kaa jin uba dulimi.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: Maa pag ni niɛ ke n bii ni le; n pii bei ni ke ni be ti sufu ni, tim nin ye nba le ti miɛfolin bee nkumin.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: N jɔɔ tidindann takpem ni bu; nin ligbingbanfaal ni bu, n jɔɔ ngbanann pɔɔn takpem, nin ti yaa bɔmugkaarin biɛn n gbee nin nmɔn le.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Taah nan foo Masedoniya a tiŋin ni, tigbanann kaa nan kan lifoorili, ti nan kan limugil ilog biɛn ni, ikɔn nan be itingbaa-n, le bijawaam mu nan be ti sufu ni.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: amaa Uwonbɔr wan sɔŋir bipoobiindam a sufu-n nan sɔŋ ti sufu Tatusi a damin na.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: Naa ye u dam n-yaa binba le nan sɔŋ ti sufu, amaa lisufusɔŋil lan wee ni nan ta sei u nbu. U nan bei ti naah ban ni kam man na, ni poobiim nin ni baga kaah be n ni man, naah li ŋa man wee nin mɔŋim ki jo naah nan mɔm man ni.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: Hali ni ye n gbɔŋ nmɔn nan bii ni sufu ke, naa mug mi, ni nan mugim mau, n kan ke n gbɔŋ nmɔn nan dam na, amaa naa nan yuuri.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: Amaa daandaan nan n jɔɔ npopeen, naa ye ni sufu kaah nan bii n bu, amaa ni sufubiil nmɔn kaah che ni binbem kpenn nbu. Ni nan ye Uwonbɔr a bamin le ni sufu bii amaa naa nan sei ni kidaŋ ti bu.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Biwonjiɛm a poobiim che bi ga nkpenim man tenn bi nŋmarim le mambu npoobiim nmɔn a bosu kaa mugir bi, amaa dulinya nan ma a poobiim jɔɔ nkum le chaan.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Ni lig biwonjiɛm a poobiim nan kaah tun nan ni ni: Ni che ni jɔɔ inunman, ki kɔn ni bamɔn bu, le ni ŋuui fii, ki san bijawaam, ki ban ni kam le ki ta ni ba sei, nin naah ŋa sili ki ban ibamɔn ngann man, naah tun man nmɔn, ni sin wɔɔn ke naa jɔɔ niba a galin tibɔr nmɔn yaan ni.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: Maah nan sɔb kugbɔŋ nmɔn ki sei ni, naa nan ye uninfo kaah tun tibir bu le n nan sɔb ki, bee naa ye uninfo wan wee u galin u nbu le n sɔb ki. Amaa n nan ban ni tɔɔl nbiɛ ni baga kaah be ti paam man Uwonbɔr a nintogil, ni man nan che n sɔb ki.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: Ni niɛ nan biɛn bu ti jɔɔ ngbanann pɔɔn. Nan ji kpiɛ ti tɔɔl a gbanann pɔɔm nmɔn bu ye nan le, ti pooi nan pen taah nan kan Tatusi wee u jɔɔ nposɔɔn nin bu.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: N faa ligbingbanl Tatusi ban ni bu-u, le naa jim ifa. Amaa taah nan bei ni nan biɛn wee ni ye ibamɔn nin, ni ma che taah faa ligbingbanl Tatusi ban nibu n mu ŋa ibamɔn na.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Waah ban ni man u sufu nni, mann kpiɛg le, ni ye u nan tiɛr ni biɛn kaah jɔɔ limɔcheel ki tenn u man ke, nin naah nan gaa u ki san u bijawaam ki jegil man.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: Ni ŋaam nmɔn na, nibu n li fir jɔɔ tidindann takpem nin ni.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: Naa kpaa talar nin n sɔb kugbɔŋ ki bei ni naah li tun ki tenn biwonjiɛm man ni.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: N nyi naah ban ni ter bi. N gbaa far ligbingbanl le ni bu Masedoniya yaab ban, ki bei bi ke nim ban be Akaya a timin ni, jirib n-yaan ni nan ŋa sili le nin ni ter, ni yaa nunmanl bu che Masedoniya yaab mu ban bin ter le.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: Amaa n tun ti nabum nan le ke taah nan far ligbingbanl nan bu-n kaa ye yɔli, amaa ni ŋan ni ŋa sili ke maah pii bei bi man ni.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: Ni ye Masedoniya a tiŋ a ninfob nan nɔɔm foon ni ban ki miin naa la ŋa sili ke, ifa li jim, le naa ye n yaa binba le ifa nmɔn yin ji amaa ti biɛn a fai, nan bu-n ti nan fa ligbingbanl nibu bi ban na.
