eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: Mambu le n tiɛr ke ni ŋan nin n balin ti nabumin, wee bi chinn gɔnn ni wee ki mann tibuchinn a piini yan wee ni nan siin limɔsiinlin ki diɛ. Le i ŋa sili ke ibuchin piin ni, ni ma wɔɔn ke ni ŋa ni tɔɔl a bamin le naa ye uba le mug ni.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Ni li tiɛr ni niɛ, wan bun idi wama ke u na yin chiɛ wama le, le wan mu bun idi nin tibuchinn ke u mu na yin chiɛ takpem le.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Uninfo biɛn n pu u piini waah tiɛr man u sufu ni, ki laa pu nin npoobiin ni, bee uba n mug wi, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr ban wan pu ipiin nin npopeen le.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Le Uwonbɔr le che U nyunyunbasanl takpem li be ni bu, wee niwann biɛn nin iyoo biɛn ke, ni li jɔɔ naah ban nan biɛn, wee ni che ke ni fir tun itoon biɛn.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: Ke naah sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, U ta U piin ki yaal sei faladam U buchin kaa jɔɔ diɛmi.

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Uwonbɔr wan tenn bininfob idibool wee bi bu-n, ki ji tenn bi tijiir wee bi ji-n, U ma le li sei ni idibool ki che yin nɔb, man le U li che ni chiɛ ibuchin di takpem.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: Ni li ŋa biwandam niwann biɛn ni, wee ki li pu kulaguŋin iyoo biɛn, tim bu ni piini nmɔn li kpann kupag ki tenn Uwonbɔr.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: Litoonl lan wee ni tun nan kaa pa ye nan tenn biwonjiɛm baah ban nan le, amaa nitɔɔl che Uwonbɔr ga npagim le takpem.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Naah ter bi nbu, ni ma wɔɔn naah ye ban, bininfob li pag Uwonbɔr nin limɔcheel lan wee ni jɔɔ ki kpenn ni binbem ki sei Masiya u bɔŋann nbu-n, nin naah por ipiin ni ki sei bi nin bininfob biɛn man.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Baah miɛ Uwonbɔr ki tenn nin, bi sufu le kun ni, Uwonbɔr kaah ta nyunyunbasanl lan kaa jɔɔ tarig ki sei ni nbu.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Ni che ti sei Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog, nin waah sei ti ipiin yan kaa jɔɔ nmagimi.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Min Pɔɔl wan jɔɔ jiininba nin nbinben ŋan Masiya ban ni, le tenn ni bimɔb nan, min wan wee ni ke n be ni ban ke n san bijawaamin amaa wee n nyan ngbaan ke n jɔɔ lipoobilin!.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: N balin ni le ke n nan dan ni ban n-yonn man ke n laa jɔɔ lipoobil a li ke maah tug man ki jiin ban tug ke ti be dulinya nan a binbem le nni.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Ni ye ibamɔn le ke ti be dulinya-n le, amaa taa kɔn dulinya nan a kɔnli.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
local: Tikunkɔntiɛr tan wee ti jɔɔ kɔn likɔnl nmɔn ni, kaa ye dulinya nan a kunkɔntiɛri. Amaa ti ye tan jɔɔ Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm man li fir bii Sintani a pɔɔm le.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: Ti pag bii limɔkpel nin kalanbaanidam ban ke bi jɔɔ nlam ki jon Uwonbɔrin. Ti nyaŋir ipotiɛ biɛn, ki che ni cheel Masiya a mɔb.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Ni ye ni jɔɔ limɔcheel ke, ni bɔɔn bu ti mu li ŋa sili ki dar tukangbirdam biɛn a tuli.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Nim lig nan wee uninfo fir ga nin u numbim a binba nle. Ni ye uba jɔɔ tidindann ke u ye Masiya yaau ke, u li nyi ke ti mu ye Masiya yaab le, ke waah ye man ni.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: Hali ifa kaa li jɔɔ mi, ni ye n fa ligbingbanl npɔɔn man wee ti Yudaau sei ti ke ti ter ni nbu ke, amaa wee kaa tir ni jaa tum ke, ifa kaa li jɔɔm mambu.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: Maa ban n li sɔb n gbann nan ki yugin ni lami.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Bininfob biba ke, Pɔɔl a gbann ni nann le ke upɔɔndaan uba-n, amaa le u nan foon ti ban n-yonn man ke, u ye ulanjiin le, le u mɔboi kaa ji jɔɔ npɔɔn ni.

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: Ni ŋan bininfob nmɔn nbiɛ ke taah kaa be ni ban wee ki sɔb tigbann nin ke ti tun man ni, ti nan foon n-yonn man ke, man le ti yin tun.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Taa ban ti ta tiba ki bigin nin ban donn biba. Ni ye bi bigin biba ki jɔɔ biba ki wɔɔn tab ke, baa jɔɔ nlami.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: Amaa tim ma, taa li don tiba ki jo ti toon kaah ye man ni, amaa ti le yin che ti gbingbanfaal be Uwonbɔr kaah sei ti itoon yan ni yaan le, itoon yan for ni ban ni.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Taa far ligbingbanl ki lagiri, a li ke taa bi dan ni ban le ke, ti bi li fa lagir, nan bu-n ti nan ta Masiya a bɔŋanni ki wɔɔn na.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: Taa far ligbingbanl ki lagir bininfob biba a toon bu. Ti jɔɔ tama ke, ni yada kaah yabirig chaa-n, ti toon kaah ye nan ni sisiigin li yabig takpem.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: wee ti li fir jo ntim man fɔg ki jon ni yaa tiŋin, ki mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn. Taa ban ti fa ligbingbanl uba kaah tun nan u yerin nin ni.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: Amaa Wan far ligbingbanl ke, un faam u gbingbanl Uyudaan bu.

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: Naa ye uninfo wan pagir uba le bi num gbee, amaa see wan wee Uyudaan a num gbeen.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: N tiɛr ke ni li jɔɔ limɔril n jɔr leen nan bu; amaa ni pii ŋaan man le.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: N jɔɔ lipiiponl nim bu, lipiiponl lan nyan Uwonbɔr ban. N ta ni ki sei chal uba le kpeŋileŋ, u ma ye Masiya, naah li ŋa man wee n ta ni ki sei u wee ni li ye kasi a piikalin.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: Amaa nlam yugir nim bu, nan bu-n n nyi bininfob kaah li dan ki nan lar ni ki ŋmarin ni potiɛri, ki ke ni laa ji nɔɔ Yisa nbamɔmi, a li ke uwaa kaah nan lar Iif man nin.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Ni ye uba dan ni ban ki nan bei ni Yisa uba a bɔr wee naa ye Yisa wan a bɔr wee ti nan pagin ke, bee kinaaŋ kan kaa ye Kasi a Naaŋ nke, bee tibɔr tan kaa ye Yisa a bɔŋanni ke, ti ma le ni gaan nbamɔm weem.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: Amaa maa tiɛr ke ban ke bi ye itoon ki ye bipɔɔndamin a ni uba jomi.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Nba ke maa ye wan kan ntinnim wee ki li pag tibɔri, amaa n jɔɔ nbiɛm. Ti che ni niɛ le won ni num kasi niwann biɛn ni.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: Ni nan ye tibir lee nin maah nan jiin nba tum ke ni kan ndom ki mɔɔn Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin yɔli ki tenn ni nnaa?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: Ni nann ke n nan su biwonjiɛm a laginn biba le ki gaan literil bi ban ke n ter ni.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: Maah nan be ni ban n-yonn man wee kaa jɔɔ niba-n, amaa maa nan mugir ni ke ni seim niba, ti nabum ban nyan Masedoniya nan seim maah ban nan na. N ŋmag nba le kaa ban n li mugir uba, n le ji yin ŋa man le.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: Ni ye ibamɔn le ke Masiya a bɔŋann nin a bamɔn kaah be n ban ni bu, uba kaa be Akaya a tingbɔŋ nan yaan ki che n ta n gbingbanfaalin ki jiɛn ni!.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: Ke babuu? Ni niɛ wɔɔn ke maa ban ni lee? Uwonbɔr ma nyi maah ban ni.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: N le yin tun le ke maah tun man ni, ni ma li che ban ban bin ta biba kpaag nin ti-n nlaa kan nsan nin tiwann tan wee bi far ligbingbanl n bu.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Bi yaa ninfobol ye tibɔkar a tooi le, ki lar ke bi ye bitoontum le, ki ta biba kpann Masiya a tooi.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: Naa ye ligarili. Man le Sintani baantɔɔl nan ta uba kpann nween a malaka.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: Naa ye ligaril li, ni ye Sintani a toontum ta biba kpann bitoontun buchim ke. Bi joorimin le bi na li kan bi toon nmɔn kaah kpaan bi man.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: Jigin n li pag ke: Uba nlaa yim kujɔrugi. Amaa ni ye ni yim kujɔrug ke, ni gaam naah bi li gaa kujɔrug man ni, wee n fir fa ligbingbanl wama.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: Maah far ligbingbanl nan bu-n maa pag ke Uyudaan ni, amaa n pag le ke kujɔrugin.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Bininfob takpem kaah far igbingban ke dulinya yaab kaah ŋaan man ni, n mu li fa n gbingbanl.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Ni nunween takpen nan bu le che ni mɔ ni, le ni jɔɔ limɔril nin tijɔri!.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Ni jɔɔ limɔril nin uba kaah ta ni ki ŋa u yomi, bee ki pen ni bee ki ŋalig ni, bee ki yin ni yɔli, bee ki pebir ni ntapam!.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: Ni ye ifa le ki seim nin ti daburigi kaah kaa nan che ti tun man ni! Uninfo uba kaah jɔɔ lipoobil ki li fir fa ligbingbanlin, min pag le ke kujɔrugin-woo, le ki mu jɔɔ lipoobil ki li fir fa ligbingbanl.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Bi ye Hiiburu yaab lee? N mu ye Hiiburu a ninfou le. Bi ye Iziral yaab lee? N mu ye Iziral a ninfou le. Bi ye Abiraham a yaabili lee? N mu ye Abiraham a yaabil le.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Bi ye Masiya a toontum lee? Min jon ba, ki kɔɔ tiloondirin ke naa jɔɔ tarigi, le bi boom inaanbaan te-n, le n kan kininbɔŋ kan mal nkum jigin.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Itaa iŋmu le Juu yaab boom inaanbaan pitaa nin iwiɛ .

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Rom yaab nan boom ilanbɔn nfim mitaa. Bininfob nan gabirim itangbag isaŋ iba ki nyi ke n kpou. Tiŋalin nan labirim ki chigin nnyumin nfim mitaa, le n be limɔgilin liwonmunl nin kunyiɛmuŋ.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: N sanchonl a poolin, n nan ti ga kininbɔŋ ikpen ni, ki ji ga kininbɔŋ bininfob ban feen ban, nin n tɔɔl a tiŋ a ninfob ban, nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab ban, ki ji ga kininbɔŋ ntintakpemin, nin itindoo-n nin imɔgin nin n nabum ban choon ki larin ban.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: N tun itoon nin linunmanla, ki be kaa dɔ jiɛn ni; nkom nin nnyunyum nan ti chorim le, amaa le maa ji; le tiwar choom le n wan yee waŋ kaa foomi.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: Naa ye nima a binba le n nan kan ni, biwonjiɛmin a laginn biɛn a mugil bu le che n baga be bi bu.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: Ŋma gbarig, wee min kaa gbarigaa? Ŋma wee bi che u kɔɔ tibirin, wee n sufu kaa gaan nŋmi?.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: Ni ye n yin fa ligbingbanl ke, n li fa ligbingbanl wɔɔn n daburigi nbu le.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Ti Yudaau Yisa a Baa Uwonbɔr wan ŋan nin npagim iyoo biɛn ni, U ma nyi maah kaa por tibɔkari.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: Maah nan be Damasiko a tiŋin n-yonn man ni, ubɔr Aritasi a bɔrbig nan ban un choom le ki nan che bitɔtogib si kitiŋ nmɔn a timansanmɔnn ki guu ke bin choom.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: Amaa le bi nan taam ki kpeen kukachiɛgin, ki nan taam lann kitiŋ nmɔn a gonl a bolin, le n nan nyan uja nmɔn a nɔɔn.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: Ni le ye n li far ligbingbanl le. Hali nin naah kaa jɔɔ tinyɔɔr nan biɛn ni, n li wɔɔn ni zii nin tibɔkpiirikaar tan nyan Uyudaan ban.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: N nyi uja uba wan ye Masiya a ninfou, wee Uwonbɔr nan taa u don papaab. Ni bin piig nin ina sɔ. Maa nyi njee ni nan ye tigbanann le bee naah kaa nan ye tigbanann ni, maa nyi, Uwonbɔr a binba le nyi.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Le n nyi uja nan naah ye tigbanann bee naah kaa ye tigbanann ni maa nyi, amaa Uwonbɔr ma nyi.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: le u don paab, nin yaan nmɔn le u nan gbir imɔbon yan wee uninfosaal kaa jɔɔ npɔɔn nin un pagi.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: N li fa ligbingbanl uja nan a bosu bu, amaa maa li fa ligbingbanl n baantɔɔl bu, see n daburigi a binba bu.

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: Hali ni ye n gann ki ke n fa ligbingbanl ke, maa li ŋa kujɔrugi, nan bu-n n yin pag ibamɔn le. Amaa maa yin fa ligbingbanli, wee nin che uba npagim ki jo npagim man kpaan nim mi ki jiin maah tun man bee maah pag nan ni.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: Nan bu wee maa far ligbingbanl ki tan lagirin ye maah nan kan kpiir wɔɔn takpem nbu, le nkunkon man ye Sintani a toom min chɔgim ki paar be n gbanann ni ke nin mugirim wee n laa donn n baa ki tan lagiri.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: N balin Uyudaan nfim mitaa ke un nyan nkunkon nmɔn n ban na.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: Amaa le u beim ke, Maah saŋ kunyunyunbaag man ni kpaan nin ŋa, N pɔɔm jɔɔ yaag idaburigin le. Ni bu n li fa ligbingbanl takpem n daburigi yin bu, wee Masiya a pɔɔm li be n ban.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: Mambu, Masiya le che n num gbee, n daburigin, n sugirin, limugilin, fala a poolin, ki ga tibɔmugkaar. Ni ye idaburig choom ke, iyaayoo le n jɔɔ npɔɔn.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: N ŋa nba le kujɔrug, amaa nim le che n ŋa man. Nim le bi li pagim, nan bu-n hali maa le ye niba ke, amaa ban lar ke bi ye itoon pɔɔndamin, kaa jomi.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: Nan wɔɔn ntoon kaah ye wan ye siɛra nin limagil nin ligaril a toonl le. N nan tun ni niɛ biɛn ni ban nin limɔril le takpem.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Ba a bɔgirim be nim nin ni wonjiɛn tab bimiɛnin a sisiigin naa? Maah kaa nan ye litugil ki sei ni nbuu? Ni jiɛn per ma.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: Daandaan nan n ŋa sili le ke n dan nan gɔnn ni wee nin kpiɛ nfimitaa. Maa nan yin ye litugil le ki sei ni, nan bu-n naa ye naah jɔɔ nan nle n ban ni, amaa nim le n ban. Nan bu-n naa ye nbuwaab le ban tiwann ki siig ban ye bi, amaa ban ye bi le ban tiwann ki siig nbuwaab.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: Ni li mɔŋim pam, ni ye n ta n faal biɛn nin npɔɔm ki ter ni ke. Ni ye n ban ni takpem ke, nim ma yin bam wama lee?.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: Ni che ni li ye man na, maa ye litugil ki sei ni. Nin ni ma biɛn ni, ni ke n ye kibibirig le ki pen choo ni!.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: Maah tun bininfob ban ni ban nin, bi ni uba jin ni dulim min buu?.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: N nan balin Tatusi ke un dan ni ban, ki ji nan che ti na a big kiba mu chiɛn u. Tatusi nan nyi ki jin ni dulim waa? Min nin u ma kaa piɛ kinaaŋ le nin nsan n baa?.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: Ni tiɛr ke ti degin tiba le ni ban naa? Ti be Uwonbɔr a nintogil le ki pag ke ban be Masiya-n ni; n jɔtiib, nan biɛn wee ti tun ni, ni ye nan li sei ni ngbananpɔɔn le.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: N lam yugir nin n dan wee kaa na yin kan ni maah ban ni li be man nni, wee ni mu kaa yin kam naah ban n li be man ni. N san bijawaam le nba ke limɔkpel na li be, bee lipiiponl, bee lijagaal, bee nbɔgirim bee kutingbiig, bee libɔɔnsul bee kalanbaani chɔgiŋ, nin kuŋmaruŋ.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: N san bijawaam le ni ye n ji jirin ni ban ke Uwonbɔr li seim jiininba ni nintogil, n na li jɔɔ lisufubiil nin ban tun tibir wee kaa la kpenn bi binbemin ki nyan tijaginn nin iwanjaab toon nin nwanchɔgin binben, ni ma nin ye bi tooi.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: Ni niɛ le yin kpiɛ maah gɔnn ni nfim mitaa. Ni kpaa talar le nin tibɔr biɛn li jɔɔ siɛradam bilee bee bita.

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: N pii nan sei ni ikpaan maah nan be ni ban nfim milee nna. Daandaan nan hali maah kaa le ji be ni ban ni, n le ji kpaan ni le. N nan jirin ke, maa yin jiɛn ban pii chinn ŋa tibir bee ban gurin a tuli a darimi.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: naah ban ni biɛ ke Masiya le sin jɔɔm ki pagi. Waa ye kidaburig le ni sisiigin ni, u jɔɔ npɔɔn ni ban.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: Nin ni bamɔn, bi nan kpaa u lidapunpunl bu u daburigi ni, ni biɛn yɔli u jɔɔ limiɛfol Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm bu. Man nmɔn bu le ti mu jɔɔ u daburigi nmɔn, ni biɛn yɔli Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm bu ti mu li jɔɔ limiɛfol nin Yisa ki tun ki tenn ni.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Ni mag ni bau njee ni sin be nin yadaa; ni baan ni bau. Naa nyi ke Masiya be ni ban naa? Wee naa ye ni lu ni magilin le-n.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: Le n ga jin ke ni li biɛ ke taa lu limagilin ni.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: Daandaan nan ti miɛ Uwonbɔr ki tenn ni ke ni laa tun niba a biri. Naa ye ke bininfob n kan taah nyaŋ limagilin man ni, amaa ti ban ni tun nan ŋan, hali gbaa ni yin nann ke ti walin gur le-n.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Taa li fir tun niba ki bii ibamɔn nmɔn ni, amaa ti le yin tun ibamɔn le.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: Ni mɔ ti le n-yonn man wee ti jɔɔ idaburig amaa wee nim pɔɔ ke; ti wonmiɛl ye nan li mann ni le.

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
local: Mambu le n sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tenn ni, ni ye maa be ni ban ke, wee ki nan dan ni ban n-yonn man ke n laa fiin n ŋuul npɔɔm bu. Ti yudaau nan seim npɔɔn nmɔn ke n ter ni le, naa ye n bii ni.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: Ni joorimin n nabum, n chabir ni le, ni li ban nbinben ŋan na, ni gbirim maah len nan nan, ni li jɔɔ lipotiɛl libau, ki li be nin nposɔɔn, le Uwonbɔr wan jɔɔ njiɛm nin nposɔɔni li be nin ni.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Ni li poor tab nbamɔma.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: Biwonjiɛmin biɛn poorin ni.

eng: From Paul, an apostle who is not sent from human authority or commissioned through human agency, but sent through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead.
local: Min Pɔɔl wan ye Masiya a toom nan, maa nyann bininfob ban ni bee naa ye uninfo le tunimi, amaa Yisa Masiya nin ti Baa Uwonbɔr, wan fiin u nkumin nin.

eng: and from all the brothers and sisters with me. To the churches in Galatia.
local: nin n nabum ban biɛn be n ban, Le sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki poor nim biwonjiɛm a laginn tan be Galantiya a timin:.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti Baa Uwonbɔr nin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be ni bu.

eng: He gave himself for our sins, so he could deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.
local: u ma ti yudaau Yisa nmɔn le ta uba ki siin ti taalin ti yaa toonbirin, ke un nyan ti dulinya birig kan a yonn wee ti be nan, ke ti Baa Uwonbɔr kaah ban man nin.

eng: To God be the glory forever and always! Amen.
local: Uma wan ye kupag hali nin n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmi. Ibamɔn.

eng: Im amazed that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ to follow another gospel.
local: Ni garim pam nin naah labir weem ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr wan yin ni Masiya a nyunyunbasanl bu-n wee ki labir nɔɔ tibɔr tan be ti binba nmɔn.

eng: Its not really another gospel, but certain people are confusing you and they want to change the gospel of Christ.
local: taa le ye tibɔŋann ma kolibi. Ibamɔn buu bininfob biba li yin ta kuŋmaruŋ ki ŋa ni sufu ni ki degin ni Masiya a bɔŋann nmɔn.

eng: However, even if we ourselves or a heavenly angel should ever preach anything different from what we preached to you, they should be under a curse.
local: Amaa hali tim bee malaka le nyan papaab ki yin mɔɔn tibɔŋann wee ti be tiba kaa ye tan wee ti pii mɔɔn ki sei nin nke, che nŋmi man kaa jɔɔ njunimin ngaa u!.

eng: Im repeating what weve said before: if anyone preaches something different from what you received, they should be under a curse!.
local: Ke taah pii nan len man nin, le n ji bei ni daandaan nan: Ni ye uba mɔɔn tibɔŋann wee taa ye tan wee ni nan gaa-n ke, che nŋmi man kaa jɔɔ njunimin ngaa u!.

eng: Am I trying to win over human beings or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldnt be Christs slave.
local: N gbaar ki ban bininfob le n ke n toon ŋan bee Uwonbɔr aa? Bee n ban bininfob a num le n gbee maa? Ni ye n le la bi gbaar ban bininfob a num le n gbeem ke, maa bi yin ye Masiya a yomu wi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel I preached isnt human in origin.
local: N nabum, n ban ni li nyi ke tibɔŋann tan wee n nan pagin, naa ye uninfosaal le ŋa ti.

eng: I didnt receive it or learn it from a human. It came through a revelation from Jesus Christ.
local: Maa gaa ti uninfosaal ban ni, naa ye uba mu le ta ti ki wɔɔnimi; amaa, n gaa ti Yisa Masiya wan kpiir ti ki wɔɔnimin ban le.

eng: You heard about my previous life in Judaism, how severely I harassed Gods church and tried to destroy it.
local: Ni pii gbir maah nan jɔɔ nbinben man n-yonn man wee n nan nɔɔ Juu yaab a wonjiɛn samin a bɔra, nin maah nan mugir biwonjiɛm a laguŋ linunmanl ki gbaar ban n bii liwonjiɛnl man.

eng: I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my peers, because I was much more militant about the traditions of my ancestors.
local: N nan lii Juu yaab takpem tim Juu yaab a wonjiɛn samin, min nin ban nan yegin, n nan jɔɔ inunman takpem ki nɔɔ n yaajam a kaal kaah wɔɔn man.

eng: But God had set me apart from birth and called me through his grace. He was pleased.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr, wan nan gannim ke baa la ma min, naah nan nan gbee U num n-yonn man nin le U yim U nyunyunbasanl bu.

eng: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might preach about him to the Gentiles. I didnt immediately consult with any human being.
local: ki kpiir U bija wan be n ni-n ki wɔɔn ke n ta u ki mɔɔn ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaabin, maa nan gbeer uba.

eng: I didnt go up to Jerusalem to see the men who were apostles before me either, but I went away into Arabia and I returned again to Damascus.
local: n mu kaa jon Jerusalam ke n tan kan ban nan chinn ye itoon n ni, amaa libol nmɔn n nan jon Arabiya le, ni bɔɔn bu le n jir Damasiko.

eng: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days.
local: Ibin itaa a bɔɔn, le n nan jon Jerusalam n tan gɔnn Piita ki biɛ u, le ki nan be u ban iweehn piig nin iŋmu.

eng: But I didnt see any other of the apostles except James the brother of the Lord.
local: Maa nan kan Yisa a tooi bimiɛnin a ni uba, see ti Yudaau a waal Jemisi a binba.

eng: Before God, Im not lying about the things that Im writing to you!.
local: Uwonbɔr buu, naa ye tibɔkar le n por ki sɔb tenn ni.

eng: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le n jon Siriya nin Silisiya.

eng: but I wasnt known personally by the Christian churches in Judea.
local: N-yonn nmɔn, biwonjiɛm a laginn ban nan be Judiya a tingbɔŋin ki be Masiya ni-n kaa nan nyimi.

eng: They only heard a report about me: The man who used to harass us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.
local: Bi le nan gbir bininfob biba le kaah pag ke: Uja wan nan mugir ti-n, mu nin mɔɔn yada kan wee u nan ban un choo bii nle ki tenn bininfob.

eng: So they were glorifying God because of me.
local: Le bi don Uwonbɔr a yinl min bu.

eng: Then after fourteen years I went up to Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and I took Titus along also.
local: Ibin piig nin ina a bɔɔn le n ji nan jon Jerusalam, min nin Banabasi. N nan ta Tatusi tɔɔl jon na.

eng: I went there because of a revelation, and I laid out the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles for them. But I did it privately with the influential leaders to make sure that I wouldnt be working or that I hadnt worked for nothing.
local: Uwonbɔr le nan kpiir wɔɔnim ke n jo, ki tan bei bi tibɔŋann tan wee n ti mɔɔn ban kaa ye Juu yaab ban nin. Amaa n nan yin biwonjiɛn sanliibin a binba le ki wɔɔn bi, nan bu-n, n nan san le kaa ban maah tun nan nin bee nan wee n la yin tun nin n gab yɔli.

eng: However, not even Titus, who was with me and who was a Greek, was required to be circumcised.
local: Hali Tatusi, wan nan be n ban nin, nin waah le nan ye Girig a ninfou nmɔn biɛn ni, baa nan mugir u ke un jii u punli.

eng: But false brothers and sisters, who were brought in secretly, slipped in to spy on our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, and to make us slaves.
local: Tibɔr nmɔn nan nyan nnabum man por tibɔkar tenn bi ba ke bi mu ye Yisa a bɔɔnɔɔbin le, bi nan be ti sisiigin le ki pigir ti ki ban bin biɛ taah gaa ti ba Masiya bu man wee ki ŋa ti nsiim a yomi.

eng: We didnt give in and submit to them for a single moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to be with you.
local: Taa nan chee sei bi hali wama, naah li ŋa man wee tibɔŋannin a bamɔn li be nin ni.

eng: The influential leaders didnt add anything to what I was preaching—and whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God doesnt show favoritism.
local: Ban wee bi lig bi ki miin bi ye ban kpaa talarin, baah nan ye bininfob ban nin kaa jɔɔ nbɔgirim min ban ni; Uwonbɔr kaa ji bininfob tibɔr baah nann man ni, bininfob nmɔn kaa kpiɛgim niba imɔleen n-yaan ni.

eng: But on the contrary, they saw that I had been given the responsibility to preach the gospel to the people who arent circumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.
local: Amaa, baah kan nan n ban ye Uwonbɔr kaah gannim ke n mɔɔn u bɔŋann nin ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaab ke waah nan gann Piita ke un mɔɔn tibɔŋann nmɔn ki sei Juu yaab man nin.

eng: The one who empowered Peter to become an apostle to the circumcised empowered me also to be one to the Gentiles.
local: Uwonbɔr wan nan tun nin Piita wee u nan ye ntoon ki sei Juu yaabin, Uwonbɔr nmɔn le tun nin n mu le n ye ntoon ki sei ban kaa ye Juu yaabi.

eng: James, Cephas, and John, who are considered to be key leaders, shook hands with me and Barnabas as equals when they recognized the grace that was given to me. So it was agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, while they continue to go to the people who were circumcised.
local: Jemisi nin Piita ki kpiɛ Jɔnn ban wee bi lig ki miin bi ye bininfojabin, bi tɔɔl nan biɛ ke Uwonbɔr le seim itoon gannkaa nmɔn; ni ma che bi nan sei min nin Banabasi inɔɔ. Ni ma nan ye chee sei tab a nɔɔseil. Tim nin bi ma nan chee sei tab ke min nin Banabasi li mɔɔn tibɔŋannin ki tenn ban kaa ye Juu yaaba, wee bi mu li mɔɔn ti ki tenn Juu yaab.

eng: You irrational Galatians! Who put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was put on display as crucified before your eyes!.
local: Nim Galantiya yaab, tijɔri nan! Unyɔgdaan u la baa ŋmarin ni sufuu? Yisa Masiya a kum lidapunpunlin bu a bɔr nan kar ki wɔɔn ni npaan nin ni tɔɔl a numin.

eng: They asked only that we would remember the poor, which was certainly something I was willing to do.
local: Nan biɛn wee bi nan pag joor ye le ke ti li tiɛr bilanjiim bu, nan wee n jɔɔ linunmanl ki ban n tun.

eng: I just want to know this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the Law or by believing what you heard?.
local: N ban n biɛ niwann niba le kpeŋileŋ ni ban: Naah ŋmag Uwonbɔr a siimin bu le che ni gaa Kinaaŋin bee naah gbir tibɔŋannin ki ŋa ti yada-n buu?.

eng: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he was wrong.
local: Piita kaah nan foon Antiɛg nan, n nan yii u bɔrin le u numin, nan bu-n u le sin nan jɔɔ igalin le.

eng: Are you so irrational? After you started with the Spirit, are you now finishing up with your own human effort?.
local: Ni jɔrig lee? Nim ban nan piin nin Uwonbɔr a Kasi a Naaŋ a pɔɔm nan, ni nin gbaar ban ni ta uninfosaal a pɔɔm le ki jooraa?.

eng: He had been eating with the Gentiles before certain people came from James. But when they came, he began to back out and separate himself, because he was afraid of the people who promoted circumcision.
local: Bininfob ban nan nyan Jemisi ban ki dan nin kaah kaa la nan foon nin, u nan lagin ki ji tijiir nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin le. Amaa bijab nmɔn kaah nan nan foon n-yonn man nin, le u jir bɔɔn ki yiɛr kaa ji lagin ji tijiir nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ban ni, nan bu-n u nan san bijab ban nan nyan bipunjiib a paabim ki dan nin le.

eng: Did you experience so much for nothing? I wonder if it really was for nothing.
local: Naah jin fala wan takpem nan ye yɔli lee? Ke ni sin ye ke ni ye yɔli ke.

eng: And the rest of the Jews also joined him in this hypocrisy so that even Barnabas got carried away with them in their hypocrisy.
local: Le Juu yaab ban nan be niin nmɔn mu kpiɛ Piita bu u larug nmɔn yaan, bi larug nmɔn nan boon Banabasi tɔɔla.

eng: So does the one providing you with the Spirit and working miracles among you do this by you doing the works of the Law or by you believing what you heard?.
local: Uwonbɔr nan ta U Kasi a Naaŋin ki sei ni nin ligaril a toon yan wee U nan tun ni sisiigin nin ke naah ŋmag nsiim nbu le, bee naah gbir tibɔŋannin ki ŋa ti yada n buu?.

eng: But when I saw that they werent acting consistently with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of everyone, If you, though youre a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you require the Gentiles to live like Jews.
local: Maah nan nan kan baah tun man nin kaa nɔɔ tibɔŋannin a bamɔn yin, le n bei Piita bininfobin biɛn a nintogil ke, A ye Juu a ninfou le, amaa a be le ke ban kaa ye Juu yaabin, ŋaa nann Juu a ninfou wi. Ni ŋa nii wee a mugir ban kaa ye Juu yaab ki ban bin nɔɔ Juu yaab a kaalaa?.

eng: Understand that in the same way that Abraham believed God and it was credited to him as righteousness, .
local: Man le, ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ki len ke; Abiraham nan ŋa Uwonbɔr le yada, le ni wɔɔn ke u ye ubuchin le.

eng: We are born Jews—were not Gentile sinners.
local: Tim ban wee bi ma ti wee ti ye Juu yaab, wee kaa ye ban kaa ye Juu yaab tibirdam nan.

eng: those who believe are the children of Abraham.
local: Mambu, ni biɛm ke bininfob ban ŋa yada nan ye Abiraham a yaabili le.

eng: However, we know that a person isnt made righteous by the works of the Law but rather through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ. We ourselves believed in Christ Jesus so that we could be made righteous by the faithfulness of Christ and not by the works of the Law—because no one will be made righteous by the works of the Law.
local: ti nyi ke naa ye uninfo wan ŋmag nsiim le bi ke u ye uninfo ŋan Uwonbɔr ban ni, amaa ban ŋa Yisa Masiya yada-n. Ti tɔɔl ŋa Masiya yada le ke nin che ti bɔr li ŋan Uwonbɔr ban, ti yada n bu, amaa naa ye taah ŋmag nsiim n bu, uba kaa li fir ŋa uninfo ŋan Uwonbɔr ban nsiimin bu.

eng: But when it saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles righteous on the basis of faith, scripture preached the gospel in advance to Abraham: All the Gentiles will be blessed in you .
local: Ni ji sɔb dɔ Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, bininfob ban kaa ye Juu yaab nan a bɔr na li ŋaŋ bi yada bu. Baah kaa la nan gbir tibɔŋannin n-yonn man nin le U nan chinn mɔɔn ti sei Abiraham ke, Fin bu, N li kaab tikaar jaa dulinya nan yaab biɛn bu.

eng: But if it is discovered that we ourselves are sinners while we are trying to be made righteous in Christ, then is Christ a servant of sin? Absolutely not!.
local: Ni ye ke, taah ban ti ŋa bininfoŋam Masiya nin, ni nan wɔɔn ke ti baantɔɔl ye tibirdam ke, ni ji wɔɔn ke Masiya si tibirdam a chaŋ lee? Naa ye man kolibi.

eng: Therefore, those who believe are blessed together with Abraham who believed.
local: Mambu ban ŋa u yada kan tikaar ke Abiraham, wan ye yada daan nin, kaah kan man nin.

eng: If I rebuild the very things that I tore down, I show that I myself am breaking the Law.
local: Ni ye n bi jir maa nan wee n nan choo bii n le ke, ni ma bi wɔɔn ke n ye wan bii nsiim le.

eng: All those who rely on the works of the Law are under a curse, because it is written, Everyone is cursed who does not keep on doing all the things that have been written in the Law scroll .
local: Bininfob ban biɛn nɔɔ nsiim ke bin kan nŋmarimin, limɔpool be bi bu, nan bu-n ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Wan biɛn kaa nɔɔ nsiim man biɛn sɔb dɔ nsiim a gbɔŋin ke, limɔpool be u bu.

eng: I died to the Law through the Law, so that I could live for God.
local: Uwonbɔr a siimin bu le n kpo sei nsiim nmɔn, ke n fir ki be Uwonbɔr kaah ban man.

eng: Im saying that as long as the heirs are minors, they are no different from slaves, though they really are the owners of everything.
local: Maah len man nmɔn wɔɔn le ke ufaajir nmɔn kaah le la ye kibigin, nin waah le jɔɔ nan nmɔn biɛn yɔli, waa yin jɔɔ nbɔgirim nin uyomi.

eng: But since no one is made righteous by the Law as far as God is concerned, it is clear that the righteous one will live on the basis of faith .
local: Ibamɔn, nsiimin bu ke uba kaa be wee Uwonbɔr ke u bɔr ŋan ni, nan bu-n, Ubuchin le be yada bu.

eng: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. And the life that I now live in my body, I live by faith, indeed, by the faithfulness of Gods Son, who loved me and gave himself for me.
local: Bi nan kpaam lidapunpunl bu le nin Masiya le naa ji ye min le be, amaa Masiya nmɔn le be nim. Limiɛfol lan wee n jɔɔ be nin tigbanann nin, n be nin yada le Uwonbɔr a bija-n bu, wan jiɛm wee ki ta u ba seimin.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
local: U yin be ban guu win nin u toon tun ninkpeebin a nɔɔn le, hali ki nan kpaa u baa kaah nan siin u n-yonn man nin.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: Ye n ba bu le Masiya nan nyan ti tiyomirin. Mambu mann sir nbamɔma, ki laa ji ta tiyomir a naajadaag paan a baa bu.

eng: The Law isnt based on faith; rather, the one doing these things will live by them .
local: Amaa nsiim kaa nɔɔ nin yada; nan bu-n, Uninfo wan tun nsiimin kaah wɔɔn man nin li be nsiim nmɔn bu.

eng: I dont ignore the grace of God, because if we become righteous through the Law, then Christ died for no purpose.
local: Maa yii Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanlin ni, ni ye nbuchin binben bi nɔɔ nsiim bu le ke, Masiya bi kpo yɔli le.

eng: In the same way, when we were minors, we were also enslaved by this worlds system.
local: Man mu le ye ti, taah la nan ye nbuwaab Yisa a samin yaan nin, dulinya nan a maabiyiɛrug nan ŋmag ti le ke biyomin.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Ni gbirim! Min Pɔɔl bei ni le ke ni ye ni la ŋmag lipunjiil a siimin ke, ni wɔɔn ke Masiya kaa jɔɔ tinyɔɔr tiba biɛn ki sei ni.

eng: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us—because it is written, Everyone who is hung on a tree is cursed .
local: Masiya nan ta u ba le ki mann nkpenim a manug ki fee ti nyan nsiim a mɔpool lan nan nɔɔ ti-n ki jaa, waah nan gaa ti a sisibol ki sir ki che limɔpool nmɔn jin u nbu, nan bu-n ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Limɔpool be wan biɛn bi jɔn u busub bu.

eng: But when the fulfillment of the time came, God sent his Son, born through a woman, and born under the Law.
local: N-yonn nmɔn kaah nan nan foon biɛn nin, le Uwonbɔr tun U tɔɔl a bija, wan wee upii ma u, wee nsiim ŋmag u.

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: N bei ni le jigin, uninfo biɛn wan ŋmag lipunjiil a siimin ke, ni kpaa le nin un ŋmag nsiim nmɔn biɛn.

eng: He redeemed us so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and that we would receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
local: U nan sei ti nkpenim a manug le ke Masiya bu le ban kaa ye Juu yaabin mu nkan tikaar tan wee Uwonbɔr nan ŋa Abiraham bu-n ni, ke ni ye ti ŋa yada ke, ti li kan Kinaaŋ kan wee U nan siin limɔsiinl ke U li sei ti-n.

eng: This was so he could redeem those under the Law so that we could be adopted.
local: ke un ŋa nkpenim a manug ki nyan ban be nsiim nmɔn yaan nin, wee ti kpann Uwonbɔr a bibamɔm.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: Nim ban pɔɔn ni ba ke Uwonbɔr n len ke ni bɔr ŋan nsiim a ŋmagim bu-n, ni pii bɔgir ni ba Masiya ban na, ki nyan ni ba Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl n-yaan na.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Ill use an example from human experience. No one ignores or makes additions to a validated will.
local: N nabum, n ban n ta idaa biɛn a binbem le ki mag wɔɔn ni tibɔr nan. Bininfob kaah lagin ki poo tipoor tan ke, uba kaa li fir bii ti bee ki ta niba kpiɛ ni bu man nin, man le ye ni niɛ mu.

eng: Because you are sons and daughters, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
local: Naah ye Uwonbɔr a buwaabin bu le Uwonbɔr tun U big nmɔn a Naaŋ le ki dan ki nan be ti sufu ni, le kinaaŋ nmɔn yi ke, Ti dindaau, ti baa.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: Amaa yada bu, le kinaaŋ sei ti npɔɔn le ti jɔɔ tama ki guu nbuchin binben a yonn.

eng: The promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It doesnt say, and to the descendants, as if referring to many rather than just one. It says, and to your descendant, who is Christ.
local: Limɔsiinl nmɔn nan siin ki sei Abiraham nin u yaabil le. Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ kaa ke, Nin a yaabili yi wee ni wɔɔn ke kuninfogbaluŋi amaa u nan ke Nin a yaabil, le ni wɔɔn ke uninfo uba u ma ye Masiya.

eng: Therefore, you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter, and if you are his child, then you are also an heir through God.
local: Mambu, ŋaa ji ye uyomi, amaa a nin ye Uwonbɔr a big le; ŋaah ye U big n bu, le U ŋaaŋ U faajiru mu.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: Ni ma bu Masiya ban, taah jii ti pun bee taah kaa jii kaa kpaa talari. Nan kpaa talar le ye yada kan be npaanin nin njiɛm.

eng: Im saying this: the Law, which came four hundred thirty years later, doesnt invalidate the agreement that was previously validated by God so that it cancels the promise.
local: Maah wɔɔn nan nmɔn ye nan le: Uwonbɔr le nan poo tipoor, ibin kobina nin ibin pita kaah jon a bɔɔn le u ta nsiim mu ki sei bininfob, ni ma bu nsiim nmɔn kaa li fir bii Uwonbɔr a poor tan wee u nan poo siin nmɔn ni.

eng: At the time, when you didnt know God, you were enslaved by things that arent gods by nature.
local: N-yonn man nan jon wee naa la nan nyi Uwonbɔr nan, ni nan ye biyom le ki sei Uwonbɔr kaah naan tiwann tan kaa ye iween nin.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Ni nan ti tun nbamɔm le. Ke ŋma ŋɔɔr ni ke ni laa ji nɔɔ ibamɔn nmɔn naa?.

eng: If the inheritance were based upon the Law, it would no longer be from the promise. But God has given it graciously to Abraham through a promise.
local: Ni ye nsiim a ŋmagim bu le Uwonbɔr tenn bininfob U faal ke, mambu ke waa bi li sei uba lifaal U mɔsiinlin bu, amaa ni nan ye Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu le U sei Abiraham U faal nmɔn limɔsiinlin bu.

eng: But now, after knowing God , how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless world system? Do you want to be slaves to it again?.
local: Amaa daandaan nan ni nin nyi Uwonbɔr, bee Uwonbɔr nyi ni, ke ba ŋa wee ni ji ban ni jir ki nɔɔ dulinya nan a maabiyiɛrug kan ye idaburig nin kunyunyunbaag a binbem nan naa? Ni ban ni kpann tiwann nmɔn a yomi le jigin naa?.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: Kularug nmɔn kaa nyan Uwonbɔr wan yin ni ban ni.

eng: So why was the Law given? It was added because of offenses, until the descendant would come to whom the promise had been made. It was put in place through angels by the hand of a mediator.
local: Ke babu wee Uwonbɔr nan siin nsiim nmɔn naa? Nsiim a biim bu le Uwonbɔr nan ta nsiim nan ki sei bininfob ki kpiɛ U mɔsiinl bu, ki ban nsiim nmɔn li ŋmag bi hali nin n-yonn man wee Abiraham a yaabil lan wee U ma Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinl ki sei-n kaah na li foon. Le malakam ta nsiim nmɔn ŋubin usandanfaar a nɔɔn.

eng: You observe religious days and months and seasons and years.
local: Ni ji iweehn nin iŋmal nin iyoo nin ibin a chiig le!.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Boroboro a nyɔgim wama le che boroboro a yom fugir ki do.

eng: Now the mediator does not take one side; but God is one.
local: Usandanfaar nan kaa si uninfo uba bu; amaa Uwonbɔr ye uba le.

eng: Im afraid for you! Perhaps my hard work for you has been for nothing.
local: N san ni bijawaam le, maah tun nan ki jiin ni paamin nan gab yɔli bu.

eng: So, is the Law against the promises of God? Absolutely not! If a Law had been given that was able to give life, then righteousness would in fact have come from the Law.
local: Mambu nsiim kɔn nin Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinlin lee? Naa ye man ni! Ni ye Uwonbɔr bi siin nsiim man li fir sei uninfo limiɛfol ke, U bi li ke bininfob ye bibuchim le U ban, nsiim nmɔn a ŋmagim bu.

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: Uyudaan bu n jɔɔ tidindann ke naa ji li jɔɔ lipotiɛl liba kpiɛ. Uninfo wan ŋmarin ni potiɛl nmɔn ke, Uwonbɔr li dar u daan a tul, ni baga kaa be waah ye wan ni.

eng: I beg you to be like me, brothers and sisters, because I have become like you! You havent wronged me.
local: N nabum n balin ni le, ni li be ke min na, ke maah be ke nim man nin. Naa galimi.

eng: But scripture locked up all things under sin, so that the promise based on the faithfulness of Jesus Christ might be given to those who have faith.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin bei ke, uninfo biɛn be tibir a loondiigin le, naah li ŋa man wee Uwonbɔr kaah nan siin limɔsiinl ke U li sei ban ŋa Yisa Masiya yada lifaal lan nin, bi yada nmɔn bu le U yin sei bi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: N nabum, ni ye n la bi mɔɔn lipunjiil a bɔr le ke, ba ŋa wee bi ji mugirim maa? Ni ye man ke igalin yan nan ŋa lidapunpunl bu n gab yɔliu.

eng: Before faith came, we were guarded under the Law, locked up until faith that was coming would be revealed.
local: Yada nan kaah kaa la nan foon nin, nsiimin nan ŋa ti biloondiikɔɔb le, le ki loon ti ki nan kpaa n-yonn man wee Uwonbɔr nan kpiir yada a ŋaam a bɔr ki wɔɔn ti-n.

eng: You know that I first preached the gospel to you because of an illness.
local: Ke naah nyi man nin, maah nan bu-n bu le n nan chinn ki mɔɔn tibɔŋann nan ki sei ni.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: Min bi ban ke bininfob ban mugir ni lipunjiil bu nan n bin jiɛn lipunjiil wee ki li faar bi bau.

eng: so that the Law became our custodian until Christ so that we might be made righteous by faith.
local: Mambu, nsiim nmɔn nan ye uguguur le ki jɔɔ ti chaa Masiya ban, naah li ŋa man wee Uwonbɔr n len ke ti bɔr ŋan yada bu.

eng: Though my poor health burdened you, you didnt look down on me or reject me, but you welcomed me as if I were an angel from God, or as if I were Christ Jesus!.
local: Hali nin n wiɛn nmɔn kaah nan mugir ni nan biɛn na, amaa naa nan yim yɔli bee ki yii mi, ni gaam le ke Uwonbɔr a malaka-n, ki ji gaam ke Masiya baantɔɔlin.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: N nabum, Uwonbɔr le nan yin ni le ni kpann ban ye biba. Amaa ni li nyi ni nɔɔ ye nba nmɔn bu ki tun tigbanann a kɔlisa a toon; amaa ni li tun tenn tab nin njiɛma.

eng: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian.
local: Daandaan nan yada a ŋaam a sam kaah dan nan, nsiimin kaa ji ye ti guguuru wi.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Ni mɔmin biɛn be laa? N li fir ji siɛra ke, ni ye ni bi li fir ke, ni bi li chee ki logir ni numbili ki seim.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Nsiim nmɔn biɛn li lagin ki ŋa lisiinjal lan len ke: Li jiɛ a kunkɔɔn tab ke ŋaah jiɛ a ba man nin.

eng: You are all Gods children through faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Ni biɛn nan ye Uwonbɔr a buwaab le naah ŋa Masiya yada-n bu.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Amaa daandaan nan n nin kpann ni dundaau le maah bei ni ibamɔn nin buu?.

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: Ni ye ni gbaa boo tab ki tenn tab ndam ke, ni li nyi ke ni li bii tab.

eng: All of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
local: Nim ban wee bi fun ni Uwonbɔr a nyunful Masiya a yinlin nin, nim le ta Masiya ki yee ke kuwan yeewaŋin.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
local: Bininfob biba jɔɔ inunman ki ban ni ta ni baga ŋa bi ni, amaa naa ye ni ŋam bu le bi ŋaan nmɔn ni. Bi ban bin ta ni ŋa bicham ti ban, naah li ŋa man wee ni li jɔɔ inunman ki ta ni baga ŋa bi ni.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: N bei ni le, ni li nɔɔ Kinaaŋ a binbema, wee tigbanann a bir a kɔlisa a toon laa be nin ni.

eng: There is neither Jew nor Greek; there is neither slave nor free; nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
local: Mambu, nbɔgirim kaa ji be ban ye Juu yaab nin ban kaa ye Juu yaab a sisiigin ni, kaa be biyom nin ban ye bi ba nan a sisiigin ni, kaa be bijab nin bipiib mu a sisiigin ni, ni ma bu ni biɛn ye nba le Masiya ni.

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: Ni ŋan ni li jɔɔ inunman, ni ye ni tun nin ni ŋam bu, wee ni li ye man ki jɔɔ chaa ki laa ye n-yonn man wee n nan be ni ban a binba.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Tigbanann kaah ban nan nin kaa chee sei kinaaŋ kaah ban nan nin ni, le kinaaŋ mu kaah ban nan nin kaa chee sei tigbanann kaah ban nan nin ni. I kɔn nin tab le, ni ma le che naa fir tun naah ban nan ni.

eng: Now if you belong to Christ, then indeed you are Abrahams descendants, heirs according to the promise.
local: Ni ye Masiya le ye ni ke, man ke ni ye Abiraham a yaabili le, ki li ji u faal ke Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinlin kaah wɔɔn man nin.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: N bijiɛkaam, ni wum le ke nmarwiɛn kaah choo upii ke ni wuu man nin, ki nan kpaa n-yonn man wee ni na li chegir Masiya a sam nan yaan.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: Amaa ni ye kinaaŋin lii ni sam ke, nsiim kaa ŋmag ni.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: N bi ban n li be ni ban daandaan nan wee ki mann kpenn n kukɔl, nan bu-n n lam ŋmarin le ni bu.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
local: Tigbanann a binbem be npaan ni le, ni ma ye: Iwanchɔgin toon lipiibanlin bee lijabanlin, tijaginn a toon, nwanchɔgin binben.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Ni beim ni, nim ban ban nsiim li ŋmag ni nan, naa nyi nsiim nmɔn kaah len man naa?.

eng: idolatry, drug use and casting spells, hate, fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, conflict, selfishness, group rivalry.
local: nin libawol manil nin kusɔɔg; kininaŋ, likɔnl, lipiiponl, liŋuul, npooton a bam, kumaabiyiɛrug, nbɔgirim.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke Abiraham nan jɔɔ nbijabum milee le, uba a na nan ye uyom le wan gurin mu a na nan ye upii wan ye uba.

eng: jealousy, drunkenness, partying, and other things like that. I warn you as I have already warned you, that those who do these kinds of things wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: nin linunchɔril, lidanyugbeel, nin lidanyugbeel a toonbir, nin ban tun man a booli. N bei ni le ke maah pii nan bei ni man nin, ban tun man nmɔn kaa na li kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a naamin ni.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: U bija wan wee upiiyomin nan maa-n, u nan ma u le ke bininfob kaah maa man nin; amaa u bija wan wee upii wan ye uba-n nan maa-n, u nan ma u le ke Uwonbɔr kaah nan siin u mɔsiinl man nin.

eng: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
local: Amaa kinaaŋin a looi le ye njiɛm, nmɔn, nposɔɔn, limɔril, tiŋann, nbinben ŋan, si nba bu.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Ni niɛ li fir ta, pag wonn a poolin ke, bipiib bilee nan si Uwonbɔr a poor a kerim milee a sisiimin le, uyom nmɔn a yinl li nan ye: Hega; le u si tipoor tan wee Uwonbɔr nan poo Sinai a jɔɔl a paabin a sisiim, le ki nan ma u big tiyomirin.

eng: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like this.
local: nin jiininba, ki kpiɛ choonba. Nsiim nba kaa ji be wee ki li bii ban tun nan ni.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Daandaan nan Hega si ki sei Sinai a jɔɔl lan be Arabiya a tiŋin nin le, ki nann dulinya nan a Jerusalam le, nan bu-n u ma nin u buwaab be tiyomirin le.

eng: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the self with its passions and its desires.
local: Masiya kaah ye ban nin ta tigbanann a kɔlisa nin bi binben biimin biɛn ki kpaa lidapunpunl buu.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: Amaa Jerusalam kan be papaab nan ma ye u ba le, le ki ye ti na.

eng: If we live by the Spirit, lets follow the Spirit.
local: Taah be kinaaŋin bu-n, ni che ti nɔɔ kinaaŋ nmɔn a tabogi.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: Ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Upiiŋɔɔbee, che ni li mɔŋa, fin wan kaa la nyi ma nbuwaab nan; li yigin ki le sɔɔlin na, fin wan kaa la nyi nmarwiɛn a tom nan; nan bu-n upii wan a chal yii u nan a buwaab yab ki jon upii wan jɔɔ chal nan a buwaaba.

eng: Lets not become arrogant, make each other angry, or be jealous of each other.
local: Ni laa che ti li jɔɔ kalanbaani nin tab, bee ki fiin tab a ŋuui, bee ki li jɔɔ linunchɔril nin tabi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: N nabum daandaan nan ni nann Azig le, ni ye Uwonbɔr a buwaab le, U mɔsiinlin bu.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, baah nan ma wan ke bininfob kaah maa bi buwaab man nin, nan mugir wan wee bi ma u Kinaaŋ a pɔɔmin nin, ni ye man le daandaan nan.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin bei ke nii? Ŋɔɔr upiiyomin nin u bigin ki jaau, upiiyom a big nin upii wan ye u ba nan a big kaa li fir lagin por lifaali.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: Mambu n nabum, taa ye upiiyom nmɔn a buwaabi, amaa ti ye upii wan ye u ba nan a buwaab le.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if a person is caught doing something wrong, you who are spiritual should restore someone like this with a spirit of gentleness. Watch out for yourselves so you wont be tempted too.
local: N nabum, ni ye ni kan uba tun titoonbir ke, nim ban be nin Kinaaŋin ni ter mu soon wee un mann ki sir nbamɔm, amaa wee ki li nyi ni ba bu, naa ye man ke ni mu li kɔɔ libiginlin.

eng: Carry each others burdens and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
local: Ni li tug tab a tugi ya man, ni ye ni ŋaan man ke, ni li gbeen Masiya a siimin.

eng: If anyone thinks they are important when they arent, theyre fooling themselves.
local: Ni ye uninfo tiɛr ke u ye niba, wee kaah sin ye niba ke, u lar u ba le.

eng: Each person should test their own work and be happy with doing a good job and not compare themselves with others.
local: Uninfo biɛn nmag u ba a toon ya. Wee ki nin fir ki fa u gbingbanl u toon ŋan bu, ki laa jɔɔ u ba magir u kpiɛgi.

eng: Each person will have to carry their own load.
local: uninfo biɛn ntum u tɔɔl a tugil.

eng: Those who are taught the word should share all good things with their teacher.
local: Bininfob ban gaa Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin a wɔɔmin, ni kpaa le nin bin por baah jɔɔ niwann ŋann nan biɛn ki sei wan wɔɔnn bi.

eng: Make no mistake, God is not mocked. A person will harvest what they plant.
local: Ni laa che bin lar ni: Uwonbɔr kaa ye kuwanlawaŋi. Uninfosaal chiɛ waah bun niwann nan le.

eng: Those who plant only for their own benefit will harvest devastation from their selfishness, but those who plant for the benefit of the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
local: Wan bun u tɔɔl a gbanann a bam a wan booli ke, u na yin chiɛ nkum le; wan mu bun iwanbool yan gbee kinaaŋin a num ke, u na yin chiɛ limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin le kinaaŋ ban.

eng: Lets not get tired of doing good, because in time well have a harvest if we dont give up.
local: Mambu ni laa che ti bag itoon ŋan a tum bu, ni ye taa jiɛn itoon ŋan a tum ke ti na li kan tinyɔɔr n-yonn man chaan nan.

eng: So then, lets work for the good of all whenever we have an opportunity, and especially for those in the household of faith.
local: Mambu, taah kan nsan nin ti li tun itoon ŋan, ni che ti li tun itoon ŋan ki tenn bininfob biɛn na, naah dii tɔɔ ban bu le ye ban ye biwonjiɛm a niin yaab.

eng: Look at the large letters Im making with my own handwriting!.
local: Ni kan maah ta min tɔɔl a nɔɔ ki sɔb isɔb gbingbem yan ki sei na!.

eng: Whoever wants to look good by human standards will try to get you to be circumcised, but only so they wont be harassed for the cross of Christ.
local: Bininfob ban ban bin wɔɔn biba a ŋam npaan ni nin bi ma mugir ni ke ni jii ni pun. Nan bu che bi ŋaan man ye baa ban biba nmugir bi Masiya a dapunpunlin bu.

eng: Those who are circumcised dont observe the Law themselves, but they want you to be circumcised, so they can boast about your physical body.
local: Ban jiil bi pun nmɔn gbaa kaa nɔɔ nsiimin ni, nin ni ma biɛn ni le ki ban ni jii ni pun wee bi li jɔɔ far bi gbingban ni gbanann a binbem bu.

eng: But as for me, God forbid that I should boast about anything except for the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The world has been crucified to me through him, and I have been crucified to the world.
local: Min kaa li nyi ki fa ligbingbanli, see ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a dapunpunlin a binba bu, u dapunpunl nmɔn le ku kutingbɔŋ nan a kɔlisa n gbanann nin, le n mu a kɔlisa nyan kutingbɔŋin na.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt mean anything. What matters is a new creation.
local: Uninfo wan jii nin wan kaa jii kaa ye niba; nan kpaa talar le ye kuwonaaŋ pɔŋin.

eng: May peace and mercy be on whoever follows this rule and on Gods Israel.
local: Uwonbɔr a posɔɔm nin u nyunyunbasanl li be ban nɔɔ nwɔɔm nan bu, hali nin ban ŋa Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin yada-n ban ye Juu yaab nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin biɛn.

eng: From now on, no one should bother me because I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
local: N bɔjoor kaar ye nan le, uba laa ji mugirimi, n pii jɔɔ Yisa a daagim n gbanann nin.

eng: Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
local: N nabum, ti Yudaau Yisa a nyunyunbasanl li be nin ni naam. Ibamɔn.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will. To the holy and faithful people in Christ Jesus in Ephesus.
local: Min Pɔɔl wan ye Masiya a toom Uwonbɔr a bamin nin, N sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tenn Uwonbɔr a ninfogann kaab ban be Efesusi, ki si bi yada-n Masiya ban nin:.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti Baa Uwonbɔr nin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be nin ni.

eng: Bless the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! He has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing that comes from heaven.
local: Pagim Uwonbɔr wan ye ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a Baa, wan ŋa tikaar jaa ti bu, tikaar tan biɛn nyan papaab ki ye Kinaaŋ a kaar tan be nin Masiya nan.

eng: God chose us in Christ to be holy and blameless in Gods presence before the creation of the world.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah kaa la nan naan dulinya n-yonn man nin, le U nan gann tim ke ti li be kasi ki laa jɔɔ igalin U ban ni. U jiɛm bu.

eng: God destined us to be his adopted children through Jesus Christ because of his love. This was according to his goodwill and plan.
local: U pii nan lol ke U li ta ti ki kpann U buwaab Yisa Masiya bu, U nan lol man le U nungbeelin nin U bam.

eng: and to honor his glorious grace that he has given to us freely through the Son whom he loves.
local: che ti pag Uwonbɔr U jilima nin U nyunyunbasanl lan wee U sei ti yɔli U bijiɛkaagin ni.

eng: We have been ransomed through his Sons blood, and we have forgiveness for our failures based on his overflowing grace.
local: Yisa a chamin bu le ti kan nkpenim a manug, ni ma ye tibir a jiɛn per, Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl takpenlin bu.

eng: which he poured over us with wisdom and understanding.
local: man wee u kpaar tinyɔɔr takpem jaa ti bu nin nlam nin nbiɛm biɛn.

eng: God revealed his hidden design to us, which is according to his goodwill and the plan that he intended to accomplish through his Son.
local: Le U kpiir wɔɔn ti nan wee U nan woon lol U bamin ke un tun nin Masiya, U nungbeel a ŋamin nin.

eng: This is what God planned for the climax of all times: to bring all things together in Christ, the things in heaven along with the things on earth.
local: n-yonn nmɔn nan foo ke, U ni li tun ki gbeen U lol nmɔn, ki lagin waah nan naan nan papaab nin tum biɛn, wee Masiya li ye uyudaan.

eng: We have also received an inheritance in Christ. We were destined by the plan of God, who accomplishes everything according to his design.
local: Masiya ni le Uwonbɔr nan gann ti mu, ki ta ti kpann U tɔɔl a ninfob nin waah ban niwann biɛn ntun man U bamin.

eng: We are called to be an honor to Gods glory because we were the first to hope in Christ.
local: wee tim ban chinn jɔɔ tama Masiya ni-n, nsei U kupag nin jilima.

eng: You too heard the word of truth in Christ, which is the good news of your salvation. You were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit because you believed in Christ.
local: Ni tɔɔl nan kpiɛ ki be nin Masiya le, naah nan gbir imɔleen yan ye ibamɔn nin, ki ye ni feeja a bɔŋann nin. Naah ŋa yada-n bu, le Uwonbɔr ta U yaa daagim ki daag ni, lan ye limɔsiinl Kasi a Naaŋin.

eng: The Holy Spirit is the down payment on our inheritance, which is applied toward our redemption as Gods own people, resulting in the honor of Gods glory.
local: Kasi a naaŋ nmɔn li ye lifaal nmɔn a siɛra hali nin n-yonn man wee ti na li kan nkpenim a manug tim ban ye U ninfobin, tim li pagir U ki tenn U kupag.

eng: At one time you were like a dead person because of the things you did wrong and your offenses against God.
local: Nim bu ke, ni nan ye bitakpiib le nin ban bii nsiim ni toonbir bu.

eng: Since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all Gods people, this is the reason that.
local: Mambu n-yonn man wee n nan gbir naah ŋa ti Yudaau Yisa yada nin naah jiɛ Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab a bɔr man nin.

eng: You used to live like people of this world. You followed the rule of a destructive spiritual power. This is the spirit of disobedience to Gods will that is now at work in persons whose lives are characterized by disobedience.
local: Naah nan be nsiim a biim nin ni toonbirin ki nɔɔ dulinya nan a sam nin kutingbɔŋ nan a yudaau a naamin, nin kinaaŋ kan tun nin bininfob ban kaa jɔɔ limɔcheelin.

eng: I dont stop giving thanks to God for you when I remember you in my prayers.
local: n tenn Uwonbɔr kunɔɔpoog ni bu kaa ta jiɛn ni, ki tiɛr ni bu n wonmiɛlin.

eng: At one time you were like those persons. All of you used to do whatever felt good and whatever you thought you wanted so that you were children headed for punishment just like everyone else.
local: Ti biɛn nan be nsiim a biim nin tibirin nmɔn yaan le, ki tun ti tɔɔl a gbanann a kɔlisa a toon, ki tun nan mɔ ki sei ti gbanann a bam nin ti sufu. A li ke bi ma-n, ti nan ye ban li ji Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋin a fala le.

eng: I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, will give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation that makes God known to you.
local: N le miɛ ti Baa wan ye ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a Wonbɔru, ki ye ti Baa wan ye jilimadaan nin ke, Un sei ni Kinaaŋ a lam nin kpiir wɔɔn, wee ni mann biɛ U nbamɔm.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr a jiɛm kaah yab ki sei ti-n bu, wan a nyunyunbasanl yab.

eng: I pray that the eyes of your heart will have enough light to see what is the hope of Gods call, what is the richness of Gods glorious inheritance among believers.
local: N ji miɛ Uwonbɔr le ke Un ligir ni sufu wee ni biɛ tama kan bu wee U yin ni-n, ki ji biɛ U jilima takpenl a faal lan wee U nan siin limɔsiinl ki sei U ninfogannkaabin.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: le U che ti jɔɔ limiɛfol nin Yisa, hali nin taah le nan kpo nin taah bii nsiimin. U nyunyunbasanl bu le ni kan feegab.

eng: and what is the overwhelming greatness of Gods power that is working among us believers. This power is conferred by the energy of Gods powerful strength.
local: nin U pɔɔn takpem man yab kaa jɔɔ magim wee U ta sei tim ban ŋa U yada-n. Npɔɔn nmɔn tun ki nann lipɔɔn gbingbemil le.

eng: And God raised us up and seated us in the heavens with Christ Jesus.
local: Le Uwonbɔr lagin fiin tim nin Masiya , ki kaan tim nin uma paab a naamin, taah be nin Masiya nan bu.

eng: Gods power was at work in Christ when God raised him from the dead and sat him at Gods right side in the heavens.
local: U pɔɔm nmɔn le U nan ta ki fiin Masiya nkumin ki ta U kaan U nɔɔjiim paam papaab.

eng: God did this to show future generations the greatness of his grace by the goodness that God has shown us in Christ Jesus.
local: U nan tun ni niɛ ke n-yonn man chaan nan u li wɔɔn uninfo biɛn u nyunyunbasanl lan kaa jɔɔ magimin, ki wɔɔn ti u binbenŋam Masiya bu.

eng: far above every ruler and authority and power and angelic power, any power that might be named not only now but in the future.
local: niin le Masiya be ki jɔɔ npɔɔn man jon nnaam a pɔɔm, ki jon tininkper a pɔɔm, ki jon ipɔɔn biɛn, nin nnaam a yerin, ki jon iyin yan be daandaan nan nin yan la yin dan n-yonn man chaan nin na.

eng: You are saved by Gods grace because of your faith. This salvation is Gods gift. Its not something you possessed.
local: Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu le ni kan nŋmarim, naah ŋa Yisa yada-n bu, naa ye ni tɔɔl a toon ŋan bu, ni ye Uwonbɔr a piini le.

eng: God put everything under Christs feet and made him head of everything in the church.
local: Le Uwonbɔr ta niwann biɛn ŋa u nɔɔn, le ki gann u ke u li ye biwonjiɛn a laguŋ a yudaau.

eng: Its not something you did that you can be proud of.
local: uba kaa li fir fa ligbingbanli, nan bu-n naa ye u tɔɔl a toonl li.

eng: which is his body. His body, the church, is the fullness of Christ, who fills everything in every way.
local: Biwonjiɛm a laguŋ nmɔn le ye U gbanann, le U gbanann nmɔn gbee nin U ma Masiya a pɔɔm, le U mu gbee nin niwann biɛn.

eng: Instead, we are Gods accomplishment, created in Christ Jesus to do good things. God planned for these good things to be the way that we live our lives.
local: Uwonbɔr le naan tim, Yisa Masiya bu le u sei ti itoon ŋan a binbem, ke ti li tun man wee u pii nan lol ke ti li tun nin na.

eng: So remember that once you were Gentiles by physical descent, who were called uncircumcised by Jews who are physically circumcised.
local: Mambu ni li tiɛr ke n-yonn man nan jon wee nim ban kaa nan ye Juu yaab wee Juu yaab nan yi ni tipunjorin, wee ki sin yi biba ban jii-n, .

eng: At that time you were without Christ. You were aliens rather than citizens of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of Gods promise. In this world you had no hope and no God.
local: Ni li tiɛr ke iyaayoo nmɔn ni nan bɔgir nin Yisa u, baa nan kaan ni kpiɛg Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaabin ni, ni nan ye bicham le, kaa ji kpiɛ limɔsiinl a poorin yaan ni, kaa jɔɔ tama, kaa jɔɔ Uwonbɔr dulinya nan yaan ni.

eng: But now, thanks to Christ Jesus, you who once were so far away have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
local: Amaa daandaan nan, ni nin be nin Yisa le, nim ban a bol nan fɔg nin Yisa nan, ni nin fɔɔr ki mal wa, Yisa Masiya a chamin bu.

eng: Christ is our peace. He made both Jews and Gentiles into one group. With his body, he broke down the barrier of hatred that divided us.
local: Nan bu-n uma baan tɔɔl le ye ti posɔɔm, u nan che tim Juu yaab nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin lagin ki ŋa uninfo uba ki tir ligonl lan nan ye nbɔgirim a gonl ki be ti sisiigin nin ki jaa.

eng: He canceled the detailed rules of the Law so that he could create one new person out of the two groups, making peace.
local: u tɔɔl a gbanann le u nan ta junn Juu yaab a siim nin bi kɔɔi biɛn. Ke un che nposɔɔn ndan ibol ilee nmɔn a sisiigin, wee bin lagin ki li ye libol liba.

eng: He reconciled them both as one body to God by the cross, which ended the hostility to God.
local: Masiya a kum lidapunpunlin bu, ku kidataag kan be bi sisiigin nin, ki taa bi ki lagin tigbanann tiba kpeŋileŋ, ki taa bi jir mɔŋ nin Uwonbɔr.

eng: When he came, he announced the good news of peace to you who were far away from God and to those who were near.
local: U nan dan ki mɔɔn nposɔɔn a bɔŋann sei nim ban a bol nan fɔg ki mɔɔn nposɔɔn a bɔŋann nmɔn ki sei ban nan mal.

eng: We both have access to the Father through Christ by the one Spirit.
local: Masiya bu le kinaaŋ kiba nmɔn ta ibol ilee nmɔn biɛn foon ti Baa Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: So now you are no longer strangers and aliens. Rather, you are fellow citizens with Gods people, and you belong to Gods household.
local: Mambu, nim ban kaa ye Juu yaabin kaa ji ye ibol iba yaab bee bichami, amaa nim nin Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaabin nin ye kitiŋ kiba a buwaab le, ki ye Uwonbɔr a niin yaab.

eng: As Gods household, you are built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets with Christ Jesus himself as the cornerstone.
local: lifiɛl lan wee Yisa a tooi nin bilinlemin nan tɔɔn li bu-n, li ma bu le n mu tɔɔn le Yisa Masiya ye litangbagil lan ye lifiɛl nmɔn a pɔɔm.

eng: The whole building is joined together in him, and it grows up into a temple that is dedicated to the Lord.
local: Uma bu le kudiig a maan biɛn tɔɔ tab bu, ki che ni choo tab ki kpann kasi a wonjiɛn niin Uyudaan ni.

eng: Christ is building you into a place where God lives through the Spirit.
local: Uma bu le ni mu lagin ki maa kudiig kan yaan wee Uwonbɔr be nin Kinaaŋin.

eng: This is why I, Paul, am a prisoner of Christ for you Gentiles.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu, le min Pɔɔl wan ye uloondiikɔɔr Masiya nin ban kaa ye Juu yaabin bu-n.

eng: Youve heard, of course, about the responsibility to distribute Gods grace, which God gave to me for you, right?.
local: N nyi ke ni pii gbir ke Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl a toon bu, le U nan ta litoonl nan seim ke n tun nim bu.

eng: God showed me his secret plan in a revelation, as I mentioned briefly before .
local: ni ma ye waah nan kpiir tibɔwoonkaar ki wɔɔm, ke maah pii sɔb ti kugbɔŋ nan yaan wama-n.

eng: when you read this, youll understand my insight into the secret plan about Christ.
local: Ni ye ni karin maah sɔb nan nmɔn ke, ni li biɛ maah nyi Masiya a bɔwoonkaar man.

eng: Earlier generations didnt know this hidden plan that God has now revealed to his holy apostles and prophets through the Spirit.
local: Uwonbɔr kaa nan kpiir tibɔwoonkaar nmɔn ki wɔɔn n-yonn man nan jon nin a ninfobi, amaa daandaan nan Kinaaŋin kpiir tibɔwoonkaarin ki wɔɔn Uwonbɔr a kasi a tooi nin u linlema.

eng: This plan is that the Gentiles would be coheirs and parts of the same body, and that they would share with the Jews in the promises of God in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: U bɔwoonkaar nmɔn ye nan le, tibɔŋann nmɔn bu le ban kaa ye Juu yaabin fir ki lagin jin lifaal nin Iziral yaab, ki lagin ye gbanann tiba a ninfob, ki jɔɔ waah nan siin limɔsiinl lan Masiya bu-n ki por nin tab.

eng: I became a servant of the gospel because of the grace that God showed me through the exercise of his power.
local: Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu le n kpann tibɔŋann nan a yomu Uwonbɔr a piini yan wee u seim u pɔɔm a toon yin nin.

eng: God gave his grace to me, the least of all Gods people, to preach the good news about the immeasurable riches of Christ to the Gentiles.
local: Min le joor kaa yabi Uwonbɔr a ninfob nmɔn biɛn a sisiigin, amaa ni biɛn yɔli, Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu le U gannim ki seim nsan ke: N mɔɔn ki wɔɔn ban kaa ye Juu yaabin Masiya a jɔɔm man kaa li fir ban kan ni.
