eng: But our people should also learn to devote themselves to doing good in order to meet pressing needs so they arent unproductive.
local: Ni kpaa le nin ti ninfob ntinn ki ta biba ki sei itoon ŋan a tum, ki ter bi lidaal biɛn baah ban nan, wee ki laa be nbinben man kaa jɔɔ tinyɔɔri.

eng: Everyone with me greets you; greet those who love us faithfully. Grace be with all of you.
local: Ban biɛn be n ban nin poor ni. Poor ban ban ti, bi yada bu-n na. Linyunyunbasanl li be ni biɛn bu.

eng: Im giving you this commission in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is coming to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearance and his kingdom.
local: Uwonbɔr a nintogil nin Masiya wan na yin ji ban fo nin ban kpo a bɔr, wee ti lig waah na yin degirin nin u naamin, le n kpaaniŋ nan:.

eng: Preach the word. Be ready to do it whether it is convenient or inconvenient. Correct, confront, and encourage with patience and instruction.
local: Li mɔɔn tibɔr nmɔn na; ŋaam sili iyoo biɛn, ki li tenn bi ikpaan ki gbeer bi, ki pɔɔgir bi gbanann ki li jɔɔ limɔril nin maaninba a wɔɔnim.

eng: There will come a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. They will collect teachers who say what they want to hear because they are self-centered.
local: Iyoo chaan le bininfob kaa ji na yin pelig ibamɔn a wɔɔmi. Amaa le ki nɔɔ bi sufu a bam, bi na li lagin ban wɔɔnn takpem ke bin pag bi tuli kaah saag ki ban bin gbir nan ni.

eng: They will turn their back on the truth and turn to myths.
local: Bi na yin ŋal bi tuli le ki yii ibamɔn a gbirim, amaa le ki sin pelig nwɔɔm man kaa ye ibamɔn nin.

eng: But you must keep control of yourself in all circumstances. Endure suffering, do the work of a preacher of the good news, and carry out your service fully.
local: Amaa fin ma, li ŋmag a ba n-yonn biɛn, ki li pɔɔn a ba a fala a jimin; ki wɔɔn bininfob tibɔŋann nan, ki li tun litoonl lan wee Uwonbɔr ta seiŋin biɛn na.

eng: Im already being poured out like a sacrifice to God, and the time of my death is near.
local: Bi pii kpaarim wur le ke kumanug a daamin, le isaŋ foou nin n fɔr dulinya nan yaan na.

eng: I have fought the good fight, finished the race, and kept the faith.
local: N kɔn likɔnŋanla, n diɛ n taguunla, ki sin ŋmag n yada.

eng: At last the champions wreath that is awarded for righteousness is waiting for me. The Lord, who is the righteous judge, is going to give it to me on that day. Hes giving it not only to me but also to all those who have set their heart on waiting for his appearance.
local: Daandaan nan n buchin yuchigirig ŋa sili ki dɔ guum, kan wee Ubuchin yudaan wan ye ubɔjirin na yin ta ki yɔm lidaal nmɔn, naa yin ye n yaa binba, amaa u na li yɔ ban guu waah na yin degirin lidaal lan nin tɔɔl.

eng: Do your best to come to me quickly.
local: Pɔɔn a ba wee ki dan n ban weem ni.

eng: Demas has fallen in love with the present world and has deserted me and has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
local: Nan bu-n, Demasi kaah ban dulinya nan nbu che u jiɛnim le ki jon Tesalonika. Kiresensi mu jon Galantiya, le Tatusi mu jon Dalimasiya.

eng: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark, and bring him with you. He has been a big help to me in the ministry.
local: Lug a binba le be n ban. Yin Mag wee fin nin uma nnɔɔ dan, nan bu-n u terim n toon yin pam.

eng: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
local: N tun Tachikusi Efesusi a tiŋin na.

eng: When you come, bring along the coat I left with Carpus in Troas. Also bring the scrolls and especially the parchments.
local: A chaan ke a ta n war a daala nni, kan wee n nan ta jiɛn Karipusi ban Tirosi a tiŋin ni, nin n gbann sɔb kaarin nan kpaa talar nin n gbann tan kaa la sɔbin.

eng: Alexander, the craftsman who works with metal, has really hurt me. The Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
local: Alɛgzanda wan ye ucha-n ŋaam nan kaa ŋan na. Uyudaan li yɔ u ki jiin waah tun nan nmɔn bu.

eng: But watch out for him, because he opposes our teaching.
local: A mu li nyi a ba bu ki ta jiin u paam, nan bu-n u yii ti bɔrin na.

eng: No one took my side at my first court hearing. Everyone deserted me. I hope that God doesnt hold it against them!.
local: Chinchinn maah nan kɔn n ba bu-n, u ba kaa nan kpiɛ n bu, amaa bininfob biɛn nan yiim le. Uwonbɔr n laa dar bi tuli.

eng: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that the entire message would be preached through me and so all the nations could hear it. I was also rescued from the lions mouth!.
local: Amaa le Uyudaan sir n bɔɔn ki seim npɔɔn, wee min bu U bɔrin n fir mɔɔn nbamɔm wee ban kaa ye Juu yaabin ngbir ti. Le u nan nyam ugbegin a mɔn.

eng: The Lord will rescue me from every evil action and will save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Uyudaan li nyam niwanbirilin kaah li legim nan yaan biɛn, wee ki nan taam kun U paab a naamin yaan soon. Uma ye npagim nin isaŋ yan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi. Ibamɔn.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila and the household of Onesiphorus.
local: Poor Pirisila nin Akula nin Onesiforusi a niin yaaba.

eng: Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus in Miletus because of his illness.
local: Erasitusi nan paar Korenti a tiŋin na, le Tirofimusi mu buŋ le n jiɛn u Miletusi a tiŋin.

eng: Try hard to come to me before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters say hello.
local: Pɔɔn a ba ki dan wee kutafaag a yonn n nin foon. Ebulusi, nin Pudensi nin Linusin nin Kiladiya nin bi nabumin biɛn poorin.

eng: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.
local: Uyudaan li be nin a naaŋ. Linyunyunbasanl li be a bu.

eng: From Paul, a slave of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. Im sent to bring about the faith of Gods chosen people and a knowledge of the truth that agrees with godliness.
local: Min Pɔɔl wan ye Uwonbɔr a yomu nin Yisa Masiya a toomin, le Uwonbɔr tum u ninfo gannkaab ban, ke n ter bi bi yada-n wee bin biɛ ibamɔn a bɔr tan wɔɔn nwonjiɛn binben nin.

eng: Their faith and this knowledge are based on the hope of eternal life that God, who doesnt lie, promised before time began.
local: yada nmɔn nin nbiɛmin tɔɔ tama kan wee bi jɔɔ ke bi na li kan limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmin bu le, ke Uwonbɔr, wan kaa por tibɔkarin, kaah nan siin limɔsiinl ke u na li sei ti limiɛfol nmɔn n-yonn man wee dulinya kaa la nan piin nin.

eng: God revealed his message at the appropriate time through preaching, and I was trusted with preaching this message by the command of God our savior.
local: le iyoo kaah nan nan foo-n le u nyan tibɔr nmɔn nween ni ke Uma Uwonbɔr wan ye ti feeru win kaah wɔɔnim ke n mɔɔn ti man nin.

eng: To Titus, my true child in a common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our savior.
local: N sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tenn fin Tatusi wan ye n big bamɔŋin, nin taah lagin ki jɔɔ yada kiba kan nin bu. Ti ba Uwonbɔr nin Masiya wan ye ti feeru win a nyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be niŋ.

eng: The reason I left you behind in Crete was to organize whatever needs to be done and to appoint elders in each city, as I told you.
local: Nan bu wee n nan jiɛn niŋ Kereti a tiŋin nin nan ye nan le, n nan ban a gbaar itoon yan nan gur kaa diɛ n le ki sin gann bininkpeeb ntim biɛn nin ke maah nan wɔɔniŋ man nin.

eng: Elders should be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, and have faithful children who cant be accused of self-indulgence or rebelliousness.
local: Naa ŋan nin uninkpel li jɔɔ igalin ni, u li ye wan jɔɔ upii uba kpeŋileŋ, le u buwaab mu li ye ban jɔɔ yada, ki laa ye tukangbirdami.

eng: This is because supervisors should be without fault as Gods managers: they shouldnt be stubborn, irritable, addicted to alcohol, a bully, or greedy.
local: Nibu, uwonjiɛn sanliir kaah ŋmag Uwonbɔr a toon yin ni, naa ŋan nin u li jɔɔ igalin bee ki mugir u tabi, ki laa ye wan a ŋuul fii yɔli yɔli, ki laa ye udanyugbeeri, ki laa ye ukɔnban bee ki laa ban tinyɔɔr tan kaa jɔɔ jilima.

eng: Instead, they should show hospitality, love what is good, and be reasonable, ethical, godly, and self-controlled.
local: Amaa ni kpaa talar le nin u li gaan bicham, ki li ye wan ban nan ŋan, ki li ye wan fir chor u ba, ki li ye wan jɔɔ lasaab, ki li be kasi nin uba a ŋmagimin.

eng: They must pay attention to the reliable message as it has been taught to them so that they can encourage people with healthy instruction and refute those who speak against it.
local: Ki li ŋmag ibamɔn a bɔr tan wee bi nan wɔɔn win kenken, wee ki fir pɔɔg u kpiɛtiib a gbanann nin ibamɔn a wɔɔmin, wee ki fir nyaŋ ban biɛn kaa cheel tibɔŋann nmɔn nin.

eng: In fact, there are many who are rebellious people, loudmouths, and deceivers, especially some of those who are Jewish believers.
local: Nan bu-n, limɔyiil a ninfob yaba, ban pag yɔli yɔli nin kularug a ninfob, nin ban jann nɔɔ lipunjiil a kaalin.

eng: They must be silenced because they upset entire households. They teach what they shouldnt to make money dishonestly.
local: Ni kpaa talar le nin bin ŋmin, nan bu-n bi choon kɔɔ bininfob biɛn a niin yin le ki wɔɔn nwɔɔm man kaa kpaan nin bi li wɔɔn ni, bi wɔɔn man ki ban tinyɔɔr tan kaa jɔɔ jilima le.

eng: Someone who is one of their own prophets said, People from Crete are always liars, wild animals, and lazy gluttons.
local: Hali bi yaa linlenn uba len ke, Kereti yaab ye tibɔkardam le iyoo biɛn, bi nann tiwankor le, ki ye bigbanyagdam nin bijijib.

eng: This statement is true. Because of this, correct them firmly, so that they can be healthy in their faith.
local: Siɛra nan ye ibamɔn le. Ni ma bu fuun bi bu choba, wee nin che bin mann sir bi yada-n nbamɔm.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to Jewish myths and commands from people who reject the truth.
local: ki laa pelig Juu yaab a wɔɔnim man kaa ye ibamɔn nin bee bininfob ban yii ibamɔn a siimin ni.

eng: Everything is clean to those who are clean, but nothing is clean to those who are corrupt and without faith. Instead, their mind and conscience are corrupted.
local: Bininfob ban be kasi-n, bi wan biɛn be le kasi, amaa bininfob ban kaa be kasi wee kaa jɔɔ yada-n ma ban, niba kaa be ki be kasi. Nan bu-n bi sufu nin bi potiɛri biɛn ŋmarin na.

eng: They claim to know God, but they deny God by the things that they do. They are detestable, disobedient, and disqualified to do anything good.
local: Bi len ke bi nyi Uwonbɔr, amaa le bi toon yin wɔɔn ke bi yii wa. Uwonbɔr nan bi binbem nmɔn. Bi ye tukangbirdam le kaa li fir tun litoon ŋanli.

eng: This is why its necessary for us to pay more attention to what we have heard, or else we may drift away from it.
local: Mambu ni kpaa talar le nin ti li ŋmag ibamɔn yan wee ti nan gbirin, wee niba laa ta ti ki nya ibamɔn nmɔn yaan ni.

eng: If the message that was spoken by angels was reliable, and every offense and act of disobedience received an appropriate consequence.
local: Nan bu-n Malakam kaah nan pag tibɔr tan sei ti yaajamin nan wɔɔn ke ni ye ibamɔn le, le ban nan yii tibɔr nmɔn nan kan litudaril lan kpaan bi ke.

eng: how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? It was first announced through the Lord, and then it was confirmed by those who heard him.
local: ti yin ŋa nii wee ki ŋmara, ni ye ti yii nŋmarim man polig nan ke naa? Nŋmarim man wee Uyudaan nan chinn pag mi bɔrin, le ban nan gbir U bɔr nmɔn jin ti siɛra ti ban.

eng: God also vouched for their message with signs, amazing things, various miracles, and gifts from the Holy Spirit, which were handed out the way he wanted.
local: Uwonbɔr tɔɔl nan jin mi siɛra nin ligaril a siɛra nin lijinjeel a toon nin ligaril a toon balbal, nin waah nan ta U kasi a Naaŋ a piini ki tol bi waah ban man.

eng: God didnt put the world that is coming under the angels control.
local: Uwonbɔr kaa len ke malakam le ye dulinya kan wee ti pag nan a yudaau wi.

eng: Instead, someone declared somewhere, What is humanity that you think about them? Or what are human beings that you care about them? .
local: Amaa uba mu nan jin siɛra Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Uninfo ye baa wee a potiɛl be u ni, Uninfosaal a bija wan wee a baga be u ni-n naa?.

eng: For a while you made them lower than angels. You crowned human beings with glory and honor. .
local: A nan ŋa u le ki che u jilima waar kaa foo malakam yaali; le a sei u jilima nin kupag a yuchigirig.

eng: You put everything under their control. When he puts everything under their control, he doesnt leave anything out of control. But right now, we dont see everything under their control yet.
local: ki ta niwann biɛn ki ŋa u nɔɔn. Uwonbɔr kaah ta niwann biɛn ki ŋa Yisa a nɔɔn nin, Uwonbɔr kaa nan che niba gur kaa ye u yaan ni. Amaa daandaan nan taa kan waah ye niwann biɛn man ni.

eng: However, we do see the one who was made lower in order than the angels for a little while—its Jesus! Hes the one who is now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of his death. He suffered death so that he could taste death for everyone through Gods grace.
local: Amaa ti kan Yisa, wan wee Uwonbɔr nan jiin u tum wama malakam ban nin, daandaan nan, u chig kupag nin jilima a yuchigirig le waah jin nkum a fala-n bu, wee Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl bu u nan che u kpo uninfo biɛn bu-n.

eng: It was appropriate for God, for whom and through whom everything exists, to use experiences of suffering to make perfect the pioneer of salvation. This salvation belongs to many sons and daughters whom hes leading to glory.
local: Waah yin ta nbuwaab takpem ki kɔɔn kupagin nin, ni nan kpaan ke Uwonbɔr wan nan naan niwann biɛn, nin wan bu wee niwann biɛn be-n, Un ŋa bi feegab a daau kasi u fala a jim bu.

eng: This is because the one who makes people holy and the people who are being made holy all come from one source. That is why Jesus isnt ashamed to call them brothers and sisters when he says.
local: Yisa le che bininfob be kasi, le kasi dam nmɔn nin Yisa ye iniin yaab. Ni bu ifa kaa jɔɔ Yisa nin un yin bi ke u nabumi.

eng: I will publicly announce your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you in the middle of the assembly. .
local: U len ke, N li mɔɔn a yinl ki sei n nabum; biwonjiɛm a nintogil n li gaah ilan ki don a yinl.

eng: He also says, I will rely on him. And also, Here I am with the children whom God has given to me. .
local: Le ki ji len ke, N li gaa Uwonbɔr ki ji. Le ki ji nan len ke, Min sɔ do, nin n buwaab ban wee Uwonbɔr ta seimin.

eng: Therefore, since the children share in flesh and blood, he also shared the same things in the same way. He did this to destroy the one who holds the power over death—the devil—by dying.
local: Baah ye nbuwaab ki jɔɔ tigbanann nin nchamin, ni bu le Yisa mu nan kpann uninfo ke uma nin bininfob li jɔɔ tigbanann tiba, wee u kumin ke u nan bii Sintani wan ŋmag nkum a pɔɔmin.

eng: He set free those who were held in slavery their entire lives by their fear of death.
local: wee ki fee ban a binbem biɛn ŋa bi tiyomir nkum bu dulinya nan yaan nin ki gab.

eng: Of course, he isnt trying to help angels, but rather hes helping Abrahams descendants.
local: Ni ye ibamɔn le, naa ye malakam le U teri, amaa Abiraham a yaabili le U ter.

eng: Therefore, he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every way. This was so that he could become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, in order to wipe away the sins of the people.
local: Ni nan bu ni nan kpaa talar le u li be ke u nabum kaah be man nin niwann biɛn nin, wee ki fir kpann biminmanib a yudaau wan yab ki si nba bu nin wan ye unyunyunbasann u toon yin ki sei Uwonbɔr, ki ta uba ki mann tibir a manug ki sei bininfob.

eng: Hes able to help those who are being tempted, since he himself experienced suffering when he was tempted.
local: Yisa tɔɔl kaah nan kan nbiginim ki jin fala nbu, u li fir ter ban wee nbiginim kan bin.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: Mambu, n nabum ban be kasi nan, wee ki kpiɛ Uwonbɔr a yimin yaan nin, ni li tiɛr Yisa buu, wan ye ntoon nin biminmanib a yudaau wan wee ti ŋa u yada tiwonjiɛn samin nin.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: U nan si nba bu le ki sei wan gann win, a li ke Moziz kaah nan si nba bu u toon yin ki sei Uwonbɔr a ninfob biɛn man nin.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: Yisa le ye wan kpaan jilima ki jon Moziz, a li ke uniinmaar kaah jɔɔ jilima ki jon iniin nin tɔɔl man nin.

eng: Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
local: Iniin biɛn jɔɔ wan maa i le, amaa Uwonbɔr le ye wan maa niwann biɛn.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: Moziz nan si nba bu le Uwonbɔr a ninfob biɛn ban waah ye utoontunn ki be Uwonbɔr a niin nin ki ji siɛra, ki wɔɔn bi Uwonbɔr kaah yin len man n-yonn man la chaan.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: Amaa Masiya ma si nba bu le Uwonbɔr a ninfob biɛn bu ki ye U bija, tim le ye Uwonbɔr a niin yaab, ni ye ti li jɔɔ lipoobil le nin tama kan wee ti far ligbingbanl ki bu-n.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: Ke Kasi a Naaŋ kaah len ke: Dinan, ni ye ni gbir U kukɔl ke.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: ni laa ta niba ŋa tukangbirdami, wee ki kɔnn bininkpeeb ke ni yaajam kaah nan mag u n-yonn man wee bi nan be kukunkɔɔg a paabin ni.

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: nan yaan wee ni yaajam nan magim ki tan kpaa ibin pina, wee bi num kan nan wee N tun ni.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: Mambu le N ŋuul nan fii bi nɔɔm nmɔn bu, le N len ke, Bi sufu ŋmarin le iyoo biɛn, le baa nyi N san yi.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: Le N nan poo tipoor N ŋuufiiŋin bu ki ke, Baa li kan lifooril N ban ni.

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: N nabum, ni li nyi wee ni ni uba laa jɔɔ sufu birug kan li che un fɔɔr ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr wan ye limiɛfol daan nin ban ni.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: Amaa ni li pɔɔgir tab a sufu idaa biɛn na, taah la be Dinan nin, wee ni ni uba laa ye tukangbirdaan tibir a larug bu.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Nan bu-n ni ye ti ŋmag ti chinchinn a dindann nbamɔm ki tan kpaa ti miɛfol a joorim ke, tim nin Masiya na yin ye nba le.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: A li ke maah pii bi len ke, Dinan, ni ye ni gbir U kukɔl ke, ni laa ta niba ŋa tukangbirdami, ke ban kɔnn nin bininkpeeb kaah nan ŋa man ki yii Uwonbɔr nni.

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Ŋmam le nan gbir Uwonbɔr a kukɔl, wee ki yii nnaa? Naa ye ban biɛn wee Moziz nan nyan bi Ijibir nnaa?.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Ŋmam bu wee Uwonbɔr a ŋuul nan fii bi bu ibin pinaa? Naa ye ban tun tibir nbu, ban a gbanann nan paar kukunkɔɔg bu nnaa?.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Ke ŋmam wee U nan poo tipoor ke baa li kan lifooril U ban nin na, wee naa ye ban yii U mɔb nnaa?.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: Ni bu, ti kan ke baa nan fir kɔɔ lifooril nmɔn yaan ni, nan bu-n bi kaa jɔɔ yada-n bu.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Mambu, Uwonbɔr kaah siin limɔsiinl ke U li sei ti lifooril lan nin kaah la si nbu, ni che ti choo tiba wee uba laa nan lann lifooril nmɔn ni.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Ti mu gbir tibɔŋann nmɔn ke baah nan gbir ti man nin le; amaa baah nan gbir tibɔŋann nmɔn taa nan jɔɔ tinyɔɔr ki sei bi, nan bu-n baa nan ta tibɔŋann nmɔn ki kpiɛ bi yada ni.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: Daandaan nan, tim ban ŋa yada nan kan lifooril nmɔn na, a li ke Uwonbɔr kaah len ke, Mambu le N poo bimɔb N ŋuul kaah fii-n bu ki ke, Baa li kan lifoorili. Nin ni ma biɛn na U pii nan diɛ U toon yin dulinya nan a piinimin le.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: Ni ma bu, niba yaan U pag ki jiin iweehn ilile nmɔn paam ke, Iweehn ilile daal le Uwonbɔr nan nan diɛ U toon biɛn le ki foor.

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: Le Uwonbɔr ji nan len nkarim nmɔn yaan ke, Baa li kan lifooril N ban ni.

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Ni le la biɛn nin ye man le ke biba na li kɔɔ lifooril nmɔn yaan, amaa ban nan chinn ki gbir tibɔŋann nmɔn nin kaa nan kɔɔ lifooril nmɔn yaan ni, nan bu-n bi mɔyiil nbu.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: Ni ma bu, le Uwonbɔr ji siin lidaal liba, ki yi lidaal nmɔn ke dinan, ni bɔɔn bu ni nan yuura le u ni nan che Defid pag lidaal nmɔn a bɔr, a li ke naah pii nan len man Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Dinan, ni ye ni gbir U kukɔl ke, ni laa ŋa tukangbirdami.

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Nan bu-n ni ye Josuwa nan bi sei bi lifooril ke, Uwonbɔr kaa ji nan bi li pag lifooril daal a bɔr ni bɔɔn bu.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: Mambu lifooril daal le la si le ki sei Uwonbɔr a ninfob;.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Wan kɔɔ Uwonbɔr a foorilin ke, udaan tɔɔl kan u tɔɔl a lifooril la, a li ke Uwonbɔr kaah nan foor man ni.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: Ni ma bu, ni che ti pɔɔn tiba wee ki kɔɔ lifooril nmɔn yaan, wee ti ni uba laa nɔɔ limɔyiil a sam ki nan lu.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Uwonbɔr a mɔbonl fo le ki tun itoon. Ki ka ki jon ijumɔɔŋ ilee, ki fin jiil uninfosaal ki piir u naaŋ nin u miɛfol, ki kɔɔ for u tugiri nin u kpabi a nsugirim, tibɔr nmɔn le ji feen ki biɛr usaal a sufu a binbem, nin u potiɛl kaah be man.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah naan nan biɛn niba kaa be wee ki woo wee waa ga ni. Niba kaa degi, niwann biɛn dɔ npaan ni le Uwonbɔr a numin, wan wee ti biɛn na yin kpiir ta tun nan ki wɔɔni.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Mambu taah jɔɔ biminmanib a yudaau wan ye Uwonbɔr a Bija Yisa wan nan don paabin, ni che ti li ŋmag ti yada kan ti ŋa nan kenken na.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Taa jɔɔ biminmanib a yudaau wan kaa li fir jogir ti, ti daburigin ni, amaa ti jɔɔ wan nan kan nbiginim niwann biɛn nin, a li ke taah be man nin, amaa waa nan jɔɔ tibiri.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Ni bu ni che ti fɔɔr mal Uwonbɔr wan ye unyunyunbasann nin a naangbɔŋin ban nin tidindann, wee ti kan linyunyunbasanl n-yonn man wee ti ban.

eng: From Paul, who is a prisoner for the cause of Christ Jesus, and our brother Timothy. To Philemon our dearly loved coworker.
local: Min Pɔɔl, wan ye uloondiikɔɔr Masiya bu-n, nin ti na a big Timoti le sɔb kugbɔŋ nan, Ki tenn ti jɔ kpaarimɔɔn, Filemɔɔn wan ye ti toontunn kpiɛgin.

eng: Apphia our sister, Archippus our fellow soldier, and the church that meets in your house.
local: nin ti ninsa Apifiɛ, nin Achipusi wan ye ti tɔtogkpiɛg nin biwonjiɛm a laguŋ ban lagin a niin nin:.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Ti Baa Uwonbɔr nin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be ni bu.

eng: Philemon, I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: Iyoo biɛn n le poor n Wonbɔru le ki tiɛr a bu n wonmiɛlin.

eng: because Ive heard of your love and faithfulness, which you have both for the Lord Jesus and for all Gods people.
local: nan bu-n n gbir ŋaah ŋa Uyudaan Yisa yada wee ki jiɛ Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab biɛn man na.

eng: I pray that your partnership in the faith might become effective by an understanding of all that is good among us in Christ.
local: N miɛ Uwonbɔr le ke a li tun nin linunmanl ki li por yada kan wee a jɔɔ-n, wee nin che a biɛ nbamɔm niwann ŋan nan biɛn wee ti jɔɔ Masiya ni-n.

eng: I have great joy and encouragement because of your love, since the hearts of Gods people are refreshed by your actions, my brother.
local: A jiɛm nmɔn seim nmɔn le takpem nin ngbananpɔɔn, nan bu-n fin n nabig, a pɔŋ Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab a sufuu.

eng: Therefore, though I have enough confidence in Christ to command you to do the right thing.
local: Mambu, hali maah bi ye n li jɔɔ lipoobil Masiya bu ki li fir wɔɔniŋ ŋaah bi ye a tun nan nin.

eng: I would rather appeal to you through love. I, Paul—an old man, and now also a prisoner for Christ Jesus.
local: amaa n baliŋ le njiɛm bu. Min Pɔɔl, wan ye uninkpel wee daandaan nan ki ye uloondiikɔɔr Yisa Masiya bu-n.

eng: appeal to you for my child Onesimus. I became his father in the faith during my time in prison.
local: n baliŋ le, n bija Onesemosi bu, wan kpann n bija, n-yonn man wee bi nan lolim tikulorin.

eng: He was useless to you before, but now he is useful to both of us.
local: N-yonn nba waa nan jɔɔ tinyɔɔr a ban ni, amaa daandaan nan u jɔɔ tinyɔɔr ki sei min nin fin biɛn.

eng: Im sending him back to you, which is like sending you my own heart.
local: Uma wan ye n poobil a ninfou win le n jɔɔ u jiin a ban.

eng: I considered keeping him with me so that he might serve me in your place during my time in prison because of the gospel.
local: Baah lolim tikulorin tibɔŋann nin a mɔɔnim bu, n bi li ban u li be n ban do ki si a taalin ki terim.

eng: However, I didnt want to do anything without your consent so that your act of kindness would occur willingly and not under pressure.
local: Amaa maa ban n ŋa niba wee ŋaa nyi man bu, wee a yin ter nan ke ni laa ye limugili, amaa wee ni li ye a tɔɔl a bam.

eng: Maybe this is the reason that Onesimus was separated from you for a while so that you might have him back forever.
local: Niba ke waah nan fɔr a ban wama-n, nan ye le ke u nan jirin ke u li be a ban isaŋ biɛn.

eng: no longer as a slave but more than a slave—that is, as a dearly loved brother. He is especially a dearly loved brother to me. How much more can he become a brother to you, personally and spiritually in the Lord!.
local: kaa ji ye uyomi, amaa wan jon uyom, ki ye kinabig jiɛkaag. N jiɛ u kaga amaa fin le jann jiɛ u ki jom, waah ye uninfosaal ki ye kinabig Uyudaan ni-n bu.

eng: In the past, God spoke through the prophets to our ancestors in many times and many ways.
local: Yaayoo nni, Uwonbɔr nan taar isaŋ takpem nin isan balbal le ki che bilinlem pag tenn ti yaajam.

eng: So, if you really consider me a partner, welcome Onesimus as if you were welcoming me.
local: Ni ma bu ni ye a nyi ke n ye a toontunn kpiɛg ke, gaa mu ke ŋaah bi li gaam man nin.

eng: In these final days, though, he spoke to us through a Son. God made his Son the heir of everything and created the world through him.
local: amaa idaa yan joor nan, u che U bija le pag sei ti, wan wee U ŋau niwann biɛn a faajiru win, nin wan bu wee U nan naan dulinya nan nin.

eng: If he has harmed you in any way or owes you money, charge it to my account.
local: Ni ye u nan galiŋ bee u jin a pɔnl le ke, che ni li nɔɔ min na.

eng: The Son is the light of Gods glory and the imprint of Gods being. He maintains everything with his powerful message. After he carried out the cleansing of people from their sins, he sat down at the right side of the highest majesty.
local: Uwonbɔr a pag le che U bija-n nyagin, ki jɔɔ Uwonbɔr a nunaŋ, ki ji ye wan ŋmag niwann biɛn u mɔleen a pɔɔm bu. Ni bɔɔn bu waah nan ban tibir a ŋunnim ki sei ti-n, le u kal Jilimadaan Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam papaab.

eng: I, Paul, will pay it back to you . Of course, I wont mention that you owe me your life.
local: Min Pɔɔl, tɔɔl a nɔɔ le n ta sɔb nan. N li yɔ, kaa yin tiɛr ke min le ye fin tɔɔl gbaa.

eng: And the Son became so much greater than the other messengers, such as angels, that he received a more important title than theirs.
local: Le Masiya yab jon malakam biɛn, ke liyinl lan wee Uwonbɔr ta sei win kaah yab jon malakam a yin man nin.

eng: Yes, brother, I want this favor from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
local: N na big, n bi ban ke n kan tinyɔɔr tiba le a ban ti yudaau bu; pɔŋ n sufuu Masiya ni.

eng: After all, when did God ever say to any of the angels: You are my Son. Today I have become your Father? Or, even, I will be his Father, and he will be my Son? .
local: Malaka u la ba wee Uwonbɔr la nyi len ke, Fin le ye N bijaa; dinan a daal N ŋa a Baa? Bee, N li ŋa u Baa, wee u mu n ŋa n bijaa?.

eng: Im writing to you, confident of your obedience and knowing that you will do more than what I ask.
local: N jɔɔ tidindann a mɔcheel lan wee a jɔɔ-n bu, ni bu le n sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tenniŋ, ki nyi ke a pii li tun ke nin jo nan wee n ke a ŋaa-n.

eng: But then, when he brought his firstborn into the world, he said, All of Gods angels must worship him. .
local: Uwonbɔr kaah nan ta U bininkpeegin ki dan dulinya nan yaan nin, U ji nan len ke, Che Uwonbɔr a malakam biɛn n jiɛn u.

eng: Also, one more thing—prepare a guest room for me. I hope that I will be released from prison to be with you because of your prayers.
local: Nan kpiɛ: Mann kuchandiig ki guun ma, nan bu-n n jɔɔ tama ke Uwonbɔr li taam ki jiin nin ni ban, ke naah miɛ Uwonbɔr man nin.

eng: He talks about the angels: Hes the one who uses the spirits for his messengers and who uses flames of fire as ministers. .
local: U nan len ke malakamin paam ke, U ŋa U malakam le bi be ke ntafaamin, Le u toontum mu be ke nŋmipeen nin.

eng: Epaphras, who is in prison with me for the cause of Christ Jesus, greets you.
local: Epafarasi, wan ye n loondiig kɔɔ kpiɛg Masiya bu-n pooriŋ.

eng: But he says to his Son, God, your throne is forever and your kingdoms scepter is a rod of justice. .
local: Amaa U bija-n ma paam U nan len ke, A naangbɔŋin, Uwonbɔr aa, a yin gbaa be le hali nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi, le a buchin li ye a naam a jaangbiɛg.

eng: as well as my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
local: Le Mag, nin Arisitakusi, nin Demasi, nin Lug, ban ye n toontunn kpiɛtiibin mu poorin.

eng: You loved righteousness and hated lawless behavior. That is why God, your God, has anointed you more than your companions with the oil of joy. .
local: Ŋaah ban nbuchin binben ki nan npooton ni; mambu Uwonbɔr, wan ye a weemin, nan kpaar nmɔn a kpam ki jaa a bu ki seiŋ tininkper tan yab jon a kpiɛtiib a ninkper.

eng: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
local: Ti yudaau Yisa Masiya a nyunyunbasanl li be nin ni naam.

eng: And he says, You, Lord, laid the earths foundations in the beginning, and the heavens are made by your hands. .
local: Uwonbɔr ji len ke, Npiinimin, Uyudaan aa fin le nan fiɛ dulinya nan a fiɛl, le paab mu ye a nɔɔi kaah tun nan.

eng: They will pass away, but you remain. They will all wear out like old clothes. .
local: Bi biɛn na li kpo, amaa fin le yin gbaa be le; bi na li char ke tiwanyee wan kaah char man nin.

eng: You will fold them up like a coat. They will be changed like a person changes clothes, but you stay the same, and the years of your life wont come to an end. .
local: A na li kpab bi ke likinkenlin; bi biɛn na li kpenn ke likinkenlin. Amaa fin kaa yin kpenn ni, le a bi-n mu kaa jɔɔ diɛmi.

eng: When has he ever said to any of the angels, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet like a footstool? .
local: Malaka u la ba wee Uwonbɔr la nyi ki bei u ke, Kalim n nɔɔjiim paam, hali nin maah na li che, a dataam n kpann a taai a tɔɔnbol laa?.

eng: Arent all the angels ministering spirits who are sent to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?.
local: Naa ye malakam biɛn ye nnaam ban tun itoon le ki tenn ban biɛn yin kan nŋmarimin naa?.

eng: Every high priest is taken from the people and put in charge of things that relate to God for their sake, in order to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
local: Bi gann biminmanib a yudaau biɛn bininfob a sisiigin le, ki ta siin u kpiɛtiib a taai Uwonbɔr ban, ke u jɔɔ ipiin nin bi bir a manug ki tenn Uwonbɔr.

eng: The high priest is able to deal gently with the ignorant and those who are misled since he himself is prone to weakness.
local: U li fir ŋmag ban kaa nyi-n, nin ban borin soon, nan bu-n u tɔɔl ye wan jɔɔ idaburig le.

eng: Because of his weakness, he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the people.
local: Ni ma bu le ni kpaa talar nin un mann u tɔɔl a bir a manug, nin bininfobin a bir a manug.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Uba kaa li fir ta biminmanib a jilima nmɔn ki sei ubaantɔɔli; amaa see ke Uwonbɔr yin udaan, a li ke waah nan yin Arɔnn man nin.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Masiya mu kaa nan taa kupag nmɔn ubaantɔɔl a bam bu ke un ŋa biminmanib a yudaau wi. Amaa Uwonbɔr le nan bei u ke, A ye N Bija le; dinan le N kpann a Baa.

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Le ki ji len niba yaan ke, A ye uminmann le wan a toon yin be ke Melikizɛdɛg a toon kaah nan ye man nin hali nin n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ diɛm.

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Yisa kaah nan be dulinya nan yaan n-yonn man nin, u nan miɛ Uwonbɔr le ki bei U waah ban nan, ki mɔr nkukɔpɔɔn nin tinyinyinn ki ŋaan U, Uwonbɔr wan li fir fee u nkumin nin, waah nan jɔɔ jiininba ki tenn Uwonbɔr nin limɔcheelin bu le U nan gbir u miɛm.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: Hali nin waah le nan ye Uwonbɔr a Big nmɔn biɛn ni u nan tinn limɔcheel le u fala a jimin.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: waah nan nan ŋa nan kpaan ki diɛ-n, le u nin ye feegabdaan ki sei ban biɛn chee u mɔbin.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: le Uwonbɔr gann u ki ŋa u biminmanib a yudaau wan a toon be ke Melikizɛdɛg a toon kaah nan be man nin.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Ti jɔɔ tibɔr takpem ki bi li pag ki jiin tibɔr nan paam, amaa le ni pɔɔ nin ti mann wɔɔn ni taai nan bu-n naa tinn ki biɛr weemi.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: Ibamɔn buu n-yonn nan, ni kpaan ni li ye ban wɔɔnn le, amaa le ni ji ban uba n wɔɔn ni Uwonbɔr a bɔr a piinimin a bamɔn. Ni ban nbiim le, naa kpaan nin tijiir tan kpiɛgi!.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Wan la mɔɔ nbiim ke, udaan la ye kiwatirig le, wan a lam kaa la gɔnn ni, nin tibuchinn a wɔɔmin paami.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: Amaa ban chegir le kpaan nin tijiir tan kpiɛg, bi ma ye ban nan gbaa tinn ki biɛ nan ŋan nin nan bir a bɔgirim.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: Mambu ni che ti jiɛn Masiya a bɔr tan ye npiinim a wɔɔnimin, wee ki ta nninkpelam ki wɔɔn ki jo nintogil, naa ji kpaa talar nin ti ji mann bil lifiɛl a bɔr tan wɔɔn ke ni jiɛn titoonbir tan ye nkum a samin a nɔɔma, amaa wee ki ŋa Uwonbɔr yada.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: nin Uwonbɔr a nyunful a wɔɔnim, nin inɔɔ a paanim bininfob bu, nin n kum a fiim, nin litudaril lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: Ni ye Uwonbɔr chee ke, ti li ŋa man.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ni li pɔɔ ki sei bininfob ban wee Uwonbɔr wonn bi numin, ban nyi len paab a piini wee ki nan por kasi a Naaŋ a piini ki sei tabin.

eng: and tasted Gods good word and the powers of the coming age.
local: Ni li pɔɔ ki sei ban len Uwonbɔr a bɔr a ŋam nin n-yonn man chaan nin a bɔr.

eng: They are crucifying Gods Son all over again and exposing him to public shame.
local: ni ye bi lu bi yada ni ke, baa ji li fir kan nsan man wee bi li nɔɔ ki kpenn bi binbemi. Nan bu-n bi lum nmɔn bu bi ji jiin Uwonbɔr a Bija-n le ki kpaag lidapunpunl bu ki jinn u ifa npaan ni.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Nkpatam man nyu utaal a nyum man lur mi bu-n wee ki ŋaan tijiir ki tenn ban ko nan, Uwonbɔr le ŋaan tinyɔɔr ŋaan nkpatam nmɔn bu.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: Amaa nkpatam man wee ikunkon nin ijakpiɛgar pa-n mi dɔ le yɔli, limɔpool a daŋ li be nkpatam nmɔn bu. Le njoorimin nŋmi n nan gaa npaan nin nmɔn.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: N nabum hali nin taah le pag man nan biɛn na, ni biɛn yɔli ti jɔɔ tidindann, ti nyi ke ni jɔɔ tiwann ŋann tan nyan ni feegabin.

eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Uwonbɔr ye wan ŋan le, Waa li tam ni toon nin naah jiɛ U man ki ter U ninfob ki la biɛ nin ter bin bu.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Ti ban ni biɛn li jɔɔ inunman le hali nin ndiɛm, wee nin che naah jɔɔ tama nan bu-n nin sin ŋa ibamɔn.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: Taa ban ni ŋa bigbanyagdami, amaa ni toog ban nan ŋa yada wee ki jɔɔ limɔrilin na, wee nin che ni kan lifaal lan wee Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinlin.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah nan yin siin limɔsiinl ki sei Abiraham, wee uba kaa nan be ki jon U wee U li poo udaan a yinl bu-n, U nan jir poo Ubaantɔɔl a yinl bu le.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: Ki len ke, Ibamɔn, N sin li kaab tikaar ki jaa a bu, ki seiŋ iyaabil takpem.

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Abiraham kaah nan jɔɔ limɔril ki guurin, ni bɔɔn bu le u nan kan nan wee Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinlin.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Bininfob poog ban jon bi a yinl bu le, le tipoor nmɔn cheel ki ku limɔkpel biɛn.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Nan bu-n Uwonbɔr kaah nan ban Un wɔɔn ban na yin kan U mɔsiinlin a gbeemin, ibamɔn ke waa yin kpenn waah bon man nin bu, le U nan poo tipoor.

eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: Uwonbɔr ŋa tiwann tilee nan le taa jɔɔ kpenimi, ti ma ye limɔsiinl nin tipoor tan bu wee ni li pɔɔ nin Uwonbɔr n por tibɔkar. Mambu tim ban kan wunwoon bol u ban ni, ni ŋan nin ti li ŋmag ti tama kan be ti nintogilin nin lipoobil kenken.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Tama kan wee ti jɔɔ nmɔn li ŋmag ti sufu le ki baa kenken. Tama nmɔn le jon lagir likinkenl lan gɔɔn nin bu, ki kɔɔ kasi a diig n-yaan ke uninfosaalin.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: nan yaan wee Yisa nan chinn jon ki tan kɔɔ nin ti-n, un nan kɔɔ tim bu le. Le ki ŋa biminmanib a yudaau wan kaa jɔɔ diɛm, ke Melikizɛdɛg a toon kaah nan ye man nin.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
local: Melikizɛdɛg nmɔn nan ye Salem a bɔru le nin Uwonbɔr wan be paab a minmanu. Abiraham kaah nan jon ki tan ton ki nyaŋ bibɔrtiib wee ki chaa kun nin, le Melikizɛdɛg nan to u nsan bu le ki kaab Uwonbɔr a kaar ki jaa u bu.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: le Abiraham nan nyan waah jɔɔ nan biɛn piig a poolin libaba ki sei u. Chinchinn, Melikizɛdɛg a yinl a taai le ye Tibuchinn a bɔru; le ki ji ye Salem a bɔru le li mu a taai ye Nposɔɔn a bɔru.

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: Waa nan jɔɔ baa bee na, kaa jɔɔ yaajasan, kaa jɔɔ maa daali bee kum daali, u nann Uwonbɔr a Bija le, ki ye u minmanu hali nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛm.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Ni tiɛr ki lig uja nmɔn kaah nan ye lininfo takpenl man: Hali ke ti yaaja Abiraham nan nan nyan waah to bitɔb ki fee nan biɛn a piig a poolin libaba le ki ta sei u!.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Uwonbɔr nan siin nsiim ke ban ye Liifai a yaabili wee ki ye biminmanibin li gaan bi tɔɔl a ninfob Juu yaab kaah jɔɔ nan biɛn piig a poolin libabau, hali nin bi nabum nmɔn kaah le ye Abiraham a yaabili yin na.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: Uja nmɔn kaa nan kpinn Liifai yaabi, amaa le u nan gaa niwann biɛn piig a poolin libaba Abiraham ban, ki kaab tikaar jaa uma wan nan gaa Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinlin bu.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: Naa jɔɔ nbiɛnimi, wan kaab tikaar jon wan wee u kaab jaar u bu nan na.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: Biminmanib ban gaan niwann biɛn piig a poolin libaba bininfob ban nin nan kpeg le; amaa Melikizɛdɛg ma kaa kpo, u be u miɛfolin ke Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ kaah len man nin.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: Uba li fir len ke Liifai, ban nan gaan piig a poolin liba-n, Abiraham le bi nan ti for le u jɔɔ tenn bi.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: nan bu-n Melikizɛdɛg kaah nan to Abiraham nsan bu n-yonn man nin, ki miin baa laa nan ma Liifai.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Ni ye Liifai a bol ban ye biminmanibin a toon yin nan bi ŋan ke , ba ŋa wee ni ji nan kpaa talar ke uminmann uba n ji dan ki ye wan be ke Melikizɛdɛg, kaa ye Arɔnn a maalimin naa?.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: Ni bu ni ye bi kpenn biminmanibin ke, see ke nsiim mu kpenn.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Tibɔr nan kaah pag ki jiin ti yudaau wan paamin, waa ye wan nyan Liifai a bolin ni, le u bol a ninfo uba kaa la nan nyi ki tun kumanug a toon Uwonbɔr a manug a binbinl bu.

eng: Its clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, but Moses never said anything about priests from that tribe.
local: Ti nyi ke ti yudaau nmɔn nan nyan Juda a maalimin le, amaa Moziz kaa pag niba ki jiin bi bol nmɔn paam ke bi ye biminmanibi.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: Ni ye biminmanib a yudaau wan nann Melikizɛdɛg degirin ke, nan wee ti pag nmɔn li mann wonn binum.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: ki ye wan kpann uminmann kaa ye wan nɔɔ u yaajam a siim kaah wɔɔn man nin amaa ki ye limiɛfol lan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛmin a pɔɔm.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Ni ma bu ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, A ye uminmann le, wan a toon yin be ke Melikizɛdɛg a toon kaah nan ye man nin hali nin n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ diɛmi.

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Uwonbɔr tir nsiim kpegimin le ki tabin ngbaan mi daburigi nin mi kaa jɔɔ nyɔɔr n bu.

eng: . On the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
local: , mambu le Uwonbɔr wɔɔn ti tama kan ŋan, nan che ti fɔɔr mal Uwonbɔr.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: Le Uwonbɔr nan poo tipoor! Amaa le biba nan ŋa biminmanib a yudam le waa nan poo tipoori.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: amaa Yisa nan ŋa biminmanib a yudaau nin tipoorin le, n-yonn man wee Uwonbɔr nan bei u ke, Uyudaan poo tipoora le kaa yin kpenn U potiɛli A ye uminmann wan a toon kaa jɔɔ diɛm le.

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: Tipoor nan bu, le Yisa ta uba ki siin ke tipooŋann n tun.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Biminmanib nmɔn yaba, nan bu-n nkum gen bi toon le baa fir tun chaari;.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: amaa Yisa kaah be nin n-yonn man kaa jɔɔ diɛmin, u minmanug a toon nmɔn yin gbaa be le.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: Mambu u li fir fee ban dan Uwonbɔr ban u ma bu-n iyoo biɛn ki gab, nan bu-n iyoo biɛn u be le ki miɛ ter bi.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: Biminmanib a yudaau nmɔn le tenn ti taah ban nan, ki ye wan be kasi, kaa jɔɔ igalin ni, kaa jɔɔ tijaginn ni, le Uwonbɔr che u be u binba kaa ŋmar nin tibirdamin ni, ki don u yinl ki jon ban biɛn be paab a naamin yaan.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: Waa be ke ban nan ye biminmanib a yudamin ni, waa nan chinn mann kumanug lidaal biɛn u tɔɔl a bir bu, wee ki nin mann bitab a bir bu. U nan mann kumanug nba bu le ki sei bi biɛn bi bir bu waah nan ta uba ki mann kumanugin.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Ni ma bu nsiimin nan gann ndaburim le ki ŋaan bi biminmanib a yudam; amaa tipoor, tan nan ŋɔɔrin nsiimin a bɔɔn nin, nan gann Uma Uwonbɔr a Bija, wan bi ŋa u kasi hali nin isaŋ yan kaa jɔɔ diɛmin le.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Nan bu wee ti len ni niɛ nan ye nan le: Ti jɔɔ biminmanib a yudaau, wan kal paab a naamin a naangbɔŋ nbu Jilimadaan a nɔɔjiim paamin.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: wan tun wonjiɛn bol, ibamɔn a wonjiɛn nanboŋ kan wee Uyudaan baantɔɔl tii ki-n, wee naa ye usaal le tii-n.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Biminmanib a yudaau wan biɛn wee bi gann biɛn ye u li tenn ipiin le ki sin ji li mann kumanug, ni bu che ni kpaa le ke uminmann yudaan nmɔn li jɔɔ nan wee u li ta mann.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: Ni ye u nan bi be dulinya nan yaan ke, waa nan bi li ye biminmanib a yudaau wi, nan bu-n bininfob pii nan be ki mann nsiim nmɔn kaah wɔɔn man nin le.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Wonjiɛn bol kan yaan wee bi tun ibamɔn a toon yin ki nann le ke paab a naamin a jinjiimin. Ni niɛ bu le bi nan kpaan Moziz n-yonn man wee u na yin tii kuwonjiɛn nanboŋin ke: Che niwann biɛn n ŋa ke maah nan wɔɔniŋ kuwonjiɛn nanboŋ kan lijɔɔl a paabin.

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: Amaa itoon yan wee Yisa gaa-n yab jon bi yaa toon ke, tipoopɔnn tan wee Yisa si bininfob nin Uwonbɔr a sisiigin nin kaah jon tipookpegirin man nin, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinl ŋanl nmɔn tɔɔ lifiɛŋanlin bu le.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Ni ma bu ni ye chinchinn a poor nmɔn kaa nan bi jɔɔ kuŋmaruŋ ke, baah ji nan bi li ban tipoor tiba jigin ni.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr nan kan bininfob a galin le ki len ke: Uyudaan le len ke, n-yonn chaan, le N na li poo tipoopɔnn ki sei Iziral Yaab nin Juda yaab.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: Uyudaan len ke tipoor nmɔn kaa na yin nann, tan wee N nan poo sei bi yaajam n-yonn man wee N nan da bi nyan Ijibir a tingbɔŋin nni, nan bu-n baa nan ŋmag tipoor nmɔn nbamɔmi, le N nan labir ki jiɛn bi, Uyudaan le len.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: N-yonn nmɔn nan jon ke, maah li poo tipoor tan sei Iziral yaab sɔ, Uyudaan le len. N li ta n siim ki ŋa bi potiɛrin le ki sɔb nsiim nmɔn ŋa bi sufu ni. Le n na li ye bi Wonbɔru, le bi mu na li ye n ninfob.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Uba kaa ji na li wɔɔn u kunkɔɔn tau bee u nabig ki ke, Li nyi Uyudaan ni, nan bu-n bi biɛn na yin nyim le, naah nyan nbuwaabin ki tan kpaa bininkpeebin.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: Nan bu-n N na li jiɛn bi pootomin ki per bi le kaa ji li tiɛr bi bir bu jigin ni.

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Uwonbɔr kaah nan yin tipoor nan ke, Tipoopɔnn nin le U ŋa tipoor tan chinn nin tipookpegir tan kaa ji tun ni; le niwann nan mu ŋa niwann kpegin ki por li bor kaa li yuuri.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Chinchinn a poorin nan jɔɔ liwonjiɛnl a siim le nin dulinya nan a wonjiɛn bol.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Bi nan tii kunanboŋ le. Ki yi ki poolin niba yaan ke kasi a bol nan yaan wee fila a titɔɔnbol nin teebul nin kasi a boroboro kan wee bi jɔɔ tenn liwonseil a mann bolin nan be.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: Kunanboŋ kiba mu ji nan be kunanboŋ nmɔn a poolin le likinkenl takpenl nan deg ku bu, le bi yi ki ke kasi mu a kasi a bol.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: kunanboŋ nmɔn a poolin nan jɔɔ salima a binbinl nan yaan wee bi nan nyɔɔr nnyɔgkusim ki tenn Uwonbɔr, le Uwonbɔr a poor adaka kan wee salima nan tab ki bu-n mu be niin. Adaka nmɔn a poolin nan jɔɔ salima a binbog kan gbee nin tijiir tan wee bi yi maana nan, nin Arɔnn a jaangbiɛg kan nan bunn tifarin, nin itangbag ilee yan wee bi nan sɔb nsiim ki paan i bu-n nan kpee niin.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: Le tiwann naan wann tan jɔɔ jilima ki nann bininfob nin tikpinkpann nan tɔɔ adaka nmɔn bu, le bi kpinkpann nmɔn deg nan yaan ye tibir a jiɛnper a bolin. Amaa taa li fir pag tibɔr nmɔn biɛn daandaan nan ni.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Ni ye niwann biɛn a sili nan ti ŋa ke, biminmanib nan ti kɔɔ le iyoo biɛn chinchinn a nanboŋ n-yaan ki tan tun bi wonjiɛnl a toon.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: Amaa biminmanib a yudaau a binba le nan ti kɔɔ kunanboŋ kan be poolin nin taaliba libinl a poolin, u nan ti ŋmag ncham le ki kɔɔ, nan wee u li ta mann ubaantɔɔl a bir bu, nin bininfob kaah tun tibir tan wee kaa nyi.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Kasi a Naaŋ nan wɔɔn ni niɛ le ke, nsan man chaa kasi mu a kasi a bolin kaa la loori, chinchinn a wonjiɛn nanboŋin kaah la si n bu.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: Ni niɛ le ye nan mag ki wɔɔn n-yonn man wee ti be daandaan nan, ni wɔɔn ke ipiin nin kumanug kan wee bi nan mann nin kaa nan fir ŋunn uwonjiɛn a potiɛli.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: Ni nan ye tijiir, nin tiwann nyuwann, nin kuchiig daal a kaal nin dulinya nan a siim kaah wɔɔn man hali ki nan kpaa tipoopɔnn nin a yoo.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: Masiya kaah nan dan wee ki ye biminmanib a yudaau wan ye tiwann ŋan tan pii nan be-n, u nan kɔɔ kuwonjiɛn nanboŋ kan yab ki ŋan jon kan be dulinya nan yaan, wee naa ye bininfob le tii ki-n yaan le.

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: Waa nan ŋmag iŋɔɔb nin nnaabum a cham le ki kɔɔ nin yaan ni; amaa nba bu le u nan kɔɔ kasi mu a kasi a bolin ki ta u tɔɔl a cham ki mann kumanug kan ye nkpenim a manug kan kaa jɔɔ diɛm Uwonbɔr ban ki sei ti.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Bi minmanib nan ti taar iŋɔɔb nin inaaja a cham le nin kinaasiɛg kan bi nyɔɔ ki a fatam le ki jɔgin bininfob bu ban jɔɔ tijaginn bi kaal bu-n wee nin ŋa bi kasi bi gbanann nin.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: Mambu ke, Masiya ma a chamin tun nan jon man na, uma wan kaa jɔɔ igalin wee kinaaŋ kan be u ni kaa jɔɔ diɛmin nan che u ta uba ki mann kumanug ki sei Uwonbɔr, ki ŋunn ti potiɛri a toon yan yin liin ti kɔɔn nkumin nin, wee nin che ti fir jiɛn Uwonbɔr wan foo-n!.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: Ni ma bu le Masiya ye ti sandanfaaru tipoopɔnn n-yaan, ke nin che ban wee Uwonbɔr yin bin kan Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinl lan ye lifaal lan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi. Nan bu-n Masiya pii nan kpo ke un da ban nan tun tibir n-yonn man wee tipookpegirin la nan si-n le, ki nyan bi tibir nmɔn a tudarilin.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
local: Ni ye uninfo por u faal waah nan kpo ke u faalin ji man ke, naa tun itoon ni, see ke u nan kpo.

eng: This is because a will takes effect only after a death, since its not in force while the one who made the will is alive.
local: Nan bu-n uba kaa li fir ji uninfo a faal wee udaan la fo; amaa see ke udaan nan kpo.

eng: So not even the first covenant was put into effect without blood.
local: Ni nan bu le che hali chinchinn a poorin kaa nan bi li fir tun itoon wee ncham kaa be.

eng: Moses took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the Law scroll itself and all the people after he had proclaimed every command of the Law to all the people.
local: Moziz kaah nan ta Uwonbɔr a siimin biɛn ki wɔɔn bininfobin biɛn n-yonn man nin, le u ta nnaabum a cham nin nnyum, nin tipeekormann nin iduufala ki lol tabin tab ki ta bɔ ncham nin nnyumin yaan ki jɔg jɔg Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ kpabkaagin nin bininfobin biɛn bu.

eng: While he did it, he said, This is the blood of the covenant that God established for you. .
local: Le ki len ke, Ni niɛ le ye tipoorin a cham, tan wee Uwonbɔr ke ni li ŋmagin.

eng: And in the same way he sprinkled the meeting tent and also all the equipment that would be used in the priests service with blood.
local: Man mu jigin le u nan ta ncham nmɔn ki jɔg jɔg kuwonjiɛn nanboŋin bu nin niwann nan biɛn nan be kunanboŋin nmɔn a poolin wee bi jɔɔ mann bi kaal a manugin.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: Ibamɔn buu nsiimin wɔɔn ke ncham le ye min ŋunn niwann biɛn, ni bu ncham kaa nyan ke tibir a jiɛnper kaa be.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Ni nan kpaa talar le, nin bi li mann kumanug ki ŋunn tiwann tan nann paab a wan yɔɔrin, amaa paab a wan yɔɔrin tɔɔl jɔɔ kumanug kan ŋan jon ti niɛ nan.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Nan bu-n Masiya kaa nan kɔɔ bininfob kaah maa wonjiɛn bol lan nan nann wonjiɛn bol bamɔnlin yaan ni; paab baantɔɔl le u nan don, le ki si ti taalin Uwonbɔr a nintogil.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Waa nan don paab nmɔn ke u ta li gbaa jɔɔ uba ki mann kumanug ke biminmanib a yudaau kaah ti kɔɔ kasi mu a kasi a bol ibin biɛn ki ŋmag ncham man kaa ye u tɔɔl a cham ki tan mann man nin ni.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: Ni bi ye man ke Masiya nan bi li ji fala n-yonn takpem naah nyan dulinya a piinimin. Amaa daandaan nan u degirin ti bu nba bu le, iyoo nan a diɛmin ki ta uba ki mann kumanug ki nyan tibir.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: A li ke uninfo kaah ye un kpo nba bu man nin, wee ni bɔɔn ke bin ji u tibɔr man nin.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: man mu le Masiya a manugin nan mann nba bu ki nyan bininfob takpem a bir; le u ji li jirin, kaa ye u nan ji tibir a fala, amaa u li ŋmag feegab ki nan sei ban guu win.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Yada a ŋaam ye taah kaa jɔɔ nbiɛnim nin nan wee ti jɔɔ tama ni bu-n, wee ti num kaa ga nin.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu le yaaja yonn a ninfob nan jɔɔ kupag.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Yada bu le ti biɛ ke Imɔleen le Uwonbɔr nan ta naan dulinya, ni bu naa ye nan wee ti ga nan le Uwonbɔr nan ta naan dulinya.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Yada bu le Ebiɛl nan mann kumanug ŋaŋ ki sei Uwonbɔr ki jon Kenn a manug. Yada bu le Uwonbɔr nan pag u ke u ye ubuchin le, Uwonbɔr kaah nan pag nan ŋan ki jiin u manugin paamin. Yada bu le u le la pag, hali nin waah le kpo nan.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Yada bu le Uwonbɔr nan taah Enog dulinya nan yaan; le waa kan nkumi; le uba kaa ji nan kan wi, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr le nan ta u don paab. Nin Uwonbɔr kaah nan nan ta u don paabin, U nan pag u ke uma ye wan gbee Uma Uwonbɔr a num.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Naa ye yada ke uba kaa li fir tun wee nin gbee Uwonbɔr a numi, nan bu-n wan biɛn dan Uwonbɔr ban ke ni ŋan udaan li jɔɔ yada ke Uwonbɔr be, ki ye wan tenn ban ban u nin inunman tinyɔɔr.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Yada bu le Nowa, Uwonbɔr kaah nan kpaan u ikpaan ki ta jiin tiwann tan paam wee u num kaa la nan kan nin, le kasi a jawaam nan che u kpee kuŋaluŋ kan fee uma nin u niin yaab gabin. U yada bu le Uwonbɔr nan bii dulinya le Nowa kpann ufaajir u buchin tan nyan u yada nin.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Yada bu, Abiraham nan chee Uwonbɔr a yim ki jon kitiŋ kan wee Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinl ke U li ta kitiŋ kan ki sei win, u nan chee Uwonbɔr a mɔb le ki jon, hali nin waah kaa le nan nyi kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ni, ni biɛn yɔli u nan jon na.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Yada bu le u nan ŋa u kukɔɔbol limɔsiinl a tiŋin n-yaan ki be ke uchaan kaah ŋaan man kitinchaŋin nin, u nan be Tinanbonn nin le uma nin Azig nin Jekɔb ban mu nan ye limɔsiinl nmɔn a faajibin.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Nan bu-n u nan lig nintogil le ki guu kitiŋ kan a fiɛl wee Uwonbɔr nan loon wee ki maa ki-n.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Yada bu le Abiraham nin u poo Sɛra wan nan ye upiiŋɔɔb ki ji porin nan kan npɔɔn ki ma ki big, nan bu-n bi nan ŋa Uwonbɔr wan nan siin limɔsiinlin le yada.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Hali nin Abiraham kaah kaa ji nan fɔg nin nkumin gbaa, uma uja uba nmɔn a yaabili le nan yabig ke iŋmabil yan be titalangbann nin ni, ki ji yab ke limɔgil a gbaam a tanbiidil yan kaa jɔɔ kanlin.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Bininfob nan biɛn la nan be nin bi yada le ki kpo. Baa nan gaa tiwann tan wee Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinlin ni; amaa bi nan kan tiwann nmɔn dindanl le ki jɔɔ npopeen. Bi nan chee ke bi ye kitiŋ kiba a ninfob le ki ye bicham dulinya nan yaan.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Bininfob ban pag man nin nmɔn wɔɔn ke bi ban kitiŋ kan ye bi tɔɔl yaag le.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Ni ye bi nan bi tiɛr kitiŋ kan yaan wee bi nan fiin nbu ke, na bi nan bi kan nsan ki jira.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Amaa bi nan ban kitiŋ kan ŋan le, ki be papaab. Mambu naa jɔɔ Uwonbɔr ifa nin bin yin U ke bi Wonbɔr wi, ni ma bu U mann kitiŋ le ki guu bi.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Yada bu Abiraham, Uwonbɔr kaah nan mag u ligin, u nan ta u bija Azig ke un mann kumanug ki sei Uwonbɔr. Uma wan nan gaa limɔsiinlin ji nan yin ta u bijagaŋ nmɔn le ki mann kumanug.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: hali nin Uwonbɔr kaah le nan bei u ke, Azig bu le bi na li tiɛr a yaabili bu-n.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Abiraham nan tiɛr ke Uwonbɔr li fir fiin utakpiir nkumin, pag wonn a poolin, u sin nan gaa Azig ki nyan nkumin.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Yada bu le Azig nan kaab tikaar jaa Jekɔb nin Isɔɔ bu ki jiin iyoo yan la chaan nin paam.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Yada bu Jekɔb, kaah nan yin kpe-n, u nan kaab tikaar ki jaa Josef a naachim bu ubaba, le ki nyii u jaangbiɛg bu ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Yada bu Josef a kum kaah nan malin, u nan pag Iziral yaab a nyam Ijibir a tiŋin, ki ji wɔɔn bi waah kpo ke bin ŋa u kpabi man.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Yada bu le Moziz a baa nin u na nan ta u ki woon iŋmal itaa, baah nan maa u a bɔɔn. Bi nan kan ke Moziz ye kibiŋiŋaŋ kan kaa jɔɔ kpiɛg le, mambu baa nan san ubɔr a siimin ni.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Yada bu Moziz kaah nan yabig, u nan yii le kaa ban bin ke u ye ubɔr Faro a bisa a bigi.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: U nan chee nin un ji fala nin Uwonbɔr a ninfobin, ki jon nin un ji tibir a mɔm man kaa yuurin na.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: U nan tiɛr ke u jin ifa Masiya bu ke ni jɔɔ tinyɔɔr jon Ijibir a jɔɔma, nan bu-n u num nan tɔɔ u nyɔɔr tan be nintogilin bu le.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: Yada bu Moziz nan nyan Ijibir, kaa san bijawaam ubɔr a ŋuul bu; u nan jɔɔ limɔril le, nan bu-n u nan kan Uwonbɔr wan wee baa ga-n le.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: Yada bu le u nan ŋmag Yuglagir a chiigin, ki nan che bi ta ncham ki jɔg jɔg inaangan bu ke nkum a malaka wan ku nbininkpeemin nan foon ke, un laa ku Iziral yaab a bininkpeemin ni.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Yada bu le Iziral yaab nan don limɔgmanlin ke kitinkooŋin; amaa Ijibir yaab ma kaah nan bigin ke bin do-n, nnyum nan jin ba.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Yada bu Iziral yaab kaah nan choon jin Jeriko a goi a lapoob iweehn ilile-n, ni bɔɔn bu i nan lur le.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Yada bu baah nan ku ban ye limɔyiildamin, baa nan ku Rahab wan nan ye lipiijagɔɔrilin ki kpiɛ bi bu, nan bu-n waah gaa Iziral a ninfob ban jon bin pigir kitiŋin ki ligin ki woon u niin nin bu.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: Ke n ji yin len ke baa? Maa jɔɔ isaŋ nin n pag ni Gidion, nin Barag, nin Samisin, nin Jafita, nin Defid, nin Samɔɔl nin bilinlemin biɛn a bɔri.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: ban a yada bu wee bi nan kɔn ki nyaŋ ntim a bɔrtiib, ki tun ibamɔn a itoon, ki kan nan nan ye Uwonbɔr a mɔsiinlin; ki ye ban nan loon igbegin a mɔɔŋi.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: le ki junn nŋmipeen a tuntom, le ki guun ŋmar takoobi a kumin; ban a daburigi nan kpann npɔɔn, le ki nan to bitɔb nin npɔɔn ki nyaŋ itindoo a tɔtob.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Bipiib biba a takpiib le Uwonbɔr nan fiin bi nkumin, le bi ji be nin bi miɛfol. Biba nan kan limugil, amaa le baa chee bin guun nin, naah li ŋa man wee bin kan nkum a fiim man ŋan.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Bi nan daar bibam nyɔɔrug le ki boo bi, le ki lol bibam tikulor ki kɔɔn bibam mu tiloondirin.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Bi nan gabir bi itangbag, ki jiil bibam por milee lee, ki ta takoobi ki ku bibam. Bibam nan yee tipeegbann le nin tiŋɔɔgbann, ki ye faladam, le bininfob mugir bi ki ŋaan bi nan kaa ŋan.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Dulinya kaa nan kpaan bi. Bi nan choon yelin le kukunkɔɔg nin ijɔɔ a paab, ki dɔɔ titangbafiɛrin nin ifalin.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Bi niɛ nan biɛn bi yada bu le Uwonbɔr nan pag bi, amaa le bi ni uba kaa gaa nan wee Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinl ke U li sei bin ni.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: Uwonbɔr nan bon ibonn yan ŋan le ki sei ti ke tim nin bi ma nan lagin ki be ke u li ŋa bi kasi.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Mambu taah jɔɔ siɛradam takpem ban gɔɔn jin ti nan, ni che ti ta nan biɛn li legin ti samin ki gab nin tibir tan fir chor ti-n, ni che ti nyigin ki guun ti taguunl lan dɔ guu ti nan.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Ni che ti ta ti num ki tɔɔn Yisa bu, wan ye ti yada a piinim nin njoorimin. U nan jin fala lidapunpunl bu nin limɔril kaa san ifa, nmɔn man nan dɔ guu nbu, ki kal Uwonbɔr a naangbɔŋ bu u nɔɔjiim paam.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Ni li tiɛr waah kan limugil tibirdam ban kaa nan chee win a nɔɔn nin bu, wee nin laa che ni gbanann ngbarig wee ni ta ni yada gabi.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Naah kɔn nin tibirin, naa la kɔn tan foo nan yaan ye ni nyan ni chami.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Ni tam imɔleen yan wee Uwonbɔr ta pɔɔg ni gbanann ki yin ni u buwaab ki ke: N big, Uyudaan dar a tul ke, laa yin li yɔli, ni ye U fuun a bu ke, laa che a gbanann ngbɔɔ.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: nan bu-n Uyudaan kpaan ban wee U jiɛ-n le, le ki dar wan biɛn wee U chee sei u ke u ye a bigin a tul.

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Chee sei limugil a tuntomin na ke ni ye ikpaan le; Uwonbɔr ŋmag ni le ke u buwaabin. Bibolinba wee ki baa kaa kpaan kaa?.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Ni ye baa kpaan ni , man ke naa ye u baantɔɔl a bimarimi.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Nan kpiɛ, ti biɛn nan jɔɔ ti baam ban ma ti tininforin nin, ki kpaan ti le ti tenn bi jilima ni bu. Mambu ni ŋan ti chee sei wan ye ti naaŋ a Baa-n ke nin jo man wee ki fo!.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Ti baam nan ti kpaan ti le wama baah tiɛr ke ni ŋan man; amaa Uwonbɔr ma kpaan ti le naah ŋan sei ti bu, naah li ŋa man wee ti por u kasi a binbemin.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Ikpaan a yoo kaa jɔɔ nmɔni, amaa ni ye nton le. Ni bɔɔn bu ke, le nim ban mu tinn nmɔn nkan nbuchin binben nin nposɔɔn.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: Mambu, ni tann ni nɔɔi yan bagin nin ni dun yan kaa pɔɔ nna.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: Ni mann nsan man tel ki sei ni taai, wee ni laa nyaŋ kuwɔbigi, amaa wee un kan kutebug.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Ni pɔɔn niba ki li jɔɔ nposɔɔn nin bininfob biɛn wee ki li be kasi; naa jɔɔ kasi a binbem ke uba kaa li fir kan Uyudaan ni.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Ni laa che uba n nan lann Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanli, wee nin laa che nton a sibonl npa ki yabig ni sufu-n ni wee nin ta kuŋmaruŋ ki dan ki bii bininfob takpemi.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Ni laa che ni ni uba n tun iwanchɔgin toon upiibanlin bee ujabanlin ni, bee kaa jɔɔ nwonjiɛn binben ke Esɔɔ, wan nan kɔɔr u ninkper a faal kisaayiig kiba bu nni.
