eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu, a li ke naah nyi man nin, waah nan nan ban un gaa lifaal nmɔn a kaarin u baa nan yiiu, waa nan fir kpenn u baa a potiɛli, nin waah le nan ban tikaar nmɔn ki mɔr nin tinyinyinin biɛn yɔli.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Nim kaa foo lijɔɔl lan wee ni li fir ma li wee li gaan nŋmi nni; nan yaan wee nibɔɔngem ki ŋmin sulim wee ntafapɔɔrim daar;.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: kunaatuŋ kaah mɔr nan yaan bee likukɔlin kaah pag ileen yan, wee ban nan gbirin nan balin ke u laa ji pag ileen nmɔn sei bi.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: nan bu-n baa nan li fir ŋmag nsiimin kaah wɔɔn man nin ke: Hali ni ye kuwankobug le maa lijɔɔl nmɔn ke, bin gabir ki itangbag ki kuu.

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Baah nan kan nan nin nan jɔɔ bijawaam le, le Moziz len ke Bijawaam le jɔɔm le n gbanann jegil.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Ni li jiɛ ni kpiɛtiib ke ni nabuma.

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Amaa nim foon Zayɔnn a Jɔɔlin ban na, papaab a Jerusalam n-yaan, Uwonbɔr wan fo-n a tiŋin, ni foon malakam turil mu a tuturil a mɔmin a laguŋ n-yaan na.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Ni laa tami wee ki li gaan bichama, nan bu-n ni ŋaan man ke, bibam gaan malakam le kaa nyi.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: ni foon biwonjiɛm ban ye nbininkpeemin ban na, ban a yin sɔb dɔ papaabin. Ni foon Uwonbɔr, wan ye bininfob biɛn a bɔjiru win ban na, bininfob buchim ban a naaŋ ŋa kasi.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Ni li tiɛr ban be tiloondirin nin bu, a li ke ni gbaa tɔɔl be tiloondirin le-n, nin ban wee bi mugir bin a li ke ni mu ji bi fala nmɔn le-n.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: ni foon Yisa wan ye usandanfaar tipoopɔnn n bu ban na, nin ncham jɔg kaam man pag ibamɔn ki jon Ebiɛl a chamin ban na.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Lipiikɔɔnl nin lijakunl ye ti biɛn li tenn li jilima le, lipiikɔɔnl nin lijakunl a didɔɔbol ye ni li be kasi le, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr na li ji ban tun iwanchɔgin toon nin tibɔr bipiib banlin nin bija banlin.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Ni laa nyi yii Uwonbɔr wan pag nan ni. Ni ye baa nan ŋmar baah nan yii wan a kpaan yin dulinya nan yaan nin ban ke, ŋma nin nan chaa baan timaa, ni ye ti labir ti bɔɔn kpaa wan be papaab ki kpaan ti nan ke naa?.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Ni choo niba nin ilig a jiɛma, wee ki che ni num ngbee nan wee ni jɔɔ-n, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr len ke, Maa li nyi ki jiɛn ni; maa li nyi ki yii ni.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn, Uwonbɔr a kukɔl nan che dulinya dam le, amaa daandaan nan u siin limɔsiinl ki ke, Maa ji yin che dulinya a binba n dami, amaa n li che paab mu n dam.

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Ni ma bu le ti pag nin tidindann ke, Uyudaan le ye wan terim; ni bu jawaam kaa jɔɔmi. Uninfosaal li fir ŋaam baa.

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Imɔbon yan len ke, N ji li chee-n, wɔɔn ke tiwann tan dam ki ye tiwann naan kaarin na li choo nyan ki jiɛn tan kaa yin damin paar.

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Ni li tiɛr ni sanliib ban nan pag Uwonbɔr a bɔr ki sei nin. Ni li tiɛr bi binbem kaah nan nan joor man na, wee ki li toog bi yada kaah nan ye man nin.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Mambu taah gaan nnaam a yerin man kaa li fir dam nan, ni che ti pag Uwonbɔr, ki li jiɛn u nin jilima nin bijawaam.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Yisa Masiya kaah won be man won nin yaan nin, u le be man le dinan, u le yin gbaa be man le nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: ni ma bu Ti Wonbɔru nmɔn ye nŋmi man gaan le.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Ni laa che nwɔɔncham nan a booli biɛn nboon ni. Ni ŋan nin ti sufu n kan npɔɔn Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanlin bu, naa ye ikaal a jiir tan kaa ter ban ji ti nni.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Ti jɔɔ kumanug a binbinl lan wee ban tun itoon tiwonjiɛn nanbonn nin kaa jɔɔ nsan nin bin ji li bu a jiiri.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Ni ma bu biminmanib a yudaau nan ti ŋmag tiwankor a cham le ki kɔɔ kasi mu a kasi a bolin le ti ye tibir a manug, amaa le ki nyɔɔ tiwankor nmɔn bi laguŋ nmɔn a gbaam.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Mambu le Yisa mu nan jin fala ntingbaan kitiŋ nmɔn a timansanmɔnn, ki ta u tɔɔl a cham ke un nyan bininfob tibirin.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Ni che ti jo u ban kulaguŋ nmɔn a gbaam, ki tan ji ifa yan wee u jin nin.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Nan bu-n dulinya nan yaan taa jɔɔ kitiŋ kan li gbaa be, amaa ti lig kitiŋ kan la yin danin le.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Mambu, Yisa a yinlin bu, ni che ti li pagir Uwonbɔr kumanug a pag kan nya timɔgban yin ki wɔɔn U yinlin.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Ni laa tam itoon ŋan a tumi, ki li por tab naah jɔɔ nan na, man a manug le Uwonbɔr a num gbee.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Ni li jɔɔ limɔcheel nin ni sanliib wee ki chee sei bi ninkper a pɔɔma. Bi baga be nin ni, ki ye bininfob ban na li wɔɔn baah tun nan. Ni chee sei ba wee bi toon li ye nmɔn, ki laa ye litugili, naa ye man ke naa na yin ŋan sei ni.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Ni li miɛ Uwonbɔr ki tenn ta. Ti nyi ke ti jɔɔ potiɛri yan ween le ti ban ti li be jilima a binbemin niwann biɛn nin.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: N tɔɔl le balin ni le ke, ni li miɛ Uwonbɔr a wee Un che n jirin ni ban daandaan nan.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: Uwonbɔr wan ye nposɔɔndaan wan nan ta tipoor tan kaa jɔɔ diɛmin a cham ki fiin ti yudaau Yisa wan ye upeekpaaŋanin nkumin nin.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: un sei ni nan ŋan biɛn wee ni ta tun u bam, ki che ni tinn nan li gbee u num, Yisa Masiya, wan ŋan nin npagim iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm bu. Ibamɔn.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: N nabum, n balin ni le ke, ni li jɔɔ limɔril wee ki gaa n kpaan nan a bɔra, nan bu-n tibɔr wama le n ta sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki sei ni.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: N ban ni li nyi ke bi loor ti nabig Timoti kuloondiigin ki jaau. Ni ye u foon weem ke, n li ta u ki dan wee ti nan kan ni.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Ni poor ni sanliib nin Uwonbɔr a ninfobin nan. Itali yaab poor ni.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl be ni biɛn bu.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Nsiimin ye tiwann ŋann tan chaan nin a jinjiim le, amaa naa ye tiwann ŋann bamɔn nmɔn tɔɔl le ye ni. Ni ma bu kumanug kan gbaa mann ibin biɛn kaa diɛrin, kaa yin fir ŋa ban fɔɔr mal bin jiɛn Uwonbɔrin kasi.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Ni ye biminmanib nmɔn nan bi li fir ke, baa nan bi li ta kumanug ki jiɛn naa? Ni ma bu na biwonjiɛmin bi kan nŋunnim nba buu le nin diɛ, le bi sufu kaa ji bi li bii bi toonbir bu.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Amaa kumanug nmɔn a manim libinl biɛn nmɔn nan ye nan tiɛr bininfob le bi bir.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: nan bu-n inaaja nin iŋɔɔb a cham kaa li fir nyan tibiri.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Mambu Masiya kaah dan dulinya nan yaan nin, u nan bei Uwonbɔr ke: Ŋaa ban kumanug nin ipiin nin, amaa tigbanann le a ŋa ti sili ki seim;.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: A num kaa nan gbee kumanug kan wee bi nan mann ki nyɔɔr ti nŋmi ni nin tibir a manugin ni.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Le n nan len ke, Min sɔ, ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋ kpabkaagin ki jiin n paam ke, N dan n nan tun a bam le, Uwonbɔr aa. .

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: Chinchinn u nan len ke, Ŋaa ban kumanug nin ipiin nin tiwankor tan wee bi nyɔɔ nŋmi, nin tibir a manug kan wee bi ti mann tibir bu-n nni .

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Le u len ke, Min sɔ, N dan n nan tun ŋaah ban man le. Le Uwonbɔr ta chinchinn a manugin ki gab, le ki siin kumanug kan kpiɛ milee nan.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Uwonbɔr a bam nmɔn bu le U ŋa ti kasidam Yisa Masiya kaah nan ta u gbanann ki mann kumanug nba bu ki sei ti-n bu.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Biminmanib biɛn si le lidaal biɛn ki tun bi wonjiɛn toon; ki le gbaa sagir ki mann kumanug kuba nmɔn, kan kaa li fir nyan tibiri.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Amaa uminmann u niɛ man nan mann kumanugin nba bu le kpeŋileŋ tibir bu, le ki kal Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: Ki guu iyoo yan wee Uwonbɔr na li che u dataam n kpann u taai a tɔɔn bol.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: nan bu-n kumanug kiba nmɔn le u che ban wee u nyan bi tibirin ki ŋaan bi kasi hali nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛm.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Kasi a Naaŋin mu jin kumanug nmɔn a siɛra ki sei ta. Chinchinn u len ke:.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: Uyudaan len ke, Tipoor tan wee N yin poo sei bi sɔ n-yonn man chaan nan a bɔɔn bu, N li ta N siimin ki ŋa bi sufu ni, le ki sɔb nsiim nmɔn ki ŋa bi potiɛrin.

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Le u ji len kpiɛ ke: Bi bir nin bi tukangbir a toon yin, maa ji li tiɛr i bu.

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Nan yaan wee tibir a jiɛn per be-n, naa kpaa talar nin tibir a manug ji li be.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: Mambu, n nabum, taah jɔɔ tidindann ke ti na li kɔɔ kasi mu a kasi a bolin Yisa a chamin bu-n.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: nsanpɔɔn man fo wee ti kan likinkenlin loor ki sei ti-n, n ma ye, Yisa a gbanann.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: nin taah jɔɔ uminmann wan yab ki ŋmag Uwonbɔr a niin nin.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: ni che ti fɔɔr mal Uwonbɔr nin sufu ŋaŋ nin yada kan gbee nin ibamɔn na, waah jɔgin ki ŋunn ti sufu nyan kasi, wee ti nyan igalin ti potiɛlin ni, wee ki fun tigbanann mu nnyum ŋami.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Ni che ti li ŋmag taah jɔɔ tama kan ki pag ki bɔrin wee ki laa yeŋi, nan bu-n wan siin limɔsiinlin nmɔn ye yada daan le.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Ni che ti baga li be taah li ŋa man ki li pɔɔgir ti kpiɛtiib a sufu, wee nin che ti fir wɔɔn ti jiɛm nin tab kaah ye man ki li tun itoon ŋan.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: Ni laa che ti ta tab ban a lagim ki jiɛn ni, a li ke bibam kaah ŋaan man nin ni, amaa ni che ti li mann pɔɔg tab a gbanann, a li ke naah kan Uyudaan a dan daalin kaah fɔɔr maligin man nin.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Taah biɛ ibamɔn yan wee bi wɔɔn ti nan a bɔɔn bu, ni ye ti ji be ki tun titoonbir ke, kumanug kuba kaa ji gur ki li ŋunn tibir nmɔn ni.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: amaa taah guu tibɔr a jim nin nŋmi takpen man na yin gaa Uwonbɔr a dataamin a jawaam le be.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Ni ye uninfo nan ti yii Moziz a siimin ke, wee bininfob bilee bee bita jin u siɛra ke, bi nan ti ku udaan le kaa san u kunyunyunbaagi.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Mambu, ni tiɛr ke baa a tudaril kpaan nin uninfo wan taah Uwonbɔr a bija a taal bu nan n kan naa, wan ŋa ncham man ye Uwonbɔr a poorin tijaginn a ŋaar, wee ncham nmɔn ŋunn udaan, wee udaan ji sug Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl a Naaŋin naa?.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Nan bu-n ti nyi wan nan len ke, Ni ye min le nkɔn jiin; n li yɔ, ki ji len ke, Uyudaan li ji u ninfob tibɔr.

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: Ni jɔɔ bijawaam le nin uninfo n lu Uwonbɔr wan ye limiɛfoldaan nan a nɔɔn.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Ni tiɛr iyoo yan nan chinn nin, wee ni nan gaa nwonween nin a bɔɔn bu, wee ni nan ye fala a jim nin linunmanlin, amaa naa nan nyaŋ ni.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Iyonn iba bi nan ti sugir ni le ki mugir ni npaan ni; le iyonn iba mu ke ni ti kpiɛg ban wee bi mugir nbu le ki ji fala.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: Ni nan jogir biloondiikɔɔbin le, le ki chee sei ban yug ni wan yɔɔrin nin nmɔn, nan bu-n ni tɔɔl nyi naah jɔɔ nan nin kaah ŋan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Mambu ni laa ta ni dindanin ki gabi; nan bu-n ni nyɔɔr na li yab.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: Ni ŋan nin ni li jɔɔ limɔril wee ni nan fir tun Uwonbɔr a bam ke, ni li kan nan wee U nan siin limɔsiinl ke U li sei nin.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: Ni ma bu n-yonn wama nan yaan, Wan chaan nin li dan kaa li yuuri.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: Amaa N yaa ninfo buchin win li be u yada ni. Ni ye u jir bɔɔn ke, N num kaa li gbee wi.

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Amaa tim kaa ye ban sul jir bɔɔn wee ki gab yɔli nni, amaa ti ye ban ŋa yada nin ban kan feegabin le.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Min Jemisi wan ye Uwonbɔr nin Uyudaan Yisa Masiya a yomu-n: N poor Uwonbɔr a ninfob ibol piig nin ilee yan yal be dulinya nan a logi ina nan biɛn nin.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: N nabum, ni ye nbigim man biɛn dan ni bu ke, ni che ni li ye nmɔn takpen ki sei na.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: nan bu-n ni nyi ke ni yada a magim mann kpiɛg ni limɔril le.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: Ni kpaa le nin limɔril n diɛ mi toonl wee nin che ni gbee bininfob nbamɔm, wee niba n laa par ni.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Ni ye ni ni uba par nlam ke, u yaadaan n miɛm Uwonbɔr wan tenn bininfob nin tibuchinn wee kaa galin uba-n li ta sei u.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: Amaa u miɛ ke, ni kpaa le nin un ŋa Uwonbɔr yada ki laa biɛn ni, nan bu-n wan jɔɔ nbiɛnim nann limɔgil a nyungbɔŋ le, man wee nfaam daar mi ki yeŋin.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: U yaadaan n laa tiɛr ke u li kan niba Uyudaan ban ni;.

eng: They are double-minded, unstable in all their ways.
local: u ye ipotiɛ ilee daan le, kaa si bogiliba nan biɛn wee u tun ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: Kinabig kan jɔɔ jiininba iyoo biɛn n nan kan ndom ke, ki na li fa ligbingbanl.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
local: Amaa wan ye uwandaan dulinya nan yaan wee Uwonbɔr nan jiin u tum ke, u daan li far ligbingbanla, nan bu-n u na yin kpo le ke nsipuumin.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: Babu nween pɔɔ wee ntuton nin fa kisiigin ke, npuumin koor le ki lu le mi ŋamin bii. Man mu le ni ye ki sei uwandaan, n-yonn man wee u tiɛr ke u toon li jo nintogilin, iyaayoo le u kpeg.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Tikaar be wan jɔɔ limɔril limagil a poolin nin bu, nan bu-n ni ye u nyaŋ limagil ki diɛ ke u li kan u naan yuchigirig kan ye limiɛfol wee Uwonbɔr nan siin limɔsiinl ke u li ta sei ban ban win.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Ni ye nbigim kan wan ke, un laa len ke Uwonbɔr li bigin mi. Nan bu-n libiginl liba kaa be ki li fir bigin Uwonbɔr i, le U mu kaa bigin uba;.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: amaa uninfo biɛn ga nbiginim, n-yonn man wee u potiɛ bir a bam dar u ki chor u nle.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: Tɔ, ni bɔɔn bu a bam nmɔn choo lipool ke, mi yin ma tibir le, le tibir nmɔn nan yabig ke, mi mu nmaa nkum.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: N nabubankaam, ni laa che bin lar ni.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Nan ŋan nin nan be kasi biɛn ye ipiin yan nyan paab le, ki nyan ti Baa wan jɔɔ papaab a weemin, wan kaa kpenn ke nbɔɔm kaah kpenn man nin.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: U yaa wiib bu le U ma ti, ibamɔn a mɔleen yin bu, ke nin che ti kpann u bininkpeem waah naan nan biɛn ni.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: N nabum ni li nyi ni niɛ nan bu: Ni ŋan nin uninfo li gbir weenweem, ki li pag soonsoon, wee u ŋuul mu n laa fii weenweemi.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: Uninfosaal a ŋuufiiŋ kaa che Uwonbɔr a buchinn forin ni.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: Mambu, ni nyam niwann nan biɛn ye tijaginn nin nbinben biin nin, wee ki jiin niba tum ki gaa imɔleen yan wee bi ta sei nin, imɔleen nmɔn le li fee ni ki jaa.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Ni laa pelig imɔleen nin a binba, wee ki lar niba. Ni tum yaah len man nin.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: Wan pel imɔleen nan amaa wee kaa ŋa yaah len man nin ke, u nann uninfo kaah lig u nundaag niɛnu ni le.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: le u lig uba ki diɛ ke, ni bɔɔn bu u fɔr ke libol nmɔn yaan le un tam u nunaŋ kaah ye man.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: Amaa uninfo lig Uwonbɔr a siim man be kasi wee ki tenn wiibin, wee ki le gbaa ŋaan man kaa tamir nan wee u gbirin, amaa wee ki ŋaan imɔleen nin kaah len man nin ke, tikaar li be ubu waah yin tun nan biɛn nin.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Ni ye uba tiɛr ke u ye uwonjiɛn wee kaa chor u lanbil ke, u lar uba le, u wonjiɛnl nmɔn ye yɔli le.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Liwonjiɛnl lan wee ti Baa a num gbeeg ke li ye kasi kaa jɔɔ tibir ye nan le: Nkpeebim nin ipukɔɔ a terim bi bɔmugkaarin nin choonba ki jiin dulinya nan a ŋmaruŋ paam.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: N nabum naah ŋa ti yudaau Yisa Masiya wan ye kupag daan nin yada man ni, ni laa gann tabi.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Ni ye uba dan ni laguŋin ki yee tiwanŋann nin salima a gbiɛŋ, wee faladaan mu nan kɔɔn ki yee tiwanchar ke.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: ni ye ni sei wan yee tiwanŋann nin jilima ki bei u ke, Kikaabol ŋaŋ sɔ kalim amaa wee ki bei faladaan nin ke, Fin li si doo, bee Kalim tum, ke.

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: naa ye nganim le ni ŋa ni sisiigin nmɔn naa? Ki labir bibɔjib ni potiɛbir buu?.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: N nabum; ni pela man: Naa ye Uwonbɔr le gann ban ye faladam kutingbɔŋ nan a poolin, ke bi li ye biwandam yada a ŋaam bu, wee ki nan ji paab a naam a faal lan wee Uwonbɔr siin limɔsiinl ke u li sei ban ban win naa?.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: Amaa le nim sug faladam. Naa ye biwandamin le jɔɔ ni ki mugir ki chor ni chaa tibɔr a jim a dirin ni naa?.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: Naa ye bi ma pag ki bii Masiya a yin ŋanl lan wee Uwonbɔr ta sei ni nnaa?.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Ni ye a ŋmag nnaam a siim man be Uwonbɔr a mɔleen yin nin ki ke, Li jiɛ a kunkɔɔn taba ke ŋaah ban a ba man nin, ke a ŋa man ke a ŋa choba.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: Ni ye a gann bininfob ke a ŋa tibira ki ye wan bii nsiim.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Wan ŋmag nsiimin biɛn, amaa wee ni ŋa le u bii nsiim nmɔn miba ke, u bii nsiim man gurin biɛn na.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: Amaa wan len ke, Laa tun iwanchɔgin toon ni, uma le ji len ke, Laa ku. Ni ye ŋaa tun iwanchɔgin toon amaa wee ki ku ke, a ye wan bii nsiim le.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Ni li paga, wee ki sin li tun ke bininfob ban wee bi na li ji bi tibɔr nsiim man tenn feeja-n.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: nan bu-n, ban kaa san bikpiɛtiib kunyunyunbaagin, Uwonbɔr mu na li ji bi tibɔr kaa yin san bi kunyunyunbaagi. Linyunyunbasanl jon tibɔr a jima.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: N nabum, ni ŋam ye baa, ni ye uninfo ke u jɔɔ yada wee u yada nmɔn kaa tun itoon ke, yada nmɔn a bosu li fir fee u jaa?.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Che ti ke a nabig wan ye kibijag bee kibisiɛg be wee kaa jɔɔ tiwanyeer bee tijiir tan li ji.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: Le ni ni uba bei u ke, Li chaa nin nposɔɔn na, ki ji bei u ke, Uwonbɔr li seiŋ wee a ji gbee, wee kaa ŋa nan wee u ban ki sei u ke, ni ŋam ye baa?.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: Ni ye man le, yada a ŋaam a binba wee yada nin itoon kaa nɔɔ tab ke, yada kpiiŋ le yen.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: Amaa uba li len ke, Fin le jɔɔ yada, le n mu tun itoon. Wɔɔm a yada kan kaa tun itoon nin ni, wee n wɔɔniŋ n yada nin n toon yan wee n tun.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: Ni ŋa yada ke Uwonbɔr ye u ba le kpeŋileŋ. Ni ŋan! Nnaanbiim gbaa tɔɔl ŋa yada man le ki san bijawaam.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: Fin kujɔrug nan, a ban a biɛ ke yada wan kaa tun itoon ye yɔlii?.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: Naa ye itoon ŋan a tum bu le Uwonbɔr nan ŋa ti yaaja Abiraham ubuchin, waah nan ta u big a Azig paan kumanug a binbinl bu ke un mann kumanug ki sei Uwonbɔr nnaa?.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: Ni kan ke u yada nin u toon nan lagin tab le ki tun, le u yada nan ŋa nbamɔm u toon a tum bu.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: Le ni nan gbeen Uwonbɔr a mɔleen yan len ke, Abiraham nan ŋa Uwonbɔr yada, le U ŋa u tibuchinn, le ki nan yin u ke Uwonbɔr a jɔ.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Ni kan ke uninfo a toon ŋan bu le bi ke u ye uninfoŋan amaa naa ye u yada a binba bu.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: Naa ye man mu jigin le Rahab wan nan ye lipiijagɔɔril nmu bi nan yin u ubuchin, waah nan ta kudiig ki sei ban nan dan bin nan pigir lig kitiŋ nmɔn ki ji nyan bi wee baa ku bin nnaa?.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: A li ke tigbanann tan kaa jɔɔ kinaaŋ kaah ye tigbanann kpiinn man nin, man le yada a ŋaam kan kaa tun itoon mu ye yada kpiiŋ.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: Ba che ikɔn nin imɔkpel be ni sisiigin naa? Naa ye ni nyan ni gbanann a bam man be nin ni ki kɔn nin ni n lee?.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Ni ban niba amaa le kaa ga ni. Ni bu le ni jɔɔ linunchɔril ki ku bininfob, amaa naa li fir kan nan wee ni ban nmɔn ni. Ni jɔɔ imɔkpel ki kɔn nin tab. Naa jɔɔ naah ban nan nni, nan bu-n naa miɛ Uwonbɔr i.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: Ni ye ni miɛ ke, naa ga, nan bu-n ni miɛ nin tipotiɛbir le, ni miɛ ke ni ta ŋa ni baantɔɔl a gbanann a bam le.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the worlds friend becomes Gods enemy.
local: Nim ban kaa si bogiliba nan, naa nyi ke nim ban jɔɔr dulinya nan kujɔtiigin ŋa Uwonbɔr ni dataag lee? Wan jɔɔr dulinya nan kujɔtiig ke, udaan ŋa Uwonbɔr u dataaga.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Bee ni tiɛr ke Uwonbɔr a bɔr kaah len man ni kaa jɔɔ bu lee? Ke Kinaaŋ kan wee Uwonbɔr ta ŋa ti ban nin ki jɔɔ linunchɔril takpem maa?.

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: Amaa le u san ti kunyunyunbaag takpem. Mambu le Uwonbɔr a bɔr len ke, Uwonbɔr yiin kalanbaanidam le, amaa le ki sin san ban jɔɔ jiininba kunyunyunbaag.

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: Mambu ni taam niba ki sei Uwonbɔr a. Ni tom Sintani, wee un guun ki jiɛn ni.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Ni fɔɔr mal Uwonbɔr a, wee Uwonbɔr mu nfɔɔr ki mal ni. Nir ni nɔɔi ya, nim tibirdam nan, ki ŋa ni sufu kasiu, nim ipotiɛ ilelee dam nan.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Ni li jɔɔ npoobiin na, ki li mɔr ki kuun na. Kpenn ni laar ŋa timɔra ki che ni mɔm nlabir lisufubiila.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Jiin ni ba ki sei Uyudaan na, wee Un yuur ni paab.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: N nabum, ni laa jɔɔ kutingbiig nin tabi. Wan pag tibɔbir ki sei u nabig bee ki jin u tibɔr ke, u pag galin nsiimin le, ki jin nsiim nmɔn tibɔra. Ni ye a jin nsiim tibɔr ke, ŋaa ŋmag mi le nmɔn, a ye wan jin nsiim nmɔn le tibɔr.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Uwonbɔr a binba le siig nsiim, ki ji li fir ji bininfob tibɔr, ki biɛ li fir fee bininfob gab, bee ki bii bininfob. Amaa fin, a ye ŋma ki li fir ji a kunkɔɔn tau tibɔraa?.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Ni pela man, nim ban ti ŋa ke ni len ke, Dinan bee won ti wo li jo kitiŋ poolin ki tan ji libinl niin ki kpeŋ kpoŋkpoŋ ki kan ilig.

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: Ba ŋaa? Naa nyi nan wo li ŋa won ni. A miɛfol ye baa? Ni nann le ke liŋunl kaa lu ke naa yuur wee ki fɔr man ni.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: Amaa naah ye ni pag man ye nan le, Ni ye Uyudaan a bam ke, ti li be ki tun ni niɛ bee ni niɛ.

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: Ke naah ye man nin, ni far igbingban le ki jɔɔ kalanbaani, ni gbingbanfaal nmɔn ye yɔli le.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Mambu wan nyi itoon ŋan yan bi ye un tun, wee kaa tun ke, u ŋa tibira.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Nim bijɔɔndam, ni pela man, ni li mɔra ki li kuun na, nan bu-n fala kan yin dan ni bu-n bu.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: Ni jɔɔmin biɛn bura, le ipɔgliɛn liɛ ni wann yɔɔr biɛn ki bii u.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: Ni salima nin ni kurninann nin kɔɔ likurila. Likuril nmɔn le li ye siɛra ki li gaa ni gbanann ke nŋmi-n. Ni taah ifaa le ki woon idaajoorikaan.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Lig! Ni jin bitoontum ban tun ni kpaan nin dulima, kaa yɔn bi. Le bi mɔr, bidichiɛb nmɔn a mɔr kɔɔ Liwonbɔr Gbingbemil a tuli yin na.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Ni be naah ban man le dulinya nan yaan, ki nɔɔ ni gbanann a bam, ki don ni ba. Ni ŋaam nmɔn polig ni gbanann le lidaal lan wee bi na yin kɔr ni.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Ni nan pag ki bii bipopendam ma, ki ku ban kaa nan kɔnn nin ni-n.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: Mambu n nabum, ni mɔra, hali ki nan kpaa ti Yudaau a jirim. Ni lig ukpaar kaah jɔɔ limɔril man ki guu u kpaab kaah na li ŋa tijiir, naah yuur unalantaal a yonn ki nan kpaa utakotaal a yoo.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Ni mu li jɔɔ limɔrila, ki li si nbamɔma, nan bu-n Uyudaan a damin mala.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: Ni laa ŋulin nin tabi, n nabum, ni ye man ke bi na li ji ni tibɔr. Ubɔjirin si ntimɔsan!.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: N nabum, nim le ye limagil ki jɔɔ limɔril fala a poolin, ki si bilinlem ban mɔɔn Uyudaan a yinlin a taai-n.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Ke naah nyi man ni, ni ye tinyɔɔr le ki sei ban jin fala. Ni gbir Job kaah nan jɔɔ limɔril man u fala a jimin, ki ji kan ni joorim Uwonbɔr kaah tun nan ki sei wa. Uyudaan ye Unyunyunbasann le.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: N nabum, nan jon ni biɛn ye le ke, laa poo paab a naamin bee dulinya bee niba a wan ni. Che a Nn li ye nn, le a Aai mu li ye aai, wee bi laa galiŋi.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Ni ni uba be tibɔmugkaarin naa? Un miɛm Uwonbɔr. Ni ni uba jɔɔ lipoopenl laa? Ni che un gaah ilan ki pag Uwonbɔr.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: Ni ni uba buu? Un yim biwonjiɛm a ninkpeeb wee bin miɛ Uwonbɔr sei u ki sin war nkpam ki jaa ubu Uyudaan a yinlin.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: Bi miɛ nin yada ke, ubaar nmɔn li pɔɔg, Uyudaan li fiin u paab. Ni ye u ŋa tibir le ke, u li kan jiɛn per.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: Mambu, ni kpiir ni bir ki wɔɔn taba, ki li miɛ Uwonbɔr ki tenn taba wee ni kan kutebug. Ubuchinn a wonmiɛl jɔɔ npɔɔn le mitoon yin.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: Elaja nan ye uninfosaal le ke taah ye man nan, u nan miɛ nin linunmanl ke utaal nlaa nu, le utaal kaa sin nan nun kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ki tan kpaa ibin itaa nin iŋmal iloob.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Le u ji nan nan miɛ Uwonbɔr, le utaal nun, le tijiir naan.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: N nabum, ni ye ni ni uba bor kaa nɔɔ ibamɔn a samin, wee uba ta u jiin nin ibamɔn a sam nmɔn yaan ke.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: ni li tiɛr ni niɛ: Wan biɛn fir labir tibirdaan wan borin ki jiin siin ibamɔn a samin yaan ke, udaan fee tibirdaan nmɔn nkum a nɔɔn na, ki li che tibirdaan nmɔn n kan feegab u bir takpemin bu.

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Min Piita wan ye Yisa Masiya a toomin, Le sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tenn Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab ban ye bicham ki be dulinya ni, ki yal kɔɔ ntimin ki be Pɔntusi nin Galantiya nin Kapadosiya nin Asiya nin Batiniya.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: ban wee bi nan gann, wee ti Baa Uwonbɔr pii nan nyi ke bi li gann bin, ŋa bi kasi, Kasi a Naaŋ a toon bu-n, ki che bi chee Yisa a mɔb le u cham n ŋunn bi: Linyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be ni bu takpem.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Ipoor ye Uwonbɔr wan ye ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a Baa-n yaal le! U nyunyunbasanl takpenlin bu ti kan nmaal pɔɔn man be tama kan fo-n, waah nan fiin Yisa Masiya nkumin.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: ki sei ti lifaal lan kaa li nyi buri, bee ki bii bee ki wiiri. Le li be paab ki guu nim.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: ban wee Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm guu ni-n, ni yada bu-n, hali nin feegab a nyɔɔr kaah na li foon, tan ŋa sili ki na li kpiir iyoo yan na yin joorin.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu ni mɔ ni le takpem, hali naah le jin fala kan daandaan nan wee kaa li yuurin a booli biɛn nin yɔli.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: I niɛ nan dan le, ke ni yada kan ŋan jon salima kan burin, hali nŋmi kaah le mann salima kan li fir bii-n gbaa, yada nmɔn li wɔɔn ke ni ye ibamɔn le, ki ta kupag nin jilima dan ni joorimin, ni ye Yisa Masiya nan kpiirin ke.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: Hali naah kaa le kan u nni, amaa ni jiɛ u; nin hali naah kaa le la kan u daandaan nan gbaa, ni ŋaa u yadau, mambu le ni gbee nin nmɔn man kaa jɔɔ magimi.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: nan bu-n ni gaan ni yada a nyɔɔr le, nin ni naam a feeja.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: Ki ta jiin feegab nan paam, bilinlem ban nan pag ke linyunyunbasanl li foon ni ban nin, bi nan feen le nin linunmanl le bi baga be niin takpem.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: ke bin kan iyoo yan nin naah li ŋa man wee Masiya a Naaŋ kan be bi ni nan chinn kan le ki wɔɔn bi Masiya a fala a jim nin kupag kan li ŋɔɔrin.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: Ni nan kpiir ki wɔɔn bi ke baa nan tun tenn biba le, amaa nim. Bilinlemin kaah nan pag tiwann tan wee ni gbirin wee ban mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin mɔɔn ti sei ni Kasi a Naaŋ kan nyan paabin a pɔɔm bu-n. Hali malakam ban bin biɛ tiwann nmɔn le.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Mambu, ni ŋaam ni potiɛl sili ki tun itoon; ni choom niba; ki ta ni tama biɛn ki ŋa linyunyunbasanl lan wee bi na li sei nin, n-yonn man wee Yisa Masiya na yin kpiirin nin.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Naah ye nbuwaab ban jɔɔ limɔcheelin, ni laa ji nɔɔ kɔlisabirug, kan wee naa la nan nyi wee ki nɔɔ nni.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Amaa Uwonbɔr wan yin ni kaah be kasi man nin, ni mu li be kasi nin ni toon biɛn nin na;.

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: ni ma bu ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Ni li be kasiu, nan bu-n min Uwonbɔr be le kasi.

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Nan bu-n naah yin ti Baa wan ji bininfob biɛn a bɔr nin bi toon kaah ye man wee kaa gann nin, ni bu ni che ni binbem li be ke ni ye bicham le dulinya nan yaan ki li san Uwonbɔr bijawaam nin jilimau.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Nan bu-n ni nyi ke naa ye tiwann tan boon ke tikurninann bee salima le Uwonbɔr nan ta mann nkpenim a manug ki fee ni ki nyan nbinben man kaa jɔɔ bu wee ni yaajam nan ta sei ni-n ni.

eng: Instead, you were liberated by the precious blood of Christ, like that of a flawless, spotless lamb.
local: amaa ni nan ye Masiya wan a cham ŋan nin le, Masiya wan ye kipeebig kan kaa jɔɔ igalin bee kidaŋi.

eng: Christ was chosen before the creation of the world, but was only revealed at the end of time. This was done for you.
local: Uwonbɔr nan gann u le ki nin naan dulinya, amaa le ki nan kpiir u wɔɔn idajoorikaa nan yaan nim bu.

eng: who through Christ are faithful to the God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory. So now, your faith and hope should rest in God.
local: Uma bu le ni ŋa Uwonbɔr yada, wan nan fiin Yisa nkumin ki sei u kupag, mambu ni yada a ŋaam nin ni tama be Uwonbɔr ban le.

eng: As you set yourselves apart by your obedience to the truth so that you might have genuine affection for your fellow believers, love each other deeply and earnestly.
local: Naah mann niba kasi wee ki jɔɔ ibamɔn a mɔcheelin wee ki jiɛ ni nabum ibamɔn ni, ni li jiɛ tab nin ni sufu biɛn na.

eng: Do this because you have been given new birth—not from the type of seed that decays but from seed that doesnt. This seed is Gods life-giving and enduring word.
local: Ni ma bu, ni ji kan nmaam jigin na, naa ye biwanboolib ban buri, amaa biwanboolib ban kaa bur, Uwonbɔr a mɔleen yan jɔɔ limiɛfol, ki be iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛmin bu.

eng: Thus, All human life on the earth is like grass, and all human glory is like a flower in a field. The grass dries up and its flower falls off, .
local: Ni ma bu Bininfob biɛn nann timɔɔr le, le bi pag biɛn nann nmɔɔpuum man be kulɔŋ; timɔɔr koor le, le nmɔɔpuum mu lur.

eng: but the Lords word endures forever. This is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.
local: amaa Uyudaan a mɔleen ma le gbaa be le. I ma ye imɔleen yan wee bi nan ta mɔɔn sei ni.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: Mambu, ni jiɛn niwann nan ye kininaŋ nin kularug, nin bilinlarib a toon nin linunchɔril nin kutingbiig a booli biɛn na.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Ni li be ke nbiliɛmin, ki li jiɛ Kinaaŋ a biim man be kasi-n na, wee nin che ni yabig ni feegabin.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: daandaan nan naah len ki biɛ ke Uyudaan ŋan ni.

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: Naah chaan Uyudaan ban nin, wan ye limiɛfol a tangbagil lan wee bininfob yii win, amaa le Uwonbɔr a num gbee u le U gann win.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: ni mu tɔɔl be le ke itangbag yan fo-n, le U ta ni ki ma iniin wee ni li ye kasi a minmanib, wee ni li mann kinaaŋ a manug kan wee Uwonbɔr a num gbee Yisa Masiya bu-n.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: Nan bu-n ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke: Lig, n ta litangbagil liba ki bil Zayɔnn, litangbagil gann kaal lan jɔɔ linungbeel ki ŋmag lifiɛl, le wan gaa u jin biɛn kaa li ji ifa.

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Ki ta jiin nim ban ŋa yada-n paam, litangbagil nmɔn jɔɔ linungbeel. Amaa ban kaa ŋa yada ma ban, Litangbagil lan bitanmaab yii lin nan labir litangbagil lan ŋmag nmaan nni le.

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: nin, Litangbagil lan che bininfob gbiɛr nin litangbagil takpenl lan che bininfob lur. Bi gbiɛr li le, nan bu-n baah kaa chee Uwonbɔr a bɔrin bu, wee ni mu ye Uwonbɔr kaah siin bi man le yen nin.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: Amaa nim ye bininfob gannkaab le, ki ye bininfob ban ye ibɔrniin a minmanib, ki ye kasi a bol, ki ye Uwonbɔr yaab, ke ni pag Uma wan yin ni ki nyan ni libunbɔnlin ki ta ni ŋa ligaril a wonweem n-yaan nin.

eng: Once you werent a people, but now you are Gods people. Once you hadnt received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
local: Isaŋ iba naa nan ye bininfobi, amaa daandaan nan ni ye Uwonbɔr a ninfob le; isaŋ iba naa nan kan linyunyunbasanli, amaa daandaan nan ni kan linyunyunbasanla.

eng: Dear friends, since you are immigrants and strangers in the world, I urge that you avoid worldly desires that wage war against your lives.
local: N jɔtiib, n balin ni le, naah kaa ye dulinya nan yaab wee ki sin be dulinya nan ki ye bichamin, ni jiɛn titoonbir a kɔlisau, tan kɔnn nin ni naamin.

eng: Live honorably among the unbelievers. Today, they defame you, as if you were doing evil. But in the day when God visits to judge they will glorify him, because they have observed your honorable deeds.
local: Ni li be nbinben ŋan nmɔn a bosuu ban kaa ye biwonjiɛmin a sisiigin na, hali baah le galin ni ke ni tun tibirin na, ni biɛn yɔli ni li che bin kan ni toon ŋan ki sei Uwonbɔr kupag lidaal lan wee U nan yin gɔnn ti.

eng: For the sake of the Lord submit to every human institution. Do this whether it means submitting to the emperor as supreme ruler.
local: Ti Yudaau bu, ni li cheel ban wee bininfob siin bi tininkper a pɔɔmin biɛn a mɔɔŋi ya, naah ye ubɔraa wee u pɔɔm jon tininkper a pɔɔm.

eng: or to governors as those sent by the emperor. They are sent to punish those doing evil and to praise those doing good.
local: bee bitinŋmagib, bi ma le u tun bi ke bin dar tibirdam a tuli wee ki pag ban tun itoon ŋan nin.

eng: Submit to them because its Gods will that by doing good you will silence the ignorant talk of foolish people.
local: Nan bu-n ni ye Uwonbɔr a wiib le ke ni tun itoon ŋan nan li loon tijɔri tan kaa nyi niba-n a mɔleen.

eng: Do this as Gods slaves, and yet also as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil.
local: Ni li be ke ban ye bi ba-n, amaa ni laa jɔɔ ye nba a binbem nmɔn ki tun tibiri; ki li be ke Uwonbɔr a toontum kaah be man nin.

eng: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Have respectful fear of God. Honor the emperor.
local: Ni sei uninfo biɛn jilima kan kpaan u: Ni li jiɛ ni wonjiɛn tab a linlaginn na, ni li san Uwonbɔr ki li tenn ubɔr yudaan jilimau.

eng: Household slaves, submit by accepting the authority of your masters with all respect. Do this not only to good and kind masters but also to those who are harsh.
local: Nim ban ye biyomi, ni gaam ni yudam a mɔɔŋi nin jilima biɛn, wee nin laa ye ban ŋan wee bininfob a num gbee bin a binba, amaa hali nin ban a pooi to-n gbaa tɔɔl.

eng: Now, it is commendable if, because of ones understanding of God, someone should endure pain through suffering unjustly.
local: Nan bu-n, ni ye usaal kan nan kpaan a nan wi wee ki sin jɔɔ limɔril waah tiɛr Uwonbɔr bu-n ke, u li kan npagim Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: But what praise comes from enduring patiently when you have sinned and are beaten for it? But if you endure steadfastly when youve done good and suffer for it, this is commendable before God.
local: Amaa a ŋa tibir wee bi booŋ a toonbir nmɔn bu wee a jin fala ke, baa nyɔɔr wee a kan naa? Amaa ni ye a jin fala itoon ŋan bu ki jɔɔ limɔril ke, ni ma le a li kan npagim Uwonbɔr ban.

eng: You were called to this kind of endurance, because Christ suffered on your behalf. He left you an example so that you might follow in his footsteps.
local: Ni nan bu le Uwonbɔr nan yin ni, nan bu-n Masiya nan jin fala ni bu-u, ki wɔɔn ni limagil, ke ni li nɔɔ u tabogi ya.

eng: He committed no sin, nor did he ever speak in ways meant to deceive.
local: Waa nan tun niba a biri, le kularug mu kaa nan nyi nyan u mɔnn ni.

eng: When he was insulted, he did not reply with insults. When he suffered, he did not threaten revenge. Instead, he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
local: Baah nan sugir win, waa nan sug bi ki jiin ni; waah nan jin fala-n, waa nan fann uba, amaa u nan ta u ba le ki ŋa Uwonbɔr wan ji bininfob biɛn a bɔr kaah kpaa man nin a nɔɔn.

eng: He carried in his own body on the cross the sins we committed. He did this so that we might live in righteousness, having nothing to do with sin. By his wounds you were healed.
local: Uma tɔɔl le nan tu ti yaa bir u gbanann nin lidapunpunl bu, wee nin che ti kpo tibir bu ki kan limiɛfol nbuchin binben nin ban; u daŋin bu le ni kan kutebug.

eng: Though you were like straying sheep, you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your lives.
local: Ni nan nann ipee yan bor le, amaa daandaan nan ni jir Upeekpaar nin Wan guu ni tinaamin ban na.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: N nabum, ni takpem nlaa ban ni ŋa ban wɔɔnn ni, nan bu-n, ni pii nyi ke tim ban wɔɔnn nin, a bɔr a jim na li pɔɔ ki jo bininfob biɛn yaal.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Tiwann takpemin, ti biɛn tun ki bura. Ni ye uba kaa nyi ki pag burin u pipagirin ke, u yaadaan ye kasi daan le, ki li fir ŋmag u gbanann nbamɔm.

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Ni ye ti lee itaan a mɔɔŋi itangalig ke yin ŋa taah ban man ke, ti li fir labir bi gbanann ki kpaa taah ban nan paam.

eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Bee ni taam tiŋalin ki ŋa siɛra. Hali tiŋalin kaah le yab man wee ntafaam daar ti chaan, ni biɛn yɔli, amaa kisalaabig le wan donn n jɔɔ donn tiŋalin, le ki jɔɔ chaa waah ban nan paam.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: Lilanbil mu ye kiwanbig man le tigbanann nin, amaa le ki far ligbingbanl takpem. Lig! Nŋmi wama kaah lu timɔɔrin ke min nyɔɔ kutingbɔŋ man.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: Lilanbil mu tɔɔl ye nŋmi le, ki ji ye dulinya nan a bɔbir ki be tigbanann a yagi biɛn, ki tenn tigbanann nmɔn biɛn tijaginn, ki jɔɔ nŋmi nyɔɔr tigbanann nmɔn a binbemin biɛn, le Sintani a ŋmiim man kaa junn nin mu nnyɔɔ lilanbil nmɔn.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Tiwankor a booli nin nnɔɔm a booli nin iwaalab a booli nin iwanfol yan be limɔgilin biɛn nan, uninfo le jɔɔ ni biɛn a pɔɔm.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: amaa uba kaa jɔɔ lilanbil a pɔɔmi. Lilanbil jɔɔ tibir, ki gbee nin tilabir tan ku.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: Lilanbil le ti jɔɔ pagir ti yudaau nin ti Baa, le ki biɛ jɔɔ li ki sugir bininfob, ban wee Uwonbɔr naan bi wee bi nann win.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: Bimɔb biba nmɔn le tibɔŋann nin tibɔbir nya. N nabum, naa kpaan ke ni li ye man ni.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Kinyunbuŋ kiba li fir bun nnyum man mɔ ki ji bun nnyum man ye n-yaam maa?.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: N nabum, bikinkam a sub li fir lo tisaan naa? Bee kigansiig li fir lo ikinkan naa? Man mu le ni ye kinyunbuŋ kan bun n-yaam a nyum kaa li fir bun nnyum man mɔ.

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: Ŋma ye ulandaan nin ubiɛndaan ni sisiigin naa? Ni che un wɔɔn ni u binben ŋam a lam a toon yan ye jiininba a toon nmɔn.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: Amaa ni ye ni jɔɔ lipiiponl nin npooton ni sufu ni ke, ni laa far igbingban Mambu bee ki yii ibamɔn ni.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: Nlam nmɔn a bosu kaa nyan paab a naamin ni amaa mi ye dulinya nan a lam man kaa gbee nin Kinaaŋ ki ye ulinlaril yaamin le.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Nan yaan wee linunchɔril nin kujaŋuuŋ a don nba a potiɛl be-n nin yaan le kuŋmaruŋ nin titoonbir be.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: Amaa nlam man nyan paab a naamin nin, chinchinn mi ma ye kasi ki ye man jɔɔ limɔril nin jiininba, ki gbee nin linyunyunbasanl nin nbinben ŋan, nin ibamɔn a pagim.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: Ban jɔɔ limɔril ki bu tibur nin nposɔɔn nin, bi na li chiɛ nin tibuchinn.

eng: Wives, likewise, submit to your own husbands. Do this so that even if some of them refuse to believe the word, they may be won without a word by their wives way of life.
local: Nim biniinpiib, man mu le ye ni, ni li cheel ni chiɛb a mɔɔŋi ya, ni ye bi ni uba kaa ŋa Uwonbɔr a bɔr yada ke, ni binbem bu bi li ŋa yada, ke naa kpaa talar nin ni pag niba gbaa.

eng: After all, they will have observed the reverent and holy manner of your lives.
local: ni ye bi kan ni kasi nin ni jilima a binbem ke.

eng: Dont try to make yourselves beautiful on the outside, with stylish hair or by wearing gold jewelry or fine clothes.
local: Ni laa che ni ŋinŋam be npaan ni, a li ke ni yukper a lolim bee salima a liɛnbim, bee ikinken ŋan a yeem bu.

eng: Instead, make yourselves beautiful on the inside, in your hearts, with the enduring quality of a gentle, peaceful spirit. This type of beauty is very precious in Gods eyes.
local: Amaa ni che niba a manim li be ni sufu ni, nŋinŋan man kaa wiiri ki be soon nin kinaaŋin, ni ma ye nŋan man wee Uwonbɔr a num gbee takpem.

eng: For it was in this way that holy women who trusted in God used to make themselves beautiful, accepting the authority of their own husbands.
local: Yaayoo ni, kasi a piib ban nan ŋa Uwonbɔr yada-n, man le bi nan ŋaan ki ŋaŋir biba. Bi nan cheel bi chiɛb a mɔɔŋi le.

eng: For example, Sarah accepted Abrahams authority when she called him master . You have become her children when you do good and dont respond to threats with fear.
local: ke Sɛra wan nan chee Abiraham a mɔb wee ki yi u ke u yudaau win. Ni ye Sɛra a bisiɛm le, ni ye ni tun nan ŋan wee kaa san bijawaam ke.

eng: Husbands, likewise, submit by living with your wife in ways that honor her, knowing that she is the weaker partner. Honor her all the more, as she is also a coheir of the gracious care of life. Do this so that your prayers wont be hindered.
local: Nim bipiidam, man mu le ye ni, ni mu li cheel tenn ni piiba, ki li nyi ke bi ye bilanjiim le ni ban, ki li tenn bi jilima ke nim nin bi ma biɛn na li gaa Uwonbɔr a piinŋan yan ye limiɛfolin, wee niba laa li fir legin ni wonmiɛli.

eng: Finally, all of you be of one mind, sympathetic, lovers of your fellow believers, compassionate, and modest in your opinion of yourselves.
local: Nan joor, ni biɛn li jɔɔ nmɔkpaau wee ni baga li be tabin na; ni li jogir taba, ki li jiɛ tab ke nnabumin, ki li san tab kunyunyunbaag nin jiininba.

eng: Dont pay back evil for evil or insult for insult. Instead, give blessing in return. You were called to do this so that you might inherit a blessing.
local: Ni laa jɔɔ tibir ki yɔ tibiri bee ki laa sug ban sug ni ki jiin ni, amaa ni li kaab tikaar ŋan tenn ban kaa ŋaa ni choba, ni niɛ nan bu le Uwonbɔr nan yin ni ke ni nan kan tikaar a faal.

eng: For those who want to love life and see good days should keep their tongue from evil speaking and their lips from speaking lies. .
local: Ni ma bu ni sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin ke, Wan jiɛ limiɛfol wee u num nkan idaa yan ŋan ke, un choom u lanbil tibɔbir a pagimin nin u mɔgbai kularug a leen yin.

eng: They should shun evil and do good; seek peace and chase after it. .
local: Ni kpaa le nin un jiɛn tibir a tum wee ki li tun itoon ŋan; ki li ban nposɔɔn a sam wee ki li nɔɔ mi.

eng: The Lords eyes are on the righteous and his ears are open to their prayers. But the Lord cannot tolerate those who do evil. .
local: Nan bu-n Uyudaan a num paa bibuchim bu le, le ki pelig bi wonmiɛl iyoo biɛn, amaa Uyudaan a num kaa paa ban tun titoonbirin bu.

eng: Who will harm you if you are zealous for good?.
local: Ni ye a num man itoon ŋan a tum bu ke, uba be ki li fir seiŋ kidaŋaa?.

eng: But happy are you, even if you suffer because of righteousness! Dont be terrified or upset by them.
local: Amaa hali ni le ye ni ji fala itoon ŋan bu ke, tikaar be ni bu. Ni laa san bininfob kaah san nan bijawaamin nni; ni laa che ni lam yugiri.

eng: Instead, regard Christ the Lord as holy in your hearts. Whenever anyone asks you to speak of your hope, be ready to defend it.
local: Amaa ni taam Masiya ki ŋa ni sufu ni wee u li ye ni Yudaau. Ki sin li ye ban ŋa sili iyoo biɛn ki guu uninfo wan biɛn li baan ni nan bu wee ni jɔɔ tama. Amaa ni ŋa man nin nbinbem ŋan nin jilima.

eng: Yet do this with respectful humility, maintaining a good conscience. Act in this way so that those who malign your good lifestyle in Christ may be ashamed when they slander you.
local: ki che ni potiɛl li ŋan na, wee ban pag bii ni nin kininaŋ ni binbenŋam Masiya ni-n nji ifa bi tingbiig nmɔn bu.

eng: It is better to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
local: Nan bu-n ni ye ni jin fala itoon ŋan a tum bu wee ni ye Uwonbɔr a bam ke, ni sɔ nin ni ji fala titoonbir bu.

eng: Christ himself suffered on account of sins, once for all, the righteous one on behalf of the unrighteous. He did this in order to bring you into the presence of God. Christ was put to death as a human, but made alive by the Spirit.
local: Nan bu-n nba le Masiya nan kpo ki sei tibirdam biɛn, ubuchin nan gaa ban kaa ye bibuchim a kum ki kpo, ke un che ni foo Uwonbɔr ban. Bi nan ku u le tigbanann ni le u ji kan limiɛfol Kinaaŋ bu.

eng: And it was by the Spirit that he went to preach to the spirits in prison.
local: Kinaaŋin bu le u ji nan jon ki tan mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin ki sei nnaam man be kuloondiigin ni.

eng: In the past, these spirits were disobedient—when God patiently waited during the time of Noah. Noah built an ark in which a few lives were rescued through water.
local: ban nan jɔɔ limɔyiil iyaayoo yan nan jon nin wee Uwonbɔr nan jin limɔril ki guu bi Nowa a yonn, wee Nowa nan be kpee kuŋaluŋin. Kuŋaluŋ nmɔn yaan bininfob wama le nan kɔɔ ki ye bininfob biniin, bi man nan kan nŋmarim nnyum nmɔn yaan.

eng: Baptism is like that. It saves you now—not because it removes dirt from your body but because it is the mark of a good conscience toward God. Your salvation comes through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
local: le nnyum nan, nin si Uwonbɔr a nyunfulin lan fee ni mu gabin a taai yin, kaa ye tigbanann a jaginn a funya, amaa tipoor tan wee ti poo ke ti li jɔɔ nbinben ŋan ki jiin Uwonbɔr paamin bu, li fee ni ki gab Yisa Masiya a kum a fiim bu.

eng: who is at Gods right side. Now that he has gone into heaven, he rules over all angels, authorities, and powers.
local: Yisa wan nan don paab ki be Uwonbɔr a nɔɔjiim paam nin Malakam nin tininkper a pɔɔm nin ipɔɔn biɛn yan cheel u mɔbin.

eng: Therefore, since Christ suffered as a human, you should also arm yourselves with his way of thinking. This is because whoever suffers is finished with sin.
local: Mambu, Masiya kaah nan jin fala u gbanann nin man nin, ni ŋaam sili ki li ŋmag u binbem nmɔn na, nan bu-n wan jin fala u gbanann nin nin diɛ tibir a manuga.

eng: As a result, they dont live the rest of their human lives in ways determined by human desires but in ways determined by Gods will.
local: Naah ŋa man nin, waa che u binbem a joorim dulinya nan yaan ye tigbanann a kɔlisa, amaa Uwonbɔr kaah ban man.

eng: You have wasted enough time doing what unbelievers desire—living in their unrestrained immorality and lust, their drunkenness and excessive feasting and wild parties, and their forbidden worship of idols.
local: Nan bu-n ni nan jin isaŋ takpem le iyoo yan nan jon nin ki tun nan wee ban kaa ye biwonjiɛmin gann ki tun. Bi nan tun nan ye nwanchɔgin toon, nin kɔlisabirug, nin lidanyugbeel, nin lidanyugbeel a toonbir, ki lagin tab ki nyu ndaam gbeeg, ki mann ibawol.

eng: They think its strange that you dont join in these activities with the same flood of unrestrained wickedness. So they slander you.
local: Bi tiɛr ni ye tichann le naah kaa ji kɔɔn nin titoonbir a toon takpemin ni, mambu le bi lagin tisugir ki tunn ni.

eng: They will have to reckon with the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
local: Amaa bi na li pag baah tun man ki sei wan ŋa sili ki li ji ban fo nin ban kpo a bɔrin.

eng: Indeed, this is the reason the good news was also preached to the dead. This happened so that, although they were judged as humans according to human standards, they could live by the Spirit according to divine standards.
local: Ni nan bu le bi nan mɔɔn tibɔŋann nin hali ki sei ban kaa ji be bi miɛfolin daandaan nan nin, wee bin ji bi tibɔr uninfosaal a gbanann bu, amaa le bi li kan limiɛfol kinaaŋin bu.

eng: The end of everything has come. Therefore, be self-controlled and clearheaded so you can pray.
local: Tiwann biɛn a diɛm mala. Mambu ni che ni potiɛri li ween na wee ki li chor ni ba wee ni li fir miɛ Uwonbɔr.

eng: Above all, show sincere love to each other, because love brings about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Nan jon niwann biɛn ye ni li jiɛ tab le nbamɔm, nan bu-n njiɛm deginn tibir takpem bu le.

eng: Open your homes to each other without complaining.
local: Ni li gaan tab kuchɔŋa ki laa ŋulin tab bu.

eng: And serve each other according to the gift each person has received, as good managers of Gods diverse gifts.
local: Uninfo biɛn n taam ipiin yan wee Uwonbɔr sei win, ki ter u kpiɛtiib nbamɔm nin nŋmagŋam Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl kaah ye man balbal.

eng: Whoever speaks should do so as those who speak Gods word. Whoever serves should do so from the strength that God furnishes. Do this so that in everything God may be honored through Jesus Christ. To him be honor and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Ni ye uba pag ke, u li pag tibɔr tan ye Uwonbɔr a mɔleen ya. Ni ye wan yin tun ke, u li tun nin npɔɔn man wee Uwonbɔr ta sei win na, wee niwann biɛn ni Uwonbɔr a yinl n kan kupag Yisa Masiya bu. Kupag nin npɔɔn ye u yaan le hali nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi. Ibamɔn.

eng: Dear friends, dont be surprised about the fiery trials that have come among you to test you. These are not strange happenings.
local: N jɔtiib, ni laa che nin gar ni nin naah ji limagil a nanwi a fala-n ni, a li ke ni ye tiwanchann le ni ban ni.

eng: Instead, rejoice as you share Christs suffering. You share his suffering now so that you may also have overwhelming joy when his glory is revealed.
local: Amaa ni che ni li mɔ na nin naah por ki ji Masiya a fala-n bu, wee ni nan kan nmɔn takpen u pagin a kpiirimin.

eng: If you are mocked because of Christs name, you are blessed, for the Spirit of glory—indeed, the Spirit of God—rests on you.
local: Ni ye bi sug ni Masiya a yinlin bu ke, ni kan tikaara, nan bu-n Kinaaŋ a pag nin Uwonbɔr be nin ni.

eng: Now none of you should suffer as a murderer or thief or evildoer or rebel.
local: Ni ye ni jin fala ke, nin laa ye ke uninfokur a fala a jimi bee unaayig bee wan tun titoonbir biɛn ni, bee wan tugin bininfobi.

eng: But dont be ashamed if you suffer as one who belongs to Christ. Rather, honor God as you bear Christs name. Give honor to God.
local: Amaa uninfo jin fala waah ye uwonjiɛn nbu ke, u laa san ifa, amaa udaan npag Uwonbɔr nin waah jɔɔ uwonjiɛn a yinl nmɔn na.

eng: because its time for judgment to begin with Gods own household. But if judgment starts with us, what will happen to those who refuse to believe Gods good news?.
local: Ni ma bu isaŋ foou nin Uwonbɔr npiin ki ji U niin yaab tibɔra; ke ni ye ni yin piin nin tim ke, ke ban kaa chee Uwonbɔr a bɔŋann nin yaal na li be nii?.

eng: If the righteous are barely rescued, what will happen to the godless and sinful?.
local: Le, Ni ye ni pɔɔ ki sei nin bibuchim nkan nŋmarim ke, ke ban kaa jɔɔ nween nin tibirdam yaal na li be nii.

eng: So then, those who suffer because they follow Gods will should commit their lives to a trustworthy creator by doing what is right.
local: Mambu, ban ji fala Uwonbɔr a bam bu-n bi le li tun nan ŋan na, ki ta biba ki sei bi yada daan wan naan bin ki le li tun nan ŋan iyoo biɛn na.

eng: From Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who received a faith equal to ours through the justice of our God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Min Simɔnn Piita wan ye Yisa Masiya a toontunnu nin u toomin, Le sɔb kugbɔŋ nan ki tenn ban wee Uwonbɔr nin ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a buchin binbem bu che bi kan yada kan ŋan ke ti yaagin:.

eng: May you have more and more grace and peace through the knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.
local: Uwonbɔr a nyunyunbasanl nin nposɔɔn li be ni bu takpem, naah biɛ Uwonbɔr nin ti Yudaau Yisa nbu.

eng: By his divine power the Lord has given us everything we need for life and godliness through the knowledge of the one who called us by his own honor and glory.
local: Waah jɔɔ Uwonbɔr a pɔɔm man nin bu che ti jɔɔ taah ban nan limiɛfolin biɛn nin nwonjiɛn binben taah biɛ u nbu, wan yin ti u baantɔɔl a pag nin u ŋam bu-n.

eng: Through his honor and glory he has given us his precious and wonderful promises, that you may share the divine nature and escape from the worlds immorality that sinful craving produces.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu, le u sei ti imɔsiin takpen yan ŋan wee ni ma bu nin che ni por Uwonbɔr a wonaaŋ a pɔɔm wee ki ŋmar dulinya a ŋmaruŋin kan nyann tibir a kɔlisa-n.

eng: This is why you must make every effort to add moral excellence to your faith; and to moral excellence, knowledge.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu, ni pɔɔn niba ki ta nbinben ŋan ki kpiɛ ni yada ni, ki ji che nbiɛm nkpiɛ nbinben ŋan nin;.

eng: and to knowledge, self-control; and to self-control, endurance; and to endurance, godliness.
local: ki ta choonba ki kpiɛ nbiɛn nin; ki ta nnyiginim ki kpiɛ choonba-n, ki ta nwonjiɛn binben ki kpiɛ nnyiginimin;.

eng: and to godliness, affection for others; and to affection for others, love.
local: ki ta kunabitiig a ŋann ki kpiɛ nwonjiɛn binben nin, ki ta njiɛm ki kpiɛ kunabitiig a ŋann nin.

eng: If all these are yours and they are growing in you, theyll keep you from becoming inactive and unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ni ye nbinben ŋan nan a bosu magir ni ki kpiɛ ke, mi li che naa li walin gurin bee naa li ye ban kaa jɔɔ nyɔɔri, ni biɛm ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya ni.

eng: Whoever lacks these things is shortsighted and blind, forgetting that they were cleansed from their past sins.
local: Amaa ni ye wan kaa jɔɔ nbinben nan ke, udaan a num kaa ga nan fɔgi u jɔɔn na, ki pii tam ke Masiya nan ŋunn u bir kpegirin na.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, be eager to confirm your call and election. Do this and you will never ever be lost.
local: Mambu, n nabum, ni biɛn pɔɔn niba nin linunmanl ki che ni yim nin ni gannimin li ye ibamɔn na. Nan bu-n ni ye ni ŋa man ke, naa li lu.

eng: In this way you will receive a rich welcome into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ.
local: man le li che ni ga ipoor ŋan ki kɔɔ nnaam man kaa jɔɔ diɛm ti Yudaau nin ti feeru Yisa Masiya nin.

eng: So Ill keep reminding you about these things, although you already know them and stand secure in the truth you have.
local: Mambu, n le yin gbaa tiɛr ni le tiwann nmɔn bu, hali naah pii nyi ki si kenken ki baa ibamɔn yan wee ni jɔɔ daandaan nan ni.

eng: I think its right that I keep stirring up your memory, as long as Im alive.
local: N tiɛr ke ni ŋan nin n li ligir ni potiɛl, n miɛfol kaah la be n gbanann nin.

eng: After all, our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me that I am about to depart from this life.
local: nan bu-n naa li yuuri le n ta ti bil ngbaan, ke ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya kaah kpiir wɔɔnim man nin.

eng: Im eager for you always to remember these things after my death.
local: N li pɔɔn nba niwann biɛn nin, ki ŋa nan kpaa talar ke n ŋa, wee ni bɔɔn bu n nan fɔɔr ni ban ke ni li fir tiɛr ni niɛ nan biɛn bu.

eng: We didnt repeat crafty myths when we told you about the powerful coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. Quite the contrary, we witnessed his majesty with our own eyes.
local: Taa nɔɔ nlam a teeni isaŋ yan wee ti nan taah wɔɔn ni ti Yudaau Yisa Masiya a pɔɔm nin u dam a bɔrin ni, amaa ti ye siɛra jib le ban a num nan kan u jilima-n.

eng: He received honor and glory from God the Father when a voice came to him from the magnificent glory, saying, This is my dearly loved Son, with whom I am well-pleased.
local: Nan bu-n u nan kan jilima nin kupag ti Baa Uwonbɔr ban n-yonn man wee, likukɔl nan nyan U jilima a pagin yaan ki len ke, N bija sɔ, wan wee N jiɛ; uma le N num gbee.

eng: We ourselves heard this voice from heaven while we were with him on the holy mountain.
local: Ti baantɔɔl a tuli nan gbir likukɔl nan kaah nyan paab ki len man, tim nin uma kaah nan lagin be kasi a jɔɔl bu-n.

eng: In addition, we have a most reliable prophetic word, and you would do well to pay attention to it, just as you would to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
local: Le ti ta bilinlem a mɔboi yin ki ŋa ibamɔn, ni pɔɔn niba ki mann pel ya, a li ke nwonween kaah wonn libunbɔnlin man nin, ki nan kpaa kutaag kaah na li wonn wee njakpanyiɛn a ŋmabili nwonn ni sufu ni.

eng: Most important, you must know that no prophecy of scripture represents the prophets own understanding of things.
local: Nan jon ni biɛn, ni kpaa le nin ni biɛ ke ilinlen leen yan sɔb Uwonbɔr a gbɔŋin nin kaa nyan ulinlenn baantɔɔl a kar wɔɔn nin ni.

eng: because no prophecy ever came by human will. Instead, men and women led by the Holy Spirit spoke from God.
local: Ni ma bu-n ilinlen leen kaa nan nyan uninfosaal a sufu a bamin le, amaa Uwonbɔr a Kasi a Naaŋ le nan be bininfob ban le ki che bi pag ilinlen leen.

eng: But false prophets also arose among the people. In the same way, false teachers will come among you. They will introduce destructive opinions and deny the master who bought them, bringing quick destruction on themselves.
local: Amaa tibɔkar a linlem mu nan be bininfob nmɔn ban, ke ban wɔɔnn tibɔkar kaah na li be ni mu ban man nin. Bi na li woon ki ta nan kaa ye ibamɔn ki wɔɔn ni, hali ki yii Uyudaan wan jɔɔ npɔɔn biɛn wee ki nan daa bin, ki ban nbiin man yin dan weem ki jaar biba bu.

eng: Many will follow them in their unrestrained immorality, and because of these false teachers the way of truth will be slandered.
local: Bininfob takpem na li nɔɔ bi fai nmɔn a sam, le ki che ibamɔn a samin kaa jɔɔ jilima.

eng: In their greed they will take advantage of you with lies. The judgment pronounced against them long ago hasnt fallen idle, nor is their destruction sleeping.
