local: Le bininkpeebin a ni uba beim ke, Laa mɔri! Lig, wan ye ligbeginl wee ki nyan Juda a maalimin nin, ki ye Defid a yaabilin, u nyaŋ niwann biɛn na. U li fir tabir ndaagim mulile-n ki loor kugbɔŋ kpabkaag nmɔn.

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: Le n kan Kipeebig, le ki nann ke bi kɔr ki le-n, le ki si Kunaangbɔŋin a sisiigin, le tiwann naan wann tan fo tina-n nin bininkpeebin gɔɔn u. Kipeebig nmɔn nan jɔɔ iyinh ilile le nin inunbil ilile, ni ma nan ye Uwonbɔr a naam milile ban wee u nan tun bi dulinya nan a logi ina-n yaan biɛn ni.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: Le u dan ki nan gaa kugbɔŋ kpabkaag nmɔn wan ka kunaangbɔŋin bu a nɔɔjiimin ban.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: Waah nan gaa kugbɔŋ nmɔn ki diɛ-n, le tiwann naan wann tan fo tina-n nin bininkpeeb pilee nin bina-n sɔɔn tum Kipeebig nmɔn a nintogil. Bi biɛn nan jɔɔ tibiɛr le nin salima a yir, le nnyɔgkusim gbee bi sam nmɔn yaan, ki ye Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaabin a wonmiɛl.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
local: Le bi gaah ilan pɔɔn ki ke, Fin le kpaan nin a yuur kugbɔŋ nmɔn ki loor ndaagimin, nan bu-n fin le bi nan kɔr, le a ta a cham ki da bininfob ki sei Uwonbɔr ki nyan imaal biɛn nin ileen biɛn, nin bininfob biɛn nin ibol biɛn ni.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: Le a jin bi nnaam, ki ta bi ŋa biminmanib ke bi li jiɛn ti Wonbɔru, le bi na li kal kunaangbɔŋin bu.

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: Le n lig ki gbir malakam takpem a kukɔli, le bi kanl ye turil turil, nin turpiig a turpiig. Le malakam nmɔn nan gɔɔn kunaangbɔŋin nin tiwann naan wann tan fo tina-n nin bininkpeebin.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: Ki gaah ilan nin ikukɔl yan pɔɔ ki ke, Kipeebig kan wee bi nan kɔrin le kpaan, nin un gaa npɔɔn nin lifaal, nin nlamgɔrikaam, nin ngbananpɔɔn, nin jilima nin kupag nin ndom.

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Le n gbir nan biɛn wee Uwonbɔr nan naan, wee ni be paab, nin kutingbɔŋ nan yaan, nin kitakpiin tiŋ nin nan be nnyumin, nin nan biɛn be i yaan, kaah gaah ilan ki ke, Ndom nin jilima nin kupag nin npɔɔn li be wan ka kunaangbɔŋin bu nin Kipeebigin bu, hali nin isaŋ yan kaa jɔɔ diɛm.

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Le tiwann naan wann tan fo tina-n len ke, Ibamɔn le bininkpeebin sɔɔn tum ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr.

eng: After this I looked and there was a door that had been opened in heaven. The first voice that I had heard, which sounded like a trumpet, said to me, Come up here, and I will show you what must take place after this.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le n nan lig, ki kan paab a naam a naanganl kaah loor ki yaa. Le likukɔl lan wee n pii nan chinn gbir wee li nann kunaatuŋin beim ke, Donn do, wee n wɔɔniŋ nan kpaa talar ki na yin tun ni niɛ nan biɛn a bɔɔn.

eng: At once I was in a Spirit-inspired trance and I saw a throne in heaven, and someone was seated on the throne.
local: Libol nmɔn n nan be nin Kinaaŋ le, ki kan kunaangbɔŋ kaah be paab le uba ka ki bu.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Le wan nan ka ki bu nmɔn nan nyagin le ke sataŋ nin lichabilin. Le uwaakpajatooŋ nan jin kunaangbɔŋ nmɔn ki tan to litangbagil lan nann kisikpinnsiig.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: Le tinaangbann pilee nin tina nan gɔɔn kunaangbɔŋ nmɔn, le bininkpeeb pilee nin bina nan ka ti bu. Bi nan yee tiwann penn le ki chig salima a naam a yuchigirim bi yuli bu.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Le kitajug nyagin ki nyan kunaangbɔŋ nmɔn yaan, nin utaal a dundunl nin titintann. Le filaam bilile si niin ki gaan. Ni niɛ nan le ye Uwonbɔr a naam milile.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: Le niwann niba mu nan be kunaangbɔŋ nmɔn a nintogil ki nann niɛnu a maar tan yaalin ke limɔgilin. Niɛnu a maarin a sisiigin, kunaangbɔŋ nan be, le tiwann naan wann tan fo tina nan be ki gɔɔn ki. Le bi bɔɔn nin bi nintogil biɛn nan ye inunbil.

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: Chinchinn a wanfolin nan nann ugbegin le, le wan kpiɛ milee mu nann unaaja, wan kpiɛ mitaa n mu a num nann uninfosaal, le wan kpiɛ bina-n mu nann libaanweenl lan yugiri paab.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: Tiwann naan wan tan fo tina nmɔn biɛn nan jɔɔ tikpinkpann tiloloob, le bi gbanann biɛn ye inunbil, hali nin bi ŋmiɛn nin na. Le nween nin kunyiɛg biɛn baa jiɛn ni le ki ke, Kasi, kasi, kasi, le ye Uyudaan Uwonbɔr Npɔɔn biɛn daan, wan nan be, ki la be, ki la yin dan.

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: Ni ye tiwann naan wan tan fo tina-n nan sei wan ka kunaangbɔŋ bu ki gbaa be kaa jɔɔ diɛmin kupag, jilima nin ipoor ke.

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: bininkpeeb pilee nin bina-n lur wan ka kunaangbɔŋin bu n yaa taatum le, ki jiɛn uma wan be wee kaa jɔɔ diɛmin. Bi chigir bi naam a yuchigirim le bil kunaangbɔŋin yaan ki len ke:.

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: Ti Yudaau Uwonbɔr, a sin kpaan, a gaa kupag nin jilima nin npɔɔn na nan bu-n fin le nan naan niwann biɛn, a bam bu le bi nan naan niwann biɛn, le ki fo be.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le n kan malakam bina kaah si dulinya nan a logi ina-n yaan ki choo nfaam man nyan ilog ina-n nin ŋmag ke mi laa daar kɔɔ kutingbɔŋin ni bee limɔgilin ni bee ki daar busub bu.

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: Le n ji kan malaka uba le u nyan liwonpɔɔnl paam ki ŋmag Uwonbɔr wan fo-n a wann tabin wann. Le u yin nin nkukɔpɔɔn ki bei malakam bina ban wee bi sei bi npɔɔn ke bin sei kutingbɔŋ nin limɔgilin kidaŋin ke:.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: Ni laa sei kutingbɔŋ bee limɔgil bee isiig kidaŋi, hali nin taah na li ta tiwann tabin wann ki tabin ti Wonbɔru a toontum a yutun bu.

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites.
local: Le n nan gbir baah tabin ibol yan biɛn nyan Iziral a kanlin: Le bi kanl nmɔn nan ye turikobig nin turpina nin turina, ibol yan biɛn nyan Iziral.

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand.
local: Juda a mɔfiigin bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin bi, le Rubenn a mɔfiigin bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin, le Gad a mɔfiigin bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin.

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand.
local: Aser a mɔfiigin bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin, le Nafitali a mɔfiigin bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin, le Manaasa a mɔfiigin bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin.

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand.
local: le Simiɛn a mɔfiigin, bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin, le Liifai a mɔfiigin, bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin, le Isakar a mɔfiigin, bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin.

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
local: le Zebulun a mɔfiigin, bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin, le Josef a mɔfiigin, bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin, le Bɛnjamɛnn a mɔfiigin, bininfob turpiig nin turilee le bi nan tabin.

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le n lig ki kan kuninfogbaluŋ n nintogil ban wee uba kaa li fir kan bi kanli, bi nan nyan ininfobool, nin imaal, nin bininfob, nin ileen biɛn nin le, ki si kunaangbɔŋin ban Kipeebigin a nintogil. Bi nan yee tiwanpenn le ki ŋmag tikpikpagbann bi nɔɔn.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: Le bi mɔɔ nin nkukɔpɔɔn ki ke: Feegab ye Uwonbɔr yaan le, wan ka kunaangbɔŋ bu-n, ki ji ye Kipeebigin yaan le.

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Malakam biɛn nan si le ki gɔɔn kunaangbɔŋin, nin bininkpeebin nin tiwann naan wann tan fo tina-n. Le bi nan lu ki ta bi num chigin tum kunaangbɔŋin a nintogil ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: ki ke: Ibamɔn! Ndom nin kupag, nin nlamgɔrikaam nin kunɔɔpoog nin jilima nin npɔɔn nin ngbananpɔɔn ye ti Wonbɔru yaan le hali nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛmi. Ibamɔn.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Le bininkpeebin a ni uba baanim ke, Ban yee tiwanpenn nan ye ŋmam maa, bee bi nyan laa.

eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Le n jiin ke, N yudaau, fin le nyi. Le u len ke, Bi ma ye ban nyann limug takpenl a poolin; ki ye ban wee bi ta Kipeebigin a cham ki ŋunn biwan yɔɔr le ti nin peen.

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: Mambu, Bi be Uwonbɔr a naangbɔŋin a nintogil le ki tun tenn Uwonbɔr nween nin kunyiɛg u wonjiɛndiigin; le wan ka kunaangbɔŋ bu-n li nyann u nanboŋin ki piin bi bu.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Nkom kaa ji li choo bi; nnyunyum kaa ji li choo bi, nween mu kaa li faa bi, bee nan ton biɛn nin.

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: Nan bu-n Kipeebig kan ka kunaangbɔŋ bu ki be bi sisiigin nin, le yin ye bi peekpaaru; u li ta bi ki jo limiɛfol a nyunbuŋin. Le Uwonbɔr li pir bi num a nyinyinn biɛn.

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: Le n kan Kipeebigin kaah tabir chinchinn a wan tabin waŋ mulile-n a poolin. Le n gbir tiwann naan wann tan fo tina-n a ni uba a kukɔl kaah tann papaab ke utaalin ki ke, Dan.

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: Le n nan lig ki kan utanpeen n nintogil! Le wan jig utaan nmɔn ŋmag butɔm, le bi sei u nnaam a yuchigirig, le u nyan ke unyaŋirin ki sin bon un nyaŋ.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: N-yonn man wee Kipeebigin tabir kuwan tabin waŋ kan kpiɛ milee-n, le n gbir tiwann naan wann tan fo wan kpiɛ milee-n kaah len ke, Dan.

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Le utaan uba ji nyan ki ye utanman. Le wan jig utaan nmɔn nan gaa npɔɔn ke un choo nposɔɔn nyan dulinya nan yaan, ki che bininfob n kɔr tab. Bi nan ta takoobi takpeŋ le ki sei u.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: N-yonn man wee Kipeebigin tabir kuwan tabin waŋ kan kpiɛ mitaa-n, le n gbir kuwan naan waŋ kan fo ki kpiɛ mitaa-n kaah len ke, Dan! Le n lig, ki kan utanbɔn n nintogil! Wan nan jig utaan nmɔn nan ŋmag nan wee bi jɔɔ magir tiwann yɔɔrin tilee le u nɔɔn.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: Le n gbir tifur tiba le ti nann likukɔl ki nyan tiwann naan wann tan fo tina nmɔn a sisiigin ki len ke, Iyoo a kuliba kuba ye utoontunn a ween nba a yɔr le, ikɔɔn mu a kuliba itaa mu ye utoontunn a ween nba a yɔr, amaa laa bii nkpam nin ndaami.

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: N-yonn man wee Kipeebigin tabir kuwan tabin waŋ kan kpiɛ mina-n, le n gbir kuwan naan waŋ kan fo ki kpiɛ mina-n a kukɔl kaah len ke, Dan.

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: Le n lig ki kan utanpeen lalag! Le wan jig utaan nmɔn a yinl ye Nkum, le Kitakpiin tiŋ yaab nɔɔ u bɔɔn ki mal u. Le Uwonbɔr sei bi npɔɔn ke bin ku dulinya nan a ninfob nbɔgirim mina a poolin nkerim nbau, ke bin ta takoobi, nin nkom, nin iwiɛn, nin dulinya nan a wan bir ki ku bi.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: Waah nan tabir kuwan tabin waŋ kan kpiɛ miŋmu-n, le n kan ban wee bi nan kɔrin a tinnaam kumanug a binbinlin a nyagilin, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a mɔleen nin siɛra kan wee bi nan ŋmagin.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: Le bi nan yin nin nkukɔpɔɔn ki ke, Uyudaan wan jɔɔ npɔɔn biɛn, fin wan ye kasi nin ubamɔndaan, ni yin yuur ki tan kpaan niin a tarig wee a ji dulinya nan yaab tibɔr ki jiin ti cham a pɔnlaa.

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Le bi nan sei bi biɛn daalapeen fufɔbir, ki bei bi ke bin guur wamau hali nin bitoontum tab nin bi nabum ban wee bi na yin ku ke baah nan ku bi miɛnin man nin a kanlin nan foo.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: Le n lig ki kan waah tabir kuwan tabin waŋ kan kpiɛ miloobin. Le kutingbɔŋ dam takpem. Nween labir libunbɔnl ki nann botu a kinkenl lan wee bi ta tiŋɔɔkor ki naan nin, le uŋmal mu mann ke nchamin.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: le iŋmabil nyan titilangbann nin ki nan lu tum, a li ke ntafapɔɔrim kaah ti ŋa ke mi daar bikinkam a sub wee iloonkal ker lur man nin.

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: Le kitilangbɔŋ kpab kpab ke kugbɔŋ kpabkaagin, le ijɔɔ nin igungon yan be nnyum a sisiigin fɔɔr i sisibol.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Le dulinya a bɔrtiib, nin nbɔrbum, nin bitɔtogib a sanliib, nin biwandam, nin ininfojal, nin biyom biɛn nin ban kaa ye biyom woo titangbafiɛrin nin titanpirin.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: Le bi yin ijɔɔ nin titanpirin ki len ke, Ni lu ti bu ki woon ti ni wee wan ka kunaangbɔŋin bu-n laa ji kan ti, wee Kipeebigin mu a ŋuufiiŋin laa pii ti!.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: Nan bu-n, bi ŋuul a fiim a daal takpenl nmɔn foon na, ke ŋma li fir siraa.

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ miŋmu mu pee u naatuŋ, le n kan liŋmabil kaah nyan kitilangbɔŋ bu ki nan lu tum. Le bi sei liŋmabilin libogil lan nyɔɔ kaa jɔɔ tarig nmɔn a piim.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: N-yonn man wee u loor libogil lan nyɔɔ kaa jɔɔ tarigin, le tikpinnyir nyan niin ki nann nŋmi takpen a kpinnyir, le tikpinnyir nmɔn nan bɔŋ nween nin titilangbann.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: Le itɔɔn nan nyan tikpinnyir nmɔn yaan ki lunn tum, le Uwonbɔr sei bi npɔɔn ke inɔɔn kaah jɔɔ npɔɔn man dulinya nan yaan nin.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: Uwonbɔr nan bei bi ke bi laa sei dulinya nan a mɔɔr bee tiwann bu wan bee isiigi kidaŋi, amaa bin sei bininfob ban kaa jɔɔ Uwonbɔr a daagim bi yutun bu-n a binba.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: Uwonbɔr kaa nan sei bi npɔɔn ke bin ku bininfobi, amaa ke bin mugir bi ki tan kpaa iŋmal iŋmu. Ntiton man wu ke unɔɔn kaah jun uninfo ke ni wu man nin.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: Iyaayoo bininfob na li ban nkum, amaa le kaa li kan ni; bi na li ban bin kpo amaa le nkum na li guun ki jiɛn bi.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: Itɔɔn nmɔn nan nann itaan yan wee bi ŋai bitɔb a sili-n le. Bi nan chig niba bi yuli bu le ni nann salima a naam a yuchigirig, le bi num paam nann bininfosaalib a num paam.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: Bi kor nan nann bipiib a yukper le, le bi nyin mu nann igbegin a nyin.

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: Bi nan jɔɔ ngbingban tegirim man nann tikur a gbingban tegirim le, le bi kpinkpann fu ke itaan takpem kaah dar torokom ki chaa bitɔb a tom ke ni fu man nin le.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: Bi jɔɔ ijoo le ki junn ke inɔɔn nin, bi jɔɔ npɔɔn bi jo-i nmɔn yaan ki li fir mugir bininfob iŋmal iŋmu.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Bi nan jɔɔ wan ye ubɔr le u ye libogil nmɔn yaan a malaka, ki ye ubiir.

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: Chinchinn a nan bir ma jon na; nan bir milee la chaan.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ miloob mu pee u naatuŋ, le n gbir likukɔl liba nyan salima a binbinlin lan be Uwonbɔr a nintogilin.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: Le likukɔl nmɔn bei malaka wan kpiɛ miloob ki jɔɔ kunaatuŋin ke, Lor malakam bina ban wee bi lol bi nkpen takpen man wee bi yi ke Yufiratiisi n yaan ki jaau.

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: Le u nan lor malakam ban pii nan ŋa sili ki guu isaŋ, nin lidaal, nin li ŋmalu, nin libinl nmɔn ke bin ku bininfob a bɔgirim nkerim mitaa a kerim nba-n.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: Le n gbir bitɔtogib ban jig itaan nin a kanl, le bi kanl nan ye tur a tur a kobilee. N gbir bi kanl la.

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: Maah nan kan itaan nin bitanjigib nmɔn nin, n zii ni, ni nan nann nan le: Bitanjigibin nan jɔɔ bigbingban tegirim man man ke nŋmi-n, ki be ke buginu, ki ye nduuyom a li ke lichibilin. Le itaan nin a yuli nann igbegin a yuli, le nŋmi nin tikpinnyir nin lichibil nyan bi mɔɔŋin ni.

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: Le imag itaa yan ye nŋmi nin tikpinnyir nin lichibil yan nyan itaan a mɔɔŋi ni nan ku bininfob a bɔgirim nkerim mitaa a kerim nba.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: Itaan nin a pɔɔm nan be bi mɔɔŋin nin bi joi ni le, nan bu-n bi joi nan nann iwaa le, ki jɔɔ iyul, le bi jɔɔ junn bininfob.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: Le bininfob ban gur wee imag nan kaa ku bi nan, kaa nan chee ke bin kpenn ki ta bitɔɔl kaah maa nan nin ki gabi, baa nan ta npɔniim a jiɛnim ki jiɛn ni, nin ibawol yan wee bi ta salima ki maa i, nin salima nin tikur ninann nin tikurdiin nin itangbag nin tidaar, kaa chee bin ta bi bawoli yan kaa gaa bee kaa gbiri bee kaa choon nin gabi.

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Le kaa chee kpenn bi binben ki jiɛn lininfokuli, nin inyɔg a toon, nin iwanchɔgin toon, nin nnaayigimin.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: Le n ji kan malaka takpeŋ uba kaah nyan paab ki chaa jiirin. U nan yee kitilangbɔŋ le ke likinkenlin, le uwaakpajatooŋ be u yul a paab; le u num paam nann nween, le u taai mu nann nŋmipeen.

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: U nan ŋmag kigbanbig kpabkaag le, le ki kpar ki be u nɔɔn, le u ta u nɔɔjiim a taal ki siin limɔgilin, le u nɔɔgam a taal mu si limɔgil nmɔn a dool.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: Le u tiɛn paab takpem ke ugbegin nin. Waah tiɛn man ki diɛ, le utaal a tintann a kukɔli nkerim milile-n pag.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: Utaal a tiɛm kerim milile nmɔn kaah pag ki diɛ, le n ŋa sili ke n sɔb; amaa le n ji gbir likukɔl liba nyan paab ki len ke, Li ŋmag utaal kaah tiɛn nkerim milile man nin a sufu-n na, wee ki laa sɔb ti ki bili.

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Le n kan malaka wan a taaliba tɔɔ limɔgil a nyum bu wee litɔ mu tɔɔ limɔgil a doolin kaah don u nɔɔjiim paab.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: Le u poo Uwonbɔr wan be ki le gbaa be-n ke, wan nan naan paab nin nan biɛn be bi ni, nin dulinya nin nan biɛn be dulinya ni, nin limɔgil nin nan biɛn be limɔgilin ki len ke, Nguurim kaa ji be!.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: Amaa idaa yan wee malaka wan kpiɛ bilile-n na yin pee u naatuŋ ke, Uwonbɔr a wool a bɔr li gbee, a li ke waah nan bei u toontum bilinlemin man nin.

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Le likukɔl lan wee n bi gbir wee li nyan paabin ji beim ke, Li chaa, ki tan gaa kugbɔŋ kpabkaag kan kpar ki be malaka a nɔɔn wee u tɔɔ nyum nin limɔgil a dool bu-n.

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Le n jon malaka nban ki tan bei u ke un ta kigbanbig kpabkaag nmɔn ki seim ni. Le u beim ke, Gaa ki ki ŋman na. Ni yin mɔ a mɔnn le ke tiseerin, amaa le ki mɔŋ a poolin.

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: Le n gaa kigbanbig kpabkaag nmɔn u nɔɔn ki ŋman. Le ki sin mɔ n mɔnn ke tiseerin, amaa maah nan ŋman diɛ le ki mɔŋ n poolin.

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: Le bi nan beim ke, Ni kpaa le nin a pag ilinlen leen ki jiin bininfob, nin ibol, nin ileen balbal, nin bibɔrtiib paam takpem.

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Le bi ta ligbingbaanl lan nann kijaangbiɛg wee bi jɔɔ magir tiwann nin ki seim, ki beim ke, Li chaa ki tan mag Uwonbɔr a wonjiɛn niin nin i binbinlin na, wee ki kan biwonjiɛn ban be niin nin.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: Amaa laa mag iwonjiɛn niin a loobin ni, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr ta nloob nmɔn ki sei ban kaa ye biwonjiɛmin na. Bi na li chonn ki taan kasi a tiŋ nmɔn bu ki tan kpaan iŋmal pina nin ilee.

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: Le n na li sei n siɛradam bilee nmɔn npɔɔn wee bin pag ilinlen leen ki tan kpaa iweehn turil nin kobilee nin piloob ki yee botu a kinkenl.

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: Siɛradam bilee nmɔn le ye Olif a siigi ilee-n nin fila a tutɔɔnbol a kerim milee-n, yan si kutingbɔŋ a Yudaau a nintogilin.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Ni ye uba nan ban un sei bi kidaŋ ke, nŋmi li nya bi mɔnn ki nan nyɔɔ bi dataam nmɔn ki ku bi. Wan biɛn nan ban un sei bi kidaŋ ke, ni kpaa le nin udaan nkpo man.

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: Siɛradam nmɔn jɔɔ npɔɔn man li fir choo utaal ke waa li nu n-yonn man wee bi be ki pag ilinlen leen nin; ki ji jɔɔ npɔɔn man li fir che nnyum nkpann ncham, ki ji li fir che imag a booli biɛn dan dulinya bu baah ban man nin.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: N-yonn man wee bi nan diɛ bi siɛra a toon nmɔn ke, kuwanbiigin na li nyann libogil lan nyɔɔ wee kaa jɔɔ tarigin yaan ki to bi bitɔb ki nyaŋ ki ku bi.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: Bi gbanann na li dɔɔn kitintakpeŋ a sanjal bu, ntim man wee pag woon a poolin bi yi mi ke Sodom nin Ijibir , nan yaan mu jigin wee bi nan kpaa bi Yudaau lidapunpunl bu-n.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Le bininfob na li nyan imaal biɛn, nin ileen balbal biɛn, nin ibol biɛn nin, ki nan lig bi gbanann nmɔn iweehn mun itaa nin ligenl amaa kaa yin chee bin pii tigbanann nmɔn ni.

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Kutingbɔŋ nan a ninfob a pooi na li penn bi bilee nmɔn a kum bu, ki gel igel ki sei bi kpiɛtiib ipiin, nan bu-n bilinlem bilee nmɔn che ban be dulinya nan yaan jin fala pam.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: Iweehn itaa nin ligenl nmɔn kaah nan jon a bɔɔn, le kufoorug kan ye limiɛfolin nyan Uwonbɔr ban ki nan kɔɔ bi, le bi fii sir paab, le bijawaam choo ban kan bin takpem.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: Le siɛradam bilee nmɔn gbir nkukɔpɔɔn kaah nyan paab ki bei bi ke, Ni donn paab do. Le bi don kitilangbɔŋin ki jon paab, le bi dataam nan si ki lig bi.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Iyaayoo nmɔn le kutingbɔŋ dam takpem, le kitiŋ nmɔn a niin piig a poolin nkerim nbaba lu, le bininfob turilile nan kpo kutingbɔŋ nmɔn a damim ni, le bijawaam nan choo ban gurin, le bi sei Uwonbɔr wan be paabin kupag.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: Ni lig! Niwanbirin nan kpiɛ milee-n jon na; niwanbirin nan kpiɛ mitaa-n li foon ke naa yuuri.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ milile mu pee u naatuŋ, le ikukɔpɔɔr be paab ki tann ki len ke: Dulinya a naam kpann ti Yudaau nin U Masiya yaala, le u li ŋmag u naam hali nin isaŋ yan kaa jɔɔ diɛm.

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: Le bininkpeeb pilee nin bina ban nan ka bi naangbann bu Uwonbɔr a nintogilin lu ki ta binum chigin tum ki poor Uwonbɔr.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: ki len ke: Ti tenniŋ kunɔɔpoog, Uyudaan Uwonbɔr wan jɔɔ npɔɔn biɛn, Wan pii nan be ki le la biɛ nin be, nan bu-n a gaa a pɔɔm man yaba le ki piin a naam a ŋmagim.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: Le ibol a ŋuui fii; le A ŋuufiiŋ foon. Isaŋ foou nin bitakpiib a bɔr a jima nin A yɔ A toontum ban ye bilinlemin, nin A ninfogannkaab nin ban tenn A yinl jilima-n, ban gbee bininfob, nin ban kaa gbee bininfobi, ki bii ban bii dulinya.

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Le Uwonbɔr a wonjiɛn niin yan be paabin loor, le n kan Uwonbɔr a kaal adaka iwonjiɛn niin a poolin. Le utaal a jugin nyagin ki dun ki tann, le kutingbɔŋ dam, le itanyunbɔl gbingbem nan lunn.

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: Le kutunwiɛŋ nan sir limɔgil a dool. Le n kan kuwanbiig kaah chaa nyan limɔgilin. Ku nan jɔɔ iyinh piig le nin iyul ilile, ki chig nnaam a yuchigirim piig u yin bu, le iyin yan sugir Uwonbɔr nan sɔb paa u yuli nmɔn biɛn bu.

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: Maah nan kan kuwanbiig kan nmɔn nan nann unamuun le, amaa le u taai nann ke ubakar a taai, le u mɔb nann ligbeginl a mɔb. Le kutunwiɛŋ ta u pɔɔm, nin u naangbɔŋ nin tininkper a pɔɔm ki sei kuwanbiigin.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: Kuwanbiig nmɔn a yul liba bu nan nann le ke li kan kidaŋ takpem le-n, amaa kidaŋ takpeŋ nmɔn a biɛl nan bugira. Ni nan gar dulinya yaab le bi nɔɔ kuwanbiig nmɔn.

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Le bininfob nan jiɛn kutunwiɛŋ nmɔn waah ta tininkper a pɔɔm ki sei kuwanbiigin nbu, le bi ji nan jiɛn kuwanbiigin mu ki len ke, Ŋma nann kuwanbiig nan naa? Ŋma be ki li fir to kuwanbiig nan bitɔbaa.

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: Le kuwanbiig nmɔn nan kan bimɔb ki pag kalanbaani a leen nin tisugir ki ta u ninkper a pɔɔm ki tun ki tan kpaa iŋmal pina nin ilee.

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: Le u nan yaar u mɔb ki sugir Uwonbɔr, ki gbii kutingbiig U yinl nin nan yaan wee U be, nin ban be paab.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Bi nan sei u npɔɔn ke un kɔnn Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab wee ki nyaŋ bi. Le bi ji sei u tininkper a pɔɔm ke u li ye imaal, nin bininfob, nin ileen, nin ibol biɛn.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: Bininfob ban biɛn be dulinya nan yaan wee bi yin kaa sɔb ŋa Kipeebig kan wee bi nan ku-n a miɛfol a gbɔŋin yaan dulinya nan a piimin nin, bi biɛn nan yin jiɛn kuwanbiig nmɔn le.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen.
local: Wan jɔɔ litul ke, ni che un gbir.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: Wan ye bin nan choo u tiyomir ke, see ki bi nan choo u tiyomir wan ye bi nan ta takoobi ki ku u ke, takoobi nmɔn le bi yin ta ki ku u. Mambu ni ŋan nin Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab li jɔɔ limɔril nin nyiginim ki ŋa Uwonbɔr yada nin ibamɔn.

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: Le n ji kan kuwanbiig kuba kaah nyan dulinya nan yaan. U nan jɔɔ iyin ilee le a li ke kipeebig, amaa le ki pag a li ke kutunwiɛŋin.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: Le ki sir chinchinn a wanbiig a ninkper a pɔɔm ki che dulinya a ninfob biɛn jiɛn chinchinn a wanbiigin, kan nan kan kidaŋ wee ni bugir.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: Le kuwanbiig nmɔn ŋa ligaril a siɛra takpenl, hali ki che nŋmi nyan paab ki lunn nin le bininfob kan mi npaan nin.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: Chinchinn a wanbiigin kaah sei u npɔɔn man wee u si u taalin ki fir ŋa siɛra a toon yin, ni bu che u fir lar dulinya yaaba. Le ki bei bi ke bin maa litingbanl nin jilima lan nann chinchinn a wanbiigin kan nan kan takoobi a daŋ amaa wee kaa kpe-n.

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: Le Uwonbɔr nan sei kuwanbiig kan kpiɛ milee nmɔn npɔɔn ke kin foor jaa litingbanl lan jɔɔ chinchinn a wanbiigin a nunaŋ, wee lin fir pag wee ban yii kaah jiɛn li ke lin ku bi.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: Le u mu ji mug bininfob biɛn, ban kaa gbee bininfob nin ban gbee bininfob, ban jɔɔ nin ban kaa jɔɔ, ban ye biba nin ban ye biyom, ke un daag bi nɔɔjiim bee bi yutun bu ndaagim.

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: wee nin che uba laa ji fir da niba bee ki kɔɔr niba wee naa ye udaan jɔɔ kuwanbiig nmɔn a daagim le, ndaagim man ye u yinl nin u kanl.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: Ni niɛ nan ban nlamgɔrikaam le. Ni ye uba jɔɔ nlam ke, ni che un kanh tiwanbir a kanl, nan bu-n ni ye uninfo a kanl le. U kanl le ye kobiloob nin piloob nin iloob le.

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: N-yonn man wee u nan tabir kuwan tabin waŋ kan kpiɛ milile-n, le paab nan ŋmin chii ki tan kpaa minti pita.

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Le n nan kan malakam bilile-n kaah si Uwonbɔr a nintogil, le Uwonbɔr sei bi tinaatunn tilile.

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: Le malaka uba nan ŋmag nnyɔgkusim a salima a saŋ ki tan sir kumanug a binbinl ban. Le bi sei u nnyɔgkusim takpem ke un kpiɛ Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaabin bu bi wonmiɛlin, le u ta paan salima a binbinlin bu kunaangbɔŋin a nintogil.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: Le nnyɔgkusimin a kpinnyir, nin Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab a miɛm nyan malaka nan a nɔɔn ki don paab.

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Le malaka nmɔn yuur kisaŋin, ki mɔɔn nŋmi kumanug a binbinlin ki gbeen kisaŋin, ki kpaar yaal kutingbɔŋ bu; le utaal dun ki tann ki nyagin le kutingbɔŋ dam.

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Le malaka bilile ban ŋmag tinaatunn tilile-n ŋa sili ke bin pee bi naatunn nmɔn.

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: Le chinchinn a malaka pee u naatuŋ, le itanyunbɔl nin nŋmi ŋmarin nin ncham ki lunn kutingbɔŋ nan bu. Le kutingbɔŋ bɔgir nkerim mitaa le nkerim nba gaa nŋmi, le isiig bɔgir nkerim mitaa le nkerim nba gaa nŋmi, le timɔsɔɔn biɛn gaa nŋmi.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ milee mu pee u naatuŋ, le niwann niba nann lijɔɔl takpenl le nŋmi gaan li bu, le bi ta li ki gab limɔgilin. Limɔgil nmɔn a bɔgirim mitaa le nkerim nba nan kpann ncham.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: le limɔgil nmɔn a wanfoli a bɔgirim mitaa, le nkerim nba kpo, le tiŋalin a bɔgirim mitaa, le nkerim nba bii.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ mitaa-n mu pee u naatuŋ, le liŋmabijal lan yab ki ween ke limumɔl a ŋmiimin nyan kitilangbɔŋ bu ki nan lu ikpen nin nnyunbum a bɔgirim mitaa-n a kerim nba ni.

eng: The stars name is Wormwood, and a third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the water, because it became so bitter.
local: liŋmabil nmɔn a yinl le nan ye Kpajarig. Le nnyum nmɔn a bɔgirim mitaa-n a kerim nba kpann kunyuntuntoŋ, le kunyuntuntoŋ nmɔn nan ku bininfob takpem.

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ mina-n mu pee u naatuŋ, le bi faa nween nin uŋmal nin iŋmabil biɛn le bi wonweem a bɔgirim nkerim mitataa a kerim nbaba bɔŋ. Lidaal a bɔgirim a kerim mitaa, nkerim nba kaa nan jɔɔ nwonween ni, kunyiɛg mu a bɔgirim a kerim mitaa-n, nkerim nba mu nan ye man le.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: Iyaayoo le n lig, ki gbir libaanweenl kaah be paab ki laan ki mɔr nkukɔpɔɔn ki ke: Ni li bir! Ni li bir! Ni li bir ki sei dulinya, nan bu-n malakam bita ban gur wee ki la yin pee bi naatunn nbu.

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: Le n lig ki kan Kipeebig n nintogil le ki si Zayɔnn a jɔɔl bu, le bininfob turikobig nin turpina nin turina kaah si u ban, ban wee bi nan sɔb u yinl nin u Baa a yinl ki tabin bi yutun bu-n.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: Le n nan gbir likukɔl kaah nyan paab, le li fur nann nnyum man puu a fur bee utaal kaah ti tann man ni. Maah nan gbir likukɔl lan nmɔn a fur nan nann ke bibiɛfaab kaah far bi biɛr le-n.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Le bi nan gaah ilan pɔɔn kunaangbɔŋin nin tiwann naan wan tan fo tina-n nin bininkpeebin a nintogil. Uba kaa nan li fir ki tinn ilan nmɔn ni, see bi ma bininfob turikobigin nin turpina nin turina ban nan kan nŋmarim dulinya nan yaan nin a binba.

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
local: Bi ma ye ban kaa bii biba lipiibanlin ni, nan bu-n bi ŋmag biba le chasi. Bi nɔɔ Kipeebigin kaah chaa nan yaan biɛn le. Bi ma ye ban wee Uwonbɔr nan da bininfob a sisiigin, ke bin ŋa chinchinn a ninfob ki sei Uwonbɔr nin Kipeebigin.

eng: No lie came from their mouths; they are blameless.
local: Bi mɔɔŋi kaa nan nyi por tibɔkari; baa nan jɔɔ igalin ni.

eng: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead with eternal good news to proclaim to those who live on earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
local: Le n kan malaka uba kaah be paab ki yugir, ki ŋmag tibɔŋann tan kaa diɛrin ke un pag sei bininfob ban be kutingbɔŋ nan yaan, ibol, nin imaal, nin ileen nin bininfob biɛn.

eng: He said in a loud voice, Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship the one who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.
local: Le u pag nin nkukɔpɔɔn ke, Ni li san Uwonbɔr wee ki li tenn U kupaga, nan bu-n U bɔjil a yoon foou. Ni li jiɛn wan nan naan paab, nin kutingbɔŋ, nin limɔgil nin nnyunbuma.

eng: Another angel, a second one, followed and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She made all the nations drink the wine of her lustful passion.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ milee nɔɔ u bɔɔn ki len ke, Ki lu! Babilɔnn kan ye kitiŋ takpeŋ wee ki nan mug bininfob a booli biɛn wee bi nyun ndapɔɔrim ke u wanchɔgin toon yin luu.

eng: Then another angel, a third one, followed them and said in a loud voice, If any worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or their hands.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ mitaa mu nɔɔ bi bɔɔn ki len ke, Ni ye uba jiɛn kuwanbiigin nin kuwanbiig nmɔn a nunaŋ wee ki gaa ki daagim u yutunl bu bee u nɔɔ bu ke.

eng: they themselves will also drink the wine of Gods passionate anger, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath. They will suffer the pain of fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and the Lamb.
local: udaan mu li nyu Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ a daam man wee U minn ŋa U ŋuufiiŋ a dapɔɔrim a yiigin. Bi na li ta lichibil a ŋmiim ki mugir udaan malakam ban be kasi nin Kipeebigin a nintogil.

eng: The smoke of their painful suffering goes up forever and always. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, and those who receive the mark of its name.
local: Le bi fala a jim a ŋmiim a kpinnyir na li dug ki do paab hali nin isaŋ yan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm. Lifooril kaa na li be nween bee kunyiɛgin ki sei ban jiɛn kuwanbiigin nin kuwanbiig nmɔn a nunaŋin, bee ban ga kuwanbiig nmɔn a yinl a daagimi.

eng: This calls for the endurance of the saints, who keep Gods commandments and keep faith with Jesus.
local: Ni niɛ nan bu ni kpaan nin Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab ban chee Uwonbɔr a siim wee ki ŋa Yisa yada-n li ye ban li jɔɔ limɔril nin nnyiginim.

eng: And I heard a voice from heaven say, Write this: Favored are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit, so they can rest from their labors, because their deeds follow them.
local: Le n gbir likukɔl nyan paab ki len ke, Sɔba: Tikaar be bitakpiib ban kpo nin Uyudaan nin, naah nyan daandaan nan ki jɔɔ chaa. Le Kinaaŋ len ke, Nn, bi li foor bi baginn nin, bi ŋaar nmɔn li nɔɔ bi.

eng: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud. On the cloud was seated someone who looked like the Human One. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
local: Le n nan lig ki kan kitalangbɔŋ peŋ, le uba ka niin ki nann Uninfosaal a bija ki chig salima a naan yuchigirig, le ki ŋmag gɔrug kaaŋ u nɔɔn.

eng: Another angel came out of the temple, calling in a loud voice to the one seated on the cloud: Use your sickle to reap the harvest, for the time to harvest has come, and the harvest of the earth is ripe.
local: Le malaka uba mu ji nyan iwonjiɛn niin ki tiɛn paab ki yin wan ka kitalangbɔŋin yaan nin ke, Taam a gɔrug ki chiɛ, nan bu-n nchiɛm a saŋa foo, nan bu-n kutingbɔŋ a dii beer foo nchiɛma.

eng: So the one seated on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
local: Le wan nan ka kitilangbɔŋ bun ta u gɔrug ki fiɛn fiɛn kutingbɔŋ bu, le ki chiɛ kutingbɔŋ.

eng: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.
local: Le malaka uba mu ji nyann paab a wonjiɛn niin yin, le ki mu ŋmag gɔrug kaaŋ.

eng: Still another angel, who has power over fire, came out from the altar. He said in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, Use your sharp sickle to cut the clusters in the vineyard of the earth, because its grapes are ripe.
local: Le malaka wan guu nŋmi mu ji nyann libinbinl n-yaan, ki yin nkukɔpɔɔn ki bei malaka wan ŋmag gɔrug kaaŋin ke, Taam a gɔrug kaaŋin ki lagin igbiin bon yan be kutingbɔŋ nan a gbiinsiig bu-n na, nan bu-n ki gbiin mann na.

eng: So the angel swung his sickle into the earth, and cut the vineyard of the earth, and he put what he reaped into the great winepress of Gods passionate anger.
local: Le malaka nmɔn ta u gɔrug ki fiɛn fiɛn kutingbɔŋ bu, le ki lagin kutingbɔŋ a gbiin ki gab baah ŋmaŋ Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ a silooi a daam nan ban nin.

eng: Then the winepress was trampled outside the city, and the blood came out of the winepress as high as the horses bridles for almost two hundred miles.
local: Le bi nan nyan kitiŋ nmɔn a gbaam ki kɔɔ baah taan isiloon ki toog ndaam nan yaan nin, le ncham nyan ndaam a toon bolin ki puu, ki do paab ki tan yegin itangalig a tarig maal kobig nin piniin nin .

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: Le lijinjeel nin ligaril a siɛra takpenl nan degirin paabin: Le upii uba nan yee nween a li ke likinkenlin, le uŋmal be u taatum, le u chig nnaam a yuchigirig kan jɔɔ iŋmabil piig nin ilee u yul bu.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: U nan jɔɔ lipool le ki kuun imariwiɛn kaah choo u nbu.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Le siɛra liba mu ji degirin paab a naamin ni: Ki ye kutunwiɛŋ kan man ki jɔɔ iyul ilile nin iyinh piig, le nnaam a yuchigirig milile chig iyul nmɔn bu.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: Le kutunwiɛŋ nmɔn ta u jool ki tigin iŋmabil a bɔgirim a poolin mitaa nkerim nba ki jaa kutingbɔŋin, le kutunwiɛŋ nmɔn jon tan si upii wan yin maa nmɔn a nintogil ki guu, ke upii nan maa ke un ŋman kibiliɛgin.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: Le upii nmɔn nan maa kibijag, kibijag kan na yin ta kukurug a jaangbiɛg ki ŋmag dulinya yaab biɛn ni. Le Uwonbɔr ta u don paab u naangbɔŋin bu.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Le upii nmɔn guun ki jon kukunkɔɔg a paab, nan yaan wee Uwonbɔr nan mann npaan ki bil guur upii nmɔn ke bi li lig u ki tan kpaa iweehn turil nin iweehn kobilee nin iweehn piloob.

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Bitɔb le nan be paab. Le Maakil nin u malakam nan ton kutunwiɛŋ, le kutunwiɛŋ nin u mu a malakam ton jiin.

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: Amaa kutunwiɛŋ a pɔɔm kaa nan yabi, le bi nyaŋ bi, le bi lann binbebol paab.

eng: So the great dragon was thrown down. The old snake, who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world, was thrown down to the earth; and his angels were thrown down with him.
local: Le bi nyan kutunwiɛŋ takpeŋ nmɔn gab, u pii ye uwaa wan nan be le yaayoo-n, wee bi yi u ke ulinlaril bee Sintani, wan liir dulinya yaab a sam ki che bi borin, le bi nyan uma nin u malakam ki gabin dulinya nan yaan.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Iyaayoo le n gbir nkukɔpɔɔn miba nyan paab ki ke: Daandaan nan feegab nin npɔɔn nin ti Wonbɔru a naam dan na, nin U Masiya a naam a ninkper a pɔɔm foona. Nan bu-n wan nan galin ti nabumin, wan nan galin bi ti Wonbɔru a numin nween nin kunyiɛgin, bi nyan u gabin tuma.

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: Bi nyaŋ wa kipeebig a cham bu nin bi siɛram bu baa nan jiɛ bi miɛfolin ki tan kaagi ki sul jir bɔɔn ki nyan nkumin ni.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: Mambu, ni che ni li mɔn na! Paab nin nan biɛn be niin ni! Amaa tibɔbir be dulinya nin limɔgil bu, nan bu-n ulinlaril kaah jiirin ni ban nin! U gbee nin kiŋuufiiŋ le, nan bu-n u nyi u saŋi kaah kaa yabi.

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Kutunwiɛŋ nmɔn kaah nan kan baah nyan u jaa dulinya nan yaan nin, le u nin nɔɔ ke un choo upii wan maa kibijagin.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Le Uwonbɔr nan ta libaanweenl takpenl a kpinkpann tilee ki sei upii nmɔn ke un yug ki jo kukunkɔɔg a paab nan yaan wee Uwonbɔr mann npaan ki guun win ke bi li jin u ki tan kpaan ibin itaa nin ligenl ki jiɛn uwaa-n wee u laa pii wi.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Le uwaa nmɔn nan sii nnyum u mɔnn a li ke nkpenn a nyumin, ke nnyum nmɔn npuu ki tan ta upii nmɔn.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: Amaa le kutingbɔŋ nan ter upii nmɔn ki yaar u mɔb ki ta nnyum man wee kutunwiɛŋ sii u mɔn nin ki nyun.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Iyaayoo le kutunwiɛŋ nmɔn a ŋuul nan fii upii nmɔn bu, le u lol bitɔb ki jon ke un tan tob upii nmɔn a buwaab ban gurin, ban chee Uwonbɔr a siim wee ki jɔɔ Yisa a siɛra-n.

eng: Then I saw another great and awe-inspiring sign in heaven. There were seven angels with seven plagues—and these are the last, for with them Gods anger is brought to an end.
local: Le n ji nan kan ligaril a siɛra lanpolig paab: Ki miin malakam bilile ŋmag imag a booli ilile yan yin joorin, nan bu i yin joorin, i bu le Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ ndiɛ.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels, Go and pour out the seven bowls of Gods anger on the earth.
local: Le n gbir likukɔl kaah nyan iwonjiɛn niin n-yaan ki bei malakam bilile-n ke, Ni li chaau ki tan kpaar Uwonbɔr a ŋuufiiŋ a yir tilile-n ki jaa kutingbɔŋ buu.

eng: Then I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire. Those who gained victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name were standing by the glass sea, holding harps from God.
local: Le n kan niba le ni nann limɔgil lan ween ke niɛnu-n ki ŋmar nin nŋmi, le ban kɔn ki nyaŋ kuwanbiigin nin kuwanbiig a nunaŋin nin u yinl a kanlin kaah si tab limɔgilin ki ŋmag tibiɛr tan wee Uwonbɔr sei bin.

eng: So the first angel poured his bowl on the earth, and a nasty and terrible sore appeared on the people who had the beasts mark and worshipped its image.
local: Le chinchinn a malaka-n jon ki tan kpaar nan be u yiigin ki jaa kutingbɔŋ bu, le ibiɛ yan bir, ki wu takpemin choo ban jɔɔ kuwanbiigin a daagimin nin ban jiɛn kuwanbiigin a nunaŋin.

eng: They sing the song of Moses, Gods servant, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and awe-inspiring are your works, Lord God Almighty. Just and true are your ways, king of the nations.
local: le ki gaa Moziz a Wonbɔru a toontunnu nin Kipeebigin a lai-n: A ŋaar ye tiŋaar gbingbem le nin ligaril, Uyudaan Uwonbɔr Npɔɔn biɛn daan. A sam ŋan le ki ye ibamɔn, Fin le ye iyaayoo a bɔru.

eng: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and the sea turned into blood, like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ bilee mu kpaar nan be u yiigin ni ki jaa limɔgilin, le nnyumin labir ke utakpiir a chamin, le tiwann naan wan tan fo biɛn ki be limɔgil nmɔn yaan nin kpo.

eng: Who wont fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? You alone are holy. All nations will come and fall down in worship before you, for your acts of justice have been revealed.
local: Ŋma kaa na li saŋa, Uyudaan aa, le ki ta kupag ki sei a yinlaa? Nan bu-n a binba le be kasi. Ibol biɛn na li dan ki nan jiɛniŋ a nintogil, nan bu-n a buchinn toon-yin kpiira.

eng: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they turned into blood.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ bita mu kpaar nan be u yiigin ki jaa ikpen nin nnyunbumin bu, le mi kpann ncham.

eng: After this I looked, and the temple in heaven—that is, the tent of witness—was opened.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le n lig ki miin paab a wonjiɛn niin yan wee bi yi ke kuwonjiɛn nanboŋ wee ki ye siɛra-n choo loora.

eng: Then I heard the angel of the waters say, You are just, holy one, who is and was, because you have given these judgments.
local: Le n gbir malaka wan ŋmag nnyumin kaah len ke: A bɔjil i nan ye ibamɔn le, fin wan be nin wan nan be, kasidaan nin, nan bu-n A jin tibɔr nin ibamɔn na;.

eng: The seven angels, who have the seven plagues, came out of the temple. They were clothed in pure bright linen and had gold sashes around their waists.
local: Le malakam bilile nyan iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn yaan ki ŋmag imag ilile-n. Bi nan yee itan a wan yɔɔr tan nyagin ki be chasi, ki ta salima a changban ki lol paan bi gbingban bu.

eng: They poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
local: nan bu-n bi nan nyan a ninfogannkaab nin a linlem a chama, le a ta ncham ke bin nyu, naah kpaan bi man.

eng: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls full of the anger of the God who lives forever and always.
local: Le tiwann naan wan tan fo tina-n a ni uba ta salima a yir tilile tan gbee nin Uwonbɔr wan be wee ki li gbaa be kaa jɔɔ kumin a ŋuufiiŋin ki sei malakam bilile nmɔn.

eng: And I heard the altar say, Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and just.
local: Le n gbir likukɔl kaah nyan libinbinlin ban ki len ke, Nn, Uyudaan Uwonbɔr Npɔɔn biɛn daan, a bɔjil ŋan le ki ye ibamɔn.

eng: The temple was filled with smoke from Gods glory and power, and no one could go into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven last angels were brought to an end.
local: Le Uwonbɔr nan che U pag nin U pɔɔm a kpinnyir nan kɔɔ ki gbeen iwonjiɛn niin, le uba kaa ji nan fir kɔɔ iwonjiɛn niin nmɔn yaan nin hali nin malakam bilile ban ŋmag imag ilile-n kaah nan nan diɛ.

eng: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to burn people with fire.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ bina-n mu kpaar nan be u yiigin a poolin ki jaa nween bu, le ki sei nween npɔɔn ke un ta nŋmi ki nyɔɔ bininfob wee bin kab.

eng: The people were burned by intense heat, and they cursed the name of the God who had power over these plagues. But they didnt change their hearts and lives and give him glory.
local: Nŋmi-n a tuntoŋ nan kɔɔ bi takpem pam, hali ke bi nan sugir Uwonbɔr wan jɔɔ limagil a pɔɔmin a yinl, amaa bi le nan yii le kaa kpenn bi binbem ki pag wi.

eng: The fifth angel poured his bowl over the beasts throne, and darkness covered its kingdom. People bit their tongues because of their pain.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ biŋmu mu kpaar nan be u yiigin ki jaa kuwanbiigin a naangbɔŋ bu, le libunbɔnl lu kuwanbiig nmɔn a naamin. Le bininfob ŋman bi lanbili ntiton nmɔn yaan.

eng: and they cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores; but they didnt turn away from what they had done.
local: le ki sugir Uwonbɔr wan be paab naah wu bin nin bi biɛi nbu, amaa le kaa chee bin kpenn bi binbem nan wee bi tun nin bu.

eng: Then the sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up so that the way was ready for the kings from the east.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ biloob mu kpaar nan be u yiigin ki jaa nkpenn takpen man wee bi yi mi ke Yufiratiisi bu, le li nnyum fɔɔr ki jiɛn npaan sei bibɔrtiib ban nyan Liwonpɔɔnl paamin.

eng: Then I saw three unclean spirits, like frogs, come from the dragons mouth, the beasts mouth, and the mouth of the false prophet.
local: Le n nan kan nnaanbiim mitaa le bi nann ke ititalin, bi nan nyan kutunwiɛŋin, nin kuwanbiigin nin tibɔkar a linlemin a mɔɔŋin le.

eng: These are demonic spirits that do signs. They go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty. .
local: Bi ye npɔniim a naam ban ŋaan ligaril a siɛra, le bi chaa dulinya munl nan biɛn a bɔrtiib ban, ki lagin bi ke bin to bitɔb Uwonbɔr Npɔɔn biɛn daan a datakpenl daal.

eng: Look! Im coming like a thief! Favored are those who stay awake and clothed so that they dont go around naked and exposed to shame.
local: Lig! N li dan ke unaayigin! Tikaar be wan kaa dɔɔ jiɛn, wee ki ŋmag u wann yeewann, wee u laa nan choon gaan wee u yul nkpo.

eng: The spirits gathered them at the place that is called in Hebrew, Harmagedon.
local: Le bi lagin bibɔrtiib nmɔn bogiliba nan yaan wee Hiiburu yaab a leen nin bi yi ke Amagedɔn nin.

eng: Then the seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out from the temple, from the throne, saying, It is done.
local: Le malaka wan kpiɛ bilile mu kpaar nan be u yiigin ki jaa nfaamin, le nkukɔpɔɔn nyan kunaangbɔŋ n-yaan ki tiɛn iwonjiɛn niin nin ke, Ni ŋaau.

eng: There were lightning strikes, voices, and thunder, and a great earthquake occurred. The earthquake was greater than any that have occurred since there have been people on earth.
local: Le utaal piin ki nyagin ki dun ki tann nin tifur, le tum dam, uninfosaal a binbem kutingbɔŋ nan yaan, kutingbɔŋ kaa la nan nyi ki dam ki nann man ni, kitiŋ a damim nmɔn nan bir pam.

eng: The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered Babylon the great so that he gave her the wine cup of his furious anger.
local: Le kitintakpeŋin bɔgir nkerim mitaa bu, le ibol a tim lur. Le Uwonbɔr nan tiɛr Babilɔnn kan ye kitintakpeŋin bu, le ki ta U ŋuul a yiinyɔɔg kan gbee nin u ŋuufiiŋ a daami ki sei Babilɔnn yaab ke bin nyu mi biɛn na.

eng: Every island fled, and the mountains disappeared.
local: Igungon yan biɛn be nnyum a sisiigin nin nan guun na le ijɔɔ biɛn bor.

eng: Huge hailstones weighing about one hundred pounds came down from heaven on the people. They cursed God for the plague of hail, because the plague was so terrible.
local: Le itanyunbɔl gbingbem nyann kitilangbɔŋin ki kpiɛg ke linaalin, ki nan lur bininfob bu. Le bi poog imɔɔŋ ki jaar Uwonbɔr waah che limagil lan lur bi bu-n bu, nan bu-n limagil nmɔn nan bir pam.

eng: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was filled with light because of his glory.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le n ji kan malaka kaah nyan paab ki chaa jiirin. U nan jɔɔ tininkper a pɔɔm takpem. Le u wonweem man nyan u ni wonn dulinya biɛn bu.

eng: He called out with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a home for demons and a lair for every unclean spirit. She is a lair for every unclean bird, and a lair for every unclean and disgusting beast.
local: Le u tiɛn paab nin nkukɔpɔɔn ke, Ki lu! Babilɔnn kan ye kitintakpeŋin luu! Babilɔnn kpann npɔniim a binben bola nin nan yaan wee nnaanbiim gɔɔr nin nan yaan wee nnɔɔm man jɔɔ tijaginn kaa kpaan binib le gaa i gɔɔr.

eng: because all the nations have fallen due to the wine of her lustful passion. The kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth became rich from the power of her loose and extravagant ways.
local: Nan bu-n kutingbɔŋ nan a ninfob pii nyun ndapɔɔrim ke u wanchɔgin toon yin. Dulinya a bɔrtiib tun iwanchɔgin toon nin wa, le kpoŋkpoŋ dam kpann biwandam u wanŋan banl nmɔn bu.

eng: Then I heard another voice from heaven say, Come out of her, my people, so that you dont take part in her sins and dont receive any of her plagues.
local: Le n ji gbir likukɔl liba nyan paab ki len ke: Ni nya u ban na, n ninfob, wee u bir nmɔn laa tabin ni, wee u magil liba laa kan ni;.

eng: Her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God remembered her unjust acts.
local: Nan bu-n u birin paan paan le ki tan pii paab, le Uwonbɔr tiɛr u galin nmɔn bu.

eng: Give her what she has given to others. Give her back twice as much for what she has done. In the cup that she has poured, pour her twice as much.
local: Ni jiin sei u waah sei ni nan nin; Ni yɔ waah ŋa nan ki sei ni a kerim mileeu. Ni ŋmarin ndapɔɔrim nkerim milee u tɔɔl a yiinyɔɔgin ki sei wa.

eng: To the extent that she glorified herself and indulged her loose and extravagant ways, give her pain and grief. In her heart she says, I sit like a queen! Im not a widow. Ill never see grief.
local: Ni sei mu limugil nin npoobiin takpema, a li ke kupag nin liwanŋanl banl lan wee u ti jɔɔ tenn u baantɔɔlin. U far ligbingbanl le u sufu ni ki ke, N ka le ke upiibɔrin; maa ye lipukɔɔli, le maa li nyi mɔɔ tikpemɔri.

eng: This is why her plagues will come in a single day—deadly disease, grief, and hunger. She will be consumed by fire because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
local: Mambu weemba daal u magil nmɔn na li nyaŋ u: Nkum, nin tikpemɔr nin nkom na li choo u. Nŋmi le na li gaa u, nan bu-n Uyudaan Uwonbɔr wan ye npɔɔn daan le ji u tibɔr.

eng: The kings of the earth, who committed sexual immorality with her and shared her loose and extravagant ways, will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke from her burning.
local: N-yonn man wee kutingbɔŋ nan a bɔrtiib ban tun iwanchɔgin toon nin upii nmɔn nin ki jin u wanŋan banlin a mɔmin nan kan nŋmi man gaan win a kpinnyir kaah dug ke, bi li mɔɔ tikpemɔr upii nmɔn bu.

eng: They will stand a long way off because they are afraid of the pain she suffers, and they will say, Oh, the horror! Babylon, you great city, you powerful city! In a single hour your judgment has come.
local: U fala nmɔn a jim na li choo bi bijawaam, bi na li sir nan yaan fɔg ki mɔr: Woo! Woo, Kitintakpeŋaa, Babilɔnn aa, kitiŋ kan jɔɔ npɔɔn! Kukurug kiba a poolin le a tudaril foon.

eng: The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their cargoes anymore.
local: Dulinya a kpoŋkpoŋ dam na li mɔɔ tikpemɔr u bu, nan bu-n uba kaa ji na li be ki daa bi tugi jigin ni.

eng: cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; all those things made of scented wood, ivory, fine wood, bronze, iron, and marble.
local: itug yan ye salima, nin tikurninann, nin itangbag yan a daag pɔɔ, nin nliɛm man nyagin, nin ikinken ŋan, nin itan man geuŋ nin itan yan ŋaan bolbol nin itan man a wan yɔɔr, nin tidaar tan ŋan, nin tiwann tan wee bi ta uloob a nyin ki naan ni, nin tidaar tan a daag pɔɔ, nin tikurdiin, nin tikurpɔɔr, nin ichabpeen;.

eng: cinnamon, incense, fragrant ointment, and frankincense; wine, oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves, even human lives.
local: itug yan ye ilanjobon nin tiwann tan tenn tikpinn nbaam, tiwann tan nyɔɔr ki jɔɔ nbaam, nin isiig a nyinyinn nin nnyɔgkusim, nin ndaam nin Olif a kpam, nin n-yom ŋam nin idi, nin inaa nin ipee; itaan nin i torokom; nin tigbanann nin bininfob a naam.

eng: The fruit your whole being craved has gone from you. All your glitter and glamour are lost to you, never ever to be found again.
local: Bi na li len ke, Iloon yan wee a ban nin bor a ban na. A jɔɔm nin tiwann tan ŋan niŋ biɛn bora, kaa ji li fir kan ni.

eng: The merchants who sold these things, and got so rich by her, will stand a long way off because they fear the pain she suffers. They will weep and mourn, and say.
local: Le kpoŋkpoŋ dam ban kɔɔr tiwann nmɔn kitiŋ nmɔn yaan wee ki kpann biwandamin upii nmɔn bu-n, na li sir nan fɔg, le nin ŋa bi bijawaam upii nmɔn a fala a jim bu. Bi na li mɔɔ tikpemɔr.

eng: Oh, the horror! The great city that wore fine linen, purple, and scarlet, who glittered with gold, jewels, and pearls.
local: ki mɔɔ paab ke, Woo! Woo, kitintakpeŋaa, kan nan yee ikinken ŋan nin itan man geuŋ nin itan man, le ki nan nyagin nin salima, nin itangbag yan jɔɔ kudaag, nin nliɛm!.

eng: in just one hour such great wealth was destroyed. Every sea captain, every seafarer, sailors, and all who make their living on the sea stood a long way off.
local: Kukurug kiba a poolin njɔɔm takpem biɛn bor! Le ban donn tiŋalin a yudam nin ban kɔɔ tiŋalin nin biŋalindom nin ban biɛn ji limɔgil nmɔn yaan nin, na li sir nan fɔg.

eng: They cried out as they saw the smoke from her burning and said, What city was ever like the great city.
local: N-yonn man wee bi nan kan nŋmi man gaan win a kpinnyirin, le ki fabin ke, Kitintakpeŋ nan nan jɔɔ kpiɛgaa.

eng: They threw dust on their heads, and they cried out, weeping and mourning. They said, Oh, the horror! The great city, where all who have ships at sea became so rich by her prosperity—in just one hour she was destroyed.
local: Bi na li minn titann ki gabir bi yuli bu, le ki mɔr tikpemɔr ki kunn ke: Woo! Woo, kitintakpeŋaa, nan yaan wee ban biɛn nan donn tiŋalin nan kpann biwandam upii a jɔɔm bu-n! Le kukurug kiba a poolin le u jɔɔm nmɔn nan bor!.

eng: Rejoice over her, heaven—you saints, apostles, and prophets—because God has condemned her as she condemned you.
local: Nim ban be paabaa, ni che ni li mɔ ni u buu! Ni che ni li mɔ na, Uwonbɔr a ninfob nin itoon nin bilinlem, Uwonbɔr jin u tibɔra waah nan ŋa ni man nin bu. .

eng: Then a powerful angel picked up a stone that was like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, With such violent force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, and it wont be found anymore.
local: Le malaka wan pɔɔ yuur litangbagil lan polig ke linaal takpenlin, ki gab limɔgilin ki len ke: Nin limugil nmɔn Bi na li tir Babilɔnn kitintakpeŋ nmɔn ki jaa tum, le baa ji li kan ki.

eng: The sound of harpists and musicians, of pipers and trumpeters, will never be heard among you again. No craftsman of any kind will ever be found among you again. The sound of the hand mill will never be heard among you again.
local: Bibiɛfaab nin bilangaab nin binaakpaapeeb nin binaatunpeeb a lai-n kaa ji na li gbir a ban ni. Kpoŋkpoŋ daan uba kaa ji na li be a ban ni. Linaal a mɔr kaa ji na li gbir a ban ni.

eng: The light of a lamp will never shine among you again. The sound of a bridegroom and bride will never be heard among you again because your merchants ran the world, because all the nations were deceived by the spell you cast, and because.
local: Le fila a wonweem kaa ji na li won a bu jigin ni. Lipiichanil daan nin u bɔɔnh a kukɔl kaa ji na li gbir a ban ni. A kpoŋkpoŋ damin nan ye dulinya a ninfob gbingbemin le. A nyɔgimin kɔɔn ibol biɛn kulɔŋa.

eng: the blood of prophets, of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on the earth was found among you.
local: Babilɔnn nmɔn ni, le bi nan kan bilinlem nin Uwonbɔr a ninfob gannkaabin a cham nin ban biɛn wee bi nan ku kutingbɔŋ nan yaan nin.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the judgment upon the great prostitute, who is seated on deep waters.
local: Le n kan malakam bilile ban ŋmag tiyir tilile-n a ni uba dan ki nan beim ke, Dan wee n wɔɔniŋ lipiijagɔɔril wan jɔɔ liyinl a tudaril kaah yab man, wan ka nnyum takpen bu-n.

eng: The kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on earth have become drunk with the wine of her whoring.
local: Kutingbɔŋ a bɔrtiib dɔɔn nin wa, le kutingbɔŋ nan yaab nyun u wanchɔgin toon yin a daam ki gbeeu.

eng: Then he brought me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a desert. There I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
local: Le malaka nmɔn nan taam ki jon kukunkɔɔgin Kasi a naaŋ a pɔɔm bu. Nin yaan le n nan kan upii uba kaah ka kuwanbiig kan man bu, le iyin yan sugir Uwonbɔr sɔb ki paa kuwanbiig nmɔn a gbanann biɛn bu, le kuwanbiig nmɔn jɔɔ iyul ilile nin iyinh piig.

eng: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing, and she glittered with gold and jewels and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup full of the vile and impure things that came from her activity as a prostitute.
local: Upii nmɔn nan yee tiwann yɔɔr tan man nin tan man geuŋ le, ki nan nyagin nin salima, nin itangbag nin nliɛm man ŋan ki nyagin. Ki ŋmag salima a yiinyɔɔg u nɔɔn, le kiyiinyɔɔg nmɔn gbee nin tijaginn tan ye titoonbir nin iwanchɔgin toon.

eng: A name—a mystery—was written on her forehead: Babylon the great, the mother of prostitutes and the vile things of the earth.
local: Liyinl le nan sɔb paa u yutunl bu ke, TIBƆWOONKAAR BABILƆNN KITINTAKPEŊIN IPIIJAGƆƆR BIƐN A NA KUTINGBƆŊ NAN A WANBIR BIƐN A NA.

eng: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the saints and the blood of Jesus witnesses. I was completely stunned when I saw her.
local: Le n kan ki miin upii nmɔn nyun Uwonbɔr a ninfogannkaab a cham le ki gbee, ban a cham nan jin siɛra Yisa bu-n. Maah nan kan win, le ni ŋaam ligaril takpem.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and the seven-headed, ten-horned beast that carries her.
local: Le malaka nmɔn baanim ke, Ba ŋa wee a che ni gariŋaa? N li wɔɔniŋ tibɔwoonkaar ki jiin upii-n nin waah nan jig kuwanbiig kan nin paam kan nan jɔɔ iyul ilile nin iyinh piigin.

eng: The beast that you saw was and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. Those who live on earth, whose names havent been written in the scroll of life from the time the earth was made, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and will again be present.
local: Kuwanbiig kan wee a kan nin, nan be le, le kaa ji be daandaan nan ni, le ki na li nyan libogil lan nyɔɔ n-yaan ki jo nan yaan wee bi li bii u. Le kutingbɔŋ yaab ban a yin kaa sɔb be limiɛfol a gbɔŋin n-yaan dulinya a piinimin nin, bi nan kan kuwanbiig nmɔn ke ni li gar bi, nan bu-n u pii nan be, le kaa ji be daandaan nan nin, le ki ji na li dan.

eng: This calls for an understanding mind. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings.
local: Ni niɛ nan ban npotiɛm le nin nlamgɔrikaam. Iyul ilile-n ye igungon ilile yan wee upii-n ka-n le.

eng: Five kings have fallen, the one is, and the other hasnt yet come. When that king comes, he must remain for only a short time.
local: I mu ji ye bibɔrtiib bilile le. Biŋmu luu, uba be, wan gurin kaa la dan nin; amaa u nan dan ke, ni kpaa le nin un be n-yonn wama.

eng: As for the beast that was and is not, it is itself an eighth king that belongs to the seven, and it is going to destruction.
local: Kuwanbiig kan pii nan be wee kaa jin nan be-n, le ye ubɔr wan kpiɛ biniin nin. U ye bibɔrtiib bilile-n a ni uba le, le ki chaa u biim a bol.

eng: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings, who havent yet received royal power. But they will receive royal authority for an hour, along with the beast.
local: Iyinh piig yan wee a kan nin le ye bibɔrtiib piig ban kaa la gaa bi naamin, amaa kukurug kiba a poolin bi li gaa tininkper a pɔɔm ke bibɔrtiibin ki kpiɛ kuwanbiig nmɔn bu.

eng: These kings will be of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
local: Lol liba le bi jɔɔ ki na li ta bi pɔɔm nin bi ninkper a pɔɔm ki sei kuwanbiigin.

eng: They will make war on the Lamb, but the Lamb will emerge victorious, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings. Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
local: Bi na li to Kipeebigin bitɔb, amaa Kipeebigin na li nyaŋ bi, nan bu-n kipeebig nmɔn le ye biyudam a yudaau nin bibɔrtiib a bɔru, le ban na li be u ban ye ban wee u yin ki gann bi, wee bi ye yadadam ki nɔɔ win.

eng: Then he said to me, The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
local: Le malaka nmɔn beim ke, Nnyum man wee a nan kan nin, wee lipiijagɔɔrilin ka mi bu-n, ye bininfob le, kuninfogbaluŋ, ibol nin ileen balbal.

eng: As for the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will destroy her and strip her bare. They will devour her flesh and burn her with fire.
local: Kuwanbiigin nin iyinh piig yan wee a nan kan nin na li nan lipiijagɔɔrilin, ki fee u faal biɛn wee ki jaa u lijakpalomil, bi na li ŋman u gbanann ki nyɔɔ u nŋmi.

eng: because God moved them to carry out his purposes. That is why they will be of one mind and give their royal power to the beast, until Gods words have been accomplished.
local: Nan bu-n Uwonbɔr le nan ta ibon nmɔn ki ŋa bi potiɛmin ke bin tun Uma Uwonbɔr kaah lol man, ni bu le U che bi ŋa mɔbiba ki ta npɔɔn sei kuwanbiigin, hali nin Uma Uwonbɔr a bɔr kaah na li gbeen.

eng: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.
local: Upii wan wee a nan kan nin, n ye kitintakpeŋ kan ŋmag dulinya nan a bɔrtiib le.
