eng: After this I heard what sounded like a huge crowd in heaven. They said, Hallelujah! The salvation and glory and power of our God!.
local: Ni bɔɔn bu le n gbir niba a mɔr, le ni nann ke kuninfogbaluŋ a kukɔlin ki nyan paab ki tann ke: Ti don Uwonbɔr a yinl! Feegab nin kupag nin npɔɔn ye ti Wonbɔru yaan le.

eng: His judgments are true and just, because he judged the great prostitute, who ruined the earth by her whoring, and he exacted the penalty for the blood of his servants from her hand.
local: nan bu-n U bɔjil ŋan le ki ye ibamɔn. U bii lipiijagɔɔril takpenlin na, wan ta iwanchɔgin toon ki boon kutingbɔŋin. U dar u tul ki jiin U toontumin a cham buu.

eng: Then they said a second time, Hallelujah! Smoke goes up from her forever and always.
local: Le kuninfogbaluŋin ji kunn ki len ke: Ti don Uwonbɔr a yinl! Tikpinnyir tan nyan u nin do paab le hali nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛm.

eng: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshipped God, who is seated on the throne, and they said, Amen. Hallelujah.
local: Le bininkpeeb pilee nin bina nin tiwann naan wan tan fo tina-n sɔɔn tum ki jiɛn Uwonbɔr, wan nan ka u naangbɔŋ bu-n. Le bi kuun ke, Ibamɔn, Ti don Uwonbɔr a yinl.

eng: Then a voice went out from the throne and said, Praise our God, all you his servants, and you who fear him, both small and great.
local: Le likukɔl nyan kunaagbɔŋin yaan ki len ke: Ni don ti Wonbɔru, nim ban biɛn ye u toontum, wee ki san u, ban kaa gbee bininfob nin ban gbee bininfob.

eng: And I heard something that sounded like a huge crowd, like rushing water and powerful thunder. They said, Hallelujah! The Lord our God, the Almighty, exercised his royal power!.
local: Iyaayoo le n ji gbir nan nann kuninfogbaluŋ a fur, le ki nann nnyum puukaam a fur bee utaal kaah tann a fur ki len ke, Ti don Uwonbɔr a yinl! Nan bu-n ti Yudaau Uwonbɔr wan ye Npɔɔn biɛn daan nin le ŋmag ti.

eng: Let us rejoice and celebrate, and give him the glory, for the wedding day of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
local: Ni che ni li mɔ ta wee ti pooi li peen na wee ti sei U kupag! Nan bu-n Kipeebigin a piichanil a yoo foou, le u bɔɔnh ŋa u ba siliu.

eng: She was given fine, pure white linen to wear, for the fine linen is the saints acts of justice.
local: Le Uwonbɔr sei u bɔɔnh nmɔn itan ŋan yan nyagin ki be chasi, ke un yee.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Write this: Favored are those who have been invited to the wedding banquet of the Lamb. He said to me, These are the true words of God.
local: Le malaka-n beim ke, Sɔb ke: Tikaar be ban wee bi yin bi ke bi nan ji Kipeebigin a piichanil a jiir nan bu! Le ki ji len ke, Ni niɛ nan le ye Uwonbɔr a mɔleen ibamɔn.

eng: Then I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters who hold firmly to the witness of Jesus. Worship God! The witness of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
local: Iyaayoo le n lu u taatum ki jiɛn u. Amaa le u beim ke, Laa ŋa man ni! N ye a toontum kpiɛg le nin a nabum ban ŋmag Yisa a siɛra-n. Li jiɛn Uwonbɔr a! Nan bu-n Yisa a siɛra ye Kinaaŋ a linlen leen le.

eng: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he judges and makes war justly.
local: Le n nan kan paab kaah loor, le utanpeen si niin n nintogil, le wan jig ki a yinl ye yada daan nin ibamɔn. Le u ji tibɔr nin tibuchinn, ki tog bitɔb.

eng: His eyes were like a fiery flame, and on his head were many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
local: Le u numbili nann nŋmipeen, le nnaam a yuchigirim takpem chig u yul bu. Liyinl liba nan sɔb paa u bu le uba kaa nyi li taai, see uma a binba.

eng: He wore a robe dyed with blood, and his name was called the Word of God.
local: Le u wan yee wan man nin ncham, le u yinl ye Uwonbɔr a Mɔbonl.

eng: Heavens armies, wearing fine linen that was white and pure, were following him on white horses.
local: Le paab a tɔtogib nɔɔ u, ki jig itanpeen ki yee itan ŋan yan peen ki be chasi.

eng: From his mouth comes a sharp sword that he will use to strike down the nations. He is the one who will rule them with an iron rod. And he is the one who will trample the winepress of the Almighty Gods passionate anger.
local: Le takoobi kan ka nyan u mɔn, le u li ta ki kɔn nyaŋ ibol. U li ta kukurug a jaangbiɛg ki ŋmag bi. Ki li taan bi ke baah taan isiloon a daam man nin, ni ma ye Uwonbɔr wan ye npɔɔn biɛn daan a ŋuul nin u ŋuufiiŋ.

eng: He has a name written on his robe and on his thigh: King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: U wann yeewann nin u panl bu, le liyinl liba nan sɔb paa ke: BIBƆRTIIB BIƐN A BƆRU NIN BIYUDAM BIƐN A YUDAAU.

eng: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he called out with a loud voice and said to all the birds flying high overhead, Come and gather for Gods great supper.
local: Le n kan malaka kaah si nween, ki kunn nkukɔpɔɔn ki yi nnɔɔm man yugir paab ki ke, Dan, ni lagin taba wee ki nan ji Uwonbɔr a jɔɔg a jiir takpen nan.

eng: Come and eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, and the flesh of horses and their riders. Come and eat the flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.
local: wee ni fir ŋman bibɔrtiib, nin bitɔtob a yudam, nin bipɔɔndam, nin itaan nin bitanjigib, nin bininfob biɛn ban ye bi ba nin ban ye biyom, ban gbee bininfob nin ban kaa gbee bininfob a nann.

eng: Then I saw that the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies had gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.
local: Le n kan kuwanbiigin nin kutingbɔŋ nan a bɔrtiib nin bitɔtogib kaah lagin bin to utaan jigirin nin u tɔtogib bitɔb.

eng: But the beast was seized, along with the false prophet who had done signs in the beasts presence. The two of them were thrown alive into the fiery lake that burns with sulfur.
local: Amaa bi nan choo kuwanbiig nmɔn na nin tibɔkar a linlenu wan nan sir u taalin wee ki tun ligaril a siɛra-n. Siɛram nan le u nan ta bii ban nan gaa kuwanbiigin a daagim a potiɛl le bi jiɛn u nunaŋin. Bi bilee nmɔn la nan fo le bi ta bi jaa lichibil a ŋmiim a mɔgilin yaan.

eng: The rest were killed by the sword that comes from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds ate their fill of their flesh.
local: Takoobi kan nan nyan utaan jigirin a mɔn nin, le u nan ta ku ban gurin biɛn, le nnɔɔm biɛn jin bi gbanann kpiinn ki nan gbee.

eng: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the abyss and a huge chain.
local: Le n kan malaka kaah nyan paab ki chaa jiirin tum, ki ŋmag libogil lan nyɔɔ kaa jɔɔ tarigin a piim nin tikulor takpen u nɔɔn.

eng: He seized the dragon, the old snake, who is the devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years.
local: Le u nan choo kutunwiɛŋ nmɔn, wan nan ye yaaja yoo a waau, ki ye ulinlaril bee Sintani, ki lol u bil ibin turilin.

eng: He threw him into the abyss, then locked and sealed it over him. This was to keep him from continuing to deceive the nations until the thousand years were over. After this he must be released for a little while.
local: Le u ta u ki gab libogil lan nyɔɔ kaa jɔɔ tarigin yaan, le ki loon ki pɔl legin u bu, ke u laa ji fir lar ibolin jigin nin, ki nan kpaa ibin turilin kaah na li diɛ. Ni bɔɔn bu ni kpaa le nin bi nan lor u ki jaa wama.

eng: Then I saw thrones, and people took their seats on them, and judgment was given in their favor. They were the ones who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and Gods word, and those who hadnt worshipped the beast or its image, who hadnt received the mark on their forehead or hand. They came to life and ruled with Christ for one thousand years.
local: Iyaayoo le n kan tinaangbann, le bi sei ban ka ti bu-n tininkper a pɔɔm ke bi li ji tibɔr. Le n kan baah nan tugir ban a yuli yin a naam, baah nan jin Yisa a siɛra nin Uwonbɔr a mɔleen n bu. Baa nan jiɛn kuwanbiigin bee ki nunaŋi, kaa gaa ki daagim bi yutun bu bee bi nɔɔi bu. Bi jirin bi miɛfolin le bi ma nin Yisa lagin ŋmag nnaam ibin turil.

eng: Favored and holy are those who have a share in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over them, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and will rule with him for one thousand years.
local: Tikaar nin kasi be ban kpiɛ wee Uwonbɔr chinn fiin bi nkumin nin bu. Nkum man yin kpiɛ milee-n kaa jɔɔ bi pɔɔmi, amaa bi na yin ye Uwonbɔr nin Yisa a minmanib le, bi ma nin Yisa na li lagin ki ŋmag nnaam ki tan kpaa ibin turil.

eng: When the thousand years are over, Satan will be released from his prison.
local: Ibin turil nmɔn a tarig nan jon ke, Uwonbɔr li che bin loor Sintani kuloondiigin ki jaa.

eng: He will go out to deceive the nations that are at the four corners of the earth—Gog and Magog. He will gather them for battle. Their number is like the sand of the sea.
local: le u li nya ki tan lar ibol yan be kutingbɔŋ a logi ina nan biɛn, ubɔr Gog nin u tiŋ Magog nan, u na li lagin bi ki lol bitɔb. Bi kanl na yin nann le ke limɔgil a gbaam a tanbiidilin.

eng: They came up across the whole earth and surrounded the saints camp, the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
local: Le bi lagin ki jon ilog biɛn, ki kpa bi tan foo Uwonbɔr a ninfob a laguŋin, le ki gɔɔn bi, kitiŋ kan wee Uwonbɔr jiɛ nin. Amaa le nŋmi nyan paab ki nan gaa bi.

eng: Then the devil, who had deceived them, was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet also were. There painful suffering will be inflicted upon them day and night, forever and always.
local: Le Sintani wan lar bin, Uwonbɔr ta u ki gab lichibil lan gaan nŋmi-n ni a mɔgilin yaan, nan yaan wee bi nan ta kuwanbiigin nin tibɔkar a linlemin ki gabin. Ni yaan le bi yin ji fala nween nin kunyiɛg hali nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ diɛm.

eng: Then I saw a great white throne and the one who is seated on it. Before his face both earth and heaven fled away, and no place was found for them.
local: Le n kan kunaangbɔŋ pɔŋ takpoŋ, nin wan ka ki bu-n. Le kutingbɔŋ nin kitilangbɔŋ nan guun fɔɔr u ban kaa ji jɔɔ binbeboli.

eng: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and scrolls were opened. Another scroll was opened too; this is the scroll of life. And the dead were judged on the basis of what was written in the scrolls about what they had done.
local: Le n kan bitakpiib, ban gbee bitakpiib nin ban kaa gbee bitakpiib kaah si kunaangbɔŋin a nintogil, le tigbann kpaar. Le kugbɔŋ kuba mu ji kpaar, ki ma ye limiɛfol a gbɔŋ. Le bi jin bi tibɔr baah tun nan wee Uwonbɔr sɔb ŋa tigbann nmɔn yaan nin.

eng: The sea gave up the dead that were in it, and Death and the Grave gave up the dead that were in them, and people were judged by what they had done.
local: Le limɔgil nan nyan bitakpiib ban be li ni ki jaa, le nkum nin kitakpiin tiŋ nyan bitakpiib ban be bi ni ki jaa. Le bi jin bi biɛn tibɔr baah tun nan bu.

eng: Then Death and the Grave were thrown into the fiery lake. This, the fiery lake, is the second death.
local: Le u ta nkum nin kitakpiin tiŋ ki gab nŋmi a mɔgilin yaan. Nŋmi a mɔgil nmɔn le ye nkum man kpiɛ milee.

eng: Then anyone whose name wasnt found written in the scroll of life was thrown into the fiery lake.
local: Ni ye wan a yinl kaa nan sɔb ŋa limiɛfol a gbɔŋin yaan ke, bi nan ti ta u le ki gab nŋmi a mɔgilin yaan.

eng: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the former heaven and the former earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
local: Le n kan paab pɔŋ nin kutingbɔŋ pɔŋ, nan bu-n chinchinn a papaab nin chinchinn a tumin nan fɔɔra, le limɔgil kaa ji be.

eng: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, made ready as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
local: Le n kan kasi a tiŋin, Jerusalam pɔŋin, kaah nyan paab Uwonbɔr ban ki chaa jiirin ki mann uba ke ujakunn nin ki guu u bɔɔnh.

eng: I heard a loud voice from the throne say, Look! Gods dwelling is here with humankind. He will dwell with them, and they will be his peoples. God himself will be with them as their God.
local: Le n gbir likukɔl kaah nyan nnaam a gbɔŋin yaan ki len ke, Daandaan nan Uwonbɔr a binben bol be bininfob ban, le U li be nin bi. Bi li ŋa U ninfob, le Uwonbɔr mu tɔɔl li be nin bi ki ye bi Weem.

eng: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more. There will be no mourning, crying, or pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.
local: Uwonbɔr na li pir bi num a nyinyinn biɛn. Iyaayoo nkum bee tikpemɔr bee timɔr bee nan wu kaa ji na li be, nan bu-n niwann kpegin nin biɛn fɔɔra.

eng: Then the one seated on the throne said, Look! Im making all things new. He also said, Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.
local: Le wan nan ka kunaangbɔŋ bu-n len ke, Lig, n ŋaan niwann biɛn niwann pɔnn le! Le ki ji len ke, Sɔb ki bila, nan bu-n imɔleen nmɔn ye ibamɔn le ki ye gaa ji.

eng: Then he said to me, All is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will freely give water from the life-giving spring.
local: Le u beim ke: Ni ŋau. Min le ye Wan chinn nin Wan joor, Npiinim nin Ndiɛm. Nnyunyum kaah jɔɔ ban nin, n li sei bi limiɛfol a nnyum, man ye nnyunbunkaamin yɔli.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will inherit these things. I will be their God, and they will be my sons and daughters.
local: Wan fir ki nyaŋ ke udaan li kan lifaalin biɛn, le N li ye u Wonbɔru le u mu li ye N big.

eng: But for the cowardly, the faithless, the vile, the murderers, those who commit sexual immorality, those who use drugs and cast spells, the idolaters and all liars—their share will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death.
local: Amaa ban kaa jɔɔ lipoobil, nin ban kaa jɔɔ yada, nin bipootondam, nin bininfokub, nin ban tun iwanchɔgin toon lipiibanl nin lijabanl, nin ban ye binyɔgdam, nin bibawomanib, nin tibɔkardam biɛn na yin be lichibil a ŋmiim a mɔgilin yaan. Ni niɛ le ye nkum man kpiɛ milee.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the bride, the Lambs wife.
local: Le malakam bilile ban ŋmag tiyir tilile wee ti gbee nin imag ilile-n yan joorin a ni uba beim ke, Dan wee n wɔɔniŋ ujakunn nin, wan ye kipeebig nmɔn a bɔɔnh.

eng: He took me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a great, high mountain, and he showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
local: Le u nan taam Kinaaŋin ki don lijɔɔl takpenl lan kper a paab, ki tan wɔɔnim Jerusalam kan ye Kasi a Tiŋin, le ki nyan paab Uwonbɔr ban ki chaa jiirin tum.

eng: The city had Gods glory. Its brilliance was like a priceless jewel, like jasper that was as clear as crystal.
local: Ki nyagin nin Uwonbɔr a pag, ki ŋan ki nyagin ke nliɛm man a daag pɔɔ, ki be ke sataŋin, le ki ji ween ke niɛnu a maarin.

eng: It had a great high wall with twelve gates. By the gates were twelve angels, and on the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israels sons.
local: Le kitiŋ nmɔn jɔɔ ligonl takpenl, lan kper ki jɔɔ itimansanmɔn piig nin ilee, le malakam piig nin bilee ka guu itimansanmɔnn piig nin ilee nmɔn. Bi nan sɔb Iziral yaab a boli piig nin ilee-n a yin le ki tabin itimansanmɔn nmɔn bu.

eng: There were three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west.
local: Itimansanmɔnn itaa le nan be liwonpɔɔnl paam, le itaa be nnɔɔjiinpaam, le itaa be nnɔɔgan paam, le itaa mu be liwonlul paam.

eng: The city wall had twelve foundations, and on them were the twelve names of the Lambs twelve apostles.
local: Kitiŋ nmɔn a gonl nan maa tɔɔn ifiɛ piig nin ilee bu le, le Kipeebigin a tooi piig nin bilee-n a yin nan sɔb ki paa ifiɛ nmɔn bu.

eng: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring rod with which to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
local: Malaka wan nan beim man nin, nan ŋmag salima a jaangbiɛg kan wee u jɔɔ magir tiwann yɔɔr, ke un mag kitiŋ nmɔn nin ki timansanmɔnn nin igon.

eng: Now the city was laid out as a square. Its length was the same as its width. He measured the city with the rod, and it was fifteen hundred miles. Its length and width and height were equal.
local: Kitiŋ nmɔn bi nan loon ki nann kitinmɔg le, le ki fufɔgim nin ki yinyalim ye nba. Le u nan ta u salima a jaangbiɛgin ki mag kitiŋ nmɔn, le ki fufɔgim ye maal turil piig nin turil bilee, ki fufɔgim nin ki yinyalim nin ki kpikperim biɛn nan ye nba.

eng: He also measured the thickness of its wall. It was two hundred sixteen feet thick, as a person—or rather, an angel—measures things.
local: Le malaka nan mag kitiŋ nmɔn a gonl a li ke uninfo kaah magir tiwann man nin, le li pupoligim nan ye itaadann kobig nin pina nin ina.

eng: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was pure gold, like pure glass.
local: Sataŋ le bi nan ta maa ligonl nmɔn, le kitiŋin baantɔɔl ye salima bamɔn, ki nyagin ke niɛnu a maar.

eng: The city walls foundations were decorated with every kind of jewel. The first foundation was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was chalcedony, and the fourth was emerald.
local: Bi nan ta itangbag yan jɔɔ kudaapɔɔrug le balbal ki mann kitiŋ nmɔn a gon a fiɛl, chinchinn a fiɛl a tangbagilin le nan ye sataŋ, lan kpiɛ ilee nan ye litangbag buginu, le lan paa itaa nan ye litangbag sɔɔn kpiɛ kpiɛ, lan paa ina a tangbag nan nann kisikpinnsiig.

eng: The fifth was sardonyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, and the eighth was beryl. The ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
local: lan paa iŋmu nan ye litangbag mann, lan paa iloob nan ye lichabil, lan paa ilile nan ye litangbagil lan ye nduuyom sɔɔn, le lan paa iniin nan ye litangbag sɔɔn lan nann buginu, lan paa iwiɛ nan ye litangbagil lan ye nduuyom, lan paa piig nan ye litangbagil lan peen faa, lan paa piig nin liba nan ye litangbagil lan mann ke titoog, le lan kpiɛ piig nin ilee-n nan ye litangbagil lan man ke nduuyom.

eng: The twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was made from a single pearl. And the citys main street was pure gold, as transparent as glass.
local: Le itimansanmɔn piig nin ilee nmɔn nan ye nliɛm piig nin milee le, le ntimansanmɔn nba biɛn nan ye nliɛm nba le. Kitiŋ nmɔn a sanjal nan ye salima bamɔnl le ki ga ke niɛnu-n.

eng: I didnt see a temple in the city, because its temple is the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb.
local: Maa nan kan iwonjiɛn niin kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ni, nan bu-n Uyudaan Uwonbɔr Npɔɔn biɛn daan nin Kipeebigin le ye ki wonjiɛn niin.

eng: The city doesnt need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because Gods glory is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
local: Kitiŋ nmɔn kaa ban nween bee uŋmal a wonweem li nyagin ki bu, nan bu-n Uwonbɔr a pagin le tenn ki nwonween, le Kipeebigin ye nin yaan a fila.

eng: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
local: Ibol biɛn na li choon kitiŋ nmɔn a wonweemin, le dulinya a bɔrtiib na li ta bi ŋinŋam ki kɔɔ niin.

eng: Its gates will never be shut by day, and there will be no night there.
local: Lidaal kaa be wee ki timansanmɔn li loon ni, nan bu-n kunyiɛg kaa na yin be niin ni.

eng: They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
local: Le ibol biɛn na li ta bi pag nin jilima ki kɔɔ niin.

eng: Nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is vile and deceitful, but only those who are registered in the Lambs scroll of life.
local: Le niwann nan biɛn jɔɔ tijaginn bee ban biɛn tun ifa bee kularug a toon kaa na li kɔɔ kitiŋ nmɔn yaan ni, amaa ban a yin sɔb be Kipeebigin a miɛfol a gbɔŋin a binba.

eng: Then the angel showed me the river of life-giving water, shining like crystal, flowing from the throne of God and the Lamb.
local: Iyaayoo le malaka nan wɔɔnim nkpen man ye limiɛfol a nyum, ki ween ke niɛnu a maarin, ki puu nyan Uwonbɔr nin Kipeebigin a naangbɔŋin.

eng: through the middle of the citys main street. On each side of the river is the tree of life, which produces twelve crops of fruit, bearing its fruit each month. The trees leaves are for the healing of the nations.
local: ki jiir kitiŋin a sanjal a sisiigin. Le limiɛfol a siigi nan si ikpengon ilee nmɔn biɛn bu, le isiig nmɔn lon iloon itaa piig nin ilee, uŋmal biɛn ke i lo le. Le isiig nmɔn a far ye kutebug ki sei ibol biɛn.

eng: There will no longer be any curse. The throne of God and the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will worship him.
local: Limɔpool kaah ji na li be niin ni, Uwonbɔr nin Kipeebigin a naangbɔŋ na li be kitiŋ nmɔn yaan, le U toontum na li tun tenn u.

eng: They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
local: Bi na li kan u num paam, le u yinl na li paa bi yutun bu.

eng: Night will be no more. They wont need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, for the Lord God will shine on them, and they will rule forever and always.
local: Kunyiɛg kaa ji na yin be nin yaan ni. Baa ji na li ban fila a wonweemi, bee nween a wonweemi, nan bu-n Uyudaan Uwonbɔr na li sei bi nwonween. Le bi na li ji nnaam ki li be hali nin iyoo yan kaa jɔɔ ndiɛm.

eng: Then he said to me, These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants what must soon take place.
local: Le malaka nan beim ke, Imɔleen nan ye ibamɔn le ki ye gaa jin. Uyudaan wan ye bilinlem a naam a Wonbɔru tun U malakam bin tan wɔɔn U toontumin nan kpaa nin lin tun daandaan nan.

eng: Look! Im coming soon. Favored is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll.
local: Ni lig, n chaan le daandaan nan! Tikaar be ban ŋmag ilinlen leen a mɔboi yan be kugbɔŋ nan yaan nin bu.

eng: I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. When I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown them to me.
local: Min, Jɔnn, le ye wan gbir ki kan ni nan biɛn. N-yonn man wee n nan gbir wee ki kan ni-n, le n sɔɔn malaka wan gbaa wɔɔnimin a taatum ki jiɛn u.

eng: But he said to me, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters, the prophets, and those who keep the words of this scroll. Worship God.
local: Amaa le u beim ke, Laa ŋa ni! N ye fin nin a nabum ban ye bilinlemin a toontunn kpiɛg le, nin ban biɛn nɔɔ kugbɔŋ nan kaah len man nin. Li jiɛn Uwonbɔr a.

eng: Then he said to me, Dont seal up the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll, because the time is near.
local: Le u beim ke, Laa ta tiwann tabin wann ki tabin ilinlen leen a mɔboi yan be kugbɔŋ nan yaan bu, nan bu-n iyoo mala.

eng: Let those who do wrong keep doing what is wrong. Let the filthy still be filthy. Let those who are righteous keep doing what is right. Let those who are holy still be holy.
local: Che wan tun tibir li gbaa tun tibira; che wan tun tijaginn li gbaa tun u jaginn na; che wan tun ibuchin toon li gbaa tun ibuchin toon na; le wan be kasi u li gbaa be kasiu.

eng: Look! Im coming soon. My reward is with me, to repay all people as their actions deserve.
local: Ni lig! N chaan le daandaan nan! N nyɔɔr be n ban, le n li ta sei uninfo biɛn nin waah tun nan.

eng: I am the alpha and the omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
local: Min le ye Alifa nin Omega, Chinchinn nin Njoorim, Npiinim nin Ndiɛm.

eng: Favored are those who wash their robes so that they may have the right of access to the tree of life and may enter the city by the gates.
local: Tikaar be ban ŋunn bi wann yɔɔr bu, bi na li kan nsan limiɛfol a siigin ki nɔɔ itimansanmɔnn nin yaan ki kɔɔ kitiŋ nmɔn yaan.

eng: Outside are the dogs, the drug users and spell-casters, those who commit sexual immorality, the murderers, the idolaters, and all who love and practice deception.
local: Ban kaa na yin be kitiŋ nmɔn a poolin ye: Ibɔ, nin ban tun inyɔg a toon, nin ban tun iwanchɔgin toon lipiibanl nin lijabanl, nin bininfokub, nin bibawomanib, nin ban jiɛ ki sin tun tibɔkar a toon.

eng: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to bear witness to all of you about these things for the churches. Im the root and descendant of David, the bright morning star.
local: Min, Yisa, le tun n malaka ke u nan sei biwonjiɛm a laginn siɛra nan. N ye Defid a tuŋ le ki ye u yaabil, ki ye kutaayɔg a ŋmabil lan ween.

eng: The Spirit and the bride say, Come! Let the one who hears say, Come! And let the one who is thirsty come! Let the one who wishes receive life-giving water as a gift.
local: Le Kinaaŋ nin ki bɔɔnh nin len ke, Dan! Wee ki che wan gbir nlen ke, Dan! Nnyunyum jɔɔ wan ke, ni che un dan; le wan ban ke ni che un gaam limiɛfol a nyum sei kaam man ye yɔli.

eng: Now I bear witness to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll: If anyone adds to them, God will add to that person the plagues that are written in this scroll.
local: N kpaan wan biɛn gbir ilinlen leen a mɔboi yan be kugbɔŋ nan yaan ke: Ni ye uba ta niba ki kpiɛ imɔleen nmɔn bu ke, Uwonbɔr tɔɔl li kpiɛ imag yan a bɔr sɔb be kugbɔŋ nan yaan udaan bu.

eng: If anyone takes away from the words of this scroll of prophecy, God will take away that persons share in the tree of life and the holy city, which are described in this scroll.
local: Le wan mu nyan niba ilinlen leen a mɔboi a gbɔŋ nan yaan ke, Uwonbɔr li nyan udaan a tol lan be limiɛfol a siigin nin kasi a tiŋ kan a bɔr sɔb be kugbɔŋ nan yaan nin.

eng: The one who bears witness to these things says, Yes, Im coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!.
local: Wan jin ni niɛ nan biɛn a siɛra len ke, Nn, n chaan le daandaan nan. Ibamɔn. Dan, Uyudaan Yisa.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all.
local: Uyudaan Yisa a nyunyunbasanl li be nin Uwonbɔr a ninfob. Ibamɔn.
